Title: Elderly killed in Road accident
content: A senior citizen was killed in an accident near the Nashik Road Police Station. The deceased has been identified as Lakshman Mankar 79, a resident of Chadegaon Village near Nashik Road. The Nashik Road Police said Mankar was riding his motorcycle when another motorcycle knocked his vehicle down from behind. The accident took place on Friday at around 4 PM. The elderly succumbed to the injuries on Tuesday late night in another incident. A 73-year-old man from Gangapur Road was grievously injured in an accident on Tuesday.

Title: UP to build hospitals in border areas
content: The deputy chief minister of Lucknow, Brajesh Pathak, announced on Thursday that the state government will build 50 to 100 bed hospitals on the interstate borders of Uttar Pradesh, which have less resources so that people of such areas can get free of cost treatment in their home district and do not have to run to big cities. A blueprint is being prepared for the number of hospitals and resources required for the project. emphasis will be on resources to deal with dengue malaria and chikungunya, besides treatment of infectious diseases, gall bladder stones and other serious diseases. In case of Road accidents, primary level treatment will also be given to the injured. Doctors specialising in pathology, radiology, medicine, orthopaedics, and surgery, and paramedics will be deployed in the hospitals from the advice of the doctor to the facility of investigation. Relief will be given to patients by strengthening the structure of the health department. Doctors will be deployed so that patients do not have to run to the big hospitals of the city for minor ailments. This will reduce the number of people suffering from minor ailments in big hospitals, and serious patients will be able to get better treatment.

Title: Police plan to introduce e-challan, induct 150 highway patrol vehicles
content: The Chandigarh government has announced plans to establish an effective challan system and introduce an echallan system across Punjab. The echallan system will be introduced first in big cities, including Ludhiana, Amritsar, and Jalandhar. Over 1400 cameras have already been installed in Ludhiana for the purpose of echallans. The traffic wing also has plans to revive the existing highway patrol system and introduce 150 highway patrol vehicles to reduce Road accidents and fatalities. The traffic wing will promote a sense of responsibility among all users of the road, and 15 road safety professionals will join the traffic police wing. The traffic wing also plans to introduce cutting-edge technologies like smart barricades, 5-genabled CCTV cameras, contactless challans, and speed management systems. The traffic wing will also focus on research on the causes of traffic congestion and Road accidents in the region, identify potential solutions, and develop strategies to reduce their occurrence.

Title: OHRC orders ₹5L to kin of girl killed in accident
content: The Odisha Human Rights Commission (OHRC) is asking the state government to pay Rs. 5 lakh compensation to the parents of a plus III student who was run over by a truck in front of Ravenshaw University in September 2019. The commission is also asking the government to ensure better management of traffic, and to take other measures to prevent accidents like this from happening in the future.

Title: Use seat belts and wear helmets; don’t drive when drunk, says District magistrate Vishak G Iyer in Kanpur
content: The Kanpur District Magistrate, Vishak G. Iyer, on Thursday inaugurated the Road Safety Month at Vikas Nagar Depot to create awareness about traffic rules for safe and smooth traffic movement. The District Magistrate in his address stated that Kanpur District was surrounded by national highways from all sides and there was a great need for people to be aware of traffic rules last year as per the government data over 618 people died in Road accidents for flouting traffic rules. All the people should follow the traffic rules use seat belts helmets etc mobile phones should not be used while driving never drive a vehicle in an inebriated state. He added he said that in this traffic month different awareness programs would be organized by the traffic department according to the roster every day to make all the public aware about traffic rules and safety standards. On this occasion, the District Magistrate honoured Sp Singh and Sanjay Kumar Singh who saved lives by helping the injured during an accident on the road and taking the injured to the hospital. During the inauguration of Road Safety Month, Assistant Divisional Transport Officer Administration, Mr. Sudhir Verma, Arto Udayveer Singh Sunil Dutt, Ambuj, and Manvendra Singh besides Basic Education Officer, Mr. Sur

Title: Injured in Road accident, Maharashtra BJP MLA Jaykumar Gore discharged from Pune's Ruby Hall Clinic
content: The accident was reported on December 24 on the Pune-Pandharpur road in Satara district near Malthan. He was admitted to a Pune hospital along with his driver and two guards.

Title: UP to build hospitals in border areas
content: Deputy chief minister Brajesh Pathak on Thursday announced that the state government will build 50 to 100 bed hospitals on the interstate borders of UP which have less resources so that people of such areas can get free of cost treatment in their home district and do not have to run to big cities.

Title: Two die in Road accident near Trichy
content: Five others, including the truck driver, suffered injuries in the accident that happened near Padalur in Perambalur district.

Title: Road accidents high, review safety steps: SC panel to Odisha CM Naveen Patnaik
content: The Supreme Court Committee on Road Safety has urged chief minister Naveen Patnaik to review the road safety scenario in Odisha in view of an alarming rise in accident fatalities in the state. The SC panel is likely to send some of its officials to the state in the next two weeks to inspect the compliance of its orders.

Title: 5 die in bus-truck collision near Mathania in Jodhpur
content: Five persons died and about three dozen were injured in a head-on collision of a passenger bus and a truck near Mathania in Jodhpur on Friday afternoon. Three of them died on the spot while two others succumbed in the hospital in Jodhpur.

Title: Coimbatore range gets DIG, Tirupur gets CoP
content: This is a brief summary of a news article about a new deputy inspector general of police in the coimbatore range. Vijayakumar took over from Muthusamy on Friday and will focus on addressing public grievances and preventing crimes against women and children. He is from the 2009 batch of IPS officers and has served in various positions in the past, most recently in Chennai where he was the deputy commissioner of police. Praveen Kumar Abhinapu took over as the new commissioner of police on Friday, and he plans to focus on preventing Road accidents and crimes against women and children. He is from the 2005 batch of IPS officers and has served in various positions in the past, most recently in Salem as the inspector general of police. He won the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister's Medal for Outstanding Devotion to Duty in 2018.

Title: Killer roads: Ludhiana’s accident death rate saw 28% rise in 2021
content: A recent report from the state government has revealed that Ludhiana's road safety rate is the worst in the past three years. This has raised serious concerns over road safety in the district. At 160 health burden due to accidents recorded an increase of 28 in 2021 as compared to 2020 when the rate stood at 125 according to Punjab Road accidents and Traffic Report 2021.

Title: Steep rise in accident cases, deaths in Thiruvananthapuram last year
content: The state crime records bureau in Kerala has revealed that there has been an alarming rise in the number of accidents and deaths in the district in the past couple of years. In 2022, about 170 people lost their lives in around 1750 accidents. 107 people died in 2020 and that number rose to 112 and 170 in 2021 and 2022 respectively when it comes to traffic accidents. In 2018, there were 2129 accidents and 178 deaths, and in 2019, there were 1995 accidents and 177 deaths. However, the reason for the low accident death rates in 2020 could be the pandemic restrictions and lockdown, but gradually when the restrictions were lifted, there was a surge in the number of deaths and accidents. Meanwhile, the officials of the motor vehicle department opined that the active involvement of the department helped to reduce the number of Road accidents and deaths. When we take a look at the number of vehicles on the road in 2010 and in 2022, one can easily understand the fact that it has doubled. However, the number of accidents and deaths has not doubled after the pandemic restrictions were lifted. There was a gradual rise in the number of accidents and deaths, but it stands well below the figures before the pandemic. Motor vehicle inspector at the motor vehicle department said that the officials of the

Title: Three migrant workers killed in Road accident in Punjab's Bhullar
content: Three migrant labourers were killed and two others sustained serious injuries when a speeding car hit them and overturned near Bhullar village on Muktsar-Bathinda road late Friday night.

Title: Belagavi's Chikodi official hailed for saving Road accident victim
content: A police officer in Belagavi, India, stopped his car to help a person who had been in a car accident and was bleeding heavily. The officer, Madhav Gitte, also contacted an ambulance and took the injured person to the hospital. The injured person is said to be stable and out of danger. The officer is from the 2020 batch of IAS officers.

Title: SC pushes for e-monitoring to check over-speeding
content: Cause-wise analysis of Road accidents revealed 55.9% (87,050 out of 1,55,622 deaths) and 27.5% (42,853 out of 1,55,622 deaths) of fatalities were due to speeding and dangerous/careless driving or overtaking, respectively, in 2021.

Title: Couple returning from wedding killed in road crash in Chhattisgarh’s Durg
content: A couple died after their scooter collided head-on with a car on the bridge over Shivnath river in Chhattisgarh’s Durg on Saturday night.

Title: Two die in Godda Road accident
content: In a Road accident on Friday night, two employees of an outsourcing company working for the Adani Power Plant were killed. Five people have died in Road accidents in the district since January 1. The deceased are Brambhadeo Pandit of Lehri Tola in Uttar Pradesh and Vinod Kumar of Sahibganj in Uttar Pradesh. They were killed on their way home when a speeding car hit their motorcycle at about 7:30 PM near Pakadiya village under the town police station. The collision was so intense that the duo was thrown 50 meters away. The driver of the car, who is yet to be identified, was seemingly drunk and is being treated at a private hospital.

Title: Now, Odisha cops use tougher IPC section to check drink driving
content: The Odisha Police has started imposing Section 279 of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) against drunken drivers in order to increase the overall conviction rate in the state. Until recently, the cops were sending prosecution reports to lower courts against drunk drivers under Section 185 of the Motor Vehicles Act, but this is not treated as an offence nor does it end in conviction such cases are normally disposed of by courts following imposition of certain fine amounts on the violators. Odisha Police asked Superintendents of Police (SPs) to add Section 279 of the IPC along with the 185 of the Motor Vehicles Act to ensure that the case is treated as an offence. An offence can be registered against cognisable crimes falling either under the IPC or under the Special and Local Laws (SLL) only if it is linked to an offence. Only IPC or SLL sections that result in conviction result in a conviction in drunken driving cases. Mv sections end in payment of fine which is not treated as a conviction. So far, 1000 drunken driving cases have ended in conviction in the state in the last three months, with Angul Khurda and Jharsuguda districts topping the list with 280 and 230 convictions, but only 600 criminal cases resulted in conviction in

Title: Bike rider dead as garbage truck knocks him down
content: A two-wheeler rider died in a Road accident in Trichy on Friday evening and a case has been booked against the truck driver. A 17-year-old boy was riding his bike when he hit a 12-year-old girl in Nadupatti on Friday morning. The girl was taken to Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Government Hospital in Trichy and later handed over to the rider's relatives.

Title: Cop killed in accident on Pratapgarh-Amethi highway
content: Inspector Amar Singh Raghuvanshi, 45, died after his car was allegedly hit by a speeding truck on Pratapgarhamethi Highway near Kalyanpur Road on Saturday morning. The incident took place under the limits of Antoo Police Station of Pratapgarh. According to police, the impact of the collision was such that both vehicles overturned and the inspector was trapped inside the car. It took over three hours to pull him out after cutting the car gate. Senior police officials rushed to the spot and sent the body for postmortem. The driver of the truck managed to escape.

Title: Seven days into new year, 4 killed in Road accidents
content: In the new year, four people have died in Road accidents in Nashik City. The police are trying to crack down on motorists who are driving recklessly. One of the main reasons for these accidents is that drivers are speeding. The police are also trying to enforce traffic rules more strictly. There have been three accidents in Gangapur, one in Ambad, and one in Bhadrakali so far this year.

Title: MBA student from Sitamarhi dies in Jaipur Road accident
content: A 22-year-old MBA student from Bihar was killed after his SUV rammed into railings on Mahal Road in Jaipur on Thursday.

Title: Delhi hit-and-run case: Kanjhawala witness under drug cloud, Delhi Police probing
content: Delhi Police is probing the antecedents of Nidhi, who was accompanying Anjali, the victim in the Kanjhawala accident, after it emerged that she was allegedly caught by Government Railway Police (GRP), Agra, in 2020 in a drug case. Meanwhile, the Delhi cops have found traffic challans worth a few thousand rupees pending against her.

Title: Bus runs over woman in Ultadanga
content: A woman in her 60s died after being hit by a bus on a main road near a crossing on Saturday night. The bus was stopped at a red light when the woman, who was walking in the opposite direction, came in front of it. The bus driver was unable to stop in time and the woman was killed. The bus has been seized by the police and the driver has been arrested. Locals say the accident happened around 10:30pm.

Title: Kavasam Foundation extends support to victims of crime in Coimbatore
content: The Kavasam Foundation was created in Coimbatore, India, to help victims of crime and Road accidents. The project is the brainchild of City Police Commissioner V Balakrishnan, Managing Director of Adwaith Lakshmi Industries, and will be chaired by Lakshmi Industries' Chairman, Balakrishnan. During the launch event on Friday, Balakrishnan said the Foundation will help families of the victims monetarily find jobs and psychological support, as well as provide educational assistance. Coimbatore Collector G.S. Sameeran said he will support taking the model to other parts of the state. At the event, benefits worth 10 lakh were given to 25 families. Managing Trustee of PSG Sons' Charities, L.Gopalakrishnan, promised to help 100 children who need educational support through PSG Maanavar Illam V.Lakshminarayanaswamy of Suguna Group said the firm will also offer educational support.

Title: MNC exec, kin die after SUV overturns in TT Ngr
content: A bhopal man lost control of his car after hitting a speed breaker, and the car overturned, killing him and his brother-in-law. The brother-in-law was also injured, and is currently in the hospital. The police have registered a case and are investigating.

Title: 40 killed in Senegal bus disaster
content: Forty people were killed and 85 were injured when two buses crashed in a head-on collision overnight on an arterial road in Senegal's central Kaffrine region, the government said on Sunday.

Title: Doctors in Delhi perform complex face rebuilding of accident victim
content: A team of doctors from Sir Ganga Ram Hospital has successfully performed a complex surgical procedure to pull out an accident victim — a 20-year-old Delhi-based IT professional — from the jaws of death and reconstructed his face which was crushed beyond recognition.

Title: Youth dies in Road accident
content: A 24-year-old man was killed in a Road accident near Narapam village in Kothavalasa mandal in Vizianagaram district. The deceased was identified as Kj Sai, a native of Deverapalle in Anakapalle district. Police said the accident took place when Sai was returning from Vizag City to his home town on a bike. A case has been registered.

Title: Jammu police trace family that conspires its death in car accident to evade huge loans
content: Jammu police have traced a couple from from Haryana’s Panchkula who faked their own deaths, along with their minor daughter, in a staged car accident to evade paying back large loans.

Title: Elderly man dies in Road accident
content: A 65-year-old man died after being knocked down by a truck driver in Nashik on Friday. Kiran Mahadu Jadhav, a resident of Mhasrul, was identified as the victim based on the complaint of his son. The truck driver, identified as Rajesh, has been arrested and police are investigating the case.

Title: Haber Road accident exposes threat posed by encroachments
content: A 32-year-old bike rider was mowed down by a truck on Friday on Heber Road in Trichy. The accident has exposed the threat posed by encroachments on arterial roads in Trichy City, which have been increasing the probability of twowheeler riders being knocked down from behind. In Friday's incident, the bike rider had taken a right turn around Parangiri Velu Park on Heber Road when he was fatally knocked down by the truck from behind while bad road and unsafe conditions for vehicles to take turns were blamed for the incident. The role of encroachments was undermined, Heber Road which connects Trichy District Court and Cantonment Police Station with congested Bhima Nagar and Palakkarai was once used for public transportation over the years as the road was encroached by shops, private and government buses were not allowed due to traffic bottlenecks. The recent eviction drives were not comprehensive enough to curtail shops from misusing public space. "Eateries have encroached around 3 feet 4 feet of vacant space if encroachments are evicted public transportation can be brought back on Heber Road," said a resident of Bhima Nagar.

Title: Vizag cops find 69 black spots to put brakes on road mishaps
content: The city of Visakhapatnam in India has identified 69 locations where accidents and deaths have occurred in the past few years. The black spots include a point on the Telugu Talli Flyover, a few spots on the NH16 stretch between Gajuwaka Maddilapalem and Yendada, and some at Baaji Junction and Urvasi Junction. Every year, at least five to six accidents occur at these locations, and deaths due to accidents have increased to 333 in 2022 from 329 in 2021. The police also observed that it is difficult to rectify some of the black spots, and they will put up more caution boards to alert the motorists to reduce mishaps.

Title: 'Increase bonfires & shelters, ensure no one sleeps in open'
content: Deputy chief minister Brajesh Pathak on Sunday directed officials to increase the number of bonfires and improve arrangements at night shelters to provide relief to the destitutes in harsh weather conditions.

Title: Kerala home secretary V Venu, wife, son injured in Road accident
content: Kerala home secretary V Venu, his wife Sarada Muraleedharan who is the additional chief secretary of the local-self government department, son Sabari, driver Abhilash and family friends - Pranav and Saurav - were injured in an accident near Kayamkulam in Alappuzha district in the wee hours of Monday.

Title: Doda police nab family that faked death in road mishap to evade huge loans
content: Doda police on Sunday brought back from Haryana a couple who faked their death along with their minor daughter in a Road accident under a well thought out plan to evade huge loans that they had taken. They are yet to be formally arrested.

Title: Pune-Mumbai Expressway stretch saw more crash deaths in 2022 than 2021
content: The stretch between Kiwale toll post and Amrutanjan Point on the Pune-Mumbai expressway saw as many as 28 Road accident deaths last year, up from 22 in 2021.

Title: Delhi witnesses season's longest fog spell; rail, air traffic hit
content: Delhi saw cold wave conditions for the fifth consecutive day on Monday and the season's longest fog spell which crippled road, rail and air traffic movement. India Meteorological Department officials said the Palam observatory, near the Indira Gandhi International (IGI) Airport, recorded visibility levels below 1,000 metres from 8.30 pm on Sunday to 4.30 pm on Monday.

Title: Delhi colder than most places in Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh for 5th consecutive day
content: On Monday, Delhi saw cold wave conditions for the fifth day in a row as very dense fog reduced visibility to just 25 metres.

Title: Men drive, women die: Indian roads have a gender problem
content: While everyone asks how a body was dragged 12 km without police stepping in, nothing prevents the Delhi hit-and-run from happening again. It also reveals a massive blind spot

Title: 2 killed, 1 injured as truck rams 3 two-wheelers
content: A woman and two men were killed when a truck crashed into three twowheelers in Kerala. The deceased have been identified as Lisa Anthony 38 of Panayikulam and Naseeb 35 of North Paravur. Raveendran 54 of North Paravur was also injured and was admitted to a private hospital. The incident happened around 1030am on Monday on the stretch between Cheranellore Signal and Varapuzha Bridge in front of an Indian Oil Petrol Pump. Lisa was on her scooter while Naseeb and Raveendran were on their bikes. There were no pillion riders on the three twowheelers the vehicles were headed towards Cheranellore and the truck was also heading in the same direction. One among the bikers was about to turn towards the petrol pump when the truck rammed all three vehicles.

Title: Drivers cock a snook at Rs 5,000 fine for flouting rule in Guwahati
content: The Department of Transport in Guwahati, Assam, recently announced that driving on the wrong side of the road would attract hefty fines.

Title: Burning of electric cables under bridge poses threat
content: The article discusses how motorists are having problems with smoke and pungent fumes emanating from burning cables under the bridge connecting Vijayawada and Mangalagiri. This is causing many accidents and people are requesting authorities to take action.

Title: 1 killed, four injured in 2 Road accidents in Indore division
content: A man was killed and four others were injured in two accidents in Indore Division on Monday morning. The first accident happened in Ratlam District when a couple riding a bike was hit by an earth mover from the front in Matwala village. The husband, Babulal Singar, died on the spot and his wife, Seeta Singar, was injured. Their child, Tejpal, had a close shave in the accident. Police said the family residents of Banswara District in Rajasthan were traveling from Barkheda to Bajna when the accident took place. They were rushed to hospital by their relatives traveling with them on another vehicle. Doctors declared Babulal dead and Seeta was admitted for treatment. Police said Rawati Police Station incharge, Patiram Rawate, told TOI, "The death has been recorded and the earth mover has been seized. The investigations further in the matter are ongoing." In another accident, three youths were injured after being hit by a crane around 4 am in Burhanpur. They were admitted to a private hospital for treatment. Police said the injured were identified as Abhilash Kumar 26, Anil Gadhe 26, and Ajay Ghete 28. They were returning

Title: Friends & cop mowed down man for Rs 50 lakh insurance sum in Hyderabad
content: Shadnagar police solved an alleged murder, staged as a Road accident in December 2021, and arrested four persons, including a head constable, on Monday.

Title: NHAI takes up traffic-calming measures on Mumbai-Agra highway
content: The Nashik division of the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has taken up 'traffic-calming measures' on nine junctions of the Mumbai Agra highway to prevent Road accidents.

Title: NHAI takes up traffic-calming measures on Mumbai-Agra highway
content: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) is taking measures to prevent Road accidents on nine junctions on the Mumbai-Agra Highway. These measures include installing rumbler strips, cateyes, solar blinkers, and other safety measures. After the Mirchi Hotel accident on October 8, all departments concerned have started taking efforts to prevent Road accidents in the district. NHAI officials say the road safety works are being done through the contractors, and that the results of the accident calming measures will be evident soon.

Title: Caught in Delhi fog? Here’s how you can reach home safe and sound
content: Turn on the hazard lights, drive at a slow pace and don’t tail vehicles. Use the horn periodically, employ low beam headlights and, above all, have no distractions like loud music.

Title: Biker dies in Road accident
content: A man died after being hit by a bus and another man was injured. The man who died was from Mogarahalli Manti Village in Srirangapatna Taluk and the man who was injured is from Manti Village in the same Taluk. The accident happened on Monday evening and the man who died was 25 years old. The man who was injured is 24 years old and he is currently in the hospital.

Title: Teenager killed in Road accident in Kochi
content: An 18-year-old was killed in a Road accident at Puthiya Road on Willingdon Island on Monday night. The deceased has been identified as Ajmal Hamsa of Thankalam, Kothamangalam. The youth died after a car collided with his two-wheeler.

Title: Haryana to initiate special drive to identify new accident-prone areas
content: The Haryana traffic police is all set to have a fresh look into the accident-prone dark spots and to identify new dark spots to prevent Road accidents in the state.

Title: Maximum Road accident deaths reported from Kanpur-Sagar road
content: The Kanpur district administration is serious about preventing Road accident deaths, especially on highways. A joint team of the traffic and regional transport department is working to reduce deaths due to accidents on Kanpur's main highway, the Kanpur-Sagar National Highway. The team has suggested constructing tenfoot over bridges in some villages to identify the accident prone areas.

Title: Keep tabs on kids: Sawai Man Singh Hospital to parents
content: The Jaipur Sawai Man Singh SMS Hospital is urging the public to be careful when flying kites during the upcoming kite flying season. Many incidents of children falling from rooftops and Road accidents of children chasing falling kites have been reported in the beginning of January.

Title: Rich tributes paid to Sandhu couple
content: Sentiments prevailed as the management, and staff of Spring Dale Senior School remembered and paid rich tributes to their mentors late Dr Shivinder Singh Sandhu, former Managing Trustee Spring Dale Educational Society and Manveen Sandhu, former Principal, Spring Dale Senior School, who passed into eternity on this day, 14 years ago.

Title: Only 32 stretches in city prone to jams: Nagpur traffic police survey
content: Frequent traffic jams are witnessed in almost every locality. But according to a study conducted by Nagpur traffic police, there are only 32 stretches across the city that are prone to frequent traffic jams. The study was conducted by traffic branches of 10 zones.

Title: ICU patient suffers burns; doctor & hospital employees booked in Pune
content: The Koregaon Park police have registered a case of negligence causing burn injuries to a 70-year-old patient under treatment in the ICU of a city hospital against the doctor and the staff attending him.

Title: ICU patient suffers burns; doc & hospital employees booked
content: A 70-year-old patient is treated in the ICU of a city hospital after slipping into hypothermia. The patient's son, a lawyer, files a complaint for negligence against the doctor and the staff attending him. The hospital's joint medical superintendent tells the media that the patient was brought to the hospital at 1am on January 5 in a comatose state, and that his vitals were unstable. The patient was operated on January 5 with highrisk consent, and did not show any improvement. The patient went into hypothermia on January 5 evening, and was rewarmed to increase his body temperature. The patient suffered superficial burns on his abdomen, covering an area of around 4. The patient's son tells the media that the condition of his father was stable after the surgery, but that he suffered burn injuries because of the negligence of doctors and nurses. The incident could have been averted had they remained vigilant.

Title: Boy's speeding motorcycle on wrong side crashes into car in Pune
content: A boy (16) suffered serious back injury and two other teenagers (15 and 18) riding pillion on his bike fractured their legs after the motorcycle, on the wrong side of the road, collided with a car on January 2 at Ambi village in Maval taluka.

Title: 5 dead & 5 hurt in 3 separate Road accidents in Nashik city & rural
content: Five people were killed and five others were injured in three separate Road accidents in Nashik city and parts of the rural areas on Tuesday and Wednesday.

Title: 1 dead, 2 injured in Road accident in Lucknow
content: A speeding car knocked down a 62-year-old man on two-wheeler and injured two others on Naka flyover in Lucknow late on Wednesday night.

Title: Take steps to reduce Road accidents in Haryana: Vij
content: The home minister of Haryana, Anil Vij, has directed the police to identify areas in the state where accidents are happening frequently due to stray animals, and to take steps to reduce accidents. He also instructed the officials to install grills on roadsides, to take action to prevent illegal cuts on roads, and to set up a committee to oversee the deployment of road safety officers.

Title: Three charred to death following car-truck collision in UP's Hamirpur
content: Three persons were charred to death as a car crashed into a stranded truck due to dense fog on Bundelkhand Expressway in Hamirpur early on Thursday morning.

Title: Govt engineers to undergo training on road safety
content: The state government in Odisha is planning to launch a training curriculum for road engineers in order to hone their knowledge on road safety. This is expected to improve defective road designs that often lead to accidents. The engineers need to take a test and if they fail to clear it, their annual increment may not be sanctioned.

Title: State plans training course on road safety for engineers
content: The state government in Odisha is planning to launch a training curriculum for road engineers in order to hone their knowledge on road safety. This is expected to improve defective road designs that often lead to accidents. If an engineer fails to clear a test after completing the training, their annual increment may not be sanctioned.

Title: 6,626 killed in Road accidents last year in Bihar, most victims youth
content: In 2022, 6626 people died in Road accidents in Bihar. In 2021, 5476 people got injured in Road accidents, and 7660 people were killed in Road accidents. In 2020, 8639 people were killed in Road accidents. State transport commissioner Seema Tripathi said that the majority of Road accident victims are in the young age group, especially college students who drive vehicles carelessly or overtake other vehicles or speeding. "During the road safety week from January 11 to 17, awareness drive will be held in all districts of Bihar through radio jingles, nukad natak' and short movies to sensitise commuters to drive safely by following motor vehicles act," she said. "The department is also taking measures to curb Road accidents by identifying not only the black spots but also the vulnerable points black spots are such areas where 10 accidents and 10 deaths took place within a year and the vulnerable points are those where more than three persons died and less than 10 accidents took place within a year. With the help of the nhai road construction department and rural works department, we are trying to identify the problems in such black spots and vulnerable points across the state. Measures like speed breakers, rumble strips, signage, zebra crossing and street lights are being taken as per

Title: 33rd Road Safety Week starts; many events planned
content: The 33rd Nashik Road Safety Week started on Wednesday with an inauguration program organized by the Nashik Regional Transport Office (RTO).

Title: Crash deaths on Mumbai roads down 27% to 259 in 2022
content: The crash-fatality ratio in Maharashtra has gone to an alarming 42 deaths for every 100 mishaps and this needs to be curbed, said state transport commissioner Vivek Bhimanwar at the inauguration of Road Safety Week at NCPA on Wednesday.

Title: One killed, 11 injured in 2 Road accidents in Jalaun
content: A person was killed and 11 others were injured in separate accidents on Wednesday due to dense fog. In the first accident, a driver of a bolero was killed while four students were injured after it rammed into a stationary dcm truck on the hamirpur highway in udanpur under the limits of kadaura police station area of Jalaun. The students were on their way to write their exam. Similarly, seven persons were injured and traffic on the jhansikanpur national highway was disrupted for nearly an hour after a pileup involving 10 vehicles due to dense fog on Wednesdays morning. The accident took place near sankat mochan mandir in atta police station area on the highway. Additional Superintendent of Police Aseem Chowdhary said that on receiving information police and ambulances reached the spot and rescue operation was launched at both the accident sites. "The injured were transported in ambulance to the medical college for treatment in the first accident which took place in kadaura area a bolero rammed into a stationary dcm truck while in the second accident 10 vehicles collided with each other apparently due to dense fog traffic has been restored after the accidental vehicles were removed from the highway."

Title: Road accidents linked to fog claimed 13,372 lives in 2021
content: Road accidents caused due to foggy and misty weather conditions claimed 13,372 lives in 2021 and another 25,360 were left injured — more than half were grievously injured. Among states and UTs, Uttar Pradesh recorded a maximum of 3,782 fatalities, followed by Bihar (1,800) and Madhya Pradesh (1,233) on this account. Goa, Andaman and Nicobar Islands and Lakshadweep did not report any road death due to this reason.

Title: Five good samaritans feted for helping accident victims in Chhapra district
content: The district transport department in Chhapra, India, on Wednesday honored five people who had acted as good Samaritans during Road accidents. The department has started a road safety week from January 11 to 17, during which various activities will be conducted to raise awareness about road safety. Among the people honored were residents of Beldari village under Doudpur police station, Ashok Kumar Sah from Sajjanpur under Dariapur police station, Manoj Kumar Singh from Bahoran Singh Ka Tola Manjhi under Manjhi police station, and Ajmatullah Khan from Kopa village. The department has recommended these names to the respective police stations, and the five individuals were presented with a bouquet and a shawl by the district magistrate and the superintendent of police. The department has also stated that during this week, eye checkup camps will be organized for commercial drivers and their health will be examined to minimize Road accidents.

Title: Rajasthan Road Safety Institute inaugurated
content: State transport minister Brijendra Ola on Wednesday inaugurated the State Road Safety Institute at HCMRIPA in the city.

Title: Events herald start of road safety week
content: The road safety week in Jharkhand started with a quiz on Wednesday. This was followed by a distribution of helmets on Thursday. The road safety week ended on Wednesday with a parade in which school students and cadets of the National Cadet Corps participated. Another program was held on Wednesdays at the toll plaza in Ranchi. Around 50 helmets were distributed among the pillion riders. The transport department would soon display banners and hoardings with messages on road safety at the busy crossings across the state capital. Aawareness programs like online quiz contests and half marathons would be organized in schools to promote road safety practices. In light of the prevailing foggy weather, regular checking drive would be held for red light and retro reflective sheeting on vehicles to avoid accidents. Joint transport commissioner Pradeep Kumar said “the district transport officers have been asked to conduct medical examination of the drivers engaged with commercial transport. The focus is on examining their eye sight color blindness and blood pressure. The exercise would be held at bus stands and other places determined after discussion with vehicle owners’ associations.”

Title: Regret inability to curb road fatalities, says Goa DGP
content: Goa director general of police (DGP) Jaspal Singh on Wednesday held a meeting with his top officials to review policing in 2022.

Title: 10 dead, 22 injured as bus carrying pilgrims to Shirdi collides with truck in Nashik; pall of gloom in Ambernath
content: The deceased were travelling by a luxury bus to Shirdi. The injured have been shifted to the Sinnar Rural Hospital and a private hospital in Sinnar.

Title: India lacks laws to deal with peegate-like offences
content: Air India incident is being dealt with criminally, but real issue is the harm done to the victim passenger

Title: Biker hit by truck dies
content: A biker died in a Road accident near a village in a taluk in Srirangapatna district. The biker was from the village of Tm Hosuru and was heading to Nilanakoppalu when the accident happened. A case has been registered at the Srirangapatna rural police station.

Title: Four labourers die, 20 hurt in Seraikela-Kharsawan accident
content: A Road accident in Seraikelakharsawan district killed four people and injured 20 others. The victims were from different parts of Chaibasa district in West Singbhum district in eastern India.

Title: Decks cleared, GMDA to build 4 footbridges on Golf Course Road
content: In a big relief to pedestrians, the construction of the four proposed foot overbridges (FOB) on the busy Golf Course Road is likely to start soon.

Title: Govt's goal is to cut down on accidents by 50% by 2024 end: Gadkari at Auto Expo
content: Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways, Nitin Gadkari, along with Minister of Heavy Industries, Mahendra Nath Pandey, officially inaugurated the Auto Expo 2023 here on Thursday.

Title: Auto Expo 2023: Nitin Gadkari bats for green hydrogen export and more safety features in vehicles
content: Nitin Gadkari also spoke about road safety in the country and the need for automakers to proactively make vehicles safer. The minister stated that India records 500,000 Road accidents and 100,000 related fatalities every year and the ministry aims to reduce this number by at least 50 percent by 2024 end

Title: 525 died in the vicinity of Delhi Metro stations: Report
content: The vicinity of Delhi Metro stations isn’t too safe for people. According to WRI India, 525 people died due to Road accidents near Metro stations in the capital in 2020-2021.

Title: Over 500 died at 100 junctions in 2020-21 in Delhi, loss pegged at Rs 1,100 crore
content: Over 500 people lost their lives in accidents at 100 traffic intersections in the city in 2020-2021, according to a report by World Resources Institute India. Despite the Covid lockdown in 2020 and a major part of 2021, as many as 2,432 people lost their lives in road incidents across the capital. Two-wheelers and pedestrians were the most vulnerable on the roads.

Title: BR Hills to host Chikka Jatre tomorrow after five years
content: The Chikka Jatre celebrations will be held after the Sankranti festival, after a five-year gap. The Chikka Jatre will be held on the very next day of Sankranti festival, Monday. The Brahmorathotsava also known as Dodda Jathre will be held in May. Local tribal residents who reside in the hamlets in the Brt Tiger Reserve offer special pujas on this occasion to Lord Ranganathaswamy. The temple was shut for devotees due to renovation works, and later Covid19 the authorities had not conducted the jatre when the temple reopened for devotees in April after the completion of renovation work. The Chamarajanagar district administration now decided to hold the Chikka Jatre. Over 500yearold temple atop the picturesque 5100foot Biligiri hills attached to the Brt Tiger Reserve attracts many devotees from all over the country. Following public outcry when the temple reached a crumbling stage, the departments of tourism archaeology and museums, and others renovated the structure at a cost of Rs. 24 crore. They replaced pillars repaired the sanctum sanctorum, and changed the flooring. Muzarai public works and other departments contributed Rs.

Title: Video of Gauri Shankar Bisen shouting at official viral, BJP red-faced in Madhya Pradesh
content: Gauri Shankar Bishen, a former minister for agriculture development in the state of Madhya Pradesh, was caught on video using crude language against a government official in a meeting. Bishen is the chairman of the Madhya Pradesh Pichhra Varg Kalyan Ayog – the state's welfare commission – and enjoys cabinet rank status. In the video, an irate Bishen is seen admonishing the regional transport officer (RTO) of the district of Balaghat, Avinash Gadpal. The administrative meeting which was held in Lalbarra Police Station on Thursday was attended by several government officials, including the subdivisional police officer (SDPO) of the police station in charge and RTO. A woman was recently killed in a Road accident in Balaghat after which the meeting was called. The meeting was also attended by Bishen. The former minister alleged that official vehicles of officials do not follow speed limits and 60 of people, especially those who ride two-wheelers in the district, do not have licenses. Bishen is seen shouting at the top of his voice while using offensive words against the RTO. The former minister even says that if he sees a single vehicle racing above speed limits, then "RTO tujhe

Title: Delhi court raps cops for ‘casual’ probe of murder case
content: A Delhi court has rapped cops for their “casual, highly unprofessional and absolutely insensitive” approach in investigating the unnatural death of an 85-year-old retired Border Security Force official.

Title: UP CM Yogi Adityanathgives financial aid of Rs 34 lakh to kin of deceased home guard
content: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath on Saturday gave financial assistance of Rs 34 lakh to the kin of a deceased home guard, who died in a Road accident earlier in March last year.

Title: Bihar second in accident deaths due to fog, mist
content: Bihar registered 1,800 deaths, the second highest in the country, due to foggy and misty weather conditions in 2021, as per the latest report on 'Road accidents in India 2021' released by the ministry of road transport and highways. Uttar Pradesh was on top with 3,782 fatalities due to similar reasons.

Title: BTech student killed as truck hits his Bullet in Greater Noida
content: A 24-year-old engineering student died while his friend — a BTech student of the same college as Sweety’s — was injured after a truck hit their Bullet in Greater Noida’s Alpha-1 early on Saturday. The truck driver has been arrested, police said.

Title: With 75 new models on display, Auto Expo 2023 set to attract 1 lakh visitors today
content: The Auto Expo 2023, being held at the Expo Mart in Greater Noida, has created a buzz not only in NCR but across the country. In new age cars, exhibited at the expo, makers have taken full care of modern technology as well as safety.

Title: Neeyum Njanum completes 800 episodes, team celebrates with a cake-cutting
content: Neeyum Njanum has completed the 800 episodes milestone. The team celebrated the same with a cake cutting. During the celebration, actor Shiju surprised director Janardhanan with a special gift

Title: How Sweety's friends, strangers united to save her life
content: An appeal to save the life of a 22-year-old student left in a coma by a hit-and-run driver on New Year’s Eve has raised Rs 29 lakh in two weeks. The response, from friends and teachers to police and strangers, is a reaffirmation of the best of humanity

Title: 27-yr-old Navy officer killed in accident in Kochi
content: A navy officer was killed in a Road accident in Fort Kochi on Sunday. The officer has been identified as Balasubramanyam from Tamil Nadu. The officer was travelling on his bike when a private bus collided with it. The driver and the bus have been taken into custody and a case is being registered against them.

Title: 1,559 helmet rule violations in Ernakulam registered in December
content: Neither the fact that a majority of Road accident casualties are two-wheeler riders who don't wear proper helmets nor the massive awareness programmes by authorities have been successful in slamming the brakes on helmet rule violations in Ernakulam, show data from the enforcement wing of the motor vehicles department

Title: One killed, 4 injured in tempo-bus collision
content: A person was killed in a Road accident, and four others were injured. The driver of the private bus managed to escape, and the police are investigating.

Title: Bhiwadi, Sriganganagar buck traffic fatality trend
content: Jaipur is a city in India that has strict enforcement of traffic rules and strategic planning that has led to a drastic decline in accident casualties in the city. In 2021, there were 232 deaths in Road accidents in Bhiwadi district, but in 2018 there were only 263 deaths. In Sriganganagar district, there were 177 deaths in Road accidents last year, but in 2021 there were only 306 deaths. This is due to the city's strict enforcement of traffic rules and strategic planning.

Title: Ramgarh traffic violators greeted with flowers, warned
content: The district transport department and the national highways authority of India (NHAI) started a vehicle checking drive on Saturday in Ramgarh district. The goal of the drive is to raise awareness about the importance of following traffic rules and to reduce Road accidents. violators were offered flowers and asked to follow traffic rules, and more stringent action will follow if violations continue.

Title: Bokaro priest cycles across town to create awareness
content: This is a story about a priest who has started wearing a bicycle helmet and riding around town to raise awareness about road safety. He has been very successful, and has even started to get famous. He has two children and he worries about them getting killed in accidents. He is doing this to try and make a difference.

Title: Congress says Gauri Shanka Bisen’s conduct against official ‘indecent’
content: Congress spokesman KK Mishra said that Gauri Shankar Bisen's name has become synonymous with "indecent" conduct. Mishra asked whether an FIR would be filed against Bisen for the kind of language he has used for a tribal officer under the Prevention of Atrocities Against Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes Act.

Title: Sugar mill senior official dies in Road accident in Ludhiana's Samrala
content: ​​​​Police officials identified the deceased as Amrinderpal Singh Dilawari of Mohali, who worked as chief engineer of a Sugar Mill in Budhewal village near Kohara of Ludhiana.

Title: ACL injuries in road crash worse than those sustained in sports
content: While anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injuries are usually caused by stress exerted on the knees during sports and fitness activities, in India, Road accidents are a major reason for this injury, the latest victim, of course, being Rishabh Pant. The cricketer recently underwent surgery on his knee for injury to the ligament, which is one of the strong bands of tissue that connect the thigh bone to the shinbone.

Title: Himachal Pradesh CM directs officers to develop advance warning system to mitigate disasters
content: In the wake of the recent land subsidence in Uttarakhand's Joshimath, Himachal Pradesh chief minister Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu on Monday directed the officers to develop an advance warning system to mitigate disasters and improve the disaster management response capacity system.

Title: Two students killed in Road accident in Karnataka
content: Sources said the accident took place when they were returning after attending classes at a evening college.

Title: ‘Train school drivers, issue norms for children’s safety’
content: The district administration directed school inspectors on Monday at a meeting with district school inspectors DSIR during the road safety week that management should take responsibility and dismiss such drivers immediately from work if an accident occurs, management should also inform district administration so that he is not given a school vehicle to drive in future. The district magistrate said that all school children should be made aware of road safety rules at the time of daily prayers so that the children can also make their parents aware about them in view of fog. He said the departments concerned should ensure markings on the roads and install signs like school ahead’ and keep the speed slow’ so that Road accidents can be avoided. He asked DSIs to issue a tollfree whatsapp number on which people can provide information about bad roads and stray animal issues so that accidents can be avoided.

Title: Friendship taken for a ride: Man booked over car fraud
content: A man was booked for refusing to pay for the vehicle he offered to purchase from his friend and threatening to kill him when asked for money. Muneef Khan of lakhimpur kheri informed the police that his friend, Sooraj Chaurasiya, a resident of Jankipuram, had borrowed his vehicle in 2020 for some work. Sooraj did not return Muneef's vehicle and later asked him to sell it to him for Rs 27 lakh. He also gave Rs 30000 as an advance amount for the deal. Muneef said he got busy in the treatment of his cousin who had sustained injuries in a Road accident and could not remind Sooraj about the remaining amount recently. He asked him to pay the money, but Sooraj threatened to kill him. Station house officer Jankipuram Brijesh Kumar said that a case under the charges of criminal breach of trust has been registered against the accused Sooraj and action will be taken after the investigation is complete.

Title: Audit points to missing, faulty signages on highways in state
content: The state of Odisha in India has a problem with poorly marked roads that lead to accidents. Recently, an audit was conducted on 3000 kilometers of roads in the state, and it was found that at least 2150 kilometers of roads do not have any signages at all. Additionally, it was found that many of the roads with signages are either badly placed or missing. The state transport department has asked the district collectors to address the issue.

Title: Identify quake-prone areas: Sukhu
content: The chief minister of Himachal Pradesh, Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu, has ordered his officers to develop an advance warning system in order to improve disaster response. He also ordered the identification of the earthquakeprone areas in Chamba, Kangra, Kullu, and Kinnaur districts, with detailed reports of their landslides, sinking zones, and black spots where fatal Road accidents are frequent. The revenue minister, Jagat Singh Negi, and the chief parliamentary secretary, Kishori Lal, were also in attendance. The deputy commissioners of Solan, Mandi, Bilaspur, Sirmaur, and Kinnaur districts shared their inputs with the chief minister. The chief minister called for studying the natural disasters of the past few years, particularly where land had subsided or slipped. He directed for enhancing the support being given to the state electricity board through the state disaster response fund, amending the state disaster relief manual, and mapping the glaciers for seismic activity. The principal secretary, Onkar Sharma, briefed the chief minister about the state’s disaster management plan for the earthquakeprone areas. The chief minister received a presentation on why Joshimath was sinking and how Uttarakhand had managed relief and rehabilitation.

Title: Collector instructs RTO to take steps to curb accidents
content: The district administration is shifting back to its core works after hectic schedules of PDBGIS summits. Officials were asked to dispose of cases registered at the cm helpline portal and samadhan online portal on a priority basis. A drive will be launched to dispose of the pending cases according to official sources. Indore is presently on 14th position in terms of cases pending on the cm helpline portal, while the deputy director of the obc department and assistant commissioner of the tribal welfare department were issued show cause notices on failing to perform as per the set bars. The collector emphasized on repairing the service road of the bypass and launching a drive jointly by the administration traffic police and transport department against those parking vehicles illegally. The collector asked the regional transport officer to take required steps to check Road accidents. The collector reviewed arrangements and preparations proposed for upcoming events, including that of Khelo India meetings of the G20 summit, Republic day, and anand utsav. Tableaux on different schemes, programmes, and achievements of the state government will be put on display.

Title: 3 DPS students selected for Inspire Awards
content: Three students from Delhi Public School Bokaro have been selected for the Inspire Awards 202223. They will be awarded a cash prize of Rs 10000. Rupesh Kumar of class 10 has been selected for making a special device and app to prevent Road accidents. Anjali Sharma of class 9 has been selected for making a chaircumspecial crutch with various facilities for disabled people. Ayushi Sharma of class 6 has been selected for making a special sanitary pad box for school girls which is based on a biometric system.

Title: One dies, 3 hurt in separate mishaps
content: In Visakhapatnam, one person was killed and three others suffered injuries in two separate Road accidents. In the first incident, a 35-year-old autodriver died in an autocar collision near Rugupalem. In the second incident, two bikes collided near Tallapalem, resulting in three injuries.

Title: 45% rape cases registered lastyear in state false: DGP Mishra
content: The director general of police in Jaipur, Umesh Mishra, said that the number of false rape cases in Rajasthan has increased from 45 in 2018 to 68 in 2019. Mishra said that this increase is due to the state police filing cases promptly when they receive complaints, rather than reflecting an increase in crimes. The number of rape cases registered in Rajasthan is high, but the conviction rate is much better than the national average. The state police are in touch with agencies like the FBI to take on organized gangs from other states who are striking in Rajasthan. Mishra said that the state police are also focused on combating cyber crimes, taking action against those who are registering false cases, and curbing Road accidents.

Title: UP sanctions Rs 7.4 crore for road safety campaign
content: The Uttar Pradesh government has sanctioned Rs 7.4 crore for creating road safety awareness in the state. Rakesh Kumar Malpani, special secretary, issued a letter and sanctioned the amount.

Title: Driver fatigue-led Road accidents: Approaches India could take to prevent these
content: Driver fatigue-led Road accidents | A crucial problem that causes numerous car accidents annually is driver fatigue. Due to the incapacity of a driver to halt or swerve to prevent or minimise the impact, accidents caused by driver sleepiness are much more inclined to result in fatalities or severe accidents.

Title: Principal killed as car hits tree
content: A 60-year-old principal of a school died in a car accident on Monday morning in Ujjain district. The principal, Rekha Pillai, was traveling to DPs in Ujjain from Indore on Monday morning when her car rammed into a tree near Dhatravada village. She was rushed to hospital, where doctors declared her dead. Police said Rekha was principal of DPs for the last four to five years and would drive a car to Indore every Saturday and then return to school on Monday morning. Nagjhiri police said the body was handed over to family members after postmortem. DSP Vinod Meena said the accident is under investigation.

Title: 2 killed & 4 injured in clash between 2 groups in Dausa
content: Two people, including a woman, were killed and four others sustained injuries during a clash between two groups in Dausa district on Monday, police said. Superintendent of police, Dausa, Sanjeev Nain said members of two families in Peeloda village clashed with each other over an old dispute involving a Road accident.

Title: Engineer dies as car hits cattle carcass on highway in Punjab
content: An official of a sugar mill died in a Road accident after his car hit a carcass of a cattle on Chandigarh-Ludhiana road near Samrala late on Sunday evening. The victim - Amrinderpal Singh Dilawari of Mohali - was a chief engineer of a sugar mill at Budhewal village near Kohara of Ludhiana.

Title: Road safety remains a major concern in Patna
content: The state capital faces its own set of problems when it comes to road safety. From jaywalking, talking over phone while driving, ignorance of lane-discipline to haphazard parking, causing road safety concerns. It has often been noticed that most pedestrian accidents occur because of jaywalking or crossing the busy road unlawfully.

Title: 45% rape cases registered last year in Rajasthan false: DGP Umesh Mishra
content: The registration of false rape cases has gone up to 45% last year in the state, said director general of police (DGP) Umesh Mishra in the city on Monday, while stating that the crime data has shown an increase because the state police filed FIRs promptly when they received complaints.

Title: 3 killed in separate Road accidents in Korba
content: A five-year-old boy and two adults died in two accidents in Chhattisgarh on Monday evening. In the first accident, a speeding trailer driver hit a car, injuring a secl worker and killing him. In the second accident, a father and son died after they were hit by a car.

Title: Awareness will bring down Road accidents: E S’bhum DC
content: The article discusses how the city of East Singhbhum is seeing an increase in accidents, and how the government is trying to address the issue. The article also discusses how parents and children can help to reduce accidents by being aware of traffic rules, and by using caution when driving.

Title: Murder, he wrote: Telangana govt employee stages own death for Rs 6 crore insurance
content: A Telangana man who supposedly died in an accident turned out to be a key accused who staged his own death using another man's body to claim Rs 6 crore insurance. He was arrested.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Family issues behind suicide of CISF men?
content: Family issues are said to be the reason behind the suicide of a CISF sub-inspector and a jawan within a span of 24 hours at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota on Monday.

Title: Truck ferrying ore crushed Gadchiroli driver, suspect cops
content: Mystery over the death of a 31-year-old truck driver, Nikesh Chokhare, near Maddigudam iron ore stock yard near Yelchil village in Aheri taluka in south Gadchiroli on Monday continues as the investigators are yet to identify the vehicle involved in the suspected hit-and-run case.

Title: Delhi HC upholds recovery of damages from dad of underage driver
content: Delhi High Court has dismissed an appeal challenging the grant of recovery rights to an insurer against the registered owner of the car whose son was involved in a Road accident leading to the death of a 42-year-old man in 2013.

Title: Close to 1,000 juvenile drivers challaned in ’2022
content: In Ludhiana, police issued 956 challans against underage drivers in 2022. This is the highest number of challans issued against underage drivers in a year

Title: Road safety drive to focus on honking, speeding, pollution
content: Kolkata is holding a road safety week from February 6-12. The week will focus on issues like honking and speeding, as well as the management of school traffic and ensuring safety for pedestrians. Cops are planning to include school and college students to act as agents for creating public awareness. A meeting was held by senior officers on Tuesday at Lalbazar to finalize the road map for the event. "We plan to inaugurate the event either on February 5 or 6, it will conclude on February 12," said DC Traffic Sunil Yadav. "Each traffic guard will choose separate issues to run the awareness campaigns across the city."

Title: In Gujarat, road safety to be taught in Classes 6-12
content: The state government has prepared a draft on road safety lessons which will be introduced in the curriculum of government schools from the next academic year.

Title: Biker dies after being hit by car in Delhi's Shahdara, accused held after he returns to spot
content: A 22-year-old bank cashier died after the bike he was riding was hit by a car in Vivek Vihar in Shahdara district on Wednesday. The accused driver has been arrested.

Title: Changes required in Right to Health Bill, say medics
content: The Indian Medical Association (IMA) in Rajasthan pointed out that the proposed Right to Health Bill will have a long-term effect of degradation and disappearance of quality healthcare services in the state. The IMA has appealed to the state government to implement it by amending the bill properly to get the support of doctors, private hospitals, and doctors' associations registered their disapproval towards certain provisions of the bill and termed it as "draconian and illogical laws."

Title: Biker hit by truck dies
content: A biker died in a Road accident in Maddur town on Tuesday night. Police said Abhishek 27 from Hadavanahalli of Kunigal was hit by a truck while he was going from Mysuru to Maddur. He was rushed to the Government Hospital in Maddur but he did not survive. They said Doctor Ends Life. A 54-year-old private medical practitioner was found hanging at her residence in Srirampura on Tuesday. Dr. Ganga Bhagirathi had lost her husband six years ago and lived alone at her house. She practised at a private clinic in HD Kote according to sources she was fighting depression. Ashokapuram Police have taken up a case.

Title: 2 dead in Road accident in Patna
content: A driver of a pick-up van and his helper died when their vehicle collided head on with a school van on Bailey Road flyover in Patna on Friday morning.

Title: Youth and minor girl from Delhi killed in accident on Maharashtra's Samruddhi
content: A 19-year-old youth and 14-year-old girl from Delhi were killed in a suspected Road accident on the Samruddhi Super Expressway. The deceased youth has been identified as Aseem alias Buchan Abbas of JJ Colony, Delhi. The fatal accident took place on Tuesday evening near the Sawangi Bridge, and the area falls within the jurisdiction of the Phulambri Police Station of Aurangabad Rural. On learning about the accident, police teams went to the spot and shifted the bodies to the Government Medical College and Hospital. The teens' families were informed of the accident, but due to the delay in their arrival to claim the bodies, the police got the bodies shifted to Mortuary-Based Cold Storage. Police said they are in touch with their Delhi counterparts to check if any missing complaints or a case of kidnapping had been registered with them. Sub-Inspector Damodar Waghmare, who is the investigation officer, said the police are also trying to ascertain if the two Delhi teens were trying to cross the Super Expressway when they were run over by a speeding goods truck or did they take any extreme step. A truck bearing a Tamil Nadu registration number has been seized and a case has been registered against its

Title: Woman killed in two-wheeler accident
content: A 40-year-old two-wheeler rider died in a Road accident in Irumbanam near Tripunithura on Thursday. The deceased was identified as Mary Sujin Malar, 40, wife of Sajan Murukeshan of Chitrapuzha. According to police, the twowheeler skidded by the edge of the road and the scooter overturned, she fell onto the road and got run over by a truck. Police initiated action against 9946 twowheeler riders for not wearing helmets in December as part of a special drive to improve road safety. They also booked 1173 motorists for not wearing seatbelts in the monthlong drive. They also took action against 643 drivers of public transport vehicles for not wearing correct uniforms, 362 for not having registration certificate of vehicles, 146 for driving without licence and 126 twowheeler riders for riding around with more than one pillion rider. 743 persons were booked for driving under influence.

Title: Four killed in separate Road accidents in YSR and Chittoor districts of Andhra Pradesh
content: 11 pilgrims from Proddatur in YSR district were returning back to their native in a hired vehicle after a pilgrimage to Tirumala on Friday morning.

Title: Former Maharashtra health minister Deepak Sawant injured in Road accident near Mumbai
content: Maharashtra's former health minister Deepak Sawant suffered injuries after his car was hit by a dumper truck on Mumbai-Ghodbunder highway on Friday.

Title: Good Samaritans in accidents to be rewarded in Gujarat
content: The state government on Thursday relaunched the scheme giving awards to Good Samaritans who help victims of Road accidents. Chief minister Bhupendra Patel also launched the website of the Gujarat Road Safety Authority.

Title: Police create green channels for transporting live heart in Visakhapatnam and Tirupati
content: The city police in Visakhapatnam on Friday facilitated the transport of a live heart by ensuring a green channel for non-stop movement of an ambulance to the airport.

Title: MV Act claims clause must be humane: Telangana high court
content: Telangana high court on Thursday said the 2019 amendment to the Motor Vehicles Act of 1988 will have to be revisited to make it more humane and practical.

Title: 3 brain-dead patients save lives of 11 people
content: Three brain-dead patients, including a young serving soldier who completed his training and took oath as a "sepoy" on December 24 only, have helped save the lives of 11 people after their families decided to donate their vital organs. Two donations took place at R&R hospital while the third one happened at Medanta hospital in Gurgaon.

Title: Good Samaritans to spread road safety awareness
content: The state government is planning to engage those who save accident victims in raising awareness on road safety. The "saviours who are acknowledged as good samaritans by the transport department" will be roped in to hold awareness camps in their areas as well as on social media platforms. "Many injured persons often die due to delay in getting treatment such lives can be saved if the victims are shifted to nearby hospitals on time" states the state commerce and transport minister. The state government's good samaritan scheme offers a reward of Rs 2000 to those who save lives of Road accident victims by shifting them to hospitals. The purpose of rewarding good samaritans is to encourage other citizens to come forward and help accident victims.

Title: Youth abducted for ₹4L ransom
content: A youth from a village in Kadaba taluk in Udupi district was abducted for a ransom of Rs. 4 lakh on Thursday. The complainant, a 26-year-old man from the same village, received a call from one Siddiq on Thursday asking him to come to Gandhi Park in Uppinangady for work. Siddiq and Irshad Mata, along with Shafi Gadiyara, were in a car when they took the complainant to a house in Mallur where some unidentified persons allegedly assaulted him. They made the complainant call Sharuk asking him to come to Kadambuthe accused took Sharuk and his friend Faizal from Kadambu to a house in Mallur where they assaulted the complainant and Sharuk later the accused allowed the complainant and Faizal to leave but demanded a ransom of Rs. 4 lakh to release Sharuk. The police registered a case under various sections of IPC against Siddiq, JCB Karavelu, Irshad Mata, Shafi Gadiyara, and others. A woman lost Rs. 79,000 to online fraud after a fraudster who introduced himself as an official from the Mescom swindled her. The complainant had received a message on her mobile phone on September 3

Title: Man dies as car collides with RTC bus
content: A man died and his wife was injured in a fatal car accident on NH 216 in Krishna district. The couple was from Kesanapalli village in Malkipuram mandal of Konaseema district and were travelling from their village to Vijayawada in their car. The accident occurred on the intervening night of Friday near Tunnel Bridge at Garisepudi village. A complaint has been lodged by one Adabala Mohan Rambabu, the brother of the deceased, stating that an unidentified driver had rammed the car driven by his brother. A case has been registered.

Title: Man working as a female dancer loses legs and identity in Ara accident
content: A man from Bihar, who had lost both of his legs in a Road accident, is now battling to prove his male identity because he was registered as a woman in the hospital records. This deprives him of all the benefits of government welfare schemes that this poor man could urgently use at this moment. The hapless patient, with both his legs amputated up to his knees, lies on the beds at Patna Medical College and Hospital, hoping for justice. Santosh Sah, dressed as a female dancer, was on his way to perform at a social function at Ara in Bhojpur last month when he was hit by a speeding truck. The grievously wounded person was rushed to the local Ara Sadar Hospital by the villagers.

Title: Dead woman gives new lease of life to teen
content: A 48-year-old woman in Vizag died after a car accident, and her family decided to donate her organs. The police in Vizag helped arrange a green channel for an ambulance to transport the organs to a hospital in Tirupati. The ambulance arrived at the airport at 9:15am, and the organ was transported to Tirupati in a flight for transplantation. Assistant Commissioner of Police West Zone Annepu Narasimha Murthy said that the organ was successfully transplanted into a boy at the Sri Padmavathi Children's Heart Centre in Tirupati.

Title: Artiste from Bihar loses legs, and identity, in accident
content: After losing both his legs in a Road accident, a man from Bihar is now battling hard to prove his male identity because he is registered as a woman in the hospital records - not once but twice, thus depriving him of all the benefits of government welfare schemes that this poor man earning livelihood as a female dancer urgently required at this moment. The hapless patient with both his legs amputated up to knees lies on the beds at PMCH hoping for justice.

Title: Brijbhushan Sharan Singh: Strongman from Gonda caught off-guard by wrestlers' swift move
content: While the six-time MP has seldom looked back in politics, despite facing more than 30 criminal cases at one point of time, including one under the draconian Terrorist and Disruptive Activities (Prevention) Act (TADA), it's wrestling that has forced him to take two steps backwards.

Title: Woman killed, husband injured in Road accident
content: A married woman was killed and her husband severely injured after a speeding trailer knocked them down near bhaisma chowk in Korba district on Saturday. The victims, Kenia Bai and Pyare Lal, residents of Nehru Nagar in Batari were returning home after attending a family function according to the police. The trailer smashed the two-wheeler and Kenia Bai came under the wheels of the trailer due to the impact she died on the spot while her husband sustained injuries. The Urga police officials registered a case of hitandrun and also booked a case against the trailer driver who has been absconding. The dead body was sent for the autopsy while the injured husband is admitted in the nearby district hospital. Meanwhile, local gathered near the accident side and staged a demostations to protest the frequent accidents in the area.

Title: Strongman from UP's Gonda caughtoff-guard by wrestlers’ swift move
content: Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh is a politician from India. He has been involved in many controversies in his career, but is most well-known for his work in the Indian Parliament.

Title: 182 killed in 461 accidents in Nashik city in 2022
content: In 2022, 182 people died in 461 accidents in Nashik City, India. The main reasons for these deaths were overspeeding and riding without helmets. Police officials said that an analysis of the data revealed that overspeeding was responsible for more than 70 of the fatalities. Additionally, 48 people died due to dangerous driving and 54 bikers had fatal accidents as they were not using helmets. Three others were killed due to wrongside driving and two died when they met with an accident while speaking on their cell phone. There are around 25 accident blackspots across the city. After the horrific accident at the Mirchi Hotel Chowk in October last year, the Nashik Municipal Corporation initiated steps to set up speed calming measures at these blackspots. Police Commissioner Ankush Shinde told TOI, "efforts will be made to bring down the number of Road accidents in the city. It is also necessary for people to follow the traffic rules and drive their vehicles cautiously for their own safety and the safety of others on the streets." There is also rising concern about the increasing number of vehicles on city roads every year, and thousands of new vehicles are added to city roads every year. This has led to traffic jams becoming common across the city. The traffic police said

Title: Transport museum, Cyber Hub also on G20 team’s tour list
content: The state government of Gurugram is promoting tourism during the upcoming G20 summit meetings in the city. After shortlisting Museo Camera Sultanpur Bird Sanctuary and Pratapgarh Farms, authorities have now proposed to arrange short trips to Heritage Transport Museum in Bilaspur Aravali Biodiversity Park and Cyber Hub for the foreign delegates.

Title: In Gurugram, 5 create ruckus at police station, booked
content: Five people have been booked for creating a disturbance at a police station in Gurgaon. The disturbance started when the five people arrived to the police station after a car accident that resulted in no injuries. The five people started shouting and abusing a constable working as a computer operator. A complaint was filed and a FIR was registered.

Title: Chamarajanagar constable attempts suicide
content: A constable in a district in Karnataka attempted to commit suicide by setting himself on fire in front of the office of the district police superintendent. A man from a district in Tamil Nadu, who is also from the police force, is now in the hospital after he was hit by a bike while he was traveling to Mandya from his home.

Title: New Town speed breakers: Motorists say too many & too high; cops cite rules
content: Following the death of an Aliah University student in a Road accident near an intersection in New Town, the authorities have installed multiple speed breakers on several roads in the township. While police say the bumps are slowing down cars, motorists complain that eight successive speed breakers after a rumble strip have turned these roads into "car breakers".

Title: 182 killed in 461 accidents in Nashik city in 2022
content: Overspeeding, rash driving and helmetless riding were the main reasons for the death of 182 people in 461 accidents in Nashik city in 2022, revealed the data compiled by the Nashik city police.

Title: Not as good as real, but new prosthetics give confidence to live independent lives
content: Life can be hell for people who lose their limbs in accidents or to some disease. And while prosthetics manufacturing in India lags in terms of technology and utility achieved in advanced countries, modern science still provides the assistance that those without limbs need to shrug off dependence on others.

Title: Five from Haryana killed after car collides with truck in Rajasthan's Sikar district
content: Five people from Haryana died in a Road accident in Rajasthan's Sikar district while returning after offering prayer at Salasar temple near Sujangarh, police said on Monday.

Title: 75-year-old killed in Road accident in Ahmedabad
content: A 75-year-old woman Nayna Shah, a resident of Paldi, died after she was hit by a speeding car.

Title: 2 killed, 40 injured as tourist bus falls off bridge in Odisha's Dhenkanal
content: Two persons were killed and 40 others injured after a tourist bus fell off a bridge near Panchabati in Dhenkanal town on national highway-55 early on Monday morning.

Title: 2 die, as car skids off Kinnaur road
content: Two people died in a car accident, and two others were injured. The injured are said to be out of danger, and a police team inspected the scene of the accident. The car was on its way from Bhawanagar to Nicharrevenue Horticulture and Tribal Development Minister Jagat Singh Negi met the families of the victims and the survivors and attended the two funerals.

Title: Two killed in two accidents
content: A 24-year-old man was killed after his bike was hit by a goods carrier, and a 50-year-old man died on the spot after he lost control over his bike and it hit a roadside tree.

Title: How Gonda strongman Brij Bhushan Singh's career soared before wrestlers’ move put him on the mat
content: The footnote to six-time MP Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh's political CV would be a long chronology of criminal cases — at one point totalling 30 FIRs, including one filed under the erstwhile Terrorist and Disruptive Activities (Prevention) Act.

Title: 2 die, as car skids off Kinnaur road
content: Two people died and another two were injured in a Saturday-night Road accident in Nichar tehsil of Kinnaur district when their car fell into a 100-metre gorge.

Title: In Bengaluru, insurance firm alleges fake policy prepared for tractor
