content: Two persons died after a lorry rammed into their car near Ramakrishnapuram in Sattenaplli of Palnadu district. The deceased were identified as N Rajyamma (53) and her nephew Nagineni Suresh (35), a pastor

Title: Man, 59, ends life in Karnataka's Yadagiri a day after son’s death in road mishap
content: A 52-year-old man died by suicide by jumping into an open well at Madraki of Shahapur taluk in Yadagiri district a day after his son died in a Road accident.

Title: Hyderabad traffic police aim to reduce road fatalities by 15% this year
content: Additional commissioner of traffic G Sudheer Babu directed officials to rectify 65 newly identified black spots across the city on Saturday.

Title: Fan art of late actor Sarath holding her baby boy leaves Soniya in tears; see video
content: Sharing its video online, the actress wrote, 'Thank you so much. I don't know whether I should be happy or sad about this. It feels special to see my son in Sarath's hands. So happy but also feeling some unexplainable pain'.

Title: Five crushed to death by speeding car on Kalka-Shimla highway
content: Five migrant labourers were crushed to death by a speeding car along Kalka-Shimla national highway near Dharampur in Solan district of Himachal Pradesh on Tuesday morning.

Title: Trader, labourer die in 2 separate Road accidents in Delhi
content: A trader and a labourer died in separate Road accidents in north and west Delhi on Sunday.

Title: MLA for roundabouts, traffic management in Mohali
content: AAP MLA from Mohali Kulwant Singh took up the longstanding issue of traffic and Road accidents in Punjab Vidhan Sabha and underlined the need to ease traffic at intersections from Airport Chowk to Kharar.

Title: NRI murder in Hyderabad: 1 convicted, 11 accused acquitted
content: Over three years after the sensational murder of Non-Resident Indian (NRI) Chigrupati Jayaram, a local court in Nampally on Monday convicted prime accused Rakesh Reddy while acquitting 11 other accused, including three policemen.

Title: 5 killed as car rams into migrant labourers in Himachal Pradesh's Dharampur
content: A car ploughed into a group of migrant labourers in Himachal Pradesh's Solan district on Tuesday, killing five and injuring four, police said. The accident occurred near Dharampur on the Shimla-Kalka national highway around 9:20 am when they were walking to work, according to police.

Title: Five members of family among 6 killed in Road accidents in Madhya Pradesh
content: Six persons, including five members of a family, were killed and eight others injured in two separate Road accidents in Madhya Pradesh, police said on Wednesday.

Title: Three held for trying to claim Rs 2 crore life insurance using fake death certificate in Mumbai
content: A woman posing as the mother of one of the arrested men had taken out the policy in his name in April 2015, said an official of Shivaji Park police station.

Title: 8 killed, 4 injured in 2 Himachal Pradesh road acidents
content: Eight people died and four others were injured in two Road accidents in Himachal Pradesh. While a speeding car killed five migrant labourers in Solan district on Tuesday morning, three people died after their car fell into a 400-feet deep gorge in Sirmaur district on Monday night.

Title: IIT-Madras partners with SNS Foundation to train school, college bus drivers on road safety rules
content: An MoU was signed between the two organisations to train 2,000 drivers in and around Chennai in the near future.

Title: 30-year-old man, 2 others held in Rs 2 crore insurance fraud case in Maharashtra
content: The Shivaji Park police have arrested a 30-year old man who faked his own death and his two friends who helped him make an insurance claim of Rs 2 crore.

Title: Over 150 skin, eye infection cases on Holi in Noida and Ghaziabad, likely to go up
content: The District Combined Hospital (DCH) in Ghaziabad reported 47 cases of accidents on the occasion of Holi. Out of these, 30 cases were related to skin and eye reactions while playing with colours.

Title: Holi mishap numbers jump more than double in Lucknow this year
content: Holi celebrations turned unpleasant for nearly 800 people in city who were either injured in Road accidents due to drunken driving or suffered from eye and skin allergies. This number was two and a half times more than 2022 when 368 such cases were reported during the festival of colours.

Title: Karnataka to build army of trauma responders near highways
content: Expressways and upgraded National Highways (NHs) have minimised travel time. But on the flip side, the infrastructure development has also led to rise in accidents and casualties.

Title: Road accidents claim 15 lives in Bihar in 48 hours, many hurt
content: At least 15 people were killed and several others injured in separate Road accidents in Vaishali, Jamui, Saharsa, Purnia, Araria, Munger and Kishanganj districts in the last 48 hours.

Title: Hit by car, biker killed on Pune's Katraj-Dehu Road bypass
content: In a hit-and-run accident, a motorcyclist (49) was killed after he was hit by a car at Hinjewadi along the Katraj-Dehu Road bypass around 11.30am on Wednesday afternoon.

Title: 15-year-old dies in Road accident in Anakapalle
content: A 15-year-old-boy was killed in a Road accident near Geddapalem locality under S Rayavaram Mandal in Anakapalle district. The deceased was identified as Y Sairam, a native of Elamanchili. Police suspect that the boy died after being hit by an unidentified vehicle. A case has been registered and the body has been sent for postmortem.

Title: Drunk driving: Three killed in separate Road accidents
content: Three persons were killed in three separate Road accidents related to drunken driving in different parts of the city. In Banthara, a 50-year-old man identified as Lala Ram of Kurauni was killed when a speeding car knocked him down around 9.15 pm on March 8.

Title: Holi traffic prosecutions up, accidents lowest in 4 years in Delhi
content: Holi 2023 witnessed the lowest number of Road accidents in the past four years while the number of traffic violations was the highest, Delhi Police said on Thursday. Senior officials said this was due to the elaborate arrangements and layers of checks put in place for the festival.

Title: IIT-M to train bus drivers road safety
content: Indian Institute of Technology Madras' Centre of Excellence for Road Safety (CoERS) is partnering with SNS Foundation to train school and college bus drivers on road safety rules and driving behaviour.

Title: US-based Kabaddi player dies in Road accident near Machhiwara in Ludhiana
content: 50-year-old Jasdev Singh Gola lived in Ohio.

Title: February 2023 saw 12% dip in accidents: DGP
content: After recording a nearly nine per cent rise in Road accidents last year, strict enforcement of rules by the traffic police since the start of 2023 appears to have paid dividends. Director general of police (DGP) Jaspal Singh on Friday said that accidents declined by 12% this February when compared to the same period last year.

Title: Scooterist killed in Mapusa accident
content: A 28-year-old Bihar native died in a Road accident at Ganeshpuri, Mapusa, on Friday after a four-wheeler collided into the scooter he was riding. Anjuna police arrested the driver of the vehicle, Ram Surat Yadav, a native of Uttar Pradesh.

Title: Newly-wed dies after tyre burst in Maharashtra's Boisar
content: A 23-year-old newly-wed man was killed and his driver was injured after his car hit a tree following a tyre burst in Boisar early Friday.

Title: Truck runs over man, kills him
content: A 40-year-old man was killed on the spot in a Road accident after the Chennai Corporation truck ran over him at Vyasarpadi in the wee hours on Saturday.

Title: 3 pilgrims die in Road accident
content: Three devotees who were on their way to Tirumala for darshan of Lord Venkateswara were killed in a road mishap near Dharmapuri village in Nagari mandal of Chittoor district on Sunday.

Title: Couple killed in Road accident near Lalgudi
content: A Road accident claimed the lives of a newlywed couple on Saturday night near Lalgudi in the Trichy district. D Senthilkumar, 33, and J Ragini, 32, who were residents of Perambalur, were commuting on a two-wheeler when they were hit by a truck from behind while approaching Aangarai village on the Trichy-Lalgudi highway.

Title: Two dead, 11 hurt in Jamui Road accident
content: Two persons, including a two-year-old girl, died and 11 others sustained injuries when the autorickshaw they were travelling in collided head-on with a speeding truck on the Jamui-Lakhisarai Road in Jamui district on Sunday morning. The incident took place around 5am near Amba village under Town police station area, police said. The deceased were identified as Jaddu Rajak (30), the autorickshaw driver, a resident of Tilakpur village and Nisha Kumari (2), daughter of Rajkumar Yadav.

Title: Cars remain off the radar in vehicle screening in Trichy
content: The recurrence of Road accidents involving cars in Trichy city indicates inadequacy in intercepting and screening cars for violations compared to the mechanism in place for punishing violations committed by two-wheelers. Within a week, Trichy city has recorded two separate accidents involving cars, both in prominent localities witnessing a high volume of pedestrian movement.

Title: 5 Bihar-bound passengers die in e-way Road accident
content: As many as five persons died after the car they were travelling in collided with a stationary tipper truck on Purvanchal Expressway in Sultanpur on Sunday.

Title: Five dead, nine injured after car rams into SUV in UP's Firozabad
content: Five people were killed and nine others injured when a speeding car rammed into a parked SUV on the Lucknow-Agra Expressway in Nasirpur on Tuesday, police said.

Title: Blackspots down by half, but GIP mall area & Noida's Parthala Chowk among new ones
content: Traffic police have revised the list of accident-prone areas, listing 15 black spots against 34 in 2022. The list has five new names—Sector 37, GIP Mall and Parthala roundabout in the Noida Authority area, and Kachi Sadak T-Point in Echotech 3 police station area and Sirsa roundabout in Kasna police station area, which fall under the Greater Noida Authority jurisdiction. The rest are from the old list.

Title: Accident exposes fake no. plate used to avoid paying EMI
content: A Road accident has helped police find a fake number plate a car owner was using to avoid loan repayment and insurance. The fraud came to light following an RTO inspection of the car.

Title: 12% jump in Road accidents, fatalities up by 16% in Bihar
content: Fatalities due to Road accidents in the state started showing upward trend after a decline in 2020 because of travel restrictions owing to Covid-19 pandemic.

Title: 4 dead, 4 injured in head-on collision between two cars in Punjab's Sangrur
content: Four persons died and four others were injured following a head-on collision between two cars in Punjab's Sangrur district on Wednesday morning. The police said that collision took place near a village at Sunam-Mansa road.

Title: Supreme Court nixes government plea for Rs 7,400 crore additional damages for Bhopal gas disaster
content: The Supreme Court on Tuesday dismissed the Centre’s plea filed in 2010 seeking an additional compensation of Rs 7,400 crore from Union Carbide Corporation (UCC) for the victims of 1984 Bhopal gas tragedy.

Title: Amid rise in road fatalities, govt bid to find cause in Odisha
content: Following a 7% rise in Road accident deaths in the state in 2022, the government has directed district collectors and SPs to investigate the causes behind the increase and implement preventive measures.

Title: 2 held for snatching gold chain worth Rs 50,000 from elderly woman in Surat
content: Police arrested on Thursday two men for allegedly snatching a gold chain worth Rs 50,000 from a 67-year-old woman in Katargam area. The two were identified as Arjun Vaghela (19) and Naju Parmar (22).

Title: Tamil Nadu Road accident deaths down by 2%: Ma Subramanian
content: Deaths due to Road accidents in Tamil Nadu dropped by more than 2% in 2022 over 2019 following the implementation of Nammai Kakkum-48, health minister Ma Subramanian said on Thursday. The scheme provides free treatment including life-saving surgeries for up to 1 lakh for all Road accident victims.

Title: This champion is going to rise again: Yuvraj Singh after meeting Rishabh Pant
content: Flamboyant former India all-rounder Yuvraj Singh knows a thing or two about what it takes to come back from the brink as he made one of the greatest comebacks in the history of the sport.

Title: World Sleep Day 2023: Kolkata experts call for good sleep habits
content: Sleep is essential for health, and people are increasingly suffering from sleep deprivation. This can have serious consequences, including disruption of brain function and stroke, obesity, and dementia. Older working adults are the only group of people who are more likely to suffer from sleep disorders, but even children are falling victim.

Title: Two killed in Road accident in Mangaluru
content: City police commissioner Kuldeep Kumar R Jain said the accident took place after a tipper truck rammed the two-wheeler around 12.15pm. The deceased are Samuel Jesudas, 66, a resident of Sulthan Bathery and his relative Bhoomika, 17.

Title: 4 killed, 28 injured as bus overturns in Jammu and Kashmir's Pulwama
content: Four people were killed and 28 other suffered injuries when the bus in which they were travelling overturned as the driver lost control of the vehicle in Pulwama district of Jammu and Kashmir on Saturday.

Title: Nagoa man dead as Gujarati tourist's vehicle hits him
content: A speeding Gujarati tourist on Friday rammed into the bike of a Nagoa man dropping his children to school, killing him and injuring the children.

Title: Rs 62 lakh payout awarded to businessman’s kin killed in crash in Bhopal
content: A sessions court here awarded a compensation of Rs 62 lakh to the kin of a businessman, who was killed in a Road accident near 11 Mile on Misrod road. The court further ordered the owner and driver of the dumper which hit the motorbike of the deceased and the insurance company to pay an interest at the rate of 7% to the family of deceased from day of incident.

Title: As lights go kaput, commuters run risk of accidents on dark highways
content: Those driving vehicles from Ludhiana to Delhi should drive with caution, especially between Ludhiana and Khanna, as lights along the road on the National Highway are not working at many places. As per the last NCRB data, 140 persons died in Road accidents on national highways in Ludhiana in 2021.

Title: Who is Amritpal Singh, Waris Punjab De chief: All you need to know
content: The 30-year-old shot into prominence in the past 6-7 months as a separatist leader, Khalistani sympathiser and radical preacher.

Title: 6 killed, 3 injured in car-truck collision in Trichy
content: Six people, including a woman and a minor girl, were killed in a head-on collision between a car and a truck near Thiruvasi on Trichy-Namakkal main road in Trichy district in the early hours on Sunday.

Title: Kolkata cops' helmet-seat belt drive to stop accidents
content: The Kolkata Traffic Police is launching a special campaign on the correct usage of helmets and seat belts to prevent fatal accidents. The drive will begin on Monday (March 20), which is observed as the World Head Injury Awareness Day.

Title: 45 of 166 Uttarakhand thanas logged more mishaps than others: Data
content: The integrated Road accident data (i-RAD) maintained by the traffic police has revealed that 45 of the total 166 police stations in Uttarakhand reported more Road accidents than others in the past one year.

Title: Man hit by truck at Uppal, dies
content: A 34-year-old man died in a Road accident at Uppal on Saturday after he was hit by a truck.

Title: Youth killed as tractor hits bike in Andhra Pradesh
content: A 22-year-old mason was killed in a Road accident after his bike was hit by a tractor near Kaspa street at Palakonda in Parvathipuram Manyam district.

Title: 2 kids among 3 killed after vehicle falls into gorge in Rishikesh
content: Three people, including two children, were killed after a vehicle in which they were travelling fell into a gorge on Neelkanth temple road under Lakshman Jhula police limits in Pauri Garhwal on Sunday. Ten others also sustained serious injuries and have been admitted to AIIMS Rishikesh by the police.

Title: 221 drunk drivers e-challaned in 2 months in Mumbai
content: Speeding and drunk driving are the major causes of fatal Road accidents in the city during odd hours, said traffic police and experts.

Title: In past 5 years, Ramanagara stretch deadliest on Bengaluru-Mysuru highway
content: The stretch of Bengaluru-Mysuru national highway from the outskirts of the state capital and lying in Ramanagara district has claimed more lives in vehicular accidents in the past five years compared to the stretch lying in Mandya district till the outskirts of Mysuru, according to state police data.

Title: What's Maharashtra government doing to curb mishaps?: Neelam Gorhe in council
content: State legislative council deputy chairperson Neelam Gorhe on Monday asked the state government to make a statement on what action it is taking and what measures it will take to prevent Road accidents like the Sunday incident that claimed the life of a tech company's CEO who was jogging along Worli sea face on Sunday morning.

Title: Jaipur NGO that takes care of dogs, birds starts looking after camels
content: : After developing programmes for managing street dog problems in the city along with rescuing birds and animals, city-based NGO Help in Suffering is now shifting to taking care of camels.

Title: Nagpur-Mumbai Samruddhi Expressway: 100 days, 900 accidents & 31 fatalities
content: In the first 100 days since its inauguration, the Samruddhi Expressway has witnessed as many as 900 accidents, including many fatal. A study conducted by Regional Transport Office of Maharashtra government revealed that these accidents had claimed 31 lives till March 20, 2023.

Title: Karnataka woman fails to transfer insurance, loses Rs 5.8 lakh
content: While buying a used vehicle, one should promptly change the insurance papers to his/her name, else it will lead to non-qualification of claims in case of accidental damage. A 58-year-old Chikkaballapura woman learnt this lesson the hard way after her car met with a Road accident, incurring damages worth Rs 5.8 lakh.

Title: A gaushala managed by animal rights activists
content: With the deep conviction to help and rehabilitate stray and abandoned cows not on religious grounds but solely for animal welfare, this young Sikh man has not only been rescuing, providing medical treatment and shelter to 280 cows but has also rehabilitated over 200 cows so far.

Title: Youth diesas SUV hits bike in Patna
content: A youth died in a Road accident when the bike he was riding was hit by an SUV on JP Ganga Path near LCT Ghat under Patliputra police station area in the state capital on Thursday afternoon. The deceased was identified as Vinod Kumar, an LIC agent and resident of Hajipur.

Title: 1 dead, four injured as car rams truck in Tamil Nadu
content: One person died and four others were injured in a Road accident near Kattankulathur in Chengalpet district in the wee hours of Thursday.

Title: Soon, 6-week courses, night driving lessons & more in Noida, Ghaziabad
content: The UP government is planning to set up Driving Training Centres (DTC) in Noida and Ghaziabad among other districts of the state. The training is aimed to help people who want to learn driving and get a license.

Title: TOI press staff get pointers on road safety & traffic rules
content: To celebrate the ongoing Safety Month, a session on "Road Safety and Traffic Rules" was conducted at The Times of India Chinhat Press in Lucknow on Thursday. The session was conducted by deputy commissioner of police, traffic, Rayees Akhtar. The officer told TOI staff about the importance of following traffic rules, benefits of wearing helmet, and at the same time buy a helmet with the ISI mark. He also shared phone numbers of officials who can be contacted by the public in case of an emergency or distress related to traffic. The officer spoke at length on the Good Samaritan concept and apprised the staff on how to help anyone injured in a Road accident.

Title: Tattoo helps nab man on run for 15 years in Mumbai
content: A tattoo mark on the hand of a 63-year-old criminal, who had been on the run for the past 15 years, exposed his claims of being a different person, thus leading to his arrest.

Title: 2 dead in car-bike collision in Haryana's Ambala
content: Two persons died while one other was injured in a late night collision between a motorcycle and a car in Shahzadpur area of Ambala district in Haryana.

Title: Zero road fatality week to begin from April 1 in Odisha
content: As the government gears up to observe zero fatality week from April 1 to 7 across Odisha to prevent Road accident deaths, automobile manufacturers have been requested to advise the government on improving the road-safety scenario in the state.

Title: Carcass of leopard found in Bandhwari, poaching ruled out
content: A leopard carcass was found at Bandhwari village in the Aravalis off Gurgaon-Faridabad road on Friday.

Title: 4 killed, 10 critical after MUV, bus collide in Ferozepur
content: Four people, including three teachers and the driver of their vehicle, died while 10 others were injured critically in a major Road accident near Khai Feme Ki village on Ferozepur-Fazilka road on Friday morning.

Title: Couple having links with Amritpal Singh's aide Papalpreet Singh detained in Jammu
content: Papalpreet has been on the run along with Amritpal since the Punjab Police launched a crackdown against the pro-Khalistan preacher and his 'Waris Punjab De' outfit on March 18.

Title: Standoff continues over RTH Bill, talks to be held today
content: No talks were held on Friday between the agitating doctors and the state government to resolve the dispute on Right To Health (RTH) Bill as the strike entered its fifth day on Friday.

Title: Biker rams truck parked without brake lights, dies in Chennai
content: A 24-year-old biker died in a Road accident near Maduravoyal on Saturday night, after he rammed a truck that was parked on the roadside. Police arrested the lorry driver, Krishna Kumar, on Sunday.

Title: GH doctors perform complex surgery
content: Doctors at Tenkasi Government headquarters hospital have successfully performed an emergency complex fracture surgery on a man within three hours after he was admitted to the hospital with injuries following a Road accident.

Title: Even 1 Covid death scares people, but not road fatalities: CM Pramod Sawant
content: Even as public anger simmers over the spate of road traffic accident deaths, chief minister Pramod Sawant on Tuesday appealed to the people to ride and drive carefully.

Title: Goa CM Pramod Sawant appeals to people to ride and drive carefully
content: Even as public anger simmers over the spate of road traffic accident deaths, chief minister Pramod Sawant on Tuesday appealed to the people to ride and drive carefully.

Title: 'Helmet Man of India' now departs for his village, but vow to continue his endeavour to save lives
content: After failing to get any kind of financial support for his endeavour to save the lives of two-wheeler riders, Raghvendra Singh (36)--popularly known as 'Helmet Man of India' has now headed back to his native village in Bihar.

Title: Traffic travails in Bhopal: No rules, all chaos at Bawadiya Kalan ROB
content: The Bawadiya Kalan railway-over-bridge (ROB) connecting Shahpura to Hoshangabad Road is an example of traffic mismanagment that plagues many areas of the state capital.

Title: To hate stray dogs not acceptable approach from persons of civil society: Bombay HC
content: “To hate stray dogs and treat them with cruelty can never be an acceptable approach from persons of civil society, such cruelty would be against the Constitutional ethos and statutory provisions,” said Bombay high court bench of justices Girish Kulkarni and R N Laddha sounding a “word of caution” on Monday to managing committee and other members of a society.

Title: UP: ‘Helmet Man’ puts brake on his drive
content: After exhausting all his resources in distribution of over 56,000 helmets among two-wheeler riders, which helped save 30 lives in the last one decade, Raghvendra Singh (36) – popularly known as the Helmet Man of India – finally left for his native village.

Title: Maharashtra govt approves project to set up CCTV cameras across Malegaon
content: The state government has given its approval to the Malegaon Municipal Corporation (MMC) to install CCTV cameras across the textile city, the civic chief said on Tuesday.

Title: Man dies after being hit by RTC bus in Warangal
content: One person was killed, and two others were injured in a Road accident in the wee hours of Tuesday. The incident took place when a TSRTC bus rammed into a parked trolley vehicle loaded with red chilli near Mylaram village in the outskirts of Raiparthy mandal in Warangal district.

Title: To treat stray dogs with cruelty is unacceptable in civil society: Bombay HC
content: "To hate stray dogs and treat them with cruelty can never be an acceptable approach from persons of civil society, as such cruelty would be against the Constitutional ethos and statutory provisions," said Bombay high court bench of Justices Girish Kulkarni and R N Laddha on Monday to managing committee and other members of a cooperative housing society in suburban Mumbai.

Title: 5 dead, 8 injured in truck-auto collision in Maharashtra's Nanded district
content: Five persons were killed and eight were injured in a collision between a truck and an autorickshaw in Maharashtra's Nanded district on Thursday, an official said.

Title: Pregnant woman killed in Road accident in Mohali
content: A pregnant woman died when a car she was travelling in collided with a stationary truck on the Kharar-Landran highway in Mohali on Wednesday.

Title: 2 farm hands die in accident in Telangana
content: Two farm labourers were killed and 15 others sustained injuries in a Road accident at Chalivagu flyover under the Parkal police station limits on Wednesday.

Title: 5 of a family killed, 2 others injured in road accident on Phalodi-Bikaner highway
content: JODHPUR: Five persons were killed and two others injured in a fatal road accident in Phalodi on Friday afternoon. The deceased include two children and one woman. Of the two injured, one of them who was in a serious condition was taken to Jodhpur.According to sources, all of them belonged to a family from Jamba near Phalodi. The accident took place on Phalodi-Bikaner highway, when the victims' vehicle collided head on with a tanker.Those who died have been identified as the driver of the vehicle Parvat Ram (35), Urmila (38), Vikas (20), Praveen (12) and Raveena (11). The latter two were siblings.Police said that all the victims had left from their village for Phalodi town in the morning to shop for a wedding in the family and were returning when the accident happened.. .Advertisement3 persons had died on the spot"Barely 2km from Phalodi towards Bikaner, their vehicle collided head on with a tanker at about 3pm. Three persons died on the spot while two others succumbed at the hospital," police said.Of the two who sustained injuries, one has been shifted to Jodhpur with serious injuries. The other had a narrow escape and sustained only minor injuries.The collision was so intense that half the passenger vehicle had turned into a mangled mass and the front wheels of the tanker got detached from the vehicle.

Title: 11 killed in Hisar, Sirsa accidents
content: HISAR: Eleven people, including a couple, died in two road accidents in Hisar and Sirsa districts on Thursday evening and late night. While six youths were killed when their car hit a tree in Hisar, five persons, including a couple, died in Sirsa when two cars collided.An over speeding car collided with a tree near Neem bus stand on Adampur-Agroha road at around 2am on Thursday night, killing six youths while one was injured. The accident was so severe that the uncontrolled vehicle overturned on one side, breaking trees and electric poles. People took the dead out of the car by pulling the doors with ropes.The deceased have been identified as 23-year-old Sagar, 22-year-old Shobhit, both residents of Khara village, 24-year-old Arvind, 22-year-old Abhinav, 25-year-old Ashok and 23-year-old Deepak, all residents of Kishangarh village of Hisar. Bhunesh, a resident of Suratgarh, Rajasthan, is injured and is undergoing treatment.All youths were returning to their homes after attending a marriage ceremony in Adampur late at night. Villagers said when people went out for a walk in the morning, they saw the crashed car. After this the police were informed. There is mourning in Khara and Kishangarh villages after the death of the youths.. .AdvertisementEarlier on Thursday evening, five persons were killed in a collision between two cars in Nejadela village of Sirsa district. The deceased were identified as 35-year-old Gurtej Singh, his wife Paramjit Kaur, two sons, six-year-old Guntaj and six-month-old Sukhtaj, all residents of Sardulgarh of Mansa district in Punjab. Rahul, 20, a resident of Sirsa, who was travelling in another car, also died.All the above and 14-year-old Kavalpreet Kaur, niece of Gurtej Singh, were going back to Sardulgarh after getting the checkup of Sukhtaj at a private hospital in Sirsa when their car collided with a car that killed five persons while one girl was injured.Of the three youths in the second car, 20-year-old Rahul, a resident of Sirsa, died while 20-year-old Ranjit and 20-year-old Mohit were injured.

Title: Three killed in collision of trucks at Phaphamau bridge on highway
content: Prayagraj: Three persons, including a biker, were killed when two trucks collided head on at Phaphamau bridge on Prayagraj-Lucknow highway early Friday, police said.The impact of the collision was so powerful that one of the DCM mini truck fell off from the Phaphamau bridge into open land of Ganga river while the front portion of the second stone-laden truck was partially hung in air after jutting out of the railing of the bridge. The accident disrupted traffic movement for several hours leading to massive snarls till late afternoon.Sub-inspector posted at Phaphamau police station Virendra Singh told TOI: â€œThe accident took place at around 5.30am when a stone-laden truck on its way to Phaphamau from Shivkuti collided head on with a DCM mini truck approaching the city in the middle of the Phaphamau bridge. The DCM fell off from the bridge leaving the driver and cleaner dead on the spot.â€. .AdvertisementSingf added that the biker was reportedly trapped in the collision of trucks and killed on the road.Those dead were DCM truck driver Himanshu Sahu (23) and his brother (cleaner) Anshu Sahu (20), residents of Amba Nagrav (Lucknow) and a bike rider Bajrang Bahadur (48) of Pachdevra.Upon being alerted, teams of Phaphamau, Shivkuti and traffic police rushed to the spot and sent the bodies for postmortem. Traffic police also pressed in four cranes to remove the hanging truck and the movement on the bridge was stopped for more than four hours. Police said the Prayagraj-Lucknow highway route was cleared at around 1.30pm but it witnessed massive traffic jams once it was opened for commuting. Long queues of vehicles were seen on both sides and cops managed to clear road jams by 4 pm.

Title: 1 killed, 18 injured as bus overturns in Kolkata's Mayo Road
content: KOLKATA: One person was killed and 18 others seriously injured after a bus overturned and hit a motorcycle in central Kolkata's Mayo Road area on Saturday evening, police said. The minibus, which was operating on the Metiabruz-Howrah route, was heading towards the Mayo Road-Dufferin Road crossing when the accident happened around 4.40 pm, they said. The driver of the bus suddenly lost control, following which it overturned and hit a motorcycle, they said. The man who was killed in the accident is yet to be identified, police said, adding that he was apparently in his mid-30s. . .AdvertisementThose injured were being treated at the state-run SSKM hospital, a police officer said. Police are trying to establish their identities and contact their family, he said.

Title: 7 of wedding party die as vehicle falls into Hirakud canal
content: SAMBALPUR: Seven persons of a wedding party were killed and four others were critically injured after the vehicle in which they were travelling fell into the Sason canal of Hirakud dam near Bishalkhinda in Sambalpur district early on Friday.The accident occurred when some of those from the groom's side were returning to Baddhara village under Lakhanpur block in Jharsuguda district after attending the marriage ceremony at Parmanpur under Sason police station in Sambalpur.Police said 11 people were in the vehicle. The SUV fell into the canal after travelling only around 3 km from the place where they attended the wedding ceremony. After getting information, police and fire service personnel rushed to the spot and launched a rescue operation. While four persons managed to get out of the vehicle, seven bodies were retrieved from the canal.. .AdvertisementSambalpur superintendent of police B Gangadhar said that it seems the vehicle was moving at high speed when it fell into the canal. "The driver might have failed to see the turning on the road due to darkness which led to the accident. However, investigation into the matter is underway. The motor vehicle inspector (MVI) will examine whether there was any mechanical fault in the vehicle," he said. Police identified the deceased as Ajit Khamri (25), Dibya Luha (27), Saroj Seth (35), Sumanta Bhoi (35), Subal Bhoi (23), Ramakanta Bhuyan (23), besides the driver and owner of the SUV, Bhiku Bhoi (30), all from Baddhara.(With inputs by Ritesh Sharma from Jharsuguda)

Title: Biker killed by speeding truck driver
content: Nashik: A 45-year-old biker was knocked down by a truck driver on the road from Jejurkar Mala to Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar Road Naka on Thursday night. The truck driver fled the scene without informing anyone.On a complaint, the Adgaon police have registered an FIR for the offence punishable under Section 304-A of the IPC for causing death due to negligence against the unidentified truck driver. The accident victim Amit Poharkar of Hirawadi, Panchavati, was riding his bike from Jejurkar Mala to Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar Road Naka at around 2 pm. When he was near the hotel Mirchi signal, he was knocked down by a speeding truck.. .AdvertisementPoharkar succumbed to the serious injuries he suffered. The Adagon police are investigating the case.

Title: 2 walking by road killed in twin accidents, 6 hours apart in Gurgaon
content: GURGAON: Two men walking by the side of the road were knocked down by vehicles in Bilaspur area of the city, just six hours apart.A truck driver walking towards a transport office after dinner at a dhaba died on the spot after being hit by a lorry from behind around 11.30pm on Friday. A private firm worker walking home after night shift was mowed down by an SUV around 5.30am on Saturday.Originally from Indira Nagar in Uttarakhand, Saveer worked for a transport company in Bilaspur as a truck driver. He had had a meal at a dhaba and had just reached near a toll plaza close to his office when a truck crashed into him from behind and fled the spot.Saveer, his brother Javed said, was thrown in the air and landed on another side of the road. He suffered grievous injuries to the head. Passersby rushed him to a government hospital in Sector 10, where doctors declared him dead on arrival.. .Advertisement"My brother was just walking by the side of the road when he was knocked down. Tell me, was he at fault? He was the victim of reckless driving," Javed told TOI.Following a complaint by him, a case was registered against unknown persons under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death in accident) of the IPC at Bilaspur police station.Just six hours later, Sanjeev was walking home to Bhorakala village with his colleague Hemant when an SUV rammed him from behind and sped away. He died before he could be wheeled into the emergency ward."We were returning home after night shift when a white SUV hit him. I was thrown to the side of the road under the impact. But he suffered grievous injuries and could not be revived," Hemant said, adding that he could not note down the registration number of the SUV in the darkness.A case was lodged at the Bilaspur police station under the same IPC sections.Police said they were scanning CCTV footage to identify the vehicles. Till Saturday evening, no arrest had been made. "No one could note down the registration numbers of the vehicles. We are trying to identify the vehicles and their drivers," said Subhash Boken, a spokesperson for Gurgaon police.The death of pedestrians in road accidents is not uncommon in the city. Walkers account for every third fatal crash here. In 2022, the city reported 1,040 accidents in which 404 people died and 886 suffered injuries. Of these 404 who died, 137 were pedestrians.On February 1, a schoolbus driver was crushed to death near IFFCO Chowk. The man was hit by a vehicle while crossing the road near the U-turn flyover and dragged for a few metres. His body was mangled beyond recognition.

Title: 15-year-old killed in head-on collision between two-wheeler and crane in Delhi's Keshavpuram
content: NEW DELHI: A collision between a crane and a two-wheeler in Keshavpuram area of northwest Delhi resulted in the death of a 15-year-old boy and injury to his friend, police said on Sunday. At midnight, the incident occurred on the carriageway towards Inderlok, close to a restaurant, they said. According to the police, Arib and his 18-year-old friend Irfan while taking a joyride on a scooter after having dinner met with an accident.The crane was returning after finishing the task it had been given by an organisation, they said. . .Advertisement The crane driver fled the spot leaving the two boys injured on the road, a senior police officer said.  They were taken to a nearby hospital where Arib was declared dead while Irfan is under treatment, he said.  A case has been registered under sections 279 (rash driving), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 304 A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code, police said.  The accused driver has been identified and efforts are being taken to nab him, they said.  Police are also scanning through CCTV cameras installed in and around the accident spot to ascertain the sequence of events.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: 16-year-old among 2 dead in Kolkata's Dufferin Road bus accident
content: KOLKATA: A 16-year-old and another unidentified person were killed and 17 others injured - one of them critically - after a minibus reportedly turned on its side and fell on a motorcycle coming from the opposite direction. The bus also hit two more bikes, said cops. The accident was reported around 4.38pm on Saturday on the busy Dufferin Road, opposite the Gandhi statue, barely meters from the Mayo Road crossing.The minibus on the Metiabruz-Howrah route, was heading towards Howrah when the accident took place. The cops said one of the deceased has been identified as Farhan Ahmed Khan (16), one of the two on the bike on which the minibus fell, while the other, cops suspect, is the bus conductor, aged between 40-45 years. Farhan was a resident of Karaya. Even as the bus passengers claimed Farhan was the bike rider going towards Kidderpore Road, cops said they were ascertaining if he was the biker or riding pillion.Cops said the second bike rider is critical. Among the 17 others who were admitted to SSKM Hospital, six were discharged by night. The bus driver fled after the accident. Among the injured, most are from the Port and Kidderpore areas, said cops. "The conductor was crushed underneath the bus. The rear glass of the bus had to be broken to rescue him before a police ambulance rushed him to hospital," said police.. .Advertisement"We have studied the footage and found that the bus was not speeding much. In fact, it was being driven parallel to another bus. There was no vehicle in front of it. Prima facie, this looks like a mechanical failure as the CCTV footage too shows the bus suddenly swerving to its right. It got dragged to the opposite flank only after it fell. The 2009 minibus had one more year before being scrapped but its CF and insurance are all in place," said a senior Lalbazar officer. The Fatal Squad of Traffic Police is likely to take over the probe and the bus owner has been summoned for questioning. Cops will slap a case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder on the bus driver based on passengers' accounts.

Title: 60 goats, sheep killed after truck transporting them topples
content: Krishnagiri: Thirty goats and as many sheep were killed after a truck that was carrying them met with an accident near Kurubarapalli in Krishnagiri late on Friday night. Police said K Shankar, 31, a truck driver, his friends M Nagaraj, 32, and S Ramesh, 31, all from Papparapatty in Dharmapuri, had purchased the cattle from Maharashtra and were returning in Shankarâ€™s truck when the incident took place.â€œThe animals were tied together using a rope. Around 11pm on Friday, the truck was near Somanathapuram in Krishnagiri when Shankar saw a tyre lying on the road. He lost control of the vehicle when he tried to drive around the truck. The animals fell on road and were crushed under the truck which toppled to its side. Shankar and his friends suffered minor injuries,â€ said a police officer. It took two hours to clear the carcasses off the road, during which traffic was halted. Kurubarapalli police registered a case TNN

Title: Youth killed in road accident
content: Visakhapatnam: A 20-year-old man was killed and his friend suffered injuries when their scooter hit a stationary lorry near Maruti Plaza under Gajuwaka police station limits in Vizag city. The deceased was identified as K Gunasekhar, an employee with a private firm. Police said Gunasekhar and his friend Ajay Kumar were returning home from Siripuram on a scooter when they hit a stationary lorry. Gunasekhar died in the accident and Ajau Kumar was admitted to a hospital. A case has been registered. TNN

Title: 2 from Thrissur killed in Tamil Nadu road accident
content: KOCHI: Two persons from Thrissur - Lilly Varghese (63) of Nellikunnu and Jerald Jimmy (9) of Varantharapilli Pallikunnu - were killed when a tourist bus carrying them to Velankanni shrine overturned near Okkanadu in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, on Sunday morning.Police said the bus had 47 pilgrims and around 40 were injured in the accident. They have been admitted to various hospitals in the area. The group had gone from Ollur St Antony's Forane Church.TN chief minister M K Stalin has announced a solatium of 2 lakh each to relatives of the deceased and 50,000 each to those who suffered grievous injuries.

Title: Army man among three killed in road mishap in Muzaffarnagar
content: AGRA: Three persons including a soldier of the Madras Regiment and his relatives died, while his two other family members sustained severe injuries in a road accident in the Khatauli area of the Muzaffarnagar district on Saturday evening. The incident occurred when the car in which they were traveling hit the road divider and later collided with a truck. Following the accident, police rushed to the spot and launched a rescue operation. The deceased were identified as soldier Sohanveer Singh (30), his mother Rajesh Devi (55), and a relative Ankit (25). The deceased's wife and minor son were admitted to the local hospital for treatment. While the boy is stable, his mother was referred to a higher medical centre in Delhi. Khatauli police station SHO Sanjeev Kumar said, "The bodies were sent to the post-mortem house, and the family members of the victims were informed. The victims are residents of the Chhuchai village in Meerut and were en route to Muzaffarnagar to meet their relatives. Further investigation is on." According to DSP Ravi Shankar Mishra, the truck involved in the accident was seized and police are looking for the absconding driver. "A case will be registered into the matter once we receive the complaint," he said. "Sohanveer was posted in Shimla and had come home on leave. He was supposed to go back to his posting after four days. His wife was referred to Delhi for treatment," said Jagveer Singh, former chief of the Chhuchai village.

Title: Pregnant woman among 3 killed in Chhatarpur accident
content: BHOPAL: Three people including a pregnant woman were killed while four were injured in a road accident in Chhatarpur on Monday morning.The incident occurred on highway near Ujra village under Gadhimalhara police station area of the district around 11:45 am when an SUV and a hatchback car collided head-on.The three deceased, on their way to Chhatarpur for medical treatment, were identified as residents of Mahoba in Uttar Pradesh. The mother-daughter duo and the driver of the car were killed in the accident and the deceased pregnant womanâ€™s brother sustained injuries. Besides all three passengers of the SUV were injured and admitted to district hospital.The deceased were identified as Pooja Sen (28), who was eight months pregnant, her mother Guddo Rajesh Sen (45) and Devendra Ashok Soni (29), who was driving the car. Poojaâ€™s brother Rahul Sen (25) sustained injuries. Aditya Nigam (24), Amita Nigam (22), and Ayansh Nigam (13), who were travelling in the SUV were also injured.TNN. .Advertisement

Title: Girl kills self, alleges bias by her parents
content: Rajkot: A 20-year-old college girl hanged herself to death at her home on Sunday evening. In a note that she left in her room, the undergraduate student alleged that her parents used to discriminate between her and her younger brother. The University police have registered an accidental death and started an investigation. According to the police, the deceased Devangi Trada (20) was alone at her home on University road on Sunday evening when she took the extreme step. Her parents had gone to their relativeâ€™s house and her brother was out attending a birthday party of his friend. Devangiâ€™s family lives in a two-storied house. The first floor is occupied by Devangiâ€™s uncle and aunt while she and her family lived on the ground floor. As Devangiâ€™s parents were away, her aunt came to check on her. When she did not open the door despite knocking on the door several times, Devangiâ€™s aunt became suspicious. She broke open the door and found Devangi hanging by the ceiling. She also found a note in the room and informed the police and her parents. The police said that in the note, Devangi wrote, â€œMy parents bought a mobile phone for my (younger) brother but they denied me one. They (her parents) discriminate between me and my brother. My family has no feelings for me and because of that frustration I am taking this step.â€ Devangi is the eldest of two siblings. Her father operates a drilling machine used in construction activities. . .Advertisement

Title: 83-yr-old killed while crossing SG Road
content: Ahmedabad: An 83-year-old man from Ghatlodia died on Monday morning after he was hit by a speeding car while he was crossing SG Road near the SGVP campus in Chharodi on Sunday evening.According to the FIR filed with SG-1 Division traffic police, Rashmin Bharucha, 44, a resident of Tragad, said his father, Navin , died in the accident. Rashmin said he got a call at around 9pm on Sunday from his sister telling him their father was at a private hospital. Rashmin rushed to the hospital and found his father unconscious and under treatment. The car driver was identified as Kirit Patel, a resident of Sector-2 of Gandhinagar, and he had taken Navin to hospital. Navin died during treatment at around 4.50am on Monday. SG-1 Division traffic police registered a case of causing death by negligent driving against Patel.

Title: Techie dies after scooter skids
content: Bengaluru: A 23-year-old techie riding pillion on her friendâ€™s scooter succumbed to head injuries after their gearless two-wheeler skid on NICE Road near Hosakerahalli Junction on Sunday evening, police said.JP Nagar resident V Anantakumar and R Sulochana from Banashankari 3rd Stage, software engineers with different firms, were riding home from Koramangala on the formerâ€™s scooter when the accident happened around 6pm, police said.â€œThe rear tyre of the scooter seems to have gone flat. As a result, it skid off the road. Anantakumar and Sulochana sustained injuries and were shifted to Victoria Hospital. Sulochana died on Monday morning,â€ a traffic cop said, adding: â€œAnantakumar was wearing a full-face helmet, while Sulochana had a half helmet. When they fell down, Sulochanaâ€™s helmet came off and her head hit the road. The injury proved fatal.â€Man, daughter killed . .AdvertisementA 47-year-old man and his four-year-old daughter were killed after a speeding car rammed their bike from behind at Sabarapalya in Magadi of Ramanagara on Monday morning. The car driver fled leaving behind the vehicle. Locals rushed to the spot and called an ambulance, but Yogesh, a private company employee, and his daughter Harshita passed away before it arrived.

Title: 7 tourists die as avalanche strikes Sikkim's Nathu La
content: GANGTOK: A massive avalanche swept away a group of tourists in east Sikkim's Nathu La area near the China border on Tuesday, killing at least seven of them and injuring 13.The avalanche hit the arterial Jawaharlal Nehru Marg, which connects state capital Gangtok to Nathu La which is at an altitude of 14,450 feet, at 11.30am, burying five to six vehicles with around 30 tourists and drivers under the snow, the Army said.Seven bodies were fished out and 23 people, including six tourists, rescued. The injured were admitted to STNM Hospitals in Gangtok, around 55km away. Among the dead are a woman and a child, a state official said, adding three of the deceased are from Nepal, two from UP and two from Bengal.. .AdvertisementThe search operation was called off in the evening due to inclement weather. "Distressed by the avalanche. Condolences to those who have lost their loved ones. I hope the injured recover soon. Rescue operations are underway and all possible assistance is being provided to those affected," PM Narendra Modi said. Union home minister Amit Shah said the Centre was closely monitoring the situation. Sikkim CM Prem Singh Tamang visited the state-run STNM Hospital in Gangtok at night and met the injured persons. He said 13 injured tourists were brought to the hospital, and nine of them were discharged after fast-aid. The tourists were not permitted to travel beyond Milestone 13 due to inclement weather, but they forced the tour operators and the drivers to take them to the area â€” between Milestones 13 and 17 â€” where the avalanche struck, a police officer said. At the time of the accident, people were taking photographs near a stream, senior police official Tenzing Loden Lepcha claimed. Rescue workers used a JCB and shovels to dig up the snow for survivors. Some 350 people in 80 vehicles, stranded on the road because of the snow, were brought back to Gangtok, officials said. The Sikkim East district has been experiencing rainfall and snowfall in the higher reaches since Friday. Forecasts and nowcasts on rainfall, thunderstorms and lightning have been issued.

Title: 2 killed in separate road accidents in Igatpuri
content: Nashik: Two persons were killed in separate road accidents in Igatpuri on Sunday. In both the accidents, the bikers who were riding were responsible for their deaths. A 19-year-old youth, Krushna Bhagat, died in one accident while a 35-year-old biker, Macchindra Gaikone was killed in the other one. The Igatpuri police said that Bhagat was riding his bike on the wrong side from Nandgaon Sado towards Pimpri Phata on Sunday at 6pm. His bike collided against a water tanker and caused him to fall. The other accident took place on the new Samruddhi Highway flyover, which passes through Igatpuri. The biker Gaikone, a resident of Talogh village was speeding on the under-construction flyover when his bike accidentally passed over a stone and he fell down. This incident took place at 8.30pm on Sunday.. .AdvertisementBhagat and Gaikone both suffered serious injuries in the accident resulting in their death.

Title: One killed, 19 injured as UPSRTC bus overturns in Balrampur
content: LUCKNOW: A man was killed while 19 others suffered injuries after an Uttar Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation bus carrying them overturned in Balrampur district on Thursday. The bus was going from Kanpur to Tulsipur.The police and rescue team reached the spot and started the relief work. The identity of the deceased has not been ascertained as of yet. CM Yogi Adityanath has expressed grief over this incident and directed officials to carry out rescue work.Additional SP, Balrampur, Namrata Srivastava said that the accident took place at Kuano road connecting Balrampur to Gonda."All the injured were sent to the district hospital, the condition of two passengers was serious, and they have been referred to Gonda," she said.. .AdvertisementShe also said that what exactly caused the bus to overturn is being probed.

Title: 3-year-old killed in accident in Pune, driver held
content: PUNE: A garbage truck ran over a three-year-old girl standing near a road divider in Ambedkarnagar, Market Yard, around 4.30pm on Monday. The child was identified as Poonam Yadav from Ambedkarnagar. The police arrested the truck driver, Bharat Sable (24) of Market Yard, on the charge of negligence causing death.Sub-inspector Chetan Bhosale said the girl had stopped near the divider while playing when the truck, which was going to Market Yard, hit her and she came under the rear wheels. She died on the spot.The victim's father, Jaisingh Yadav, said: "My daughter was a kindergarten student. She was playing with her two friends when she was hit by the truck. Her friends later informed us of the accident." . .Advertisement

Title: Father-son duo killed in road accident
content: Coimbatore: A 60-year-old man and his 32-year-old son were killed after a bus hit their moped at Kittampalayam Four Road Junction on Karumathampatti â€“ Annur Road on Thursday.The deceased were identified as V Thangavel, a farmer, and his son T Nandakumar, employee of a private firm. They were residents of Ganapathypalayam near Vagarayampalayam.Thangavel, who was riding the moped, was planning to drop his son at a bus stop at Karumathampatti to catch a bus to Kodumudi in Erode district on Thursday morning. â€œWhen they reached Kittampalayam Four-Road Junction, a private polytechnic collegeâ€™s bus hit the moped. The father-son duo sustained severe head injuries and died on the spot,â€ a police officer said.A CCTV footage of the incident went viral on social media.. .AdvertisementThe Karumathampatti police registered a case against bus driver R Selvam, 60, of Cheran Colony in Pollachi. The bodies were handed over to the family after postmortem.

Title: KSRTC bus driver moving on wrong lane kills farmer near Bengaluru Rural DC office on NH-207
content: BENGALURU: A 50-year-old farmer was killed after his bike collided with a KSRTC bus at Beerasandra village near the Bengaluru Rural DC office on NH-207 on Thursday morning. Ramesh B, a resident of Bhairadenahalli, was on his way to Doddaballapur to sell flowers from a farm in Ramanathapura village when the bus hit his bike at 10.30am, police said. Ramesh is survived by his wife and two daughters.Police said the bus was being driven on the wrong lane. It was supposed to head towards Devanahalli on the left lane, but driver Manjunath was moving on the right. He was arrested. In the FIR, cops named the project manager of a private construction firm - contracted by the highway development authorities - for not putting up signboards during road work.Locals, including friends and relatives of Ramesh, staged a protest and refused to shift his body from the accident spot for over 3 hours. Only after cops promised to take strict action against the officials responsible for not erecting signboards during the ongoing construction work, the protesters relented.. .Advertisement

Title: Constable killed in accident in Jalna
content: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar: Two including a police constable serving with the Jalna police have been killed in an accident on Wednesday at Gondhankheda Phata on Jafrabad â€“ Tembhurni road of Jalna district.According to the police two constables were heading towards Valsa to pay obeisance at the Hanuman temple, when they had a head-on collision with a speeding bike coming from the opposite direction.The deceased have been identified as police constable Nitin Matkari (30), and Ganesh Pawar (32), who was riding the other bike. The pillion rider behind Pawar, Harischandra Mudgal (28) and police constable Rahul Jangle (27), who was on the bike with Matkari, were seriously injured. The accident was so severe that the constable died on the spot. Pawar, who was rushed to the hospital with the other two, succumbed to his injuries the same night. . .Advertisement

Title: Truck-bike crash kills 1
content: Bengaluru: A 24-year-old private firm employee was killed when a speeding tipper truck rammed his motorbike in Hennur, east Bengaluru, around 11pm. Shashidhar L, resident of Nagavara and hailing from Tirupati, was riding back home from office when the accident occurred near Narayanapura bridge. The truck driver fled leaving behind his vehicle.BMTC bus mows down bikerIn another accident near Sadahalli gate in Chikkajala, a BMTC bus mowed down a 40-year-old biker on Thursday morning. Raju H, a truck driver, was riding to Chikkaballapur to meet a friend when the accident took place on a service road at 7am. The BMTC bus, heading from Sadahalli towards Yelahanka, rammed Rajuâ€™s bike.. .Advertisement

Title: Passenger, bus conductor killed as MSRTC bus rams into tree in Nashik
content: NASHIK: Two women, including a bus conductor were killed when a Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus rammed into a tree in Nashik district on Friday. The district controller of MSRTC, Arun Siya, said that the bus was carrying 13 passengers from Saptashrungi Gad to Manmad when the accident occurred at about 2pm on Chandwadâ€“Deola road. As per the officer, the locals quickly helped in shifting the injured to local hospitals. Sarika Lahire (34), the bus conductor of the bus, suffered grievous injuries. She was taken to Chandwad sub-district hospital where the doctors declared her brought dead. . .AdvertisementAnother woman passenger who suffered grievous injuries died while being shifted to Nashik Civil Hospital.

Title: 1 killed, 19 injuredas bus turns turtle
content: Lucknow: A man was killed and 19 others sustained injuries when a bus of UP State Road Transport Corporation (SRTC) overturned and fell into a ditch in Balrampur district on Thursday. The bus was going from Kanpur to Tulsipur, Gonda. A police and rescue team reached the spot.Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed grief over this incident and directed officials to carry out rescue work. The accident took place at Kuano road connecting Balrampur to Gonda. According to the police, when the bus, coming from Kanpur reached Khanti area of Balrampur around 5 am, it started wobbling and overturned. â€œAll the passengers, who had sustained injuries, were admitted to the district hospital,â€ said one Pradeep Yadav, who was also injured in the accident.Additional SP, Balrampur, Namrata Srivastava said that the deceased was identified as Rajesh Kumar, a resident of Mau. He worked as a health worker in CHC Shridattaganj. Driver of the bus Ajay Dwivedi told the police that the accident occurred when a truck suddenly came in front of his bus. MD, UPSRTC, Sanjay Kumar announced a compensation of Rs 7.50 lakh for the deceased.. .Advertisement

Title: Sand-laden tractor kills 4 of family in Alwar, protests erupt
content: ALWAR: A 40-year-old man and his three children were killed after a tractor-trolley laden with sand (bajri) hit an autorickshaw in the Kathumar area of Alwar district on Thursday.The accident triggered a huge protest in the area as locals set the tractor on fire and pelted police vehicles with stones. The victims have been identified as Murai Rao, his daughter Krishna (14), and sons Nitesh (12) and Gaurav (11). Rao was driving the auto. His wife was also injured in the accident and is undergoing treatment.The incident sparked protests and a police vehicle was vandalised. Protesters refused to let the cops take the bodies to the mortuary and demanded justice for the victims.Till late night, senior police officers and district administration officials were trying to pacify the angry crowd. Locals alleged that tractors transporting illegally mined sand speed through roads, putting the lives of villagers in danger. Villagers are demanding strict action against mining operators.. .AdvertisementThey also alleged that the police and mining departments were turning a blind eye to the menace. "We have sent officials to speak to villagers because the road is still blocked. Additional forces have been deployed at the scene," an official said.

Title: 1 killed, 22 injured as bus veers off road and falls into ditch in Pune
content: PUNE: One person was killed and 22 suffered injuries when a bus veered off the road and fell into a ditch in Pune in the wee hours of Saturday. According to an official, the accident took place at around 5.30am near Malad village in the district's Baramati taluka. "The private bus, carrying 50 passengers, was on its way from Tuljapur when the driver apparently lost control and the vehicle skidded off the road and fell into a ditch," he said. "Sixteen injured people have been admitted in Pyramid Trauma Centre and the rest six in Bhigwan ICU Hospital. The bus was chartered for a pilgrimage tour of Kolhapur, Pandharpur, etc. Most of the passengers are residents of Bhavani Peth," he added.. .Advertisement(With inputs from PTI)

Title: Six of family killed in road accident in Uttar Pradesh
content: BALRAMPUR(UP): Six members of a family were killed when the car in which they were travelling collided with an unidentified vehicle in Uttar Pradesh's Balrampur early Saturday, police said. The incident took place near Vishambharpur village under Sriduttganj police station, they said.Sonu Shah (28), a resident of Deoria district who worked at a paper mill in Nainital, left for his village with his wife, children and other family members on Friday evening, Superintendent of Police (SP) Keshav Kumar said. Read AlsoUP: Six of a family killed in road accident in SaharanpurSix members of a family was killed while another suffered serious injuries after the vehicle they were travelling collided with a truck on the Delhi-Yamunotri highway on Sunday night.UP: Six of family killed in road accident on Ayodhya-Lucknow highwaySix members of a family were killed after the car they were travelling in bumped into a stationary truck in Barabanki district of UP on Wednesday morning. The accident occurred near Narayanpur in Ramsnehighat Kotwali area on Ayodhya-Lucknow highway.. .AdvertisementEarly on Saturday morning, the car collided with an unidentified vehicle near Vishambharpur village, killing Shah, his wife Sujavati (25), their children Ruchika (6) and Divyanshi (4), Shah's brother Ravi (18) and sister Khushi (13) on the spot, the SP said.Six teams have been formed to identify and seize the vehicle that collided with the victims' car, he added. The bodies have been sent for post-mortem examination. The family members of the victims were informed and they have arrived here, the police said.(With PTI inputs)

Title: Four of a family die in road accident
content: Ranchi: Four members of a same family from Chouparan died after the SUV they were traveling in collided with a trailor truck near Chandwara police station under Koderma district. The family was on way to participate at Gayatri Mahayagya in the adjoining Koderma district and the accident occured early on Thursday morning. The family members are of a prominent businessman â€” Bijay Sahu â€” of Chechakala village of Chouparan. Among those killed are Bijay Sahuâ€™s father Chandra Bhushan Naik (65), his mother Triguna Devi, 60, and another elderly relative Chanchala Devi (82) and only son of Bijay Sahu named Devraj, 5.Other family members, including Bijayâ€™s wife Sonam Gupta, are admitted in a hospital where they are undergoing treatment. TNN

Title: 1 dead, 12 injured in accident near Igatpuri
content: Nashik: A person was killed and over a dozen were injured when a speeding private luxury bus rammed a stationary truck on the Mumbai-Agra Highway near Igatpuri early on Friday.The deceased has been identified as Mehmood Shaikh (60), a resident of Vaizapur in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar district. Shaikh was carrying material in his truck from Mumbai towards Vaizapur, the Igatpuri police said. While travelling through Igatpuri, he parked the truck on the roadside, along the highway, to check the air pressure of the tyres. A speeding private luxury bus rammed the truck, killing Shaikh on the spot.The injured were rushed to rural hospitals by ambulances of the Ghoti toll plaza. PI Raju Surve of the Igatpuri police said five patients were admitted to the Igatpuri rural hospital. One was later shifted to the Nashik civil hospital and two to Shirdi. â€œWe believe a total 12 to 13 passengers were travelling ,â€ he said.

Title: Teen killed as SUV overturns after crashing into divider
content: Bhopal: An 18-year-old youth died while six others including his elder brother and friends sustained grievous injuries after the SUV they were travelling rammed into a road divider and overturned beside VIP Road near Khanugaon in Koh-e-Fiza locality in the wee hours of Friday morning. Investigating officer ASI Sunil Mishra said that the deceased Rohit Kumar Vaishav, 18, a resident of Agarwal Complex near Talaiya police outpost, was a student of class 10. He left home along with his elder brother Naresh, 20, around 4.30 am by his father's SUV and picked up his friends including Shadab, Arib, Arbaz, Sami and Faiz to go on a joy ride on VIP Road. The ASI said that Naresh was driving the SUV. They left home without informing anybody to have tea with friends and drop two of them to their homes. When they reached near Khanugaon Naresh lost control over the vehicle at high speed while negotiating a turn and it overturned after hitting a divider. They were rescued by locals and rushed to a nearby hospital, where Rohit died while his brother and friends were undergoing treatment. Rohit was sitting on the front seat beside the driver. A case was registered at Koh-e-Fiza police station and further investigations are underway. Meanwhile, in another incident reported at Berasia police station, a 31-year-old bike-borne bank employee died after he accidentally rammed a tractor trolley parked on the road on Thursday night. . .AdvertisementPolice said that the deceased Narendra Patil, 31, hailed from Jalgaon in Maharashtra. He worked as an agriculture field officer at a nationalised bank. In the evening on Thursday, when Narendra was returning home after finishing routine work, he rammed into a tractor trolley parked on the road near Imaliya village in Berasia. Narendra was wearing a helmet but the collision was such that he sustained severe injuries. He was rushed to a hospital, where doctors declared him brought dead and alerted the cops.Investigation officer ASI Ramniwas Ojha said that the locals told police that Narendra couldn't spot the trolley as a vehicle coming from the opposite direction flashed high beams. Police have registered a case and further investigations were on.

Title: Man killed as wild boar hits bike
content: Hyderabad: A 51-year-old home guard died in a road accident at Lokeshwaram in Nirmal district on Thursday night. The accident occurred while the home guard was returning home on a bike after duty in Nizamabad town. Rao was hit by a wild boar which strayed onto the road at Mohalla near Lokeshwaram. He suffered severe head injuries. Though he was rushed to a nearby hospital, he was declared brought dead, Lokeshwaram police said.tnn

Title: 7-yr-old dies, mom critical after lorry hits their car
content: Hyderabad: A seven-year-old boy, returning from a pilgrimage in Shirdi, was killed and his mother battling for life after their car was hit by a lorry in Bhainsa town â€” very close to their residence â€” early on Friday. Four others were injured and undergoing treatment in a hospital.â€œThe lorry was heading towards Nanded and the family was returning to Bhainsa after visiting Shirdi. Aditya suffered multiple injuries and died on way to a hospital in Nizamabad,â€ Nirmal police said.A case has been registered against lorry driver under section 304-A (negligence causing death) of IPC. Lorry driver fled from the spot soon after the accident. Police said the lorry came at a great speed from the opposite direction and crashed into the car. Cops said though it is a national highway, it does not have a proper road divider. Adityaâ€™s mother Kalavathi suffered serious injuries and is in critical condition.tnn

Title: Bhawani Peth pilgrim killed in bus accident, 36 injured
content: PUNE: A passenger was killed and 36 others were injured when a private luxury bus veered off the Pune-Solapur highway and tipped over in a roadside ditch at Malad village on Saturday morning.Inspector Bhausaheb Patil of the Daund police said, "Anita Sanjay Pawar (32) from Bhawani Peth died in the accident. The injured persons were moved to three different hospitals in Bhigwan and Daund. All of them are casual workers or homemakers."The bus was heading towards Pune city from Tuljapur. The accident occurred around 5.45am, when it was about 100km away from the city.Police inspector Patil said, "All the passengers in the bus were from Bhawani Peth and Kasewadi areas of the city. They had gone on a pilgrimage and visited many famous temples in the last five days. After visiting the Tuljapur and Yedai Devi temples, they had started from Solapur for Pune in the bus on Friday.". .AdvertisementHe said, "An injured bus passenger, Suraj Anil Sathe of Bhawani Peth, lodged a complaint with the police in connection with the accident. We have registered a case of rash and negligent driving against the bus driver, a resident of Warje."A source in the police said the bus driver lost control over the wheel and the vehicle veered off the road. It entered a ditch along the highway, he said.According to the Daund police, a man from Bhawani Peth had hired the bus. It left the city in the morning on April 3. The bus was supposed to return to the city on Saturday.

Title: 6 of a family killed as car collides with truck in Balrampur
content: AYODHYA: A total of six people, including a boiler plant operator along with his two children, wife and two siblings, were killed after the car in which they were travelling collided with an unidentified heavy vehicle on Saturday. The accident took place near Vishambharpur village in Sriduttganj area of Uttar Pradesh's Balrampur district.Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath expressed "deep anguish" over the loss of lives in the road accident, his office said in a tweet in Hindi.He has issued directions that financial assistance of Rs 2 lakh each be provided to the family members of the deceased without any delay, the Chief Minister's Office said. Sriduttganj station officer Vipul Pandey said that the deceased were identified on the basis of Aadhaar cards as Sonu Shah (28), who worked as a boiler operator at a paper mill in Nainital and had left for his village in Deoria with his wife Sujavati (25), children Ruchika (6) and Divyanshi (4), and two siblings Ravi (18), Khushi (13). Six teams have been formed to identify and seize the vehicle that collided with the victims' car, he added. The bodies have been sent for post-mortem examination."Around 3.30 am, Sonu allegedly dozed off following which his car swayed and changed direction and some unidentified heavy vehicle coming from the opposition direction collided head-on with his car. The impact was so strong that the car was reduced to a pulp," said Pandey.. .Advertisement

Title: Four killed as SUV hits bike, truck in Kolkata
content: KOLKATA: Four persons were killed in an accident on VIP Road in Kolkata when a compact SUV rammed into a truck after running over two bikers in the wee hours of Monday. "Witnesses have said the car was moving at a very high speed along VIP Road towards Kolkata airport swirling and swerving dangerously. At Dum Dum Park crossing, a truck was standing at a red signal and so did the motorbike behind it. The car first hit the bike before it rammed into the truck," said a senior officer of Bidhannagar City Police.The impact of the accident was so strong that the body of a woman, who was seated in the front seat of the car, shattered the windshield and then fell onto the hood.None of the car occupants wore seatbelts and the airbags didn't activate on time, killing the driver Hiralal Jaiswara (46) and the passenger on the front seat Puja Singh (30). Two other occupants at the backseat of the car -- cousins Kundan and Rajesh Mullick (both in their early 20s) -- are still fighting for their lives at RG Kar Hospital and Medical College.. .AdvertisementBike rider Rohit Kumar (23) and his friend who was riding pillion, Babu Kundu (25), too were declared dead on being taken to the hospital.

Title: Two youths killed in bike accident at Aluva
content: Kochi: A bike accident claimed two lives at Aluva early on Sunday. The deceased were identified as Varghese Thomas, 29, of Alappuzha and Sudheesh, 23, of Thrissur. According to Aluva East police, the accident occurred on the national highway near Ambattukavu Metro station around 2am.The police said their bike reportedly lost control and rammed one of the Metro pillars near the station. Though they were rushed to the Government Medical College Hospital, Ernakulam, the doctors declared them brought dead.The police said they are yet to get clarity about what led to the accident. The CCTVs in the area could not clearly capture the accident and there were no witnesses it seems. The police inspected the place and found that the bike skidded several times on the road before ramming the Metro pillar.â€œThe duo was heading towards Thrissur but their purpose is unclear. Moreover, who rode the bike too is not clear. We donâ€™t know if overspeed caused the accident or whether any other vehicle was involved. We have registered a case of motor occurrence and launched a probe,â€ said a police officer.. .AdvertisementThe body of Sudheesh has been released to the relatives after postmortem. The body of Thomas is kept in the mortuary and his postmortem will be held on Monday. The police are collecting the statements from the relatives also to get details about the duo.

Title: Man killed, wife hurt as speeding truck hits bike
content: Raipur: A man was killed and his wife was critically injured when the bike they were riding was hit by a speeding truck on Sunday morning at Baloda-Bazar road. The middle-aged couple were heading towards the market area when the accident happened. The driver fled the spot, police said. The impact was such that the bike was dragged for several meters due to which the woman was thrown on the side of the road while the man was crushed to death. The woman is undergoing treatment at district hospital.TNN

Title: One killed in road accident
content: Vijayawada: One person died on the spot and another was injured on Sunday night on Mangalagiri Bypass. The duo was under the influence of alcohol when they hit a divider at high speed. The deceased has been identified as V Joshua (20), while Karthik Babu (22) received injuries and is undergoing treatment at a local government hospital. A case has been registered. TNN

Title: Over 300 deaths/year: Booze kills more than road accidents in Goa
content: PANAJI: Even as alcoholism is a known problem in Goa, the sheer extent of the menace is disturbing, medical experts told TOI. Annually, Goa Medical College (GMC), the state's premier healthcare institute, sees over 300 deaths due to alcohol liver cirrhosis, a number comparable to road traffic accident deaths. In 2022, GMC reported 318 deaths due to alcoholic liver disease, and the previous year, it reported 356. Last year, Goa recorded 271 road accident deaths.GMC also sees five to six medium to severe cases of alcohol-related illnesses every day. And doctors say liver cirrhosis deaths have only gone up since 2018."It is a huge problem. We see a lot of patients with chronic alcoholism, who are likely to remain admitted for a longer duration," said GMC dean Dr S M Bandekar.Hospital data reveals that up to 5,500 patients are admitted to GMC every month, 25% of whom could have alcohol-related problems, according to doctors.. .AdvertisementAnd the profile of the typical patient is changing too. A few years ago, patients in their mid-forties and fifties would get liver cirrhosis, but today, it isn't common to see even men in their early twenties. The age of drinking has fallen. Also, the quantity of drink consumed in a single sitting by some is high,â€ said senior psychiatrist Dr Rajesh Dhume, who is also nodal officer, drug treatment centre (DTC), North Goa district hospital. â€œPatients come in in their thirties after they start seeing health problems. Earlier the age of (starting) drinking, more severe will be the habit.â€ The North Goa district hospital sees a lot of cases from lower economic sections. Dhume says that patients who get withdrawal symptoms and seizures have probably been drinking for at least five to 10years. â€œPeople who consume alcohol regularly are likely to develop some or the other serious health condition five to ten years down the line,â€ he said. Also, those who drink dangerously will likely bring some harm to their families and society, apart from endangering their own lives. â€œSuch people are abusive towards their family, they drive recklessly. They are a potential danger to others. They too eventually suffer from alcohol-related problems,â€ he said. While it is difficult to quantify the data of alcoholics in Goa, GMCâ€™s professor of surgery Dr Rajesh Patil said that apart from patients admitted for liver cirrhosis, a lot more who are under treatment for other illnesses could also be alcoholics. â€œNobody likes to admit that he or she is an alcoholic,â€ he said. â€œHe can be a cancer or trauma patient. On the third or fourth day of the admission, when the patient develops withdrawal symptoms, his addiction is revealed.â€ Patil, who has long been associated with Alcoholics Anonymous meetings at GMC, said that with the help of the organisation, they try to help their patients. â€œEarlier, we would ask our staff to inform patients about the Thursday meetings.
