Now, they send patients without us asking them. It is a positive development,â€ Patil said. Among male patients, he said, the commonest diagnosis is alcoholic pancreatitis. Before they are discharged, they are told to attend meetings. Their families are also counselled. â€œThe families suffer silently. Sometimes, they need more counselling and help than the patient,â€ Patil said. In most cases, a family is the last and only anchor for an alcoholic, but at some point, loved ones too give up on him in frustration, and the family thinks only the personâ€™s death would end their problems. â€œWe counsel families to change their line of thinking. They are told how to deal with an alcoholic mind, and that a revolt or revenge does not help,â€ he said. While several donâ€™t accept the helping hand and continue drinking till they perish, some do. â€œBut there are many who recover. After turning sober, they still attend meetings, this time, to help others,â€ said Patil.

Title: One killed, five injured in accident
content: Mangaluru: One person died and five others sustained injuries after the car in which they were travelling fell into a 100-metre-deep gorge between the second and third curve of Charmadi Ghat on Sunday night. The deceased is Sarojini Shetty, 64. The injured have been identified as Ujire gram panchayat president Pushpavathi R Shetty, 64, her daughter Poornima Shetty, 42, grandchildren Sakshi, 10, and Samrudh, 14, and car driver Arunakara Gowda, 35. They met with the accident when they were reportedly returning after attending a relativeâ€™s funeral. Belthangady traffic police have registered a case in this connection.Pillion rider dies A pillion rider died after his two-wheeler skidded off the road at Ponnodi in B Mooda village of Bantwal taluk on the Bengaluru-Mangaluru route of NH 75 in the early hours of Monday. According to police, the deceased is Yashodhara, 23. Rider Avinash sustained injuries in the incident. Though Yashodhara was rushed to hospital, doctors declared him brought dead. Rash and negligent riding caused the mishap. A case has been registered at Bantwal Traffic police station under IPC sections 279 , 337 and 304 (A).

Title: High-speed crash claims 4 as SUV rams bike in Kolkata, truck waiting at signal
content: KOLKATA: Two front-seat occupants of an SUV and two youths on a bike were killed and two others critically injured when the car travelling at breakneck speed rammed the bike and then crashed into a stationary truck on VIP Road in the early hours of Monday. Both the bike and the truck were waiting at a traffic signal. Such was the impact that the body of front-seat passenger Puja Singh (32) smashed through the windscreen and landed on the bonnet. The front of the car was completely mangled and the body of driver Hiralal Jaiswara (46) crushed between the steering wheel and seat. The two back-seat occupants, cousins Kundan and Rajesh Mullick, both in their late 20s, were sandwiched between the collapsed seats and had to be brought out along with the car seat. They are fighting for life at RG Kar Hospital and Medical College. None of the occupants in the car wore seatbelts and the twin front airbags did not deploy.Bike rider Rohit Kumar (28) and his friend Babu Kundu (34), who was riding pillion, were declared dead on being taken to the hospital. Kumar, a resident of Bhagalpur in Bihar, was a life insurance agent and lived in New Town. Kundu, a resident of Ultadanga's Oriyapara, used to help in his father's plywood business. Jaiswara and the Mullick brothers are all residents of Salkia in Howrah while Singh lived in Keshtopur. . .AdvertisementWitnesses said the car was moving at a very high speed along VIP Road towards Kolkata airport all the while weaving dangerously through traffic when the accident happened around 1.45am. "At Dum Dum Park crossing, the truck and bike had both stopped as the signal had turned red. But the SUV didn't stop or slow down and first hit the bike before crashing into the rear of the truck," said a senior Bidhannagar Police officer. 'Duo on bike died despite following traffic rules' Rohit Kumar had stopped at a red signal early on Monday when he was killed. Rohit, who hailed from Bhagalpur in Bihar, came to Bengal in search of better job prospects. The 25-year-old was riding the motorcycle with friend Babu Kundu (34) pillion riding when the speeding SUV crashed in to the bike. The duo were declared dead at RG Kar Hospital.Rohit used to work as an LIC agent in Murshidabad before he got a job with SBI Life and shifted to Kolkata. Even as his paternal uncle lives in Howrah with family, Rohit wanted to stay independently and preferred a rented flat in New Town's Sampurna Housing Complex in Action Area 1."Being the second among four brothers Rohit was very responsible towards his family in Bhagalpur," said Ramesh Singh, a relative.Rohit was an avid biker but friends and relatives said he always obeyed rules. "Rohit came to our house around 1.30pm on Sunday and left with my brother," said Babu's brother Shantanu. The duo then met another friend, Babu Maity, who also lived in the same locality where they chatted for some time and left."We met around 2pm and they left. I have no idea where they were heading to. I got information about the accident in the wee hours," said Maity.Babu used to help his father run the family's plywood store in Ultadanga. Rohit often visited the locality on holidays to meet his friends. "My son and his friend had obeyed all traffic rules like wearing helmets and stopping at traffic signal. They died for no fault of theirs," said Babu's father Manik Kundu.Hiralal Jaiswara, who was driving the four-wheeler, was the only earning member of the family. He's survived by his wife, a 16-year-old son and a daughter, who studies in class VIII. . "I called him at night on Sunday but he did not respond. But I was not worried as he would often come back late. At times, he did not even return. I have not told our daughter about his father's death," said Jaiswara's wife Rumki Devi."Hira was a friend and we live in the same locality. He would often take us out in the car. On Sunday night, he picked me up and my cousin Kundan. We both were at the backseat. I remember the car crashing in to something. When I regained consciousness, I found myself in hospital," Rajesh Mullick told TOI. The Mullick cousins, who are residents of Bamugachhi, are conservancy workers. While Rajesh sustained minor injuries, Kundan is on ventilation."Six of the victims were brought here. While four of them were brought dead two are undergoing treatment. The condition of one of them is very critical," said Tridip Mustaphi, additional super at RG Kar Medical College.

Title: Speeding dumper runs over security guard in Kota
content: Kota: A 56-year-old-man, who worked as security guard at a hotel, was killed on the spot after a speeding dumper ran over him on Monday morning in front of JDB girls college road under Nayapura police station in Kota. At the time of the accident, the victim was returning home on bicycle from duty.Police said the dumper driver fled away from the spot, however the vehicle was seized. A speedy dumper hit the bicycle riding man from behind, killing the victim on the spot, said Dayaram, circle inspector (CI) at Nayapura police station.The deceased man was identified as Chandra Mohan (56), a resident of Arjunpura under Borkheda police station of the city, who worked as security guard at a hostel in Chhawani area. Police handed over the body to family after postmortem later in the day on Monday and lodged a case of negligence driving against driver, who is yet to be nabbed, he said. tnn

Title: 3 dead in Muzaffarnagar road accident
content: AGRA: Three persons, including one woman, were killed in an accident on the Budhana-Barot road in the Muzaffarnagar district on Monday, said SP, rural, Atul Kumar Srivastava. According to police, the victims were returning from the Pilkhuwa village of Meerut late at night, in a Bolero car, when its driver fell asleep and the car crashed into a tree. The entire incident was captured on CCTV which later went viral on social media. The deceased were identified as Poonam Devi (50), a resident of Jalalpur village in the Titavi area, and her relatives Wajis Kumar (30), and Vinit Kumar (35). Two others identified as Manoj (28), and Ankur (25) received injuries during the accident and were referred to the district hospital for treatment. â€œThe incident took place under the Budhana police station area at around 1.30 am. It seems that the driver dozed off or there could be some other reason as well. The matter is being investigated and the families of the deceased have been informed about the incident,â€ Srivastava said.

Title: 4 men picking up fallen wheat run over and killed in Dhar
content: INDORE: Four labourers, including a father and son, who were gathering up wheat that had fallen on the road from their vehicle, were killed when a speeding pick-up hit them in MP's Dhar on the intervening night of Monday-Tuesday.Sardarpur police station in-charge Pradeep Khanna said that the accident took place on Indore-Ahmedabad highway around 12:30am. Munnalal Lodha, 47, a resident of Dhar, was on his way home with wheat from Rajgarh in a tractor trolley when some of the sacks fell on the road near Bheru Chowki village, around 100km west of Indore. Munnalal parked his vehicle by the roadside and called his son Navdeep, 29, who arrived with a few labourers. They were picking up the fallen sacks when a pickup sped out of the darkness and ran them over, said Sandeep Lodha, who saw the accident. Munnalal, Navdeep, Lavkush, and Arjun Singh died on the spot. . .AdvertisementOther labourers and passersby took them to a community health centre in Sardarpur where they were declared dead on arrival. SDOP Ram Singh Meda said that the pickup driver has been arrested. The bodies were handed over to the families after post-mortem examination.Munnalal owned a grocery store in Dabri village of Naugaon in Dhar district. Navdeep operates a DJ vehicle. He is survived by his wife Monika and son Aarush.

Title: 2 die as trucks collide on NH-2
content: HAZARIBAG: A driver and a helper died in an accident involving three trucks at Danua Bhanua ghati section of the NH-2 in Hazaribag on Monday late night. Their bodies were retrieved on Tuesday, Barhi SDPO Nazir Akhtar said. Two others who sustained injuries trapped in the trucks were also pulled out. Police said efforts are on to ascertain if any more person is trapped.All the three trucks fell into a gorge after the collision. Rescue operation was underway since Monday night, police said. In the last fortnight, more than a dozen accidents have occurred here in which four people were killed and several injured."We have informed the NHAI authorities as well as the transport department of the district to take immediate action to contain regular accidents here. Due to yesterday's accident, traffic between Delhi and Kolkata remained suspended for more than 18 hours. Traffic had to be diverted," Akhtar said. District transport officer Vijay Kumar stressed on displaying warning boards to caution people driving through this patch. "NHAI authorities should take immediate corrective steps, including realignment of the road, if needed," he said.Nilesh Pandey, team leader of NHAI Hazaribag division, admitted some "defects" in the stretch. "We have given a proposal to the NHAI headquarters in New Delhi to convert this section into six lanes so that the frequency of accident can be minimised. Also, alcohol test of the drivers should be conducted regularly. For construction of new alignment, we have recently submitted a fresh proposal worth Rs 288 crore. If needed we have to acquire about 69.7 hectares of forest land more and the government has to give speedy clearance. It will involve felling of about a 1,000 trees and some 60 people will be displaced," Pandey said.

Title: 7-year-old boy killed in hit-and-run
content: Pune: A seven-year-old boy was killed in a hit-and-run accident in Nehrunagar in Pimpri on Tuesday evening.The Pimpri police have identified the victim as Shivaji Raosaheb Kakade. The incident occurred when the boy was playing along the side of the road and was hit by a car. His father has filed a complaint against the unknown driver. Senior inspector Shankar Awatade of the Pimpri police told TOI, â€œWe have identified the vehicle with the help of its registration number and summoned the owner to check who was driving the car at the time of the incident.â€ Awatade said that the boy's father works as a casual worker and stays in Nehrunagar in Pimpri. The police said the driver has been booked under sections 304-A (causing death by negligence), 337, 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 279 (rash driving) of the Indian Penal Code.. .Advertisement

Title: 10 pilgrims killed, 31 injured in separate accidents in Punjab's Hoshiarpur
content: JALANDHAR: 10 pilgrims were killed and 31 others injured in two separate accidents while they were on the way to Ravidas shrine in Khuralgarh of district Hoshiarpur's Garhshankar sub-division. Seven pilgrims were killed and 20 were injured, including six seriously, when a truck, also carrying pilgrims, mowed them down on Wednesday late night near a shrine related to guru Ravidas. All were going there in connection with Vaisakhi. In a separate accident in the wee hours on Wednesday, three persons were killed and 11 were injured when a tractor trolley, carrying rations for langar along with a water tanker to the same shrine, fell in a gorge near village Garhi Mansowal.  The deceased were from district Muzaffarnagar of UP and from Yamunanagar of Haryana. The injured included people from these places as well as from Punjab. . .Advertisement "The victims were walking down towards the shrine when the truck coming from behind on the descending road mowed them down. Four of the victim were killed on the spot while there died sometime later. We rushed to Civil Hospital Garhshankar in the night,â€ said Sant Satwinder Singh Heera, president of All India Ad-dharam Mission, which manages the shrine named Charan Chhoh Ganga. He said that truck driver fled from the scene and it remained park in the middle of the road.  The accident took place at a distance of around 500 meters from the shrine. The Ravidasia-Ad-dharmi community has been converging at the shrine, which stands in the memory of Guru Ravidas, on Vaisakhi in the last some years.  As the injured were taken to Civil Hospital Garhshankar, six of them were referred to PGI Chandigarh due to their serious condition and 14 are being treated at Garhshankar hospital.  Senior civil and police officials rushed to the hospital and Punjab assembly deputy speaker Jai Singh Rori, in whose constituency the area falls, also visited the injured and said that state government would give grant of Rs two lakh to the families of the each dead.  This accident on Wednesday night took place at a gap of around 20 hours from another accident related to the same pilgrimage. Pilgrims from village Bodal district Ludhiana, were travelling towards the shrine in a tractor trolley along with rations for the langar and a water tanker was also towed behind the trolley. Heera told that apparently some imbalance took place and the tractor trolley and the tanker fell in a gorge near village Garhi Mansowal 5-6 kms away from the Khuralgarh. The deceased in this accident included a man in sixties, a teenager and youth in twenties.

Title: Forest officer dead, 2 injured as vehicle falls into gorge in Uttarkashi
content: UTTARKASHI: A forest officer was killed and two other forest employees were critically injured, after the vehicle they were traveling in, fell into a gorge on Gangori-Sangamchatti Road in Bhatwari block of Uttarkashi district on Wednesday.  According to sources, ranger Shankaranand Bhatt (35), accompanied by two forest workers, was going for a field visit, when the accident took place. Shankaranand died on the spot.  Soon after getting the information, teams of SDRF and forest department reached the spot and recovered the ranger's body, and injured persons were sent to the district hospital. Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer, said, "The body of Shankaranand has been handed over to his family. The two injured employees, identified as Lal Singh Maher (50) and Harsh Lal (50), have been sent to a higher medical facility in Dehradun for further treatment." Meanwhile, locals have blamed the bad condition of the road for the accident. . .Advertisement

Title: AC repairer killed in hit-&-run
content: MUMBAI: AC repairer Alfaiz Qureshi (22) lost his life in a road accident on Tuesday morning on a flyover in front of IndiaBulls building in Lower Parel. Police booked a person for rash and negligent driving and causing death by negligence. Police suspect the accused dashed Qureshi and fled. Qureshi was traveling to a client's house when the accident occurred at 11.30am on Tuesday. Qureshi's elder borther, Shavez, lodged a complaint with N M Joshi Marg police against the person. Police filed an FIR and are searching for the accused. -

Title: Two killed in road accidents
content: Mangaluru: A two-wheeler rider died after a KSRTC bus hit his vehicle at Jakribettu in Bantwal taluk on Wednesday. The deceased is Sesappa,34. He had sustained injuries on his head and hands. He was taken to a the Bantwal Government Hospital, and later shifted to a private hospital in Thumbe, where doctors declared him dead. In another road accident reported near KPT Circle in Mangaluru city, a 67-year-old pedestrian died on Wednesday. The deceased is Kamalaksha Nayak, a resident of Vyasa Nagara in Kadri. A car from Kuntikana going towards KPT Circle, hit Nayak while he was crossing the national highway near Ganesh Travels Bus Yard. Though he was taken to a private hospital in the city, he was declared brought dead.Man wanted in NDPS case heldMangaluru city police arrested Ameer Suhail,27, on Wednesday, on whom a warrant was issued, in connection with a case under sections of the NDPS Act in 2017. The court has remanded him in judicial custody. TNN

Title: Gurgaon: Crash that killed BPharm student â€˜triggered by a car on wrong sideâ€™
content: GURGAON: A car being driven on the wrong side of the road triggered the fatal accident that ended with a truck crashing into a two-wheeler and killing BPharm student Neha near Vatika Chowk on Wednesday, eyewitnesses have said.They told TOI on Thursday that Neha (22) was riding pillion and her grandfather Chandra Bhan (60) was driving the scooter on Southern Peripheral Road (SPR) when the car suddenly appeared in front of them.Bhan had to slam the brakes, resulting in the scooter skidding. It threw both the riders onto the road, and in the path of the truck that ran over them. Neha, a Gurugram University student, died on the spot, and her grandfather was injured.. .Advertisementâ€œA car was approaching from the wrong side and by the time the elderly man noticed it, it was too late. He pulled the brakes and the truck rammed into their scooter,â€ said Veeru Vatsalya, who has been running a tea stall on the road side for the past four years.The truck driver, booked for death by negligence and rash driving among other sections, fled from the spot. He is absconding.When asked, investigating officer Narender Kumar said cops will look into all angles.â€œWe are yet to talk to the eyewitnesses in detail about the accident. The truck driver is still at large,â€ he said.Last year, traffic police and district officials had listed Vatika Chowk among the 36 accident-prone blackspots, based on an assessment of fatal crashes in areas in Gurgaon. Locals say wrong-side driving is common near the busy intersection that connects SPR and the Sohna Elevated Road (NH-248A).The crash that claimed Nehaâ€™s life took place around 400 meters ahead of the Vatika Square on SPR as the pair was going home to Palra village. Both the riders were wearing helmets.Currently, commuters on SPR going towards Badshapur have to take a U-turn that is around 200 metres from Vatika Chowk. This usually translates to a total distance of around 2.4 kilometres. Many commuters, locals allege, drive on the wrong side of the road to get to the intersection and cut their travel time by 10-15 minutes. Traffic cops are actively working to check those violating traffic rules, IO Narender Kumar added.There have been similar accidents in the city in recent months. On October 30 last year, a speeding car driven on the wrong side of Sohna Road hit a two-wheeler, killing an 18-year-old college student. A 40-year-old domestic help riding a bicycle was killed when a car, allegedly being driven beyond the speed limit and on the wrong side of the road, lost balance and rammed another car before hitting the cyclist near Vatika Chowk on October 22.

Title: 3 killed in road accident in Uttar Pradesh
content: BULANDSHAHR: Three men were killed when their motorcycle collided with a tractor-trolley here, police said on Friday. The accident took place near Pahawati check post under the Arnia police station area, they said. Station House Officer Yagyadatt Sharma said, "Sashi Kapoor, Viresh and Sachin, all residents of Aligarh, were injured when their motorcycle collided with a tractor-trolley." "They were rushed to a hospital were doctors declared them brought dead," he added. The trio were returning from Bulandshahr and were heading towards Aligarh, police said. . .AdvertisementThe bodies have been sent for postmortem examination and their family members have been informed, police added.

Title: 11 killed in two road accidents in Karnataka
content: MADIKERI/TUMAKURU: Eleven people were killed in two road accidents in Karnataka on Friday. Six people, including two women and three children, were killed and two injured grievously after the car they were travelling in collided head-on with a government bus at Sampaje Gate in Kodagu district, police said. They were going from Maddur in Mandya district to Sullia in Dakshina Kannada district when their vehicle was hit by the Karnataka State Road Transport Corporation bus. In the other mishap, five people, including four of a family comprising a couple and two children, were killed and seven injured in a collision involving a private bus and a sports utility vehicle at Sira in Tumakuru district. They were residents of Bengaluru and were on their way to Challakere in Chitradurga where they hail from, the police said. . .AdvertisementThe impact of the accident was so severe that the SUV was badly mangled and bodies were mutilated. It is said that the bus crashed into a road divider before hitting the SUV. Those injured were in the bus and they have been admitted to a nearby hospital.

Title: Techie dies in road accident
content: Visakhapatnam: A 25-year-old software professional was killed after he lost control over his vehicle and skidded off the road while trying to avoid hitting a pedestrian near Balighattam in Anakapalle district. The deceased was KV Naidu, a native of Sivapuram in Narsipatnam and a software professional in Chennai. TNN

Title: One Israeli killed, dozens injured in bus accident in South Korea
content: SEOUL: A bus carrying Israeli tourists overturned on a rural road in central South Korea, local police told AFP Friday, leaving one person dead and dozens more injured. The bus carrying 33 Israeli tourists was traveling from the popular historic city of Gyeongju in the southeast to nearby Chungju when it flipped over late Thursday after taking a sharp turn, police said.  "The steep road caused the bus to slide down as it was changing gears, and it appears the driver lost control and hit the nearby trees, which caused it to flip onto its side," Chungju police told AFP. Images in local media showed a coach with a broken windscreen lying on its side on a tree-lined road, with debris strewn around it.. .Advertisement The Israeli Foreign Ministry in Jerusalem said that, according to South Korean officials, one Israeli woman had been killed, with the rest of the passengers injured, including some seriously. The South Korean driver and tour guide were also injured and taken to hospital, local authorities said. According to investigators, the Israeli passengers on the bus ranged in age from their fifties to seventies and had arrived in the country on April 6. They were due to visit a national park in the northeast and depart the country on Monday after spending a day in an area of Chungju known for its spas.  South Korea's foreign ministry said it would provide all necessary support to the victims following a request from the Israeli embassy in Seoul.

Title: 10 pilgrims on way to Ravidas shrine killed in 2 accidents
content: JALANDHAR: Ten pilgrims were killed in two separate accidents linked to a shrine associated with Guru Ravidas in Garshankar sub-division of Punjabâ€™s Hoshiarpur district since Wednesday night.In the first tragedy, seven devotees were killed and 20 injured, six of them seriously, when a truck carrying pilgrims, mowed them down on Thursday night near a shrine associated with Guru Ravidas at Khuralgarh, in Garhshankar. They were going there for Baisakhi celebrations.In another accident in the wee hours of Wednesday, three persons were killed and 11 injured when a tractor-trolley carrying rations for langar and a water tanker to the same shrine, fell into a gorge near Garhi Mansowal village in Garhshankar subdivision. The victims in the first accident were from Muzaffarnagar district of Uttar Pradesh and Yamunanagar of Haryana. The injured also included pilgrims from Punjab.. .Advertisementâ€œThe victims were walking down towards the shrine when the truck, bearing pilgrims from Khanna, hit them from behind. Four of the victims were killed on the spot while others died sometime later,â€ said Sant Satwinder Singh Heera, president of All India Ad-dharam Mission, which manages the shrine named Charan Chhoh Ganga.He said the truck driver fled the scene leaving the vehicle parked in the middle of the road.The accident took place around 500 metres from the shrine. The Ravidasia-Ad-dharmi community has been converging on the shrine, built in the memory of Guru Ravidas, on Baisakhi over the last couple of years.Initially the injured were taken to Civil Hospital Garhshankar, from where six of them were referred to PGI Chandigarh due to their serious condition.Senior civil and police officials as well as Punjab assembly deputy speaker Jai Singh Rori, in whose constituency the area falls, visited the injured. Rori announced that the CM had announced aid of Rs 2 lakh to the families of each of the dead and Rs 1 lakh to each of the injured.The truck driver was booked for culpable homicide not amounting to murder.

Title: 43-year-old inspector killed after BEST bus hits his motorcycle in Mumbai
content: MUMBAI: A 43-year-old police inspector died on Friday after his motorcycle was dashed from the rear by a speeding BEST bus in Vakola in the western part of Mumbai, an official said.Inspector Pravin Ashok Dinkar of Marine Drive police station was on his way to work from his official quarters in Kole Kalyan in Santacruz East when the accident took place at 9am near New Modern School on Nehru Road, he said. "He sustained serious injuries and died after being admitted in VN Desai Hospital. The process of registering a case is underway. Senior officials, including joint commissioner (Law and Order) Satya Narayan, visited the hospital," PTI quoted the Vakola police station official as saying.With PTI inputs. .Advertisement

Title: Maharashtra: Three, including couple on bike, crushed to death as truck overturns in Virar
content: MUMBAI: Three people, including a couple on a motorbike, were killed when a truck overturned and crushed them on the Narangi Bypass road in Virar (west) on Friday. Preliminary investigations found that the two-wheeler was moving on the wrong side of the road.Arnala police said the couple identified as Ashok Shukla and Rama were on their two-wheeler. The truck carrying construction material was headed to Global City at around 10am. The truck driver, who escaped unhurt, told the police that the two-wheeler was coming in the wrong direction. In order to save the riders, the truck driver changed direction of his vehicle but lost control and the truck overturned. The couple and an unidentified person came underneath the truck.Locals rushed to rescue the couple trapped underneath the truck. Fire brigade personnel reached the spot and with the help of cranes the truck was moved aside and the bodies were taken out.

Title: SUV loses control, kills street vendor
content: Ludhiana: An elderly person died whereas two others suffered injuries after being hit by an SUV near Hadiyan chowk on Kohara road. The deceased was identified as Pritpal Singh (60) of Bhaini Sahib and injured persons included Baljinder Singh of Rajgarh. Pritpal was having his meal near his ice cream stall on the roadside and a person was standing next to him. Sources said the SUV coming from Kohara lost its balance, crossed over to the other side of the road, rammed an Eucalyptus tree and then hit the two persons. Police officials said in the accident, Pritpal died on the spot while an unknown person standing nearby was brought to a government health centre at Machiwara in serious condition. Though the SUV driver escaped from the spot, while another occupant in the car, Baljinder Singh, sustained injuries and was taken to a hospital. Sources said big potholes on the road might have caused the accident. Cops from Koom Kalan police station shifted the body of the deceased to a government hospital for the postmortem. . .Advertisement

Title: 6 killed, 8 injured as SUV collides with tree in UP's Shravasti
content: LUCKNOW: As many as six people were killed and eight others sustained injuries after an SUV carrying them collided with a tree in Shravasti district on Saturday. The accident took place at Sonrai village under Ikauna police station area.The deceased were identified as Heeralal (30), Mukesh Kumar (30), Putti Lal (30), Viru (09), and Harish Kumar (42) and Rama Devi, her age could not be ascertained. She hailed from Balrampur and Harish from Punjab, rest were from Shravasti.Superintendent of police, Shravasti, Prachi Singh, said that all the 14 passengers were inside the SUV and were coming from Ludhiana district of Punjab after attending a funeral.. .Advertisement"Around 6am when they reached Sonrai village, one of the front tyres of the vehicle burst and it lost control before it careened off the road and crashed into a tree. The impact of collision was such that the entire vehicle was reduced to a pulp," said Singh.SP, Singh, further said that Harish was at the wheels and failed to control the vehicle.Soon after the incident, locals informed police and a team went to the spot and with the help of locals and an earthmover took out the victims from the vehicle and and got them admitted to the district hospital. However, doctors declared six dead and referred others to Bahraich.

Title: 11, including 5 kids, killed in two accidents in Karnataka
content: MADIKERI/TUMAKURU: Eleven people were killed in two road accidents in Karnataka on Friday.Six people, including three children, were killed after the car they were travelling in collided head-on with a government bus at Sampaje Gate on the Kodagu-Dakshina Kannada border, police said. The family was heading to Sri Kukke Subrahmanya Temple when their car collided with a KSRTC bus coming from Sullia.Among the eight passengers in the car, one was killed on the spot while five others died on the way to hospital. Two are seriously injured. All the victims are natives of Mandya and Malavalli.In the other mishap, five people, including four of a family, were killed and seven injured in a collision involving a private bus and a sports utility vehicle at Sira in Tumakuru district. They were residents of Bengaluru and were on their way to Challakere in Chitradurga where they hail from. . .AdvertisementDeceased had invited friend to join himThe deceased in the Sampaje Gate accident are Kumara, 35, who was killed on the spot, Shilpa 29, Yashas Gowda, 8, Priyanka, 35, three-year-old Manushree and eight-month-old Nishika.Eight-year-old Biyan Gowda and Manjunath, 40, have been injured in the accident. They were treated in a hospital in Sullia and have been shifted to Mangaluru for further treatment. Kumara from Malavalli invited his friend Manjunath from Mandya to join him on the trip to Kukke temple. Manjunath was accompanied by his wife Priyanka and daughter, Manushree.In the accident on the outskirts of Tumakuru, a private bus jumped the median and collided with their four-wheeler near Nandihalli on the outskirts of Tumakuru, about 70km from Bengaluru. Five persons, including a woman and two children, travelling in the MUV were killed on the spot. The private bus had around 30 passengers. tnn

Title: Woman killed, couple on bike injured as dumper truck overturns in Virar
content: MUMBAI: A pedestrian was killed, while a couple on a motorbike sustained severe injuries after they came underneath a dumper that overturned on Narangi bypass road in Virar on Friday. The Arnala police said pedestrian Sujata Shelar (35) was crushed under the dumper. The couple, Ashok Shukla (54) and Rama (53), was rescued and moved to a hospital in Mumbai. The dumper loaded with construction material was headed to Global City around 10 am. The dumper driver, who sustained minor injuries, told the police that the motorbike was coming from the wrong direction, and in order to save the riders, he changed direction but lost control. Earlier on Thursday, a biker, Vinod Vishwakarma, had a miraculous escape after he landed underneath a cement mixer that had overturned in Kashimira.

Title: Excavator on wrong side fatally hits scooter rider in Mumbai
content: MUMBAI: Even while traffic police were carrying out a special campaign against driving in the wrong direction, a scooterist was killed and his pillion rider was injured at Dahisar after an excavator in the wrong direction rammed into them. Dahisar police registered an FIR against driver Rajesh Yadav. The scooterist, Rajinder Singh, 27, worked as a delivery boy with an online grocery delivery app. He frequently commuted with his friend, Sumit Mhaislage, 27, who was also a delivery boy.Around 4.30am on Friday, Rajinder's brother, Jitendra, received a phone call from Mhaislage. Rajinder and Mhaislage had been heading back home on the scooter when, at 3.45am, an excavator hurtled towards them in the wrong direction and rammed into them on Rawalpada bridge of Western Express Highway (WEH). Both were thrown off the scooter. Rajinder suffered serious chest injuries and broke his arm. Mhaislage also suffered minor chest injuries. Bystanders rushed Rajinder and Mhaislage to civic-run Babasaheb Ambedkar Hospital in Kandivli, where Rajinder succumbed to his injuries. Jitendra reached the hospital after receiving Mhaislage's call. They went to the location where the crash had occurred and found that work was on but the road had not been shut for traffic and an excavator was carrying sand in the wrong direction. Jitendra approached Dahisar police.Between April 6-13, traffic police took action against 11,134 motorists for driving in the wrong direction.. .Advertisement

Title: Techie dies as cab rams his scooter in Bengaluru
content: BENGALURU: In a hit-and-run case, a 46-year-old software engineer riding back home from work was killed after a cab rammed into his two-wheeler in Bellandur in the early hours of Friday. The deceased is Amit Bhargav, a resident of Kasavanahalli and from Haryana. On Friday, Bellandur traffic police traced the car and arrested the cabbie, Basavaraj. The accident occurred around 12.30am on Kasavanahalli main road and the car, which rear-ended Bhargavâ€™s gearless scooter, sped away. Bhargav is survived by his wife and two minor daughters.

Title: Woman on way to see brother in hospital dies in Ghaziabad crash
content: GHAZIABAD: A 36-year-old woman died and her 59-year-old father suffered grievous injuries when the scooter they were riding was hit by a car in Raj Nagar area on Friday.Police said Maninder Kaur and her father Balwinder were headed to a hospital with food for her brother when the accident occurred around noon near the railway station. Abhishek Srivastava, ACP (Kavi Nagar), said the two had taken a turn from the Ring junction when the car hit them from behind."Passersby called police and the two were taken to a private hospital. Maninder was referred to GTB Hospital in Delhi when her condition deteriorated. She died there during treatment. Balwinder is critical too," he added.. .AdvertisementKaramjeet Singh, the woman's brother, said his sister was coming to meet him at the hospital, where he was admitted with high fever. "I got news about the accident from police. Police asked our family to visit the hospital as soon as possible. We learnt that my sister is no more while my father is battling for life," he added.Karamjeet said his sister was separated from her husband for the past five years and stayed in Sihani gate with her daughter (5). "The accident has shocked us. My sister was taking care of the entire family," he added.The car fled the spot after the accident, police said. "Everything happened so fast that no one could note down the number. We have formed two teams to nab the accused driver. One police team is checking CCTV footage within a 500-metre radius of the accident spot. We are waiting for a complaint to register an FIR in the case," Srivastava said. According to NCRB data, hit-and-run cases in Ghaziabad saw a massive rise in a year - from 61 in 2020 to 197 in 2021.The city saw 880 accidents last year. A total of 363 people died and 638 were injured in these accidents. In 2021, there were 824 accidents. On January 29, a 24-year-old MBA graduate died and her elder sister was grievously injured after their scooter, being driven on the wrong side of the road, was hit by a car in Raj Nagar Extension. She was killed just 19 days before her wedding. The car driver was arrested and booked for death by negligence and rash driving, among others.On February 19, a 26-year-old Aman Kumar died after his scooter was hit by an unidentified vehicle near the Vaishali metro station.

Title: Cidco resident killed in accident; wife & son injured
content: Nashik: A 40-year-old died in an accident, while his wife and his son escaped with minor injuries after they were hit by a speeding truck in the Cidco area on Friday morning.The deceased person has been identified as Shashikant Avsarkar, a resident of Vijay Nagar in Cidco.The police said Avsarkar, his 38-year-old wife Swapnali, and son 9-year-old Swaroop were travelling on a bike towards the Burkule Hall at around 5.45pm on Friday when a speeding truck coming from the opposite direction knocked them down.Shashikant suffered serious head injuries and died on the spot due to heavy bleeding. . .AdvertisementMeanwhile, his wife and son escaped with minor injuries.Soon after the accident, the police arrived at the scene and arrested the truck driver, Harshal Gore (21), also a resident of Cidco. The police said that on a complaint, they have registered an FIR for the offence punishable under sections 304-A (causing death due to negligence), 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), and other relevant sections of the IPC.The police said that deceased worked for a private company.

Title: Former MLA Patil Neeraja Reddy dies in car crash in Andhra Pradesh
content: TIRUPATI: Former Alur MLA Patil Neeraja Reddy met with a road accident near Itikyala village in Gadyal Jogulamba district and died while undergoing treatment at Kurnool on Sunday. According to Kodandapuram police, 52-year-old Neeraja Reddy was travelling from Hyderabad to Kurnool. The back tyre of her speeding car burst all of a sudden, the driver of the vehicle lost control resulting into the overspeeding SUV turning turtle and crasheing into the farmlands abutting the national highway 44.Though Neeraja Reddy was rushed to a private hospital at Kurnool immediately, she breathed her last at the hospital on Sunday.Neeraja Reddy, a native of Thernakal village in Devanakonda mandal, got elected as an MLA on a Congress party ticket from Alur in the combined state of Andhra Pradesh in the 2009 general elections.. .AdvertisementShe joined the YSRCP for a brief while in 2019 but later joined the BJP and was appointed as the state executive member.Her husband Patil Seshi Reddy had also served as Pathikonda MLA but was killed in a faction attack in 1996.

Title: Teacher killed in road accident
content: Raipur: A school teacher riding a bike was killed on the spot, and three persons critically injure after the two-wheeler collided with a speeding car in Farasgaon of Kondagaon district on Sunday morning.Police have identified the deceased as Pramod Kumar Minj, a resident Themgaon village of Makadi block and a school teacher at a middle school. Accoding to the police, Pramod was heading towards Badedongar village area when he was allegedly hit by a speeding car. Another bike coming from behind rammed into car. The car had two people in it while one person was riding the bike coming from behind, police added.Police rushed to the spot and sent Pramodâ€™s body for autopsy. The injured were sent for primary treatment to the nearby community health centre. The body will handed over to the family after post-mortem, police said, adding that a case has been registered at the Farasgaon police station area. . .Advertisement

Title: Youth killed in accident
content: Kochi: A youth was killed in a bike accident on Refinery Road, Tripunithura, around 12.30am on Sunday.The deceased has been identified as Vimal (26), son of Venugopal of Irumpanam. Sources said that the two-wheeler in which Vimal was riding somehow met with an accident and the cause of the accident was yet to be known.Though he was rushed to a private hospital, he succumbed to his injuries. Hill Palace police registered a case and launched an investigation.

Title: 9, including 2 Tamil Nadu natives, killed in road accidents
content: TIRUPATI: Nine persons were killed in road accidents that took place in different districts. Four persons died in an accident around midnight on Saturday along the Chittoor-Kadapa highway near Kothapalli cross in Ramapuam mandal of Annamayya district. Two cars from opposite directions collided head-on leading to the deaths of Penamala Lakshmamma (65), P Narasayya (41), Chinnakka (60) and driver Raja Reddy (35). Three motorists were killed near Chennur in Kadapa district on Sunday. The deceased are yet to be identified. Two Tamil Nadu motorists were killed in another road accident at Varadaiahpalem mandal in Tirupati district on Sunday. The deceased were identified as Yuvaraj (21) and Viswanath (18).

Title: 3 of a family killed in road accident, 2 injured in Kerala
content: THRISSUR: An elderly couple and their 11-year-old granddaughter were killed when their car collided head-on with a KSRTC bus at Thalikulam near Triprayar on Sunday morning. Two others, who are injured, have been hospitalized.Police gave the names of the deceased as Puthenpurayil Padmanabhan (81) of Paravur Thattanpadi in Ernakulam, his wife Parukutty (79) and their granddaughter Abhirami. While Padmanabhan and Parukutty died on the spot, Abhirami succumbed to her injuries in the hospital.Abhirami's parents, Shaju (49) and Sreeja (44), who were injured in the accident, are undergoing treatment at a private hospital in Thrissur. The car was heading to Guruvayur Temple from Paravoor while the bus was plying from Guruvayur to Ernakulam. Meanwhile, a 40-year-old man, who tried to lift a gold chain from the ill-fated car, was rounded up by people engaged in rescue operations. The accused, Babu Pattadath of Kanajani Ambalakkad, was passing by in a scooter when he noticed the accident. . .AdvertisementThe chain had slipped down from Parukutty's neck. He mixed with those engaged in rescue operations, and lifted the gold chain from the car, and kept it concealed in his dress. But rescue workers traced the chain from his dress and handed him over to the police.

Title: Dumper runs over 25-year-old biker, 1 held
content: Mumbai: A 25-year-old biker died in a road accident in Mankhurd. The biker was identified as Vipul Bhoir. Police said he was killed when a dumper rammed into his bike and ran him at Mankhurd. People who witnessed the accident rushed him to Rajadwadi hospital where he was declared dead before admission. Mankhurd police have arrested the dumper driver. tnn

Title: Father-son duo killed, 4 others injured in Lakhimpur Kheri road accident
content: PILIBHIT: Two persons including a 30-year-old man and his six-year-old son were killed, while four others of the family were injured in a road accident on the Dhakerwa-Nighasan state highway in Lakhimpur Kheri district on Sunday evening, said, Nighasan police station SHO Prabhatesh Shrivastav.According to the police, all six victims were residents of Belrayan village, and were travelling on a bike when it rammed into a sugarcane-laden trolley parked at the roadside. Upon receiving the information, police rushed to the spot and shifted all six victims to the local community health centre.The father-son duo identified as Nasir Ahmad and his son Jabir, were declared dead on arrival. Meanwhile, Nasir's wife Julekha, and their two sons and daughter identified as Sameer, Amir, and Khusbu respectively sustained multiple injuries in the incident. They were referred to the district hospital for treatment after preliminary medical aid.The SHO said, "The two bodies have been sent to the post-mortem house for autopsy. So far, the police were not presented with any complaint in the matter." . .Advertisement

Title: Three killed as van ploughs into pedestrians
content: Kochi: In a tragic incident, a van carrying parcels ploughed into a group of pedestrians on Monday morning, killing three including a toddler. The incident happened at Koovelipady in Madakkathanam.The deceased have been identified as Prajesh, 36, of Manjalloor village, his daughter one-and-a-half-year-old Alana and neighbour Mary, 60. Police said that Prajesh and Alana were taken to a private hospital at Thodupuzha while Mary was rushed to Muvattupuzha General Hospital. However, they succumbed to their injuries.The speeding goods van had hit the victims walking by the road along the Thodupuzha-Muvattupuzha state highway. Police said that the accident happened at 7.45am.Vazhakulam police arrested the driver of the van, Eldho, 22, and registered an FIR against him for reckless driving. Police registered an FIR under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way) and 304-A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC. TNN. .Advertisement

Title: 68-year-old biker killed in hit-and-run in Maharashtra
content: PUNE: A 68-year-old man, who was riding a two-wheeler, was killed in a hit-and-run accident along the Pune-Mumbai highway in Chinchwad on Saturday.According to police, the deceased, Pradip Kadam, was travelling towards his home in Pimpri. An autorickshaw coming from the opposite direction dashed into his motorcycle near a departmental store in Chinchwad. â€œKadam fell on the road due to the impact and sustained injuries,â€ an officer from Chinchwad police station said.He added that the locals rushed Kadam to a hospital where he was pronounced dead by the doctors. â€œWe are searching for the autorickshaw driver who fled from the spot after the accident,â€ the officer said.

Title: Five die as hoarding falls on tyre shop near eway during rainstorm in Maharashtra
content: PUNE: Five construction workers, including four women, were killed and three others were injured as a huge advertising hoarding made up of iron rods and bars came crashing down because of strong winds on a tyre repair shop at Mukai Chowk, near Kiwale, around 5.15pm on Monday.Amid thunderstorm and rain, the workers had taken shelter under the tin shed of the shop on the service lane of the Pune-Mumbai corridor of Katraj-Dehu Road bypass, barely 500m from the start of the Pune-Mumbai Expressway. Ravet police identified the deceased as Anita Roy (45), Shobha Tak (50), Varsha Kedare (40) and Bharati Manchal (30) of Dehu Road, and Ram Aatmaj (20) of Uttar Pradesh. The injured people are Vishal Yadav (20) of Uttar Pradesh, and Rehmad Mohammed Ansari (21) and Rinki Dilip Roy (45) of Dehu Road. They were taken to a nearby private hospital.PCMC commissioner Shekhar Singh said according to preliminary information, the hoarding was unauthorised. Traffic along the bypas was disrupted for nearly an hour as fire tenders, rescue vehicles, ambulances and police reached the spot to rescue the trapped workers. It took an hour for the traffic police to clear the road.. .AdvertisementPimpri Chinchwad's municipal commissioner Shekhar Singh said the civic authorities were confirming details related to the crashed hoarding. "According to our preliminary information, this was one of the unauthorised hoardings against which civic action was stayed by the high court. We will release more details later," he said, adding that their immediate priority was the search and rescue operations and contacting the relatives of the deceased and injured.The civic body's disaster management cell head, Omprakash Bahiwal, said the fire brigade got the call about the accident around 5.40pm and accordingly, the rescue and search operation started. He said, "The hoarding was huge. Its size could be up to 80ft x 160ft." Senior inspector Shivaji Gaware of the Ravet police said, "The group of workers executed their day's work at a nearby construction site and were returning to their rooms when strong wind and rain started in the area. They took shelter in the tyre repair shop when the hoarding crashed on the tin shed. It is an open plot leading to a roadside eatery. There were two hoardings at the spot and one of them collapsed. It was made up of iron rods and bars."The fire brigade officials used cutters to break the iron rods of the hoarding structure. An earth mover was pressed into the service to execute the rescue and relief work.Vishal Yadav, one of the injured victims recuperating in a private hospital at Ravet, told TOI, "I took shelter in the tyre repair shop with my colleagues when it started raining heavily. There were many people with me. Suddenly, something heavy fell on us and it was all dark after that. I have suffered head injuries. I do not know what happened to my colleagues."An IMD official said, "A cumulonimbus cloud cell had formed over Talegaon-Dabhade, parts of Pimpri Chinchwad and Pirangut, among other areas on Monday. This is a thundercloud and usually associated with rain, lightning and gusty winds. Such winds can gust at a speed of 30-40kmph. This wind speed can cause trees/tree branches to snap and fall, among other such instances."(With inputs from Neha Madaan & Piyush Bhusari)

Title: 4 killed in road accident near Kadaba in DK
content: Mangaluru: Four persons, including a four-year-old child, were killed and 20 suffered injuries in an accident between a car and a multi-utility vehicle (MUV) at Nettana of Bilinele near Kadaba in Dakshina Kannada around 2.30pm on Tuesday.The deceased are Ullas, 28, Ganesh, 26, Tanvik, 4, who were travelling in a car, and Nethravati, 28, who was in the MUV. The car occupants are from Tagare in Belur taluk of Hassan, while those in the MUV are from Chandanahalli in Belur taluk.Some of the injured are Meghana, Nishchal, Kusuma, Varsha, Sangeetha, Tejas, Susheela, Anand, Ganavi, Deepa, Hamsani, Shivani, Shobha, Raghu, Sanju, Rajesh and Renuka.Police said that more than 20 persons were travelling in total in both vehicles. It is learnt that those who were in the car were on their way to Subrahmanya from Dharmasthala and those in the MUV were returning from Subrahmanya.. .Advertisement

Title: 2 jawans among 5 killed in Ramgarh accidents
content: Ramgarh: Five people, including two Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) personnel, were killed in two road accidents in Ramgarh in the last 24 hours. In the first incident, two CISF jawans posted at Patratu Vidyut Utpadan Nigam Limited (PVUNL) were run over by an unidentified vehicle on road 4 on Monday.Patratu SDPO Birendra Kumar Chaudhary said, â€œThe CISF constables identified as Dharm Pal (30) from Delhi and Arvind M from Kerala, died on the spot. They were on way to an ATM kiosk when the incident occurred,â€ he said.In the other incident, three passengers, including a woman died, and 10 others were injured when the Ramgarh-bound bus they were travelling in rammed a trailer parked along NH-33 near Murpa village under Kuju police station area. . .AdvertisementThe deceased were Pramod Kumar Srivastva (47), from Saunda-D in Patratu block of Ramgarh, Md Salon (42) of Sarubera under Mandu block and Savita Devi (52) of Jhumra in Hazaribag.

Title: In Bareilly, 4-month-old girl, grandmother killed after truck hits bike, 1 injured
content: PILIBHIT: Two persons including a four-month-old girl and her grandmother were killed, while one other was injured in a road accident on the Bareilly-Haridwar NH 74 on Tuesday, said Jahanabad police station SHO Ashok Pal.According to police, the victims were on their way to a doctor's appointment, when the bike they were travelling on was mowed down by a speeding sugarcane-laden truck. While the infant died on the spot, her uncle Anas Hussain (19), and grandmother Sugra Begum (50) were severely injured. They were rushed to the district hospital for treatment, where Begum succumbed to her injuries. "The victims were residents of Chandoi village in Pilibhit district. Hussain was riding the bike and the girl was seated in her grandmother's lap behind him. During the collision, Begum fell on the road along with the girl and got crushed under the truck's wheels. The driver fled the spot, but police seized the truck and registered an FIR against him," SHO said. . .Advertisement

Title: 5 die in 2 accidents in Bikaner, Churu dists
content: Jaipur: Five persons were killed and four others injured in two separate accidents in Bikaner and Churu districts on Monday evening. Police said two bikes collided near Sridungargarh in Bikaner district on Monday evening in which three youths were killed. â€œNone of them was wearing helmets and sustained grave head injuries,â€ said police. The deceased were identified as Narayan Singh, Motiram and Takhat Singh. â€œNarayan Singh died on the spot while Motiram and Takhat Singh succumbed on the way to a hospital,â€ the officer added. One person identified as Dungarram is critical and is undergoing treatment at trauma centre of a government hospital. The matter came to light at 10.30pm on Monday. â€œAll of them were profusely bleeding and with the help of ambulances, they were taken to a nearby hospital,â€ the officer added. . .AdvertisementIn another accident, two bus passengers from Jhunjhunu to Churu were killed in a head-on collision with a fire tender near the sleeper factory on Bisau road in Churu. â€œTwo persons died and four were injured,â€ said an officer. TNN

Title: DTC bus crushes man in Delhi's RK Puram, driver held
content: NEW DELHI: A DTC bus hit a 25-year-old man killing him in southwest Delhi's RK Puram on Tuesday.Police said he was crossing the road to get to the bus stop.The deceased, Satyam Sharma, lived at a rented accommodation in RK Puram Sector 5 and worked as a driver for a company in Bhikaji Cama Place.At 8am the police got a PCR call about the accident. The team that visited the accident spot on Tamil Sangam Marg found Sharma's body lying close to the DTC bus. Blood was coming out from Sharma's head.. .AdvertisementA boy who runs a tea stall near the accident spot said: "I heard that he was coming from the other side of the road. When he was crossing from a gap between an electric pole and a steel fence at a divider, the accident happened."Police said he was crushed under the wheels. The driver fled the spot but was arrested in RK Puram. The bus runs between RK Puram and Wazirpur. The driver was identified as Sanjeev Kumar of Haryana. An officer said they were probing if the victim was getting late for office and that's why was crossing the road to get to the bus stop.Kumar's family lives in Ambedkar Nagar in UP. They have been informed about the incident.A foot overbridge is located barely 100-150 metres from the spot but few people use it.According to Delhi Police data, DTC buses were involved in 37 fatal crashes that killed 39 people in 2022.In the last four years (2019-2022), cluster and DTC buses were involved in 93 fatal accidents each. Last year, DTC was involved in 37 such cases and cluster buses in 21.

Title: 35-year-old woman killed in road accident
content: Raipur: A 35-year-old woman was killed in an accident when her two wheeler collided with a trailer in Bilaspur.According to police, the deceased, Rita Sidar, 35, was a private school teacher. The incident took place around 7.30 am, when she going to work. A stray dog dashed in front of her two-wheeler and she lost control over her vehicle. She swerved into the wrong side of the road, when a speeding trailer hit her vehicle. The passerby notified the police and the woman was rushed to a nearby hospital, where she was declared brought dead.Man dies after truck hits him in BilaspurAn overspeeding truck killed a 40-year-old man when it hit the deceased, Govardhan Suyavanshi, a resident of Semartaal village of Bilaspur district. The accident took place around 12.30 pm and the witnesses reported the incident to police. Locals gathered around the body and protested against the frequent road accidents. The locals demanded compensation for the victimsâ€™ family and strict action against the truck driver. . .AdvertisementLater the crowd was dispersed. The kin of the deceased received Rs 45,000 as financial assistance.A case was registered in Koni police station and further investigations are on.

Title: 2 killed after bike hits truck
content: Kolkata: Two persons died after their bike hit a truck on Kidderpore Road near Ghora Crossing around 8.30pm on Tuesday. Cops said the victims, biker Subhendu Das (48), a resident of Belgachhia Road near Milk Colony, and his pillion rider, Subrato Roy (48), a resident of Mahajati Nagar in Birati â€” who worked in different consumer goods companies â€” came under the wheels of the truck. The accident happened when the victims were heading towards Red Road from Taratala. Both of them were wearing helmets. The two were rushed to SSKM Hospital where they were declared dead. The driver, Rameshwar Roy (52), who hails from Biharâ€™s Muzaffarpur and resides in Bhowanipore, stopped the truck after the accident. He was arrested and a case of death due to rash and negligent driving was registered against him at the Hastings police station. TNN

Title: 3 killed as car hits divider & overturns
content: Indore: Three youths out for late night tea and snacks were killed and two seriously injured after the driver of the car lost control and the vehicle hit a divider before overturning on Dewas road.Madhav Nagar police station in-charge Manish Lodha said that the accident took place at Mahananda Nagar square at around 1:30 am. A group of friends was on its way to Topkhana area from Adarsh Nagar Nagjhiri when it met with an accident. After hitting the divider, the four-wheeler overturned on the spot trapping everyone inside. Some locals and passers-by rushed to the spot and took out the occupants from the car.While two of them died on the spot, one succumbed at hospital. The other two were admitted for treatment. The deceased were identified as Adnan, 19, Afsan, 17, and Kaif Mansoori. Those injured were Alfez Khan, 18, and Rehan, 15. Family members said that Adnan and Afsan were class 12 students and Kaif a pharmacy student.The police said that bodies of the youths were handed over to their families after post mortem examination. Inspector Lodha said that they were yet to record statements of the injured.. .Advertisement

Title: 19-year-old student of PAU killed in truck-scooter crash
content: Ludhiana: A 19-year-old student of PAU died after a speeding truck rammed her scooter in Parkash Nagar of Jawaddi Kalan on Tuesday evening. She was returning home from Punjab Agricultural University (PAU) when the accident occurred.The victim, Ravneet Kaur, was a student of BTech (first year) and a resident of Gill road near ITI. The police have lodged an FIR against the truck driver, who is at large. Ravneetâ€™s father Pushpinder Singh, a trader, said his daughter commuted on a scooter. On Tuesday evening she was returning home from the university when a speeding truck hit her scooter near Jawaddi Kalan. Some passersby rushed her to a nearby private hospital where doctors declared her dead.Besides some commuters, Pushpinder too informed by the police about the accident. ASI Surjeet Singh from Dugri police station said when the truck hit the girlâ€™s scooter, some passersby raised an alarm after which the driver stopped the truck. However, the driver fled leaving the vehicle at the spot. Police have identified the accused as Kuldeep Singh of Baba Jeevan Singh Nagar of Ludhiana. Raids are being conducted for his arrest. A case under Sections 279 (Rash driving), 304-A (causing death by negligence) and 427 (mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the IPC has been registered against him.. .AdvertisementPAU officials claimed Ravneet was an excellent and disciplined student. She was a NCC cadet, who participated in the Republic Day parade.

Title: 4 killed in separate road accidents
content: BAREILLY: Three persons were killed and another injured after a truck collided with a pickup van on Moradabad-Farrukhabad highway in UP's Budaun on Thursday morning, police said. All three persons travelling in the pickup van died on the spot, police said, adding the injured was admitted to a district hospital. The straw-laden truck collided head-on with the van near a temple in Faizganj Behtar area of the district, said police. The victims have been identfied as Haji Imran (50), a cattle dealer from Moradabad, his sons Izhaar (20) and Izhaan (22), and the driver Gulnawaz Khan (35). Out of them, Izhaar has survived with critical injuries, said SHO Faizganj Behtar Siddhant Sharma. He said, "The bodies have been sent for autopsy. The truck has been seized but the driver has managed to flee. A case has been registered in the matter." In another accident, three bike-borne men were hit by a speeding bus in Alapur area. They were rushed to hospital where one of them-Mohammed Raiul succumbed to his injuries while his friends-Abdul Kalaam (18) and Mohd Shanu (18) were referred to a private hospital in Bareilly,, said police. Kanwardeep Singh

Title: 45-yr-old killed in road accident
content: Mohali: A 45-year-old woman was killed after a Mahindra pick-up hit her while she was standing on the roadside near her shop at Siswan T-point in Mullanpur on Wednesday night. The victim is Malika of Siswan, Mullanpur. The driver is absconding.

Title: 2 kids hit by lorry, killed
content: Hyderabad: Two children were killed while crossing a road in Sangareddy on Thursday after a lorry hit them. Local police said that the incident took place at Nandikandi village. The two boys -- Mohammed Riyan, 14, and Mohammed Shakeel, 6 -- were killed on the spot whereas Shakeelâ€™s father Ferooz escaped with injuries. Police said that the three were crossing the road when the accident took place. Following the accident, the locals staged protest in support of the victimsâ€™ relatives. A case was registered against the lorry driver under section 304a (negligence causing death) of IPC. tnn

Title: Rain fury: Two dead, trees uprooted, vehicles damaged
content: Nagpur: Two persons killed during the fierce thunderstorm and strong winds that hit the city on Thursday evening, which also left dozens of trees uprooted, damaging several vehicles and a kuccha house. Several areas also witnessed power outages for a long time. Ajni railway station too remained in darkness for over two hours.â€œNMC fire bridge personnel and city policemen responded to scores of rescue calls as trees fell on vehicles, and a wall collapsed at Byramji Town, near Gondwana Square, while NMCâ€™s teams were busy clearing roads,â€ NMC additional municipal commissioner Ram Joshi told TOI.TOI visited the accident site and found that three trees fell inside JP Heights Colony situated along Nagpur-New Delhi railway line, resulting in the collapse of the compound wall of the society. One of the trees fell on the kuccha house, trapping a youth and his mother of a four-member family.â€œAfter being alerted by locals, firefighting personnel and city police rushed to start rescue operations,â€ said Mangalwari zone assistant municipal commissioner Vijay Humne, who too was camping there to monitor the rescue operation. SDRF team too joined the rescue operation, said deputy chief fire officer B P Chandankhede. Locals said recently the Central Railway carried out an anti-encroachment drive alongside the railway tracks. However, one of the encroachers came back and started living in a kuccha house.The rescued man was rushed to Indira Gandhi Medical College and Hospital, where doctors declared him dead. The man identified as Aman Yadav had sustained serious head injuries, while his mother Jyoti Yadav too remained trapped for a couple of hours. However, she was rescued in an unconscious state. Jyoti too died in the wall collapse incident, said officers of Sadar police later. Most of the calls for uprooted trees were received from south-west and west assembly constituencies, said fire officials. Several vehicles were damaged by the falling trees across the city. With large trees keeling over during the squall, some roads in Laxmi Nagar, near Metro Bhavan, around Tatya Tope Udyan behind Tatya Tope Hall, Atrey Layout and Ring Road had to be temporarily closed off, and Nagpur city saw traffic jams that lasted till late night. Near Tatya Tope Udyan, a big branch of a tree fell on an electricity cable, bringing down cables and blocking the road. Functioning of street lights and traffic signals too remained affected due to power outage.

Title: 7 killed, 40 hurt after bus rams truck in Ayodhya
content: AYODHYA: In a horrific road accident in Ayodhya, at least 7 persons were killed on the spot while over 40 passengers were injured, many of them seriously, when the bus they were travelling in collided with a truck on Gorakhpur national highway. The accident occurred late on Friday evening.The impact of the collision was so hard that the truck coming from the opposite direction turned turtle and the bus got buried under it.Soon after the accident, the district administration swung into action and began a rescue operation. Talking to TOI, Ayodhya district magistrate Nitish Kumar said, â€œMore than a dozen ambulances have been deployed. We are taking out those stuck inside the vehicle after the crash.The injured are being taken to the district hospital and medical college.â€ Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath expressed grief over the loss of lives in the accident, his office said in a tweet in Hindi. The chief minister also directed the district administration to take the injured to the hospital immediately and provide them with proper treatment. Ayodhya chief medical officer Dr Ajay Raja said, â€œUntil now, seven persons have died and more than 40 persons are injured in the road accident. The rescue op

Title: Police urge caution as woman dies in accident in Coimbatore
content: COIMBATORE: Two days after a 25-year-old woman was killed when her shawl got entangled in the rear wheel of the bike she was riding pillion, the city traffic police on Friday requested women to be cautious while travelling on two-wheelers.S Afrin of Kurichi in the city, an MBA student, was returning home with her friend S Sylesh, 25, after attending a farewell party in the college at Sulur on Wednesday night. When they reached Ravathur Junction on L&T Bypass, Afrin's dupatta got caught in the rear wheel of the motorcycle. She fell on the road and was dragged for a few metres. Though Afrin was rushed to a private hospital near Singanallur, she died without responding to the treatment.Many youngsters who own motorcycles, fail to fix sari guard on them, endangering lives of women who travel with them, said N Mathivanan, deputy commissioner of police. "Women should be alert while travelling on two-wheelers and prevent shawl and sari from getting stuck in the wheel. You may have to pay a heavy price for negligence," he said.The officer also urged car owners to be alert and take steps to curb freak accidents. "Last Saturday, 43-year-old A Sreepan of Gandhi Nagar, a passenger autorickshaw driver, was killed after a car rammed his auto at Athipalayam Pirivu near Ganapathy. Car driver S Charles Anthony, 52, of Sundaram Garden at Ganapathy, tried to apply the brakes, but his footwear got stuck in the accelerator. Sreepan sustained internal bleeding and passed away in a private hospital.". .AdvertisementMotorists should act responsibly not only to save their life, but also to save the lives of others on the street, Mathivanan added.

Title: Two die in separate accidents in Trichy
content: TRICHY: Two people died in two different road accidents in Trichy on Thursday. A Roobi, 38, from housing board at Nagamangalam who was working as a sanitary worker at Indira Ganesan college of engineering at Manikandam, was killed in an accident inside the campus. On Thursday afternoon, when she was walking on the road inside the campus, a speeding car dashed her from behind. She was tossed into the air before landing on the road and sustained injuries on her head. She was rushed to Mahatma Gandhi memorial government hospital (MGMGH) in Trichy where the doctors declared her dead. . .AdvertisementBased on a complaint from her husband D Arockiyam, 45, Manikandam police took up the investigation and identified the car driver as M Seenivasan, 42, of Vannarapillai street near Gandhi market in Trichy. He was booked for rash and negligent driving causing death. In another incident, a 52-year-old man was hit by a car and killed on the spot near Mannachanallur.The deceased, K Murugan of Thillampatti was riding his bicycle near rice mill at Poonampalayam on Trichy â€“ Thuraiyur road on Thursday morning. He suffered injuries on his head and died on the spot when a car which came in the same direction hit him. His body was taken to government hospital in Srirangam for post-mortem. The Mannachanallur police investigated the case and caught the car driver V Rajendran, 54, of Ganapathy Nagar in Thiruvanaikovil in Trichy and booked him for rash and negligent driving causing death.

Title: 5 die as truck falls into 30-ft gorge in Mizoram
content: AIZAWL: Five persons, all from the Chakma community, were killed as the pickup truck they were travelling in ran off the road and fell into a gorge at West Phaileng village in Mamit district on the Mizoram-Tripura-Bangladesh border around 11am on Friday.The vehicle bound for Marpara village was carrying 24 people, which rolled down a 30-metre deep gorge, the police said."They were on their way back from Rajiv Nagar where they had gone to attend celebration of Biju, a Chakma festival," the police added.The victims include four women - Bonomota, Ayushi Chakma, Siki Chakma, Kajola. . .AdvertisementThe man has been identified as Dev Kumar Chakma.

Title: Woman, senior citizen among 4 killed in separate road accidents
content: Nagpur: A 22-year-old woman, Puja Pathe, was killed in an accident on the road between Khapri Square and Panjri village on Saturday morning. Puja was heading towards Mihan when the accident took place. Mini trucker Suresh Kolte was booked by Beltarodi police.In another incident, Kewju Patil, 85, was killed in a hit-and-run case at Hingna market on Friday. Hingna police have registered an offence against an unidentified motorist.Dharmesh Vishwakarma, 26, was killed while his pillion rider Rohit Nimbulkar was injured after they were hit by a truck on the bridge in front of a cancer hospital near Jamtha on Friday. Hinga police have filed a case.On Friday, an unidentified person was hit by a 35-year-old biker Sumeet Dehankar on Amravati Road. The person was declared dead at a private hospital on Saturday. Wadi police have booked Dehankar. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Road accident in Jammu leaves Himachal Pradesh woman pilgrim dead, four injured
content: JAMMU: A woman pilgrim from Himachal Pradesh was killed while four others suffered injuries when their car collided with another vehicle on Katra-Jammu road near Domail in Jammu district.According to the police, while the pilgrims were returning home after offering prayers at the sacred shrine, the car collided head-on with a traffic police recovery vehicle near the Muri area of the Katra-Domel road."In the mishap, a woman, identified as Shivani (21), a resident of Himachal Pradesh died while four others â€” Vivek Kapoor (43), Muktrika Kapoor, Monika Kapoor and Rishita Kapoor (11) â€” all residents of Noorpur in Himachal Pradesh and are from one family sustained injuries," Police said," Police said."The injured were rushed to Community Health Centre (CHC) in Katra from where they were shifted to Government Medical College and Hospital (GMC&H), Jammuâ€.. .Advertisement

Title: Run over by truck, cop dies in Godhra
content: Vadodara: An assistant sub-inspector (ASI) attached to Panchmahal police died in a road accident in Godhra town on Saturday morning. He was run over by a truck near the B V Gandhi Petrol Pump on the Godhra-Dahod road.The incident took place around the afternoon when ASI Prabhatsinh Pagi was on his way to the superintendent of police office. He was posted with the reader branch of the office, sources said.Pagi came under the rear wheel of the left side of the truck and received severe injuries. He died on the spot. His body was sent for an autopsy and handed over to the family later. The body was taken to his native village Chhavad in the Godhra taluka. Sources said that the last rites will be performed on Sunday.The Godhra A Division police station registered an offence regarding the incident. Pagi had been promoted recently and was to retire in 2025, sources said. This is the second policemen in the region to have died in road accident this month. Recently, constable Lalbha Rathod from Gotri police station in Vadodara was killed in an accident involving a tipper. tnn . .Advertisement

Title: Three Tirunelveli natives killed as bus falls into gorge
content: Idukki: Three persons, including an eight-year-old boy, lost their lives as the mini bus in which they were travelling from Tirunelveli in Tamil Nadu to Munnar fell into a gorge at Erachilpara near Poopara here on Saturday evening.Most of the 20 occupants of the vehicle, who were on their way to attend a wedding in Munnar, suffered injuries in the incident. The accident took place at a bend in the road around 7pm.The deceased have been identified as C Perumal, 59, Valliyamma, 70 and Suseendran, 8. It is learnt from Santhanpara police that two of the injured persons are in a very critical condition.

Title: 4 dead, 22 injured as truck rams into bus on new Katraj bypass in Pune
content: PUNE: Four people, including two women, were killed and 22 others injured after a multi-axle truck, carrying sugarcane from Kolhapur to Mumbai, hit a private luxury bus from behind near the Swami Narayan Temple on the killer stretch between the Katraj viaduct and the Navale bridge around 2.15am on Sunday. The injured were taken to private hospitals at Erandwane and Dhankawadi in Pune city. The bus, belonging to a private tours and travel operator, was going to Mumbai from Satara. The accident-prone stretch, which is notorious for a downward gradient starting from the Katraj viaduct (also known as Dari Pul) up to the Navale bridge, is part of the new Katraj bypass that links Pune city with the National Highway to Bengaluru at one end and to Mumbai at the other end. The same has witnessed multiple accidents, including pile ups of vehicles and deaths, over the last few years and has posed a major challenge to the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) and other agencies in terms of reducing accidents. . .AdvertisementEarly on Sunday morning, vehicular movement either way to and from Satara was affected for a couple of hours as the police and the fire brigade teams mounted an elaborate effort to remove the mangled remains of the truck and the bus using heavy duty cranes and other equipment. The road was cleared by 4am for normal traffic. Police and fire brigade officials said most passengers on board the bus, which was moving at a steady pace, were asleep when the truck hit their vehicle from behind. "Most of the victims, including the deceased, were from the bus and we are in the process of ascertaining their identities," said a police officer, adding, "We are also ascertaining if the victims included those who were in the driver's cabin of the truck."

Title: 1,242 people died in Yamuna expressway accidents since 2012
content: AGRA: At least 7,256 accidents have been reported on the 165.5km-long stretch of the six-lane Yamuna expressway resulting in 1,242 deaths and 10,520 injuries over the past 12 years (January 2012 to March 2023), revealed data provided by the Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority (YEIDA), a UP government undertaking, in response to a recent RTI query.As per the data, the majority of accidents on the e-way occurred when drivers "dozed off". It also contributed to most of the fatalities and injuries, followed by "negligent driving" and "overspeeding".At least 488 people were killed and 3,873 injured in 3,207 accidents caused when drivers slept at the wheel, while 263 deaths and 2,373 injuries in 1,164 accidents were attributed to "negligent driving/others" and 1,302 accidents caused by "overspeeding" with 197 deaths and 1,816 injuries. The data also stated that 90 people lost their lives in 760 accidents caused by "tyre bursts". Drunk while driving caused 263 accidents with 87 deaths. Dense fog caused another 341 accidents and 76 deaths while "pedestrians on the e-way" caused 86 accidents and 24 deaths.. .AdvertisementIn contrast, the ministry of road transport and highways in its latest report 'Road Accidents in India 2021' had mentioned that "over-speeding constitutes the main violation associated with accidents, accident-related deaths and injuries", for the fourth consecutive year.KC Jain, the Supreme Court lawyer who filed the RTI, said on Saturday: "The data is alarming. A detailed study should be conducted and preventive measures taken in a time-bound manner. It is surprising that most of the deadly accidents were caused when drivers dozed off."He added: "The drivers should not drive continuously for more than three hours. In a day, the driver's number of hours at the wheel should not exceed eight to ensure he remains alert. A bulk of the accidents that I have studied took place between midnight and 4am and in the post-lunch period of 3pm and 6pm."In its response, YEIDA stated that they have no record to count the speed violations on the expressway.
