It said: "Traffic police issue challans for overspeeding. The speed limit on the Yamuna e-way is 100 km/h. Electronic monitoring cameras are installed at 20 locations. All data is sent to the National Informatics Centre (NIC) server regularly. The e-monitoring devices have been connected with the NIC since April-2018 for auto-generation of challans."

Title: 3 killed as car rams truck in Rajgarh
content: Bhopal: Three people lost their lives in a road accident in Rajgarh district on Saturday morning. Two others were injured in the incident that took place at around 4:30 am on the National Highway near Bilapura of Pachor police station area.According to police, five people were travelling in a car from Sheopur to Ujjain for Mahakal darshan when the accident occurred. While passing through Bilapura, the car collided with a truck parked on the roadside, after which the car was blown to pieces. The driver of the car, Sunil Yadav, along with Amit Sharma and Deepak Sharma died on the spot, while two other passengers, Ram Milan and Rajpal Gurjar, were injured. It is suspected that the driver may have dozed off while driving.Police said they had to cut the car with a cutter machine to pull out trapped bodies. The injured, Ram Milan and Rajpal Gurjar, were taken to hospital for treatment, but due to the non-availability of beds, both of them were made to lie on the same bed for treatment. Rajpal Gurjar, one of the injured passengers, said that all five passengers were sleeping in the car when the accident occurred. He and Ram Milan Yadav are the only ones who survived, while the whereabouts of the other three passengers are unknown. tnn

Title: 3 killed in fire following collision between two trailers in Rajasthan's Barmer
content: JAISALMER: Three people were burnt alive in a fire that broke out after collision between two trailers under Gudamalani police station in Barmer district on Monday morning. The incident took place at Alpura village near Gudamalani Aduram petrol pump in Barmer district. A total of four people, including the driver, were there in both the trailers.Gudamalani police station officer Ramesh Dhaka said, "A trailer was going from Bikaner towards Sanchore. The second truck loaded with tiles was going from Barmer to Balotra. Meanwhile, there was a huge collision between the two trailers on Mega Highway Gudamalani near Aaduram Petrol Pump near Alpura. Within minutes, both the trailers caught fire.". .AdvertisementHe said, "After this incident, there was a jam on the highway. There were long queues of vehicles on both sides. At the same time, the fire brigade was called from RGT. Till then, both the trailers were burnt over 60-80 per cent. Pradeep (23), resident of Dharnok Nokha Bikaner, and Laxmanram, resident of Dharnok Nokha Bikaner, were in the trailer filled with tiles. Pradeep was burnt alive, whereas Laxmanram was seriously injured. His treatment is going on in the hospital."At the same time, Mohammed Hasaf Sharif's son Samu Khan, resident of Jajhu Bikaner, was in a trailer filled with soil who was burnt alive. The other person sitting in the truck has not been identified yet, investigation is on in that regard.The police officer said, after controlling the fire with the help of fire brigade and local people, the three badly burnt bodies were taken out from there by cutting the trucks, and they have been kept in the mortuary for post-mortem. The fire has been brought under control and the traffic movement on the highway is now normal. According to the information received from another source, Kaka-Bhatija, residents of Bikaner, were going towards Balotra by filling tiles in the trailer. At the time of the accident, nephew Pradeep was driving the trailer and uncle Laxmanram was sitting nearby. With the collision, the trailer caught fire and the nephew got trapped inside. Kaka Laxmanram saved his life by jumping. Both the trailers were burnt on sight.

Title: Brother of Kushalgarh MLA killed in road accident
content: Jaipur: Balwan Singh, brother of Banswaraâ€™s Kushalgarh MLA Ramila Khadiya, was run over by a speeding truck on Sunday afternoon and died before he could reach hospital, police said. He had stepped out of a car and was standing along the road near Nangal Pulia on the expressway in Kardhani police station area of Jaipur when he was run over. He was rushed to hospital where he was declared as brought dead, police said. TNN

Title: Pothole claims life of scooterist
content: Chandigarh: A 40-year-old scooterist was killed after his two-wheeler skidded due to a pothole on the road near a light point at Dhanas on Saturday late night. The victim is Rajinder Singh, a resident of Maloya. Passersby noticed him lying injured and informed the police around 11 pm. A PCR vehicle took him to Government Multi-specialty Hospital, Sector 16,where doctors declared him dead on arrival. Cops said Singh lost balance over his Honda Activa due to a pothole and no other vehicle hit him. Police on Sunday conducted a post-mortem. Three people have lost their lives in road accidents in the past one week in the city.

Title: 3 of family killed in accident in UP's Khautali
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: Three members of a family died after a Swift car crushed them while they were returning home after celebrating Eid with their relatives. The accident happened on the Upper Ganga Canal road under Khatauli police station area on Saturday night.According to police, Mohd Ishtakar (45) and his wife Malika (38) were returning home when their motorbike broke down near Buada Kalan village. Ishtakar then called his brother Nawab (40), who soon reached the spot. Both the brothers were trying to fix the bike when a speeding car ran over them, killing the two on the spot. The injured woman died in hospital later.Two people in the car were also seriously injured in the accident and are admitted to hospital. SHO of Khatauli police station, Sanjeev Kumar, said, â€œThe bodies were sent for postmortem, and action against the car driver would be taken accordingly.â€In a separate accident in Muzaffarnagar, one man died and his brother was seriously injured while they were on their way to fetch milk for Eid. The accident took place in Pinna village under the city police station area on Saturday night.. .Advertisement

Title: 3 of family dead in Khatauli accident
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: Three members of a family died after a Swift car crushed them while they were returning home after celebrating Eid with their relatives. The accident happened on the Upper Ganga Canal road under Khatauli police station area on Saturday night. According to police, Mohd Ishtakar (45) and his wife Malika (38) were returning home when their motorbike broke down near Buada Kalan village. Ishtakar then called his brother Nawab (40), who soon reached the spot. Both the brothers were trying to fix the bike when a speeding car ran over them, killing the two on the spot. The injured woman died in hospital later. Two people in the car were also seriously injured in the accident and are admitted to hospital. SHO of Khatauli police station, Sanjeev Kumar, said, â€œThe bodies were sent for postmortem, and action against the car driver would be taken accordingly.â€. .Advertisement In a separate accident in Muzaffarnagar, one man died and his brother was seriously injured while they were on their way to fetch milk for Eid. The accident took place in Pinna village under the city police station area on Saturday night.

Title: Five dead, 22 injured after truck crashes into private bus in Pune
content: PUNE/KOLHAPUR: Three men and two women were killed and 22 others were injured after a multi-axle truck rammed a private bus that was going from Kolhapur to Dombivli in Thane on the stretch between the Katraj viaduct and Navale bridge in Pune around 2.15am on Sunday.Police identified the deceased passengers as Ravindra Koregaonkar (46) and his mother Suvarna (83) from Radhanagari in Kolhapur, Nisha Bhaskar (36) from Shashtri Nagar in Kolhapur, and Arun Sawant (54) from Kalyan (East) in Thane. Later in the evening, another passenger, Sanju Gawli (37) from Ambernath near Kalyan also died. His wife Pallavi is in a critical condition. Koregaonkar, an employee of a post office in Dombivli, was taking his mother along to show her his new flat. He also wanted to help her get over the loneliness she had been feeling since his father's death less than six months ago.Pravin Koregaonkar, Ravindra's cousin, said: "He wanted to alleviate his mother's grief...It is shocking that three members of the family died within six months. We will stand by the family in their time of need.". .AdvertisementNisha Bhaskar, meanwhile, was travelling with her six-year-old daughter Adhira to her parents' home for the summer vacation. While the child survived with injuries, the mother died.The injured victims, including the truck driver, were taken to private hospitals in Erandwane and Dhankawdi in Pune city, as well as Sassoon General Hospital."The truck driver claimed he lost control over the vehicle due to a brake failure. The truck has been damaged in the accident. We will call RTO officials to inspect the vehicle and verify his claim," senior inspector Vijay Kumbhar of the Bharati Vidyapeeth police said. The injured bus driver Haridas Mandhare (47) of Wai lodged a complaint against the truck driver later in the day.The bus belonged to a private tours and travel operator, while the truck was carrying sugar bags from Sangli to Mumbai. Both vehicles turned on their side after the truck rearended the bus near the Swami Narayan temple in Narhe Ambegaon.The accident-prone stretch, which has a continuous downward gradient starting from the Katraj viaduct (also known as Dari Pul) up to the Navale bridge, is part of the Katraj bypass that links Pune city with the National Highway to Bengaluru at one end and to Mumbai at the other end. Over the past few years, the stretch has witnessed multiple accidents, including a pile up of 48 vehicles last November, and has posed a major challenge to the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) and other agencies. Twenty-eight-year-old Sushant Pawar from Karad was returning to Murbad near Kalyan in the bus after meeting family members. â€œI boarded the bus around 10.20pm on Saturday from Karad and even posted a selfie to my friends from the bus. It could have been my last selfie. We had dinner in Umbraj following which I went to sleep,â€ Pawar told TOI. â€œAround 2.15am, I woke up to a loud noise. The bus moved forward rapidly like it was doing a nose dive. The front section of the bus touched the road and there was another sound of a tyre burst and then the bus fell on its right side.â€ He added that a young girl from the row next to him fell on him when the bus turned on its side. â€œThere was chaos all over. About five minutes later, someone opened the emergency exit and pulled me out. I have sustained internal injuries in the incident,â€ Pawar said. Early on Sunday morning, vehicular movement to and from Satara was affected for a few hours as the police and fire brigade removed the mangled remains of the truck and the bus using heavy-duty cranes and other equipment. The road was cleared by 4am. Senior inspector Vijay Kumbhar of the Bharati Vidyapeeth police told TOI: â€œThe truck driver, Sunny Yadav, its owner and the latterâ€™s son, all residents of Sangli, were among those injured. We will register a case against Yadav for rash and negligent driving, resulting in the death of the four passengers.â€ The bus had picked up passengers from Kolhapur, Sangli and Satara before reaching the viaduct, he said. â€œAfter crossing the viaduct, the bus turned left on to the downward gradient towards Navale bridge. Barely a few meters after this, the truck rammed the bus from behind, resulting in both the vehicles turning on their sides. Most passengers on board the bus, which was moving at a steady pace, were asleep when the accident happened.â€ The officer added: â€œThere were about 30 passengers in the bus and most are from Satara, Sangli and Kolhapur who have moved to Kalyan and adjoining areas for jobs.â€ Raju Kamble (40) of Kolhapur was among those injured in Sundayâ€™s accident. He works for a private firm in Titwala near Kalyan. He and his wife Pooja were returning from Kolhapur after attending a festival.

Title: Man, brother killed in Tehri road accident
content: HARIDWAR: Two brothers - one of them was to get married on May 1 - were killed and two others critically injured when a car they were travelling in fell into a 100-meter-deep gorge in Tehri Garhwal on Monday, police said. They were going to Rishikesh from Tehri Garhwal to shop for the marriage. Amit Singh, 30 and Vikas Singh, 38 - both residents of Mundala village - were killed, while Girdhari Lal, 60 and his granddaughter Meenakshi, 25 - resident of Nai village - were admitted to AIIMS Rishikesh. Muniki Reti police station SHO Ritesh Saha said, "Police started rescue operation with the help of SDRF soon after getting information. Amit, who was to get married on May 1, died on the spot. Vikas died during treatment at hospital."

Title: One killed, another injured in road accidents across capital
content: Raipur: A person was killed and another injured in two separate accidents reported from different parts of the state capital in the last 24 hoursIn the first accident on Monday, a person was crushed and his body was cut into two when a car ran over him. The incident took place in Bhatagaon region of Raipur when a hatchback car was speeding on the road and it hit an auto rickshaw. The impact was such that it changed the direction and went breaking the railing on the roadside, running over a youth. Later, it screeched to a halt inside the bushes. People gathered at the spot to check the youth. However, his body lay disfigured and mangled beyond recognition. Police said that the driver was in inebriated condition and was arrested. The other incident took place in Nava Raipur when a biker came in between and sustained severe injuries. According to police, two cars were coming from opposite direction dashed into each other but a biker came in between them. TNN

Title: 2 minors among 3 dead in road accident
content: Prayagraj: Three persons, including two minors, were killed and three others sustained injuries in a road accident in Kaushambi district on Monday, police said. The incident took place when a youth along with his wife and four other kids was returning home after offering prayers at Kada Dham. Police said the bike in which they were tarvelling was hit by a speeding truck near Kasiya petrol pump road under the limits of Kokhraj police station on Monday afternoon. The deceased have been identified as Shivkumar (40), his daughter Suhani (5) and Anjali (14 years and daughter of one HP Patel), all residents of Tikra village.. .AdvertisementPolice said three more injured persons were sent to Manjahnpur district hospital for treatment.On Sunday evening, two youths had died and two others sustained injuries when two speeding bikes collided head-on near Sayara over-bridge road under the limits of Saini police station of Kaushambi district. The injured were admitted at a hospital for treatment.

Title: 4 die in 2 road accidents in Barmer district, 3 burnt to death
content: JAISALMER: Four people died in two road accidents in Barmer district on Monday, including three who got burnt alive after two trailers collided head-on on the mega highway in Alpura village near Gudamalani Aduram petrol pump around 4.30am on Monday.In the second incident, a truck driver was killed in an accident that took place near Devka village under Alsubah Shiv police station area. The deceased has been identified as Kurban Khan, a resident of Rajdal village. In the Gudamalani accident, a fourth person jumped out of the burning trailer and saved his life. He was rushed to a hospital with serious burn injuries. Ramesh Dhaka, SHO of the Gudamalani police station, said a trailer going from Bikaner to Sanchore collided with a truck loaded with tiles going from Barmer's Ramji Ki Gol to Balotra.

Title: Youth dies after truck hits his bike on NH-91
content: NOIDA: An 18-year-old youth was killed and his friend was injured after the bike they were riding was hit by a truck on NH-91 on Monday morning. The two were on their way to Badaun when the accident took place.The deceased has been identified as Rohit Singh, a native of Badaun, while his friend, Sachin (18) is a resident of Ghaziabad.â€œThe two boys were on their way to Badaun from Ghaziabad. Around 4am, they were passing through NH-91 in Dadri when an unidentified truck hit their motorcycle from behind. The bike fell on the road due to the impact of the collision and was run over by the rear tyre of the truck. Rohit died on the spot,â€ an officer said.Police have sent the body for an autopsy and are awaiting a report for further legal proceedings. Meanwhile, Sachin has been hospitalised, where he is being treated for his injuries.. .Advertisement

Title: 3 killed, 2 injured as speeding truck rams into auto-rickshaw in Berasia
content: Bhopal: Three were killed and two were injured when a speeding truck rammed into a CNG auto-rickshaw riding ahead of it on Vidisha-Bhopal Road in Berasia locality on Monday morning. The auto-rickshaw mangled and got stuck under the truck as the errant truck driver dragged it for a few metres.Locals spotted the accident and informed the police after which Berasia police reached the spot. The injured were taken out with the help of an earth mover as the auto-rickshaw was stuck under the truck.The injured were rushed to the hospital where Habib Khan (60), a resident of Jhikaria village in Berasia, Pawan Jatav (26), a resident of Raipura village in Vidisha, and Mohan Jatav (38), a resident of Suakhedi village in Vidishawere were declared brought dead. The auto-driver and Habibâ€™s 22-year-old daughter were severely injured.There were six people on board the auto-rickshaw including the three deceased, driver and two women.. .AdvertisementBerasia SHO, Girish Tripathi said that Pawan and Mohan boarded the auto-rickshaw in Vidisha and were coming to Berasia. On the way, Habib along with his wife and 22-year-old daughter boarded the vehicle from Jhikaria village in Berasia. When the auto-rickshaw reached near the bio-coal factory in Kulhore village in Berasia on Vidisha-Bhopal highway, a speeding truck, loaded with animal fodder, coming from behind hit the auto-rickshaw and dragged it for a few metres.The horrific incident was captured on a CCTV camera fitted at the bio-coal factory near the spot.Three were killed and two including a woman were injured. The injured womanâ€™s mother escaped with minor bruises as she was thrown out of the auto-rickshaw.The errant truck driver fled the spot after the incident leaving his vehicle behind. The police had registered a case against the driver and search was on to nab him.Habib was a farmer, and Pawan and Mohan were both photographers in Vidisha.

Title: 4 die in 2 roadaccidents inBarmer dist, 3burnt to death
content: Jaisalmer: Four people died in two road accidents in Barmer district on Monday, including three who got burnt alive after two trailers collided head-on on the mega highway in Alpura village near Gudamalani Aduram petrol pump around 4.30am on Monday.In the second incident, a truck driver was killed in an accident that took place near Devka village under Alsubah Shiv police station area. The deceased has been identified as Kurban Khan, a resident of Rajdal village. In the Gudamalani accident, a fourth person jumped out of the burning trailer and saved his life. He was rushed to a hospital with serious burn injuries. Ramesh Dhaka, SHO of the Gudamalani police station, said a trailer going from Bikaner to Sanchore collided with a truck loaded with tiles going from Barmer's Ramji Ki Gol to Balotra. Both the trailers caught fire within minutes, and the fire swelled fast, engulfing the four people, including the two drivers, in both the vehicles. Two of the three dead men were identified as Pradeep, 23, son of Ramchandra and a resident of Dharnok Nokha in Bikaner, and Samu Khan, son of Mohammed Hasaf Sharif and a resident of Jajhu in Bikaner.The third victim, who was with Samu Khan, is yet to be identified. Laxmanram, son of Bharmal and a resident of Dharnok Nokha in Bikaner, jumped out of the truck driven by Pradeep, his nephew. Laxmanram is under treatment. After controlling the fire with the help of fire brigade and locals, police pulled out the three badly burnt bodies by cutting parts of the trucks. The accident caused a massive jam on the highway, which was cleared after around two hours.. .Advertisement

Title: Five killed in separate road accidents in UP
content: BAREILLY: At least five people were killed and one was injured in two separate road accidents in Uttar Pradesh's Lakhimpur Kheri and Moradabad districts. In the first accident, three people died when their bike crashed into a sugarcane-laden cart in Kheri on Monday night, said Sharda Nagar SHO Ashok Kumar. He said, "Four youths on the same bike, without helmets, got into an accident due to low visibility at night. Three persons died and the surviour is in stable condition. We have informed their parents." In the second incident, two bikers died after hitting a truck coming from the opposite direction on the Haridwar National Highway under the limits of Kanth police station in Moradabad early on Tuesday. Both vehicles caught fire due to the collision. While one of the bikers was charred to death, the other succumbed to his injuries. The driver of the truck fled the spot. Circle officer Arpit Kapoor said, "The bodies were sent for autopsy and an FIR will be registered against the driver."

Title: Man dies in bike accident in Pune while fleeing after killing sister-in-law, attacking brother
content: PUNE: A man (25) fleeing on a bike after hacking his sister-in-law to death and severely assaulting his half-brother was killed in a head-on collision with a car early on Tuesday morning in Amble village near Shirur, about 70km from the city.Police identified the deceased assailant as Anil Bendre. His deceased sister-in-law, Priyanka Sunil Bendre (28), was a software engineer. She was about to go abroad next week with her husband Sunil (30), also a software engineer.Sunil, half-brother of Anil, suffered severe injuries in the assault. He is recovering in a private hospital in Pune. Anil's father, Balasaheb Bendre, filed a complaint with the Shirur police."Anil assaulted his stepbrother and sister-in-law with a sharp knife and bludgeoned them with a dumbbell around 4am on Tuesday when they were sleeping on the terrace of their single-storied ancestral home," inspector Sureshkumar Raut of the Shirur police said.. .AdvertisementHe said, "Our investigation so far revealed that Anil was disturbed because he was frequently losing job. He attacked his stepbrother, Sunil, and his wife in frustration."Man dies in accident after killing sis-in-law  Inspector Sureshkumar Raut of the Shirur police said the assailant, Anil Bendre, was a commerce graduate. He worked in a few private companies in Pune. His stepbrother Sunil also worked in the city and had recently purchased a flat.The police inspector said Anil was not consistent at his work and so lost jobs frequently. "He was disturbed by it and started consuming liquor. Last week, his father, Balasaheb, brought him back to their native village. He had advised him to look after their farm instead of doing odd jobs," Raut said.He said Anil was not happy over it and used to quarrel with his parents. "He had locked himself in one of the rooms on Sunday night and ransacked it," Raut said.Against this backdrop, Sunil and his wife got a job offer abroad. They were about to leave the country next week."Before shifting there, Sunil had come to Amble village with his wife to stay with his parents. After dinner, the duo had gone to sleep on the terrace on Monday night," Raut said.He said Anil went to the terrace around 4am carrying the dumbbell and the knife. "He stabbed his sister-in-law and brother and bludgeoned them with the dumbbell," Raut said.Hearing their scream, Balasaheb and his other relatives rushed to the terrace. "Anil tried to attack his father, too. But he ran down and fled on his motorcycle seeing other relatives. As soon as he reached the main road near their house, his motorcycle collided head-on with the car. He died on the spot," Raut said.

Title: Truck crushes man to death, five injured
content: Indore: One person was killed after being run over by a truck in Dhar on Tuesday early morning. Before crushing the man to death the truck hit a four-wheeler injuring five who were travelling in it. Dhar SP Manoj Kumar Singh said that the accident took place on Mangod Manawar road when a speeding truck hit a four-wheeler from the rear and the car hit a two-wheeler rider coming from the opposite direction. The two-wheeler rider, Nandram, fell off the vehicle and mowed down by the truck.An eyewitness said that the truck driver dragged the four-wheeler for several feet before it stopped by the roadside after hitting the two-wheeler rider.People travelling in the four-wheeler â€” Ghanshyam, his wife Kala and their relatives Priyanshu, Shubham and Pappu â€” all residents of Dudhiya were injured in the accident. Police said that a case was registered against the driver. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Couple, 10-year-old girl killed in road accident in Haridwar
content: HARIDWAR: Three people, including a 10-year-old girl, were killed in a road accident near Motichor flyover falling under Railwala police station on Wednesday. According to the police, they were returning from Rishikesh, when a Uttarakhand roadways bus hit the bike they were riding, from behind. The deceased have been identified as Asma (30), her husband Shahban (30) and Misbah (10), all residents of Sarai village in Haridwar. Asma was five months pregnant and the couple got married a year ago. SHO Raiwala police station Kuldeep Pant told TOI, "They went to Rishikesh on a holiday and were returning to Haridwar when the accident took place. They died on the spot. We have sent the bodies to AIIMS-Rishikesh for postmortem.". .Advertisement

Title: Father-son duo killed in road accident in Aligarh
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: Tragedy struck a family that was preparing for the wedding of a daughter when her 60-year-old father and 36-year-old brother were crushed to death after a speeding tractor coming from the opposite direction rammed into their bike under the Barla police station area of Aligarh on Wednesday afternoon.According to the police, the deceased were identified as Vijay Singh and his son Chander Sain. They were en route to Chhatri village of Hathras when the accident took place. After the incident, police rushed to the spot and shifted the victims to the district hospital where they were declared dead on arrival. Later, their bodies were sent for autopsy. Barla police station SHO Subodh Panwar said, â€œThe family of the deceased was informed about the accident. We are looking for the driver, who fled the spot after the accident. A case will be registered once the family files a complaint.â€Dharmveer Singh said, â€œMy father and brother worked as labourers, and they were busy preparing for my sister's wedding on May 3 in Chhatari village. They were going to her in-law's house for a ritual.â€. .Advertisementâ€œThe family was happy and preparing for their daughterâ€™s wedding. The father-son duo left in the morning and we got the bad news hours later. Our happiness has turned into sorrow,â€ said Ravinder Singh, a relative of the deceased.

Title: Two killed in road mishap in Uttar Pradesh
content: BULANDSHAHR: Two men were killed and another was injured when their car crashed into a divider and overturned in Arnia here on Thursday, police said. Station House Officer (SHO) Yagya Datta Sharma said, "Three people travelling in a car were headed to Aligarh from Meerut. The vehicle hit a divider near Raunda and overturned."While Sarvesh (35) and Arshad (31) died on the spot, the third occupant, Sahnawaz, suffered serious injuries and is undergoing treatment, the SHO added.

Title: Road accident near Paderu kills 3
content: Visakhapatnam: Three persons, including a couple, were killed and another person suffered injuries when the car in which they were travelling plunged into a gorge on the ghat road, near Vantlamamidi under Paderu mandal in ASR district on Wednesday night. The deceased Ch Subba Rao (55), his wife Ch Maheswari (50), and the carâ€™s driver K Umamaheswar Reddy (35). The injured person, S Poornachandar, has been admitted to a hospital in Paderu. According to police driver lost control over the vehicle while negotiating a steep curve and that led to the accident.TNN

Title: Pune CA killed after ready-mix concrete truck knocks down his motorcycle
content: PUNE: A 34-year-old chartered accountant from Ambegaon was run over by a ready-mix concrete truck near Shaninagar Chowk on the Jambhulwadi Road, around 1.35pm on Tuesday. Police said the victim, identified as Makrant Gopal Zinde, was headed to Katraj from Ambegaon on his motorcycle, when the truck hit his bike. Zinde fell on the road and came under the rear wheels of the heavy vehicle. The Bharati Vidyapeeth police later booked the truck driver on the charge of rash and negligent driving causing death under the IPC. Sub-inspector Mohan Deshmukh said, "Zinde had got married to a medical practitioner barely six months ago. He had some work to attend to in Katraj and had left home to reach Shaninagar Chowk, where he slowed down slightly. At this moment, the truck collided with his bike from behind." The truck driver halted after crossing the chowk. "We detained him from the spot," said Deshmukh. On Wednesday, a retired assistant sub-inspector and his wife had died on the spot also after a truck hit their scooter at Kesnand Phata in Wagholi on the Pune-Ahmednagar highway.

Title: 6 people killed in separate accidents across state
content: Thrissur/Kottayam: Six people lost their lives in separate accidents across state late on Wednesday night and Thursday. In Thrissur, four persons were killed in three road accidents on Thursday. One person was killed in a road accident in Alappuzha and another in Kottayam late Wednesday night.In the first accident, two persons were killed, and four others were injured when their car rammed truck at Nattika near Triprayar around 3am. All hailed from Alathiyur near Tirur in Malappuram, and the mishap occurred while returning after a trip to Kodaikanal. Police gave the names of the deceased as Muhammed Rizan (18), son of Naduviapparambu Rasakh and Saphwan (20), son of Moochikkal Shaji. The injured have been admitted to two private hospitals in Thrissur. . .AdvertisementThe car was crushed in the impact of the collision, and police and fire-brigade personnel had to break open the car to take out the occupants. In the second accident, a 23-year-old man died when his bike hit a compound wall on the roadside at Valapad near Triprayar on Thursday morning. The deceased has been identified as Meleyil Veettil Jubin of Pazhanji near Kunnamkulam.Iron sheets slip off truck killing pedestrian In another tragic incident, a 55-year-old guest worker lost his life when iron sheets slipped off a moving truck and fell on him as he was standing on the roadside at Chalakudy on Thursday evening. The victim has been identified as Mohan Singh hailing from Agra in Uttar Pradesh. He was a painter attached to a contractor for NH works. He was waiting for the vehicle to pick him up to return to the lodge after completing the job painting the median on the NH at Chalakudy. Meanwhile, a 46-year-old man was killed after his cycle was hit by a car on the NH at Mannancherry in Alappuzha late on Wednesday night. Tomy, a native of Mararikulam Thekku panchayat, who was a painter, was returning home after shopping in Pathirappally. The car, in which a couple was travelling, was coming in the opposite direction. Though they rushed Tomy, who sustained a serious head injury, to the hospital, his life could not be saved. The deceased leaves behind wife Sheeba and son Christopher.In Pravithanam near Pala in Kottayam, a youth was killed after his two-wheeler lost control and rammed a compound wall on Wednesday night. Ulanad native Stephin Thomas, 28, is the deceased. The deceasedâ€™s friend, who was riding pillion, was admitted to the medical college hospital with injuries. The accident was along the Pravithanam-Payyappara road. A police team took the injured to the hospital. However, Thomasâ€™s life could not be saved.

Title: JSR school boy riding pillion killed in accident
content: JAMSHEDPUR: A Class VII student of the Beldih Church School, Bistupur, died when a speeding school bus hit the scooter he was riding pillion on the Bistupur-Jugsalai road near the Tata Steel powerhouse gate under Jugsalai police station limits on Thursday. The deceased, Lalit (14), was a resident of Ram Tekri Road in Jugsalai. The boyâ€™s father, Vikas Prasad, said he was taking Lalit to school when the bus hit them from the rear. He said the incident happened near the Tata Steel powerhouse gate and the scooter turned turtle.The pedestrians rushed the father-son duo to the Tata Main Hospital (TMH), where doctors declared the boy brought dead.Prasad said, "The bus fled the spot. I could not see its number plate or any other details.". .AdvertisementJugsalai police station in-charge Tarun Kumar said they are probing the incident."We are verifying the details of the vehicle for further action," he added.The school held a condolence prayer on its premises for the deceased, a school official said.Earlier on April 30, one Aditya Prasad (13) was killed in Sidhgora when a speeding bike rammed into his bicycle near Baridih.In another accident involving students on September 10 last year, 12 students of the Loyola School were injured when a speeding car rammed into the school van ferrying them at Telco.

Title: Woman killed, son hurt as SUV enters hut in Kota district
content: Kota: A 42-year-old female labourer was killed and her 20-year-old son sustained critical injuries after a speeding SUV ran over the two when the woman was cooking a meal and her son was sitting outside their makeshift hut at Dusshera Madan under Kishorepura police station of the city early Thursday morning.Police said a critically injured Geetabai and her son Ramesh, both natives of Jhabua district in Madhya Pradesh, were rushed to Maharao Bhim Singh (MBS) Hospital, where Geetabai succumbed to her injuries. Ramesh is under treatment there.Rameshwar Prasad, a sub-inspector at Kishorepura police station, said the mishap occurred at around 6am. A police team reached the spot upon receiving information and rushed the injured mother-son duo to hospital. The driver of the SUV was arrested, and the vehicle was seized, he said.Geetabai, her husband Kanharam, a son and daughters had reached Kota for daily-wage labour on April 19, and they were living in a makeshift hut around 10-12 metres off a road at Dusshera Ground, said police. â€œThe driver of the SUV said during interrogation that he could not control the speed of the vehicle as the brake did not work because a plastic water bottle kept in the driver-side door had accidentally stuck under the brake. Investigations are on,â€ said Harlal Meena, the circle inspector at Kishorepura police station.. .Advertisement

Title: UP man picked up by police; wife, nephew chasing on scooter killed in accident
content: MEERUT (UP): A woman and her nephew chasing a vehicle with a police team and her husband onboard were killed in a road accident in UP's Meerut, the police said on Friday. Superintendent of Police (Rural) Kamlesh Bahadur said Chitra (45) and her nephew Mohit (26) were following the vehicle carrying her husband Chetan Prakash Garg, on a scooter. Earlier in the day, a team of Hapur Police had raided their house here in connection with a cheque bounce case involving Garg and were taking him to Hapur. "The scooter was hit by a truck on the Bulandshahr-Meerut highway near Lohia Nagar. The duo were killed on the spot," he added. . .Advertisement The truck driver fled the spot abandoning his vehicle, the police said.  Meanwhile, Garg's relatives alleged that the police came to his house in plain clothes and forcibly took him in a private vehicle.  Responding to the allegation, the SP said since Garg had a bailable warrant against him, he was released on bail shortly after.

Title: Woman dies, son injured in accident
content: Nashik: A 45-year-old woman was killed and a six-year-old boy injured after they were knocked down by a speeding motorcycle on Tuesday.The deceased has been identified as Karuna Jogdand while the injured is a six-year-old boy, Ishant Unhavane. Police have arrested the motorcyclist. He has been remanded in police custody for three days.Nashik Road police said the incident took place at 11pm when Jogdand along with her family friends, including the boy, had gone for a walk. Jogdand and her family friends were walking along Canal Road. As they passed Adarsh Sweets, a speeding motorcyclist hit the group from behind, as per the police. In the accident, Jogdand suffered severe injuries on her head and chest. She died while undergoing treatment in a hospital on Wednesday. While the child received injuries on his leg. His condition is stable, the police said. tnn

Title: Car shop helper dies as lift hits him on head
content: Bengaluru: A 26-year-old man employed as a helper at an automobile shop near JC Road here was killed on the spot after a lift carrying goods suddenly moved downwards and hit his head.The deceased is Vikas Kumar, a resident of City Market and originally from Uttar Pradesh. â€œThe accident occurred around 7pm, when Vikas was moving boxes carrying electrical items from the racks. The lift, which was on the third floor, suddenly moved down and hit Vikas on the head,â€ a cop said. Vikas died even before the other workers could pull him from under the lift.Based on a complaint filed by Lallu Ram Gaur, Vikasâ€™ father, police have booked Binoy and Sanjay, owners of Sanjay Automobiles, for causing death. Gaur claimed no safety gear was provided at the workplace. tnn

Title: Three of family killed, 3 injured in road accident
content: Raipur: In a tragic road accident, three persons of a family, including an elderly woman and a teenage girl, were killed and three others were seriously injured after their car was hit by a speeding truck on in Balod district on Friday.The incident took place in the Dondilohara police station area on Friday morning. According to police, the family was returning from the temple after performing the pooja of their newly bought car. They had just reached the Dondilohara-Dallirajhara main road near Sahagaon when a cattle suddenly ran onto the road. The driver of the car reportedly lost control of the vehicle while trying to save the stray cow but eventually collided with the truck coming from the front.The deceased has been identified as 38-year-old Champa Lal Sahu, his 16-year-old daughter Khushi Sahu, and 55-year-old mother Ahilya Bai. The injured include 60-year-old Ram Ji Sahu, his wife Yamuna Sahu (32 years), and nephew Riddhik Sahu (9 months). All the victims belong to the same family, the police added.As the information was circulated, the police reached the spot. During the investigation, it was found that the truck driver had absconded.. .AdvertisementMeanwhile, the injured were immediately rushed to a nearby hospital for primary treatment, and the deceased were sent for further autopsy.

Title: Army ambulance falls into gorge in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri, two jawans killed
content: JAMMU: Two Army jawans were killed after an ambulance carrying them skidded off the road near the Line of Control (LoC) and rolled into a deep gorge at Keri sector in Jammu and Kashmirâ€™s Rajouri district on Saturday.Official sources said, "The Army ambulance met with an accident near Dungi Nala near the Line of Control (LoC) when its driver lost control while negotiating a sharp curveâ€."Two Army jawans â€” the ambulance driver and a soldier â€” sustained critical injuries and shifted to Rajouri Hospital but reportedly succumbed to injuries," they added.Sources identified the deceased jawans as Havildar Sudhir Kumar of Bihar and Sapper (Spr) Paramvir Sharma of Rajouri.. .AdvertisementMeanwhile, the concerned police have registered a case and further investigations are on.

Title: Army ambulance falls into gorge in J&K's Rajouri, two personnel killed
content: RAJOURI/JAMMU: Two Army personnel were killed on Saturday when an ambulance of the force skidded off the road and plunged into a gorge in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri district, officials said.The Army ambulance met with an accident near Dungi Gala near the Line of Control when its driver lost control while negotiating a sharp curve, the officials said.They added that the ambulance driver and a soldier died in the accident. Their bodies were retrieved from the gorge by rescuers.

Title: Cops arrest trader, wife dies in accident while following police van in Meerut
content: MEERUT: After Hapur police arrested a trader in an alleged cheque bounce case, his wife and her nephew lost their lives in a fatal road accident at Hapur Road in Meerut when they were following the police van in which the trader was being taken to the police station on Thursday night. Soon after the incident, the police released the accused trader, Chetan Prakash Garg. Chitra Garg (42) and her nephew, Mohit Garg (24), were killed when their two-wheeler collided with a truck. Chitra died on the spot, and Mohit succumbed to injuries in the hospital. Pramod Garg, Chetan's elder brother, said, "A group of six policemen, including a woman constable, had reached our house around 9 pm and arrested my younger brother, Chetan. We tried to confront them but failed to do so; they had told us that the police were taking him to Transport Nagar police station, but instead of going to the mentioned police station, they tried to take him towards Hapur."He further added, "As soon as his wife realised that the cops were carrying him to Hapur, she, along with her nephew, immediately started following the van and met with an accident. When the police got the information about the accident, they immediately released Chetan.". .AdvertisementSant Sharan Singh, SHO of Transport Nagar police station, said that "Hapur police had informed us before raiding the trader's house at 8:20 pm, and a team of Transport Nagar police had accompanied them."SP (Rural), Kamlesh Bahadur, informed, "We have taken the truck into police custody and will lodge an FIR under the relevant sections of the IPC once we receive a complaint from the kin; the truck driver fled the scene and will be nabbed soon."

Title: Elderly man, student killed in separate Barmer mishaps
content: JAISALMER: Two persons were killed in separate accidents in Barmer district on Friday.According to police, in the first accident, an elderly person, identified as Manglaram (55) died on the spot after being hit by a vehicle on Siwana Samdadi state highway. He was returning home after grazing cattle. The vehicle also hit many goats. After the accident irate people blocked the road. Police reached the spot. SDM and DSP also reached the spot and persuaded the people and cleared the jam, police said.DSP Neeraj Kumari Sharma said that people were demanding to arrest the vehicle driver and to give compensation to the kin of the deceased. After the assurance to arrest the driver soon and SDM sent the demand for compensation to senior officers, the villagers left the place, he said. In another accident, a girl, identified as Shanta, studying in Class 9, was returning home after giving examinations in Gudamalani police station area in Barmer on Friday when near Loonva Jagir village a bolero hit her and dragged her for 200 feet. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Three killed, many injured in two separate road accidents in Jammu and Kashmirâ€™s Reasi
content: JAMMU: Three people were killed and several others injured in two separate road accidents in Jammu and Kashmirâ€™s Reasi district late on Saturday. On Saturday evening, information was received that one EECO van on its way to Devigarh from Reasi fell into a deep gorge and caught fire due to which three persons died on the spot, police said.â€œThe deceased were identified as Mohan Chand (32), a resident of Devigarh; Karnail Singh (47), a resident of Gull, Tote and Nasib Singh (65), a resident of Kargh, Tote,â€ police said.Meanwhile, in another accident, an S-presso car met with an accident at Chaklass on Mahore-Gool road in which three people were injured and shifted to Sub Division Hospital (SDH), Mahore. . .AdvertisementAfter first aid, all the injured were referred to District Hospital (DH) in Reasi.The injured have been identified as Mohd Waseem (18), Shaista (21), both resident of Mulass Mahore and Manjeet Singh (18), a resident of Jamaslan Mahore also in Reasi district.

Title: Father-daughter duo killed in road accident
content: Pilibhit: A 42-year-old man and his 18-year-old daughter died while four others were injured in a road accident on Saturday morning after a speeding Maruti Eeco car rammed into a bike on Bhind-Lipulekh NH 731 near Saidpur village of Pilibhit district. After the incident, police rushed to the spot and took the injured persons to the district hospital for treatment, and sent the bodies for autopsy. The two deceased were identified as Harish Kumar and his daughter Moni, residents of Barha village. According to police, the car fell into a roadside ditch after the collision, injuring three passengers in the vehicle. However, the driver fled the spot. Neoria police station SHO Udai Veer Singh said, â€œA police team was sent to Badaun in search of the absconding driver. An FIR has been lodged in the matter.â€

Title: 14-year-old girl killed in accident
content: Ludhiana: A 14-year-old Class IX student of a government school died in a road accident near Jugiana village in Sahnewal when a speeding truck hit her cycle and ran her over on Saturday morning. The victim, Sweety Kumari, was a student of Government School, Pava village. After the accident, her family members blocked the national highway. They were later pacified by the police. Inspector Inderjit Singh, SHO, Sahnewal police station said that a case has been registered against the accused driver, Sarabjit Singh of Bathinda, who has been arrested.

Title: Father-daughter duo killed in road accident in Pilibhit
content: PILIBHIT: A 42-year-old man and his 18-year-old daughter died while four others were injured in a road accident on Saturday morning after a speeding Maruti Eeco car rammed into a bike on Bhind-Lipulekh NH 731 near Saidpur village of Pilibhit district. After the incident, police rushed to the spot and took the injured persons to the district hospital for treatment, and sent the bodies for autopsy. The two deceased were identified as Harish Kumar and his daughter Moni, residents of Barha village. According to police, the car fell into a roadside ditch after the collision, injuring three passengers in the vehicle. However, the driver fled the spot. Neoria police station SHO Udai Veer Singh said, "A police team was sent to Badaun in search of the absconding driver. An FIR has been lodged in the matter."

Title: 3-year-old girl, dad killed in road mishap in Udham Singh Nagar
content: RUDRAPUR: A man and his three-year-old daughter lost their lives while his wife was seriously injured in a tragic road accident near Poliganj in Khatima town of Udham Singh Nagar district on Saturday evening.The deceased were identified as Hardev Singh, 42, and his daughter, Arshdeep, residents of Banbasa in Champawat district. Hardev's wife, Simranpreet Kaur, 35, was admitted to the hospital with serious injuries.According to reports, the family was returning on their scooty after visiting their land in Tandavijaisi village on the UP border when an e-rickshaw suddenly turned on the road, causing their scooty to collide with it. The family fell on the road, and a speeding Canter coming from behind rammed into all three of them. Upon receiving the information, the police team rushed to the spot, and the injured woman was immediately transported to the hospital. Hardev and his daughter died on the spot, and their bodies were sent for autopsy, said Sandeep Pilkhwal, in charge of the Satrahamil police post.

Title: 3 of a family killed in accident, mob blocks highway near Mansar
content: Nagpur: Three of a family, including an eight-year-old girl, died in an accident near Amdi village (close to Mansar) on Nagpur-Jabalpur highway on Saturday after coming under the rear wheels of a container truck. The deceased have been identified as Nikki Bawane (35), his daughter Ishani, and his mother Bagvantabai, in her 70s. Nikkiâ€™s five-year-old son, Yug, suffered injuries. Local villagers blocked the road for sometime after the accident, paralyzing traffic on the highway. Officials from the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI), public works department (PWD) and other departments had to be summoned to the spot to diffuse tension.The accident occurred after the bike which the family was riding was grazed past by a Delhi-bound container coming from Hyderabad direction. . .AdvertisementSources said Nikki lost control of the bike. The bike and Yug fell on left while Nikki, Ishani and Bagvantabai rolled down on right before coming under the rear wheels of the container truck.As per senior inspector Hridaynarayan Yadav of Ramtek police station of Nagpur rural, it is still not clear how exactly the accident took place but the two vehicles were moving in the same direction. â€œThe accident occurred at the junction of the service road from Amdi village and the Amdi Phata flyover. We are not sure whether the bikers had come from the service road or the flyover,â€ said Yadav.The Bawane family, native of Pitesur near Godhani, were on the way to Seoni in Madhya Pradesh to attend a marriage. While the bodies were shifted to Ramtekâ€™s rural hospital, the injured boy was rushed to Nagpur. Senior inspector Vijay Mahulkar of district traffic branch of rural police said mitigating steps were suggested at the spot. â€œIt was decided to have road markings in fluorescent paints, rumble strips and yellow blinkers in adequate numbers to alert motorists and warn them against speeding,â€ said Mahulkar.

Title: One dead, 22 injured as state govt bus rams parked truck
content: Dindigul: A 41-year-old man was killed while 22 others were left injured after a Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) bus collided with a parked truck on Saturday.According to police, the TNSTC bus, going to Theni from Coimbatore, was crossing Dindigul district in the early hours of Saturday. The bus, driven by Soundararaja Perumal, was carrying around 45 passengers and the conductor Velmurugan. As the bus was crossing Thethupatti village around 4am, the driver lost control and the bus rammed a loaded truck, which was parked on one side of the road. One of the passengers, S Ayyappan, 41, from Coimbatore, who was seated close to the front end of the bus, died on the spot. Twenty-two others, including children, were injured. They were rushed to the hospital. The Kannivadi police were investigating.In another accident on Saturday, two people were killed after a car dashed into a sign board near Melur in Madurai district. According to the police, a family of five were travelling in a car from Chennai to Virudhunagar district. On Saturday morning, when the car was crossing Muni Kovil in Melur, the driver allegedly lost control and the car rammed the sign board. The front portion of the car was damaged. Though the injured were rushed to hospital, two people died on the way. The Melur police were investigating.. .Advertisement

Title: Man, daughter killed as tractor rams bike in Gunga
content: Bhopal: In a major accident during thunderstorm and rain in Gunga locality reported on Friday evening, a speeding tractor-trolley driver allegedly rammed a bike killing a 35-year-old man and his eight-year-old daughter and seriously injuring a neighbour who was pillion riding.The errant tractor driver fled the spot after the incident with his vehicle and Gunga police registered a case against him.SHO Gunga Ramesh Rai said that the deceased were identified as Jagdish Kushwah, 35, and his daughter Sushma Kushwah, 8, both residents of Triveni village in Gunga. Jagdish was a farmer while Sushma was a primary school student. The injured was identified as Santosh Sen, also a resident of Triveni village.SHO Rai said that on Friday Jagdish along with his daughter Sushma and neighbour Santosh had gone to visit Taravali temple by his bike. It was raining and heavy wind was blowing in the area. Around 5.30pm, when they were returning home from the temple and reached between Peepalkheda and Rodia village, a speeding tractor-trolley driver coming from the opposite direction hit their bike. The trio fell on the road and sustained severe injuries.. .AdvertisementThe errant tractor driver did not stop and fled with his vehicle. Acting on information from the locals, police reached the spot and rushed the injured to hospital where Jagdish was declared brought dead.

Title: Army ambulance falls into gorge in Rajouri, two personnel killed
content: JAMMU: Two Army personnel lost their lives when an ambulance skidded off the road and rolled into a deep gorge near the Line of Control (LoC) in Keri sector of J&Kâ€™s Rajouri district on Saturday.The accident took place near Dungi Nala when the ambulance driver lost control while negotiating a sharp curve, officials said. The ambulance driver and a soldier sustained critical injuries and were rushed to a hospital, but succumbed.Sources identified the deceased as havildar Sudhir Kumar from Bihar and sapper Paramvir Sharma of Rajouri.

Title: Biker killed in road accident in Telangana
content: HYDERABAD: One person died in a road accident on the bypass road in Shadnagar on Sunday after his bike hit a car. Police said that the biker was travelling from JP Dargah to Shadnagar, when he hit a car. The biker was allegedly over-speeding and suffered multiple injuries. He later succumbed to his injuries. Police reached the spot after being informed about the incident by locals. Police said that the victim is from Maheshwaram.

Title: 5 of family killed as SUV collidies with tractor-trolley
content: Varanasi: Five members of a family were killed in a road accident on Purvanchal Expressway in Azamgarh district late on Saturday night, when their SUV rammed into a bamboo-laden tractor-trolley from behind. According to reports, the family was going to Ghazipur from Lucknow. The accident took place at 11:30 pm near Khadarampur village under Ahraula police station area of the district. Due to darkness, the driver could not see bamboos protruding out of the trolley and hit it from behind. The collision was so impactful that the roof of the SUV flew away. The bamboos penetrated the chest of many people sitting inside the vehicle. The passengers sitting on the front seat got trapped under the steering.tnn

Title: 3 killed in two road accidents in South Goa
content: VASCO: Three youngsters lost their lives in two separate accidents in Verna and Vasco on Sunday.In one case, three persons who were travelling on a scooter were being followed by a Vasco police vehicle as scooterist Prakash Bind (30) was travelling triple seat on Sunday evening and violating the MV Act.However, when they reached near a junction, close to the under construction fish market project, the scooter hit the rear wheels of KTC shuttle bus, heading towards the bus stop at Khariwada from the internal road via the F L Gomes Road from Hotel Kamaxi.Bind died on the spot, while the first pillion rider Arun Kumar Saroj (21) died on the way to hospital. The second pillion rider, identified as Ritesh Kumar Saroj (29), is said to be critical and has been shifted to GMC for further treatment. The trio were residents of Birla, Zuarinagar.PSI Rohan Nageshkar is further investigating the case.In the other accident on early Sunday morning, MacArthur Pereira (23), a resident of Utorda, died after his car collided head-on with a mini-truck at Utorda junction on NH66.Verna police said that the accident occurred when Pereira was heading to his residence from Panaji. When he reached Utorda junction at 6.10am, his car collided with the truck coming from the opposite direction.It is not clear how the accident occurred, but police are investigating whether Pereira had dozed off.Police have registered a case of an accident. The body of the deceased was handed over to his relatives for the last rites after the postmortem examination. Police sub-inspector Anant Gaonkar is investigating the case under the supervision of PI Diogo Gracias.

Title: Garage owner killed for not repairing flat tyre, 2 held
content: Nashik: A 25-year-old owner of a garage shop, who primarily fixed flat tyres, was killed by four people with a sharp weapon near his roadside shop in the Panchavati area on Saturday night.Soon after the first information report (FIR) was registered with the Adgaon police, crime branch personnel swung into action and arrested two people and detained a minor, suspected to be accused in this case. The fourth suspect us yet to be arrested.The deceased person has been identified as Gulam Rabbani Mohammad. Police inspector (PI) Vijay Dhamal of crime branch (unit 1) said that Mohammad was working in his garage located in the Panchavati area on the Tapovan-Jejurkar Mala road stretch when the accident took place. . .Advertisementâ€œThe four suspects, who were riding on the same bike, went to the garage and told Mohammad to fill air in the bikeâ€™s tyres. Mohammad initially denied serving them claiming that he was busy,â€ Dhuman said. â€œWhen he did check the tyre, he told the suspects that the valve needed to be changed. This irritated the four men and was followed by an argument between them and the tyre repair shop owner. The four accused and beat Mohammad and one of them took out a chopper-like sharp weapon and attacked him,â€ Dhumal added. Mohammad suffered serious injuries and was rushed to the Nashik civil hospital, where he succumbed to his grievous injuries. Meanwhile, the police swung into action and combed CCTV footage of the area to find their suspects. They arrested Yash Pawar (18), a resident of Sant Kabir Nagar in Dwarka, and Prasad Pawar (24) of Shiv Nagar, Panchavati, besides detaining a minor. The case was investigated under the guidance of commissioner of police (CP) Ankush Shinde, deputy commissioner of police (Crime) Prashant Bacchav and other senior officers of the city police department.

Title: Five of a family killed in accident on Purvanchal expressway in UP
content: VARANASI: Five members of a family, including a couple, died in a road accident on Purvanchal Expressway in Azamgarh district late on Saturday night when the vehicle they were travelling in hit a bamboo-laden tractor-trolley from behind.Police said that the family was going from Lucknow to Ghazipur by an SUV when around 11.30pm, their vehicle hit a tractor-trolley near Khadarampur village.Due to darkness, the driver could not see bamboos protruding out of the trolley and hit it from behind. The impact was so strong that the roof panel of the SUV flew off. The bamboos penetrated the chest of the occupants of the vehicle. Local residents rushed to the spot and informed the police. All accident victims were from Deoria. .AdvertisementCops reached the accident site and took out the bodies from the SUV. Five persons, including three women, died on the spot. The deceased were identified as Kailash (45), his wife Neetu (38), Dukkhi (43), Guddi (40), and Rani (11). The bodies were sent for post-mortem. One of the occupants, Kiran, who sustained grievous injuries, was rushed to the district hospital from where she was referred to the trauma centre in Varanasi for treatment. SP, City, Shailendra Lal said all the deceased were residents of Manduadih in Deoria district.

Title: â‚¹62L payout for bank manager killed in crash
content: Bhopal: A sessions court ordered for payment of Rs 62 lakh compensation to the family of a bank manager killed in a road accident. Brother of the deceased, who was also injured in the mishap, would also get Rs 4 lakh as compensation. Advocate Sunny Hingorani, who appeared in the case for the family of the deceased bank manager, said Syed Anwar Ahmed, who was a manager in Cooperative Central Bank at Bhopal, was returning to Bhopal in a car after attending a marriage ceremony in Aligarh on February 15, 2021. Early in the morning, the driver of the car he was travelling in, hit a stationary truck on Shivpuri highway from behind. Syed Anwar Ahmed was grievously injured in the incident. He was taken to hospital for treatment, where he succumbed to his injuries during treatment. His brother was also injured in the incident but he survived.. .AdvertisementThe court of sessions judge Jayant Sharma ordered the driver and owner of the car which Ahmed had hired and the insurance company which had insured the car to pay a compensation of Rs 62 lakh 35 thousand to the family of the deceased and Rs 4 lakh 41 thousand to his brother as compensation.

Title: Car hits trailer after tyre bursts, 4 killed
content: Udaipur: Four persons were killed when a speeding car hit a trailer coming from the opposite side on the other side of the road after the car's tyre burst. While three persons died on the spot, one person died in the hospital during treatment. The accident took place near Madna on Chittorgarh-Kota highway on Saturday morning.According to Begu circle inspector Bhagwanlal Meghwal, the passengers in the car were driving towards Uttar Pradesh from Ahmedabad. The speeding car lost control when its tyre burst and was flung on the other side of the road. It hit the trailer which was coming from the other side of the road.Based on the Aadhaar cards which were recovered from the car, the deceased have been identified as Vishwakarma Ram, Umashanker, Jitendra and Raju. . .AdvertisementHowever, the actual identification will be possible only after the arrival of the relatives. Three bodies have been kept in Begu mortuary while the body of Raju who died during treatment has been kept in Chittorgarh mortuary. tnn

Title: Car driven by minor rams e-rickshaw in Kanpur; class VI schoolgirl killed, 5 injured
content: KANPUR: A 12-year-old girl student of class VI died while five others were injured when a 15-year-old boy learning to drive a car rammed his vehicle into an e-rickshaw carrying schoolchildren near Dolphin crossing in Vishnupuri area of Kanpur city on Monday morning.According to police, the car was being driven by the minor who apparently lost control over the vehicle and rammed it into the e-rickshaw, causing injuries to its driver and four schoolchildren. All the injured were rushed to a private hospital in Sarvodaya Nagar, where one of the children, identified as Kalpana, succumbed to injuries.Kalpana was daughter of Vinod Kumar Agnihotri, a resident of Makdi Kheda Road who runs a sweet shop.The minor driving the car managed to flee but the man who was supervising him was nabbed by the passers-by and handed over to the police.. .AdvertisementDCP (Central) Pramod Kumar said, "The man, Bhairav Jha, 40, has been taken into custody and the police are now in the process of registering a case against him." E-rickshaw driver, Amit, who also sustained wounds, is undergoing treatment at the hospital."The e-rickshaw driver had picked up students from the Makri Khera area in Kalyanpur in his e-rickshaw. When he was on way to drop the children to Nand Lal Khanna school in Nawabganj, his e-rickshaw was hit by a recklessly driven car at the Dolphin crossing. The vehicle involved in the accident has been impounded. Further investigations are on," a police official said.

Title: 3 die as bike rams SUV on e-way
content: Bengaluru: Three youths astride a bike were killed on Monday after their two-wheeler crashed into an SUV with a flat tyre, which had stopped at Jayapura gate on Bengaluru-Mysuru expressway.According to Ramanagara traffic police, the accident occurred around 12.30pm, when the trio was heading towards Mysuru. The rider was unable to notice the SUV and crashed into the vehicle. Though the rider sought to regain control of the bike, another speeding car came from behind and hit the two-wheeler. The deceased â€” Hussain, Rehman, and Roshan â€” were aged between 18 and 22. All were residents of Magadi Road and welders by profession. tnn

Title: Minor girl from MP hit by speeding car in Kota, dies
content: Kota: A 12-year-old girl was killed after being run over by a speeding car on the Kota-Baran highway under Udyog Nagar police station here on Monday morning.Police said the accident took place near Kalpatru warehouse on NH 27 at around 10am when the girl was crossing the road along with her parents, labourers hailing from Jhabua district of Madhya Pradesh. Her parents escaped injury as they were walking a few steps ahead of her.SHO Manoj Singh Sikarwal of Udyog Nagar police station said the family travelling in the car coming from Baran took the injured girl, identified as Navli, daughter of Raju, to MBS Hospital, where doctors declared her brought dead.â€œA case has been registered against the car driver, and the girlâ€™s body has been handed over to her parents after post-mortem,â€ said Sikarwal. The girlâ€™s parents are daily-wage labourers with a contractor and have been living in Kota for several months, he added.
