Title: At least 6 dead after dust storm causes crashes in Illinois
content: DIVERNON: A windstorm in central Illinois kicked up dangerous clouds of blinding dust off farm fields Monday, causing numerous crashes that killed at least six people on Interstate 55, police said.The late morning crashes involved 40 to 60 cars and multiple tractor-trailers, two of which caught fire, Illinois State Police Maj. Ryan Starrick said.He said at least six people died, all in the northbound lanes, and more than 30 people on both sides of I-55 were transported to hospitals with injuries.â€œThe only thing you could hear after we got hit was crash after crash after crash behind us,â€ said Tom Thomas, 43, who was traveling south to St. Louis.. .AdvertisementI-55 was shut down in both directions in Montgomery County, 75 miles (120 kilometers) north of St. Louis, and likely won't reopen until Tuesday.Starrick told reporters that it was a spring version of a â€œwhiteout situationâ€ typically seen in winter snowstorms. Gov. J.B. Pritzker described the scene as â€œhorrific.â€â€œThe cause of the crashes is due to excessive winds blowing dirt from farm fields across the highway, leading to zero visibility,â€ Starrick said.Dairon Socarras Quintero, 32, who was driving to St. Louis to make deliveries for his custom frame company based in Elk Grove Village, said that after his truck hit the vehicle in front of him, he exited and moved to the side of the road to ensure his safety, then returned after the chain reaction of crashes ended behind him.Socarras Quintero said the dust continued to blow ferociously as he checked on other motorists and emergency personnel arrived. He held up his backpack, which was caked with dust even though it was inside a closed truck cab.Winds at the time were gusting between 35 mph (56 kph) and 45 mph (74 kph), the National Weather Service said.â€œIt's very flat, very few trees," meteorologist Chuck Schaffer said. â€œIt's been very dry across this area really for the last three weeks. The farmers are out there tilling their fields and planting. The top layer of soil is quite loose.â€Evan Anderson, 25, who was returning home to St. Louis from Chicago, said a semi turned before striking his vehicle, sparing him from even more damage.â€œYou couldnâ€™t even see,â€ Anderson said. â€œPeople tried to slow down and other people didnâ€™t, and I just got plowed into. There were just so many cars and semitrucks with so much momentum behind them.â€Kevin Schott, director of emergency services in Montgomery County, said it was a â€œvery difficult sceneâ€ and one thatâ€™s â€œvery hard to train for.â€â€œWe had to search every vehicle, whether they were involved in the accident or just pulled over, to check for injuries,â€ he said, adding that people were "upset â€” visibly so, understandably so.â€Authorities set up staging areas away from the crash site to help travelers reunite with friends and family.

Title: 2 cops dead, 4 army jawans injured in road accident in Punjab's Fatehgarh Sahib
content: PATIALA: Two policemen, who were inspecting a road accident site in which four army jawans had suffered injuries, died on the spot after a speeding truck rammed into the bus involved in the mishap early on Wednesday on National Highway in Fatehgarh Sahib. The first accident took place at around 3.30am when the passenger bus rammed into an army truck near Dallomajra village of Sirhind in Fatehgarh Sahib district. Following the accident, the police reached the spot in sometime to divert the traffic and review the situation.However, at 4.10am a speeding truck from Rajpura side rammed into the damaged bus killing two policemen on the spot. . .AdvertisementThe deceased cops were identified as assistant sub-inspector Najar Singh and home guard jawan Kuldeep Singh.They were standing in front of the damaged bus and were dragged around 40 feet from the spot.The police identified the injured army men as constables Chauhan Vishal, Vijay, S Ramanaidu and Siuhanshu. The bus driver identified as Ranjit Singh was also injured in the mishap. The bus was heading towards Jalandhar from Delhi. Police said the accused truck driver has been identified as Sajid from Uttar Pradesh who managed to escape. Dy SP Sukhvir Singh said that a case was registered against the accused truck driver.â€œAn army major informed their control room about mishap. The injured army Jawans were initially taken to Mandi Gobindgarh hospital from where they were shifted to an army hospital for treatment. The injured bus driver was taken to the civil hospital and his condition is stable," a police officer said.

Title: Man killed in road accident
content: Kolkata: A 65-year-old man died in a road accident near airport gate no 3 on Jessore Road on Tuesday. Cops said the victim, Dilip Kumar Hari, a resident of Birati, was waiting for a bus to return home when a speeding bus hit a stationary SUV, which in turn hit the man. He was taken to a hospital where he was declared. TNN

Title: Girl killed after car driven by minor boy hits her in Tamil Nadu's Tirupur
content: COIMBATORE: A 12-year-old girl was killed after a car driven by a 15-year-old boy, a class X student, hit her in Tirupur on Monday.The boy's father was also booked for allowing his minor son to drive the car.Nallur Police said, "A Aathinarayanan, 40, a native of Ranganathapuram in Theni district, was running a mess at Kovilvazhi on Dharapuram road in Tirupur city. He was residing with his wife Gomathi, 40 and three daughters Bakyasree, 16, Dhanshiya, 14 and Deepika 12, in a rental house at Vaikkalmedu on Muthanampalayam-Kovilvazhli road in Tirupur for the last six years. His younger daughter Deepika was studying class VI in a middle school. On Monday evening, she was walking towards the mess from her home. A speeding car which was heading towards Dharapuram road, was about to hit the people who were standing infront a meat shop. The car was driven by a minor boy. The car hit the minor girl, whose head was buried under a roadside sand after the car ran over on her head and she died on the spot. The car rolled down and came to a halt.. .AdvertisementThe minor boy managed to escape from the spot. Nallur police went to the spot and sent the body to the Tirupur Government Medical College Hospital for postmortem. Police registered a case against the class X student for underage driving and involving in a fatal accident. His father Eswara Ganesan, a second-hand car dealer, was also booked for allowing minor son to drive the car in a public way.The police booked them under section 279, 304 (A) of the Indian Penal Code and section 199 (a) (offences by juveniles) of the Motor Vehicles Act. Police said the minor boy suffered injuries on his shoulder and he was admitted as an inpatient in a private hospital and the boyâ€™s father is absconding. "The father-son duo will be arrested, and the boy will be sent to juvenile home and the father will be remanded in judicial custody," said police.

Title: One killed, six injured after bus crashes into house in Himachal's Una
content: A Haryana Roadways bus crashed into the wall of a house, killing the conductor on the spot and injuring six other persons, near Baruhi village on Una-Dharamshala road in the wee hours of Wednesday.The injured included a woman and her son, who were sleeping in the room whose wall was hit by the bus.The deceased conductor has been identified as Virender, a resident of Mahendergarh in Haryana. Sangeeta Chauhan, who received serious injuries, was referred to the PGIMER, Chandigarh, by the doctors of a local government hospital.The other five injured namely Chandan Chauhan, Saniya Chauhan, Rekha, Nitin and Bhupinder Singh were taken to a nearby hospital and are presently under treatment. There were around 15 passengers travelling in the bus.. .AdvertisementAccording to police officials, the accident happened after the driver lost balance, the bus first hit a eucalyptus tree and then crashed into the wall of the room where the woman and her son were sleeping.A police officer said that prima facie it seemed the accident happened due to over-speeding. A case has been registered by the Una police.

Title: SI, driver die in accident in Mulugu
content: MULUGU: A sub-inspector (SI) and driver were killed in a road accident at Jeedikal Vagu in Eturnagaram on Tuesday. B Indiraiah was working as a SI in Eturnagaram police station and Ch Raju was a driver for the police escort vehicle.Indiraiah and Raju were on escort duty for intelligence chief T Prabhakar Rao, who was holding a meeting with Mulugu police officials in Venkatapuram. The accident took place when Raju tried to overtake the Rao's vehicle, but lost control. The vehicle overturned and both suffered head injuries and died on the spot. Indiraiah was from Palivelpula in Hanamakonda, and Raju from Tadvai mandal of Mulugu. A case has been registered under section 304(A) of IPC.

Title: Runaway crane carnage on Indore road; three minors among 4 dead
content: INDORE: A tow crane hurtling down a bridge in Indore crushed several motorists before slamming into a bus and coming to a stop in peak traffic hour on Tuesday. At least four persons, including three minors, were killed and a woman critically injured.The driver of the crane, 32-year-old Anees, claimed that the brakes failed. But Banganga police station in-charge Rajendra Soni told TOI that the vehicle was "working perfectly" when it was driven to a safe location through a green corridor. Anees has been arrested.The victims were dismembered in the accident. Body parts lay strewn on the road when TOI arrived on the scene. Soni said that the crane was descending from Banganga bridge when its brakes allegedly failed. Police say the crane was hurtling at "top speed" when it slammed into an autorickshaw, sending it crashing into a nearby pole.The crane then plowed into five persons on two motorcycles, sending them crashing against a parked bus, and then crushed them against it. Thankfully, the bus was empty. Those on the bike, however, were mangled in the accident.. .AdvertisementThere's chilling CCTV footage of the crane on its deadly rampage.One of the bikes had four riders - 16-year-old Ritesh, who was driving his mother Sharda, six-year-old brother Sharad and cousin Raj, 13, back home from a wedding. And the second one was being driven by Sunil Parmar, 56.Ritesh, Sharad, Raj, and Parmar were killed on the spot. Sharda, 40, was severely injured and is in hospital.Sharda's husband Dinesh Kishore, struggling to come to grips with the tragedy, said that they were all going back to their home in Kaveri Nagar after attending a wedding near Aurobindo area. "I stopped to meet a family member and the others went ahead. A little later I got a call that they had met with a terrible accident," he said. The family was barely 5-7 minutes from home. TOI was on the scene soon after the accident and spoke to eyewitnesses. Driver claimed brakes failedAs enormous crane went down Banganga bridge on Tuesday evening, people felt that it was attempting to steer ahead of bus plying in front of it, seconds later the locals watching realized that it was going to kill people.While driver claimed that the brakes of the vehicle had failed, Banganga inspector Rajendra Soni said that it was perfectly in place when it was steered back to the police station.The large machine which killed four persons including three children and seriously injured a woman within seven seconds had descended from Banganga bridge towards the 60-feet wide Bhagat Singh Nagar road at high speed at 6:13 pm.The people watching the incident couldn't stop themselves from shouting and screaming trying to ask the motorists to maneuver their vehicles towards the roadside but it was too late.The road was strewn with body parts and there was blood all over the spot. Hundreds of people from nearby shops and passersby stopped and rushed to the rescue of the victims but only one woman had survived the accident with severe leg injuries.A bank employee passing from the area at the time of accident had a narrow escape as he wavered to the left hearing the screams of people."I was driving my bike from near the bus. Had I not wavered to left as people shouted, I would have also been killed in the accident. I was really lucky and escaped death," said Rajnish Saini, 38.Sanini said that it was scary to watch the accident prone children.The deceased family members were on their way back home after attending a close relative's wedding. Soon after the accident all the family members rushed to Aurobindo hospital to meet the injured Sharda. The surviving husband Dinesh Kishore said that he is a daily wage labourer. His daughter Pihu also survived in the accident as she had stayed back at the wedding.

Title: 1 killed as car crashes into bike in Delhi, drives for half-a-kilometre with man on bonnet
content: NEW DELHI: A 30-year-old man was killed after a car allegedly crashed into his bike and drove half-a-kilometre with him on the bonnet on Kasturba Gandhi Marg, Delhi police said on Wednesday.The accident took place on Saturday night when the SUV car allegedly crashed into the bike that Deepanshu Verma and his cousin Mukul Verma(20) were riding. Due to the impact of the collision, Deepanshu flung into the air and landed on the car's bonnet, while Mukul fell on the road, a senior police officer said.Read AlsoMan dragged on car's bonnet for 2-3 km near Ashram Chowk in DelhiA car was caught on camera driving with a person hanging on the bonnet for around two to three kilometres, police said on Monday. The incident took place around 11 pm when the car was travelling from Ashram Chowk to Nizamuddin Dargah. The victim, however, did not suffer any injuries.However, the accused did not stop and drove the car with Deepanshu on the bonnet for about 500 metres, after which they dropped him on the road and drove off, he added. Both the victims were taken to a nearby hospital where Deepanshu was declared brought dead, while Mukul was admitted for treatment, the officer said. The accused driver was arrested on Sunday and the vehicle involved in the accident was seized, he added.(With PTI inputs)

Title: Nashik: Locals' ire boils over after truck kills 8-year-old girl
content: NASHIK: Residents of Vajreshwari Nagar along Dindori Road went on a rampage on Monday evening after a pickup truck ran over an eight-year-old girl around 6pm near the canal when she was walking on the road in front of her home.The deceased girl has been identified as Zoya Salim Shaikh from Avdhootwadi in the Panchavati area. On noticing the pickup truck knocking down the girl, residents of Vajreshwari Nagar blocked the busy Dindori Road. They ransacked the minitruck and thrashed the truck driver.The Panchavati police immediately sent a team of riot control police to control the situation. One side of Dindori Road, leading to Gujarat, was blocked for close to an hour after the accident. Sunil Wagh, a resident of the area, said, "The anger of slum residents was justified. The deceased girl's father, a labourer in the vegetable market, is one of the most modest persons in the area. His only daughter got killed despite no mistake of her. Hence, people of the area came out on the road to vent their anger."The deceased girl's aunt lodged a police complaint. An FIR has been registered under sections 304 (A) (causing death by negligence), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 338 (causing grievous hurt by an act which endangers human life) and 279 (rash driving) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) against the pickup vehicle driver, Rustam Tengse, from Hirawadi in Nashik.. .AdvertisementAssistant police inspector A K Padolkar, the investigation officer, said, "The pickup driver has not been arrested so far. He was thrashed by the mob after the accident. Hence, he has been admitted to the Nashik Civil Hospital. The condition of the driver is not serious. He seems to have suffered internal injuries and a few medical check-ups have been conducted. Once his condition improves, he will be arrested."

Title: Food delivery executive killed in road accident in Telangana
content: HYDERABAD: A food delivery executive was killed and another person suffered injuries when a car went amok at Dairy Farm Road in Alwal on Wednesday night. Police booked a case against the woman techie who was driving the car. The deceased was identified as Raju, 31, from Gandhi Colony in Secunderabad. According to police the woman techie from Alwal who was returning home from LB Nagar lost control over the vehicle at Dairy Farm road and hit the bike of the food delivery executive from behind. . .Advertisement"Raju suffered injuries and he died while undergoing treatment at a local hospital," Alwal SI Ramulu said. Another person hit by the car also suffered injuries but according to the SI, he is out of danger. After hitting the victims, the car went to the extreme left portion of the road and hit a sugarcane stall, a push cart and a cement electric pole before coming to a halt. Police apprehended the accused and booked a case against her under sections 304-a and 337 of the IPC.

Title: 11 including 10 members of a family killed in Chhattisgarh road mishap
content: RAIPUR: Eleven people, including two children between the ages of one and three, and five women, were killed in a fierce road mishap that occurred in Balod, 80km from the state capital Raipur, on Wednesday night. Ten out of the eleven victims belonged to one family.The sports utility vehicle (SUV) in which the victims were travelling was hit by a truck loaded with iron rods coming from the opposite direction on National Highway-30 under Purur police station.The impact of the accident was so fierce that ten out of the 11 people died on the spot, while a one-and-a-half-year-old child succumbed to his injuries on the way to Raipur for treatment.The head of a man got separated from his body in the horrifying accident, said police on Thursday, adding that the accident took place near Jagtara village of Purur police station area around 10.15pm on Wednesday when the victims, all residents of Soram-Bhatgaon village in Dhamtari, about 60km from the state capital Raipur, were heading for Balod to attend a marriage ceremony.The truck driver fled the spot, and a manhunt has been launched to nab him, said Purur station house officer Arun Kumar Sahu. The bodies have been sent for postmortem, he said.The deceased were identified as Dharmraj Sahu, 55, his wife Usha Bai Sahu, 52, Keshav Sahu, 34, his wife Tomin Sahu, 33, Laxmi Sahu, 45, Rama Sahu, 20, Shailendra Sahu, 22, Sandhya Sahu, 24, Ishant Sahu, 1.5 years, Yogyansh Sahu, 3 years, and the driver of the vehicle Damesh Dhruw, 19.Chief minister Bhupesh Baghel posted a tweet at 12.45am to express grief over the accident.With inputs from PTI

Title: Truck hits auto in Bihar's Sitamarhi, 6 of a family killed
content: PATNA: Six of a family among seven persons died and four others were injured when the three-wheeler in which they were travelling was badly smashed by a truck near Pakari FCI godown under Sitamarhi police station area Wednesday evening. The injured persons have been admitted to Sitamarhi Sadar hospital, police said. Enraged over the incident, the locals set the truck on fire. CM Nitish Kumar expressed grief over the death of "six people" in the Sitamarhi road accident.Mohammad Jabir Nadaf, a relative of the deceased, told police that his son Ibran's baraat party had returned to their native village Harpurwa Tuesday evening. Nadaf's daughter and her family members had arrived at Harpurwa from Basahiya to participate in the wedding reception. On Wednesday, they were returning home.The three-wheeler driver, identified as Badre Alam, was among the seven persons who died on the spot. . .Advertisement

Title: Man found dead, family cries foul
content: Visakhapatnam: A 26-year-old man from Vizag city was found dead near Gudumuru village in Laveru mandal in Srikakulam district. The deceased was identified as G Bharath Kumar, a resident of Madhurawada locality. Police suspect that Kumar was killed in a road accident. However, his family members raised doubts over his death. A case has been registered and the body was shifted for post-mortem. TNN

Title: Dehradun YouTuber dies after crashing Kawasaki ZX10R while trying to hit 300 kmph
content: A Dehradun-based YouTuber has reportedly lost his life after a tragic accident took place near Aligarh district on the Agra to Delhi expressway. The content creator, named Agastay Chauhan, was riding his Kawasaki Ninja ZX10R superbike when the accident took place. Reports suggest that Chauhan was filming content for his channel at the time of the accident and was attempting to touch 300 kmph speed on his motorcycle. The 25-year-old met his untimely demise after the motorcycle lost control and hit a divider. Medical personnel reached the spot and tried to recover the vlogger but he passed away shortly. The news has left Chauhanâ€™s 12 lakh subscribers on Youtube in shock.How safe is the MG Comet? | TOI AutoThis is not the first time Chauhan had made news. Earlier in March 2023, Dehradun police had registered a case against Chauhan for performing stunts on public roads on his ZX10R superbike. The bloggerâ€™s motorcycle had been seized and the YouTuber was also made to record an apology video as an appeal to the general public to follow traffic rules. He was also flagged for not wearing proper riding gear at the time of the incident.Hero Xoom Road Test Review | Cornering lights tested at night | TOI Auto. .AdvertisementWhile Chauhanâ€™s demise is certainly tragic news, it also sheds light on the importance of following traffic rules and driving/riding your vehicle at safe speeds. In this age of social media, we should not let online validation or likes dictate our driving habits. Driving at high-speeds is not only illegal but also extremely unsafe for both you and others on the roads. For two-wheeler riders, this incident especially highlights the dangers of stunt riding and the importance of wearing proper riding gear at all times.

Title: 4 killed as vehicle overturns in Gumla
content: Gumla: At least four persons were killed and 27 injured after a vehicle carrying a wedding party overturned in Gumla district, police said on Wednesday. The deceased include the parents of the bride. Of the injured, 12 are said to be critical, police said. Chief minister Hemant Soren on Wednesday expressed grief over the loss of lives in the accident. â€œHeartbroken by the news of the death of four people in a road accident in Dumri, Gumla. May God give strength to the bereaved family members Medical facilities are being provided to the injured people in the accident,â€ Soren said in a tweet. The deceased were identified as Sundar Gayar (55), wife Biranti Devi (50), Savita Nagesia (45) and Eranius Kondo (50) â€” all residents of Katari village under Dumri police station.The pick-up van with 35-40 people overturned near Jarda village after the driver, who was in an inebriated condition, lost control. Deputy superintendent of Gumla sadar hospital said that two persons were referred to Rims in Ranchi. â€œNine persons are under treatment at sadar hospital here, and one person with minor injuries were released already. Two have been sent to Rims in Ranchi,â€ he said.â€œThe attendees were returning home at night when the accident occurred. While four died on the spot, the injured were rushed to community health centre in Dumri,â€ an eye witness said. Dr Roshan Khalkho of CHC Dumri said, â€œOf the 27 injured, 10 were minor boys and girls."

Title: One killed, six injured as truck falls on its side near Yercaud
content: Salem: One person was killed and six others were injured after the truck they were travelling in fell on its side near Yercaud here on Wednesday. The Yercaud police identified the deceased as K Periyasamy, 32, of Omalur in Salem district.According to police, the incident took place when a team of audio and mike setters was returning to Salem from Vellakadai in Yercaud after a temple festival in a truck.â€œWhen the truck neared Valavanthi village on Yercaud-Kuppanur ghat road, the truck driver suddenly lost his control over the vehicle while negotiating a blind turn. The truck hit the side parapet wall and fell on its side,â€ said S Thaiyalnayaki, Salem rural deputy superintendent of police. . .AdvertisementPeriyasamy, he said, was trapped under the loaded materials and died on the spot.Six others - Thangaraj, 43, Raja, 32, Devaraj, 36, Sakthivel, 43, Ananth, 26 and Selvam, 29 - escaped with injuries. When alerted, the Yercaud police rushed the injured to the Salem Government Mohan Kumaramangalam Medical College and Hospital. The body was sent to the same hospital for a postmortem.Following the accident, traffic was affected on the stretch for nearly two hours.

Title: Watch: BMW M3 loses control, crashes into 7-series, nearly kills traffic cop
content: A video shared by the Fairfax County Police in the United States, shows moments before a BMW M3 lost control on the other side of a freeway and crashed into a 7-series on the opposite freeway while nearly killing a police officer. The incident took place while the cop had pulled over the BMW 7-series for a routine check. The video has since gone viral on social media, take a look at how the event took place in the video below.Speeders crash nearly proves fatal for officer on traffic stop. https://t.co/cKgxuwsLUf #FCPD pic.twitter.com/83zi7JeW3uâ€” Fairfax County Police (@FairfaxCountyPD) May 2, 2023 As we can see, the out of control BMW M3 crashes directly into the side of the stationary 7-series and the cop misses a fatal injury by near inches. According to an official statement released by the Fairfax County Police, 2018 model M3 was being driven by a 17-year-old youngster who lost control of the car at a high speed. On the other hand, the driver of the stationary 7-series suffered minor injuries and was immediately shifted to a nearby hospital. The officer on the spot suffered minor bruises as well. The juvenile driver has since been arrested and charged with reckless driving, he had another passenger with him onboard the M3 at the time of the accident.How safe is the MG Comet? | TOI AutoThe BMW M3 is an enthusiast favourite and the 2018 model came with the automakerâ€™s twin-turbo, 3.0-litre, six-cylinder engine, which produces 444 hp. The video acts as a glaring example of the risks of driving dangerously when behind the wheel and why unlicensed, underage youngsters should not be allowed to drive under any circumstance. The police department advises all parents to use the video and have a discussion with their minor children on the importance of road safety.

Title: Eight members of Rajasthan family killed before completion of their holy trip to Ajmer Sharif; two others injured
content: JAIPUR: Eight members of a family of Phagi village situated in the outskirts of Jaipur were killed and one child was injured when a truck coming from opposite direction fell down onto their moving car near Ramnagar in Dudu area on Jaipur-Ajmer highway on Thursday noon. All of them were on their way to Ajmer, situated Dargah of Khawaja Moinudhin Chisthy, however they could never reach their destination. The car under the truck was badly smashed and the bodies got glued on the road. A biker was also injured in the accident.The incident took place at 12.30pm when a truck coming from Ajmer side got its tyre burst and lost control. Thereafter, the truck fell down onto a moving Altro car in which family of nine were travelling to Jaipur, police said.â€œSome locals intimated us about the accident. With the help of cranes the truck was removed from the site. Bodies were beyond identification and got glued on the road. The driver of the truck managed to flee from the scene,â€ said Rajiv Pachar, Superintendent of police, Jaipur(Rural) said.. .AdvertisementA large number of policemen and rescue team reached at the spot. â€œBodies got cut into pieces. However one child identified as Arman(5) years got saved as he has sustained injuries and was rushed to the SMS hospital for the treatment. The relatives of this family have been intimated,â€ Pachar added.A biker also got injured in the accident was also been rushed to the hospital.It was a horrifying scene even for the police team that were collecting the pieces of the bodies. â€œThe car is fully damaged and we just got the number plate intact through which we could make the identification of vehicle and further of the deceased,â€ said Dinesh Sharma, additional SP, Jaipur(Rural)Meanwhile locals alleged that the construction work of at least six bridges is on the national highway connecting Ajmer to Jaipur side and this has led to the accident.Babu Lal Nagar, MLA from Dudu said, â€œWe will have a word with the construction company to take all the precautions and should not spill over boulders and other raw materials on the road. The deceased would be given compensation as per government norms.â€The deceased were identified as Haseena(45), Israel(29) the wife and son respectively of one Hanif, Farzana(27) wife of Israel and Rohina(8) daughter of Israel, Murad son of Hanif, Shaqeel(45), Sonu(14) and Seran(3). However, Arman(5) who too was traveling with this family in the car got injured.

Title: 10 of family among 11 killed in truck-SUV collision in Balod
content: Raipur: Eleven people, including two children and five women, were killed in a horrific road accident in Chhattisgarhâ€™s Balod on Wednesday night when a truck slammed head-on into the packed Bolero.Barring the driver, all the others were from the same family headed for a wedding. One of the victims was decapitated in the collision. The two dead kids were aged 1.5 years and 3 years.All the victims were from a family in Soram-Bhatgaon village in Dhamtari. They were around 15km from their village at 10.15pm when a truck carrying iron rods slammed into them on NH-30 near Jagtara village in Purur area, some 80km from Raipur.The impact was so fierce that 10 of the 11 people in the SUV died on the spot. The infant was the lone survivor when police and villagers arrived on the scene minutes later. He died while being taken to Raipur for treatment.The truck driver fled, and police teams are looking for him, said Purur SHO Arun Kumar Sahu. The bodies were sent for postmortem.The deceased were identified as Dharmraj Sahu, 55, his wife Usha Bai, 52, Keshav, 34, his wife Tomin, 33, Laxmi, 45, Rama, 20, Shailendra, 22, Sandhya, 24, Ishant, 1.5 years, Yogyansh, 3 years, and the driver of the vehicle 19-year-old Damesh Dhruw. Chief minister Bhupesh Baghel has expressed profound grief over the accident.

Title: Five killed after truck rams into auto on Lucknow-Bahraich road in UP
content: LUCKNOW: At least five people were killed whereas 10 others suffered injuries after a tipper truck rammed into an auto in UP's Bahraich district on Friday.The victims were returning from a "Tilak" ceremony. The accident took place in the Kaiserganj police station area of the district. Police said that the identities of the victims were yet to be ascertained.SHO, Kaiserganj, Daddan Singh, said that the accident took place at around 3am."The victims were returning home from a ceremony when the accident took place on Lucknow-Bahraich road near a private hospital," said Singh.. .AdvertisementThe injured were admitted to the district hospital.

Title: 3 killed, 7 injured in AOB accident
content: Visakhapatnam: Three persons, including a child, were killed and seven others suffered injuries in a road accident after a tractor trolley they were travelling in overturned near Kharimal along Andhra-Odisha border. The mishap occurred when fifteen people were returning after attending the last rites of a relative. The injured persons were admitted to a hospital in Odisha and the bodies were shifted for post-mortem. TNN

Title: 8 of a family killed as truck overturns on car on Jaipur-Ajmer highway
content: JAIPUR: Eight members of a family from Phagi village in the outskirts of Jaipur were killed and one child injured when tyres of truck burst and the vehicle crashed into a car coming in the opposite direction near Ramnagar in Dudu on Jaipur-Ajmer highway on Thursday.The family was on their way to Ajmer dargah when the accident took place at 12.30pm. The driver of the truck, coming from the Ajmer side, lost control of the vehicle after the tyre burst and overturned on the car in which nine from the family were travelling. A biker also got injured in the accident.The deceased were identified as Haseena (45), Israel (29), wife and son, respectively, one Hanif; Farzana (27), wife of Israel; Rohina (8), daughter of Israel; Murad, son of Hanif; Shaqeel (45); Sonu(14); and Seran (3)."Some local people informed us about the accident. With the help of cranes, the truck was removed. The bodies were beyond identification and had got glued on the road. The driver of the truck managed to flee from the scene," said Jaipur (Rural) superintendent of police Rajiv Pachar.. .AdvertisementA large posse of policemen and the rescue team reached the spot immediately after the accident. "Bodies were cut into pieces. However, one child, identified as Arman (5), who sustained injuries was rushed to the SMS Hospital for treatment and saved," Pachar said. The biker who got injured was also rushed to the hospital."The car is fully damaged and we identified the vehicle with its number plate, deceased," said Jaipur (Rural) additional SP Dinesh Sharma.Meanwhile, locals blamed the construction work going on at least six bridges on the national highway for the accident. Dudu MLA Babu Lal Nagar said, "We will have a word with the construction company to take precautions while keeping boulders and other raw material used for road construction. The deceased would be given compensation as per government norms."

Title: 3-yr-old survives in bike-car crash, 2 die
content: Bengaluru: A 51-year-old man and his daughter, 22, were killed after their bike collided with a car in Devanahalli on Wednesday.The victims are Ramanjinappa and Archana R, residents of Eliyur village near Devanahalli. Archanaâ€™s daughter Ruthwi, 3, escaped with minor injuries in the accident that took place at 1pm. Police said Ramanjinappa was riding his bike on Gokare Road, Archana was on the pillion, and Ruthwi sitting between them. A car was coming at speed from the opposite direction and the vehicles collided. The car driver left the vehicle behind and fled.3 die in 3 more accidentsA food-delivery agent, an autorickshaw driver, and an elderly pedestrian were killed in three accidents since Wednesday midnight. The first was at Kamakshipalya, Nagarabavai II Stage, when food-delivery agent Nagappa Ganganalli, 30, riding pillion on a scooter, died when it hit a parked truck around 12.15am. Siddappa Sharanappa Devura, who was riding the scooter, sustained injuries.. .AdvertisementThe second accident was near Laggere in Nandini Layout. A speeding mini-goods truck collided with an autorickshaw, killing its driver Yogesh Kumar, 20, of Banashankari. A passenger in Yogeshâ€™s auto escaped with minor injuries. The mini-goods vehicle driver is absconding.The third accident was on RV Road near Jayanagar when a speeding auto knocked down 65-year-old pedestrian Anjalamma at 5.45am. She was crossing the road to visit a temple when the auto, driven by AR Babu, rammed her. The driver has been arrested.

Title: Delivery executive dead as woman techie runs amok in car at Hyderabad's Alwal
content: HYDERABAD: A food delivery executive was killed and another biker hurt when a car driven by a woman techie ran amok at Dairy Farm road in Alwal on Wednesday night. After hitting the two bikers, the woman rammed into a sugarcane juice cart, a fast food stall and an electric pole before the car finally came to a halt. Police have booked a case against the techie, A Shivani, from Kanajiguda. While police suspect that the techie accidentally pressed the accelerator instead of brake after the initial contact with the bikes, the woman claimed she had become dizzy as her blood pressure had dropped. The woman has a valid licence and was not drunk, police said.Shivani, who is set to get married in a few days, was coming back from a tailor with two other people when the accident happened around 10.20 pm, police said. . .Advertisement"The deceased, Rasapuram Raju, 31, from Gandhi Colony in Secunderabad, suffered severe injuries and was declared brought dead at a nearby hospital. The other biker, Raj Kumar, a private employee, also suffered bleeding injuries. Luckily, no one else was hurt as the road side stall was closed. But the cement electric pole was damaged," Alwal SI Ramulu said.Immediately after the incident, police reached the place and seized the Kia car which still has a temporary registration.Shivani was reportedly taken into custody and a case was booked against her under IPC sections 304-a (causing death due to rash or negligent act) and 337 (causing injuries due to rash or negligent act).

Title: Three killed, six hurt in two separate Barmer accidents
content: JAISALMER: Three persons died and six were injured in two road accidents in Barmer district.In the first accident in RJD police station area on Thursday evening, five persons were going to attend a social programme in an Sports Utility Vehicle (SUV). On the way, the vehicle lost control and overturned in a slope near Khadali village. Two persons died on the spot while three were injured. Police sent the injured to Gudamalani hospital. The bodies were handed over to family members after post-mortem.The deceased were identified a Sawai Ram and Poonmaram while Dugarchand, Lakshman and Virdharam were injured.. .AdvertisementIn another accident on mega highway near Bandiyawas on Friday morning, a car overturned killing a person and leaving three injured.The deceased was identified as Vijayraj, a resident of Sayla, Jalore.

Title: Passenger dies in Delhi, driver hurt after tempo rammed by bus
content: NEW DELHI: A man was killed and another was critically wounded after a private school bus allegedly rammed into a tempo that turned upside down after the incident that took place in east Delhi on the Noida-Link Road near the Mayur Vihar Flyover, police said on Friday.The incident took place on Thursday. According to police, the deceased has been identified as Anis alias Irfan. The injured tempo driver, Pramod, has been referred to RML hospital. Based on his statement, a case of death due to negligence and other relevant sections of the IPC was registered. The bus driver, Yusuf, has been arrested, police said. . .Advertisement

Title: Sector magistrate's car carrying ballot boxes kills four in UP's Amroha
content: BIJNOR: A tragic incident occurred late Thursday night when a car carrying ballot boxes for civic polls hit a bike on the Sambhal-Saidnangli road close to Ujhari in Amroha district. The collision resulted in the death of four of a family, including two children, who were riding the bike. The car had 'sector magistrate' written on it, but it is unclear whether the official was present in the vehicle during the accident.The deceased have been identified as Phool Singh (40), his wife Poonam Devi (35), and his two daughters Sheetal (4) and Doli (3). The family hailed from Sebada village in Sambhal district. Phool Singh was returning to his village after visiting Hasanpur with his wife and three kids when the accident happened. The third child on the bike, who is one year old, was rescued from the scene."The driver of the car, identified as Himachal, has been detained and booked under section 304A of the IPC. The car, which was privately hired by the administration for civic polls, has been seized. Police officials are investigating the matter and trying to determine if the sector magistrate was inside the car at the time of the accident," Saidnangli SHO Sandeep Chaudhary said. There were no ballot boxes inside the car when the accident happened, he added.Circle officer Swetabh Bhaskar said that the bodies of the deceased have been sent for autopsy. The sector magistrate is an electoral post assigned during polls to manage a particular area. Sources said that the official could be anyone from a tehsildar upwards who is given special powers to oversee designated areas and confiscate unlicensed campaign materials.

Title: UP: Five killed as truck hits auto in Bahraich
content: BAHRAICH: Five persons were killed and ten sustained injuries when a truck rammed into an autorickshaw in Bahraich on Friday.All the victims were occupants of the auto and were returning from a 'tilak' ceremony when the accident took place at 3.00 am under the Kaiserganj police station. The deceased were identified as Bhagwan Prasad (45), Anil Lal (13), Khushboo (24), Harish Chandra (38) and Jai Karan (35). Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath directed officials to reach the spot and ensure best possible medical assistance to the injured. SHO, Kaiserganj, Daddan Singh said: "One Mansharam and his relatives had gone to Ruknapur village on an auto for the 'tilak' ceremony. While returning, a tipper truck coming from the Bahraich side hit their auto, carrying 15 passengers, near a private hospital on Lucknow-Bahraich road.". .AdvertisementSingh said that such was the impact that the auto was reduced to a pulp. The driver of the truck fled away after the accident.The SHO said that a police team reached the spot and rushed all the injured to hospital where five were declared brought dead. The injured were admitted to the district hospital.District magistrate, Bahraich, Dinesh Chandra, too, reached the hospital along with additional district magistrate Anirudh Pratap Singh and inquired about the well-being of the injured.

Title: UP: Five members of marriage party killed, over 15 injured in accident in Jalaun
content: KANPUR: Five members of a marriage party were killed and over 15 others sustained injuries when a bus overturned and fell into a ditch after being allegedly hit by a speeding dumper near Gopalpura under the Madhogarh police station area of Jalaun district on Saturday night.The collision was so severe that the entire roof of the bus ripped apart and fell on the other side of the road.The police identified the deceased as Kuldeep Singh (36), Raghunandan (46), Sirobhan (65), Karan Singh (34) and Vikas (32).According to police, â€œThere were 40 members of a marriage party in the bus, out of which, five people have died while more than 15 people were injured when it overturned and fell into a ditch after being hit by a dumper.". .AdvertisementPolice sources meanwhile informed that Sunil and Pramod, sons of Mulcharan Pal, residents of Madeira, were married to Sandhya and Upasana, daughters of Ramdhani of Rampura. "The accident took place while all the 'baratis' (members of the groom's side) were returning to Madeira in a bus after attending the wedding in Rampura on Saturday night. It was when the bus had reached Gopalpura, a speeding dumper coming from the opposite side hit it. Following which the bus overturned and fell into a roadside ditch," informed police sources further.Later the local people and passers-by informed the police.The police reached the spot and launched a massive rescue operation and pulled out the 'baratis' trapped in the bus and admitted them to Madhogarh Community Health Center for treatment. The doctors however declared Kuldeep Singh, Raghunandan, Sirobhan, Karan Singh and Vikas dead.Thereafter, many others critically injured were referred to Orai Medical College. DM Chandni Singh reached the medical college to take note of the condition of the injured people.Superintendent of police Jalaun Iraj Raja, who reached the spot on the information of the accident said, "We got information about the accident at around 2 o'clock in the night. After this ambulance and policemen were sent. The dead bodies are being sent for postmortem. The offender driver along with the vehicle fled after the accident. He is being traced from the CCTV installed nearby."

Title: 10 dead, 13 injured in minivan-truck collision in UP's Moradabad
content: BAREILLY: Ten people, including three children, were killed and 13 others were injured, some critical, after a minivan carrying 26 passengers collided head-on with a speeding truck driving recklessly on the wrong side of the road.The passengers were on their way to Rampur in Uttar Pradesh for a wedding function.Both the vehicles turned turtle after the accident, which took place near Khairkhata village under Bhagatpur police area of Moradabad district on Sunday afternoon.. .AdvertisementFour persons died on the spot while four others were declared dead on arrival at the hospital. Two others succumbed to injuries at a private hospital later.The death toll may rise further as the condition of several of the injured is said to be critical, police said.Chief development officer, Moradabad, Sumit Yadav, who rushed to the accident site along with police, told TOI, â€œWe are monitoring the condition of the survivors. Eight people had died initially. Seven of the critically injured were referred to a private hospital, three others to another hospital and five were taken to district hospital. During treatment, two of the survivors passed away. We have sent the bodies for autopsyâ€.The incident took place in a remote part of the village and hence it took time to shift the injured to hospitals, an official said.All the minivan victims were residents of Bhojpur area in Moradabad. The vehicle was booked by one Mohammed Abbas to attend the wedding of his sisterâ€™s daughter and son on the same day. Police are in the process of identifying the victims at the time of filing this report.SSP, Moradabad, Hemraj Meena said, â€œThe truck's driver fled after the incident. Investigation is onâ€.Mahmood Ali, an eyewitness, informed TOI, â€œThe truck actually came from the wrong side at a high speed and collided with the mini-van head-on. The victims suffered mainly head injuries. Police arrived on time and carried out rescue work but there was a delay in the availability of ambulancesâ€.

Title: BSF personnel killed, six others injured after vehicle careens into gorge in J&K's Poonch district
content: JAMMU: A BSF personnel was killed while six others were injured after the vehicle they were travelling in met with an accident in Mankote sector of Jammu and Kashmir's Poonch district on Sunday."A BSF vehicle Tata-407 (HR39 A 8662) skidded off the road and fell into a 250-feet deep gorge, resulting in injuries to seven personnel," said sources."On getting information, locals, BSF and police launched a rescue operation and evacuated the injured to a nearby health facility where one of the injured, identified as Constable Ram Chandran of 158 battalion BSF, succumbed to his injuries," they said."The injured have been identified as Feroz Ahmed, Sanjay Sarkar, Karamjeet Singh, Ajay Singh, Devender Singh - all constables, and driver/constable Emdadul Haque," the sources said, adding that the police have registered a case and launched an investigation.. .Advertisement

Title: Woman on bike taxi killed in road accident in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: A 34-year-old woman riding pillion on a bike taxi died near Teynampet on Sunday after a lorry rammed the bike. The deceased was identified as Sevika, 34, a make-up artist at a television news channel. Police said she was going to her parentsâ€™ house to seek their blessings on her birthday at the time of the accident. She was not wearing a helmet. Police are yet to identify the lorry driver. The bike rider escaped with minor injuries and is admitted at the government hospital. Police said Sevika had gone to her friendâ€™s house in T Nagar on Saturday night to celebrate her birthday. . .AdvertisementAround 4.30 am on Sunday, she booked a bike taxi and was on her way to her house in Vyasarpadi. A man identified as S Anandan was driving the two-wheeler. Polcie said the bike driver did not offer her a helmet when he started the trip.When they were travelling on Anna Salai, a lorry trailing their bike crashed into the vehicle. Due to the impact, both Sevika and Anandan were thrown off the bike. Sevika sustained severe head injuries and collapsed on the spot. Anandan, who was wearing a helmet, escaped with minor injuries. The lorry sped past them without stopping. Passersby noticed the duo and alerted â€˜108â€™ ambulance. Police rushed them to the Government Royapettah Hospital, where doctors declared Sevika dead-on-arrival. Anandan was admitted to the hospital for treatment. Upon information, the Pondy Bazaar Traffic Investigation Wing (TIW) police sent the body for post-mortem, and have launched a hunt for the lorry driver. They are tracking the vehicle with the help of CCTV footage.

Title: Biker killed in hit-and-run in Navi Mumbai
content: THANE: A 28-year-old biker was knocked down and killed by a car on Palm Beach Road in Navi Mumbai on Sunday, police said. The incident took place on the intervening night of Saturday and Sunday, an official from NRI Sagari police station said, adding, victim Pukharn Gulab Sharif, a resident of Koparkharine, sustained severe injuries, and succumbed this morning at the hospital. An offence under the relevant IPC sections has been registered against the errant car driver, the official said.

Title: Bus hit by dumper topples in Uttar Pradesh's Jalaun, five killed
content: KANPUR: Five passengers were killed and over 17 others sustained injuries when a bus carrying a wedding party, which was hit by a speeding dumper, overturned and fell into a ditch, near Gopalpura of Jalaun district late on Saturday. The collision was so severe that the roof of the bus was ripped apart and fell on the other side of the road.Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath expressed his condolences to the family members of the deceased and directed district authorities to provide proper treatment and all possible help to the injured. He wished the injured a speedy recovery.Police identified the deceased as Kuldeep Singh, 36, Raghunandan, 46, Sirobhan, 65, Karan Singh, 34 and bus driver Vikas, 32. Police said 40 members of a wedding party were traveling in the bus. Police said 40 members of a marriage party were traveling in the bus, out of whom, four persons besides the bus driver died, while 17 were injured when the bus overturned and fell into a ditch.Jalaun SP Iraj Raja said, "When we got information about the accident at 2 am, we sent ambulances and policemen for rescue. The bodies are being sent for postmortem. The driver of the dumper fled along with the vehicle and we are trying to trace him on the basis of CCTV footage."Police said Sunil and Pramod, sons of Mulcharan Pal, residents of Mandela got married to Sandhya and Upasana, daughters of Ramdhani of Rampura. "The accident took place when all the 'baraatis' (members of the groom's side) were returning to Mandela after the wedding in Rampura on Saturday night. As the bus reached Gopalpura, ut was hit by a speeding dumper from the opposite side and it overturned and fell into a roadside ditch," said police who reached the spot on being informed by the locals and passers-by.Police pulled out the dead and the injured trapped inside the bus and admitted the wounded passengers to Madhogarh Community Health Center. Doctors declared Kuldeep Singh, Raghunandan, Sirobhan, Karan Singh and Vikas dead.Several other who were critically injured were referred to Orai Medical College. Jalaun district magistrate Chandni Singh reached the medical college to take note of the condition of the injured people.National president of Samajwadi Party and former chief minister Akhilesh Yadav, expressed grief over the death of five persons and expressed condolences to the bereaved families. He demanded a compensation of Rs 1 crore each to families of all those killed in the accident and financial assistance of Rs 10 lakh each to the injured for their treatment. He said neglect by the administration and transport department is causing road accidents every day. "The government should make people strictly follow the traffic rules in order to prevent such accidents," he said.

Title: Bus carrying marriage party hit by dumper, 5 killed, 17 hurt
content: KANPUR: Five passengers were killed and 17 others sustained injuries after a bus carrying a wedding party was hit by a speeding dumper in UPâ€™s Jalaun district on Saturday.The collision was so severe that the bus overturned and fell into a ditch, while the roof of the bus was ripped apart and fell on the other side of road.UP CM Yogi Adityanath expressed his condolences to family members of the deceased and directed district authorities to provide proper treatment and all possible help to the injured.Iraj Raja, SP of Jalaun, said: â€œWhen we got information about the accident at 2am, we sent ambulances and cops for rescue operations. Bodies of the victims are being sent for postmortem. The driver of the dumper fled along with the vehicle and we are trying to trace him.â€ â€œThe accident took place when all the â€˜baraatisâ€™ were returning to Mandela after a wedding in Rampura on Saturday night,â€ he said.. .Advertisement

Title: One dead, two injured in accident on Trichy-Karur NH
content: Trichy: A 33-year-old man riding a two-wheeler was killed and his 26-year-old pillion rider was injured in a road accident on Trichy-Karur NH on Saturday. The victimâ€™s bike was rammed by a speeding car approaching the highway from the opposite direction.Police identified the deceased as C Manikandan, a native of Woraiyur. Manikandan along with his friend, identified as T Vimalkumar of Pandamangalam, was riding towards Trichy on Trichy-Karur NH. While nearing the Kadiyakurichi bus stop, a car approaching from the opposite direction hit the victimâ€™s two-wheeler.Manikandan sustained severe head injuries and was rushed to MGMGH in Trichy. However, doctors declared him as dead on arrival. Vimalkumar is undergoing treatment in a private hospital for injuries. Based on a complaint, Jeeyapuram police identified the driver of the car that caused the fatal accident as V Mohan of Tiruppur. Police added that there was another occupant in the car identified as D Suryaprakash who had sustained minor injuries and was being treated at a private hospital in Trichy. Further investigations are on.

Title: 10 dead, 13 injured in minivan-truck collision in UP
content: BAREILLY: Ten people, including three children, were killed and 13 others were injured, some critically, after a minivan carrying 26 passengers collided head-on with a speeding truck driving recklessly on the wrong side of the road. The passengers were on their way to Rampur for a wedding function. Both the vehicles turned turtle after the accident, which took place near Khairkhata village under Bhagatpur police limits of Moradabad district on Sunday afternoon. Four persons died on the spot while four others were declared dead on arrival at the hospital. Two others succumbed at a private hospital later.Passengers were on their way to Rampur for wedding functionThe death toll may rise further as the condition of several of the injured is said to be critical, police said. Chief development officer, Moradabad, Sumit Yadav, who rushed to the accident site along with police, told TOI, â€œWe are monitoring the condition of the survivors. Eight people had died initially. Seven of the critically injured were referred to a private hospital, three others to another hospital and five were taken to district hospital. During treatment, two of the survivors passed away. We have sent the bodies for autopsy.â€ The incident took place in a remote part of the village and hence it took time to shift the injured to hospitals, said an official. All the minivan victims were residents of Bhojpur area in Moradabad. The vehicle was booked by one Mohammed Abbas to attend the wedding of his sisterâ€™s daughter and son on the same day. Police are in the process of identifying the victims at the time of filing this report. SSP, Moradabad, Hemraj Meena said, â€œThe truck's driver fled after the incident. Investigation is on.â€Mahmood Ali, an eyewitness, informed TOI, â€œThe truck actually came from the wrong side at a high speed and collided with the minivan head-on. The victims suffered mainly head injuries. Police arrived on time and carried out rescue work but there was a delay in the availability of ambulances."

Title: 2 killed in road accidents in rural Nashik
content: Nashik: Two bikers were killed in separate road accidents in rural Nashik on Sunday. A 55-year-old biker died when another bike crashed into him from behind on the Nashik-Pune Road in Sinnar MIDC area around 6pm. The victim was identified as Annasaheb Nehe of Akole in Ahmednagar district. His relative Shashikala Tambe (40) from Gonde in Sinnar taluka also suffered serious injuries in the accident. The other fatal accident took place on the Wani-Pimpalgaon Road late in the night. The victim, Balu Jadhav (60), a resident of Chandwad, was going from Wani towards Pimpalgaon around 11pm when he rammed into a bike in front of him and suffered serious head injuries. He later succumbed to the injuries.The police have registered first information reports and are conducting investigations in both cases.

Title: Siblings die in road accident
content: Kullu: A youth and his elder brother were killed when the motorcycle they were riding collided head on with a car on Saturday night near Hatli village of Chamba district. The deceased have been identified as Kunal, 20, and Vishal, 22, both residents of Hatli village. According to police, the accident victims were taken to a hospital in Shahpur from where doctors referred them to the Tanda Medical College and Hospital in Kangra. While Kunal died on Saturday night, Vishal succumbed to his injuries on Sunday at the hospital. The police have registered a case. tnn

Title: Four, including pedestrian, killed in accidents
content: Nagpur: Four persons, including a pedestrian, were killed in separate accidents at different locations on Sunday in the city.Nisha Hiranwar was injured after the bike of her brother Vipin Siriah was dashed from behind at GPO Square. Hiranwar, riding pillion, was declared dead at a hospital. An offence was registered against motorist Aditya Tabhane at Sadar police station.In another incident, 28-year-old biker Surendra Zhariya lost control over his two-wheeler on Wanadongri Road, and sustained a head injury after suffering a fall. He was declared dead. A case of accidental death was registered at the Wadi police station.In another incident, pillion 65-year-old Kailash Chaware was declared dead at a hospital after his bike was hit by a tanker on Manewada Ring Road. His friend, Rameshwar Deotale, was injured too. Hudkeshwar police have booked a tanker driver after the accident.. .AdvertisementRameshwar Lonare, 36, was killed after being hit by a truck at 8th Mile. He was crossing the road. Wadi police registered an offence against the trucker Azhar Baig. tnn

Title: 3 die in accident in Nandurbar
content: Nashik: Three people were killed and two others injured in a collision between a truck and pick-up van at Prakasha village in Nandurbar district around 6.30am on Sunday. The pick-up, loaded with foodgrain for a wedding ceremony, was going towards Gujarat from Madhya Pradesh, when it collided with an oncoming truck around 6.30am. Three of the five people in the pick-up were killed on the spot, and two others seriously injured. The latter were immediately rushed to Nandurbar civil hospital for treatment. The truck driver and his assistant fled the spot. Among the deceased, two hailed from Gujarat and one from Madhya Pradesh. They were identified as Hemraj Anjane (39) and Manoj Ghatya (42) of Surat, and Bhagwanbhai Govind Panchule (48) of Gondia. The Prakasha police are investigating the case. Superintendent of police PR Patil of Nandurbar also visited the accident spot. The impact was so severe that the police had to arrange for a crane to separate the mangled pick-up vehicle from the truck and remove the victims. Foodgrains were scattered spread all over the road. Local villagers and others came together to assist the police to streamline hampered traffic.

Title: Couple killed in road accident
content: Prayagraj: A couple died in a road mishap when their bike was hit by a speeding pickup van near Phaphamau bridge under the limits of Shivkuti police station on Tuesday morning. The deceased couple has been identified as Brijesh Tripathi (48) and his wife Sangeeta (42), residents of Raibareilly. Deceased Brijesh was an ex-defence personnel. The incident, according to police, took place when the couple was on their way to their native village in Raibareilly from Naini area. Bodies have been sent for post mortem. Police have seized errant pickup van while driver managed to escape from the scene. Accident led to traffic jam the busiest phaphamau bridge on Prayagraj-Lucknow highway for hours. TNN

Title: 3 killed in accident, one victim's body dragged for 1km in Balangir
content: BALANGIR: A man and his two adolescent daughters were killed when a speeding passenger bus hit the motorcycle they were travelling in near Gaintala tehsil office on the Balangir-Subarnapur road under Puintala police station limits in Balangir district. Sources said one of the sisters fell down under the wheel of the bus after it hit the bike from behind and she was dragged for nearly half a km.The deceased were identified as Binapani Pandey (50) and his daughters Rekha (14) and Sita (17). Binapani was a marginal farmer of Brahmanipali village under Puintala block. Police said the mishap took placed around 10 am when Binapani and his daughters were going to Balangir to update their Aadhaar card details.Locals rescued Binapani and Sita and admitted them to Bhima Bhoi Medical College and Hospital in Balangir. When their condition deteriorated, they were shifted to Vimsar, Burla. Both succumbed to their injuries while undergoing treatment.Soon after the accident, locals blocked National Highway-57 near Gaintala demanding compensation to the family members of the deceased and stringent action against the driver. They blocked the road for more than six hours. Later, Tophan Bag, sub-divisional police officer, Balangir, and the Puintala tehsildar reached the spot to pacify the protesters. The deceased's family was granted Rs 50,000 compensation by the district administration. Police have arrested bus driver Jagyenswar Dash and initiated an investigation into the incident.

Title: 3 Muzaffarpur vendors crushed to death by truck
content: PATNA: Three roadside vendors were killed and one was injured when a sand-laden truck ploughed into a market and mowed them down on NH-102 (Muzaffarpur-Chhapra main road) at Rewa Ghat under the Saraiya police station in Muzaffarpur district around 6.30am on Wednesday.The victims were identified as Akash Kumar, Mahfuz Alam and Neeraj Kumar. The injured person, Javed Alam, was admitted to a local hospital in a critical condition.According to police, the truck driver lost control over the vehicle and it rammed into a "mandi" where the vendors were selling fruits. The driver, however, managed to escape after the accident.

Title: Two killed in separate road accidents in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: Two people were killed in separate incidents at Thiruvottiyur and Thirumullaivoyal. In one of the incidents, a woman who was riding her bike died while her daughter narrowly escaped in the accident at Thirumullaivoyal.In the accident at Thirumullaivoyal, the deceased, identified as M Bhagya Latha alias Latha, 50, of Devi Nagar in Annanur near Avadi, was riding on a scooter with her daughter Sherin Gilda, 23, from Thirumullaivoyal to Choolaimedu. A lorry laden with bricks hit a car and then hit Latha's scooter. In this impact, she fell to the ground. The truck that was trailing the scooter ran over Latha, killing her on the spot. Police registered a case and arrested the truck driver Ezhumalai, 39. In another accident, Ragu, 19, a native of Rajasthan, who was employed at an electronics shop, was run over by an MTC bus, route number 56-D. The police have arrested driver Suresh, 46, of Kanyakumari by the Thiruvottiyur traffic investigation wing police. Further investigations are on.

Title: Two killed in separate road accidents
content: Chennai: Two people were killed in separate incidents at Thiruvottiyur and Thirumullaivoyal. In one of the incidents, a woman who was riding her bike died while her daughter narrowly escaped in the accident at Thirumullaivoyal.In the accident at Thirumullaivoyal, the deceased, identified as M Bhagya Latha alias Latha, 50, of Devi Nagar in Annanur near Avadi, was riding on a scooter with her daughter Sherin Gilda, 23, from Thirumullaivoyal to Choolaimedu. A lorry laden with bricks hit a car and then hit Lathaâ€™s scooter. In this impact, she fell to the ground. The truck that was trailing the scooter ran over Latha, killing her on the spot. Police registered a case and arrested the truck driver Ezhumalai, 39. In another accident, Ragu, 19, a native of Rajasthan, who was employed at an electronics shop, was run over by an MTC bus, route number 56-D. The police have arrested driver Suresh, 46, of Kanyakumari by the Thiruvottiyur traffic investigation wing police. Further investigations are on.

Title: 3 children waiting for school bus killed by speeding car in Agra
content: AGRA: Three children died and three others were critically injured after a speeding car ran over them while they were waiting on the roadside for the school bus to pick them up in Dauki area of Agra's Bas Mahapat village on Thursday morning. According to police, the children were standing on the side of Fatehabad road when a car hit them, killing two of them on the spot. Another child died in hospital. The deceased were identified as Deepti Singh (15), her brother Arvind Singh (8) and their cousin Pragya Singh (9). Their bodies were sent for post-mortem. Saurabh Singh, assistant commissioner of police, Fatehabad, said, "Those injured have been identified as Gunjan, Lavinia and Aman (all in the 7-10 age group), and they are admitted to BR hospital." Dauki police station SHO Sher Singh said, "We have arrested the driver and seized the vehicle. A case has been registered." Satyawan Singh, a cousin of the victims, said, "All the victims were from a joint family of farmers. Four people were traveling in the car and they were drunk." After the incident, villagers staged a protest in the area demanding stringent action against the accused driver. Senior police officials reached the spot and tried to pacify the agitated locals, and instructed the area police to take all necessary steps in the matter.

Title: Two killed in accident on outskirts of Bengaluru, but family alleges murder plot
content: BENGALURU: Two persons were killed on the spot and another man sustained severe injuries in a collision between a car and a scooter at Kakolu village near Rajanukunte, on the outskirts of Bengaluru, on Thursday morning. But things took a curious turn by noon as family members of one of the deceased alleged that it was a case of murder.The deceased are Nagaraju, 38, and Ramaiah, 48, while the injured is Gopal, 33, all residents of Challahalli village. They worked at the Karnataka Milk Federationâ€™s cattle feed plant in Rajanukunte.The incident occurred around 5.30am when they were on their way to work. Nagaraju was riding his scooter, with Ramaiah and Gopal on the pillion. Bharath Gowda, 28, a resident of Haniyur, a neighbouring village, was on his way to home in his car. The car and scooter collided and the trio on the two-wheeler fell on the road. Two of them died on the spot. Gopal was rushed to a hospital and admitted for treatment. â€œGopal has sustained a leg fracture and his condition is stable,â€ said a senior cop. Bharath was accompanied by his friends Vinay and Nishanth in the car at the time of the accident.Argument over waterAccording to police, Nagaraju was part of campaigning for a candidate contesting from Yelahanka constituency a few days ago. He belonged to an SC community and had a sip of water in a hotel at Hesaraghatta about 20 days ago. Bharath, who is from an upper caste, allegedly objected to him drinking water in the hotel where he was having lunch. This led to a verbal altercation and Bharath allegedly threatened to kill Nagaraju once the elections were over. Now, Nagarajuâ€™s kin have alleged that Bharath intentionally hit the scooter. â€œMy brother-in-law informed me about the clash between him and Bharath. Today, Bharath carried out his threat and killed Nagaraju,â€ said Venkatesh, Nagarajuâ€™s relative.Bengaluru superintendent of police Mallikarjun Baldandi told TOI that Rajanukunte police have registered a murder and SC/ST atrocity case against Bharath based on the complaint filed by Nagarajuâ€™s family. â€œWe have appointed KS Nagaraj, deputy superintendent of police (Doddaballapur sub-division), as investigating officer. Probe will reveal whether it was a murder or just an accident,â€ said Baldandi.According to police sources, they have picked up Bharath and his two friends for interrogation.

Title: Man killed in accident on expressway
content: Ahmedabad: A 40-year-old man from New Ranip died while driving his car on the Vadodara-Ahmedabad expressway, ostensibly as he dozed off and hit the road divider. In the policeâ€™s usual practice, Vivekanandnagar police booked the deceased for his own death.Rather than investigating the exact cause, such as whether an animal came onto the road, or faulty road design, police usually book the deceased to avoid a lengthy legal procedure and reduce their work. Deenbandhu Prajapati, 40, a resident of Satyam Home Society near GST Mill Compound in New Ranip, was driving his car with his wife Mittal and their two children from Bharuch to Ahmedabad. Deenbandhuâ€™s uncle, Nayan Prajapati, in his FIR with Vivekanandnagar police said he got a call from Mittal who said they had met with an accident near Vanch village on the expressway. Nayan said Mittal told him that Deenbandhu had probably nodded off while driving. tnn

Title: 6-year-old boy killed, vice-principal mom injured in accident
content: LUDHIANA: A 6-year-old boy was killed while his mother suffered severe injuries when a speeding truck hit their scooter near Vardhman Chowk on Chandigarh road on Friday morning. The woman â€” Monika Oberoi (40) â€” is the vice-principal of a private school where his son, Vivan Oberoi, studied in nursery. They were going to the school when the accident occurred. While the child on the spot, his mother suffered severe leg injuries. Quoting the doctors, the police said the woman might lose both her legs.On getting information, Monikaâ€™s husband Sumit Oberoi, who works at a factory, and his relative rushed to the spot. Monika was taken to a nearby private hospital where her condition is stated to be critical.Deepak Rana, a schoolteacher, said he had reached the school when some parents told him about an accident near Vardhman Chowk. On getting information, they all rushed to the spot and noticed that Monika was injured and her son was killed. An ambulance was called and they were taken to the hospital.. .AdvertisementNandji Rai, a street vendor near Vardhman chowk, an eyewitness said the woman was riding the scooter while her son was riding pillion. As soon as the woman slowed down her scooter to take a turn, a truck hit the bike. The truck ran over the toddlerâ€™s head and womanâ€™s legs.The truck driver tried to escape, but onlookers nabbed him and informed the police.Inspector Bikramjit Singh, SHO, Jamalpur police station said the police have seized truck PB-13-AR-8191 and its driver Palwinder Singh of Goindwal of Tarntaran has been arrested under Sections 279 (rash driving), 304-A (causing death by negligence), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or person safety of others), 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 427 (mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the IPC.

Title: Sikar girl found dead, family says murder, cops term it accident
content: JAIPUR: A 17-year-old Class 12 girl who had gone to her friendâ€™s house was found dead under mysterious circumstances on a road under Laxmangarh police station area of Sikar on Thursday evening. The family has alleged that the girl was drugged, raped and later murdered. The family has also accused her woman friend who took her along and filed a named FIR against four persons. Police, however, said that matter primarily appears to be an accident.Her family members alleged that she was taken to a resort where she was raped. â€œPrima facie it appears to be a case of accident. The girl was on a bike with a boy and was wearing a burqa when the accident took place. But whether she was raped earlier will be investigated. Everything will be clear after post-mortem,â€ said Sikar SP Karan Sharma. The matter came to light when three minor boys had taken the girl to hospital in Laxmangarh on Thursday afternoon. â€œThey only called up the family members stating that the girl was killed in the accident. We will also go through the detailed medical report,â€ said Sharma. â€œWe are going through the CCTV footage of the place where the body of the girl was found. Two minors, who according to the family are suspects, are being interrogated,â€ he added. . .Advertisement

Title: Himachal Pradesh: Five people killed in road accident in Dharamshala
content: SHIMLA: Five persons, including three from a family, were killed while three others were injured in an accident in Himachal Pradesh's Kangra district. on Sunday afternoon.The accident took place at Rasehar village of Uthragran gram panchayat in Dharamshala assembly constituency, when the truck lost control and rolled down 100 meters into a gorge. In the accident, five people traveling in the truck died while three people were seriously injured. Those injured have been admitted to the hospital for treatment.According to sources, four persons had died on the spot while another succumbed to injuries during treatment at the hospital. As soon as the information about the incident was received, the police team rushed to the spot and started further action by registering a case in connection with the incident.. .AdvertisementChief minister Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu expressed grief over the death of persons in the accident. "The government stands by the family in this hour of sorrow and directions have been issued to the district administration to provide immediate relief to the families of victims,â€ he said. He also wished speedy recovery of the injured and directed the hospital authorities to provide best possible treatment to them.

Title: 5 women workers killed in road accident in Andhra Pradeshâ€™s Kakinada district
content: KAKINADA: Five women workers were killed after an auto-rickshaw carrying them collided with a bus in Andhra Pradeshâ€™s Kakinada district on Sunday.The accident took place on the National Highway at Tallarevu in Korangi mandal.The mishap occurred when a group of 15 women workers left Tallarevu for Yanam in the auto-rickshaw following the completion of their work at a prawn company.The injured were shifted to Government General Hospital in Kakinada.. .Advertisement

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Two killed, 15 injured in road accident on Tirumala ghat road
content: TIRUPATI: Two women were killed and 15 others sustained injuries in a gory road accident reported on the down ghat road leading to Tirupati from Tirumala on Sunday.According to traffic DSP Kondaiah, a group of devotees from Anantapur completed their pilgrimage to Tirumala and were returning back to their hometown in a vehicle which they halted near the 25th turning on the down ghat road.The driver of a speeding vehicle with 15 devotees inside, lost control of the vehicle while negotiating a turn and rammed it into the Anantapur devotees vehicle from behind.The impact of the accident was so severe that two women died on the spot and 15 others sustained injuries.. .AdvertisementThe deceased were identified as Renukamma (24), a resident of D Hirehal mandal in Anantapur district and Parvathamma (70), from Medak district in Telangana.TTD's vigilance and security wing shifted the injured devotees in two ambulances to the SVRR government general hospital in Tirupati.The bodies of the deceased were shifted for postmortem and a case was registered.

Title: ITI faculty killed in hit-and-run accident
content: Trichy: A private industrial training institute (ITI) faculty member was killed in a hit-and-run incident on the Dindigul-Trichy national highway on Friday. The victim, identified as A. Sesu Sagayaraj, 56, was a resident of Malaiyadipatti near Manapparai in Trichy and worked as a typewriting tutor at Don Bosco ITI in Vannankovil, Trichy.According to police reports, Sagayaraj was hit by an unidentified vehicle from behind near Maravanur bridge in the morning. The impact threw him into the air, causing severe injuries to his right shoulder, ribs, right hip, right ear, and hands. He was immediately rushed to the government headquarters hospital in Manapparai, where he succumbed to his injuries.The victimâ€™s son, S Devasarone, 23, filed a complaint with the Manapparai police, who have launched an investigation into the incident. Despite their efforts, the identity of the vehicle and the driver could not be ascertained, and the police booked it as a hit-and-run accident.Police have checked CCTV footage in the four-way stretch and found footage of the road at Alampattipudhur, which is 6 km away from the accident spot. After conducting a post-mortem, the body was handed over to the family.TNN. .Advertisement

Title: 11-year-old killed in accident; 3 injured
content: THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: A 11-year-old boy died, and three others sustained injuries after the tempo traveller in which they were travelling was hit by a truck at Enjivila near Kaliyakkavilai on Saturday morning. The tempo traveller carrying 15 people was coming from Ernakulam for a trip to Kanyakumari. When reaching Enjivila on Kaliyakkavilai-Thiruvananthapuram road, a truck carrying fish to the city hit the tempo traveller. People, who rushed to the spot, had to break open the vehicle to rescue the travellers. The deceased has been identified by police as Abhinav son of Binukumar of Pennattu Veedu near Chenkara at Kothamangalam. The details of the injured are not yet available. The driver of the truck, Rahul, 23, a Nagercovil native has been taken into custody. Police said the truck driver reportedly dozed off which led to the accident. A case has been registered against him under section 304-A of IPC for causing death by negligence. Title: 2 of wedding party killed, 5 critical
content: Hyderabad: At least two people were killed, and more than five people suffered critical injuries in a road accident late Friday night at Rudraram, a village in Sangareddy district of Telangana.Patancheru police said that the accident took place when a DCM vehicle carrying 40 members of a marriage party rammed into a lorry while on their return post a wedding ceremony at Chitkul, in Patancheru mandal in Medak district. The two persons, S Kishtaiah and Ramulamma, died on the spot.
