While all the other injured persons were shifted to a local hospital.Police suspected that the DCM driver was drunk at the time of accident and made him undergo drunken driving test but the result turned out to be negative.DSP (Patancheru) Bheem Reddy said that it was a case of negligence which led to the accident.

Title: Six killed as truck hits pickup vehicle in Baloda Bazaar district of Chhattisgarh
content: RAIPUR: At least six persons, including a child and woman, were killed in a road accident in the Baloda Bazaar district of Chhattisgarh. According to the police, the accident took place near Goda Bridge in the Baloda Bazar district after a pickup vehicle carrying over 2 dozen people was hit by a truck leaving six dead.The accident took place under Palari police station limits. A child and woman are also among those killed in the accident, Baloda Bazar SSP Deepak Jha said.Further details on the matter are awaited.Earlier in February this year, two persons were killed and 12 others injured when a speeding tractor-trolley overturned near Sankar village in the Janjgir-Champa district of ChhattisgarhThe incident occurred near Sankar village under the Akaltara Police Station area when the tractor-trolley carrying over 30 people was returning from a fair in Parsahi Nala on the occasion of Mahashivratri.(With inputs from ANI)

Title: 7 pilgrims returning from Tirumala killed as truck rams into SUV in Andhra Pradesh's Eturu village
content: TIRUPATI: Seven people were killed and seven others severely injured when a speeding truck coming rammed into their SUV near Eturu village in Andhra Pradesh in the wee hours of Monday.The accident took place near Eturu village in Kondapuram mandal at YSR district.According to a local CI Sudarshan Prasad, a group of 14 devotees, who were all relatives from Tadipatri in Anantapur district and Bellary in Karnataka, completed their pilgrimage to Tirumala and were returning back to their natives in the SUV on Monday morning.When their vehicle reached Etruru village along the Kadapa-Tadipatri highway, a speeding truck coming in the opposite direction rammed into their vehicle at very high speed.The impact of the accident was so severe that the whole SUV got completely smashed up, leaving seven people dead on the spot.The bodies lay scattered all over the road and the injured family members who were witness to the gory mishap were inconsolable.Kondapuram police went to the spot and shifted the seven injured people to the government hospital in Tadipatri.

Title: Only breadwinner of family dies after luxury car hits his bike
content: Rajkot: A 32-year-old man was killed in a hit-and-run incident near Ramapir Chowkdi overbridge in Rajkot on Sunday morning. Police said that a speeding Mercedes-Benz car driver hit Mayur Tanna who was riding a two-wheeler, killing him on the spot.Police have booked the car driver for rash driving, causing death due to negligence and under different sections of the Motor Vehicle Act and started an investigation. The driver of the car was yet to be arrested, said police.According to police, Mayur was the breadwinner for his family. He used to take up work independently. Mayur was a resident of the Golden Portico apartment in Madhapar village on the outskirts of Rajkot city. His family includes his parents, his younger brother and his wife.Police said the car driver hit Mayurâ€™s two-wheeler near Ramapir Chowkdi overbridge at about 6.30am. Mayur died on the accident scene due to several injuries he suffered.Soon after the accident, Mayurâ€™s brother Hardik Tanna received a phone call. When Hardik reached the spot, the 108 ambulance personnel had already declared Mayur dead. The body was handed over to the family after postmortem, said police. Based on Hardikâ€™s complaint, Gandhigram police registered an FIR against the car driver.

Title: Seven women die in Kakinada accident
content: KAKINADA: In a ghastly road accident, seven women were killed and seven others injured in Kakinada district on Sunday.The accident occurred on the bypass road at Tallarevu in Kakinada district when a bus hit an auto rickshaw in which a group of 14 women were returning home after work in an aquaculture factory.According to the police, the women hailing from Yanam were returning home from Tallarevu after working in a prawn processing unit at Sitaramapuram village. When they reached Subbarayudu Dimma centre in Tallarevu a private bus which was going from Kakinada to Hyderabad collided with the autorickshaw. Six of the occupants died on the spot while two others succumbed to injuries while undergoing treatment in a hospital.The injured were shifted to government general hospital in Kakinada. The driver of the autorickshaw escaped unhurt in the accident.All the women are residents of various localities in Yanam, which is part of Puducherry. Condition of three women is critical  The bodies of the women were scattered on the road under the impact of the collision. Two bodies were trapped in the autorickshaw and the locals removed them with difficulty. The deceased women have been identified as Sesetti venkatalakshmi and Karri Parvathi, residents of Mettakudhuru, Kalli Padma of Kothapost, Nimmakayala Laxmi of Kurasam Peta, Budapaneti Satyavathi and Bokka Anantha Lakshmi of Prawns Thippa and Chintapalli Jyothi of Venkat Nagar. Kakinada GGH superintendent Dr Hemalatha said that six women died on the spot and eight women were brought to the GGH. The condition of three women is critical. She said one woman succumbed in hospital. The remaining women were admitted to a private hospital by the management of the firm, where they worked.

Title: Car rams into house in Boudh, kills woman
content: BALANGIR: A 55-year-old woman was killed when a speeding car rammed into a roadside house in the wee hours of Sunday in Radhanagar village under Town police limits in Boudh district. The victim was identified as Paneswari Dhal. The driver of the SUV fled from the spot. â€œThe car skidded into the locality while leaving National Highway-57. It overturned after it crashed into the house and landed on the woman sleeping on a cot,â€ Madhabananda Nayak, inspector in-charge of Boudh police station, said. Sources said the speeding vehicle was coming from Charichhak and going towards Boudh when the driver lost control and rammed into everything that came in its way, including the house. Following the accident, locals blocked the Khurda-Balangir road for hours. They demanded compensation for the deceasedâ€™s family and immediate action against the driver. Traffic was disrupted for hours on both sides of the road. Later, a senior police officer came to the spot to pacify the crowd. The deceasedâ€™s family was given an assistance of Rs 10,000 from the district Red Cross fund.

Title: Mining truck ferrying iron ore crushes 12-yr-old girl, protests erupt
content: NAGPUR: Sonakshi Masram, aged around 12, was crushed under the wheels of a truck ferrying iron ore from Maddigudam in Gadchiroli to Ballarshah in Chandrapur on Sunday after her uncleâ€™s bike skidded, throwing her in the truckâ€™s path. Gadchiroli police have arrested the driver of the truck.Frequent accidents and increasing dust pollution due to mining activities have triggered resentment and protests in the district. Police sources said the iron ore from Surjagarh mining projects is being dumped at Maddigudam, from where it is taken to various destinations, including Ballarshah in Chandrapur.Sonakshi, heading towards Gondpimpri from Ashti with her maternal uncle, was killed on the spot near Ashti forest check naka on Ashti-Alapalli Road. Sources said she was riding pillion on her maternal uncleâ€™s bike, which skidded near the naka.Yet another death under mining trucks led to strong resentment among local residents of Gadchiroli. There has been a spate of accidents due to the truck traffic, including half-a-dozen fatal ones.Akash Tulsigari of Tiger Group has sarcastically tweeted, tagging the office of the chief minister, questioning whether the concept of development meant deaths too. With the mining trucks using the regular roads, without any dedicated corridor for such vehicles, accidents and dust pollution have increased in south Gadchiroli.The Tiger Group was instrumental in extending help to the accident victims, including shifting the minorâ€™s body to the hospital for the post mortem.Sources said the bike had skidded on the road throwing both Sonakshi and her maternal uncle on the road. The truck coming from behind drove over the girl, leading to her death. Some construction work is underway at the place where the bike skidded.SP Neelotpal said legal action has been initiated against the trucker. â€œPolice took proactive efforts to nab the driver,â€ said the SP.

Title: 9 killed, 2 injured in road accident in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Fatehpur district
content: PRAYAGRAJ: At least nine people were killed and two others sustained critical injuries after a tempo carrying them was hit by a speeding milk truck container in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Fatehpur district on Tuesday.The deceased include two children and two women.The incident took place near Chilli turn under Jahanabad police station area. Police said seven passengers died on the spot while two succumbed to injuries during treatment. Two other critically injured passengers were admitted to the district hospital in Fatehpur for treatment. . .AdvertisementPolice have seized the errant milk truck container. However, the accused driver managed to escape from the spot.Upon being alerted, senior police officers along with police forces rushed to the spot and carried out rescue operations. The identities of the deceased were yet to be ascertained.Inspector general of police (Prayagraj range) Chandra Prakash told TOI that the incident took place when a tempo carrying as many as 11 passengers was on its way to Jahanabad from Ghatampur collided with a speeding milk truck container heading towards Ghatampur from Fatehpur.

Title: 7 killed as truck rams into SUV in YSR district
content: TIRUPATI: Seven persons were killed and five others seriously injured in a road mishap near Eturu village in Kondapuram mandal of YSR district in the early hours of Monday. The deceased were identified as K Sudhakar Reddy, Katasani Sudha (39), K Likith Reddy (11), N Lakshmi Devi (38), K Sunil Kumar Reddy (39), Bujji Reddy (40) and K Subhadra (30).Local circle inspector Sudarshan Prasad said 12 persons from Tadipatri in Anantapur district and Bellary in Karnataka, who were also relatives, were returning from Tirumala to their native place in an SUV on Monday morning. As the vehicle reached Etruru village along the Kadapa-Tadipatri highway, a speeding truck heading from the opposite direction rammed into it. The head-on collision completely smashed the SUV, leaving seven dead on the spot. The bodies were strewn all over the road. The other pilgrims who survived sustained severe injuries and were inconsolable after witnessing the deadly accident.. .AdvertisementKondapuram police rushed to the spot and shifted the injured persons to the government hospital at Tadipatri. Kadapa SP K K N Anburajan also went to the accident spot later in the day and directed to expedite the postmortem formalities and hand over the victims' bodies to their family members without delay.

Title: 3 of Nepalese family die in Sadiya road accident
content: Dibrugarh: Three persons of a family from Nepal were killed in a road accident near the Dhola-Sadiya bridge on the NH-115 in Tinsukia district on Monday. Police said the accident occurred at 7.55 am as a pick-up van and a dumper truck collided. There were five occupants in the pick-up van, of which three were killed and two others sustained critical injuries. The deceased were identified as Binod Sonar, his wife, Dalmaya, and their son Dil Bahadur. The identity of the two injured are still not known. All of them were returning to Nepal after attending a relativeâ€™s wedding at Sadiya when the mishap took place.â€œThe accident occurred due to overspeeding. The road was slippery as it was drizzling,â€ Sadiya SP Mrinal Deka said. TNN

Title: Mother, daughter killed as SUV hits their car in Gurgaon
content: GURGAON: A 62-year-old woman and her 32-year-old daughter died and two others suffered grievous injuries when the car in which they were travelling was hit by an SUV near Sector 106 on Sunday evening.Police said Kamal Matta, a resident of Dwarka in Delhi, had come to see a property in Sector 108 along with his wife Neelam and daughter Monika. A real estate broker, Karanjeet Singh, was accompanying them. The accident occurred when the four were returning to Sohna to see a relative. Kamal, who was driving his Baleno, had just taken a U-turn when the SUV crashed into it, unable to slam the brakes in time."They were coming to visit me in Sohna and had taken the U-turn near Raheja Vedanta society. A Kia SUV that was coming from the Delhi side failed to spot the Baleno and crashed into it. Some labourers working at a construction site were the first rescuers. They arranged for an ambulance and rushed them to a private hospital. My sister and her daughter were declared dead on arrival. We also came to know that the SUV had a couple, who, too, were injured," said Yogesh Bhalla, Neelam's brother.Kamal and the broker, Singh, are undergoing treatment at a private hospital.. .AdvertisementA labourer who rushed to the accident site told TOI, "It was around 5pm. The Baleno car was taking a U-turn when an SUV hurtling down the road crashed into it. The SUV driver failed to slam the brakes in time. The Baleno was dragged for almost 20 metres after being hit. Everyone who was working at our construction rushed to help and bring out the occupants."An ambulance reached the spot in around 15 minutes and took the four to hospital. "We had arranged for some first-aid from nearby stores. The condition of two women in the car was extremely critical," he added.Following a complaint by Bhalla, an FIR was registered against the SUV driver under sections 279 (whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person), 304A (causing death by negligence), and 337 (whoever causes hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or the personal safety of others) of the IPC at Rajendra Park police station.Police said the SUV driver was speeding because he was going to see an ailing relative. No arrests were made till late on Monday evening.

Title: 5 woman, child killed in pick-up van-truck collision in Chhattisgarh's Baloda Bazar
content: RAIPUR: Five women and a six-year-old boy were killed, and 21 others injured in a road mishap when the pick-up van carrying them collided head-on with a truck in Baloda Bazar district of Chhattisgarh on Sunday night. Five injured were stated to be in critical condition. The pick-up van was carrying 35-40 people.Extending condolences, chief minister Bhupesh Baghel has announced a relief of Rs 4 lakh to each kin of the deceased.Police said that the victims had gone to attend a family function in Parsada village and were returning to Latua village on Sunday night, when the pick-van they were travelling in met with an accident.The accident took place near Gauda Pulia near Palari. Many people were trapped in the vehicle. Ambulances were called and injured were shifted to hospital and bodies were sent for post-mortem. According to information, five injured were referred for better treatment available in Raipur and others were admitted at Baloda Bazar hospital.. .AdvertisementExpressing grief over the loss of lives, chief minister said, â€œWe are all together with the bereaving families at the times of grief and directions have been given to the district administration offices to ensure best possible assistance to the kin of deceased and treatment for the injured.â€Itâ€™s common sight in the state to see pick-up vehicles and tractors overloaded with people beyond their capacity plying on roads especially in rural areas. Many similar incidents have claimed the lives of hundreds of people in the past few years.

Title: 3 on bikes die in Farrukhnagar, 2 of them on KMP expressway
content: GURGAON: Three bike riders were killed on Sunday in two separate hit-and-run accidents in Farrukhnagar â€” one near Alimudinpur and the other on the KMP Expressway.Police said Ramavatar (45), who worked with a delivery service, was on his way to Yakubnagar around 9am when a motorcycle crashed into his bike head-on in Alimudinpur.â€œThe biker fled while my uncle lay on the road with injuries to his chest and face. He died soon after. He was taken to a hospital in Rewari, where doctors declared him dead,â€ said complainant Anil Kumar. â€œWe are checking CCTV footage. We will arrest the accused soon,â€ the investigation officer said.Five hours later and around 30km away, Om Prakash (59), a labourer, and his nephew, Naveen Kumar (19), were returning home in Dhakla of Jhajjar from Palwal when a car rammed their motorcycle on the expressway.â€œMy father and Naveen had gone to Palwal to buy some medicines. When they were near Farrukhnagar, they took a turn towards Badali, when a car crashed into their motorcycle. They died on the spot,â€ Sombir, Prakashâ€™s son, said.Cops said they got information about the crash soon after. â€œWe saw the two riders lying unconscious on the road. They were taken to the government hospital in Sector 10, where doctors declared them dead,â€ said Jitender Singh, the SHO of Farrukhnagar.In both the cases, FIRs have been registered against the unidentified car driver and the bike rider under IPC sections 279 (whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person) and 304A (causing death by negligence).

Title: Assam: Woman cop who nabbed fiance killed in road accident
content: GUWAHATI: A woman sub-inspector of Assam Police, who was in the news last year for arresting her fiance months before wedding for allegedly duping huge funds from two contractors in Majuli, was killed in a road accident under mysterious circumstances in Nagaon district after her car allegedly collided with a truck on NH-37 early on Tuesday, the police said.Police said the deceased, Junmoni Rabha, was incharge of the Morikalang police outpost. She was driving her personal vehicle in plain clothes when the accident took place at Sorubhogia under the jurisdiction of Jakhalabandha Police Station in the district. "On being informed by the locals about the accident, a police team rushed to the spot and found out that the woman sitting in the driver's seat was Rabha. She was rushed to Kaliabor Sub-Divisional Civil Hospital where the doctors declared her brought dead," said Paban Kalita, OC of Jakhalabandha police station.He added that the vehicle driven by Junmoni was completely damaged. "After inspection of the accident spot, it can be said the truck had shoved her vehicle for around 20 metres. However, it cannot be said how and under what circumstances the accident took place as the incident took took place late at night and there was no witness," he said.Copâ€™s family alleges foul play Junmoni, who joined the Assam Police in 2017, was arrested for colluding with her fiance and was remanded to judicial custody, leading to suspension from service. The suspension was lifted later and she rejoined service. Family members of Junmoni have refused to accept that it was an accident and alleged foul play. They have requested chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma and DGP GP Singh to initiate a probe into the case. Her body was brought to her residence in Guwahati after post mortem and was cremated as per the rituals of their community.

Title: Six women killed as speeding truck hits autorickshaw in Andhra Pradesh
content: PONDUGALLU: Six women labourers were killed and six injured when a speeding truck collided with the autorickshaw they were traveling in at a village in Palnadu district this morning. The women, who were from Telangana, were on their way to Pulipadu village in Palnadu district to work in the chili harvest."A lemons-laden truck from Guduru heading to Lucknow hit the autorickshaw around 4.42am and sped away..,â€ Gurajala sub-divisional police officer A Pallapu Raju said.Those injured were shifted to a hospital in Miriyalaguda in Telangana, where they are undergoing treatment, he said.. .AdvertisementAll the six women were part of a 12-member party travelling in the three-wheeler.A case has been registered under IPC Sections 304 (A) and 337.

Title: Two bike-borne cousins killed in Kota road accident
content: Kota: Two bike-borne youth were killed on the spot after an unidentified vehicle hit them on Itawa bypass in Kota district on Monday night. Police handed over the bodies to their families after a post-mortem on Tuesday morning and registered a case against the unidentified vehicle driver. The two brothers were returning home in Bambooliya Khurd village from their sister's engagement ceremony in Itawa town.Deceased Ramhate Bairwa (21) was pursuing graduation in humanities while Ankit Bairwa (19) was a class-11 student.. .AdvertisementThe accident happened around 10.30 PM, killing both on the spot, said Itawa SHO at Dhanraj Meena.A local witness said due to the impact of the accident their bodies were mutilated and police had a hard time collecting their remains for the post-mortem.The death of the duo shocked the Bambooliya Khurd village, where their family was engaged in performing wedding rituals of deceased Ankitâ€™s sister, said a villager.

Title: 3 killed as car rams into lorry after tyre burst
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: Three persons were killed and another seriously injured in a road accident on the NH in Devarapalli mandal of East Godavari district on Tuesday. As per information, four persons were going to Vijayawada from Rajahmundry when the one of the tyres of the car burst near Yernagudem at around 10.30 am, which led the car to hit the divider and cross over to the adjacent lane of the NH. A container lorry coming from the opposite side hit the car, which rolled over and hit another vehicle. While two persons died on the spot, another person succumbed to his injuries on the way to the hospital. The fourth person has been admitted to a private hospital in Rajahmundry. The deceased were identified as Patnala Digvijaya Radhakrishna Swamy (47), Kaki Chakravarthy (30), and Bandubode Thulasiram (32) - all from various parts of Rajahmundry. The condition of the injured, Sk Ismail, is said to be stable. Radhakrishna Swamy was driving the car.

Title: 2 children among nine killed as milk tanker rams tempo in UP's Fatehpur
content: PRAYAGRAJ: Nine people, including two women and two kids, were killed and two others were injured when the tempo they were travelling on collided with a milk tanker near Chilli turn in Jehanabad area of Fatehpur on Tuesday, said police adding that the milk container has been seized.Expressing grief over the loss of lives in the accident, Prime Minister Narendra Modi in a tweet in Hindi said, "The road accident in Fatehpur is very sad. My deepest condolences to those who have lost their family members. I wish the injured a speedy recovery."Under the supervision of the state government, the local administration is fully engaged in helping the victims," Modi said. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath in a tweet in Hindi said, "The loss of lives in a road accident in district Fatehpur is extremely sad. My condolences are with the bereaved families. May Lord Ram give place to the departed souls in his holy feet and a speedy recovery to the injured." The district administration officials have been instructed to provide proper treatment of the injured, the CM said.. .AdvertisementPolice said seven passengers died on the spot while two others succumbed to injuries on the way to the hospital. The critically injured passengers were admitted at district hospital, Fatehpur for treatment. While police managed to ascertain the identities of five of the deceased, four could not be identified till late evening.The deceased have been identified as Asharfilal (60) of Delhi, Anil Kumar (35) of Bengali Colony (Etawah), his wife Devi (32), daughter Pallavi (3) and son Love (eight months), all residents of Etawah. Anil Kumar's other daughter Saumya (6) and Bahadur ( 52) of Moosa Nagar of Kanpur Dehat were admitted to the hospital. Inspector general of police (Prayagraj range) Chandra Prakash told TOI, "The incident took place when the tempo, carrying as many as 11 passengers, was on its way to Jahanabad from Ghatampur. It collided with the milk tanker heading towards Ghatampur from Fatehpur. Seven passengers, including two women, three men and two kids died on the spot." Superintendent of police (Fatehpur) Rajesh Kumar said, "The victims were heading towards Jahanabad to finalise a marriage proposal. We are probing the incident after registering an FIR against the unknown tanker driver under appropriate sections."Efforts are on to nab tanker driver who fled with vehicle after the accident, police said. Scores of locals assembled at the site and offered assistance to police to carry out rescue operations.

Title: Woman, daughter, 1, die in accident
content: Trichy: A woman and her one-year-old daughter were killed and another woman injured when a speeding mini-truck rammed the bike on which they were travelling at Mutharasanallur on the Trichyâ€“Karur bypass road on Monday night. The deceased were identified as K Gayathri, 30, of Mutharasanallur and her daughter K Aathvika, 1. Gayathriâ€™s friend, M Vennila, 27, was hospitalised with injuries. Vennila had called on Gayathri at her house in the evening. Later, when she began to leave, the latter offered to drop her at her house at at Kambarasampettai. They left on Gayathriâ€™s bike taking along her one-year-old daughter. Police said the accident happened near the railway crossing at Muthrasanallur where Gayathri stopped. After checking if any vehicle was coming on the Karur bypass road, she moved her bike when a mini-truck going to Namakkal from Trichy dashed against the bike. All the three including the little girl were tossed in the air before landing on the road. Gayathri sustained injuries on the back of her head and forehead while Athvika suffered injuries on the back of her head. Aathvika died on the spot while Gayathri was declared dead at a private hospital. With injuries on the back of her head and bruises on her right hand, Vennila was admitted to a private hospital. Jeeyapuram police arrested the mini-truck driver V Vijaya, 23, for rash and negligent driving. The accident happened when he was returning to Namakkal after unloading mangoes at Gandhi market.

Title: 5 killed, several injured in collision between bus and tractor-trolley in Madhya Pradesh's Shajapur
content: INDORE: Five people were killed and over a dozen injured in a head-on collision between a sleeper passenger bus and a tractor-trolley on Maksi-Ujjain road in Shajapur district of Madhya Pradesh early on Thursday morning.Maksi police station in-charge Gopal Singh Chouhan said that the bus was on its way from Madhavgarh of Uttar Pradesh to Ahmedabad when the accident took place.â€œParmatma Sharan, his wife Ram Janki and Meera Bai, wife of Ganesh Prasad Prajapati died on the spot whereas Sumitra Devi (35), and her 12-year-old daughter Radha succumbed to the injuries at the hospital,â€ said Chouhan. The driver lost control of the vehicle while overtaking and hit the trolley coming from the opposite direction.. .AdvertisementChouhan said that teams from Tarana, Kaytha and Maksi police stations were sent to the spot and started the rescue operations. The front portion of the bus was totally damaged. It took a lot of time to extricate the victims from inside the bus. A lot of villagers also helped police officials in rescuing the victims.Victim Ganesh Prajapati, resident of Gora Bhupak village of Uttar Pradesh, was traveling to Ahmedabad with his family to attend a wedding.He said, â€œI was sleeping on the first seat with my brother and my wife and brotherâ€™s daughter-in-law were sleeping on the side seat. Early in the morning, there was a sudden noise. I was thrown out of the window and fell unconscious. The villagers woke me up by throwing water on my face and I saw that my wife, brother and his daughter in law all were lying injured in a pool of blood. They were taken to the hospital.â€â€œThe damaged vehicles have been removed from the spot and further investigations are under way,â€ said Chouhan. Sandeep (25), Amit (28), Parmatma (50), Neeru (22), Kaushalya (38), Sheela (32), Pushpa Devi (40), Anjali (13), Kajal (16), Gopal (25), Kedar Singh (34), Gopal Porwal (30) and Ram Kiloni (51), were injured in the accident and have been admitted in Ujjain district hospital for treatment.

Title: 6 T agri workers killed in accident
content: VIJAYAWADA: Six agricultural workers died on the spot and 12 others received severe injuries in a road accident at Pondugala village in Dachepalli mandal of Palnadu district, near the Andhra Pradesh-Telangana border early on Wednesday. The workers hailed from Nalgonda district in Telangana.The Telangana government has announced an ex gratia of Rs 5 lakh to the kin of the deceased. The injured were shifted to the government hospital at Miryalaguda in Telangana.Police said agricultural farmhands from Telangana were travelling in a seven-seater auto to Pulipadu village of Guruzala mandal in neighbouring Andhra Pradesh to work in a farm. The accident occurred near Pondugala village when a speeding lorry hit the over-crowded auto rickshaw on the highway.. .AdvertisementGurazala DSP A Pallapu Raju told TOI that the lorry, going towards Nalgonda, hit the auto head-on coming from the opposite side at around at 4:45 am. The farm workers were headed to Pondugala in Palnadu district. After the accident, the lorry driver did not stop the vehicle and drove away.All the victims hailed from Narsapuram village in Nalgonda district. The deceased are identified as Islavath Manjula (25), Bhukya Padma (26), Bodium Shekri (35), Bhukya Sony (50), Malavath Kavitha (30) and Islavath Parvathi (38). All of them were relatives. A special team has been formed by the DSP to find the lorry. Police, after inspecting the CCTV footage, identified the registration number of the lorry and traced the vehicle in Nalgonda. The DSP said the place where the accident occurred has been identified as accident prone. Moreover, the spot where the accident occurred narrows down to a two-lane road from a fourlane state highway for about 200 metres due to land acquisition issues pertaining to about 20 houses in Pondugala village. Police said the main reason for the number of accidents is the sudden narrowing of the road without a divider. As a number of casualties were reported at the spot in the last one year, police have identified it as a black spot.

Title: Schoolboy applies for leave, killed in accident
content: Ludhiana: Returning home after applying for 2-day sick leave at his school, a 13-year-old boy was crushed to death by a truck at Kailash road on Wednesday morning. The victim, Shivam Kumar, was riding a cycle. The tipper driver fled leaving the vehicle at the spot.Shivam, a resident of Gehlewal village, had been sick for the past few days. On Wednesday, he had gone to the school to apply for two-day leave. A sand-laden truck hit his cycle. As Shivam fell down on the road, the vehicle crushed him to death.A CCTV camera installed near the spot captured the tipper driver fleeing while leaving his vehicle at the spot.On getting the information, Jodhewal police reached the spot and shifted the body to the civil hospital.Kamaldeep, Shivamâ€™s class in-charge, said, â€œThe boy visited the school in the morning and submitted his sick leave application. He also asked his friends to share the homework with him.â€His Hindi teacher said, â€œI learned from students that Shivam has met with an accident. I rushed to the spot, but he had died by then. Shivam was a very disciplined student.â€ Head constable Jaswinder Singh said the kin of Shivam had reached a compromise with the tipper driver. On the statements of Shivamâ€™s father Vikash Kumar, the police have proceeded with Section 174 of CrPC.

Title: 6 T agri workers killed in accident
content: Vijayawada: Six agricultural workers died on the spot and 12 others received severe injuries in a road accident at Pondugala village in Dachepalli mandal of Palnadu district, near the Andhra Pradesh-Telangana border early on Wednesday. The workers hailed from Nalgonda district in Telangana.The Telangana government has announced an ex gratia of Rs 5 lakh to the kin of the deceased. The injured were shifted to the government hospital at Miryalaguda in Telangana.Police said agricultural farmhands from Telangana were travelling in a seven-seater auto to Pulipadu village of Guruzala mandal in neighbouring Andhra Pradesh to work in a farm. The accident occurred near Pondugala village when a speeding lorry hit the over-crowded auto rickshaw on the highway.Gurazala DSP A Pallapu Raju told TOI that the lorry, going towards Nalgonda, hit the auto head-on coming from the opposite side at around at 4:45 am. The farm workers were headed to Pondugala in Palnadu district. After the accident, the lorry driver did not stop the vehicle and drove away.

Title: 6 agri workers killed in accident near AP-T'gana border
content: Six agricultural workers died on the spot while 12 others received severe injuries in a road accident at Pondugala village in Palnadu district, near the Andhra Pradesh-Telangana border, early Wednesday. Police said the agricultural farmhands were travelling in a seven-seater auto to neighbouring Andhra Pradesh to work on a farm. The accident occurred when a speeding lorry, going towards Nalgonda, hit the overcrowded auto head-on coming from the opposite side on the highway, Gurazala DSP A Pallapu Raju told TOI.

Title: Woman killed in hit-and-run in Adgaon
content: Nashik: A 55-year-old woman was killed in a road accident in the Adgaon area when she was knocked down by an unidentified sports utility vehicle (SUV) driver on Sunday night.The deceased woman was identified as Surekha Sharad Kothavade, a resident of Nandanvan Colony, Adgaon. The Adgaon police said that Kothavade and her husband were taking a stroll across their residence on Sunday night after having dinner. While they were walking from Bali Temple Chowk towards Park Side complex, the SUV driver knocked down the woman and sped away from the scene. She suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same.The police said the woman's husband registered a complaint against the unidentified SUV driver. . .Advertisement

Title: 5 killed in bus-trolley collision in Shajapur
content: INDORE: Five people including three women and a 12-year-old girl were killed and 13 others seriously injured when the sleeper bus they were travelling collided head-on with a tractor trolley on Maksi-Ujjain road in Shajapur district of Madhya Pradesh early on Thursday.Maksi police station in-charge Gopal Singh Chouhan said that the bus was on its way from Madhavgarh in Uttar Pradesh to Ahmedabad when the accident took place around 3:30 am."Meera Bai, wife of Ganesh Prasad Prajapat, 45, and her daughter-in-law Ram Janki, wife of Parmatma Sharan, both residents of Gora Bhupka village in Jalaun district of Uttar Pradesh and Lal Singh Chouhan 70, resident of Cheerhuli village in Auraiya district of Uttar Pradesh were killed on the spot. Another woman Sumitra, wife of Ram Kiloni, 50, and her daughter Radha, 12, both residents of Pushmara village in Jalaun district of UP succumbed to injuries at the district hospital," he said.'Chitchatting' driver lost control leading to accident . .Advertisement Victim Ganesh Prajapat, resident of Gora Bhupak village of Uttar Pradesh, who lost his wife and daughter-in-law in the accident, was traveling to Ahmedabad with his family to attend a wedding.He said, "I was sleeping on the first seat with my brother. Early in the morning, there was a loud noise and I was thrown out of the window and fell unconscious. The villagers woke me up by sprinkling water on my face and I saw that my wife, brother and his daughter-in-law all were lying injured in a pool of blood. They were taken to the hospital where the two women succumbed." The driver lost control of the vehicle while overtaking and hit the trolley coming from the opposite direction. While an eyewitness told media persons that the bus driver was chit chatting in the bus and suddenly lost control leading to the accident, head constable Nilesh Singh said that the driver was severely injured andthey were yet to record his statements. "The damaged vehicles have been removed from the spot and investigations are on," he said . TNN

Title: Two schoolgirls crushed to death by truck in Bihar's Arwal, 3 hurt
content: PATNA: Two schoolgirls were crushed to death and three others, including an elderly man, critically injured when a speeding truck ran over them on NH-139 (Patna-Aurangabad main road) under Kaler sub-division in Arwal on Thursday, police said.The victims were identified as Shivani Kumari (7) and Kajal Kumari (8). The girls' bodies were handed over to their family members after autopsy. The injured people were admitted to Arwal Sadar Hospital for treatment.The incident took place around 7am near Maraila village under the Mehandia police station area when one Ramashray Paswan (60) was on way to drop his granddaughter, Khushi Kumari (8), and some other girls of the same village to school, which was 2km away from the accident spot. The driver of a Daudnagar-bound truck from Patna lost control over the vehicle and mowed down five people.According to doctors, the condition of Ramashray and 10-year-old Keerti is critical.Locals blocked the Patna-Aurangabad main road for more than four hours after the accident and protested against the district administration. Sub-divisional police officer Rajeev Roshan and SHO of Mehandiya police station Amit Kumar reached the spot along with other police personnel and pacified the protesters.The SHO said police have seized the truck. "The driver of the truck managed to flee after the accident. Efforts are on to arrest him," the SHO added. It was the third road accident on NH-139 in the last couple of weeks. On Tuesday, three girls who were on way to their coaching classes were crushed to death after a truck ran over them under Mehandia police station area. On May 5, five persons were killed in a head-on collision between an autorickshaw and a truck near Khokri Mor on the same stretch.

Title: Maharashtra doctor couple donates organs of son killed in road accident
content: THANE/PALGHAR: A doctor couple from Virar in Palghar district in Maharashtra has donated organs of their 30-year-old son who was killed in a motorcycle accident, an Indian Medical Association official said on Saturday. The donated organs will benefit at least 11 people, said IMA's Maharashtra secretary Dr Santosh Kadam. The deceased is identified as Saket Dandvate who was killed in an accident near Bengaluru on Friday, it said. His father Dr Vineet Dandavate, the Virar president of IMA, decided to donate the organs of his son. Saket got married only five months ago. His wife Apurva also consented to the organ donation, the release said. "Saket's parents Dr Vineet and Dr Sumedha decided to donate their deceased son's organs," Dr Kadam, who heads the Organ Donation Committee in Maharashtra IMA, told PTI. He said while there is good awareness about blood donation in the country, more awareness is needed about donating organs.

Title: Techie dies in road accident near Potheri, three critical
content: CHENNAI: An IT employee died and three others suffered injuries on Thurday night after their car rammed a median near Potheri in Chengalpet.Police said Nivedha, 21, the deceased was travelling with Manoj, 22, Swarna, 20, and Anushkumar, 21. The car was going from Maraimalai Nagar towards Chennai. The car's driver lost control near Potheri and hit the median on the road. causing the vehicle to turned upside down. Nivedha was killed on the spot. Passersby rushed the injured to a nearby private hospital. Their condition is said to be critical.They told police that they were employed at an IT firm in Guduvancherry. Tambaram traffic investigation police are investigating.

Title: Four die, 8 injured as car crashes into truck in city
content: HYDERABAD: Four students were killed and eight others suffered injuries when the overloaded car in which they were travelling rammed a stationary truck parked on the roadside at Khanapur in Narsingi on Friday morning.The students - a majority of them in their Intermediate course - packed like sardines in the car were on their way to an amusement park in Gandipet when the accident took place.Prasad, 21, who was behind the wheel, is battling for his life due to critical head injuries. "The 12 youngsters were friends. The truck driver had no role in the accident. Only Prasad is to be blamed for the way he drove the car and crashed it," Narsingi inspector Shiva Kumar told TOI. Police said Prasad overtook a bus and swerved to the extreme left at high speed. As a result he was unable to control the vehicle and crashed into a parked truck on roadPolice said Prasad overtook a bus and swerved to the extreme left at high speed. As a result he was unable to control the vehicle and crashed into a parked truck on road

Title: Four riding bikes killed in Dhar
content: INDORE: Four persons were killed and one injured in separate accidents in Dhar district on Friday morning.Two youths riding a bike were killed after they were hit by a speeding bus in Pandav Gufa area of Dhar in the afternoon. After hitting the bike, the bus overturned, injuring a woman passenger travelling in it. Inspector Ranjeet Singh Baghel said that those killed in the accident were identified as Dhanter alias Guddu, a resident of Padliya and Gudiya, a resident of Jhaba village.The bus was returning to Kukshi from Bagh. At the time of the accident, there were only conductor, driver, and two passengers Sumanbai and her relative in the bus. Sumanbai received severe injuries on her hand.In another accident, two youths were killed in front of Amkha Jhamka temple in Amjhera area of Dhar after their bike collided with a pickup vehicle.Amjhera police station in-charge CB Singh said that Jitendra and Kekdiya Singar were on their way to Keshvi from Amjhera on a bike when they collided head-on with a pickup vehicle on a turning and were thrown on the road. Both received severe head injuries and died on the spot. Villagers rushed to the spot and informed the police. They were taken to the hospital where they were declared dead. The bodies were sent for postmortem examination.An eyewitness said that the bike was being driven at a high speed and it hit a pickup vehicle on a turning after the biker lost control.Traffic jams were witnessed in both the areas after the accidents. The damaged vehicles were removed from the spots and traffic movement restored. Drivers of pickup vehicle and bus fled from the spots.

Title: Bank employee killed in road accident
content: Coimbatore: A 29-year-old bank employee was killed in an accident on the Avinashi - Coimbatore route on Friday evening. The deceased was identified as S Gowrishankar of Annur, who was working in Tamil Nadu Grama Bankâ€™s Appakoodal branch. On Friday evening, after work, Gowrishankar was heading towards Coimbatore on a two-wheeler, when it crashed into a truck. He sustained head injuries and died on the spot.The Sulur police sent the body to ESI Government Hospital for postmortem. It was handed over to the family on Saturday afternoon. TNN

Title: Three of family killed in road accident in Jaipur
content: JAIPUR: Three members of a family were killed and as many injured when a speeding car hit them while they were standing on the roadside here on Sunday morning, police said. They had returned from Haridwar after performing the last rites of a family member and were waiting to be picked up by a relative near Doi Ki Dhani in Chaksu tehsil here, they said. "Three people, including a woman and her son, were killed in the accident. Three people were also injured," said Assistant Sub-Inspector Madan Choudhary. The car driver, who abandoned his vehicle and fled the spot, was probably in an inebriated condition as a liquor bottle has been recovered from the vehicle, he said. The deceased were identified as Sunita, her son Golu and her brother-in-law Sitaram. They had returned after performing the last rites of Sunita's husband Madan, the police said. They said the bodies have been kept in a mortuary of a hospital in Chaksu for post-mortem examination.

Title: Iron ore laden truck kills teacher in Gadchiroli, locals block road
content: Nagpur: An ashramshala teacher, Vasudeo Kulmethe, was crushed under a truck ferrying iron ore in front of Alapalli police chowky in Gadchiroli on Saturday. The accident occured when the truck was heading to Chandrapurâ€™s Ballarshah depot from Surjagarh mining companyâ€™s Maddigudam depot.Local residents, along with family of Kulmethe, staged a â€˜rasta rokoâ€™ for some time at the accident spot to protest movement of trucks from the depot. A section of local population is already against the mining activities which had received extension following a controversial public hearing held 150km from the mine last year.According to locals, the mining companyâ€™s trucks have already claimed 4-5 lives this year and their movement is causing pollution leading to health complications. Many shops and eateries in and around Alapalli had to be shut down due to red dust in the air, they said.This is the second death in accidents involving a mining truck in Gadchiroli within a week. Around five days back, a 12-year-old student was killed under a truck, which was carrying iron ore from Maddigudam to Chandrapur, near Ashti.SP Neelotpal told TOI that police have arrested the trucker and legal action is being initiated. â€œPolice have legally dealth with all the truckers involved in accidents,â€ said Neelotpal.Kulmethe, a schoolteacher at Katepalli near Dewulmari, was heading to Nagepalli when the truck hit his bike from behind. A moped rider had a miraculous escape as the truck came to a halt after hitting the teacherâ€™s bike.The teacherâ€™s bike collided with the moped in the front, before rolled down and getting crushed under the truck. He died on the spot.Madhukarrao Rapartiwar, a local BJP functionary, said the mining companyâ€™s trucks are required to bypass the Alapalli village. â€œBut the heavy vehicles are being driven through dense localities round the clock. Despite protests, no government agency or officials are taking any action due to pressure,â€ said Rapartiwar who was part of the agitation. According to Rapartiwar, dust rising due to passing trucks has badly affected the health of villagers from Maddigudam. â€œAt Alapalli, shops and other outlets are being closed down due to the dust, leading to unemployment," said Rapartiwar adding that there is police pressure too.Local residents claimed cops had issued notices to members of some WhatsApp groups for debating the Surjagarh mining activity and now people fear to oppose mining.Countering the claims, SP Neelotpal said police have been non-partisan. â€œThe notices were issued to some people who used objectionable language in social media. There could have been law and order situation had police not warned all the factions concerned,â€ said Neelotpal.

Title: Bengal couple on bike trip to Ladakh killed in Purvanchal Expressway crash
content: LUCKNOW: A couple on an adventure trip to Ladakh on a sports bike died in a road accident in Barabanki on Friday morning. Police said that Subrat Sanyal, 50, and his wife Parmita Sanyal, 46, who had been travelling to various destinations on their Royal Enfield for the past couple years, rammed into a divider on Purvanchal Expressway in Barabanki district early on Saturday.Subrat was an architect and his wife an interior designer. "Subrat sustained injuries on the head while his wife Parmita was tossed in the air and fell a few feet away from her husband," said SHO Deepak Pandey, based on Barabanki police's record. The couple were shifted to local CHC by the Barabanki police in Gosaiganj area. Pandey said that the couple's identity was established using their Aadhaar and mobile phones.Police said that they have contacted their families. "In the past also, the couple had gone on adventure trips to Odisha and MP," said Amitava Sanyal, their friend who works in Delhi. Amitava has already reached Lucknow. The couple left behind two children, one studies in Class 9 and the other is an engineering student in Cuttack.

Title: Man, 78, dies in hit and run; pillion rider in another accident
content: TRICHY: A 78-year-old pedestrian and a 42-year-old pillion rider of a bike were killed and a three-year-old boy was injured in separate road accidents in Trichy on Friday. M Sebastian, 78, from Keezha Panjappur was crossing the Trichy-Chennai national highway near a tea shop at Panjappur when an unidentified vehicle hit him on Friday. The speeding vehicle fled the scene. Sebastian sustained injuries. Locals rushed him to the Mahatma Gandhi memorial government hospital (MGMGH). While undergoing treatment, he died in the evening. Acting on a complaint, the traffic south investigation wing launched an investigation to ascertain the identity of the accused. The postmortem was performed on Saturday. In another accident, a 42-year-old woman was killed when the two-wheeler she was riding pillion on, was hit by a car at Kondaiyampettai. The deceased was identified as M Shanthi of Subramaniapuram. On Friday, she and her son M Karthikeyan, 3, were riding pillion with her husband Maheswaran, 48, in a bike. A car driven by A Abdul Nizar, 26, of Kundrathur in Chennai hit their bike from behind near Krishna warehouse at Kondaiyampettai on the Chennai Bypass Road. They fell from the bike. Shanthi sustained severe injuries and died on the spot. Karthikeyan and his father suffered injuries. They were admitted to the MGMGH. The traffic north investigation wing seized the car and booked Nizar. In another accident, a 10-year-old boy A Harish from Ariyamangalam was injured after the autorickshaw he was travelling overturned after the driver A Ashok, 32, from Ariyamangalam applied sudden brake to avoid hitting a stray dog on the road at Allithurai. The Somarasampettai police were investigating.

Title: 3 killed as trucks collide head-on in Janjgir-Champa dist
content: RAIPUR: Three persons were killed while another is critical in a road accident involving two trucks in near Hathnevara village in the Janjgir-Champa district in the early hours of Saturday.The police identified the deceased as 19-year-old Rahul Sahu, 27-year-old Farukh Ansari while the identity of another young man involved in the collision is yet to be established.The police said that a truck, heading towards to Bamhnidih from Champa district collided with a another truck at yhe highway. In the impact of the head-on collision, all the three bodies were badly trapped in their respective vehicles. The trapped bodies were pulled out by cutting the vehicle with a gas cutter. . .AdvertisementThe injured was immediately sent to the near by hospital for the treatmentThere were a total of four people including the driver and helper in the hiva and truck. Both the crashed vehicles have been removed from the middle of the road by calling a crane, said a police official. The case was registered while the investigation is still on, added the police.

Title: 4 members of marriage party killed, 3 injured as SUV rams into tree in UP's Banda
content: KANPUR: Four members of a marriage party were killed while three others sustained serious injuries when the SUV they were travelling in crashed into a roadside tree, at a high speed in Uttar Pradesh's Banda district on the intervening night of Sunday and Monday.The deceased have been identified as Rajeev alias Raju Tiwari (48), Devraj Dwivedi (65), Lakshmi Dwivedi (70) and Kailashi, all travelling in a Bolero.Two of the injured have been referred to Kanpur, while one is undergoing treatment at the district hospital.The 'baraat' (marriage procession) of Ankit Singh, son of Raju Singh, resident of Godhani village of Tindwari police station area, had gone to Kurhun village of Badausa police station area on Sunday.. .AdvertisementAfter 'jaimal' rituals, seven baratis left for Godhani at around 1am."The accident happened near Turra village under the limits of Badausa police station when the driver suddenly lost control over the vehicle and it rammed into a tree," said additional superintendent of police, Lakshmi Niwas Mishra.Upon receiving the information by passers-by, police reached the spot and admitted the injured to the district hospital, said an official, adding, "The bodies of the deceased have been sent for postmortem."

Title: Woman in BMW kills man in 4am accident in Delhi's Moti Nagar, held
content: NEW DELHI: A 36-year-old man was killed after his scooter was hit by a speeding car in west Delhi's Moti Nagar early on Sunday. The police have arrested the woman who was driving the car and seized the vehicle, a black BMW. The deceased, identified as Ajay Gupta, lived with his family in Basai Darapur locality of west Delhi and ran a grocery shop. The incident took place around 4am. He had gone to a hospital to buy medicines and was on his way back home. The accused woman, 28, is an architect who lives in Ashok Vihar with her family. She was returning home after attending a party at Greater Kailash, police said.The police received a PCR call about an accident near Moti Nagar Metro station at 4.08am and a team rushed to the spot. They found the damaged vehicles on the spot, but their occupants were missing. A damaged generator, hit by the car during the accident, was also found there. It emerged that the woman had taken Gupta to a nearby EBG hospital, from where he was shifted to ESI hospital by his relatives. He, however, succumbed to his injuries during treatment. Cops registered an FIR under Sections 304 A (causing death by negligence) and 279 (causing death due to negligence and rash driving) of the IPC and the accused was arrested, but let off on bail. The body was sent for autopsy. The police have collected samples of woman for an alcohol test and the report is awaited. Further action would be taken accordingly, they said. The incident led to protests by the victimâ€™s kin and locals. They shouted slogans against the police and authorities and blocked the road for some time. They were later dispersed by the police, who promised strict action. The woman claimed that the man suddenly appeared in front of her car and she was not able to control her vehicle. Cops, however, will probe her testimony. Police said they were scanning the CCTV footage of cameras installed near the spot. â€œThe car was being driven at a high speed. A mechanical inspection of the vehicle will be conducted, which will give us more information about the accident,â€ the officer said.Watch Delhi: Man dies after a BMW rams into a scooter, lady driver arrested

Title: Ex-rly official among two killed in road accidents
content: Mohali/Chandigarh: A day after a tipper truck driver crushed a former railway employee to death, he was arrested here on Sunday. The driver has been identified as Gurvinder Singh, a resident of Ballomajra.Phase VI police post in-charge ASI Baljinder Singh Mand said the accident occurred in Phase 1 on Saturday when the victim Ram Dayal Mehmi (78), who retired as senior section engineer from Indian Railways and was living in New Swaraj Nagar, was riding a scooter to pick-up his granddaughter from school in Sector 44, Chandigarh.The case was registered following the complaint of the victimâ€™s son Manoj. He said when his father reached a traffic light near Phase-1 barrier, a speeding tipper truck came from the rear and hit his fatherâ€™s scooter. Thereafter the tipper truck ran over him.ASI Baljinder Singh Mand, in charge Phase VI police post, said a case under Section 304-A of the IPC has been registered.. .AdvertisementIn another accident, a 46-year-old man was killed after being hit by a bus at Sector 29 on Saturday. Industrial area phase-1 police has arrested the bus driver. It is the fifth death in a road accident in the past one week in the city. Industrial area phase-1 police said the deceased was identified as Joginder Singh, a resident of Sector 30. The victim worked as denting and painting vehicles at the motor market of Sector 28.The accused was returning home on a bicycle when the bus hit him near mandi ground of Sector 29. Passersby gathered at the spot and informed the police about the accident. tnn

Title: Doctor dies in road accident
content: NEW DELHI: A 30-year-old doctor was killed after an RTV hit his scooter in the Okhla area, police said on Sunday, adding the accused driver has been arrested. The incident took place on Saturday near the Crown Plaza red light and Yaseen, a resident of Tughlakabad Extension, succumbed to injuries during treatment on Sunday, they said. Police scanned through the CCTV footage and the driver was arrested and his vehicle seized.

Title: Two killed in Madurai accident
content: Madurai: Two men died while another was injured after two two-wheelers collided near Peraiyur in Madurai on Saturday night.According to police, the deceased were identified as R Rajendran, 60, Virudhunagar district, and R Venkatesh, 25, of S Keelapatti near Peraiyur. Rajendran and Venkatesh were riding their two-wheelers and proceeding on Peraiyur to Watrap road. Venkatesh was accompanied by his friend, C Arunpandi, 24, as a pillion rider. As they were crossing near a gas agency in Thummanayakkanpatti, Venkatesh, who was driving in a rash manner, hit Ranjendranâ€™s two-wheeler.Due to the collision, all three suffered injuries but Rajendran died on the spot. Venkatesh and Arunpandi were immediately rushed to Peraiyur government hospital. However, doctors declared that Venkatesh was brought dead. Arunpandi was referred to government Rajaji hospital in Madurai and is under treatment.

Title: Three-year-old among five dead as SUV collides head-on with truck in UP's Deoria
content: LUCKNOW: A three-year-old girl and four other women passengers were killed after an SUV they were travelling in collided head-on with a truck in Deoria district of Uttar Pradesh on Monday.Police said that the truck was coming from Bankata area while the SUV was going to Bihar, the accident took place at Bhatparrani police station area.The deceased were identified as three-year-old Ritu Pramila Devi, Trishula, Geeta, Siddhi. The injured Anjana and Devesh Kumar were recuperating in the district hospital.Deoria superintendent of police, Sankalp Sharma, said that residents of Rudrapur's Bhartola were going to participate in a sacred thread ritual (upnayan sanskar) at Mairwa in Bihar."When they reached near Bahiyari Baghel, a truck coming from the opposite direction collided head-on. The impact was such that the SUV was reduced to a pulp," said Sharma. Locals informed the police who took the injured passengers to the hospital where five were declared dead.Senior police officers and SDM have reached the hospital to supervise the rescue operations.

Title: Kol biker couple killed in accident near Lucknow
content: LUCKNOW/KOLKATA: A middle-aged couple from the Naktala area died in a road accident in Barabanki, about 30km east of Lucknow, early on Saturday. Subrata Sanyal (53) and his wife Paramita (45) were on a bike trip to Ladakh and rammed a divider on Purvanchal Expressway. The couple, who had travelled to various destinations on their bike over the past few years, have two sons - Mayukh, a musician who studies architecture at a Cuttack institute, and Srijan, a class-IX student at an English-medium school in Narendrapur. Subrata was the proprietor of an engineering consultancy firm and Paramita was an interior designer "Subrata sustained injuries on his head while Paramita was tossed in the air and fell a few feet away from her husband," said Deepak Pandey, station house officer of Barabanki. The couple was taken to a local hospital, where they were declared dead on arrival. Pandey said the couple's identity was established with their Aadhaar cards and phones. Cops contacted their friends and relatives who rushed to UP on Saturday itself. Among the first to reach Barabanki was Subrata's friend Amitava Sanyal, who works in Delhi. "They went on bike trips to Odisha and MP earlier," he said.The Sanyals lived on the fourth floor of a building in Baishnabghata Bylane and were known as avid bikers in the neighbourhood. "They were crazy for bike rides. Age never had any effect on their love for biking and adventure trips. While their children were busy with studies, the couple continued to undertake one journey after another. It hurts to hear they died doing what they loved most," said Saraswati Mondal, a neighbour.Debanjan Saha, another resident of the building, said the two had left Kolkata on their bike early on Tuesday. "His son had told me they were on their way to Ladakh. The two were avid bikers and used to set off for long rides often. We came to know about the accident when one of their relatives came to the flat to take Sanyal's elderly mother and the couple's younger son to the accident spot," said Saha.The bodies were handed over to the sons after a post-mortem, and were cremated at Lucknow's Bhaisakund crematorium.

Title: 2 hit by truck, killed while changing tyre
content: Kanpur: Two persons died while one was injured seriously when they were run over by an unidentified speeding vehicle while they were changing a flat tyre of their pick-up vehicle on the GT road highway in Bilhaur area of the district late Saturday night.As the speeding heavy vehicle grazed the pick-upâ€™s side, the three who were changing tyre were caught between the two vehicles and suffered serious injuries, police said. Police reached along with an ambulance and took all three to Shivrajpur Community Health Center. Doctors declared one brought dead, the other died on the way to Kanpur. Police said the accident occurred around 1 am.Police identified the deceased as Fareed Ahmad of Jajmau and Vijay Shankar of Rae Bareli, while the injured was identified as Vikas and was referred to Kanpur for treatment. The three were on their way to Kanpur from Bilhaur in a pick-up vehicle and when they reached the Uttaripura area of Bilhaur, they noticed a flat tyre and parked on the side of the road to change it. â€œA heavy vehicle ran past, hitting the trio in the process. The three couldnâ€™t move as they were trapped between the speeding vehicle and their van,â€ the officer added.A few motorists passing the accident scene alerted police control room. Fareed was declared dead at Shivrajpur CHC and Vijay Shankar died on way to Kanpur, while Vikas is undergoing treatment.â€œWe have registered a case of rash and negligent driving and are on the lookout for the offending vehicle driver who fled the scene,â€ said Uttaripura police outpost incharge Satish Chandra Yadav.

Title: Three killed as speeding vehicle hits bike in Dindori
content: Jabalpur: Three youth were killed after their motorcycle was hit by a speeding vehicle in Shadol-Pandrai road near Sukulpura village in Dindori district on Sunday The deceased have been identified as Chitrabhan Marko, 25, Kailash Paraste, 26, and Manish Tekam, 25. Gadasarai police station incharge Vedram Hinauta said that there was a marriage ceremony at the house of Hira Singh, a resident of Shivri village, on Saturday. â€œThe three deceased had come to attend the marriage ceremony on their motorcycle and accident took place while they were returning home on Sunday,â€ he said.Their motorcycle was hit by a speeding unidentified vehicle near Sukulpura village, one of three youth died on the spot, while two died during treatment in a hospital. Their bodies of the three were sent for postmortem, Hinauta said. A case has been registered and probe is underway. TNN

Title: One killed after mini-bus rolls down NIBM Rd slope
content: Pune: A scooter rider died, while the pillion rider and two others seated in an autorickshaw were injured after a mini-bus rolled down the NIBM Road slope around 7.15pm on Sunday, hitting at least six vehicles before coming to a halt.The incident took place on the slope that connects the Mohammadwadi and Undri areas with the NIBM Chowk, in front of the Orchid Palace society. The mini-bus hit an autorickshaw, a scooter, a three-wheel tempo and three cars. The bus driver, who has been detained by the police, claimed brake failure as the cause of the incident, said the police. â€œWe will get the mini-bus examined by experts from the regional transport office (RTO) to verify his claims,â€ said inspector Sanjay Mogale of the Kondhwa police. . .AdvertisementThe accident caused a major traffic jam in the area for almost a couple of hours. This is the only alternative road now available for vehicular movement in view of the ongoing work on NIBM Annexe (Cloud 9) Road, which is being done to reduce a dangerous accident-prone slope. Mogale added, â€œKondhwa resident Prashant Bhanudas Ghemud (37), who was riding a scooter, succumbed to his injuries at the Sassoon General Hospital where he was shifted after being initially taken to a private hospital near the accident spot. The condition of the pillion rider was reported by the doctors as serious.â€ The inspector informed, â€œThe mini-bus was going to NIBM Chowk from Mohammadwadi. Vehicles coming from Mohammadwadi drive on an upward gradient to a point from where the road goes down and turns into a slope towards NIBM Chowk. As he approached Orchard Palace society, the bus driver lost control and the bus hit some vehicles driving ahead as well as those coming from the opposite side.â€Mogale said, â€œWe are in the process of registering a case after recording the driverâ€™s statement as well as the statements of the injured victims.â€

Title: One killed in Bithoor road accident
content: KANPUR: A 48-year-old woman was killed while five others were injured after a speeding container truck collided with a car in which they were travelling on Monday morning near Singhpur intersection of Bithoor police station area of the district. The police identified the deceased as Kusum Mishra, wife of Sunil Mishra, a resident of Shastri nagar.Kusum along with five others had gone to attend a funeral of her relative in Meerut. While returning home, a speeding container hit the car from the front on Kalyanpur Bithoor road near Singhpur intersection.

Title: 6 killed, 10 injured in bus-truck collision in Maharashtra's Buldhana
content: MUMBAI: As many as six people were killed and 10 others injured when a state transport bus collided with a container truck in Buldhana district of Maharashtra on Tuesday morning. The accident took place near Sindkhed Raja town on old Mumbai-Nagpur highway in the district, located more than 450 km from the state capital Mumbai, said an official. According to official, the bus, belonging to the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC), was going towards Mehkar in (Buldhana) from Pune when it collided with the truck."Among the deceased were four bus passengers and drivers of both the vehicles," the official said. . .Advertisement"The injured people were taken to a hospital in Sindkhed Raja town," he said.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: Telangana: Teen, granddad killed in accident
content: BHUPALPALLY: A man and his granddaughter died in a road accident when a Telangana State Road Transport Corporation (TSRTC) bus hit their bike at the Bhagirthipeta crossroads in Regonda mandal.The deceased were identified as Chinna Sammaiah (60) and his granddaughter Akshita (14), residents of Jayashankar Bhupalpally district. Police said that an over speeding RTC bus hit Sammaiah's two-wheeler as he was returning from his daughter's house along with Akshita.

Title: What the hell? Over 500 die on Chennai roads every year
content: More than 500 people die on Chennai roads every year, and most of the casualties are caused by traffic violations. As our understaffed traffic police lack in technology, many violators go scot-free, and all you can do is wonder...Sometime early last year, I was driving a rocket scientist around the city. We were approaching the Nandanam signal from the Saidapet side and were about to turn right to Chamiers Road. The traffic light on our side was green, yet several vehicles were crossing the junction from other directions. Some were driving on the wrong side of the road, some others were outside the stop line, the engines revving. A dozen people were crossing the road unmindful of the pedestrian signal that was red. A traffic constable stood under the shade of a tree, watching the chaos.The scientist friend joked: â€œYou will have to accelerate at two metres per second at an angle of incidence of 30 degrees with a diminishing radius for 12 seconds if you want to avoid a collision with that bike coming from the right. . .AdvertisementCan you attain escape velocity?â€ I slammed the brake. We laughed.Itâ€™s no laughing matter that more than 500 people die in road accidents on Chennai roads every year (and we arenâ€™t counting the deaths over road rage). In 2021, the city with a vehicular population of around 65 lakh, registered 5,034 accidents compared to 4,720 in Delhi, which has close to 80 lakh vehicles. While driving skills of motorists are presumably not too different across Indian cities, these numbers show something fundamentally wrong with the cityâ€™s traffic infrastructure, policing and motoristsâ€™ behaviour.Itâ€™s not just speeding and drunk driving that kill thousands on our roads; driving on the wrong side of the road and jumping traffic signals cause a large number of accidents, and when motorists donâ€™t wear a seat belt or helmet, the result is fatal. Signal jumping, if caught, carries the least penalty of ?500 (most of the other violations invite a fine of ?1,000; drunken driving gets ?10,000). But then, as retired Madras high court judge Justice S Chandru said, itâ€™s the certainty of punishment more than the severity of punishment that will be a deterrent. In Chennai, the wayward motorist is often certain he can get away with murder on the road.Chennai has about 3,500 traffic police personnel. While this is insufficient for a city of 65 lakh vehicles, technological upgrade, which can make up for inadequate human resources, has taken a backseat. The integrated traffic management system, which was proposed more than ten years ago for remote monitoring and imposition of road rulesin the city works in fits and starts. Senior road users tell us that incidents of traffic police taking bribes from violators have come down, but the corrupt elements have found e-transfer of bribes an efficient way to not leave the grease on the palm.Often pedestrians and motorbike riders face the most serious consequences of violations (they are violators too), but road safety has to be a collective responsibility. We can have more rules and more stringent penalties for traffic violations, but the ultimate game-changer will be behavioural changes â€“ in motorists and enforcers. Road safety awareness campaigns play a catalytic role, but the real drivers of thischange will be the person behind the steering wheel and the uniformed one at the traffic signal. The policeman should be trained and rewarded to ensure the motorist is disciplined.A G Gardiner, in his famous essay â€˜On the rule of the roadâ€™, talks about the policeman at the Piccadilly Circus being a â€œsymbol not of tyranny, but of libertyâ€. TOI today starts a campaign against traffic violations, taking a leaf out of Gardinerâ€™s book that says liberty, as in the case of roads, is not a personal affair, but a social contract.Six most common traffic offences on Chennai roads1. Wrong-side and pavement drivingIn India, we drive on the left side of the road. But a visitor to Chennai wouldnâ€™t believe that. Chennaiites drive on both sides of the road, and on pavements when they are there. The traffic police say they have booked 60,000 cases for this offence and collected more than ?1crore as fine in the first four months of 2023. Well, they should book a lot more people. Walk, ride or drive down any road in the city, and chances are youâ€™ll have to dodge two-wheelers, autorickshaws, and sometimes even cars and vans whizzing past you in the opposite direction on your left. Protest and face abuse. The traffic diversions and road blocks for metro work have made things worse. Bikers would tell you that they have no intention of taking a kilometre-long detour just so that you can drive safely. And that is the crux of the problem. Attitude. For a couple of years, people had also got used to driving any which way on empty streets during the pandemic-induced lockdown. But the road accidents are rising and itâ€™s time to drive right.2. Signal jumping and stopline violationsIf you think jumping signals and waiting well beyond the stopline at junctions will get to your destination faster, think again. For example, at least 350 vehicles can cross the Nandanam signal on Anna Salai (from Chamiers Road to T Nagar) in one 90-second cycle. But on an average, TOI found that only 120 vehicles could cross. Thirty vehicles had to wait another three minutes. This was because motorists going towards Saidapet had not stopped when their signal turned amber and they and they motorists who had just got the green signal were all dodging each other in the middle of the road. Adding to the chaos were motorists going north, who had crossed the stopline and were blocking the turn to T Nagar. This chaos plays out all day across the city. Every month, close to 45,000 motorists pay ?500 each as fine for these offences. This can be attributed to automatic number plate recognition (ANPR) cameras installed at twelve key traffic signals in the city. By next year, the police plan to cover 200 more road intersections. 3. Use of mobile phones while drivingP Velmurugan, an auto driver, is on the phone. As he weaves through traffic on Anna Salai, the conversation heats up and he loses his cool, and al-most rams a two-wheeler. Similar scenes play out all the time on city streets. People talk, and worse, watch stuff on their phones while driving, unmindful of the risk they pose to themselves and others. Every year, 30 people die in Chennai in road acci-dents caused by use of mobile phones. Under the New Motor Vehicles Act, the penalty for using a phone while driving is ?5,000. But police say technology makes their job harder. There was a time when constables used to stop cars and levy a fine if the driver was seen holding a phone. Now people use bluetooth earbuds while wearing a helmet. In cars, the hands-free system comes built-in. Many believe use of hands-free phone is safe. Road safety experts disagree.4. Driving under influence of alcoholDriving drunk is one of the ma-jor causes of fatal accidents in the city. Among 53 megacities in the country, more than 50 % of fatalities due to driving under the influence of drugs/alcohol were in Chennai â€” 236 of 462 deaths last year. The city police are on their toes, changing their check-points and manning even interior roads, but tipplers keep finding new escape routes. The penalty of ?10,000 is no deterrent. Many do not pay the fine, though an intimation is re-ceived on their mobile from the e-court sys-tem. The traffic po-lice have now warned violators that if the fine is not paid, war-rants will be obtained from courts to attach their movable property. Already, 340 such war-rants have been issued by courts. The traffic police also complain that there is little support from the state trans-port department. Though police book cases and rec-ommend suspension of driving licences, RTOs sel-dom follow through, allowing violators to go scot-free.5. Overspeeding and negligent drivingThe most ignored traffic violations are overspeeding and negligent driving. Traffic police say they have booked more than 23,000 cases of rash driving in 2022, but in most cases this is only an additional charge after a vehicle meets with an accident or a chain-snatcher or bike thief is caught. Hardly anyone is booked for overspeeding or rash driving other than bikers performing stunts on a few roads such as Kamarajar Salai. And these youngsters keep trying to hoodwink the police. Traffic police have installed six speed display boards at major junctions, but they should link these to their Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) cameras and start fining speed demons. This should not turn out like the advanced traffic management system installed at a cost of ?8.6crore on East Coast Road. No one knows if it works. And what about people who refuse to maintain lane discipline and end up holding up traffic?6. Not wearing helmets and seat beltsHelmet-less two-wheeler riders arguing with police personnel who fine them is a common sight across Chennai. In 2022, more than 100 bikers riding without helmets died in road accidents in Chennai. Nearly half of them were pillion riders. Most of the accidents happened when people drove to a market or an ATM near home and thought they didnâ€™t need a helmet for such a short distance. â€œDespite knowing the risks, bikers presume that they wonâ€™t meet with an accident, and willfully ignore the helmet rule. A few others argue helmets obstruct their vision and hearing. This is why people should wear helmets from the day they start learning to ride bikes,â€ says Prasanna, a retired sub-inspector of police. Police say most riders wear helmets, but not the pillion, and hardly ever the children perched precariously between or in front of their parents. As for wearing seatbelts in cars, the level is as low as 30% even in areas such as Anna Nagar, Velachery and Royapuram where automatic number plate recognition (ANPR) cameras have been installed to detect such violations. As for rear seat passengers, hardly anyone belts in. Experts say more awareness needs to be created among the public about how air bags work only when passengers wear seatbelts.(With inputs from Vivek Narayanan, Ram Sundaram, Ayyappan V, Sindhu Kannan, Venkadesan S and A Selvaraj)

Title: 3 tourists from Haryana killed in Haridwar accident
content: HARIDWAR: Three tourists from Rewari in Haryana were killed in a road accident on Haridwar-Delhi highway in Bahadrabad area of Haridwar on Sunday night.The tourists were coming from Delhi and owing to high speed, their car overturned at a sharp bend near Raghunath mall, under Bahadurabad police limits. The victims have been identified as Rohit (29), Hemant Yadav (23) and Deepak (23), all residents of Rewari. They were involved in small-time business. According to police, the accident site is in a "danger zone" area and several letters have been written to the NHAI in the past. The area does not have street lights, locals pointed out. SHO of Bahadurabad, Nitish Sharma, said on Monday, "The speeding car overturned and two tourists died on the spot while one succumbed to injuries at a private hospital. The vehicle's driver, Vinay Kumar, sustained injuries. He is currently out of danger."
