Title: 4 of marriage party killed, 3 injured in road crash
content: KANPUR: Four members of a marriage party were killed while three others suffered serious injuries when the SUV they were traveling in crashed into a tree on the roadside on the national highway in Banda district on the intervening night of Sunday and Monday.Driver Rajeev alias Raju Tiwari (48), Devraj Dwivedi (65), Lakshmi Dwivedi (70) and Kailashi, who were travelling in the SUV were killed. Two of the injured have been referred to Kanpur, while one is undergoing treatment at the Banda district hospital.The 'baraat' (marriage procession) of Ankit Singh, a resident of Tindwari, had gone to Kurhun village of Badausa on Sunday."The accident occurred in Turra village of Badausa when the driver lost control on the vehicle ," said police. Police reached the spot and admitted the injured to hospital.

Title: 4 of marriage party killed, 3 injured in Banda road crash
content: Kanpur: Four members of a marriage party were killed while three others suffered serious injuries when the SUV they were traveling in crashed into a tree on the roadside on the national highway in Banda district on the intervening night of Sunday and Monday.Driver Rajeev alias Raju Tiwari (48), Devraj Dwivedi (65), Lakshmi Dwivedi (70) and Kailashi, who were travelling in the SUV were killed.Two of the injured have been referred to Kanpur, while one is undergoing treatment at the Banda district hospital.The â€˜baraatâ€™ (marriage procession) of Ankit Singh, son of Raju Singh, resident of Godhani village of Tindwari police station area, had gone to Kurhun village of Badausa police station area on Sunday. After â€˜Jaimalâ€™ rituals, seven â€˜baratisâ€™ (members of groom side) left for Godhani at 2am. â€œThe accident took place at Ansh Bunglan Purwa in Turra village under the limits of Badausa police station when the driver lost control on the vehicle and it dashed into a tree,â€ said additional superintendent of police Lakshmi Niwas Mishra.On being informed by passersby, police reached the spot and got the injured admitted to the district hospital. The injured have been identified as Mohit (22), Shivshankar (30) and Devi Prasad (36), said the official and added, â€œThe bodies of the deceased have been sent for postmortem.â€

Title: West Bengal couple on bike trip to Ladakh killed in road crash near Lucknow
content: LUCKNOW, KOLKATA: A middle-aged Kolkata-based couple who was on an adventure trip to Ladakh on its bike died in a road accident early Saturday in Barabanki, about 30 km from Lucknow.Subrata Sanyal (53) and his wife Paramita (45), residents of south Kolkataâ€™s Naktala locality, died after their mobike rammed into a divider on the Purvanchal Expressway.â€œSubrata sustained injuries on his head while Paramita was tossed in the air and fell a few feet away from her husband,â€ said Deepak Pandey, SHO of Gosain Ganj police station. They were taken to a local hospital, where they were declared dead on arrival. Pandey said the coupleâ€™s identity was established by their Aadhaar cards and phones. Cops contacted their friends and relatives who rushed to UP on Saturday.The duo, who had travelled to various destinations on their bike over the past few years, are survived by two sons. â€œThey had gone on adventure trips to Odisha and MP earlier,â€ said Subrataâ€™s Delhi-based friend Amitava Sanyal, who was among the first to reach Barabanki on Saturday.â€œThey were crazy for bike rides. Age never had any effect on their love for biking and adventure trips. It hurts to hear they died while doing what they loved most,â€ said Saraswati Mondal, a neighbour.The bodies were handed over to the family following a post-mortem and were cremated at Lucknowâ€™s Bhaisakund crematorium.

Title: 2 dead, 19 injured as truck hits tractor trolley
content: PATNA: Two people, including a minor girl, were killed when a speeding truck hit a tractor trolley parked along NH-80 near Kalyan Tola under the Bariyarpur police station area in Munger district around 4am on Monday. At least 19 people were injured in the accident and the condition of seven of them is stated to be critical.The victims were identified as four-year-old Mahi Kumari and Rita Devi (60). Both of them died on the spot. The injured people were undergoing treatment at Munger Sadar Hospital.The incident took place at Bariyarpur Bazar when Mannu Mahato, a resident of Basauni village in Lakhisarai district, was returning home with his family and relatives from Deoghar after the 'mundan' ceremony of his son Sachin Kumar. He had booked two tractors to carry the family members and guests. The tractors were parked along the road and their occupants were about to get down when a sand-laden truck hit one of the trolleys.The family members blocked the NH by keeping the bodies on the road and demanded arrest of the truck driver. Police reached the spot and pacified the people.SHO of Bariyarpur police station D K Pandey said the truck was seized but its driver managed to escape. "We are conducting raids to arrest the truck driver. The critically injured people are undergoing treatment in the emergency ward of the sadar hospital," he added.

Title: 5 killed, 7 injured as truck hits SUV in Maharashtra's Amravati district
content: MUMBAI: At least five members of a family, including two children, were killed and seven others injured when a speeding truck hit a sports utility vehicle (SUV) in Maharashtra's Amravati district, an official said on Tuesday. The accident occurred at around 11pm on Monday on Daryapur-Anjangaon Road under the Khallar police station limits in Amravati, located nearly 650 km from the state capital Mumbai, he said."All the five deceased, including two children, a woman, and two men, belonged to the same family. They were returning to Daryapur after attending a marriage ceremony at Anjangaon when the speeding truck rammed into the SUV from behind due to which it landed roadside," PTI quoted the official as saying.The truck driver fled after the accident, he said.The injured persons were admitted to a hospital in Daryapur.

Title: 2 killed as speeding car ploughs into 3 bystanders
content: Ludhiana: A speeding car ploughed into three people sitting on a bench on Samrala road in Khanna, killing two of them. The third person and the car driver was injured in the Sunday night incidentThe victims were identified as Gurpreet Singh (25) and Taranveer Singh, both belonging to a locality on Samrala road in Khanna. Balwinder Singh of same area and Asarpreet Singh of Chotta Khanna, who was driving the car, were admitted to a hospital. Cops said Gurpreet, Taranveer and Balwinder were sitting on a roadside bench near the bridge on Samrala road when the car coming from Samrala side crashed into them. They added that the impact was severe as two trees were uprooted, benches were shattered, and the shutter of a shop were damaged by the car.The car driver lost the control of the vehicle resulting in the accident. Empty liquor bottles were found in the vehicle, police said. The cops suspected that the driver was drunk. . .AdvertisementGurpreetâ€™s brother Gurdeep Singh said his younger brother had come from Bahrain about 15 days back. He said his brother, Taranveer and Balwinder were sitting on a roadside bench, when a speeding car ploughed into them. Local sources said Taranveer and Balwinder were seriously injured in the accident and the former later succumbed to his injuries at a hospital on Sunday night. DSP Khanna Karnail Singh said Balwinder has been referred to GMCH in Chandigarh whereas the driver of the car has been referred to a Ludhiana hospital. The police said they have registered a case against the car driver under Sections 304 A (causing death due to negligence), 279 (Rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (Causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 427 (Mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the IPC at Khanna city one police station.

Title: Two brothers killed in hit-and-run case in Telangana
content: WARANGAL: Two brothers were killed in a road accident on Monday after they were hit by an unknown vehicle at Ananthasagar crossroads, under Hasanparthy mandal police station limits. The deceased were identified as E Shivaram (26) and his brother E Harikrishna (23), residents of Huzurabad.Hasanparthy sub-inspector (SI) D Vijay Kumar said that the two brothers had visited their native village and on Monday morning, as the duo were returning to Hyderabad, an unknown vehicle rammed their two-wheeler killing them on the spot.

Title: Leopard dies after car runs over it in Kolhapur district
content: Kolhapur: A three-year-old female leopard was killed after it was hit by a vehicle near Kisrule village on the Guhaghar-Bijapur highway.According to forest department officials, the leopard was crossing the road to reach the Kafna river when it was hit by an unidentified vehicle, leading to its immediate death. Officials said the leopard sustained injuries in the face and could be the cause of death. Officials have conducted a panchanama of the incident and the leopard has been cremated. Meanwhile, environmentalists claim that the incidents of deaths of wild animals are on the rise and accuse forest department of not creating water sources for animals within the forest, forcing them to come out into urban areas.Umakant Chavan, an environment researcher said, â€œDeath of reptiles in road accidents used to happen but now leopards, monkeys and gaurs are also being killed. The water sources in the forest area are depleting and hence wild animals are forced to come out of their habitats in search of water. Over speeding in protected forest areas should be prohibited to avoid such incidents. In South India, roads passing through forest corridors are kept closed in the night as movement of wildlife takes place at night to a maximum extent. A similar rule can be applied here as well.â€

Title: Woman & 7-yr-old daughter killed as container rams them
content: Nashik: A woman and her seven-year-old daughter were killed in a road accident after they were hit by a speeding container on the Dwarka flyover around 6.30 am on Tuesday. The deceased have been identified as 29-year-old Nafita Shaikh, and her daughter Tarannum Shaikh. The Panchavati police said Afzal Shaikh, a truck driver from Gaulane, which is 14km from Nashik city, was transporting some material to Dhule. His wife and three daughters were accompanying him. Around 6.30 am, as the family was proceeding towards the Dhule from the Dwarka flyover, Shaikh stopped on the flyover to tighten the ropes around the articles he was carrying. His wife and daughters also came out of the truck. Just then, the driver of a speeding container, who apparently did not spot the stationary truck, rammed his vehicle into it. The Panchavati police said the container driver and the truckerâ€™s family suffered serious injuries in the accident. The woman and one of her daughters were declared brought dead at the hospital. The traffic police and the Panchavati police rushed to the incident spot to ensure that there was no traffic jam on the flyover. Senior police officers said the container driver may have dozed off and that may be the reason why he failed to spot the stationary truck and the truckerâ€™s family. The flyover from Dwarka towards Jatra Hotel has only two lanes on one side of the road. If any vehicle is parked or halted there, it leaves just one lane space for other vehicles.The police said the condition of the other injured people is stable. After examining the case, a complaint will be registered.

Title: 7 dead as cruiser vehicle falls into gorge in Jammu and Kashmir's Kishtwar
content: JAMMU: At least 7 people were killed and three others injured after a cruiser vehicle skidded off the road and fell into a deep gorge at Dangduru Dam site in Jammu and Kashmir's Kishtwar on Wednesday.Police said that the vehicle (JK-B6-3095) carrying workers of under-construction Dangduru Hydel Power Project met with an accident near the site in Dachhan area and plunged into 300-feet deep gorge."Six died on the spot while one critically injured succumbed during evacuation," police said, adding, "While three others were injured, a total of ten people were travelling in the vehicle."Police said, "The injured were later shifted to district hospital in Kishtwar."The deceased have been identified as Sudesh Kumar, Akhter Hussain, Abdul Rasheed, Mubassar Ahmad, Itva, Rahul and Karan Kumar. Police said that a case has been registered and investigations initiated.Confirming the seven casualties, senior superintendent of police (SSP), Kishtwar Khalil Poswal, said, "An unfortunate road accident of cruiser vehicle occurred near site of Dangduru Power Project (Dachan area), which was ferrying workers of project after their shift got changed.""Seven people were killed while three injured have been shifted to Kishtwar for specialised treatment, " SSP KIshtwar said.Meanwhile, Union minister Dr Jitendra Singh tweeted, "Spoke to DC #Kishtwar Dr Devansh Yadav about unfortunate road accident at Dangduru Dam site. Injured being shifted to District Hospital Kishtwar or GMC #Doda as per requirement. All possible help, as required, will be provided."Jammu and Kashmir lieutenant governor Manoj Sinha in an official communique has expressed grief over the loss of lives in a tragic road accident in Kishtwar.In a condolence message, the L-G said, â€œI convey my heartfelt condolences to the members of the bereaved families and wish those injured a speedy recovery.""I have instructed the district administration to provide all necessary assistance to the affected persons, " it added.Ghulam Nabi Azad demands inquiry, compensation for victimâ€™s kinDemocratic Progressive Azad Party chairman & former chief minister J&K Ghulam Nabi Azad on Wednesday sought a thorough inquiry in the fatal accident in Kishtwar.In a statement issued on Wednesday, Azad sought an adequate compensation for the next of kins of the victim families and special treatment to those injured in the tragic accident.Referring that locals have levelled allegations about the company of using outdated transport for the employees working on the project, Azad said, "The locals also have accused the company of not providing safety equipment to the employees due to which such unfortunate incidents happen." â€œSo I demand an inquiry into the allegations and alongside adequate compensation by both the company and government to the victim families,â€ he said.Meanwhile, Apni Party president Syed Mohammad Altaf Bukhari expressed his grief and sorrow over the accident in Kishtwar. "I am deeply saddened to hear about the tragic accident. I extend my heartfelt sympathies to the families of the deceased and pray for the speedy recovery of those who were injured in this mishap," Bhukhari said.

Title: Delhi: Biker killed in Pragati Maidan tunnel; poor signal delays call
content: NEW DELHI: The 19-year-old biker who had met with an accident in the Pragati Maidan tunnel on Monday night, as reported in TOI, later died of his injuries. There was a 15-minute delay in making a PCR call because of a connectivity problem inside the tunnel which, his family now claims, may have denied him a shot at life.A TOI reporter who was passing by stopped his car and tried to use the tunnel's SOS system, but couldn't get through.CCTV footage shows the biker, Rajan Rai, driving in the carriageway towards India Gate, suddenly veering towards the right and driving into the crash barriers and toppling over to the other side. The TOI reporter, returning home in the other carriageway, spotted Rai, bleeding from the head and unconscious, with a crowd gathered around him. He stopped his car and stepped out.How unresponsive SOS system in tunnel cost a life  According to police, the accident happened around 9.45 pm, as mentioned in the FIR. However, PWD has claimed that the accident took place at 9.53 pm. The police are investigating whether the bike had malfunctioned or Rai had dozed off.The security guards were doing their best to shield Rai from the oncoming traffic and warn drivers even as they made frantic phone calls, both to their superiors and the police, but couldn't connect. They showed the reporter how they had been trying to call without success. Those who had stopped by, including the reporter, too tried to call without any result.Strangely enough, the guards - besides the other commuters - seemed to be unaware of the emergency phone system installed inside the tunnel. When the reporter pressed the button located on the SOS system near the accident spot to call for help, there was no response from the other side. There was no public announcement either till the time he was present.Only after he moved further from the accident spot, towards the ramp on Ring Road which goes towards Noida and Sarai Kale Khan, did the reporter get back connectivity. He immediately called officials in charge of the tunnel to seek help. Around the same time, 10 pm, the PCR got a call from another person who had passed through the tunnel and they rushed to the site. Rai was taken to Lady Hardinge Hospital in a critical state. He was declared dead on arrival.The police said Rai was returning from Meerut and was on his way home in Uttam Nagar. He worked as a service engineer for a company in Jasola and it was his first job. The cops recovered Rai's helmet which had got completely crushed.A case under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way) and 304 A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC has been registered.Vinay Rai, a relative of the victim, said he had called his father at 8.30 pm and said he was coming home and they would have dinner together. "He had also asked his brother to go to his friend's home and help him file a form," he said. Later, when his family tried to call him, his phone was unreachable.Delhi government's PWD, which constructed the entire transit corridor and the tunnel, has been struggling to increase mobile network connectivity inside. It had invited telecom companies in December last year to install boosters."The work has been awarded and one of the companies has already installed the boosters but others are yet to do so. Hence, the problem of network connectivity persists. We are trying our best to speed up the work," a PWD official told TOI.When the tunnel was thrown open in June last year, the authorities had claimed that it was equipped with emergency telephone SOS systems, was being digitally monitored and that a public address system had been installed.

Title: 6 die, 14 injured as bus, container truck collide head-on in Buldhana
content: NAGPUR: Six people, including three women, were killed and another 14 injured after a Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus and container truck collided head on near Palaskher Chakka village on Nagpur-Jalna highway in Mehkar taluka in Buldhana district on Tuesday morning. Drivers of the bus and the container truck were among those killed.Though the exact reason behind the accident is yet to be ascertained, police stated that based on preliminary findings, speeding by both the drivers was responsible for the tragedy.The police have identified the deceased as MSRTC bus driver Raju Kulal (42), Sheshrao Kharabe (65), Gokarna Khillare (55), Vanmala Pawar (30), Seema Joshi (45), all bus passengers. The identity of the deceased container truck driver is yet to be known. Police said the state transport bus, with 33 passengers on board, was heading to Mehkar from Pune. The container truck was moving in the opposite direction. The front portion of both the vehicles was badly damaged due to the impact of collision. . .Advertisement

Title: 2 die, 5 injured after truck hits an auto in Ghatampur
content: Kanpur: Two persons, including a 16-year-old minor girl, were killed and five others injured after an auto in which they were travelling was hit by a truck in Ghatampur area of the district on Tuesday morning.The accident took place near Ahirwan petrol pump, causing a massive traffic jam. The auto was completely crushed due to the impact of the collision.On getting information about the mishap, police reached the spot and sent dead bodies of both the deceased for the post-mortem examination. The injured have been admitted to Lala Lajpat Rai hospital.Auto driver Manoj (25), said that as soon as the auto reached near Paras Nala on Mughal Road, two trucks collided while trying to overtake. After that his auto was also hit by one of the trucks. Subsequently, his auto fell into the roadside ditch due to its impact, he said.. .AdvertisementTwo passengers, including a minor girl travelling in the auto, died on the spot. The police identified the deceased minor girl as Salni of Asha Nagar ward. The identity of the other person could not be ascertained. Five people, including the auto driver, were seriously injured in the accident.tnn

Title: Trailer driver killed after crashing into dumper on Uran h'way
content: NAVI MUMBAI: A container trailer driver died due to grievous injuries after crashing into a dumper whose driver halted his vehicle in the middle of the Uran phata-Gavhan highway road after the dumper's tyre deflated in the early hours of Wednesday. Giridhar Gore, senior inspector of CBD police station informed that the deceased trailer driver has been identified as Amar Singh (52), employed with a transport company, Sethia Roadlines, in Mulund. "The accused dumper driver has been identified as Sarapat Ali Shaikh (22), who has been booked under relevant IPC sections and the Motor Vehicle Act for causing death by negligence and parking a vehicle in the wrong position which is punishable by law," said a police officer.Inspector Gore said, "The dumper driver Shaikh has not been arrested as per the provisions of the law and served with a notice under section 41(a)(1) of the criminal procedure code for cooperating in the investigation. As the dumper's rear tyre deflated while plying along the road, the driver Shaikh parked his vehicle in the middle of the road to prevent the tyre from further getting damaged further. He sat in the dumper but he did not abide by the traffic rule as he had not parked his vehicle on the extreme left lane and also did not keep the parking indicator nor did he display the danger notice signboard behind his dumper. Hence, the container trailer crashed into the halted dumper that killed the trailer driver on the spot."

Title: Four killed in two accidents
content: Trichy: Four people were killed and four others sustained injuries in two separate road accidents in Thanjavur and Pudukottai on Wednesday. According to police, Kuzhumi, 55, a native of Chennai was heading towards Pudukottai along with his son K Palaniyappan and relatives in an SUV, to attend a funeral in Kodumbalur near Viralimalai. While traveling, Palaniyappan who drove the vehicle lost his control and hit a TNSTC bus heading towards Manapparai from Vembanur on Kodumbalur-Pudukottai road.Kuzhumi and Palaniyappan were killed on the spot while three others in the SUV and the bus driver sustained injuries. Viralimalai police registered a case and are investigating. In another incident, two including a college student were killed near Thiruvaiyaru in Thanjavur on Wednesday. According to police R Archunan, 18, a second-year college student and S Balu, 23, a catering service employee were returning from Thanjavur riding a bike. While approaching Mariyamman temple near Arasur in Thanjavur they attempted to overtake a car. During the incident, a truck coming ahead hit the bike. Both died on the spot. Nadu Kaveri police registered a case and are inquiring into it.

Title: 4 people, 150 sheep killed in accident involving two trucks in Maharashtra's Hingoli district
content: AURANGABAD: Four people and 150 sheep were killed after a truck carrying the animals hit another truck in Maharashtra's Hingoli district early on Thursday, a police officer said. The accident took place at Malegaon phata on Nanded-Kalamnuri road at around 4am, he said, adding that traffic movement on the route was disrupted for some time. The truck carrying the sheep hit the other vehicle loaded with tiles from behind, Kalamnuri police inspector Vaijanath Munde told PTI. Four people and 150 sheep were killed, the official said. The deceased persons were travelling in the truck carrying the animals, he said. Another person travelling in the same truck received injuries and was sent to a hospital in neighbouring Nanded district for treatment, the official said. The police with the help of locals later removed the trucks from the road and it was cleared for traffic movement, he said.

Title: 2 dead, 4 injured in road crash in Delhi's Welcome area
content: NEW DELHI: Two helpers of a truck were killed and four people got injured after another truck hit the stationary vehicle in northeast Delhi's Welcome area in the early hours of Thursday, police said.  According to police, the incident took place around 12.30am on Thursday on the flyover near Welcome Metro Station.  Complainant Doje, a resident of Loni in Ghaziabad, UP, reported that he works as labourer in a truck. Naurang, also a resident of Loni, is the driver of the truck. Three other helpers were also present on the vehicle at the time of the accident. They were identified as Sunil, Ravi Kishan and Satish Kumar, deputy commissioner of police (northeast) Joy Tirkey said.  The truck was carrying bricks which was to be transported to Karol Bagh, police said.  Around 12.30 am, while the truck was on the flyover, one of its front tyres burst. Doje was opening the tyre bolts when another truck hit the stationary truck from behind, the DCP said.  Two people who died in the crash were identified as Ravi Kishan and Satish Kumar. Four others -- Doje, Mahesh, Naurang and Sunil -- got injured, police said, adding that Mahesh is the driver of the second truck that came from behind.  A case under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the Indian Penal Code has been registered at Welcome police station, DCP Tirkey said.  In another incident, a vagabond died on Thursday after he was ran over by an unidentified vehicle in north Delhi's Kashmere Gate area. The police received information regarding the incident at 2.55 am, another police officer said.  The vagabond was sleeping at a pavement near Nigambodh Ghat at the time of the accident. A case under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence) has been registered, police said.  The investigation is in progress and police are trying to identify the offending vehicle, they added.

Title: Ten killed in three accidents in Madhya Pradesh
content: BHOPAL/INDORE: Ten people were killed in different road accidents in the state on Wednesday. Three persons were killed and several others injured when a bus they were travelling in overturned on the National Highway over bridge in Umaria. The passengers were on their way to attend chief minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan's programme a few kms away.In another accident in Dewas district four persons including a woman and her two children were killed and two others injured after a collision between a loading auto and a dumper truck on Indore-Bhopal bypass while three labourers died in an accident in Khandwa.In Umaria, the accident took place merely kilometers away from the venue where the chief minister's program was scheduled to be held. The incident triggered a severe traffic jam. 10L each, job to kin of deceased: CM  The traffic congestion posed a significant challenge for the authorities, who strived to clear the situation. Confirming the casualties, Kotwali police station in-charge Ragvendra Tiwari said that three died on the spot while one has been referred to Jabalpur with critical injuries. Twenty four others have sustained minor injuries and they are being treated at various government health facilities."Of the deceased, two were on a motorcycle and one was on the bus. The accident took place at an overbridge. The bus overturned and fell over the motorcycle killing the two persons who were riding it, one passenger who was in the bus also died," he added.He said that eight injured individuals were admitted to the Umaria district hospital for medical treatment.Chief minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan extended his condolences and assured full support to the family of victims. He also announced a financial assistance of Rs 10 lakh each and job to the kin of deceased.Meanwhile, Dewas SP Sampat Upadhyay said that a loading auto was hit by a heavy vehicle while crossing the divider. The dumper truck cleaner Dharmendra, a resident of Shankargarh in Dewas, Rani, a resident of Sagar district, and her two children Hrithik, 2, and Anshu, 3, died in the accident.Rani's husband Suraj, 35 and the driver of the auto-rickshaw Bablu, a resident of Raisen district sustained severe injuries in the accident. They are undergoing treatment at Dewas district hospital.The driver, Bablu told police that he was going from Bhopal to Indore. The family of Suraj had boarded the auto from Shiv Nagar locality in Bhopal and they left around 10 pm on Tuesday from Bhopal. The dumper broke the road divider, hit auto in a head on collision.In another accident that took place in Mundi in Khandwa district three labourers were killed when the pick-van they were travelling overturned after the driver lost control of the steering. Mundi inspector DK Tiwari said the incident took place around 1:30 am in Devla village and the pick-p van was carrying chillies.Three persons including Jitendra, son of Pyar Singh Bhilala, 28, Idu, son of Kalu, 30, Sukhram Bhilala, 35, all residents of Jamniya in Khargone were killed on the spot while four others including whereas Dhan Singh, 40, Vikram Bhilala, 25, Pintu Singh, 18 and Kailash, 25 residents of Balkhad village were injured in the accident. They were rushed to the hospital where they were admitted for treatment.TNN

Title: Bike taxi driver killed, pillion rider suffers injuries
content: CHENNAI: A 33-year-old bike taxi rider was killed in a road accident near Padi flyover on Tuesday night after colliding with a van. The pillion rider survived with minor injuries. The deceased has been identified as Annamalai, a native of Thoothukudi district. He was staying in a rented room in Vadapalani. The pillion rider has been identified as Gautham. On Tuesday night, he accepted the ride to pick up and drop off a customer-Gautham- from Thanthangkuppam near Villivakkam. The two headed to a restaurant in Korattur. The accident took place after Annamalai got onto the Padi flyover and took a sharp turn towards Korattur. Police said that the bike collided with a van carrying cash. Although both were wearing helmets, Annamalai was killed on the spot as the van ran over on his stomach and chest, said Thirumangalam traffic investigation wing inspector Kausalya. Police reached the spot and sent the body to Kilpauk Medical College hospital for post-mortem. Gautham was admitted to the hospital. The van driver Hemnath, 40, was arrested under the charges of death caused due to negligent driving. Police are scanning CCTV footage and further investigations are on. Earlier on May 7, a woman Sevika who hailed a bike taxi to reach home was killed in a road accident near Teynampet.

Title: 4 friends on way to get driving licence killed as car hits tree in UP's Mathura
content: AGRA: Four friends were killed and another seriously injured when a car they were travelling in veered off course and collided with a tree in Uttar Pradesh's Mathura district around 9.30 am on Wednesday. They were on their way to the regional transport office (RTO) in Baldeo town as three of them wanted to apply for driving licences. According to police, the accident took place near Pilkhuni village under Raya police station limits. SHO Ajay Kishore said locals claimed that the incident occurred when a biker abruptly changed course upon spotting a pothole on the road and in a bid to avoid hitting the biker, the car driver lost control of the vehicle and rammed into the tree. All of them were residents of Baldeo town.Those dead have been identified as Ankit Kumar (22), Achal Singh (23), Akash Kumar (21) and Yogesh Kumar (25). Meanwhile, the critically injured, Shailendra Kumar (24), has been referred to a higher medical centre in Agra , said officials.Superintendent of police (SP) Shailesh Kumar Pandey said, â€œFive people were injured and rushed to district hospital, where doctors declared four of them brought dead. The injured person is currently undergoing treatment.â€He added, â€œA probe is on into the matter and the bodies have been sent for post-mortem. The reason behind the accident is yet to be ascertained.â€ Meanwhile, UP CM Yogi Adityanath on Wednesday expressed grief over the loss of lives in the mishap.

Title: 2 youngsters killed in road accident
content: Nashik: Two youngsters riding a bike were killed in a road accident on Trimbak Road in the early hours of Wednesday as the rider ended up ramming his motorcycle against the road divider.The deceased persons are identified as Arnav Patil (23), a resident of Ambad Link Road, Kamatwade, and his friend Karan Jaybhave (22) of Shivaji Nagar Satpur. According to the cops, on Wednesday, Patil was speeding the bike towards the Civil Hospital side on the Trimbak Road when he apparently lost control over his two-wheeler and rammed into the road divider. Both Patil and Jaybhave, riding pillion on the bike, suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same.The Sarkarwada police rushed the two youngsters to the Nashik civil hospital. However, they were declared brought dead. The police said that the family members of the two boys, both college-going students, informed them that their wards were out with their friends the whole night.. .AdvertisementThe police are investigating the case further.

Title: 1 killed, 3 hurt as car rams railing near campus
content: Patiala: A woman died while three others were injured in a car accident that took place at Patiala â€“ Rajpura road near Punjabi University, Patiala, on Thursday morning. The police said that the car with four occupants lost control and hit the railing dividing the slip road from the national highway. The police said that the deceased girl was identified as Kritika (20) of Sanaur in Patiala district. The police said that car driver Piyush was also injured but was out of danger.ASI Baljinder Singh said, â€œAll of them were rushed to government hospital where Kritika was declared brought dead while the other three are undergoing treatment. We have registered a case under Sections 174 CrPC and the statement of the deceased's father was recorded. The four car occupants were moving towards Sanaur from Patiala when their car had hit the railing at 5.30 am on Thursday.â€

Title: 19-yr-old killed as bike hits garbage collection vehicle
content: Indore: A 19-year-old youth was killed and two seriously injured after a bike they were riding hit a garbage collection vehicle in MIG area on Wednesday late night. The youths were returning from a party when the accident took place.MIG police station in-charge Ajay Verma said that the accident took place near Amaltas hotel at around 11:45 pm when the youth, Krish, was on his way to Vijay Nagar with Santosh and Samar. Krish was severely injured on head and rushed to hospital where he was declared dead. Doctors said that he had lost lot of blood due to which he could not be saved. Samar and Santosh were seriously injured.Krish was pursuing BBA from a private college in Vijay Nagar and had been living with his family in Scheme 78 for the past few years. Santosh, a native of Sendhwa, resides at Rajiv Gandhi square while Samar is resident of Sapna Sangeeta Road. Both are studying engineering in city.Police registered a case and started investigation. Body of Krish was handed over to the family members after postmortem exam.

Title: Woman killed in Behala bike accident
content: Kolkata: A woman in her 40s was killed and five others were injured in four separate accidents, all involving two-wheelers, in the city on Friday.Around 11.30 am, a woman riding pillion with her husband was killed on DH Road at Pathakpara crossing. Police said a mini-truck hit the scooter and then another stationary mini-truck and a taxi. Razia Sultana (40) died on the spot while her husband Fazrul Rahman Sardar (54) suffered injuries and was admitted to SSKM.Both of them were wearing helmets but such was the impact that the woman was flung off the vehicle. Cops said there was no violation on part of the rider. Police have seized the offending vehicle but the mini-truck driver fled. Around the same time, a 28-year-old woman, Drishti Sanghvi, riding a scooter was injured when a car hit her on JL Nehru Road-Shakespeare Sarani crossing. . .AdvertisementAnother woman, Howrahâ€™s Sukanya Kundu (36), riding pillion on an app bike was injured when it was hit by a car at Ekbalpore around 12.30 pm. Two youths in their early 20s riding a bike on Maa flyover were injured when it skidded off the carriageway around 9.45am.

Title: Truck ploughs into Bihar-bound bus near Greater Noida; boy killed, 27 injured
content: NOIDA: A private bus headed to Bihar from Punjab was hit by a truck on the Eastern Peripheral Expressway in the early hours of Friday, leaving an 11-year-old boy dead and 27 other passengers of the bus injured.The accident happened at Ladpura village in the Kasna area of Greater Noida and added to the toll in the 363 accidents reported in the district in the first four months of the year. At least 128 people died and 289 suffered injuries in these road accidents, the traffic department data states.Santosh Shukla, the in-charge of Kasna police station said the accident took place around 2.30am. The bus driver allegedly dozed off while at the wheels and the bus came in the truck's way suddenly. The truck driver, police said, could not swerve the vehicle in time and ploughed into the middle of the bus. "At least 28 people, including the bus driver, suffered injuries and were taken to GIMS in Greater Noida by cops. Ashish (11), a resident of Bhojpur in Bihar, was grievously injured and died during treatment," Shukla said. All 65 passengers had boarded the bus from Khanna in Punjab. "They were mostly daily wages workers from Bihar, Nepal and West Bengal who worked in the fields in Punjab. The bus had left for Bihar on Thursday evening," Shukla said.Dr (Brig) RK Gupta, the director of GIMS in Greater Noida, where the accident victims were taken to, said at least 10 were severely injured with fractures, blunt trauma and degloving injuries. "One minor died during treatment, but all other patients are stable," Gupta said. No case has been registered in the matter as yet, police said. The families of the injured have been informed.According to DCP (traffic) Anil Kumar Yadav a bulk of the accidents reported on the expressway occur between 2am and 5am. In a number of cases, the crashes as drivers of these long-distance vehicles doze off at the wheels due to fatigue."To bring down the number of accidents and to analyse their causes, an Integrated Road Accident Database (iRAD) was launched recently. We have identified 11 accident black spots in the district so far and are planning to station police response vehicles and ambulances within a 100 metres radius of these black spots," the DCP said.

Title: Two of a family die in accident
content: Vadodara: Two persons died and five others were injured when a van they were travelling in met with an accident near Vadagam in the Khanpur taluka of Mahisagar district. All the passengers were members of the same family. The family were returning to Surat from Banswada in Rajasthan when the accident occurred on Friday afternoon. The driver of the van lost control of the vehicle and it fell in a storm water drain near the road. Ramchandra Kumavat and his daughter-in-law Sangita were killed on the spot, while five injured including Sangitaâ€™s husband and three children were rushed to Lunawada and then to Godhra hospital for treatment. Condition of the injured was stable and Sangitaâ€™s husband told the police that the driver swerved to one side of the road and was not able to regain control. tnn

Title: 5 dead as car plunges into Sharda river in US Nagar
content: RUDRAPUR: Five people, including three children, lost their lives on Thursday when the car in which they were travelling plunged into the Sharda river near the Lohia Head Power Channel at Khatima in Udham Singh Nagar district. The victims have been identified as Draupadi Devi (34), an employee of Lohia Head Power House, her daughter Jyoti (10), niece Deepika (7), nephew Sonu (5), and car driver Mohan Singh Dhami (40), all residents of Khatima. The accident took place when they were returning from the house of Devi's brother in Chakarpur Anjania village to Khatima. According to police, after finishing her work, Draupadi Devi had gone to her brother's house to pick up his children on her way home via the Sharda canal road. Police suspect the vehicle veered off the road and fell into the river. When Draupadi did not arrive home as expected, her brother Mohan Chand and his wife began searching for her. Their search led them to the car which was submerged in the river. Mohan Chand immediately informed police, who arrived at the scene and, with the assistance of divers, recovered the bodies of all five people from the vehicle. They were rushed to the local hospital, where doctors declared them brought dead. "Postmortem examinations are underway at Khatima Civil Hospital," said SHO Ravindra Singh Bishtcthat.. .Advertisement Meanwhile, a faculty member of a Kashipur-based school, Babli, 25, was killed, while another teacher and three school children, two girls and a boy, were injured, when one of three buses taking students to a picnic in Moradabad overturned in Kashipur. Superintendent of police (city) Kashipur, Abhay Singh, said, "We'll take action based on the findings of the investigation."

Title: Couple, two kids killed in road accident in UP's Pratapgarh
content: PRAYAGRAJ: A 40-year old man, his wife and two minor kids died in a road accident in Pratapgarh district on Saturday. The deceased were travelling on a bike when a car hit them. The deceased have been identified as Manoj Singh (40), a resident of village Pure Mahabal in Sangipur area of Pratapgarh, his wife Seema (37), son Shivam (5) and daughter Sapna (8). The incident took place near Prithviganj Bazar on the busy Varanasi-Lucknow highway in Pratapgarh district on Saturday morning, said the police.The four persons travelling in the car escaped with minor injuries. "The car has been seized and the driver, a resident of Jaunpur, was detained but later released on personal bond. All four bodies have been sent for post-mortem examination," said Satendra Singh, SHO of Kotwali, Pratapgarh.

Title: Six killed, six injured in two accidents
content: UJJAIN/INDORE: Six people were killed in two separate accidents in Mandsaur and Shajapur districts on Saturday.Three people including two women members of a family were killed and five others sustained injuries when the van they were travelling hit a trolley parked on the roadside on Manasa-Mandsaur Road. In another accident three persons including a husband-wife duo were killed in an accident near village Aagkhedi in Shajapur district.In Mandsaur, the passengers were returning from Ujjain after offering prayers at Mahakal temple on a van.As the van hit the stationary trolley, the nearby residents woke up to the loud sound of collision and cries of the injured and alerted the police. The injured had to be extricated from the mangled van after cutting the body with gas cutters.The injured were rushed to hospital, where doctors declared three of them brought dead. The deceased were identified as Sandeep Patidar, 35, Sushila Patidar, 65, and Jayanti Bai, 32. All three deceased were residents of Deori Khawasa, police said.The injured were identified as Chetna, 12, Naman, 8, Pappu Patidar, 32, and Kamla Bai, 54, and the driver. They were admitted to the hospital, police said.Manasa police station in-charge RC Dangi told TOI, "The incident took place at 4.45 am, when the family members were returning from Ujjain after offering prayers to lord Mahakal. The van driver lost control and hit a trolley parked on the road.""Of the five injured, four are stable and one was referred to Udaipur, Dangi said.Meanwhile, a man and his wife were killed when the bike they were riding collided head-on with another two-wheeler in Kalapipal. Another person riding the second vehicle was also declared dead at the hospital while pillion rider is serious, police said.Kalapipal police station in-charge Ravi Bhandari said, "The two-men died on the spot while the woman died in hospital undergoing treatment." TNN

Title: Mother-daughter duo killed in freak accident
content: Wardha: A woman and her one-and-a-half-year-old daughter were killed in a freak road accident near Bazarwada village on Saturday. Nilima Sayam (27) her daughter Tanushka came under the rear wheel of a tanker after falling off a bike and were killed on the spot.Police said that Rajkumar Sayam was taking his wife and twin daughters on a two-wheeler from Virul to Nerpingalai in Morshi of Amravati district for a wedding ceremony. Near Bazarwada Fata, Nilimaâ€™s sari got entangled in the wheel of the bike due to which Rajkumar lost control over the two-wheeler.As four of the Sayam fell on the road, Nilima and Tanushka were crushed under the rear wheel of a tanker that was overtaking the ill-fated bike at the precise moment. Rajkumar and the other daughter, who was sitting in front, were injured in the accident.

Title: 2 dead, 4 injured as SUV rams road roller on Pune-Bâ€™luru hâ€™way
content: Kolhapur: Two men were killed and four others were seriously injured after an SUV rammed a road roller on the Pune-Bengaluru national highway on Saturday morning. The accident took place near the Ghunki phata in Kolhapur district. The deceased have been identified as Rahul Shikhare, a 30-year-old resident of Minche village, and 28-year-old Suyog Pawar, a resident of Top village. Both villages are in the Hatkanangle tehsil of the district. The injured men are Top residents Sunil Kurne (24) and Vaibhav Chougule (23), as well as Aniket Jadhav (22) and Nikhil Shikhare (27) from Minche village. As per the preliminary information, the accident took place on the Pune-Bengaluru National Highway between Kini toll plaza and Ghunki Phata at 6.30am. Six people from Top, Shiye and Minche villages â€” who were in the business of providing lights, decoration and sound systems at events â€” had gone to Mumbai for an exhibition. After the exhibition, they left Mumbai at 11pm on Friday in their SUV (MH-48 AK 6545). On their way back home, at 6.30am on Saturday, the SUV driver made an error in judgement and rammed a road roller moving towards the Kini toll plaza. While the front of the SUV was completely smashed, even the road roller overturned due to the impact.Incidentally, as the six-laning works are ongoing here, there is no side road for vehicles to use. Therefore, traffic on the highway came to a standstill for almost half an hour following the accident.Farmers working in the nearby fields and other highway commuters rushed to the aid of the people involved in the accident. The six injured were immediately rushed to the CPR hospital in Kolhapur, however, Suyog Pawar and Rahul Shikhare were declared brought dead. Road roller driver Dadaso Dabade, a 40-year-old from Wathar village, also got minor injuries. A case has been registered and further investigation is ongoing.

Title: Former ZPTC member killed in car-RTC bus collision in Warangal
content: WARANGAL: Former ZPTC of Kesamudram, Vem Purushotham Reddy, 65, died in a road accident near Gangadevipalli village of Geesugonda mandal on Saturday. Purushotham was on his way to Warangal from Narsampet, when his car collided with an RTC bus coming in the opposite direction and he died on the spot. The impact of the collision was such that the car got totally damaged. The body was later shifted to MGM Hospital for post mortem. He was the brother of former MLA Vem Narender Reddy. A native of Arpanapally village in Kesamudram mandal, Purushotham Reddy was living in Hanumakonda with his family for a quite long time. The last rites would be conducted at Arpanapally. Geesugonda police are probing the incident.

Title: 7 students of Assam Engineering College killed, 5 injured in road accident in Guwahati's Jalukbari
content: GUWAHATI: As many as seven students of Assam Engineering College were killed whereas five others sustained injuries in a road accident that took place at Jalukbari area in Assam's Guwahati late on Sunday night, said police.Joint police commissioner, Guwahati, Thube Prateek Vijay Kumar said that seven people died in the accident."As per the preliminary investigation, we have found that the deceased persons are students. The incident took place at Jalukbari area," Thube Prateek Vijay Kumar said.More details awaited.. .Advertisement

Title: 4 killed, 3 hurt in road accident
content: PRAYAGRAJ: Four persons including three women were killed and three others sustained injures in road accident when tractor trolley carrying them jumped into a dry canal and turned turtle near Ghoogha village under limits of Koraon police station on Saturday night. Injured persons were admitted at SRN hospital for treatment. Police said the incident took place when the victims were returning from after attending a birthday celebration party. The deceased have been identified as Rita Devi (30), Suman Devi (28), Ashish Kumar (20), and Shiv Kumari (30) all residents of Koraon. Police said that a group of 20-25 persons in two separate tractors had gone to Sazi village to attend a birthday celebration. While returning, the driver of a tractor lost his control over the vehicle and the tractor along with trolley jumped into a dry canal and turned turtle. The police with help of locals rescued the trapped persons under the trolley and brought eight persons to community health centre where doctors declared three of them brought dead. The doctors referred injured persons to trauma centre in city.

Title: 4 killed in 2 accidents in Bundi and Baran dists
content: Kota: A middle-aged couple and a 70-year-old man and his 25-year-old son were killed in two separate road accidents at Talera in Bundi and at Anta in Baran respectively on Sunday evening and Saturday night.The couple, identified as Ram Naresh Meghwal (39), residents of Jharbalapura in Bundi district and his wife Sumitra (35) were killed on the spot after a speedy car hit their motorbike from the opposite direction on the Talera bypass of Bundi district.The mishap occurred at around 5.30 PM on Sunday. In the accident, the couple on the bike were killed due to the impact while the car also overturned after hitting the bike and its passengers also sustained minor injuries, said assistant sub inspector Kamlesh Gurjar.The postmortem was conducted at a community health centre in Talera, he added.Similarly, a 70-year-old man, identified as Ratan Lal Regar (70) and his son Suresh were killed on the spot after their motorbike fell into a roadside ditch on Anta â€“ Mangrole route on Saturday night.The father-son duo, residents of Mangrol, were returning home on the bike on Saturday evening when they fell into a roadside ditch killing the two on the spot, said Anta police SHO at Anta.

Title: Couple killed, two kids hurt in Raigarh road accident
content: Raipur: A couple, on their way to attend a family function, were killed when a speeding trailer rammed in their bike near Kharasia in Raigarh.The police officials said that the deceased have been identified as 30-year-old Toresh Patel and 28-year-old Bina Patel. The couple was travelling with their two childrenâ€” one-and-a-half-year-old Vedanshi Patel and a six-year-old Shreyansh Patel â€” who were also injured in the accident. The family was heading towards their destination between Ranisagar when a high-speed trailer collided head-on with their bike.The couple died on the spot. The truck driver abandoned his vehicle and fled from the spot, police added. Locals and passers-by, who saw the accident, immediately alerted the police. The injured children were rushed to the hospital, while the bodies of the deceased were sent for autopsy.Relatives of the victims were later notified about the tragic accident. The police seized the trailer and registered a case. Search for the accused driver is on, the police told TOI. TNN. .Advertisement

Title: In bid to elope, runaway bride, lover die in road accident in UP's Mirzapur
content: VARANASI: Three persons including a girl, whose marriage was scheduled on Sunday, her lover and cousin brother were killed when their motorcycle collided with a truck near Sumatia village under the limits of Jigna police station in Mirzapur district on Saturday night.Station officer Jigna Arvind Pandey on Sunday said during initial investigation of the incident it came to light that due to over-speeding the uncontrolled motorcycle collided with a truck coming from opposite direction while overtaking another vehicle near Sumatia village. All three persons, including two boys and a girl on the motorcycle were killed on the spot. Pandey said after the family members of girl and boys reached the spot they were identified as Rani, Karan and Vikas, all aged around 21 years, said police.It also came to light that marriage of Rani was fixed with a boy from Prayagraj and was scheduled on Sunday evening. Rani was not ready to marry the boy her family had chosen for her as she had an affair with a boy, who was a relative of her cousin brother. The girl, her cousin and her lover prepared a plan for her to elope. The boys reached Raniâ€™s home on a motorcycle and escaped.They had travelled only a kilometer when they met with an accident and died on the spot. Pandey said the bodies were sent for postmortem.

Title: Army truck crushes 12-yr-old boy
content: Jaisalmer: An army truck crushed a 12-year-old boy while he was crossing a road, 25 km away from Jaisalmer district headquarters on the Jodhpur route from Jaisalmer. Sadar police reached the spot. The truck was seized and after necessary action, the boy's body was handed over to his family.SHO Manoj Samariya said that Bhairon Singh, a resident of Dediya village of Jodhpur, had come to his maternal grandparentsâ€™ house at Sanwara village in Jaisalmer. He got down from the bus and was crossing the road when the accident occurred, killing him on the spot.Although the family members protested and refused to take the body, after persuasion they accepted the body. Police are investigating the case.

Title: Senior citizen killed in road accident
content: PUNE: A 76-year-old man from Beed district was killed and his 53-year-old cousin suffered severe injuries after their motorcycle was hit by a dumper at Alandi on Sunday night. The accident happened when the duo was heading towards Markal road to meet their nephew at the latterâ€™s pharmacy. An officer from the Alandi police told TOI that the victim, Tatya Dhotre, and his cousin Parmeshwar Dhotre, both from Gevrai in Beed district, had come to Alandi on Sunday evening to meet the latterâ€™s son, who also runs a pharmacy along the Alandi-Wadgaon Road. â€œThey went to meet pareshwarâ€™s son around 6.30pm,â€ the officer said.He said after spending some time, they decided to go to their nephewâ€™s pharmacy along the Alandi-Markal Road. After driving some distance, a dumper knocked their motorcycle. â€œThe victim came under the dumper and was killed on the spot ,â€ the officer said. The police have booked the dumper driver on the charge of causing death by negligence. . .Advertisement

Title: Four killed in road accident in Dindigul
content: Dindigul: Four men died after two two-wheelers collided with each other near Oddanchatram in Dindigul district on Sunday.According to police, Rathinam and Sekar from Seethapatti in Palayam area in Dindigul were proceeding East from Oddanchatram on a two-wheeler on Sunday night. Around the same time, Sudhakar of Reddiarchatram and his coworker Durairaj of Thennampatti in Dindigul, were moving West from Vedasandur. When they were crossing near Kollapatti on Vedasandur road, they collided with one another violently.Due to the impact, Rathinam, Sekar, and Sudhakar died on the spot while Durairaj suffered critical injuries. Meanwhile, another two-wheeler rider Praveen Kumar of Seethamaram Naal road in Dindigul, who was unaware of the accident, hit a truck which had halted near the accident spot and suffered injuries.Durairaj and Praveen Kumar were rushed to Oddanchatram government hospital. However, Durairaj died without responding to treatment. Praveen Kumar was referred to Dindigul government medical college hospital for further treatment. All the four bodies of the deceased were sent for post mortem examination. The Oddanchatram police registered a case and were investigating.

Title: 3 of family killed as MUV falls off bridge in Nashik
content: NASHIK: Three people, including a four year-old-girl, of a family died when the multi-utility vehicle (MUV) in which they were travelling fell from a bridge on the Nandgaon-Manmad Road in the wee hours of Tuesday.The injured people, including four women and three men, have been rushed to a government hospital in Malegaon.The deceased people were identified as Yakub Mansuri (50), Afroz Mansuri (25) and Sifa Mansuri (4).The Nandgaon police said that the family of 10 was travelling from Nandgaon to Malegaon after attending a wedding function. The person driving the car apparently dozed off when they were crossing a bridge over a river close to the Nagya Sakya dam after which the MUV ran over the railing and fell 25 feet down into the dry river.The police said that as soon as they received information, they rushed to the incident spot and with the help of the local citizens shifted the injured people to a government hospital in Nandgaon and then to Malegaon by ambulances.An officer of the Nandgaon police station said the railing of the bridge is very short owing to which the MUV ran over it and fell down.

Title: Seven students killed in Guwahati road accident
content: Guwahati: Seven students of the Assam Engineering College were killed and six others injured when the vehicle they were travelling in turned turtle after hitting a road divider and then hit a vehicle on the other side of the road in Guwahatiâ€™s Jalukbari area early on Monday. The deceased have been identified as Arindam Bhallal from Guwahati, Niyor Deka from Golaghat, Kaushik Mohan from Charaideo, Upangshu Sarmah from Nagaon, Rajkiran Bhuyan from Majuli, Emon Gayan from Dibrugarh and Kaushik Baruah from Mangaldoi. Among the six injured, three were friends of the deceased and were in the same vehicle. The other three were in another vehicle that was hit. Ten third-year students left the college premises in an SUV they had taken on rent for the day and were returning from Azara end to Jalukbari when their car first hit the road divider and then another vehicle and turned upside down at 1 am on Monday. Police said one of the three injured students was in the driverâ€™s seat. Chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, who visited the accident spot, said, â€œExtremely anguished by the loss of young and precious lives in the road accident. My deepest condolences to their parents and families. I have spoken to authorities at GMCH. All possible medical assistance is being provided to those injured.â€Meanwhile, Assam DGP GP Singh, who also inspected the site, instructed DCP (West) Padmanabh Baruah and the Kamrup (Metro) district transport officer Gautam Das to conduct an inquiry and analyse as to what exactly led to the fatal road accident.

Title: Girl killed in New Town accident
content: Kolkata: A 20-year-old girl riding pillion on a bike was killed when a commercial vehicle of a cable TV network hit the two-wheeler from behind on New Town Main Arterial Road near Eco Park Gate 2 around 10pm on Monday. Swagata Ghosh, a New Barrackpore resident, was wearing a helmet but such was the impact that it came off and she died on the spot. The rider, too, was critically injured and admitted to a hospital. Meanwhile, four persons were injured in five accidents in Kolkata between Sunday night and Monday. tnn

Title: 10 killed, 21 injured in 4 road accidents in UP
content: LUCKNOW: Ten persons, including four of a family, were killed while 21 others were injured in four accidents reported from across the state late Sunday and on Monday.Four persons of a family were killed and three injured when driver of a car lost control of the vehicle and rammed it into the divider on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway near Faguha Bhatta under the limits of Kotwali police station in Kannauj on Monday afternoon. The occupants of the car were residents of Sheikhpur village under Airwakatra police station limits of Auraiya district and were returning after attending a marriage function in Lucknow on Sunday. Some injured in critical conditionIn Jhansi, two persons including a woman died on the spot when a speeding truck hit an auto-rickshaw in Pooch area of the district on Monday. The accident occurred on the state highway near village Khilli in Pooch. The auto-rickshaw, carrying around a dozen persons from Moth to Pooch. All the injured were sent to CHC Moth from where they were shifted to Jhansi medical college in a critical condition. As per CO Moth Shweta Kumari, police were trying to locate the truck and its driver. In Etawah, two pedestrians were killed when a truck hit them after ramming a private bus from behind on the busy Kanpur-Agra highway late Sunday night. Seven of the 50 passengers on board the bus were injured and admitted to the Etawah district hospital for treatment. Police said on Monday that the accident took place on the Kanpur-Agra highway Sunday night. In Varanasi, two brothers were killed after an uncontrolled truck ran over them after hitting their motorcycle at an overbridge in Rohania on Sunday night. Police said the duo died on the spot.

Title: 4 die, 4 hurt in 2 accidents
content: Ranchi/Hazaribag: Four persons died in two road accidents in Ranchi and Hazaribag on Monday, police said. Two youths were killed in a head-on collision between a motorcycle and a truck near Kochbong under the Kharsidag police station limits of Ranchi district on Monday. The deceased are Gautam Mahli (20) and Akash Kachhap (23). Vikas Linda has been admitted to a local hospital with serious injuries, police added. In a separate incident, two persons died and three were hurt when a hydraulic vehicle collided head-on with a truck in Hazaribag . tnn

Title: Three killed as car crashes, catches fire on Samruddhi
content: Nagpur: An accident on the Samruddhi Expressway claimed three lives on Monday. Two passengers of a sedan car (MH02 CR 1459) were burnt alive after the vehicle caught fire in the wee hours. The driver of the car, Ajaykumar Bhilala, 24, managed to get out of the burning car but succumbed to the injuries at a hospital.The accident happened near Deulgaon Kol village on Dusar-Beed interchange in Buldhana district. The car hit an impact barrier on the side of the road before turning into a fireball.The identity of the two burned to death could not be confirmed till the filing of the report. Buldhana police said the three victims are likely to be from Madhya Pradesh. The Samruddhi Expressway has witnessed 39 accidents since it became operational in December last year.The car was towing an SUV. Cops said both vehicles were carrying around 20 cans of diesel. This hints at an organized diesel theft racket on the Samruddhi Expressway, said cops. The diesel was probably stolen from vehicles, especially trucks, parked on the expressway.Police said the half dozen diesel cans inside the car probably led to the fire which gutted the vehicle. Cops found 14 cans of diesel inside the SUV, which rolled for around 200m after the towing rope snapped in the accident. The passengers of the SUV fled the spot.Highway security police (HSP) SP Yeshwant Solankhe said the two vehicles entered the Samruddhi Expressway from Jalna. â€œVillagers spotted some passengers fleeing the SUV,â€ said Solankhe, adding the groupâ€™s involvement in diesel theft cannot be ruled out. â€œAfter entering the expressway at Jalna, the car travelled almost 100km before the accident,â€ he said.Assistant police inspector Sadanand Sonkamble of Bibi police station in Buldhana said the reason behind the accident is yet to be ascertained. â€œItâ€™s not clear how and why the car veered to one side and crashed into the impact barriers,â€ Sonkamble said, adding the highway security police reached the spot within a short time and shifted the bodies and the injured driver to hospital.â€œAn offence will be registered against Bhilala. We expect to get more information from his relatives,â€ Sonkamble said.

Title: 10 killed, 57 injured as bus ferrying Vaishno Devi pilgrims falls down from Bridge in Jammu, LG announces ex- gratia
content: JAMMU: Ten Mata Vaishno Devi pilgrims were killed and 57 others (majority are Biharâ€™s Lakhisarai area) were injured on early Tuesday morning as their sleeper bus skidded off from bridge on Jammu-Srinagar highway and fell down into a deep gorge in Jammu district.The Lieutenant Governor, Manoj Sinha has approved Rs 5 lakh each for the next of kin of those who have lost their lives due to a tragic bus accident in Jammu and Rs 50,000 each for those seriously injured.The Lt Governor also has directed the district administration to take care of the treatment and provide all assistance to the bereaved families.According to officials, the death toll may go up as the condition of two injured admitted in Govt Medical College and Hospital (GMC&H) Jammu is stated to be critical.According to police, the bus (UP 81 CT 3537), on its way to Katra from Amritsar met with the accident in Jhajjar Kotli area. Katra is a base camp for the pilgrims visiting the famous shrine atop the Trikuta hills."The bus skidded off the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway and rolled down the bridge over a nallah at Jajjar Kotli early this morning around 6.30 am, the officials said.They said most of the travellers were from Biharâ€™s Lakhisarai and were on pilgrimage to Mata Vaishno Devi for a religious function of their child."The accident took place at Jhajjar Kotli bridge. Ten people have died. Rescue operation is almost compete," Jammu Senior Superintendent of Police Chandan Kohli told media persons here today. "We are investigating the reasons behind the accident", he said.SSP Jammu Kohli said, "Local residents, CRPF and police personnel rushed to the spot and launched the rescue operation.Around fifty-seven injured have been admitted to the GMC hospital in Jammu and two of them are critical. CRPF Assistant Commandant Ashok Choudhary who was on the accident spot told media persons that 10 bodies have been recovered."The ill fated bus (UP 81 CT 3537) should have taken a left turn nearly two kilometers towards Katra but it the driver seemed to have lost the direction have gone to the Udhampur-Srinagar side of the highway", he said.One of the injured said, "As it was too early and majority of travellers were asleep and suddenly they felt the that something hit the bus." "It lost balance and rolled down," he said."The impact of accident was so high that the front wheels came off and were seen stuck in the parapet of the bridge" a local eyewitness added .Another eyewitness Ramesh Kumar -a traveller escaped with minor bruises said, "We were going to pay obeisance to Mata Vaishno Devi temple and perform Mundan of our child. "We were serval families together,â€ said Kumar. Meanwhile Chief Secretary Dr Arun Kumar Mehta visited the Hospital and after meeting the people injured in the bus accident said that the injured have been extended all the required help.All the injured are being medically treated, and they are being extended all help,â€ he said.After meeting the injured at Hospital, the Director General of Police (DGP) Dilbagh Singh also said, â€œWe are trying to provide the best possible care to the injured. 10 people have died and 2-3 are critically injured,â€ said Dilbagh Singh after meeting the injured.Additional Director General of Police (ADGP), Jammu Zone Mukesh Singh-visited Govt. Medical College, Jammu and enquired about the health and welfare of the injured who sustained injuries in a road accident at Jajjar Kotli this morning and were undergoing treatment there.Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh tweeted, "Just now spoke to DC Jammu Avny Lavasa after receiving the news of road accident of a bus enroute to Katra. All the injured shifted to GMC Jammu. Every possible help being provided. I am in constant touch.President Droupadi Murmu and Jammu and Kashmir LG express condolencesPresident Droupadi Murmu on Tuesday said the death of pilgrims in a bus accident in Jammu was extremely unfortunate and offered her deepest condolences to the families who lost their loved ones. She also wished speedy recovery to the injured people."The death of many pilgrims in the bus accident in Jammu is extremely unfortunate. My deepest condolences to the families who lost their loved ones. I wish the injured people a speedy recovery," the President tweeted.Jammu and Kashmir lieutenant governor Manoj Sinha on Tuesday expressed pain over the loss of lives in Jammu bus accident.Sinha also directed administration to provide all possible assistance and treatment to the injured.In a condolence message, office of LG J&K tweeted, "Extremely pained by the loss of lives in a tragic bus accident in Jhajjar Kotli, Jammu. My heartfelt condolences to the bereaved families & prayers for speedy recovery of the injured. Directed district administration to provide all possible assistance & treatment to the injured."Bihar CM Nitish Kumar announces Rs 2 lakh ex-gratia for kin of deceased Bihar chief minister Nitish Kumar expressed condolences over the death of Bihar natives in the bus accident in Jhajjar Kotli in Jammu. He announced an ex-gratia grant of Rs 2 lakh each to the next of kin.Watch At least seven people killed in bus accident on Jammu-Srinagar Highway

Title: 7 engineering students killed in Guwahati SUV crash
content: GUWAHATI: Seven students of the Assam Engineering College died while six others were injured when the SUV they were travelling in overturned after hitting a road divider, and then hit a vehicle on the other side of the road in Jalukbari area early Monday.The deceased were Arindam Bhallal from Guwahati, Niyor Deka from Golaghat, Kaushik Mohan from Charaideo, Upangshu Sarmah from Nagaon, Rajkiran Bhuyan from Majuli, Emon Gayan from Dibrugarh and Kaushik Baruah from Mangaldoi. Of the six injured, three were friends of the deceased and were travelling in the same SUV. The other injured were in another vehicle that was hit.Ten third-year students left the college premises in an SUV they had taken on rent for the day. The group was returning from Azara end to Jalukbari when their SUV first hit the road divider and then another vehicle, before turning turtle around 1am. Police said one of the injured students was in the driver's seat.Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma, who visited the spot, said he was anguished by the loss of lives. The Assam DGP instructed authorities to analyse what led to the accident.

Title: 4 including couple killed as vehicle plunges into ravine in Doda
content: JAMMU: A middle aged couple was among four killed, while another passenger sustained injuries when the vehicle they were travelling in, skidded off the road and rolled down the hill into a river in Doda district on Monday."Today evening an accident took place on Batote-Doda NHW at Ragi Nallah in which a Swift Desire rolled down a 200 metre ravine resulting in the deaths of four persons on the spot, with one injured being shifted to hospital for treatment," said Sr Superintendent of Police (SSP) Doda Abdul Qypoom."The deceased have been identified as Om Parshad (60) and his wife Radha Devi (56) of Gajoth Bhalla, Ved Parkesh (65) and Raj Kumari (56) of Badasoo Bhalla Doda," he said.

Title: Armyman among 2 killed in separate bull attacks in west UP
content: AMROHA/PILIBHIT: A 28-year-old Indian Army soldier was killed while his wife and two children were injured after a stray bull hit their two-wheeler on Hasanpur Atrasi road near Bavankhedi village in Amroha district on Monday. In another such incident in Pilibhit, a 24-year-old man, identified as Banti Lodhi, was attacked by a stray bull on the outskirts of the city when he was on his way home on Sunday, report Harveer Singh & Keshav Agarwal.The soldier, Ankit Kumar, was posted at 507 ASC Battalion in Punjab's Ferozepur. Police said Kumar died on the spot as the bull's horn pierced his stomach, while his wife, Sonam (25), and their two children suffered minor injuries. Hasanpur police station SHO Susheel Verma said, "We will catch the bull and send it to a cow shelter. Kumar belonged to a farmer family." Amroha district magistrate BK Tripathi said, "The matter has come to my attention. I have instructed the Hasanpur SDM to take serious note of the incident." Meanwhile, Banti Lodhi was on his bike around 10 pm when a stray bull hit him and he fell on the road. As he fell, the bull kept attacking him. Locals rushed him to the district hospital where doctors declared him dead. Sungarhi police station SHO Jagat Singh said, "We have informed the family of the deceased about the accident. The body has been sent for autopsy."

Title: Couple among four killed, another injured as vehicle rolls down river in Jammu and Kashmir's Doda
content: JAMMU: A middle aged couple among four was killed while another sustained injuries when a vehicle they were travelling in skidded off the road and rolled down into a river in jammu and Kashmir's Doda district on Monday.Police sources said, "The vehicle fell into Raggi Nallah and immediately after the accident, a massive rescue operation was launched by local volunteers and police."Sharing details Sr Superintendent of Police (SSP) Doda Abdul Qypoom said, "Today evening an accident took place on Batote-Doda NHW at Ragi Nallah in which one private Swift Desire car rolled down into 200 meters deep ravine resulting into death of four persons on spot and one injured shifted to hospital for treatment.""The deceased have been identified namely as Om Parshad (60) son of Amar Nath and his wife Radha Devi (56) residents of Gajoth Bhalla; Ved Parkesh (65) son of Amar Nath Betholi Bhalla and Raj Kumari (56) wife of Ram Rattan of Badasoo Bhalla Doda. "SSP Doda said, "Meanwhile injured namely Mohd Aslam (25) son of Abdul Gani of Dhansal Jammu was shifted to Hospital for treatment.

Title: Biker killed in accident in Mhalunge MIDC
content: Pune: A motorcyclist (20) died in a hospital after a truck hit him at Mhalunge MIDC on Sunday.Police have identified the victim as Bhushan Gaikwad of Khed taluka. The driver of the truck did not stop after the accident. The police have booked him for negligent driving.An officer from the Mhalunge police said the truck was coming out of the premises of a company in the MIDC, located along the Bhamchandranagar-Sadumbare Road, on Sunday evening. â€œThe truck dashed the victim, going towards Sadumbare on his bike,â€ the officer said. He said Gaikwad was taken to a nearby hospital, where he succumbed to injuries. â€œWe are searching for the truck driver,â€ the officer said. TNN

Title: Accident: Man, father-in-law killed
content: Nagpur: A 26-year-old man, Akash Jambhulkar, and his father-in-law Rajaram Durge, 55, were killed in a case of hit-and-run on Hyderabad-Jabalpur highway on Monday. The trucker had hit the bikers before fleeing from the spot.Jambhulkar was driving the bike with wife Nirdhar and father-in-law Durge as pillion riders. The three fell on the road after being hit by an unidentified truck. While Jambhulkar and Durge were killed in the accident, Nirdhar is undergoing treatment at the Government Medical College and Hospital (GMCH).Cops have registered an offence against the unidentified trucker at Hudkeshwar police station. tnn

Title: 3 riding sans helmet killed in road crash
content: NAVI MUMBAI: Three men from Boisar riding on a sports bike, at around 3am on Monday, were killed in a mishap under Nagothane police station in Raigad. The trio were riding without helmets when their sports bike skid and hit a roadside crash barrier. Two died on the spot while the third succumbed on the way to the hospital. The police are trying to gather various details related to the accident. NAVI MUMBAI: On the poorly lit road, some motorists passing by had informed the Raigad traffic police about the mishap who in turn rushed to the spot and later lodged a complaint. The possibility that the men were under the influence of alcohol cannot be ruled out. The victims have been identified as Yash Chavan (21), Omkar Deshmukh (20) and Hrushikesh Lokhande (24).

Title: 4-year-old girl among 3 of family killed as vehicle falls from bridge
content: NASHIK: Three members of a family, including a four-year-old girl, died when the multi-utility vehicle (MUV) they were travelling in fell off a bridge on the Nandgaon-Manmad Road on Tuesday morning. Seven others â€” four women and three men â€” were injured. The accident occurred in Nandgaon taluka, approximately 100km from here, at 1am. The Nandgaon police said the family of 10 was travelling from Jalna to Malegaon after attending a wedding function. The driver of the vehicle apparently dozed off while crossing a bridge over a river close to the Nagya Sakya dam. As a result, the MUV crashed through the side barrier and fell 25 feet into the dry riverbed. The deceased have been identified as 50-year-old Yakub Mansuri, Afroz Mansuri (25) and 4-year-old Sifa Mansuri. Police said as soon as they received the information, they went to the accident spot and, with the help of the local residents, shifted the injured people to the government hospital in Nandgaon, and later to Malegaon on ambulances. Their condition is now stable.Nandgaon police inspector Rameshwar Gade told TOI: â€œWe would be writing to the departments concerned to take up repair works on the bridge. The Nandgaon-Manmad road is about 20 feet wide and the bridge is narrower. The bridge has an almost non-existent sidewall. Just some barriers, of about 1-foot in height, are placed along the side of the bridge. There are also no streetlights or warning signboards near the bridge. Had there been a strong sidewall, the vehicle may not have fallen off the bridge.â€

Title: 4-year-old among 3 of a family killed as vehicle falls off bridge in Nashik
content: NASHIK: Three members of a family, including a four-year-old girl, died when the multi-utility vehicle (MUV) they were travelling in fell off a bridge on the Nandgaon-Manmad Road in Maharashtra's Nashik district on Tuesday morning.
