Seven persons - four women and three men - were injured in the accident that took place at 1 am, reports Santosh Sonawane.The deceased were Yakub Mansuri (50), Afroz Mansuri (25) and Sifa Mansuri (4).Nandgaon police inspector Rameshwar Gade told TOI: "We will write to the departments concerned to take up repair work on the bridge. The Nandgaon-Manmad Road is about 20 feet wide, but the bridge is narrow and has an almost non-existent sidewall. Only some barriers, about 1 foot in height, are placed along the sides. There are no streetlights or signboards near the bridge."

Title: 10 pilgrims from Asr die in Jammu bus accident
content: Jammu/Amritsar: Ten Vaishno Devi pilgrims, most of them from Bihar, were killed and 57 injured on Tuesday as the bus they were travelling in skidded off the road and rammed into the railing of a bridge before falling off it, officials said. The death toll may go up as Government Medical College Hospital principal Shashi Soodan said two of the injured admitted there are critical. â€œTwo persons were brought dead while others died on the spot,â€ he added.The bus, which was on its way to Katra from Amritsar, met with the accident in Jhajjar Kotli area on the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway between 6.30 and 7am. Officials said most of the travellers were members of an extended family from Biharâ€™s Lakhisarai but living in Amritsar for their livelihood and were on pilgrimage to Vaishno Devi temple for a ritual of a child of their relative from Amritsar. In Amritsar, a relative of the deceased said they came to know about the accident after his father received a call from J&K police in the morning. He said the trip to Vaishno Devi was for the mundan (rituals related to shaving a babyâ€™s hair for the first time) ceremony of his uncle Mukeshâ€™s child Tanya. Around 70 people, including around 50 relatives from Lakhisarai, had left for Vaishno Devi on Monday night, he said.

Title: Woman, daughter die in accident, 2 more critical
content: KENDRAPADA: A 26-year-old woman and her six-month-old daughter were killed while her father-in-law and her elder daughter sustained critical injuries when a truck collided with a motorcycle at Chasapada village in Jagatsinghpur district on Tuesday.The deceased were identified as Kalima Bibi and her daughter Alia Khatun. The injured persons -Mukhtar Khan (56) and Ayesa Khatun (4) - were shifted to the district headquarters hospital in Jagatsinghpur.The driver of the truck fled after the accident. Policehave seized the motorcycle and truck. "A case under IPC sections 279 (rash driving), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety) and 304A (causing death by negligence) was registered against the driver. The bodies were handed over to the family members after post-mortem," said Salil Kumar Pradhan, inspector in-charge, Balikuda police station.Locals blocked the road at Chasapada burning tyres, affecting vehicular traffic for three hours. Police rushed to the spot and pacified the agitators, the IIC said.

Title: SUV hits bike, techie dies near Eco Park
content: Kolkata: Helmets firmly strapped, riding along the lane next to the left kerb at a moderate speed, two techies were following all traffic rules when a car hit them from behind, killing the 23-year-old female pillion rider and leaving the rider critically injured. Officers of Eco Park police station have arrested the driver, Md Nishan (55), and booked him under IPC sections 304A (death due to negligence), 279 (rash and negligent driving) and 338 (causing grievous hurt).The incident that happened near Eco Park on New Townâ€™s main arterial road has brought into focus the vulnerability of riding a two-wheeler on city roads, even when one is following traffic rules. There have been several other fatal accidents involving two wheelers in the recent past in which riders were not at fault. Swagata Ghoshal, who worked with a software development MNC in New Town and Sector V, was returning to her New Barrackpore home with her colleague Sudipta Banerjee (29) when the accident happened around 9.45pm. Thevehicle that hit them was an SUV of a cable TV firm. The riders were flung on to the road and suffered multiple injuries. Upon being taken to the nearest private hospital, Ghosh was declared dead by an attending doctor and Banerjee was admitted with severe injuries. â€œOur child had maintained all precautions while riding. We need answers and want strict punishment for the car driver who killed her,â€ said Sujit Ghoshal, the deceasedâ€™s uncle. Swagata was the only daughter of her parents. Her father runs a confectionary at New Barrackpore and her mother was a homemaker. She joined the MNC four months ago. The colleague, originally from Guwahati, stayed in a rented place in New Town. â€œThe riders were not at fault. The accident happened due to negligence on part of the driver. He claimed he had fallen sick. We are are verifying his claims,â€ a senior officer said.

Title: Child among 4 dead as 2 bikes collide head-on
content: INDORE: Four persons, including a four-year-old, were killed in a head-on collision between two motorcycles in Alirajpur in the wee hours of Tuesday.At least eight people were on both the bikes, Bori police station in-charge, SI Bhim Singh Sisodiya, said. One of the motorcycles was carrying six persons, including two women and three children, he said. Inder Singh Bhuriya, 50, and his son Aarmu, 20, of Moidi village were on the other bike, headed from Bori village to Udaygarh, when the two bikes slammed into each other head-on.While Inder and Aarmu died on the spot, Guddu Bhil, who was driving the second bike, and his niece Savita Dehdiya, 4, were fatally injured and died on the way to hospital. Bike accident victims lay unattended for several minutes  Savita's mother Sangeeta, Jelam Bai Bamniya, 30, and two kids - Arvind Bamniya, 5, and Raveena, 3 - were severely injured. They are residents of Ranapur.The victims lay on the deserted road for several minutes before passing commuters noticed them and informed police. Cops suspect rider could not manoeuvre his bike to avoid the collision because he was travelling with too many people. Police have registered a case and are trying to ascertain what led to the accident. TNN

Title: Silchar woman killed by speeding car, 1 held
content: SILCHAR: A 60-year-old woman was killed after being hit by a speeding car in Fakirtilla near the National Institute of Technology (NIT), Silchar, on the outskirts of Silchar, early on Tuesday morning. The driver of the car has been arrested.Victim Gauri Nunia was a resident of Fakirtilla. As per reports, the accident took place around 6 am when Gauri was returning home after attending a puja ceremony at a temple in the area. After hitting Gauri, the speeding Ambassador car veered off the road and fell onto a low-lying area along the road. The car driver tried to flee, but the local residents managed to get hold of him.Police reached the accident spot and sent the body to Silchar Medical College and Hospital for post-mortem examination. The driver ha been arrested and the car has been seized and were taken to the Ghungoor police station, sources in the police said.

Title: SUV hits bike, techie dies near Eco Park
content: KOLKATA: Helmets firmly strapped, riding along the lane next to the left kerb at a moderate speed, two techies were following all traffic rules when a car hit them from behind, killing the 23-year-old female pillion rider and leaving the rider critically injured. Officers of Eco Park police station have arrested the driver, Md Nishan (55), and booked him under IPC sections 304A (death due to negligence), 279 (rash and negligent driving) and 338 (causing grievous hurt).The incident that happened near Eco Park on New Town's main arterial road has brought into focus the vulnerability of riding a two-wheeler on city roads, even when one is following traffic rules. There have been several other fatal accidents involving two wheelers in the recent past in which riders were not at fault.Swagata Ghoshal, who worked with a software development MNC in New Town and Sector V, was returning to her New Barrackpore home with her colleague Sudipta Banerjee (29) when the accident happened around 9.45pm. Thevehicle that hit them was an SUV of a cable TV firm. The riders were flung on to the road and suffered multiple injuries. Upon being taken to the nearest private hospital, Ghosh was declared dead by an attending doctor and Banerjee was admitted with severe injuries. Swagata was the only daughter of her parents. Her father runs a confectionary at New Barrackpore and her mother was a homemaker. She joined the MNC four months ago. The colleague, originally from Guwahati, stayed in a rented place in New Town. "The riders were not at fault. The accident happened due to negligence on part of the driver. He claimed he had fallen sick. We are are verifying his claims," a senior officer said.

Title: Man killed in accident
content: Kochi: A 47-year-old man, who was travelling on a scooter, was killed in an accident after a truck ran over him at Angamaly on Wednesday. The deceased was identified as Shibu, a resident of Karukutty, a painter by profession.tnn

Title: 1 killed, 8 injured in Anuppur accident
content: Jabalpur: One person was killed, and eight others sustained injuries when the pick-up van in which they were travelling rammed a shed built along the road in Anuppur district.The incident took place near Dola railway crossing under Ram Nagar police station of the district.Police said that the pick-van was going to Kudargarh for pilgrimage from Beohari in Shahdol, the driver of the vehicle probably dozed off leading to the accident on Wednesday morning. . .AdvertisementThis led to death of one person on the spot and 8 injured were sent to hospital for treatment, condition of one among the 8 injured is said to be critical, said police.A case has been registered and investigations are on, added police.

Title: Andhra Pradesh: Three of family killed, two injured in road accident near Yerpedu in Tirupati
content: TIRUPATI: At least three people were killed while two others suffered injuries in a road mishap reported near Yerpedu in Tirupati district early on Thursday morning.According to the Srikalahasti police, a speeding car crashed into an APSRTC bus near Merlapaka in Yerpedu mandal, resulting in the deaths of a couple and their daughter.Two others who sustained injuries were rushed to the SVRR government general hospital in Tirupati.Police said the victims were all from the same family and belonged Danthalapalle village in Mahabubabad in Telangana.. .AdvertisementSrikalahasti police registered a case and are further investigating.

Title: Car crashes into truck on roadside, 5 killed
content: Hisar: Five people, including the driver, were killed after the car they were travelling in collided with a truck parked on the roadside near Dhana village on NH-9 on Wednesday morning. The deceased have been identified as Anoop and Prakash Nepali, both residents of Bhatol Rangdan village; Pradeep, Praveen and Sandeep alias Sonu, all residents of Kharkada village. A woman was also injured in the accident and has been admitted to Agroha Medical College. The college public relation officer said the condition of the injured woman, who is also pregnant, is critical. A case was registered on the complaint of Mahabir Singh, a resident Bhatol Rangdan village, against the truck driver.

Title: Youth killed as JCB tyre bursts in Chhatarpur
content: Bhopal: A youth was killed when a tyre of a JCB machine exploded in Chhatarpur district of Madhya Pradesh on Tuesday. A video capturing a fatal accident has gone viral on social media.According to available information, the incident occurred at a stone crusher plant situated in Chandrapura, within the jurisdiction of the Civil Line Police Station. The victim was identified as Bhaiyalal Raikwar, a resident of Bamitha. The deceased was employed at Rahul Stone Crusher, owned by Rahul Budhauliya.On the day of the incident, he was dismantling parts of the JCB machine when one of its tyres suddenly exploded, leading to his death. . .AdvertisementVideo of the incident, that went viral, was captured on CCTV after which the incident came to light.The victimâ€™s body was sent for post-mortem examination at the district hospital. Later, his body was handed over to the grieving family. Investigations are underway.Watch CCTV: Youth killed as JCB tyre bursts in MP's Chhatarpur

Title: Four killed, 2 injured as auto plunges into gorge
content: Raipur: A joyous wedding celebration took a devastating turn when an auto returning from the ceremony suddenly lost control and plunged several feet in gorge, killing four people and critically injuring two.Three were killed on the spot while another died during treatment. Two other passengers sustained serious injuries and are undergoing treatment.According to the police officials, the accident took place in the Kardana Valley of Sonkyari police post area. There was a total of six people in the auto, including an eight-year-old child and a woman.The accident took place near Chatauri Kapukona on Ghaghra Road. While going to Kardana, the auto lost control and fell into a 50 feet deep gorge. . .AdvertisementFour people including the vehicle owner and his wife died in this accident. Two people seriously injured in the incident have been referred to Ambikapur. The incident took place on Wednesday afternoon. The police investigation is still on, the police added.TNN

Title: Driver killed after truck falls off Ring Road flyover
content: JAIPUR: A 35-year-old driver died after a multi-axle truck careened off a flyover on the Ring Road and plummeted 20 feet below, under the jurisdiction of Shivdaspura police station, early Wednesday morning.According to SHO Shivdaspura O P Matwaa, the victim was identified as Satyendra Kumar, a resident of Bihar. The incident occurred shortly after 5am, he said.The truck, which was transporting floor chips, was en route to Kanota from Ajmer Road near Prahaladpura village when it veered off the flyover and fell.Police mentioned that the truck was loaded with several sacks of white-colored chips, and the driver's body was buried under the heap of sacks.. .AdvertisementFollowing a difficult operation, emergency responders managed to locate the victim's body, which was later transferred to the mortuary of a private hospital for post-mortem. Law enforcement officials have already contacted the victim's family members and requested their presence in the city.Authorities have initiated an investigation into the accident to determine the exact sequence of events leading to this tragic incident. A team from the traffic police has inspected the accident site. Sources revealed that statements from witnesses and an analysis of the accident site will be crucial in determining the cause.

Title: 1 killed, 3 injured as sand-laden truck overturns in Uttarakhand's US Nagar
content: RUDRAPUR: In a freak incident that unfolded on NH-74 on Wednesday evening, a 16-tyre sand-laden truck lost control after one of its tyres burst and it overturned in the middle of the road, falling on a car and a bike, killing a 30-year-old man and injuring three others.The incident took place at Bara Chowki near Kichha in US Nagar, as the dumper truck, en route to Sitarganj, suddenly veered off course after its tyre burst and toppled, burying a car and a bike under the vehicle and a pile of sand and gravel.Vishnu Majumdar, a resident of Gehri village in UPâ€™s Bareilly district, was killed on the spot. Baba Vaibhav Bharti, riding pillion, got stuck under the truck and his legs were cut off. Cops from the Kichha and Pulbhatta police stations rushed to the spot. Fearing others may be buried under the truck, a large crane was brought in to lift the truck.. .AdvertisementThe three injured were promptly admitted to the Community Health Center in Kichha.

Title: 3 of family killed, 4 injured after autorickshaw falls into under-construction nullah in Thane's Bhiwandi
content: KALYAN: Three members of a family were killed and four others sustained injuries after an autorickshaw, which they were travelling in, fell into an under-construction deep nullah on Mumbai-Nashik Highway in Thane's Bhiwandi, on Wednesday night.The incident took place at around at 11.30pm near Bhumi world when autorickshaw driver Rakesh Chauhan, along with his family, was returning home from Juhu Chowpaty.The deceased has been identified as Munni Chauhan (Rakesh's wife), Radhika Chauhan (Rakesh's sister-in-law) and Aniksha (Rakesh's younger daughter) whereas the four injured include Rakesh Chauhan, his son Ravi and two daughters Ankita and Anjali who are undergoing treatment at state-run IGM hospital in Bhiwandi.The police said the Titwala resident along with his family had gone to Juhu Chowpaty for an outing in the morning. . .AdvertisementIt is alleged that due to darkness on road and absence of any warning symbol by MMRDA which is constructing the nullah, Rakesh could not see the nullah and his auto fell into it, leading to the tragedy.After hearing screams of Rakesh and his family members for help, passersby came to their rescue and pulled them out of the nullah using ropes and rushed them to state-run IGM hospital where doctor declared three of them dead.The Kongaon police in Bhiwandi under whose jurisdiction the incident happened have registered an accidental death case.Further investigation is under way.

Title: 2 workers dead, 15 hurt in iron factory accident
content: Bankura: Two workers were killed and 15 injured in Bankuraâ€™s Barjora on Tuesday when a crane carrying molten iron malfunctioned and hot metal fell on them at a sponge iron factory. The two deceased were identified as Mohammed Aziz (26) and Ramesh Kumar (32), both residents of Biharâ€™s Samastipur.Locals rushed to the factory after cries for help. â€œThe workers were writhing in pain. As many as 17 were rushed to Barjora Superspeciality Hospital from where 14 were shifted to a private hospital in Durgapur,â€ said Barjora MLA and Trinamool district president Alok Mukherjee. â€œWe will write to CM Mamata Banerjee for prioritising the treatment of the injured.â€ said Mukherjee.On Wednesday morning, two succumbed to burn injuries. The others are in ICU.Police inspected the site and collected samples for forensic examination.. .Advertisement

Title: 4 of family killed as SUV drags scooter for 100m after hitting it
content: LUCKNOW: An overspeeding SUV with a drunk man on wheels knocked down a scooter, killing four of a family including two minors near Gulacheen temple in Vikasnagar police station area of the city late Tuesday night. The incident was recorded on CCTV camera.On receiving information about the accident, police rushed the victims to the Trauma Centre of KGMU and seized the SUV. The victims were pronounced dead upon arrival by the doctors.CM Yogi Adityanath expressed grief over the accident and directed officials to provide all possible help while taking strict action against the accused.Those killed in the accident were identified as Ram Singh (34), his wife Gyan Devi (31) and their sons Ansh (8) and Raj (13), all of Tambour locality in Sitapur. The family had been living in Lucknow since 2007.. .Advertisement"Around 2am, the family on a non-gear scooter were passing by the Gulacheen temple in Vikasnagar when an overspeeding SUV hit them. The scooter got caught under the SUV and was dragged up to 100 meters from the place of accident," said DCP North Zone, Qasim Abidi. It was alleged that the man on the driving seat did not stop the SUV and dragged the vehicle along."The family trapped under the driver identified as Rajendra Kumar Pal of Rajajipuram in Talkatora was arrested. "The accused is a property dealer and his office is in Bakshi Ka Talab. He was heavily drunk while driving the SUV and did not apply brakes to avoid hitting the scooter," he said.He added that the SUV was abandoned at the accident site. "A police team found the owner and he confessed to his crime during interrogation on Wednesday afternoon," he said.

Title: Car crushed between two trucks, 3 of family die, one kid escapes with injury
content: HYDERABAD: A pharma company employee from Hyderabad, his wife and 13-year-old son were killed while the couple's younger son escaped with injuries after their car was crushed between two trucks at Konijerla in Khammam on Thursday. The two truck drivers suffered minor injuries. It took more than an hour to remove the bodies out of the mangled car, police said.The accident happened around 4.30 am after a truck applied sudden break to avoid another vehicle which had stopped in the middle of the road, leading to the car rear-ending it and a truck behind it ramming into the car, police said.. .AdvertisementThe deceased were P Rajesh (39), his wife P Sujatha (35), and their 13-year-old son P Ashruth. The couple's younger son, Divijith, suffered critical injuries. The family was on its way to Rajesh's native place in Wyra in Khammam district. "Rajesh could not apply breaks in time and crashed into the truck. The truck behind Rajesh rammed into the car which was sandwiched between the two heavy vehicles," Khammam commissioner Vishnu Warrier told TOI. Rajesh, wife and son were killed on the spot. Police had to separate the mangled car and the trucks using a crane. While the car was destroyed, both trucks were also damaged. Police have registered a case against truck driver Lingaiah who was behind the car. He was booked under sections 304-a (causing death due to rash or negligent act) and 337 (causing injuries due to rash or negligent act) of the IPC.

Title: 3 of family die as auto falls into exposed nullah
content: BHIWANDI: Three members of a family, including a mother and daughter duo, were killed and four others sustained injuries after their autorickshaw fell into an under-construction nullah on the Mumbai-Nashik Highway in Thane's Bhiwandi area on Wednesday night. The incident took place at around at 11.30pm near Bhumi World when autorickshaw driver Rakesh Chauhan (34) was returning home from Juhu Chowpatty along with his family. Following the accident, the Kongaon police have booked Titwala-based auto driver Chauhan for driving the vehicle at high speed and in a negligent way. In the mishap, Chauhan's wife Munni (32), daughter Anshika (2) and sister-in-law Radha (33) died, while his son Ravi (11) and two daughters Ankita (7) and Anjali (9) have suffered injuries. All four are undergoing treatment at state-run IGM hospital in Bhiwandi. Police said Chauhan's sister-in-law Radhika, who stays in Uttar Pradesh, had recently come to visit the family. Rajendra Pawar, senior PI, Kongaon police station, said, "When the rickshaw reached the highway near Bhumi World, Chauhan lost control of the speeding vehicle and it fell into the deep nullah.". .AdvertisementPolice said Rakesh somehow managed to wiggle out of the rickshaw and called for help. Initially, one person from a nearby scrap shop and another from a hotel heard his desperate calls for help and reached the spot. They then roped in other passersby and pulled out all seven family members from the nullah and rushed them to IGM hospital, where doctor declared three as brought dead. Navnath Dhavale, DCP, Zone 2, said, "Primary probe revealed negligence on part of the driver, hence an FIR has been registered against him." Rickshaw unions, however, blamed MMRDA's contractor for not installing any barricades or proper signal system at the nullah construction site. "There aren't any proper lights either," said a rickshaw driver.

Title: Three killed as bike hits bus on Nemawar road
content: Indore: Three persons were killed after a bike they were riding collided head-on with a bus on Nemawar road on Friday afternoon.Khudel police said that the accident took place on Dudhiya road when Sajid alias Illu, Hanif, and Vijay, all residents of Bagli in Dewas were on their way to Indore. The bike hit a private bus which was on its way from Indore to Chapra. The impact of the accident was such that the two-wheeler broke into three parts and all three passengers were thrown off it. They received severe head injuries and lost lot of blood. The police said that two persons died on the spot and the third was rushed to hospital where he succumbed during treatment.Khudel police recorded the case but deaths were recorded by Sanyogitaganj police station and bodies were sent for postmortem examination. tnn

Title: Four killed, 14 injured in road accident in Haryana's Panipat
content: KARNAL: Four persons including two women were killed while 14 others sustained injuries when the pickup they were traveling in was hit by a speeding truck near Rishpur village in Sanaoli of Panipat district early on Saturday morning.Police said that the passengers were on their way to Haridwar when the accident occurred.The deceased have been identified as Kanta and Munni of Kamach Khera village, Mohit of Madanpur village in Hisar and Ashwini of Lajwana village in Jind.Roshan, Sanjay (an Sarpanch of Kamach Khera), Usha, Bala, Vimala, Ravi, Sajjan, Rakesh, Ajay, Dhoop Singh, Asha, Sharda, Atul, all residents of Kamach Khera village, and Pardeep of Sahu Uklana village in Hisar, were injured in the mishap.. .AdvertisementPolice said that they were going to Haridwar to have a dip in Holy Ganga river on the occasion of Purnima. When they reached near Rishpur village on Sanaoli bypass, a tyre of their vehicle got punctured. When they were replacing the tyre, a speeding truck coming from Panipat side hit their vehicle, leaving them badly injured. A police team reached the spot and started the investigation.The injured were taken to hospital where Kanta, Munni, Mohit and Ashwini were declared brought dead.Sources said that five injured were referred to the PGIMS Rohtak due to their critical condition.Mahabir Singh, SHO, Sanoli police station said that a case has been registered against the truck driver under section 279, 304A and 337 of the Indian Penal Code. The bodies have been handed over to the family members after the post mortem examination. The investigation is on.

Title: Four killed, 8 injured in road accident near Pulicherla in Andhra Pradesh's Chittoor district
content: TIRUPATI: Four persons were killed and eight others sustained injuries in a road accident reported at Pulicherla mandal in Andhra Pradesh's Chittoor district on Saturday morning.According to the Chittoor district police wing, a group of family members from Nandyal were travelling to Tiruvannamalai in Tamil Nadu in their SUV. A cement laden truck crashed into the SUV near a private college in Pulicherla mandal along the Kadapa-Chittoor highway on Saturday morning.Four persons identified as Lakshmi Devi, Prathap Reddy, Vimala and Sivamma succumbed to injuries on the spot.Eight others who were wounded seriously have been shifted to Chittoor and Tirupati for better medical care by the police.. .AdvertisementPulicherla police registered a case and shifted the bodies of the deceased for postmortem.

Title: Balasore train accident: Over 200 killed, 900 injured in threeÂ­-train pileÂ­-up in Odisha; govt announces compensation
content:
BHUBANESWAR: Over 200 passengers died and around 900 wounded in a deadly accident involving three trains at Bahanaga Bazar station in Balasore district of Odisha on Friday evening as authorities mounted a massive rescue operation right through the night involving multiple agencies.Officials said fatalities could mountas many passengers are still trapped in the debris. DG fire services Sudhanshu Sarangi said 120 bodies recovered, the death toll may go up further.Read Also'Distressed': PM Modi on Coromandel Express train accidentPrime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday expressed grief over Coromandel Express train accident in Odisha's Balasore district and assured that all possible assistance is being given to those affected. The Prime Minister said he was distressed to hear of the accident and had spoken to Union railwayOdisha revenue minister Pramila Mallik, who visited the train mishap site, said the deaths will be in hundreds.Watch: Aerial view of Odisha train accident site where over 230 people diedFollow live updates. .AdvertisementAs the casualty count kept mounting as the night wore on, multiple agencies joined the search for survivors and bodies trapped in the heap of mangled coaches. â€œOnly after we scour the entire debris, which could take a while, will we know the final toll. As of now, it looks like the numbers will go further up," a senior railway official said.Railway authorities said two bogies of the Sir M Visveswaraya Terminal-Howrah Superfast Express got derailed first near Bahanaga Bazar Station of Kharagpur division of South Eastern Railway, about 140 km from Kharagpur around 7.10 pm.04:02Coromandel Express and Yeshwantpur-Howrah trains derail in Odisha's Balasore, over 300 injured, several deadAround the same time, Chennai-bound Shalimar-Chennai Coromandel Express (12841) coming from the opposite direction on a parallel track, hit the derailed bogies on its tracks at very high speed.Under the impact, 12 coaches of Coromandel Express got derailed. Some derailed coaches of the train hit a stationary goods train on a parallel track.1/15Coromandel Express derails in Odisha, several injured Previous NextShow Captions

â€‹As the rescue teams reached an hour after the accident, locals pulled out the injured from mangled bogies. 50 were thrown out of train through broken windows, coach doorsSuch was the impact of the crash that around 50 passengers were thrown out through the broken windows and doors of the coaches. By the time rescue teams reached the accident site, volunteers had pulled out many of the injured from the debris. 1/7Deadliest train accidents in history of Indian Railways Previous NextShow Captions

Over 260 people died and around 900 were injured in a deadliest three-train collision which took place on Friday evening, in Odisha's Balasore. The country has witnessed several tragic train accidents before also that have resulted in significant loss of life.

On August 20, 1995, Purushottam Express collided with Kalindi Express near Firozabad in Uttar Pradesh. The accident claimed the lives of around 358 people.

On August 2, 1999, Brahmaputra Mail collided with the Awadh-Assam Express near Gaisal in Assam. The accident resulted in the deaths of approximately 290 people.

The train tragedy took place on November 26, 1998 when Jammu Tawi-Sealdah Express collided with Kalindi Express near Khanna in Punjab, which resulted in the deaths of around 212 lives.

On July 8, 1981, Island Express collided with a local passenger train near Peruman in Kerala. Approximately 150 people lost their lives in the accident.

On November 20, 2016, Indoreâ€“Patna Express derailed near Pukhrayan in Uttar Pradesh. The accident claimed at least 150 deaths and more than 150 sustained injuries.

Balasore MP and former Union minister Pratap Chandra Sarangi, among the first to reach the site, termed the pileup â€œan unprecedented tragedyâ€. â€œHundreds are dead and injured. The scale is unimaginable,â€ he said.02:26'Three trains involved': Odisha Chief Secretary on Coromandel train accidentâ€œI was asleep in my berth when a big explosion happened, followed by people falling on me. I somehow came out of the train compartment. I saw many people trapped under a pile of coaches and screaming," said Swapan Kumar, a survivor from the Coromandel Express.The railway ministry announced ex gratia of Rs 10 lakh to the next of kin of those who died, Rs 2 lakh to the grievously injured and Rs 50, 000 to those with lesser injuries. Railway minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said he was on his way to the crash site. â€œMy prayers for the speedy recovery of the injured and condolences to the bereaved families. Rescue teams mobilised from Bhubaneswar and Kolkata. NDRF, state government teams and Air Force also mobilised. Will take all hands required for the rescue ops,â€ he said. Odisha chief secretary Pradeep Jena said hundreds of people had been hospitalised. â€œThe government has kept all hospitals on the Balasore Bhubaneswar route ready for this emergency.â€ After taking stock of the rescue operation, CM Naveen Patnaik said, â€œI will be flying there early tomorrow to personally oversee the effort.â€Odisha Train Tragedy: CM Naveen Patnaik Visits Accident Site to Assess Situation Four teams each of the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) and the Odisha Disaster Rapid Action Force were sent to join the rescue operation. The railways and the Odisha government set up helplines for information on the passengers. â€œDeeply anguished to know about the loss of lives in an unfortunate rail accident in Balasore, Odisha. My heart goes out to the bereaved families. I pray for the success of rescue operations and quick recovery of the injured,â€ President Droupadi Murmu tweeted.Helpline numbersThe helpline numbers are: Howrah - 033-26382217, Kharagpur - 8972073925 & 9332392339, Balasore - 8249591559 & 7978418322, Shalimar - 9903370746, Santragachi - 8109289460 & 8340649469, Bhadrak - 7894099579 & 9337116373, Jajpur-Keonjhar Road - 9676974398, Cuttack - 8455889917, Bhubaneswar - 06742534027, Khurda Road - 6370108046 & 06742492245.Watch Odisha train tragedy: Death toll climbs to 261 in one of India's worst train accidents

Title: Two killed, 11 injured in separate road accidents in Uttar Pradesh
content: BALLIA/BALRAMPUR: Two persons were killed and 11 others injured in separate road accidents in Uttar Pradesh's Ballia and Balrampur districts, police said on Sunday. A clerk of the commercial tax department was killed when his motorcycle was hit by a speeding tractor in Alavpur village in the Sukhpura area of Ballia Saturday night, they said.  The deceased was identified as Mahesh Srivastava (47), police said, adding he was returning from duty when the incident occurred.  The body has been sent for post-mortem, they said. . .Advertisement In another accident, a woman was killed and 11, including three children, were injured when their vehicle overturned on Deoria turn in the Dehat area of Balrampur on Sunday, police said.  The incident took place they were doing to a 'dargah' (shrine) in Bahraich. Razia (45) died on the spot, police said.  Among those injured, the condition of two is stated to be critical, they said.  Superintendent of Police Keshav Kumar said the body has been sent for post-mortem.

Title: Three killed in separate accidents
content: Nagpur: Three youngsters, including a 10-year-old boy, were killed in separate accidents on Friday, said the police.Sanidhya Bobde, 10, was killed after being hit by a Star Bus plying on the service road at Dhamna. He was heading to the market when the bus had dashed him leading to serious injuries. He was later declared dead at a hospital.An offence was registered at Hingna police station against the bus driver Prakash Katale for rash and negligent driving.Yahya Zafar, 16, whose SSC results were out on Friday, was declared dead at a private hospital after his moped was hit by a speeding car while crossing Gorewada Ring Road. He was heading home to Jafar Nagar when the incident took place at Awasthi Nagar. . .AdvertisementAn offence was registered at the Mankapur police station against the motorist.In yet another incident, 19-year-old Sujal Patel succumbed to his injuries at Mayo Hospital where he was rushed after ramming a Mahametro pillar near Zero Mile. He was on the bike with his friend Apurvesh Kotgaye who too was injured in the accident. tnn

Title: Four dead, 8 injured in Pulicherla road accident
content: TIRUPATI: Four persons were killed and eight others sustained injuries in a road accident reported at Pulicherla mandal in Chittoor district on Saturday morning.The four persons who died on the spot were identified as Lakshmi Devi, Prathap Reddy, Vimala and Sivamma.According to the Chittoor district police wing, a group of family members from Nandyal were travelling to Tiruvannamalai in Tamil Nadu in their SUV car. Suddenly, a cement laden truck came towards they vehicle and crashed into it near a private college in Pulicherla mandal along the Kadapa-Chittoor highway.While four members of the family succumbed to their injuries on the spot, the remaining eight passengers, who were severely injured in the accident, were shifted by the police to Chittoor and later, Tirupati for better medical treatment. . .AdvertisementPulicherla police registered a case regarding this and shifted the bodies of the deceased for postmortem.

Title: Gujarat: 73% of those killed on the roads were 18-45 years old
content: AHMEDABAD: Data from the Road Safety Council revealed that 73.48% of those who died in accidents were between 18 and 45 years old. Of the 7,618 deaths in the state in 2022 in road accidents, 5,598 were in this age group. Not wearing helmets continues to be a major factor in fatal accidents. In 2022, a total of 2,470 people who lost their lives were not wearing helmets, while this number was 2,666 in 2021. Of the 2,470 who died while not wearing helmets, 1,814 were driving and 65 were pillion riders. A total of 819 persons who died were not wearing seatbelts, with 495 of these being the driver and 396 were passengers. Officials said 6% of fatal accidents in urban areas and 15% in rural areas were reported between 6pm and 9pm. The figures show that 57.4% (4,374) were killed on state and national highways; the remainder (42.6% or 3,254 accidents) occurred inside cities, towns or on village roads. . .AdvertisementThe majority of fatal accidents, 67.08%, involved two-wheeler riders, cyclists or pedestrians. About 18% of fatalities came in accidents at junctions (1,359), with 408 at four-arm junctions, 393 at T junctions and 258 at staggered junctions. There were 84 fatalities at junctions with roundabouts. The data shows that of the 7,618 deaths, 6,265 occurred on straight roads and 774 on curved roads. Officials said 26% of accidents with fatalities involved rear-end collisions and 19% were hit-and-runs. The data shows that 16% of fatalities involved collision from the side, while 12% involved head-on collisions. Parked vehicles and fixed objects on the road were the causes of fatalities in 4% of accidents, and 8% of accidents took place while overtaking. The council's meeting in Gandhinagar on Wednesday also covered the issue of the Ambika bus stand near Kalol on the Ahmedabad-Meshana highway, where five people were killed in an accident recently. There is a foot overbridge at the site, and about 30m from the bridge this is an unauthorized parking lot, where buses line up. There are no speed breakers or signs at the site alerting drivers to the presence of the bus stand. At the meeting, the council asked the police and home department to initiate measures to curb speeding, wrong-side driving, unauthorized parking and to have strict implementation of the helmet and seatbelt rules. In its directions to the police and Road Transport Office the Road Safety council has directed that the authorities should enforce underage driving and should also check overspeeding in the cities. Also the two authorities have been directed to assist the civic bodies to remove encroachments from the footpaths. The home department representative said that 12,263 cameras have been installed across the state of which 3,343 automatically capture number plates. The state has 299 vehicles mounted with speed guns and 9,952 body-worn cameras.Catching them young The Road Safety Council made it mandatory for the state to include road safety chapters in Classes 6, 9 and 11 from this academic year, and in Classes 7,8, 10 and 12 from the next academic session in June next year. The authorities also directed school authorities to have a separate area for pick-ups and drops outside schools. The authorities also want schools to have separate pick-up areas for pedestrians and for those coming in vehicles. Educational institutions must also restrict the entry of students coming on two-wheelers without helmets.

Title: 3 killed, 6 hurt in accident involving truck & two cars
content: NASHIK: Three people from Kandivali, Mumbai, were killed and six others were injured in an accident involving a truck and two cars in the Dhotre village of Parner taluka on Saturday morning. The accident took place on the Ahmednagar-Kalyan road, about 170km from here.The deceased have been identified as Roshan Sanjay Surase, Vedant Bhavsar and his mother Priti Bhavsar. The Parner police said the accident took place at a junction on the highway. A truck driver, going towards Kalyan, swerved to his right when he saw a vehicle coming from the left side of the junction. However, the truck ended up ramming a multi-utility vehicle in which the Bhavsar family was travelling. Three in the car were killed and six others were injured. Another car, just behind the MUV, was also hit by the truck. However, no one in that car was injured. . .AdvertisementThe Parner police immediately went to the accident spot and shifted the injured people to the Ahmednagar civil hospital. The police added that the truck driver fled the scene after the accident.The Bhavsar family was coming from Kandivali (East) and going towards Shirdi to meet relatives.

Title: 3 die in accident on Samruddhi stretch opened a week ago
content: Nashik: Three people, including an 11-year-old, were killed in a car accident on the 80-km Shirdi-Bharvir stretch of the Samruddhi expressway. This stretch of the expressway, which is part of Samruddhiâ€™s second phase, was inaugurated by chief minister Eknath Shinde and deputy chief minister Devendra Fadnavis just over a week ago, on May 26.The deceased have been identified as 11 -year-old Raghwendra Pardeshi, a resident of Rajewadi Badnapur in Jalna, and Aurangabad districtâ€™s Phulamrbi taluka residents Dharamsingh Gurusinge (52) and Rajendra Rajput (48).Sinnar traffic aid post (TAP) inspector Shashikant Giri told TOI the accident occurred around 1am. â€œThere were six people, including the child, travelling in the car. It is yet to be ascertained from where the car had joined the expressway. According to initial reports, the victims were travelling towards Jalna. The driver apparently dozed off and hit the road divider at 538km (counted from Nagpur). This point is approximately 18 km from Shirdi, under the jurisdiction of the Wavi police station of Nashik Rural.â€The car got tossed over to the other side of the road divider. Passengers wearing seatbelts survived but were critically injured. All the injured people were rushed to the Kopargaon rural hospital in an ambulance by the highway police in Sinnar. . .AdvertisementThe highway police said this is the first fatal accident on the newly inaugurated expressway stretch. In the first week of the inauguration, two accidents occurred d at night.

content: NASHIK: Three people, including an 11-year-old, were killed in a car accident on the 80-km Shirdi-Bharvir stretch of the Samruddhi expressway. This stretch of the expressway, which is part of Samruddhi's second phase, was inaugurated by chief minister Eknath Shinde and deputy chief minister Devendra Fadnavis just over a week ago, on May 26.The deceased have been identified as 11 -year-old Raghwendra Pardeshi, a resident of Rajewadi Badnapur in Jalna, and Aurangabad district's Phulamrbi taluka residents Dharamsingh Gurusinge (52) and Rajendra Rajput (48).Sinnar traffic aid post (TAP) inspector Shashikant Giri told TOI the accident took place around 1. "There were six people, including the child, travelling in the car. It is yet to be ascertained from where the car had joined the expressway. According to initial reports, the victims were travelling towards Jalna. The driver apparently dozed off and hit the road divider at 538km (counted from Nagpur).". .Advertisement

Title: 18-year-old Chotu Sardar dreamt of pucca house, ventured to Kerala for work; Odisha train crash killed him
content: BURDWAN(WEST BENGAL): Chotu Sardar, who just turned 18, dreamt of building a pucca house for his family, so he joined his father -- a construction worker -- to venture to Kerala in search of work. His dream remained unfulfilled as Chotu lost his life in the train crash in Odisha's Balasore. Chotu's father Suklal survived the accident, but the sight of his dead son devastated him.As the news of Chotu's death reached his house in Korui village in Katwa in West Bengal's Purba Bardhaman district, a pall of gloom descended. Read AlsoOdisha train tragedy: Coromandel Express entered wrong track before accidentThe Shalimar Chennai Coromandel Express (12841) train had entered a wrong track moments before the mishap at Bahanaga Bazar Station in Balasore district on Friday evening in which more than 260 people were killed and 900 injured, officials said.Odisha train crash result of human error, sabotage? Amid multiple theories, probe beginsAmid different theories doing the rounds about the possible reasons for the horrific accident involving three trains near Balasore and the railway ministry maintaining that the exact cause of this unusual crash would be known only after a detailed investigation, one fact stood out â€” the. .AdvertisementSuklal has been working as a mason in Kerala for long, locals said, adding that he took his son along with him for the first time. He wanted his family to have a better life, so he wanted to train his son in masonry and took him along, they said. They were among 10 people from the village travelling on Friday's ill-fated Coromandel Express, which was involved in the triple crash in Balasore.Suklal is at present critical and undergoing treatment at a hospital in Odisha. Many members of the group are still missing, locals said. Saddam Sheikh, 28, of nearby Kaithan village also lost his life in the train crash. He was heading to Kerala to work as a construction labourer. He is survived by a one-month-old son and wife. Yead Ali Sheikh, 37, of Kurumb village in Mongalkote police station area is missing after the accident. He too was travelling on Coromandel Express in search of work. Yead Ali and Taher Ali are two brothers, who work as construction workers in Kerala. The two brothers go to the southern state alternately. Taher Ali would have returned home after Yead Ali reached Kerala, their family said. Yead Ali's wife, two children and their parents were spending sleepless nights, waiting for the news of his whereabouts. His phone has been ringing, but no one is picking up, his father said. Locals said the family does not have enough money to hire a vehicle and go to the accident site in the Balasore district. District Magistrate Priyanka Singla said five people from the district have been killed in the accident, and all of them were travelling on the Coromandel Express. State Animal Resource Development Minister Swapan Debnath, the MLA of Purbasthali Dakshin, said he is keeping a tab on the situation. The state government will provide all assistance to the victims and their families, he said. Thirteen migrant workers from Bhatar police station area were also travelling to Kerala on that train. Though injured in the accident, they managed to return home on their own -- in two groups. "We were in our seats when the accident happened around 7 pm. Our coach overturned after a loud noise. Somehow, we managed to get out through the window. Tiptoing through the bodies, we reached the main road and from there, went to Balasore town. We took a bus from Balasore, and reached Howrah," an injured Sarafat Ali Sheikh told PTI. Sheikh Putu, who was also injured, said they were going to Kerala to work as construction labourers. "After the pile-up, our coach got atop another bogey. We also managed to get out through the window. It was an indescribable situation with bodies scattered all around and injured persons lying on one another," he said. "After we got out, police removed us from the spot. We started calling up other people of our group. And after a few of us gathered, we hired a minivan and returned to Bhatar," he added. Putu claimed they did not get any help in Odisha. "After returning home, we were taken to the Bhatar hospital. From there, one person was sent to the Burdwan Medical College and Hospital. All of us are well," he said. A helpdesk has been set up at the Burdwan station by the district administration for providing assistance to the accident victims. Three trains -- Shalimar-Chennai Coromandel Express, Bengaluru-Howrah super fast and a goods train -- were involved in the pile-up, now being described as one of India's worst train accidents. The Coromandel Express crashed into a stationary goods train, derailing most of its coaches around 7 pm on Friday. A few passenger wagons of the Coromandel Express whiplashed the last few coaches of the Bengaluru-Howrah Express which was passing by at the same time.  Investigators are looking into possible human error, signal failure and other causes behind the three-train crash.(With PTI inputs)

Title: Ambulance rescuing highway crash victims on Chennai-Trichy NH hit by bus, 3 killed
content: TRICHY: Three people were killed and five others injured in a road accident involving multiple vehicles on Chennai-Trichy National Highway near Perambalur town early on Monday morning. While an ambulance was rescuing the injured victims in an accident involving a tourist van and a tractor on the highway, a speeding Omni bus approaching the highway rammed into the ambulance, thus causing fatalities.Police said that a tourist van travelling from Tiruvannamalai to Dindigul on Chennai-Trichy NH had attempted to overtake a tractor at around 2am. . .AdvertisementWhile overtaking, the tourist van hit the tractor and came to a halt over the centre median of the highway. The tractor overturned on the left side of the Chennai-Trichy NH. As the injured passengers in the tourist van alerted the 108 ambulance services, an ambulance from Perambalur reached the accident site near St Josephâ€™s school on the highway.The ambulance parked over the Trichy-Chennai NH, the other lane of the NH, was rescuing the injured passengers from the tourist van.Meanwhile, a speeding private Omni bus, proceeding towards Trichy from Chennai, to avoid hitting the tourist van at the accident site on the highway, breached the center median and rammed into the ambulance parked on the other side of the highway.The deceased included a â€˜108â€™ emergency ambulance services driver, identified as I Rajendran (45) and a native of Perambalur, and two passengers of the tourist van, identified as A Kuppusamy (60) and S Kavipriya (22), both residents of Dindigul. All three people were rescuing the injured passengers in the accident involving the van and the tractor.Police said that the impact of the collision between the Omni bus and the ambulance was such that the mangled ambulance was dragged several metres away from the accident spot. Senior officials from Perambalur district police inspected the spot and conducted inquiries. Five people injured in the accident are being treated at Perambalur government hospital.

Title: 2 killed as bike hits divider in Jagtial dist
content: Hyderabad: Two persons died in a road accident after their bike hit a divider in Jagtial. Over speeding is suspected to be the cause. One died on the spot and the other succumbed while undergoing treatment. The incident took place when they were going to Jagtial town.tnn

Title: Five from family killed in road accident near Tamil Nadu's Kancheepuram
content: CHENNAI: Five people from the same family, including a three-month old infant, were killed in a road accident near Kancheepuram along the Chennai-Bengaluru national highway late on Saturday.The lone survivor in the accident was identified as Ramajeyam, 35, of Chengam, Tiruvannamalai district. Ramajeyam's wife and three children and his cousin were killed, police said.The deceased were identified as Rathna, 28, her daughters Rajalakshmi, 5, Tejashree, 3, the 3-month-old boy and Ramajeyam's cousin, Rajesh, 29.Rathna had come to her parents home in Retteri near Chennai for the birth of her third child, while the children were there to spend their summer vacation. Since their elder daughter Rajalakshmi's school was about to reopen on Monday, they decided to go back to their native place.. .AdvertisementOn Saturday, Ramajeyam traveled to Rajesh's house in Tiruvannamalai and picked him up as he did not want to travel alone in the car to pick up his family members. From there, they came to Chennai and picked up Rathna and the children and were on their way back in the SUV.Police said Ramajeyam was driving the car to Tiruvannamalai when the accident happened near Sitherimedu.Around midnight, while moving along the Chennai-Bengaluru highway, the car's front wheel burst owing to which Ramajeyam lost control of the vehicle. The car rammed into a goods truck parked by the roadside.Due to the impact, the occupants in the car were crushed to death,.except Ramajeyam.Passerby alerted the authorities who rushed to the scene and recovered the bodies from the mangled remains of the car. They found Ramajeyam and the three-month-old child bleeding but conscious. Both were rescued with injuries and was rushed to the Kancheepuram government hospital for treatment. However, the child succumbed to injuries midway. Investigations revealed that the truck driver had halted the vehicle as it developed a mechanical snag, and he had gone to bring a mechanic to repair it.Baluchetty Chatram Police have registered a case and are investigating.

Title: 3 kids among 5 of family killed in accident on in Chennai
content: CHENNAI: Five people from a family, including a three-month-old infant, died in a road accident near Kancheepuram along Chennai-Bengaluru national highway late Saturday.The lone survivor in the accident was identified as Ramajeyam (35) of Chengam in Tamil Naduâ€™s Tiruvannamalai district. Ramajeyamâ€™s wife Rathna (28), three kids â€” daughters Rajalakshmi (5), Tejashree (3), and their three-month-old son â€” and his cousin Rajesh (29), died, police said. Rathna had come to her parentsâ€™ home in Retteri near Chennai for the birth of her third child, while her other kids were there to spend their summer vacation. Since their elder daughter Rajalakshmi's school was about to reopen on Monday, the family decided to return to their native place.On Saturday, Ramajeyam travelled to Rajeshâ€™s house in Tiruvannamalai and picked him up as he did not want to travel alone in the car to pick up his family members. From there, the duo came to Chennai and picked up Rathna and the children and were heading back in the SUV. Police said Ramajeyam was driving the car to Tiruvannamalai when the accident happened near Sitherimedu. Around midnight, while moving along the Chennai-Bengaluru highway, the carâ€™s front wheel burst and Ramajeyam lost control of the vehicle, which rammed into a goods truck parked by the roadside.

Title: Man killed, wife & kids injured as vehicle hits bike
content: Indore: A man was killed and his wife and two children were injured after the bike they were riding on was hit by an unidentified vehicle on Rangwasa Road in Rau on Saturday.The accident took place when the man, Shekhar Parmar, was returning his house in Palia with his family from his in-lawsâ€™ place on Saturday evening, police said.Just one kilometre away from his in-lawsâ€™ home an unknown vehicle hit the bike. The trio were rushed to MY Hospital, where Shekhar died, police said.. .AdvertisementRau police station in-charge Narendra Singh Raghuwanshi told TOI, â€œWe have recorded the death and trying to ascertain the identity of the vehicle driver.â€

Title: 32-year-old rider killed as speeding car rams in bike
content: Raipur: A 32-year-old helmetless bike rider was killed while another ridding the pillion was severely injured after a speeding car dashed their bike in village Ganiyari of Korba district on Saturday evening.According to Kartala police, the deceased was identified as Rajkumar Diwakar, a resident of village Ganiyari, who had gone to Sakti along with his friend Subhash to buy a cell phone on his two-wheeler. While returning around 7PM a speeding car hit their bike near village road Nagarda killing Rajkumar on the spot and injuring his friend Subhash. The driver of the car fled the spot.On receiving the information, the police reached the accident spot. The injured was sent to the district hospital for treatment while body of the deceased was sent for a post-mortem examination.A case has been registered against the car driver, and investigation is underway, added the police.TNN. .Advertisement

Title: 60-yr-old man, niece crushed under truck
content: Nagpur: Sixty-year-old Shivdas Wanjari and his niece Shruti, aged about 13 years, were killed in an accident after their two-wheeler was rammed by a truck on Mouda-Ramtek Road on Sunday.Shivdasâ€™s wife Rekha is now fighting for life at a city-based hospital. Shivdas was heading to attend a birthday party at a kinâ€™s place at Nerla from Bhugaon when the truck hit their two-wheeler. Shivdas and pillion riders Rekha and Shruti were thrown on the road.Mouda police have registered an offence against trucker Abeed Shaikh for rash and negligent driving.. .AdvertisementMan dies by suicide Nagorao Dukre, 59, died by suicide by hanging himself from a tree at Prince City Layout on Sunday. He had left home after a tiff with wife. As the family was searching for him, the body was found hanging from a tree branch. Hudkeshwar police registered a case of accidental death following a complaint by Dukreâ€™s son.Youngster booked for rapeA 30-year-old man, Gaurav Disle, was booked for rape following a complaint by a 34-year-old woman. The victim claimed that Disle was sexually exploiting her for the past seven years by promising to marry her.The woman has claimed that Disle had exploited her at several places. tnn

Title: 11-year-old among 8 killed on Samruddhi expressway over weekend
content: NAGPUR: Eight persons, including an 11-year-old boy, were killed in four accidents on the Samruddhi Mahamarg between Saturday morning and Sunday noon.Additional DG, Maharashtra state traffic, Ravinder Singal has cautioned motorists against speeding and rash driving on the e-way. "The e-way is a relatively new concept, which one should gradually get acclimatized to, rather than experimenting with speed. If speed is under control, any eventuality can be tackled without casualties," said Singal. He also warned that one must ensure mechanical fitness and road worthiness of their vehicle before driving on expressways.Early on Saturday morning, three members of a family, Rajendra Rajput (49), Dharamsingh Bhusige (51) and Raghvendra, aged about 11 years, were killed after their car rammed the divider and crossed into the opposite lane. The incident led to serious injuries to Raghvendra's parents, elder brother Shivam, and others. The accident took place near Dusalwadi village near Nashik. The family was heading to Jalna from Nashik.Later, on Saturday night, a mini truck rammed into a container truck from behind before rolling down the roadside slope after breaking the crash barrier. The driver of the mini truck, identified as Dinesh Kumar Tiwari, was trapped in the cabin.. .AdvertisementDinesh Tiwari succumbed to injuries in hospital after police rescued him. Early on Sunday morning, Vijay Mante, son Om, and nephew Tushar were killed in a hit-and-run on the freeway by an unidentified vehicle. The Mantes and their kin were heading to Beed from Washim after a marriage ceremony. The family had stopped for a water break on the roadside. â€œThree members of the family were standing near the driver side when the killer vehicle hit them,â€ said Pardeshi. One person (Vijay) was killed on the spot while the other two died in hospital. In another accident, one person was killed and another six were injured after a Nagpur-bound car turned turtle on the eway after the driver lost control. The accident took place at around 4am on Sunday, near Mehkar in Buldhana district.

Title: Karnataka accident: 5 killed, 13 injured as cruiser rams into lorry in Yadgir
content: YADGIR: As many as five people were killed and 13 others injured when a cruiser rammed into a lorry stationed at roadside in Yadgir district of Karnataka on Tuesday. The road crash occurred at Balichakra cross on National Highway 150 A under Saidapur police station limits.The accident took place when a cruiser, carrying 18 people from a family from Velugodu village of Andhra Pradesh, rammed into a lorry stationed at roadside. The cruiser commuters were said to be traveling to participate in an annual Urus of Khaza Bandenawaz in Kalaburagi.. .AdvertisementOn receiving the information, Yodgir DSP and Saidapur police went to the spot.The deceased were identified as Nayamt Ulla Maqbul (40), Muddasir Muneer, Rameeja Mohammef Khasim (50), Summi Mashum Basha (12) and Muneer. Police said all bodies are shifted to mortuary of Saidapur government hospital.Injured people are identified as Aayisha Mashum Basha (4), Anas Mashum Basha, Suhana Mashum Basha (8), Rameeja Muneer Shaik (32), Masi Ulla Nehamat Ulla (14), Seema Nyamat Ulla, Riyaz Unbee Zakeer Hussain (35), Mujju Muneer (12), Naseema Nyamat Ulla (36), Mashum Basha Khashim Sab (40), Mujakeer Zakeer (20), Haneefa Mashum Basha (30), and Sohel Khaleel (15). All injured people are admitted to the government hospital in Yadgir.

Title: 26-yr-old woman, 1-yr-old son killed in Singrauli road accident
content: Bhopal: A 26-year-old woman and her 1-year-old son were killed when the motorcycle they were ridding on was hit by a speeding truck in Singrauli district late on Sunday night. The womanâ€™s husband escaped with minor injuries.The incident sparked a protest by locals, who gathered in large numbers near the spot and demanded compensation for the deceased, and strict action against the driver and the owners of the truck.Incident took place near Mada market, under Mada police station of Singrauli district.Police said that Mamta Jaiswal (26) was travelling on a motorcycle with her husband and kid when their vehicle was hit by an unidentified truck, at around 8 pm, killing both the mother and son on the spot.. .Advertisementâ€œA case has been registered and a hunt has been launched to nab the accused,â€ Mada police station in-charge, Kapoor Tripathi told TOI.TNN

Title: Man, wife die in road accident
content: Visakhapatnam: A couple was killed and their three-year-old girl suffered severe injuries when their bike hit a roadside tree near Rushikonda.The deceased were M Peddayya (28) and his wife, M Pavani (24).Peddayya died on the spot and Pavani and the girl suffered severe injuries.Pavani died while undergoing treatment at a private hospital.. .AdvertisementThe daughter was shifted to KG Hospital in the city where her condition was said to be critical.TNN

Title: Two killed in accident near Virudhunagar
content: Virudhunagar: Two men were killed in an accident involving a government bus and a two-wheeler here on Monday. The victims have been identified as S Sethupathi, aged 26, from Maneetanpatti, and K Selvaganesh, aged 30, from Vadapatti near Sivakasi in Virudhunagar district.According to the police, Sethupathi and Selvaganesh, who were employed at a private finance company, were traveling on a two-wheeler from Virudhunagar to Sivakasi, with Selvaganesh as the pillion rider. Tragedy struck near Meesalur junction on the Virudhunagar-Sivakasi Road when a government bus, traveling in the opposite direction, collided with their two-wheeler. The impact of the collision caused fatal injuries, leading to the immediate demise of Sethupathi and Selvaganesh.. .AdvertisementFollowing the incident, their bodies were taken to the Virudhunagar Government Medical College hospital for postmortem. Amathur police registered a case, and an investigation is on.

Title: 2 killed, one injured in accident at Kasara Ghat
content: NASHIK: Two people were killed in an accident involving four vehicles in the Kasara Ghat section of the Mumbai-Agra highway early on Monday.The deceased have been identified as Istekar Khan (25) and Mustafa Khan (35) - both residents of Thane district. A truck driver, Wajid Vakil Khan (28), also from Thane, suffered serious injuries and has been admitted to the Kasara rural hospital.Assistant police inspector Amol Valzade, from the Ghoti traffic aid post (TAP) of the state highway police, said both Istekar and Mustafa died on the spot. "The place where the accident took place is known as a brake-fail point," he said.Wajid was driving the truck, loaded with chicken, towards Mumbai on the slope of the ghat section when he lost control of the vehicle and rammed another truck that was loaded with iron goods. "The iron-laden truck was moving slowly as there were two stationary vehicles - a truck and a pick-up van - in front of it," the officer said. . .AdvertisementDue to the impact, the iron-laden truck dashed into the other two vehicles and the truck loaded with chicken overturned, leading to Istekar and Mustafa's death."As soon as the accident was reported, a police team went to the spot and the injured were rushed to the hospital. The damaged vehicles were moved to the side of the road to streamline the flow of vehicles. The spot where the accident took place is an accident-prone area and necessary signboards have also been installed to warn the drivers. Had the truck driver kept the speed of his vehicle under control, the accident could have been averted," Valzade said.

Title: Rs 47L for kin of man killed in â€™18 rd accident
content: THANE: Kin of a spare part supplier who was mowed down in an accident involving a tourist car in 2018, has been awarded Rs 47 lakh by the Thane MACT. The claimants, widow of the deceased and their five kids, stated in the plea that the deceased was the sole breadwinner. The applicants submitted that the deceased on September 9, 2018, was proceeding on a bike on the Nashik highway for dinner at 2.30am. As it started raining, the victim stopped on the roadside and was crossing the road when a car hit him, leading to his death.

Title: Pune: Two killed in separate accidents in Narhe, Warje
content: PUNE: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in Narhe and Warje, along the Katraj-Dehu Road bypass, on Monday.Ambegaon Khurd resident Thakur Lakhan Kushwah (28), riding pillion on a bike, was killed after an unidentified vehicle hit the two-wheeler near the Swaminarayan temple around 4am on Monday. His friend, Vinay Bhandari, was driving the bike. Bhandari also suffered injuries. The driver of the unidentified vehicle did not stop after the accident.An officer of the Bharati Vidyapeeth police said Kushwah and Bhandari ran a food stall at Ambegaon. They were going to Dhayari for some personal work when the vehicle hit their bike. . .AdvertisementIn the other accident, pickup van driver Omkar Dattatray Pansare (21) from Junnar died after his vehicle crashed into the road divider in Warje around 4pm on Monday. The Warje Malwadi police said the accident occurred on the lane going to Katraj. The pickup van driver had lost control over his vehicle and it crashed into the road divider, the police added.

Title: 5 from Nandyal killed in K'taka road accident
content: TIRUPATI: Five people, hailing from Nandyal district in Andhra Pradesh, were killed in a road accident in Karnataka on Tuesday.According to Nandyal police, a group of devotees from Velugodu in Nandyal district went on a pilgrimage to the famous shrine of Hazrat Khwaja Banda Nawaz in Kalaburagi city to participate in the Urs celebrations. The SUV in which the devotees were travelling rammed a stationary truck, leaving five dead and 13 injured near Kalaburigi in Yadagiri district. The deceased were identified as Muneer (40), Nayamat (40), Rameeza Begum (50), Muddassir (12) and Shami (14), all residents of Velugodu.Karnataka police informed Velugodu police about the accident in which the Nandyal natives were killed. . .AdvertisementRelatives of the victims left for Kalaburagi to claim the bodies.

Title: 1 killed, four injured after car overturns
content: MUZAFFARNAGAR: One person was killed and four others injured when a car overturned on the Baraut-Muzaffarnagar road near Kakda village in Shahpur area in Uttar Pradesh's Muzaffarnagar district, police said on Tuesday. The incident took place on Monday killing Gulfam (28) on the spot, they said. SHO of Shahpur V K Sharma said the incident occurred when the occupants of the car were taking selfies while going to Muzaffarnagar to attend a wedding function. The injured were rushed to hospital where their condition was stated to be stable, said an official.

Title: 7 killed, 2 injured as dumper truck overturns on SUV in Madhya Pradesh's Sidhi district
content: BHOPAL: Seven people, including two children, were killed and two others sustained injuries after a dumper truck overturned on a sports utility vehicle (SUV) in Madhya Pradesh's Sidhi district on Thursday morning. The mishap took place between 9.30am and 10am along the Sidhi-Tikari Road near Dol village.The collision initially occurred when the dumper truck collided with the SUV, causing significant damage to both vehicles. However, the situation escalated as the dumper truck overturned onto the SUV, resulting in the fatal injuries of all seven occupants of the SUV.Upon receiving the alert, a police team arrived at the accident site. Authorities are working to gather evidence and determine the factors contributing to this tragic incident. . .AdvertisementThe injured have been provided immediate medical attention and are currently receiving treatment at a nearby hospital. The local administration has announced compensation for the families of the victims.

Title: 4 friends on holiday killed as car hits truck in Rajasthan's Sikar
content: JAIPUR: Avacation trip turned into a gruesome tragedy when a car carrying four friends, including a police constable, collided head-on with a truck loaded with bricks on Salasar Road in Sikarâ€™s Fathepur area on Wednesday.The victims were identified as constable Revant Ram Choudhary (28), Teja Ram Sihag (27), Shahrukh Khan (27), and Raju Riyaz Khan (34), all residents of Jodhpur. Revant Ram was a constable posted at Khandafalsa police station in Jodhpur. He had joined the trio after wrapping up his duty on Tuesday night.According to inspector Gur Bhupendra Singh, the group was en route to Himachal Pradesh for a vacation. â€œThe truck crashed into their car, dragging it for 50 metre before coming to a halt. The impact was so severe that the car was completely wrecked, we deployed cranes and machinery to extract the bodies,â€ he said.Cops said the car had come right under the wheels of the multi-axle truck while being dragged. The truck driver fled after the accident. Cops say the collision might have taken place when the truck was taking a U-turn but its driver failed to notice the car.

Title: Stationary wagons start rolling after storm, 6 killed
content: BHUBANESWAR/KENDRAPADA: A goods train, stationary for the past three months, accidentally rolled on six people at Jaipur Keonjhar Road station on Wednesday evening, killing them and leaving six others injured, barely days after the horrific accident in Bahanaga, barely 80 km away.East Coast Railway spokesman Biswajit Sahu said the monsoon reserve rake comprising 20 wagons carrying sand and crushed stone to be used in case of washouts during heavy rains, was stationed there for the past three months without an engine.A group of workers sought shelter beneath the carriages during a sudden rain and thunderstorm when the wagon rolled, triggering the accident, he said.Sahu said the carriages unexpectedly rolled down a short distance from their original position, probably trigged by a storm. "Since enough precautions are taken for the rakes not to move, what triggered its movement during the storm will be investigated," he said.Chief district medical officer of Jaipur Sibasis Maharana said they received six bodies at the district headquarters hospital. Two injured were referred to SCB Medical College and Hospital, Cuttack because their condition was critical, he said.The workers worked for the Indian Railways and were employed through a contractor. Locals said workers used to take rest under the standing rake in scorching heat and during rain and kept their belongings under it because it was stationed there for quite some time."I was taking shelter in a tea stall near the station to save me from the rain and when suddenly the goods train started rolling and some workers who were sitting under the train were killed and injured," said Nalini anta Behera, 35, a witness."The body parts of the workers were scattered on the track," said Amiya Jena, a local tea stall owner near the railway station.Chief minister Naveen Patnaik announced ex-gratia of Rs 5 lakh each for next of kin of the deceased from the Chief Minister's Relief Fund.

Title: Two Pilibhit tiger reserve workers killed in road accident, one injured
content: PILIBHIT: Two workers at the Pilibhit tiger reserve (PTR) died while their friend received serious injuries in an accident on Pilibhit- Madhotanda road on Thursday morning when the bike they were travelling on lost control and rammed into a roadside tree.The deceased were identified as Mahesh Kumar (28), who worked as a tourist guide, and Suraj Kumar (30) who was a safari vehicle driver at PTR. They were residents of Mustafabad forest staff colony and Joshi Colony respectively. Meanwhile, Shubhankar (24), a resident of Mustafabad Bengali Colony, was seriously injured.According to the police, the three injured persons were shifted to the Madhotanda community health center for treatment after the accident. Of them, Mahesh and Suraj were declared dead on arrival, while Shubhankar received multiple injuries and fractures in the accident.Madhotanda police station SHO Achal Kumar said, â€œThe trio was returning home on a bike from Bisalpur tehsil after attending a wedding ceremony of their friend. Of the three, Suraj Kumar was the front rider and none of them had put on a helmet. The two bodies have been sent for autopsy, and the injured was referred to district hospital.â€. .AdvertisementDilip Tiwari, sub-divisional forest officer of PTR's Mala circle, said that the jungle safari was suspended for a day and the foundation day ceremony of PTR scheduled on June 9 was also cancelled in mourning.

Title: 4 killed in road mishap in Lakhimpur Kheri
content: BAREILLY: Four people, including a six-year-old girl, were killed in a road accident in the Palia area of Lakhimpur Kheri district on Thursday afternoon, police said.The accident took place around 12 pm near the Panchmukhi Hanuman temple on the Palia-Nighasan road during a triple collision between the bike, an SUV car and a tractor-trolley, they added. The collision was so severe that the tractor split into two parts. The first part went down the roadside, while the other collided with the bike coming from behind. According to an eye witness, the four persons on the bike died on the spot.None of them was wearing helmets and died of head injuries, said police.. .AdvertisementThose dead have been identified as Mohd Zabir (34), wife Khushnuma, daughter Jannat (6) and friend Mohd Chaand (35). Police have sent the bodies for autopsy.Circle officer Palia said, â€œThe four people on the bike were on their way to Palia from Sitapur when they met with the accident. The drivers of the minivan and the tractor have also sustained injuries and have been admitted to hospital. We have informed their families about the accident. We will register an FIR after receiving a complaint.â€

Title: Two bikers killed in accidents in Mohali
content: Mohali: Two motorcycle riders were killed in two different road accidents in Mohali. One person was killed on Chandigarh-Ambala highway and the other in Sector 105 . In the first case, the victim was a 34-year-old motorcyclist who was hit by a speeding car in Sector 105. The deceased is Gaurav Sharma (34) of Khaspur village in Derabassi. Sukhwinder Lalof Manak Majra village told the police that he was going to Manak Majra village. Around 8.30pm, when he reached near Sector 105, he saw Gaurav Sharma being hit by a car bearing Haryana registration. He said that Gaurav was rushed to hospital, where he died during treatment. The car driver is at large. In the second incident, a speeding car driver allegedly hit a motorcycle rider on Chandigarh-Ambala highway in Lalru. The deceased has been identified as Rohit Sharma (18) of Lalru and his paternal uncle Ramandeep Singh, who is being treated at a local hospital, said the police. Police have seized the car and will arrest the driver.Truck overturns near Sector 7A truck overturned at the sectors 7-8-18-19 roundabout in the early hours of Thursday, causing a traffic jam. With the help of two cranes, traffic police removed the truck and only then the traffic movement became smoother on the stretch. The truck driver escaped unhurt. He said he lost control over the vehicle while trying to avoid a collision with a car. tnn

Title: 8 killed, 17 injured in road accident in northwest Pakistan
content: PESHAWAR: At least eight people, including two children of the same family, were killed and 17 others injured when a passenger vehicle fell into a deep ravine in a mountainous terrain in northwest Pakistan, authorities said on Friday.The pick-up vehicle was carrying 25 passengers of the same family who were going to Chitral from Upper Dir. It plunged into a ravine at Kochhan Gol near Lowari Top on Thursday while negotiating a sharp turn, authorities said.According to Rescue 1122 officials, four of the injured are said to be in critical condition.Road accidents are common in Pakistan and are caused mainly due to poor roads, badly maintained vehicles and unprofessional driving.. .AdvertisementPassenger vehicles are frequently crammed to capacity and seatbelts are not commonly worn, meaning high death tolls from single-vehicle accidents.

Title: Seven dead as dumper topples on SUV, scooter
content: BHOPAL: A wedding procession turned into a scene of mourning in MP's Sidhi district on Thursday morning when seven people, including two children, were killed and two injured after a dumper truck overturned on an SUV and a scooter. The Bolero was carrying eight people to a marriage ceremony in Sidhi and the dumper was coming from the opposite direction on Sidhi-Tikari Road. Around 10am, near Dol village, the dumper collided with the SUV and then toppled on top of it, crushing everyone inside. Scooterist got sandwiched between SUV and dumper  A scooterist was sandwiched between the two vehicles and killed as well. The accident site is 20km from the district headquarters and 560km from Bhopal. . .AdvertisementOther motorists alerted police and a team raced to the scene with a crane and metal-cutting machines. The scooterist's body was the last to be extricated from the mangled wreckage.There were only two survivors - Chandrabhan Yadav, 46, who was in the SUV and Pardeshi Lal, 35, the dumper cleaner. Both of them are in a critical condition in hospital. The deceased were identified as Shibbu aka Mangal Yadav, 10, Rohit Yadav, 15, Shiv Kumar Yadav, 18, Dharmendra Yadav, 19, Sukhlal Yadav, 55, Barati Rajaram Yadav, 56, and Ashish Shukla, 25. Shukla, the scooterist, was an executive for a major financial firm. The administration has announced compensation for the victims.

Title: Minor boy killed in road accident in Pratapgarh
