content: Prayagraj: A 10-year-old boy identified as, Mohd Hussain, a resident of Purwa Dilippur was crushed to death by a speeding vehicle near Baba Adhar Singh turn under the limits of Dilippur police station of Pratapgarh district on Friday afternoon. The incident, as per police, took place when the boy was crossing the road and came under the wheels of speeding vehicle. Locals also told police that boy was also dragged by the speeding vehicle for some distance. Body has been sent for post mortem. tnn

Title: Man, daughter among 3 die in road mishaps
content: Jaisalmer: In two road accidents, three persons including a father and daughter were killed, while one youth was injured in Barmer on late Thursday night.In the first accident in the Sheo area a father and daughter were travelling to Jaisalmer on a motorcycle, when they were hit by a vehicle from behind, killing them on the spot. In another incident a Scorpio hit a motorcycle in Dhorimanna area, which killed one on the spot and injured another, who has been referred to Gujarat. The victims have been identified as Modnath (25) and his 10-year-old daughter Neemb. Third victim is Manohar Lal. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Three of a family die as car hits bike in Dwarka, driver held
content: NEW DELHI: Three people, including a woman and her 10-year-old daughter, were killed after their bike collided with a Hyundai Creta car at a traffic signal in Dwarka on Thursday night. There were four people on the bike. The three pillion riders did not have helmets. The victims belonged to Madhya Pradesh.The injured biker claimed that he was going towards Sector 17 on the motorcycle from Bharat Vihar with his family when a car coming from Sector 13 hit them. Police have arrested the driver, a 24 -year-old man from Najafgarh. Both the vehicles were probably overspeeding, said an officer.Police received information regarding the incident around 11:30pm. The incident took place near the National Law University red light. A team of cops who reached the location saw the motorcycle and the car in an accidental position. The impact of the accident was such that the car flipped and turned to one side. The injured were moved to Indira Gandhi Hospital.Maate (32) and his daughter Diksha (10) who were critically injured were referred to Safdarjung Hospital after first aid. Maate's wife Foola (30) and his brother-in-law Lakhan (37) were declared brought dead. By evening, Diksha too succumbed to her injuries.. .AdvertisementHarsha Vardhan Mandava, deputy commissioner of police, Dwarka said, "The biker and his family members are doing labour work in the city. The accused, Abrar (24), a resident of Gopal Nagar, was travelling alone and on his way home when the accident took place. He did not flee from the spot and was arrested. He was medically examined and his blood alcohol level was 0. Abrar is unemployed".A case under section 279 (rash driving), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 304 A (death due to negligence) and 427 (mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the Indian Penal Code has been registered at Dwarka North police station.

Title: 5 dead in 3 separate road accidents in Hanamkonda
content: HANMAKONDA: Five persons were killed in three separate incidents in Hanamkonda district on Friday.In the first incident, a two-wheeler rammed into a truck from its rear side killing a youth and his sister. The accident occurred on the outer ring road between Karunapuram and Rampur villages. Police said the siblings were heading to Nagaram village in Hasanparthy mandal of Hanumakonda district on their two-wheeler from Hyderabad. The deceased have been identified as Poreddy Sumith Reddy and Pujitha.In a similar incident, a newly-married couple was killed in a road accident at Tanamcharla village crossroad in Mariped mandal. The couple was hit by a lorry coming from the opposite direction. The deceased have been identified as Tunagari Narayana (27) and his wife Anjaili (22). Narayana, who got married two months ago, was working as a software engineer in Hyderabad.The incident happened when the couple was coming from Hyderabad to Maripeda to visit their relatives.. .AdvertisementIn the third incident, a farmer identified as Azmeera Keema died at Meghya Thanda in Lingapuram village of Chennaraopet mandal. Keema lost control of the tractor while ploughing a field and fell into an agricultural well. The tractor sank into the well and the farmer drowned. The neighbours rushed to the spot and tried to save him. However, all their efforts went in vain.

Title: Wrong-side driving: Truck & bike collide on SPR, youth dies
content: GURGAON: A 21-year-old man was killed and his cousin injured after their motorcycle collided with a trailer truck coming from the wrong direction on the Southern Peripheral Road (SPR) in Sector 74 on Wednesday. The driver fled the spot and was arrested on Thursday.Around 4 am on Wednesday, Goldy Prajapat (21) and his cousin Nitin Kumar (35) headed to the Border Security Force (BSF) camp in Bhondsi from Kasan in Manesar on Kumarâ€™s motorcycle when a trailer truck hit their bike. The impact of the collision was such that the bike was mangled beyond recognition. According to police, the truck was being driven on the wrong side and suddenly took a sharp turn, due to which the duo couldnâ€™t stop the bike at the right time. It rammed the truck from behind.â€œThe truck was being driven recklessly on the wrong side. I could not control my bike as the truck turned suddenly, and hit it,â€ Kumar said in his complaint submitted at Badshahpur police station. Kumar, who was riding the bike, suffered minor injuries, but Goldy was thrown to the ground due to the impact and suffered grievous head injuries. He was rushed to the Sector 10 Civil Hospital, where he was declared brought dead by doctors. Passersby informed police and a team soon reached the spot. In the meantime, the truck driver fled, leaving the vehicle behind. A case was registered against the accused driver under Section 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC and relevant sections of the Motor Vehicles Act (MVA) at Badshapur police station.. .AdvertisementPolice traced the owner of the truck, who revealed that Shahzad (30), originally from UPâ€™s Meerut, was driving the truck.â€œThe accused has been identified as Shahzad. He fled the accident spot and was arrested on Thursday after we asked the truckâ€™s owner,â€ said Umesh Kumar, the officer in charge of the SPR police post.

Title: Pune: Typist killed in hit-and-run on Paud Phata flyover
content: PUNE: A 57-year-old typist working at a lawyer's office in the city died on Tuesday night after an unknown vehicle hit his scooter on the Paud Phata overbridge.The victim, Avinash Nikumbh, who hailed from Nashik and was currently residing at Warje, was rushed to a private hospital on Paud Road. Later, he was shifted to the Sassoon general hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries in the early hours of Wednesday.The accident happened when Nikumbh was going to his brother's house at the left Bhusari colony for dinner, a Kothrud police officer said. Nikumbh had shifted to Pune from Nashik just two months ago. "He was working as a typist at a lawyer's office near the Pune Railway station," the police officer said. He said that some unknown vehicle had hit Nikumbh's scooter when he was on the Paud Phata flyover. "We are checking the CCTV footage from nearby cameras to identify the vehicle," the officer added.

Title: Nagpur cop killed in accident in MP's Dhar, two colleagues left injured
content: NAGPUR:A team of personnel from Wathoda police station met with an accident at Dhar in Madhya Pradesh leading to the death of 55-year-old head constable Nandkishor Kadu. The private vehicle, in which the police personnel were travelling, rammed into a road divider on Friday. The three cops had left Nagpur and were heading towards Rajasthan in the car owned and driven by Kadu.The two other cops injured in the accident were identified as naik police constable Sachin Shripad, and head constable Radhesham Khapekar. Itâ€™s learnt that Shripad was seated beside Kadu.Sources said that cops had lunch before the accident. Kadu, father of two sons, is likely to have dozed off after the meal while being behind the wheels. The three cops, after procuring necessary permissions, were heading towards Rajasthan when the accident happened close to the Madhya Pradesh border.. .AdvertisementThe cops were supposed to visit some places in Rajasthan, and Gujarat in connection to a case of fraud and cheating registered at the Wathoda police station. The injured cops are now recuperating at a local hospital.

Title: Truck hits 6 kids; 3 crushed to death
content: JHANSI: In an early morning accident that took place on Friday, around half a dozen children, who had gone for morning walk were hit by an uncontrolled fast-moving truck. This happened on Jhansi- Kanpur highway, near village Madora which falls under Pooch police station of Jhansi. The children had gone for a morning walk and as they took a halt on the service road, a speeding truck rammed into the road,killing three and injuring two minors. A passer-by informed the police. Pooch police station SP Surendra Singh along with police force rushed to the accident spot after and sent the injured to Jhansi medical college.Abhiraj (12) died on the spot while Abhinav (14) and Anuj (15) died on way to the hospital. The other three children are undergoing treatment and are said to be in critical condition. Police were able to arrest one of the truck drivers, while the other one managed to flee along with the truck.

Title: 2 news channel employees killed in road accident in Noida
content: NOIDA: Two employees of a television news channel were killed on Sunday allegedly after their bike collided with a pick-up truck in Noida, police said.According to the police, the incident occurred at around 7.45am on a stretch in Sector 62 that leads to the Elevated Road, under Sector 24 police station.â€œThe injured were sent to the private Kailash Hospital where both succumbed during treatment. The duo has been identified as Manoj Kumar (45) and Gaurav (38), both residents of Ghaziabad,â€ police said in a statement.The pick-up truck involved in the crash has been impounded while its driver is absconding, they said.. .Advertisement"Of the two motorcycle riders, only one was wearing a helmet at the time of the incident," an official said.The deceased worked for a private Hindi TV news channel located in Film City in Sector 16A of Noida, one of their colleagues said.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: Two killed in Surajpur road accident
content: Raipur: Two persons lost their lives in a fatal road accident on Friday evening near Sakalo village on the Ambikapur-Pratappur road in Surajpur district. The incident took place in the Gandhinagar police station area when a speeding truck struck them from behind.The victims, identified as 30-year-old Naval Saay and 31-year-old Shivlal Kumar, both residents of Mathidaand village, were en route to Ambikapur after purchasing groceries. The collision occurred as they reached Sakalo village, with a speeding trailer from the opposite direction colliding with their bike.The impact of the accident was severe, causing both victims to be thrown off the road, resulting in their immediate death, as confirmed by the police. During the investigation, it was discovered that the trailer driver fled the scene after the collision. A case has been registered against the driver. The investigation is ongoing, according to the police. TNN

Title: 5 killed, 8 injured in road accident in Andhra Pradesh's Tirupati
content: TIRUPATI: Five people were killed and eight others sustained injuries in a ghastly road accident on Tirupati-Chennai national highway on Sunday.A group of employees working at a private company in Tirupati hired a passenger vehicle to visit the Anjeramma folk temple in Vadamalpeta near Puttur.The vehicle driver who went in the wrong direction to avoid a U-turn, after crossing over the SV puram toll plaza near the Vadamalpet ghat road, rammed it into a milk van coming in the opposite direction.While two persons died on the spot, several others who sustained injuries were immediately rushed to the SVRR government general hospital in Tirupati by the police. . .AdvertisementPuttur DSP K Srinivas told reporters that two passengers identified as Girijamma and Revanth died on the spot while three others, including Rekha, Ajay Kumar and the milk van driver Subramanyam died while undergoing treatment at the hospital in Tirupati later.DSP Srinivas attributed the accident to the negligence of the driver who took the vehicle in the wrong route to avoid a U-turn in order to shorten the travel distance to the local shrine.Puttur police have registered a case and are further investigating.

Title: Four of family killed as MUV hits wall on Samruddhi Expressway
content: NASHIK: Four members of a family died when their vehicle hit a roadside wall on Samruddhi Expressway near Sinnar on Monday.The deceased hailed from Shirdi and were travelling in a multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) along the Samruddhi Mahamarg route."The accident took place near Khambale village near Sinnar," said the highway police.Shashikant Giri, PI of the highway police, said, "According to initial investigations, the passengers in the MPV had been to Mumbai to see off their relatives leaving for the Haj pilgrimage and were returning home.". .AdvertisementThe victims took the Samruddhi Expessway from Bharvir and were heading towards Shirdi when the driver apparently lost control over the vehicle and crashed into the safety wall by the roadside.Three people died on the spot and one died during the course of treatment at a hospital in Sinnar.Four other injured people have been shifted to Shirdi for further treatment.The deceased have been identified as Razaq Ahmed Shaikh (55), Sattar Shaikh Lal Shaikh (65), Sultana Sattar Shaikh (50) and Faiyaz Dagubhai Shaikh (40).The highway police are examining the accident spot.

Title: 6 killed, 2 injured as car rams into lorry in Andhra Pradesh's East Godavari
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: As many as six people were killed after the car ferrying them rammed into a parked lorry in East Godavari district of Andhra Pradesh on Monday morning.The accident took place on the national highway at Ananthapalli in Nallajerla mandal in East Godavari district. Eight people were travelling in Mahindra SUV 500 car when the accident took place and two persons were injured. The deceased were coming from Hyderabad to Rajahmundry. . .AdvertisementThe deceased have been identified as Midde Sathi Babu, Midde Teja, Midde Sai, Aruna, Sravani and an eight-month-old baby. Kovvur DSP Verma said that further information is not known as the relatives are yet to reach the accident spot. The injured persons were shifted to Rajahmundry Government General Hospital.

Title: Property dealer, 1 other killed in road accident
content: Prayagraj: Two persons including a property dealer were killed in a road accident when their car collided with speeding tractor & its trolley near Tikri petrol pump road on Prayagraj Lucknow highway under the limits of Nawabganj police station late on Saturday night. The impact of collision was so powerful that the car got turned turtle after collision. Both the victims died on spot. The deceased have been identified as Ajay Kumar Yadav (30) and Sandeep Yadav (24), residents of Phaphamau and Nawabganj respectively. Police said that the incident took place around 12 :30 am (Saturday night) when a car carrying two persons was on its way to Prayagraj from Nawabganj collided with a speeding tractor and its trolley near Tikri village. Hearing sound of collision, locals rushed the spot and found both the youths trapped inside the car. Upon being alerted, a team of Nawabganj police rushed the spot and carried out rescue operation.. .Advertisement However, cops found both the occupants dead and sent their bodies for post mortem. Police said deceased Ajay Kumar was a property dealer and he along with his friend Sandeep Yadav had come to Nawabganj to drop one of the friend. While returning to Prayagraj, their car collided with a speeding stone and iron laden tractor trolley. Police said that Ajay was married and driving the car while Sandeep was unmarried and was in DJ light and sound business. Both the deceased were the only sons of their respective families.

Title: Three die in road accident in Samastipur
content: PATNA: Three persons died and three others were severely injured while returning from a wedding procession under Singhia police station area in Samastipur district Saturday night.According to sources, an SUV returning from a wedding procession hit an overturned truck on Singhia Baheri Path, killing two persons on the spot. The third died at Darbhanga Medical College and Hospital (DMCH). Three of the injured were in critical condition.The deceased have been identified as SUV driver Shyam Kumar Yadav (25) of Baheri Ujaina, Mohammad Sonu (16) and Nabi Ahmed (14) of Darbhanga. The bodies of Shyam and Sonu were sent for postmortem at Samastipur Sadar Hospital. The autopsy of the third was conducted at DMCH.Singhia SHO Krishnakant Mandal said two trucks had met with an accident at Marwada Chowk three days ago. â€œOne truck was removed, but the other was left on the roadside. The SUV driver could not see the overturned truck due to high speed and darkness, which led to the accident. An FIR has been registered in this connection,â€ he added.. .Advertisement

Title: Two injured in accident die
content: Visakhapatnam: Two more persons who were involved in Laveru road accident died and the death toll has increased to three. It may be recalled that one person was killed and over 10 people suffered injuries after a tyre of the vehicle (van) they were travelling in burst on NH-16 stretch near Budumure in Laveru mandal in Srikakulam district a few days ago. Police said the injured persons D Ramana and B Bhavani succumbed on Saturday night while undergoing treatment. TNN

Title: Biker on way to Kol from Ranchi dies in highway accident
content: Kolkata/Howrah: A young merchant navy professional and avid biker was killed on his way back home to Kolkata from Ranchi on his bike in a road accident on Kona Expressway early on Friday. Tanmay Kumar (33) was left at a Howrah morgue as an unidentified body as his newly-wed wife, parents and other family members looked for him and lodged missing complaints in Jharkhand and Bengal. Cops said Kumar had met with an accident near Jalan industrial complex on Kona Expressway around 20kms from Kolkata. Officers told Kumarâ€™s family that his phone and wallet were stolen. The bike had a Jharkhand registration number and local cops were alerted accordingly. However, his identity couldnâ€™t be ascertained, a senior officer of Howrah rural police said. It was after Kumarâ€™s family members began reaching out to senior police and government officials in Bengal and Jharkhand that Domjur police called Kumarâ€™s wife Subhasmita Maity, asking her to identify an accident victimâ€™s body.Maity and her brother Bireshwar identified Kumar. His body was handed over to the family on Sunday afternoon. â€œMy sister was married in April. They had moved to an apartment in Tiljala. My brother-in-law was an avid biker. I canâ€™t believe his passion for riding killed him,â€ he said. Family members said Kumar had gone to Ranchi for a family engagement. He left for home around 8pm on Thursday. Maity had called around 12.40am on Friday but couldnâ€™t hear him. Kumar had spoken to a friend around the same time when he said he was at Kharagpur and on his way to Kolkata. â€œAccording to CCTV footage, the biker was riding at a high speed. He rammed into a truck. He was dead by the time officers spotted him and took him to the hospital,â€ said a senior officer of Howrah rural police.. .Advertisement

Title: 51-year-old man killed, 7 of his relatives hurt in accident on Jaipur-Agra highway
content: Jaipur: A 51-year-old man was killed and seven of his family and relatives were critically injured when their SUV crashed into an unidentified vehicle on Jaipur-Agra national highway under the Bassi police station area in the early hours of Sunday. Bassi police stationâ€˜s assistant sub-inspector (ASI) Harikesh Singh said that the victim was identified as Mahesh Singh, a resident of Vaishali Nagar. â€œHe was returning home with his family members from Vrindavan in Mathura,â€ he said. Police said that the accident occurred after 2.30am when the SUV in which Singh was travelling with some of his family and relatives hit the rear of an unidentified vehicle forcing the SUV to veer off the road and lose control. â€œMahesh Singh died in the accident. Other seven passengers were transported to the trauma ward of the SMS Hospital where they are undergoing treatment,â€ said Harikesh.He said that the passengers were residents of Jaipur and returning after spending some time in Vrindavan in Mathura.. .AdvertisementThe mishap immediately sparked a commotion on the highway even as people rushed to the cries of the victims struck inside the vehicle. The ambulance reached the scene and took the injured to the hospital. The accident disrupted traffic on the busy Jaipur-Agra highway. However, authorities called a crane machine to remove the wrecked SUV from the road following which the traffic resumed. Police said that the SUVâ€™s front was destroyed in the accident. The front of the SUV was crumpled while parts of the engine were scattered all over the highway.

Title: Nine-yr-old girl and bus driver killed, 11 injured in Pune-Bengaluru highway crash
content: PUNE: The driver of a private luxury bus from Karnataka and a nine-year-old girl on board a Maharashtra state transport Shivshahi bus were both killed, while 11 others were injured, in an accident involving two buses and two container trucks, around 8.15am on Sunday. The tragedy took place in Varve of Bhor tehsil, around 30km from the city on the Pune-Bengaluru highway.Police identified the deceased girl as Rutuja Chavan of Uttamnagar in Pune and the private bus driver as Shivrajkumar Gauda (32). "Chavan was on her way to Sangli. The private bus was heading towards Pune from Karnataka," said an officer from the Rajgad police.Pune rural police said the Karnataka travel operatorâ€™s bus, which was on the way to Pune, was taking a turn to the service road for a breakfast halt, when it was hit from behind by a container truck. The force of the impact caused the heavy vehicle to turn on its side. After the collision, the truck veered out of control to the right and landed in the Satara corridor of the highway, where it collided with the Shivshahi bus. . .AdvertisementAt the same time, another container truck coming from behind also crashed into the Shivshahi bus suddenly, the police said. An officer from the Rajgad police also confirmed, â€œAfter the first impact between the private bus and the container truck, the latter jumped the divider on the route and crashed into the Shivshahi bus. Another container truck, which was plying behind the Shivshahi bus, hit its rear.â€ The accident caused disruption of traffic for three hours along the busy highway. Three cranes had to be summoned to remove the two buses and the two trucks. â€œThe bus had turned on its side blocking the Pune corridor of the highway. Post-removal of vehicles, traffic resumed normalcy,â€ the officer said, adding, â€œThe 11 injured victims were passengers from both buses. They were taken to nearby private hospitals.â€

Title: 7 of a family killed as car rams lorry
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: Seven members of a family, including a nine-month-old infant, died when the car in which they were travelling crashed into a stationary lorry on NH-16 at Ananthapalli in Andhra Pradesh's East Godavari district early Monday. Police took the lorry driver into custody. The accident took place around 6.30am when the eight-member family was returning from Hyderabad after attending a housewarming function on Sunday night to Rajahmundry in a Mahindra SUV. Six persons - Midde Satyanarayana (46), Midde Ravi Teja (24), Midde Aruna (26), Dasari Sravani (26), Relangi Lakshmi (50), and Lohit Gagan (nine months) - died on the spot. â€˜Negligent parking of lorry caused accidentâ€™Atukuri Durga (50) succumbed on the way to a hospital.. .AdvertisementMidde Sai (24), the lone survivor, was shifted to Government General Hospital in Rajahmundry. He is an AC mechanic at a car servicing centre in Rajahmundry.Kovuru DSP V S N Varma said the car, driven by Satyanarayana, hit the lorry that was parked on the left side of the road on the flyover. He said the accident occurred due to negligent parking of the lorry in the middle of the flyover on the outskirts of Ananthapalli.Sai said he was asleep when the accident happened. "When I woke up, I saw my father, brother and other relatives in a pool of blood. Someone pulled me out. Durga, too, was taken out of the car and she was unconscious," he recalled.Sai lost his father Satyanarayana - who ran an agency selling used cars - and brother Ravi Teja in the accident.The victims' bodies were taken to their native place Velicherla for last rites.

Title: Treat Williams killed in motorcycle accident
content: Actor Treat Williams, whose nearly 50-year career included starring roles in the TV series "Everwood" and the movie "Hair", died after a motorcycle crash in Vermont, state police said. He was 71. Shortly before 5 pm, a Honda SUV was turning left into a parking lot when it collided with Williams' motorcycle in the town of Dorset, according to a statement from Vermont State Police.Read AlsoPopular South actor Kazan Khan passes away due to a heart attackKazan Khan, who was popular for his role in Tamil and Malayalam movies, passed away recently after suffering a heart attack. There were no detailed report about the actor's demise since he had been off films for the last 8 years.'Snowdrop' actress Park Soo Ryun passes away at 29 after fatal fall from stairs'Snowdrop' actress Park Soo Ryun has passed away after a fatal fall. Media reports state that the actress was found 'unresponsive' despite attempts to save her life. The 29-year-old was later declared brain dead."Williams was unable to avoid a collision and was thrown from his motorcycle. He suffered critical injuries and was airlifted to Albany Medical Centre in Albany, New York, where he was pronounced dead," according to the statement. Williams was wearing a helmet, police said. The SUV's driver received minor injuries and wasn't hospitalised. He had signalled the turn and wasn't immediately detained although the crash investigation continued, police said.. .AdvertisementWilliams, whose full name was Richard Treat Williams, lived in Manchester Centre in southern Vermont, police said. His agent, Barry McPherson, also confirmed the actor's death. "I'm just devastated. He was the nicest guy. He was so talented," McPherson told People magazine. "He was an actor's actor," McPherson said. "Filmmakers loved him. He's been the heart of the Hollywood since the late 1970s." The Connecticut-born Williams made his movie debut in 1975 as a police officer in the movie "Deadly Hero" and went on to appear in more than 120 TV and film roles, including the movies "The Eagle Has Landed", "Prince of the City" and "Once Upon a Time in America".He was nominated for a Golden Globe Award for his role as hippie leader George Berger in the 1979 movie version of the hit musical "Hair". He appeared in dozens of television shows but was perhaps best known for his starring role from 2002 to 2006 in "Everwood" as Dr Andrew Brown, a widowed brain surgeon from Manhattan who moves with his two children to the Colorado mountain town of that name. Williams also had a recurring role as Lenny Ross on the TV show "Blue Bloods". Williams' stage appearances included Broadway shows, including "Grease" and "Pirates of Penzance". Colleagues and friends praised Williams as kind, generous and creative. "Treat and I spent months in Rome filming "Once Upon a Time in America'," actor James Woods tweeted. "It can be pretty lonely on the road during a long shoot, but his resilient good cheer and sense of humour was a Godsend. I really loved him and am devastated that he's gone." "Working with Treat Williams in Mamet's "Speed the Plow" at Williamstown in '91 was the start of great friendship," tweeted writer, director and producer Justine Williams. "Damn it, damn it. Treat, you were the best. Love you." "Treat Williams was a passionate, adventurous, creative man," actor Wendell Pierce tweeted. "In a short period of time, he quickly befriended me & his adventurous spirit was infectious. We worked on just 1 film together but occasionally connected over the years. Kind and generous with advice and support. RIP."Also See: Best Hollywood Movies Of 2023 | Top Rated English Movies Of 2023 | Latest Hollywood Movies

Title: Massive fire on Pune-Mumbai Expressway: 3 dead, 2 injured as tanker goes up in flames
content: PUNE: At least three people were killed and two others sustained severe burn injuries when a chemical tanker overturned and caught fire on a bridge in the Khandala ghat section of Pune-Mumbai Expressway on Tuesday.The incident affected the traffic movement on the expressway and Kune village road under the bridge.Three to four firefighting vehicles and a couple of ambulances were pressed into service as the authorities mounted an effort to douse the fire and clear the expressway for normal traffic. . .AdvertisementThe intensity of the flames bursting out of the chemical tanker was very high and engulfed a couple of vehicles on the Kune village road under the bridge, forcing the authorities to halt the traffic. After the tanker toppled, the diesel tank began to leak, and as a result of friction, it caught fire, according to additional superintendent of police (Pune Rural) Mitesh Ghatte."A woman and her two children who were travelling on a scooter under the bridge sustained severe burns," Ghatte said.Sumaiyya Bagwan, assistant inspector of the Highway Safety Patrol (HSP) said, "We have stopped the heavy vehicles at Khalapur Toll Plaza and at Urse Toll Plaza. The cars have been diverted via Pune-Mumbai highway from Lonavla exit," said Bagwan.Watch At least 3 dead after oil tanker overturns causing massive fire on Pune-Mumbai Expressway

Title: 4 of family killed in Samruddhi car crash
content: NASHIK: Four members of a family were killed and three others injured when the car they were travelling in rammed the divider on the Shirdi-Bharvir stretch of Samruddhi expressway near Khambale village of Sinnar taluka on Monday. This was the second major accident on the Shirdi-Bharvir stretch of the Samrudhhi expressway after it was inaugurated by Maharashtra CM Eknath Shinde and deputy CM Devendra Fadnavis on May 26. On June 3, three people were killed in a car crash on the stretch of the expressway. Inspector Ramnath Tandalkar of the Wavi police said the driver lost control over the vehicle while moving towards Shirdi after driving 4km from the toll plaza near Gonde Phata around 12.30am on Monday. 4 of family killed in Samruddhi car crash The accident victims from the Kankuri Road area of Shirdi had gone to Mumbai to see off their relatives leaving for Haj pilgrimage. They met with the accident on their way back to Shirdi. . .AdvertisementInspector Shashikant Giri of the highway police said three people died on the spot and one succumbed to his injuries at Sinnar rural hospital. The police identified the victims as Razaq Ahmed Shaikh (55), Faiyaz Dagubhai Shaikh (22), who was driving the car, Sattar Shaikh Lal Shaikh (68) and Sultana Shaikh (62).The police said speeding, faulty tyres and dozing off while driving were some of the main reasons behind the accidents, as there was no scope of head-on collisions on the highway. MSRTC bus, truck, car collide in rural NashikThirty-two passengers on board an Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation bus had a narrow escape as the vehicle, a pick-up truck and a car collided near Akrale Phata in Dindori taluka around 12.30 pm on Monday. Four bus passengers were injured and released from a government hospital in Dindori after treatment. An MSRTC official said the Nashik-bound bus was descending a small ghat section when the pickup truck, which was overtaking another vehicle from the opposite direction, hit it. "The bus driver steered the vehicle further to the left and it fell off the ghat road and turned on its side. The pick-up truck then hit the car that was following the bus," the official said.

Title: Man killed in road accident
content: Bhopal: A car hit a divider and rammed into a road signage pole in Bilkhiria locality in the wee hours of Tuesday, killing a contractor and injuring his relative.SHO Bilkhiria BP Singh Bains said that deceased Brijesh Singh Rajput, 36, had come to Barkheda Pathani in Govindpura to attend a wedding with a relative Durgesh Rajput. At around 12.30am, the duo left for Satlapur when their vehicle, after hitting a road divider, crashed into a road signage pole. TNN

Title: Andhra Pradesh: 5-year-old killed, five others injured in ghat road accident
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A 5-year-old boy S Vishal Teja of Gokiri village in Koyyuru mandal was killed and several others were seriously injured when an auto-rickshaw, in which they were traveling, plunged into the valley at Teppala Mamidi in Chintapalli mandal in Alluri district on Wednesday.According to sources, all were returning from a village after participating in a religious program. The auto-rickshaw met with an accident at a curve near Teppala Mamidi village Errabommala Panchayat in Chintapalli mandal when the driver could not control the vehicle. The vehicle plunged into the valley on the ghat road.Police sources said that injured persons- Venkat Lakshmi, Rajababu, and Papayamma - were admitted to a private hospital in Narsipatnam while three others- Kumari, Anusha, and Nookaratnam were shifted to Kin George Hospital in Vizag.

Title: Four killed as Methanol tanker catches fire on Pune-Mumbai expressway
content: PUNE: Four people, including a woman and two minors, were charred to death while 3 others sustained severe burns after a methanol tanker turned on its side and caught fire on a bridge near Lonavla around 11.30am on Tuesday. The incident also brought traffic on Pune-Mumbai Expressway and the road to Kune village under the bridge to a standstill for over an hour.The intensity of the flames spewing out of the tanker was so high that it engulfed a couple of vehicles on the Kune village road under the bridge, forcing Highway Safety Patrol (HSP) authorities to halt traffic between 11.40am and 12.40pm to avoid any untoward incident.Police identified three of the deceased as Savita Vare (35), her son, Kushal (8), and nephew Ritesh Koshire (17), all from Kune village. They were caught by the flames while passing the road under the bridge on a motorcycle. Ritesh was riding the bike."We are yet to identify the fourth deceased and ascertain if he was travelling in the tanker or walking along the bridge," additional superintendent of police (Pune Rural) Mitesh Ghatte told TOI.. .AdvertisementSmaller vehicles were diverted to Pune-Mumbai highway via Lonavla exit. The Pune corridor of the expressway was thrown open for traffic around 12.45pm. One lane of the Mumbai corridor was started after gaining control over the fire.The Mumbai corridor was opened for traffic after the tanker was moved from the expressway with the help of cranes in the evening. One of the cranes was engulfed by the flames of a second fire while the tanker was being moved. The crane operator jumped out to safety, a police officer said. â€œFour people have lost their lives in a tragic accident on expressway. Three people were injured in this accident. They are undergoing treatment in a hospital. The state government is monitoring the situation,â€ deputy CM Devendra Fadnavis posted on his official Twitter handle. Five fire-fighting vehicles from the Lonavla and Khopoli municipal councils, the Indian Navyâ€™s Lonavla-based training facility INS Shivaji and a couple of ambulances were pressed into service as the authorities mounted an elaborate effort to control the fire and clear the expressway. Lonavla sub-divisional police officer Satya Sai Karthik told TOI, â€œThe tanker carrying methanol from Pune to Mumbai turned on its side after hitting the railings and the divider on the bridge on the expressway. We are investigating what led to the accident.â€ He said, â€œThe highly inflammable chemical spilled out and caught fire due to friction. Apart from affecting two vehicles under the bridge, the flames also engulfed two commercial vehicles, a car and a two-wheeler.â€ Karthik said, â€œAmong the injured was the yet-to-be-identified tanker driver, Tanesh Kolatkar (23), who was in a car parked under the bridge, and Chandrakant Gurav, who was travelling in the tanker. They are undergoing treatment at a hospital in Somatane Phata.â€ The fire fighters had a tough time dousing the blaze. â€œThey had initially controlled the fire, but it intensified again. They used foam to douse the blaze,â€ the officer said. The accident caused major traffic congestion on the expressway. â€œWe had to stop the heavy vehicles at Khalapur and Urse toll plazas on either end of the expressway,â€ said superintendent of police (HSP) Lata Phad. â€œWe diverted the light vehicles going to Mumbai via Pune-Mumbai highway from the Lonavla exit," Phad added. A Pune Rural police officer said, â€œThe possibility of the tanker driver losing control over the vehicle due to a full load of the chemical and hitting the dividers cannot be ruled out. Things would be clear once we take the RTO's opinion on the tankerâ€™s fitness.â€ Puneâ€™s deputy regional transport officer, Sanjiv Bhor, told TOI, â€œAll vehicles are checked by the RTO for fitness every year.â€

Title: 2 killed as mini-truck hits bike in Lucknow's Malihabad
content: LUCKNOW: Two youths riding a motorcycle were killed in a road accident which was reported on Monday midnight in Malihabad police station area. The deceased were identified as Sachin Kashyap (19) and his brother Vipin Kashyap (16), both of Malihabad. According to police, the Malihabad police got information about the accident which took place near an eating joint close to a water park around midnight.In the accident, a motorcycle and a mini-truck ferrying milk had a head on collision and the youths riding the motorcycle suffered critical injuries. The victims were rushed to CHC Malihabad where doctors pronounced both dead.DCP West Zone, Rahul Raj said the mini-truck had been identified and the police are looking for the driver. "Eyewitnesses told police that the youths riding the motorcycle were going towards Malihabad when the mini-truck lost its control and hit the bike killing the victims on the spot," he said.. .AdvertisementThe residents expressed anger over the accident, saying over-speeding four-wheelers plying on the road were posing a danger to the lives of commuters. The family said Vipin was going to see off Sachin who was going to Lucknow where he stayed at a house on rent and was preparing for exams. Vipin was also studying at a school in Lucknow but he visited his home in Malihabad during the summer vacation. Their father Sunil Kumar works as a farmer.

Title: Three killed in road accident
content: Belagavi: Three persons were killed when their bike collided head-on with a mini-truck on the Jewargi-Sankeshwar state highway near Basavanalagaddi village of Chikodi taluk late at night on Tuesday.According to police, Prashant Bairu Khot 21, of Rupinal village and Satish Kallappa Hirekodi,28, of Kerur village died on the spot. While the third person, Yalagouda Chandrakant Patil, 23, of Devagiri village, succumbed to his injuries at the hospital.The mini-truck driver fled the spot soon after the accident, after abandoning the vehicle. A case has been registered.Workers run over by earthmover. .AdvertisementKalaburagi: Three migrant labourers died after an earthmover ran over them at a field in Neelavanji village in Devadurga taluk in Raichuru district in the early hours of Wednesday. The deceased are Visnhu, 25, Shivaram, 30, and Balaram, 28, are from Chattisgarh. Staying in Raichur, they dug borewells for a living. On Tuesday night, they dug a borewell on land owned by Balaiah till late in the night. They later slept on the same field, and the driver of the earthmover, who did not notice the sleeping labourers, ran over them, killing them on the spot. Devadurga police have registered a case and detained Basappa.Job fair on SaturdayBelagavi: Rotary Club of Venugram, Belagavi, in association with Spectrum Talent Management Limited, Bengaluru, has organised â€˜Rotary Mega Job Fair-2023â€™ on June 17 at Jyoti College premises located on Club Road. The job drive is open for all and there will be no registration or placement fee. Call for registration: 8618658961, 7290024440. MLA inspects devpt worksHubballi: The Hubballi-Dharwad Central MLA Mahesh Tenginakai inspected various Smart City works which are under way here on Wednesday. The MLA visited Nehru Stadium and Chitaguppi Hospital. Tenginakai said he has already held a review meeting with the officers of all the departments. â€œIt has been decided to look at problems and figure out which department should take the remedial action. Smart City has already taken up 35 works in our constituency, of which 32 have been completed. They will be handed over to the HDMC later,â€ he added.

Title: 5-year-old boy killed in ghat road accident
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: A five-year-old boy, S Vishal Teja of Gokiri village in Koyyuru mandal, was killed and six others were injured when an auto-rickshaw in which they were travelling plunged into the valley at Teppala Mamidi in Alluri district on Wednesday.The injured were Kudumula Venkata Lakshmi, Rata Raja Babu, Kudumula Kumari, Kudumula Papayamma, Kasipalli Anusha and Kasipalli Nookaratnam.According to sources, they were returning after participating in a religious programme. The auto-rickshawâ€™s driver lost control of the vehicle at a curve near Teppala Mamidi. Police sources said Venkat Lakshmi, Rajababu and Papayamma were admitted to a private hospital in Narsipatnam while Kumari, Anusha, and Nookaratnam were shifted to King George Hospital in Visakhapatnam.Visakhapatnam: A five-year-old boy, S Vishal Teja of Gokiri village in Koyyuru mandal, was killed and six others were injured when an auto-rickshaw in which they were travelling plunged into the valley at Teppala Mamidi in Alluri district on Wednesday.. .AdvertisementThe injured were Kudumula Venkata Lakshmi, Rata Raja Babu, Kudumula Kumari, Kudumula Papayamma, Kasipalli Anusha and Kasipalli Nookaratnam.According to sources, they were returning after participating in a religious programme. The auto-rickshawâ€™s driver lost control of the vehicle at a curve near Teppala Mamidi. Police sources said Venkat Lakshmi, Rajababu and Papayamma were admitted to a private hospital in Narsipatnam while Kumari, Anusha, and Nookaratnam were shifted to King George Hospital in Visakhapatnam.

Title: 2 Hyd students killed in Saudi road accident
content: Hyderabad: Two young students hailing from Hyderabad were killed in a road accident in Saudi Arabiaâ€™s Dammam on June 13. Another student suffered critical injuries when the car in which they were travelling rammed a tree near the Dammam governorâ€™s office. While Ibrahim Azhar, 16, died on the spot, his younger brother Ammar, 13, sustained injuries. The family hails from Hussaini Alam. Hasan Riaz, 18, also died on the spot. His family hails from Moti Galli in Charminar. Authorities, who rushed to the spot, had to cut the mangled car to extricate the bodies of the two youngsters. The families of the deceased reside in Dammam. The three teenagers were studying in the International Indian School in Dammam. The car, belonging to Azharâ€™s father, was said to have been driven by one of the students when it crashed into a roadside tree.tnn. .Advertisement

Title: 3 killed in two separate road mishaps in Kharar
content: Mohali: Three persons were killed in two separate road accidents in Mohali in the past 24 hours. In the first mishap, two were killed when a car in which they were travelling collided head-on with a truck. In another accident, one person was killed when the motorcycle he was riding pillion slipped while negotiating a turn. Both the accidents occurred in Kharar on Wednesday.  According to the investigating officer ASI Sanjay Kumar, in the first incident, six persons were travelling in a taxi bearing a Punjab number and were returning from Mohali after having dinner at around 2:30 am. They all were natives of Jalalabad and were operating as taxi drivers in Mohali. When they reached near a crossing in Mohali, a recklessly driven truck driver coming from Ropar side hit the taxi from the front. The impact was so hard that the front portion of the Swift Dzire got completely smashed. The driver of the taxi, identified as Lakhwinder Singh, died on the way to GMCH-32 whereas Harpreet Singh died on the spot, said the IO. The other three, identified as Rajan Singh, Rajan Kumar and Harjinder Singh, have suffered serious injuries and are battling for their lives at GMCH-32 and the fourth person, identified as Harmesh Singh, is out of danger. The IO said that police have registered a FIR on the statement of Harmesh against the unidentified truck driver, who fled the spot leaving the truck behind. Police have impounded the truck bearing a Solan registration number. . .AdvertisementIn the second incident, a pillion rider of a motorcycle got killed and the driver sustained major head injuries when the motorcycle they were riding skidded off the road while negotiating a turn at Khanpur crossing. They both were reportedly not wearing helmets and suffered head injuries, said IO ASI Sajay Kumar. The deceased has been identified as Rahul (21) riding pillion and the rider of the motorcycle is identified as Sanjay (21). They were coming from Mohali side after having food at around 5:30 am on Wednesday.  In Zirakpur, another person succumbed to his injuries after being hit by an unidentified four-wheeler. The deceased has been identified as Ramesh Kumar (45), who was hit by the vehicle on June 10. He was rushed to GMCH-32 but was further referred to PGI, where he died during treatment on June 14.

Title: Six of family killed in Balangir NH accident
content: BALANGIR: Six members of a family, including two minor girls, were killed and another was critically injured after the car in which they were travelling collided with a parked truck on National Highway-26 at Chuinbandh in Balangir district on Tuesday night.The deceased were identified as Atmaj Naik (24), his sister Ipsita Naik (26), her five-year-old daughter Srinika Sai, Naik's maternal uncle Diptiranjan Sai (41), aunt Rasmita (35) and their daughter Aniva (5). Atmaj's mother Arati is undergoing treatment at VSS Institute of Medical Sciences and Research (Vimsar), Burla.Police said the accident occurred around 10.30 pm when the seven, all residents of Ramjeepada under Balangir Municipality area, were returning home after attending a marriage reception in Agalpur under Duduka block. Two died on the spot and three others were declared dead in hospital. "The accident might have been caused due to the driver being unable to spot the parked truck. We are investigating the case," said Tophan Bag, SDPO, Balangir.. .AdvertisementPolice and fire service personnel rescued the victims and rushed them to Bhima Bhoi Medical College and Hospital (BBMCH) for immediate treatment, he added. Two critically injured persons were shifted to Vimsar but later one of them succumbed to injuries. â€œThe driver probably failed to notice the truck as he was blinded by the lights of vehicles coming from the opposite direction resulting in the collision,â€ said a relative. A pall of gloom spread in the locality after the news of six deaths of a family started circulating on various social media platforms. Locals blamed the district administration for the delay in construction of a bypass road due to which frequent accidents are occurring leading to loss of lives.

Title: 2 die, 5 injured as bus rolls into ditch near Bhuntar
content: Kullu: Two persons were killed and five others injured after a Himachal Road Transportation Corporation (HRTC) bus rolled down a hill near Bhuntar in Kullu district on Wednesday evening.According to the state disaster management authority (SDMA), the accident occurred near Bashona village in Bhuntar tehsil of the district.According to information, there were seven people in the bus, including the driver and the conductor.An SDMA official said the five injured persons have been shifted to the regional hospital in Kullu. He said a fire team has been dispatched to the accident spot to cut open the bus to confirm no other persons are trapped beneath the vehicle.. .AdvertisementBhuntar police station SHO Naresh Sharma, who reached the spot, said the bus rolled down a hill at least 200 meters and turned upside down. He said the cause behind the accident was being investigated.The identity of the deceased and the injured was yet to be ascertained till the filing of this report.

Title: Two killed as teen rams car into bike
content: MUMBAI: The D B Marg police on Wednesday booked a 17-year-old boy and his father on charges of causing death by negligence after the teenager, who was driving his father's car, hit a bike killing two persons on Wednesday morning. The incident took place at Chowpatty near Ideal junction while the victims were returning to Grant Road from Marine Drive. The deceased were identifid as as Kiran Khan (35) and her friend Akbar Khan (47), both residents of Grant Road area. The car was coming from Parel. "The accident took place between 5.30 am and 6.30 am. The boy had taken his father's car and a friend of his was also in the vehicle. The car hit the bike at a high speed. The victims fell on the ground," an officer from DB Marg station said. "While Khan died on the spot, Kiran succumbed to her injuries in JJ Hospital," said the officer. The boy and his friend did not sustain any injury as the air bag had opened when the car hit the bike.

Title: Six killed in two road accidents
content: Chennai: Six people died in two separate road accidents at Melmaruvathur and Sholavaram on Thursday. Three members of a family, including a 4-year-old girl, died in a road accident on the Melmaruvathur-Vandavasi highway near Sendivakkam. Police identified the deceased as Sekar, 50, his wife Rani, 42, and the couple's granddaughter Akshaya. The accident took place when all three were returning home from a family function at Alapakkam on a bike. While they were nearing Sendivakkam, an unidentified vehicle rammed their bike and fled.All three fell off the bike and died. A case has been registered and investigation is on to identify the vehicle onwer who fled the accident spot.At Sholavaram, a milk truck rammed into a trailer resulting in the death of three people, including two guest workers, inside the truck. The three were identified as Rajendran, 43, the driver Ujjal, 25, and Manik Rai, 32. . .AdvertisementAround 12.30am on Thursday, the milk truck was headed from Tiruvallur to Red Hills. When the vehicle reached Gandhi Nagar near Sholavaram, it rammed into the trailer. Due to the impact, all three died on the spot. Based on information, the Red Hills Traffic Investigation Wing police recovered the bodies and sent them to Government Stanley Hospital for postmortem. A case has been registered and an investigation is on.

Title: Korba: 2 brothers killed, 1 hurt in road accident
content: Raipur: Two brothers were killed and another friend was severely injured when the bike they were riding on collided with a tractor near Bilaspur Katghora National Highway Dumarkachhar on Thursday morning under the Pali police station area of Korba district.Police officials said that the deceased brothers were identified as Ramu Markam and Vishnu Markam, residents of Dumarkacchar village. They had gone to Katghora with a friend from the same village on a same bike. While returning to the village, a speeding tractor collided with their bike, causing the trio to fall on the other side of the road.The accident was so severe that two of the three victims died on the spot, while the third sustained severe head injuries. Local witnesses of the accident gathered at the scene and immediately informed the police. The police reached the site of the accident.The injured individuals were rushed to the nearby government hospital for treatment, and the bodies of the two deceased were sent for postmortem examination. Meanwhile, the tractor driver was taken into custody, and a report was filed against him. The tractor was seized and the investigation is underway, the police said.. .Advertisement

Title: Three tribals killed in road accident in Andhra Pradesh
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: Three tribals were killed in a road accident that took place in Alluri Sitharama Raju district of andhra Pradesh. This incident took place in matam Bhimavaram in Koyyuru mandal in ASR district. About 32 tribals were returning from the weekly shandy that took place at Pothavaram in the same mandal. As many as 32 persons have boarded a bolero vehicle and met with an accident when they reached Bhimavaram village. The iron rod connecting the engine and the body of the vehicle was broken and the body of the vehicle parted way with the engine. Three persons were killed on the spot and another 23 persons including children were injured in the accident. . .AdvertisementSeven persons who sustained severe injuries were shifted to Kakinada government General Hospital and 12 injured were admitted in GGH, Rajahmundry. The Koyyuru police led by Inspector Allu Swamy Naidu had to toil till 2 a.m on Friday shift the injured to the hospital and establish the identity of the deceased in the Pitch dark conditions.Golluri Lysone(60) belongs to Dabbikota village of M Bhimavaram Panchayat, Kakuru Muthyalamma(55) belongs to Chidikota village of M Bhimavaram Panchayat and Kuthuru Muthyalamma (35) belongs to Veedhi Lanka village of Pathakota mnadal in Y Ramavaram mandal were killed in the accident.

Title: 2 youngsters killed in Ongole road accident
content: Vijayawada: Two youngsters died on the spot in an accident at Maddipadu of Ongole on Friday early morning. The deceased, identified as M Shiva Teja (21) and G Ravi Teja (22) of Vijayawada, had gone to Ongole on a bike to attend their friendâ€™s birthday party. According to police, the incident took place on the NH at Gundlapalli when they were returning home. They hit the divider at Mahi Agro Factory near Gundlapalli. Both of them died on the spot due to severe head injuries. TNN

Title: 3 killed, 7 injured in S Assam accidents
content: Silchar: At least three persons, including one woman were killed and seven persons injured in separate road accidents in Karimganj, Cachar and Dima Hasao districts of southern Assam over the past 24 hours.Sources said one person died and five others were seriously injured in a collision between two motorbikes on NH-8 connecting Assam-Tripura at Cheragi in Karimganj district late on Thursday night. Muzakir Hossain (20), a resident of Patharkandi, died in the accident. Among the seriously injured are two bikers, three pillion riders of the two motorbikes. The condition of two of them is said to be critical. The injured, all in their teens, have been identified as Dipan Roy, Shyamal Roy, Shubham Sinha, Abdul Hannan and Ala Hossain. Reports from Haflong said amid inclement weather a youth was killed when an uprooted tree fell on the motorbike which he was riding. Two pillion riders were critically injured in the accident which happened on Friday at Diungbra in Dima Hasao district. The victims are yet to be identified. An unidentified woman died in a road accident at Natun Bazar on NH-306.

Title: Three tribals die in road accident
content: RAJAHMUNDRY: Three tribals were killed when the SUV in which they were travelling broke down at Matam Bhimavaram in Alluri Sitharama Raju district on Friday.The victims were Golluri Lysone (60), Kakuru Muthyalamma (55) and Kuthuru Muthyalamma (35).The iron rod connecting the engine and the body of the vehicle was broken and body of the vehicle parted way with the engine.According to police, 23 people including children were injured in the accident. Seven persons who sustained severe injuries were shifted to Kakinada Government General Hospital and 12 injured were admitted in GGH, Rajahmundry.. .Advertisement

Title: Four of family killed in Assam accident
content: Dibrugarh: Four members of a family, including two women, died while another member was severely injured when the car they were travelling in skidded off NH-315 and crashed into a tree in Assamâ€™s Tinsukia district early Friday.The family was on its way back home to Ledo in Tinsukia district after attending a relativeâ€™s wedding reception in Dibrugarh when the accident took place around 12.45am near the Indian Oil Corporation oil terminal at Golai under Digboi police station. The lone survivor, Ratan Gope (33), was admitted to Assam Medical College and Hospital. Ratanâ€™s wife Disha (30), mother Sadhana (57), elder brother Subhash (36) and brother-in-law Amrit Dutta (27) â€” who was at the wheel of the newly-purchased car â€” died in the mishap.â€œI was awakened by a loud sound around 12.45am and rushed out to the road. It was pitch dark and not a person was in sight. When I looked around with my torchlight, I found a car a had crashed into a tree beside the road. A woman was lying dead in the front seat. I wasnâ€™t sure about the others. I immediately stopped some passing vehicles and with their help, informed the police who arrived at the accident site within 20 minutes,â€ said a resident of the area.Police said the driver and one of the women died on the spot, while two others succumbed at the hospital.. .Advertisement

Title: 2 killed as AAP legislator's escort vehicle hits bike in Faridkot, protest follows
content: BATHINDA: An escort vehicle attached with AAP MLA Gurdit Singh Sekhon hit a motorcycle in Faridkot on Friday, killing the two men riding it and triggering a protest by locals. The accident took place near Sadiq village of Faridkot, on the road from the Faridkot town to Sadiq.Satpal Singh (52) and Nachhatar Singh (54), who were from Jhotiwala village of Faridkot, were going to their village when the Punjab Police Gypsy, which was leading the minister's cavalcade, hit the two-wheeler from behind. On coming to know of the accident, the residents of Jhotiwala assembled at Faridkot and held a protest outside the Faridkot police station by placing the bodies in front of it. They alleged that the escort vehicle was speeding and the occupants did not even take the victims to the hospital. They alleged that the escort vehicle instead sped away from the crime scene after the accident. Later, Gurdit Singh, who is the MLA from Faridkot, reached the protest site and said when the accident took place, he was not present. Police are looking into the matter and have been asked to conduct an investigation without any bias, he said.. .AdvertisementFaridkot City police station SHO Amrinder Singh said that a case under Section 304A (causing death by negligence), 279 (rash driving) and 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) of the Indian Penal Code had been registered against escort vehicle's driver, Angrej Singh.

Title: 5 killed in road accident in Konaseema
content: Vijayawada: Five persons died on the spot and eight others received severe injuries in a road accident in the early hours of Saturday at Madiki village of Alamuru mandal in Konaseema district. The dead were identified as Bodi Venkataramana (55), Pathiwada Kannayya Lingam (55), Gangiredla Srinu (35), Vegnesa Subrahmanyam (45), and Bodi Venki Naidu (45).The van from Rampachodavaram with 10 devotees for darshan of Shaniswaraswamy in Mandavalli temple, collided head-on with a car carrying three persons to Bhimavaram from Visakhapatnam airport at Madiki. According to the police, the van was speeding. Three passengers in the van and two occupants of the car were killed on the spot. TNN

Title: Four youngsters killed in separate road accidents
content: Kanpur: Four youngsters were killed in separate road accidents in the district late Friday night. Two coaching students were killed when a speeding truck hit their bike on which they were riding at Patara area in Ghatampur on Saturday morning. Both died on the spot.Police have sent the bodies for postmortem. The truck driver who was fleeing after the accident was caught by passers-by and handed over to the police.The police identified the deceased as Abhishek of Patara (18) and Ankush (22) of Raipur village of Ghatampur. â€œDriver has been arrested and booked for rash driving, police have impounded the truck involved in the accident,â€ said ACP Ghatampur Dinesh Kumar Shukla.Elsewhere, two youngsters were killed after their bike rammed into a stationary tanker in Nawabganj police area of the city on late Friday night. The deceased were identified as Golu (20) and his cousin Akash (21) of Makdi Kheda. The accident occurred at a spot in between Makdi Kheda and Bharatpur village when their bike rammed into a tanker that was parked by the road side. The two youths were returning from a family function when the accident took place. The bodies have been sent for postmortem examination, said incharge Nawabganj police station.. .Advertisement

Title: Del woman, grandson killed in road accident
content: Mohali: A woman and her grandson were killed in a collision between a pick-up vehicle and a car near the light point of Chatt in the early hours of Saturday. The victims are Kuldeep Kaur and her grandson Gurkirat Singh, both from Delhi. Narinder Singh, a resident of Delhi, said he had visited his native place in Ropar along with his wife, daughter-in-law and grandson around 20 days ago. On Saturday morning, Narinder Singh was returning to Delhi with his family members in a car. As they reached near the light point of Chatt , a speeding Mahindra pick-up allegedly hit their car.Man stops to take a leak, car snatchedThree people in a car allegedly snatched a car from a Himachal Pradesh resident who had stopped to answer a call of nature in Pinjore on Friday night.. .AdvertisementHarvinder Singh, a resident of Solan district, Himachal Pradesh, said he had gone for some work to Rishikesh, Uttarakhand in his Maruti Suzuki Swift Dzire.When he was returning home, he stopped near Ramnagar Kholi village at 11.30 pm. He alleged when he was answering a call of nature, three men snatched his car keys from his pocket and fled in his vehicle. The police have registered a case.

Title: Three killed in Tinsukia road accident
content: Dibrugarh: Three men were killed after their bike on which they were travelling collided head-on with a car in the Kakopathar area of Tinsukia district on Saturday night. One person was killed on the spot, while the other two died while undergoing treatment at the Kakopathar civil hospital. The deceased have been identified as Manash Jyoti Buragohain (31), Rupam Chutia (30) and Santanu Phukan (30) â€” all residents of Uparuban village. While Manash worked at a private firm in Guwahati, Rupam was employed at a private company in Chennai. Both of them had come back home only two days ago. . .AdvertisementPolice said the bodies of the three youths were handed over to their families after the post-mortem examination.

Title: 71-yr-old killed, 8 hurt in JNPT crash
content: NAVI MUMBAI: A 71-year-old man was killed and eight others were injured after an MUV lost control on the Panvel-JNPT road, crashing into the median and landing on the opposite carriageway where it rammed into a car on Saturday around 5.45pm. "MGM hospital, Kamothe, informed the Panvel city police about the medico-legal case as the accident victims were admitted there. Six persons were heading towards Jejuri temple in Pune in a Brezza car. Around 5.45pm, when their car reached near Gavhan Phata, a speeding Innova car travelling from Panvel towards JNPT crashed the road median and rammed into the car. The impact of the crash proved fatal for a senior citizen seated in the Brezza car. The deceased has been identified as Narayan Thote. The Innova car driver (17), a Nerul resident, has also suffered severe injuries in the crash," said Dilip Shinde, police inspector (admin) of Panvel city police station.The accused Innova car driver has been booked under relevant sections of the IPC and Motor Vehicles Act for rash and negligent driving causing death.

Title: Sub-inspector loses control, dies after car rams into tree
content: WARANGAL: Two persons, including a sub-inspector (SI), died in separate road accidents in Warangal on Sunday. In the first incident, District Crime Records Branch (DCRB) SI Soma Kumara Swmay (56), a native of Anantharam village in Geesugonda mandal, died in a road accident on Sunday afternoon. According to Geesugonda SI S Raju, Soma Kumara Swamy, working as an SI in Bhadradri-Kothagudem district, was on his way to his native village in a car. As he was heading towards Warangal, he lost control over the wheel and rammed the vehicle into a tree near Azara Thanda. He suffered serious injuries and was shifted to MGM Hospital for treatment. . .AdvertisementHe succumbed while undergoing treatment. A case was registered under Section 304A (causing death by rash or negligent act) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) and the body has been shifted to MGM Hospital for a post-mortem examination. In another incident, a 52- year-old woman was killed in a road accident in Nakalaguttta, Hanumakonda on Sunday morning. The deceased was identified as M Jaya, aresident of Balasamudram. According to Subedari SI Ch Raju, Jaya and her husband Rajeshwar Rao were travelling on a two-wheeler towards Kaloji Centre when a trolley rear-ended their vehicle, resulting in the accident. Jaya died on the spot. Rajeshwar Rao, who suffered severe injuries was shifted to MGM Hospital for treatment. Acase was registered against driver under sections 304-A and 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) of IPC.

Title: Cinematographer Navroze Contractor dies in Hosur bike crash
content: BENGALURU: Veteran cinematographer, biker and motorsport photographer Navroze Contractor, 80, was killed in a bike accident near Hosur on Sunday morning. He had gone for a ride on his sportsbike with fellow riders and was returning to Bengaluru when the accident took place. Contractor, a resident of Koramangala, was riding his Kawasaki Ninja motorcycle in the morning with his friends, a routine he followed for most Sundays over the years. "They were all riding back to Bengaluru and were on a service road connecting to the highway near Hosur. Three other bikers were on the wrong side of the road and one of them crashed into his motorcycle, killing him on the spot. An active crusader of safe riding through his career, the veteran biker's life had to sadly end by an unruly motorist," an automobile enthusiast said. . .AdvertisementHis wife Deepa Dhanraj is a noted documentary filmmaker. A famous name in Indian motorcycle riding and automotive circles, the Bengalurean had travelled all over the world, including Europe, West Asia and the length and breadth of India in the past five decades. "He was the most accomplished man in the country in the motorcycle scene. He rode thrice from India to England in the 1960s and his trip to the Himalayas on his 1956-model BSA Goldstar in 1974 is legendary," recalled Adil Jal Darukhanawala, a senior automobile historian and an author.Contractor was also a professional photographer, and covered Grand Prix races in Europe in 1960s and 1970s. An alumnus of Faculty of Fine Arts, Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda, he was also a documentary filmmaker. He was the acclaimed cinematographer of films like Framed, Duvidha, Lalach and Pehla Adhyay.

Title: 2 killed in separate road accidents
content: Rajkot: Two persons died in separate hit-and-run accidents in 24 hours ending on Monday in Rajkot. Aji Dam and Thorala police have booked unidentified persons under Indian Penal Code (IPC) sections 337, 304 A and the Motor Vehicle Act.A 40-year-old man who was crossing the road was hit by an unidentified vehicle on the national highway between Aji Dam crossroads and Greenland Chowkdi. He died due to severe head injuries.In another accident, a 75-year-old man Nana Langariya died after he was hit by a car on 80-feet Road on June 18. He succumbed to the injuries in the hospital. tnn

Title: Woman killed as speeding car rams into pole
content: Hyderabad: A 22-year-old woman died in a road accident at Peddemul in Vikarabad district on Sunday. According to Peddemul police, nine members were heading to Bidar, Karnataka, when the car they were travelling in hit a pole and over turned. The victim, Soundarya and others who suffered injuries were shifted to a local hospital, however the former succumbed to her injuries. â€œAll the passengers in the vehicle, work in the same company and had visited a temple in Vikarabad and were heading to Bidar. Overspeeding led the driver to lose control of the car and crashed into a road side pole,â€ sub-inspector of police Charan said. A criminal case was registered against the driver, Kushal, under IPC Sec 304 (A) (negligence causing death). tnn

Title: Man, daughter killed in accident near Koba
content: Ahmedabad: A 42-year-old man from Koba and his 17-year-old daughter died after a speeding SUV hit their scooter near the Gujarat BJP headquarters, Kamalam, on Monday morning.Bhaskar Parekh, a resident of Mahaveer Hills in Koba, was riding his scooter with his daughter Zarna, 17, and son Jiyan, 6, riding pillion. Bhaskar was going to drop Zarna and Jiyan off at their school when the accident took place.â€œA speeding SUV hit Bhaskarâ€™s scooter when he was moving near Kamalam. Bhaskar died on the spot while Zarna died at Gandhinagar Civil Hospital. Jiyan suffered less severe injuries,â€ states the FIR filed at Infocity police station.Parekhâ€™s neighbour, Vipul Adhyaru, was also going to drop his children off at school when he saw a crowd near Kamalam. Adhyaru said he saw that his neighbour Bhaskar and his children had been injured in the accident.. .AdvertisementAdhyaru said Shubh Patel, 19, a resident of Kalol, was driving the SUV that hit Bhaskar and his children.Bhaskarâ€™s sister, Tejal Parekh, is the BJPâ€™s whip in the Gandhinagar Municipal Corporation and her husband, Yogesh Nayi, is a former sarpanch of Koba village. Inspector L D Odedara of Infocity police said Patelâ€™s father had bought him the car recently. He was driving it at speed and in a negligent manner, which had led to the accident. Patel had two friends with him in the car. Members of the public blocked the road in front of Kamalam after the accident for around an hour and police personnel deployed at the BJP headquarters had to get them to disperse.Infocity police registered a case of causing death by negligent driving and rash driving under the IPC against Patel and began an investigation.

Title: Man, 21, killed as bus hits two-wheeler in Madurai
content: Madurai: A 21-year-old man died and a teenager suffered injuries when a private bus hit a two-wheeler they were travelling on near Andalpuram (Thevar) bridge in Madurai city on Sunday night.The deceased has been identified as R Rogan of Palanganatham. On Sunday night, he was riding a two-wheeler with a pillion rider. A private bus moving in the same direction hit the two-wheeler. Rogan died on the spot. Pillion rider K Radhakrishnan, 17, who suffered injuries was rushed to the Government Rajaji Hospital.The traffic investigation wing - I of the Madurai city police booked the driver of the private bus, K Kathirvel, 47, of Palanganatham, under various sections of the IPC.Following the accident, the traffic police on Monday erected barricades in and around the slope of the Andalpuram bridge and two service roads in bid to prevent overspeeding and wrong side driving. . .Advertisementâ€œWe found that many, especially motorists, are crossing or overtaking near the slope of the bridge dangerously instead of going further and taking a U-turn. Though traffic cones were placed, it is not being heeded to. Hence, we are placing metal barricades,â€ said a police officer. Police urged the public to adhere to taking the U-turn at the appropriate spot.The Andalpuram bridge slope which connects to TPK Road is a busy road with active vehicle movement throughout the day. There are also a few colleges nearby with students on two-wheelers frequenting the stretch. â€œMany vehicles often cross this spot at high speeds while also ignoring traffic rules, including on the service roads. Pedestrians are also at risk. Traffic police personnel should be posted on a daily basis to monitor it,â€ said J Senthil Kumar, a commuter.

Title: 2 killed, 4 injured in road accident on Yamuna Expressway
content: MATHURA (UTTAR PRADESH): Two people were killed while four others were injured after a speeding car crashed into another stationary car on the Yamuna Expressway in UPâ€™s Mathura.The deceased were identified as 70-year-old Yogendra and 32-year-old Pooja, both residents of Delhi.Police said on Wednesday the accident took place near milestone 118 under Mahavan police station limits of Mathura district.According to the police, a car was parked on the side of the expressway after getting punctured.. .AdvertisementMeanwhile, a speeding car heading from Agra to Noida rammed into the parked car from behind.At the time of the accident, two of the victims were said to be engaged in fixing the flat tyre and died in the collision. while the four people boarded in the second car were left injured and taken to a private hospital.Police are investigating the matter.With ANI inputs

Title: Woman, 40, bizman, 52, killed in 2 accidents
content: RUDRAPUR: Two persons, a woman from Haldwani and a businessman from Dineshpur town were killed in separate accidents in Udham Singh Nagar on Tuesday.The first incident occurred at Tanda Road. A family which was on its way from Malla Gorakhpur, Haldwani to Delhi was hit by a private bus from behind. A woman in the car, 40-year-old Manpreet Kaur, died of injuries. Her husband Tajinder Singh, their son Prabhjot Singh and a friend, Sukhveer Singh, who was behind the wheel, were all injured and shifted to JL Nehru district hospital in Rudrapur. The second incident happened in Rudrapur, when Suresh Kumar, a 52-year-old grocery store owner from Dineshpur was killed when the bike he was riding, with his daughter riding pillion, was hit, head-on, by a car. . .Advertisement

Title: Guard killed as car hits bike in Mohali
content: Mohali: A resident of Chandigarhâ€™s Sector 45 was killed after his motorcycle was hit by a car on the road dividing phases III and VII. Ram Kapoor worked as a security guard in a cloth showroom in Phase 3B2. His wife said she and her son were following him in a Honda Activa as he had left behind his lunch box. She said when they reached the road dividing phases III and VII, she saw a speeding car hit her husband. He was taken to civil hospital, where doctors declared him brought dead. Police registered a case.Car overturns, close shave for 4 in MorniIn Panchkula, four people, including a woman, had a narrow escape after their car overturned while negotiating a curve close to Tikkar Tal in Morni. The accident was caught on a CCTV camera installed at a local shop. Two persons fell off the vehicle. All the victims were taken to civil hospital, Sector 6. Doctors said they received minor injuries. The car was removed with the help of a crane.

Title: 2 bikers killed after speeding car jumps divider, rams them
content: Nashik: Two bikers moving in the direction of the Trimbak area from Nashik were killed when they were hit by a speeding car that jumped over a divider at the spot and knocked them down on Monday night.The accident took place near the Sanskruti restaurant on Nashik-Trimbak Road, around 8.30pm. The deceased persons have been identified as Sunil Mahale (31), a resident of Wagholi in Trimbakeshwar taluka, and Namdev Shid (36), a resident of Rajewadi in Nashik taluka. The car driver also suffered from some injuries in the accident and is being treated in a private hospital. Mahale and Shid were proceeding towards Trimbakeshwar on their respective bikes, riding almost parallel at the time of the mishap. At this point, the speeding car came from the opposite direction and jumped over the road divider. It knocked them down before coming to a screeching halt. . .AdvertisementBystander Nandu Kashinath Shinde of Rajewadi, who rushed Shid to the Nashik civil hospital, said he was on his way to work towards Satpur when he saw the rush on the other side of the road. He then came upon Shid, who is known to him, lying on the road. â€œI immediately took him to the civil hospital, where he was declared dead around 9.15pm. Shid used to travel from Satpur to Rajewadi on a daily basis,â€ said Shinde. The Satpur police said they have seized the speeding car driverâ€™s vehicle and will be getting it checked by experts from the Regional Transport Office (RTO).The car driver has claimed that his vehicle jumped over the divider as a tyre burst. However, his claim will be verified, said the police. â€œAn FIR is being registered against the car driver,â€ said inspector Pankaj Bhalerao of the Satpur police.

Title: 2 bikers killed after speeding car jumps divider, rams them
