Title: 5 of family die in hit-and-run
content: JIND: Five members of a family, including three children, were killed after the bike they were travelling on was hit by a truck on Safidon road near Nirjan village of Jind district on Tuesday. The victims have been identified as Rakesh, 42, his wife Kavita, daughter Kiran, 12, and sons Amit, 10, and Arman, 5. The sixth member of the family, Seerat, 8, who was also riding with them, is the lone survivor and undergoing treatment at a hospital, where doctors termed her condition stable. The family was on its way to Kharkara village in Hisarâ€™s Barwala from Rasoolpur village in Panipat after attending death rituals of Kavitaâ€™s father. While three family members died on the spot, two succumbed to their injuries at the Jind civil hospital. As per the police, the accident took place near Jind city, where the bike had reached after passing through several check-posts. The unidentified truck driver managed to flee from the spot, said the police who have registered a first information report (FIR) against him. â€œThe only survivor of the accident is out of danger,â€ said Jindâ€™s Civil Lines station house officer Diwan Singh. . .AdvertisementMeanwhile, investigating officer Krishan Kumar, who had reached the accident spot after getting the information, claimed that they had tried to contact the helpline issued by the health department to transport the bodies to hospital, but the responder said the ambulance service was not meant for the dead. He alleged that the non-cooperation caused significant delay in ferrying the bodies.

Title: Three of a family among six die in three accidents in Bihar
content: PATNA: Six persons, including three of a family, died in separate accidents on Monday. An autorickshaw rammed into a standing container truck, killing three of a family and injuring three others in Muzaffarpur district. The incident occurred near Murarpur Chowk when the family was returning from Muzaffarpur to Sahebganj. In Khagaria, police constable Ayush Kumar and his friend Mukesh Kumar of Gangapur died when their bike collided with an autorickshaw on the NH-31. A 16-year-old motorcyclist died in an accident on Khagaria-Alouli road. tnn

Title: Assam: Two killed in Tinsukia road accident
content: DIBRUGARH: Two people were killed in a road accident, near the Dhola-Sadiya bridge, in Tinsukia on Wednesday. The incident occurred when the car, which was carrying four passengers, was hit by a dumper truck. The impact of the collision was so severe that the rear portion of the car was completely crushed.Two persons, identified as Sukalyan Baruah (22) and Ripunjoy Phukan (28), died on the spot, while Ajonto Baruah and Ripun Sonowal sustained severe injuries and were rushed to a nearby hospital by the police. They were later shifted to a private hospital in Dibrugarh. All the four belonged to Charaideo district.State revenue minister and Mahmora MLA Jogen Mohan visited the injured at the hospital. Police said the accident occurred due to over speeding and slippery condition of the road, owing to heavy rains.. .Advertisement

Title: Nine killed, two injured as car falls into ditch in Uttarakhand's Pithoragarh district
content: NEW DELHI: At least nine people died and two seriously injured after a van fell into a ditch in Uttarakhand, on Thursday.The incident took place around 9am on Nachani-Hokra road in Munisyari block of Pithoragarh district.The SDRF and police team started rescue work and recovered five bodies, said disaster management office.The van was heading from Sama village in Bageswar district to Hokra temple for worship.. .AdvertisementThe rescue operation is still in progress.

Title: Truck driver dies in accident in Trichy
content: Trichy: A 29-year-old container truck driver was killed after his vehicle got sandwiched between a truck and a TNSTC bus on Trichy â€“ Chennai bypass road on Tuesday. Police identified the deceased as U Pandi, of Thiruppalai in Madurai. â€œPandi and his assistant Manikandan were heading towards Chennai in the truck. When they were near Ora Steel Company on Trichy â€“ Chennai bypass road, the driver of a truck moving in front of them applied the brake suddenly. Pandi was unable to stop his vehicle in time and it crashed into the truck. A TNSTC bus following them also hit the container truck from behind,â€ said a police officer attached with the traffic north investigation wing of the Trichy city police. â€œThe windshield of the container truck was broken and Pandi fell out of it. He suffered injuries on his chest and died on the spot. The driver of the TNSTC bus sustained injuries on his left eye and was admitted to a private hospital in Trichy.â€. .AdvertisementThe driver of other truck abandoned his vehicle and escaped from the spot.Police registered a case based on a complaint from Manikandan, 29, a native of Keela Panangadi in Madurai.

Title: Five of family killed in road accident in Shahjahanpur
content: SHAHJAHANPUR (UTTAR PRADESH): Five people, including two children, were killed in a road accident near Dilawarpur village under South Sehramau police station in Uttar Pradesh's Shahjahanpur district on Friday.All five people belonging to the same family â€” consisting of two women, two children, and one man â€” were riding a bike when they met with an accident and died on the spot.According to the police, the family was returning to their home in Jaitipur from a wedding ceremony in Shahjahanpur. The bike was hit on Hardoi Road near Dilawarpur Village, and the entire family instantly died due to the impacts of the collision.. .AdvertisementSouth Sehramau police reached the site. The bodies were sent off for postmortem. With ANI inputs

Title: Youth killed in bike accident
content: Thiruvananthapuram: A Kanyakumari-based youth died and his neighbour sustained severe injuries after the two-wheeler on which they were travelling collided head on with a school bus at Vellarada on Wednesday evening.The deceased was identified by police as Vijin, 23, of Kulavila Mankodu in Kanyakumari, and his neighbour Adheesh, 20, is undergoing treatment at the Medical College Hospital.They had come to Kovilloor at Vellarada for work at a shop and the accident took place while they were on their way back home. Adheesh was riding the two-wheeler on which Vijin was riding pillion. When the vehicle reached Kalunkunada, a school bus which was coming from Panachamoodu, collided head on with it. Both were thrown to a drain beside the road, and Vijin sustained severe head injuries. They were initially taken to Karakonam medical college hospital. After their condition turned worse, they were referred to Thiruvananthapuram Medical College Hospital, where Vijin succumbed to the injuries.. .AdvertisementVellarada police have registered a case against the driver of the school bus under section 304-A for causing death by negligence and 279 for rash driving. â€œThe accused is absconding and we have intensified the search for him. The condition of Adheesh is stable now,â€ police said.Vijinâ€™s body has been sent to relatives after postmortem and inquest procedures at the hospital.

Title: One killed, 18 injured as ST bus collides with truck in Nashik
content: NASHIK: A man died and 18 people were injured when a Pune-bound MSRTC bus collided with a truck at Peth Road in this north Maharashtra district around 4.30pm on Thursday.A Mhasul police officer said the driver of the truck carrying cement mix was taken to the Nashik civil hospital, where doctors pronounced him dead on arrival. The officer said the bus from Pune collided with the truck coming from Nashik in an area near Ashewadi village. The driversâ€™ cabins of both the vehicles were badly damaged.The police said 57 passengers were on board the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC) bus. An MSRTC official said 14 passengers and four crew members of the bus were injured. The official added that efforts were on to find if any other passenger was injured in the accident and visited other hospitals for treatment. The official added that another bus was arranged for the onward journey of the passengers who were not injured.. .Advertisement

Title: Man killed after vehicle hits tractor on Indore-Betma highway
content: INDORE: A 28-year-old man, who was driving a new tractor to deliver it to a showroom, was killed after his vehicle was hit by a speeding pickup vehicle on Thursday morning.The police said that the accident took place on Indore-Betma highway when Lokesh, a resident of Hatpipliya, was driving the tractor from a dhabha towards the road. Lokesh along with his brother Ram used to take vehicles from one place to another for an automobile company. On Thursday, they were on their way from Kshipra to Rajgarh to deliver two tractors at a showroom when they stopped at a dhabha in the morning to have tea. When they returned to their tractors and were about to leave, a pickup vehicle rammed into a tractor because of which it overturned injuring Lokesh seriously. He was rushed to a hospital but succumbed to injuries.

Title: Man killed in accident while trying to evade stray dog
content: Kochi: A 21-year-old died after his bike overturned reportedly while trying to avoid hitting a stray dog along the Container Road at Kothad in Kochi around 8.15am on Friday.Police said the youth, Salton of Moolampilly, fell on the road and was run over by a concrete mixer truck.The incident occurred when Salton was heading to his workplace at Kalamassery. Varapuzha police have registered a case.Police said that Salton was not speeding .. .Advertisementâ€œThe number of stray dogs in the area has increased rapidly. Children going on cycles are being chased by dogs. Many have stopped going on morning walks in fear of animal attacks,â€ said member of Kadamakudy panchayat for Moolampilly West Sajini Jyothish.

Title: 4-year-old killed, 3 injured as speeding car rams bike
content: Ludhiana: A 4-year-old child was killed while his parents and sibling suffered injuries after the motorcycle they were riding was allegedly hit by a car in Khanna. The deceased was identified as Bipanjot Singh of Attapur village in Sirhind. Bipanjotâ€™s father Uttam Deep Singh along with his wife, two children had gone to Machiwara on a motorcycle to meet his in-laws. While returning, a speeding car hit them from behind on the bridge near the bus stand in Khanna. All of them fell on the other side of the road. Uttam Deep regained consciousness after 2 minutes and found that his wife and two children were lying on the road injured. He said the passersby took them to the government hospital in an ambulance. . .AdvertisementBipanjot died during treatment at the hospital. Sources said the car driver, who probably belonged to Panipat in Haryana, was stopped by persons in another car and was handed over to police. Dr Akash Goyal, posted on emergency duty at the government hospital in Khanna, said four persons were injured in the accident and the condition of a child was very serious. Efforts were made to save him, but they could not succeed, he added. Police officials said after recording the statement of injured persons, necessary action will be taken in the matter.

Title: 5 of family killed in bike crash, toddler survives
content: MEERUT: Five members of a family of six were killed on Friday in an accident near Dilawarpur village on the Hardoi-Shahjahanpur highway. An 18-month-old girl survived the incident.According to police, all six family members were on a bike and were returning from a wedding at a nearby village around 5 am when an unidentified vehicle hit their two-wheeler from behind and sped away.The deceased have been identified as Raghuveer Singh (25), his wife Jyoti (25), their two sons, aged 5 and 3, and Raghuveer's sister-in-law Julie Devi. Julie's 18-month-old daughter, Aradhya, suffered minor injuries and was promptly admitted to a nearby government hospital. Eyewitnesses said the bodies were got scattered all across the road after the accident, and blocked the road, resulting in a traffic jam.

Title: Five killed, 43 injured in road accident in Tamil Nadu's Trichy
content: TRICHY: Five people were killed and 43 persons were injured in a road accident involving a mofussil bus of Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) and a car on the Trichy-Dindigul national highway (NH) near Manapparai in Trichy district on Sunday evening. All the deceased were the occupants of the car.Police said that the front tyre of a hatchback car moving towards Trichy on Dindigul â€“ Trichy NH suffered a burst while approaching the Kalkothanur village near Manapparai town. The sudden impact caused the speeding car to deviate from the carriageway and speed towards the opposite lanes of the highway even after crossing the highwayâ€™s centre median. The car rammed a mofussil bus of TNSTC heading to Dindigul on the Trichy-Dindigul lane of the national highway. Although the bus driver attempted to avoid hitting the car approaching from the opposite direction, the car rammed the bus. . .AdvertisementIn the impact, the bus driver lost control, and both the TNSTC bus and the hatchback car turned turtle on the vacant space adjoining the highway. In the impact, all five occupants of the car identified as P Nagarathinam, 36, M Muthamilselvan, 48, G Manikandan, 25, R Ayyappan, 20, and S Dheenadayalan 20, were killed on the spot. The victims hailed from Trichy and Karur districts. The car was completely mangled in the collision, making it difficult for the police and locals to retrieve the bodies of the deceased occupants. The bodies of the deceased were moved to the Manapparai government hospital for autopsy. Police said that at least 43 passengers of the TNSTC bus sustained injuries. While 19 of the injured passengers of the bus were treated as outpatients in the Manapparai government hospital, around 24 are undergoing treatment as inpatients in the government hospital and a few private hospitals in Manapparai town. The Vaiyampatti police have registered a case and investigating the accident.

Title: 21-yr-old rider killed as cattle comes on road
content: Bhopal: A 21-year-old man was killed after he lost control of his two-wheeler due to sudden appearance of stray cattle on the road in Ratibad locality. Investigation officer SI Gaurav Pandey said that the man, Deepak Lodhi, a resident of Lalitpur, was heading towards Bhadbhada when the accident took place. When he reached Mendora village on Kerwa Dam Road suddenly a buffalo came in front of his two-wheeler. So, he immediately took a turn and lost control of his vehicle. The vehicle skidded on the road and Deepak fell on a stone. He sustained grievous injury in his head and started bleeding profusely. Locals called an ambulance to rush him to hospital. But he died on way to hospital after tyre of the ambulance got punctured. tnn

Title: 2 killed as truck hits stationary vehicles in Satna
content: Bhopal: Two women were killed and two others were injured after a speeding truck hit a loading vehicle and a car, which were stationary, from rear end in Satna district on wee hours of Saturday.The accident took place at national highway 30 near Nadaan toll plaza under Amarpatan police station at around 5 am. One of the deceased has been identified as 45-yera-old Shashi Rai, while other woman who was also aged around 40 years is yet to be identified, said police.Amarpatan police station in charge Sandeep Bhartiya said, â€œA family was travelling in a car and one of the tyres of the car got punctured, they stopped and were trying to repair it. A loading vehicle which was behind them also stopped to help them. While they were trying to fix the puncture in the tyre two females, who were in the car, came out of the car and sat along the road side. A truck coming from the same direction hit both the vehicles from rear end. Both the women, who were sitting outside died on the spotâ€.Two others including the driver of the loading vehicle, who had stopped to help the car owners, had sustained severe injuries.. .Advertisement A case has been registered and detailed investigation is being carried out, he added.

Title: 4 of family killed as truck hits car
content: Hanumakonda: Four people of a family were killed and two others severely injured in a road accident on Sunday evening at the Patthipaka village crossroads under the Athmakur police station limits. A speeding truck hit an incoming car, resulting in four persons dying on the spot.The deceased were identified as Anumula Narsimha Swamy (50), Veldhendi Sambaraju (42), Veldhendi Akkansha (26), and Veldhendi Lakshmi Prasanna (6), all residents of Kashibugga in Warangal. The injured persons â€” identified as Anumula Rajasri(50) and Anumula Akshita(20) â€” where shifted to MGM Hospital Warangal for treatment.Speaking to the media, Athmakur inspector Bandari Kumar stated: â€œThe family went to Medaram, offered special prayers to Sammakka-Saralamma deities, and returned to Kashibugga of Warangal. While reaching the Patthipaka village crossroads on National Highway 163, a speeding truck hit the car. Four persons were killed on the spot, and two were injured. They were shifted to the MGM Hospital in Warangal for treatment.â€. .AdvertisementMinister E Dayakar Rao enquired about the incident with CP AV Ranganath and consoled the deceased families. He also ordered the MGM hospital to provide better treatment to injured persons.

Title: YouTuber Devraj Patel killed in road accident in Chhattisgarh's Raipur
content: RAIPUR: Famous YouTuber of Chhattisgarh Devraj Patel was killed in a road accident when the bike he was pillion-riding collided with a truck in the state capital on Monday. The 22-year-old YouTuber shot to fame through his YouTube channel â€˜Dil se bura lagta haiâ€™ and used to speak in Chhattisgarhi dialect.Chief minister Bhupesh Baghel expressed grief over the demise of Devraj and tweeted that Devraj who made place in the hearts of crores of people has left for heavenly abode. "He had exceptional skills and his loss is very sad," the CM said.. .AdvertisementVideos and condolences for the YouTuber comedian were widely shared on social media. Devraj had more than four lakh subscribers on YouTube and over eight crore views and was known for his style and funny videos. Police said that Devraj was returning from Nava Raipur after recording a video around 3.30pm when an accident took place near the Labhandi area. He was riding pillion with a friend Rakesh Manhar when the bikeâ€™s handle rubbed with a truck and they fell. Though his friend escaped unhurt, Patel sustained severe injuries and was rushed to hospital. He was declared dead by the time he reached hospital. The deceased YouTuber was a resident of the Mahasamund district.

Title: 22-year-old killed in accident on Kanadia Bypass
content: Indore: A 22-year-old youth was killed and two seriously injured when a four-wheeler they were travelling in overturned after being hit by another vehicle at Kanadia Bypass on Sunday morning.Kanadia police station in-charge Jagdeesh Jamre said that the accident took place near Sahara City at around 4:30 am when a speeding car was hit by another vehicle. Due to impact of collision, the car hit an electricity pole and overturned four times before falling on service road.The youths got stuck in the car and were extricated with difficulty by locals. They were rushed to a hospital where one of them, Vinay, a resident of Guru Nanak colony, was declared dead. His friends Yash, 24, and Ritika, 20, residents of Silicon City were seriously injured.The police said that Vinay owned a footwear shop in Manikbagh area and Ritika worked at a beauty parlour.. .AdvertisementRitikaâ€™s mother Anjali said that Vinay and Yash had come to their house at around 11pm and had taken Ritika with them. Anjali had initially refused to send her with them late in the night but they had promised to return early.The police said that a case was registered and they were checking CCTV footage to ascertain identity of the accused. It was suspected that the deceased was in an inebriated condition when the accident took place.

Title: Five-year-old killed after hit by tanker
content: Indore: A five-year-old was killed and two minor girls injured when they were hit by a tanker in Heera Nagar area on Saturday night.Heera Nagar police said that the accident took place in Scheme number 113 when the girl Ranu was playing with her cousins on a road. The family members saw the tanker approaching the children and managed to pull the two girls away from its path. Ranu was, however, hit by the tanker. She was rushed to a hospital where she succumbed on Sunday morning.Tanker driver Baby Singh, a resident of Raghunandan Bagh was nabbed by locals and handed over to the police. They claimed that he was in an inebriated condition at the time of accident. Police said a case was registered and the body sent for postmortem examination. TNN. .Advertisement

Title: 12, including 7 of a family, killed as 2 buses collide head-on in Odisha
content: BERHAMPUR: As many as 12 members of a marriage party were killed when the private bus they were travelling in collided head-on with an OSRTC bus near Digapahandi on Berhampur-Taptapani road in Odisha, late on Sunday night. "Seven others sustained injuries in the accident," said Berhampur SP Saravana Vivek M.The injured were shifted to MKCG Medical College and Hospital and Digapahandi hospital where they are undergoing treatment. Two of the injured have been shifted to SCB Medical College and Hospital in Cuttack, sources said.. .AdvertisementAccording to sources, the deceased include seven members of a family and their relatives.The accident took place when they were returning to Khandadeuli near Digapahandi after attending the party at Berhampur.Police rushed to the spot immediately and rescued the injured and shifted them to the medical centre, said SP.The special relief commissioner said that Rs 30,000 each has been sanctioned to the injured for treatment under the supervision of Ganjam district administration.PM, CM expresses griefWhile expressing grief over the tragic incident, Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh each to the next of kin of deceased and Rs 50,000 each to the injured.Chief minister Naveen Patnaik also expressed deep grief over the deaths of people in the bus accident and announced an ex-gratia of Rs 3 lakh each to next of kin of deceased and free medical treatment for the injured.

Title: 8 killed, 7 hurt in collision between truck and passenger vehicle in Ratnagiri
content: MUMBAI: At least eight people were killed and seven others injured when a speeding truck collided with a passenger vehicle in Maharashtra's Ratnagiri district on Monday.Police said the accident occurred at Asud on the Dapoli-Harnai Road when a truck heading towards Dapoli from Harnai being driven on the wrong side of the road rammed into a three-wheeler vehicle, carrying 15 passengers on Sunday afternoon.Five passengers were killed on the spot and three others succumbed to their injuries at a hospital, PTI quoted the official as saying, adding that seven persons were injured.In a tweet, Maharashtra chief minister Eknath Shinde expressed grief and announced an ex-gratia of Rs 5 lakh to the kin of each deceased which will be paid from the Chief Minister's Relief Fund.. .AdvertisementWith PTI inputs

Title: Woman run over by train, dies
content: Ahmedabad: A 32-year-old woman from Ranip died when a train ran over her near D Cabin locality on Saturday. According to Sabarmati police, Nimisha Panchal, a resident of Vijaynagar Society on Ramji Mandir Road in Ranip, has been identified as the victim. The cops are yet to ascertain whether she was killed accidentally or had committed suicide. Sabarmati police have registered a complaint of accidental death and begun investigation.

Title: 4 of family dead as car rams into stationary tractor-trolley
content: BAREILLY: Four people, including two children, were killed and two others suffered critical injuries after a speeding car crashed into a stationary tractor-trolley on Moradabad-Farrukhabad highway in UP's Budaun district late on Saturday night. According to police, the car was trying to overtake a vehicle when it crashed into a trolley parked on the roadside. All the victims belonged to the same family and were on their way home to Arsis Barkhin village after attending a wedding ceremony in Chandausi. Circle officer Alok Mishra said, "The tractor-trolley broke down and its driver left it on the road. There were no reflectors on it. We will register an FIR against the tractor driver for causing death by negligence and other relevant sections of IPC after receiving a complaint from the victims' family." Police said Pitambar Yadav, a farmer, his nephew Jitendra Yadav (27), and other members of the family had gone to attend a wedding ceremony in two cars. While returning, Pitambar's wife, Meetal Yadav (31), and children Shivanya Yadav (7) and Arnav Yadav (5) decided to leave early and boarded Jitendra's car.  To reach home early, Jitendra was speeding and met with an accident. The left portion of the car was completely damaged, and four persons -- Jitendra's mother Surajshree Yadav (61), wife Shashi Yadav (24), Shivanya, and Arnav died on the spot. Police had to use a gas cutter to take out the bodies. Jitendra and Meetal sustained serious injuries and were admitted to a medical college in Budaun city for treatment.. .Advertisement

Title: Kolkata: Stolen bus rams car on VIP Road, 3 of family killed
content: KOLKATA: A stolen private bus that was being chased by cops and some bus owners crashed into a car waiting at the Lake Town traffic signal on VIP road, killing a 54-year-old businessman, his 23-year-old son and 73-year-old mother at 3am on Monday. The family was on the way back after the wedding of the businessman's daughter at a New Town venue. The impact left the blue hatchback in a crumpled mess, instantly killing rear-seat passengers Shiv Shankar Rathi and his mother Kamala, and son Srivatsa, who was in the co-driver's seat. Driver Ankit Kumar Mahato (20) survived with a broken neck.The bus continued to speed even after hitting the car but rammed a truck waiting at the Dakshindari traffic signal a little ahead and came to a halt. The bus driver, Hazibullah Molla (40), tried to flee but was caught by traffic cops. . .AdvertisementThe Rathis were returning to their Maniktala home after completing the wedding rituals of Ayushi and Devendra Gupta, both doctors, at a banquet hall off City Centre II. Police said Shiv Shankar and his mother suffered multiple injuries while Srivatsa, who was strapped to the front left seat, had his spine fractured.Accused lost job a yr ago for rash drivingKolkata: Driver Hazibullah Molla, who had stolen the bus, has been charged with IPC sections 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) that attracts a jail term of 10 years to life imprisonment, 279 (rash driving) and 379 (theft). â€œThe accident has left the other members of the family completely devastated. The 25- year-old bride, Ayushi, hadnâ€™t yet changed out of her wedding outfit when she got the death news of her father, brother and grandmother. She is losing consciousness on and off and her mother, too, is also in a very sorry state,â€ said Deepak Daga, a family member. Police said Molla used to be abus driver on route 44 (Baguiati-Howrah) but was fired a year ago because of rash driving, ill temper and allegations of stealing diesel. He possibly had a duplicate key that he used to steal the bus and flee the terminus around 1.20 am on Monday. â€œOne of my drivers saw him stealing the bus and alerted us,â€ said Sujit Ghosh, owner of the stolen bus. â€œWe rushed to the terminus and also alerted Baguiati police, who also reached the spot. While we were searching for the vehicle, we suddenly saw it zip past the terminus around 3am. We immediately gave chase in cars and bikes. Cops too began chasing the vehicle, which sped up and hit the car waiting at the traffic signal,â€ Ghosh said. Cops at Lake Town crossing immediately alerted traffic operators at Dakshindari, who switched off the signal and the bus crashed into a truck that had come to a halt at the crossing. Shiv Shankar Rathi owned an electrical goods store near Poddar Court.

Title: Couple, five-year-old daughter killed in car-tanker collision in Odisha's Koraput
content: KORAPUT: A couple and their five-year-old daughter were killed when the car in which they were travelling collided head-on with an acid loaded tanker on NH-26 near Pungar under Pottangi police station in Koraput district, on Tuesday.The deceased have been identified as Jeypore natives, Satrughan Pradhan (35), his wife Rasmita (25) and their daughter Subhalaxmi (5), said police.Police said the incident took place between 5am and 5.30am, about 30 km from Koraput. While the family was travelling from Jeypore to Visakhapatnam, the tanker was on its way from Berhampur to Damanjodi.â€œThe collision was severe, resulting in the immediate death of all the three occupants of the car under its the impact. The car has been badly damaged,â€ said Kaibalya Seth, IIC Pottangi police station.. .AdvertisementBeing informed about the incident from locals, a team from Pottangi police station rushed to the spot and retrieved the bodies for autopsy. As the accident had occurred on the centre of the road, the accident led to traffic disruption on the NH-26 for a few hours.â€œThe road was eventually cleared for traffic and a case has been registered. The driver of the tanker has been detained for interrogation and an investigation is underway to determine the cause of the accident,â€ the SDPO said.Earlier, on Sunday night, in a heart-wrenching incident an accident had claimed at least 12 wedding revellers in Ganjam district.

Title: Patna judge hurt, 3 of his family killed in road accident
content: BHAGALPUR: Three family members of Praful Singh, a judicial magistrate posted in a Patna district court, were killed and three others, including himself, were injured in a road accident near Tengraha Mor on NH-107, Saharsa district, in the early hours of Monday.According to sources, the judicial magistrate's vehicle overturned and fell into a ditch after being hit by a speeding vehicle coming from the opposite direction. The victims were returning from a family function at Shahzadpur, Madhepura district.While the judicial magistrate's father and two uncles died on the spot, he and his niece and nephew were severely injured. The dead were identified as Praful's father, Ranjit Singh (65), and his two uncles - Narad Singh (70) and Sachin Singh (55).Banka ADJ and District Legal Services Authority (DALSA) secretary Prabhakar Jha said the judicial magistrate had been recently transferred to Patna from Banka. "Praful and the two injured children were admitted to a nearby sub-divisional hospital for treatment," Jha added.. .AdvertisementSimri Bakhtiyarpur police station SHO Krishna Kumar said police have begun an investigation. "The bodies of the victims have been sent to a hospital for autopsies," he added.

Title: 8 dead, 7 hurt as truck rams into mini van in Ratnagiri
content: NAVI MUMBAI: Eight persons, including two kids, were killed and seven others injured when a speeding truck collided with a crowded mini van carrying 15 at Asud village in Ratnagiriâ€™s Dapoli taluka Sunday afternoon. The accident occurred when the truck moving on the wrong lane rammed the van on a road turn. The truck driver has been arrested and booked for causing death by negligent and rash driving. CM Shinde expressed grief and announced ex-gratia of Rs 5 lakh to the kin of each deceased.

Title: Two on bike ram another, crushed to death by car
content: Chennai: Two brothers, who were on a bike, were killed in a road accident on Kamarajar Salai on Sunday. They rammed a two-wheeler on the beach road and were thrown off their bike on to the ground where a car hit and crushed them to death.Police said the deceased men Praveen, 30, a resident of Varadhappa Mudali Street in Sowcarpet, and his brother Rathan, 28, were riding towards Santhome along Kamarajar Salai. Another bike rider Dev Sharma, 20, of Uttar Pradesh, merged on to the road. Praveen who tried to stop the bike crashed into Sharmaâ€™s bike. . .AdvertisementA car on the same stretch ran over the two and in this impact, the two brothers were killed. Dev Sharma is under treatment at the Government Kilpauk Medical College and Hospital.Police have booked a case against the car driver Abdul Syed, 26, a resident of Johhny Johnkhan Street in Royapettah.

Title: Seven killed in separate road mishaps in Doda, Ramban; 13 injured
content: JAMMU: At least seven people were killed and 13 others injured in two road mishaps when vehicles in which they were travelling in skidded off the roads and fell into a deep gorges on Bhaderwah Pathankot road in Doda district and in Rampaari area of Ramban district of Jammu and Kashmir on Tuesday.Police from Doda said that the Trax vehicle (JK06-5071) ferrying passengers met with the accident near Guldanda in which five people were killed on the spot and 12 sustained injuries."The injured were immediately evacuated to a nearby hospital by local volunteers and police personnel," police added."The deceased have been identified as Ghulam Nabi (47) of Gundana Doda, Vinod Kumar (50) of Monda Bhaderwah, Barqat-ul-Lah (52) of Babore Doda, Sher Chand (69) of Kathal Kathua and Firdous Ahmed (28) of Chabnari Banihal.. .AdvertisementSenior superintendent of police (SSP, Doda) Abdul Qayoom said, "Five people were declared brought dead while 12 injured have been hospitalised." "One with serious injuries is being airlifted to GMC Jammu while one has been referred to Doda." A case has been registered.â€œSpoke to DC Doda, Vishesh Mahajan after receiving information about the unfortunate casualty of five persons in a road accident on Bhaderwah-Pathankot road and 12 injured. Some shifted to GMC Doda, two more seriously being airlifted to Jammu. All further assistance, as required will be provided. I am in constant touch,â€ Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh tweeted.In another incident, at least two people died and another was injured after a vehicle they were traveling in rolled down and fell into a gorge in Rampaari area of Ramban district on Tuesday.Police said, an Alto 800 (JK02N-1856), coming from Ramsoo towards Banihal, rolled into a deep gorge resulting death of two person namely as Mohd Afzal Malik (32) and Mohd Azmat (55), both resident of Sarbagni Ramban while Shabir Ahmed Malik (35), also from Sarbagni, was shifted to SDH Banihal for medicare.

Title: Youth killed in Parimal hit and run
content: AHMEDABAD: A 23-year-old man from the New Vasna area of the city was killed in a hit and run after a speeding loading rickshaw hit his scooter at the Parimal underpass in Ambawadi on Monday morning. According to the FIR filed with M Division traffic police, the deceased, Naimil Shah, was a resident of Swaminarayan Park in New Vasna and worked in Navrangpura as a junior web developer for an IT company. Naimil's father, Rakesh Shah, who works for the water supply department of the Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation, in his FIR said he had only recently bought a scooter for Naimil. Naimil would go to work around noon every day. "I left for my office around 7am on Monday and last saw Naimil at home," Rakesh said. . .AdvertisementHe added that he got a call from an unknown man around 11.30am on Monday. "He told me Naimil had been involved in an accident with a loading rickshaw driver at the Parimal underpass. He told me Naimil had suffered severe injuries," said Rakesh in the FIR. Rakesh rushed to the spot and saw Naimil lying dead on the road. "Naimil had severe injuries on his head and between his eyes. His scooter and helmet were lying on the road," said Rakesh. He said that onlookers told him that a loading rickshaw, going at high speed, had hit Naimil and then sped away. Inspector Hansa Kataria of M Division traffic police said police could not identify the driver of the loading rickshaw as they could not get the registration number of the vehicle. "We also did not find any relevant CCTV footage to identify him," said Kataria. M Division traffic police registered a case of causing death by negligence along with relevant sections of the Motor Vehicles Act against the unknown driver.

Title: 1 killed as part of house slab collapses in Pune
content: PUNE: A 50-year-old man died, while his brother (60) suffered injuries after a portion of the slab of their rented house in an old two-storied building on Dastur Meher Road caved in around 7.30 pm on Monday. An official from the Pune Cantonment Board (PCB) said, â€œSeepage of water from the buildingâ€™s roof and water tank seemed to have weakened the structure over time, resulting in the collapse of the slab portion.â€ Police and fire brigade officials identified the deceased as Stanley Dâ€™souza and the injured as Jerry Dâ€™souza. They were staying on the ground floor of the building.The fire brigade received a call relating to the incident around 7.30pm. â€œA fire tender reached the site and started clearing the debris. The team rescued the two brothers from debris and took them to the Sassoon hospital where Stanley succumbed to his injuries,â€ Rohit Ranpise, the PCBâ€™s fire superintendent, told TOI.

Title: Bihar: Judge's father, 2 uncles die in Saharsa accident
content: BHAGALPUR: Three family members of a judicial magistrate posted in a Patna district court were killed and three others, including the judge himself, were injured in a road accident near Tengraha Mor on NH-107 under the Simri Bakhtiyarpur police station in Saharsa district in the wee hours of Monday.According to sources, the vehicle of the judicial magistrate, Praful Singh, overturned and fell into a ditch after being hit by another speeding vehicle from the opposite direction.While the judicial magistrate's father and two uncles died on the spot, he and his niece and nephew sustained severe injuries in the accident.The victims were identified as Praful's father Ranjit Singh (65) and his two uncles - Narad Singh (70) and Sachin Singh (55). The judicial magistrate's niece, Tanya Kumari (13), and nephew, Mayank Kumar (10), were injured in the accident.. .AdvertisementProbe begins into accident: SHO The accident occurred when Praful and his family members were returning to their native village Sardiha under the Bakhtiyarpur police station area after attending a family function at Shahzadpur in Madhepura district. Banka ADJ and secretary of District Legal Services Authority (DALSA), Prabhakar Jha, said the judicial magistrate was transferred to Patna from Banka recently. â€œPraful and the two injured children were admitted to a nearby sub-divisional hospital for treatment,â€ Jha added. SHO of Simri Bakhtiyarpur police station, Krishna Kumar, said police have begun an investigation into the accident. â€œThe bodies of the victims have been sent to a hospital for autopsy,â€ he added.

Title: 12 of marriage party killed as buses collide in Odisha's Ganjam
content: BERHAMPUR: A private bus carrying members of a wedding party collided with a state-owned bus killing 12 people, including two children and four women, and injuring eight others near Digapahandi in Ganjam district, late on Sunday night.The victims are relatives of state finance minister Bikram Keshari Arukha. The passengers were on their way from Berhampur to Khandadeuli after attending the marriage reception of Sonali Pradhan (24), the niece of former Rajya Sabha MP Renubala Pradhan. Among the deceased were two of Sonali's sisters, family sources said. Berhampur superintendent of police Saravana Vivek M said most of the victims were members of a marriage party from the bride's side and belonged to two families. The private bus and a Berhampur-bound Odisha State Road Transport Corporation (OSRTC) bus collided with each other around 12.30 am on the two-lane road.Renubala, who was in Khandadeuli at the time of the accident, said, "The bride left for her in-laws' house in Berhampur at around 7.30 pm. Other relatives went to attend her reception at around 8.30 pm. They were returning from Berhampur when the accident took place," she said.. .AdvertisementThe deceased were identified as Sugyani Kumari Pradhan (31), her husband Subhendu Pradhan (32), their son Debansu (3), Sugyani's sister Rupali Pradhan (23), Sibaram Pradhan (25) and his son Hardik (1), Sanjay Medini Ray, Ramesh Pradhan (62) and his brother Sitaram Pradhan (60), Tripti Pradhan (48), Litu Nayak (34) and Sangita Dakua (32). The deceased belonged to Muzagarh, Khandadeuli, Singipur and Gada Gobindapur villages. The injured persons, including the two drivers, are undergoing treatment at MKCG Medical College and Hospital and their condition is stated to be stable.Police sources said the private bus suffered the maximum damage reportedly because it was more than 25 years old, was rickety and running without a fitness certificate.

Title: Wedding joy turns into nightmare as Kolkata businessman, mom & son crushed at Lake Town signal on way back home
content: KOLKATA: Sarala Rathi, 50, the only surviving member of the Rathi family â€” apart from her daughter who got married on Sunday â€” could have been killed in the early morning car crash on Monday had she not shifted to another car just before leaving the New Town banquet hall where her daughter got married. The Rathis â€” businessman Shiv Shankar, 54, his mother Kamala, 73, and 23-yearold-son Srivatsa, 23 â€“ were all killed in a road accident on their way back home from the wedding when a bus-jacker hit their car while trying to escape with the vehicle with cops and bus operators in pursuit.â€œMy sister was also travelling in the same car but other relatives insisted that she joined them in another car and said that they would drop her home. So, she shifted to another car at the last moment. Had she been there, we doubt if she could have come out of the crash alive,â€ said Saralaâ€™s cousin, who was also at the wedding on Sunday. . .AdvertisementOnce she was back home, she began to worry about her husband, mother-in-law and son since they were taking longer than usual to arrive and started calling them. Some calls went unanswered and then a cop took a call on Shiv Shankarâ€™s mobile and informed her about the accident while asking her to reach RG Kar Medical College and Hospital at the earliest. â€œShe called us in a panicstricken voice around 4 am and asked us to rush to the hospital. She called other family members as well, including her daughter and all of us reached the hospital only to find all of them dead. On the happiest day of their lives, the family has been left completely devastated,â€ said Deepak Daga, a relative. The newly wed daughter, Ayushi, a doctor with a private hospital off EM Bypass, rushed to the hospital in her wedding attire along with her husband Devendra Gupta. She was initially not told about the deaths but upon learning, she fainted. So did Sarala, as relatives took both of them back to their Maniktala home. â€œThe two have been shellshocked and are losing consciousness every now and then. It has been such a shocking incident that none of us have words to console the two,â€ said Rajkumar Baheti, another relative. The Rathis ran a family business of electrical goods and have a shop named Shiva Electricals near Poddar Court in central Kolkata. The car too was registered in the companyâ€™s name and carried a doctorâ€™s sticker as Ayushi used to drive the same to and from hospital mostly. Srivatsa was pursuing MBA and was helping his father in the business. Kamala was a homemaker and her husband Tara Chand had died around 10 years ago. â€œShiv Shankar was a great man. He not only provided for his own family with the business but also used to support many other families of his relatives and friends. His death is a great loss to all of us,â€ said Jai Kumar Dugari, another relative.

Title: 5 killed as mini truck falls into river in Madhya Pradesh's Datia
content: BHOPAL: Five people were killed when a mini truck carrying around 50 labourers veered off a bridge and fell into the river in Datia district of Madhya Pradesh late on Tuesday night.The incident occurred in Buhara village under the jurisdiction of the Dursada police station.Preliminary reports suggested that the mini truck, en route from Bheleti village in Gwalior to Jatara village in Tikamgarh for a wedding celebration, lost control while crossing the bridge, causing it to overturn and plunge into the river. The authorities have revealed that the bridge was under construction at the time of the accident.. .AdvertisementUpon receiving the information, the police arrived at the scene and initiated rescue operations. The bodies of all five victims have been recovered from the river.Datia superintendent of police (SP) Pradeep Sharma went to the accident site to oversee the efforts to rescue and recover any remaining individuals.Expressing his condolences, home minister Narottam Mishra said that the victims were members of the Khatik community from Balehari, who were traveling in the mini truck to attend a wedding in Jatara (Tikamgarh). Mishra said, "The truck lost control, leading to a detachment of one of its wheels and a subsequent plunge into the Buhara river. The bodies of the recovered victims include a 65-year-old woman, an 18-year-old youth, and three children aged between 2 and 3 years. Those who were injured are currently receiving medical treatment."

Title: Mom, daughter among 5 killed in 3 accidents
content: DEHRADUN/PITHORAGARH: Five people, including a mother-daughter duo, were killed in three separate accidents that occurred in Rudraprayag and Pithoragarh districts of Uttarakhand on Tuesday.In the first road mishap, 60-year-old Kamla Devi and her 47-year-old daughter Laxmi Devi died after the car they were traveling in fell into a 300 m deep gorge at Khankra, near Sirobagad, in Rudraprayag district. Their bodies were recovered by SDRF team following a search and rescue operation.â€œThe incident occurred when the driver stepped out of the car without pulling the hand brake. As a result, the car, which was parked on a descent, with both women inside it, started moving forward and plunged into a gorge,â€ said inspector Lalita Negi, SDRF. In the second incident, two persons died after their car skidded off the road and fell into a deep gorge in Hokara village, almost 26km away from Kapkot in Bageshwar district. The deceased persons were identified as Kushal Singh Koranga (41) and Yamuna Devi (32), both residents of Kapkot.Meanwhile, Manoj Kumar Joshi was killed in an accident that occurred at Dilli bend at Ghat in Pithoragarh district in the wee hours, said Bhupendra Singh Mahar, district disaster management officer of Pithoragarh.. .Advertisement

Title: 2 die in accidents in Trichy, Pudukottai
content: Trichy: Trichy central region police have begun inquiry into the death of two people in separate accidents, including a hit-and-run case, in Pudukottai and Trichy on Monday. According to the complaint lodged with Viralimalai police by VAO Bhuvaneshwari on Monday, an unidentified man aged about 35 years was found dead on the road at Mathiripatti late on Sunday. She said the man was killed after he was hit by an unknown vehicle. The body was sent to government headquarters hospital in Manapparai. Viralimalai police booked a case of hit-and-run fatal accident and conducted inquiries. In the other case registered by Thuvakudi police attached to Trichy district police, V Rajeshwari, 28 of Mudukkupatti near Thiruverumbur was riding pillion on her husband K Velmuruganâ€™s bike on the way to Periyar Nagar on Sunday night. She said a speeding MUV drove in a rash and negligent manner and dashed against the bike. Velmurugan fell down and sustained injuries. Local people rushed him to the government hospital in Thuvakudi where doctors declared him dead. The driver of the car identified as Sivakumar absconded. Thuvakudi police have booked a case and are investigating.

Title: 3 of family die as car collides with tanker in Koraput dist
content: KORAPUT: A couple and their five-year-old daughter, on their way to a marriage ceremony, died on the spot when the car in which they were travelling collided with a tanker on National Highway-26 near Pungar in Koraput district on Tuesday morning. The deceased have been identified as Satrughan Pradhan (35), his wife Rasmita (25) and their daughter Subhalaxmi.The police said the accident took place between 5 am and 5.30 am. The family was travelling from Jeypore to Visakhapatnam while the tanker, carrying acid, was going from Berhampur to Damanjodi."The impact of the collision was so severe that it resulted in the immediate death of all three occupants of the car. The car was badly damaged," said Kaibalya Seth, inspector in-charge, Pottangi police station.The marriage of Rasmita's brother was to be held at the Jagannath Temple in Pujariput under Borigumma block on Tuesday. As per tradition, the family had left Borigumma at around 4 am to invite the bride's family, who live in Ralegada under Pottangi police station limits, to attend the event.. .Advertisement Satrughan, who drives a pickup van, had borrowed the car from the firm's owner. Following the accident, the marriage was cancelled. "We are shattered. We can't believe it happened. We have not informed my mother about the accident. We don't know how she will react," said Budu Pradhan, Rasmita's brother.The accident occurred in the middle of the highway, forcing traffic to be disrupted for a few hours. "The road was eventually cleared for traffic and a case has been registered. The driver of the tanker has been detained for interrogation. Investigation is underway to determine the cause of the accident," Seth said.On Sunday night, a head-on collision between two buses killed 12 members of a wedding party in Ganjam district."These accidents serve as a reminder of the importance of road safety and the need for efforts to prevent them from recurring in future," said Sanjit Patnaik, a Koraput-based activist.

Title: 3 killed as bike collides with car
content: Lucknow: Three persons, including a nine-year-old child, were killed after the bike they were riding collided head on with a car on the Gonda-Utraula road on Wednesday. Police said that the deceased were identified as Mishri Lal (55), Akshay (9) and Neeraj (30).Circle Officer, Shilpa Verma told TOI that the accident had taken place around 9am near Sohila village under Dhanepur police station.On getting information, police personnel reached the spot and took them to the Mujehna Community Health Centre. In view of their critical condition, they were referred to the Gonda Medical College, where doctors declared them dead, she said.â€œNeeraj was a resident of Kolhua Bankata village of Motiganj police station area and was going to the city by bike on Wednesday morning to get medicine along with his uncle Mishrilal and grandson Akshay. All three of them had reached near Sohila lake when a speeding car coming from the opposite side hit their bike,â€ said Verma. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: 1 killed, 8 injured as pickup van carrying dancers overturns
content: Balangir: One person was killed and eight injured when the pick up van carrying members of a devotional dance troupe overturned at Bhainsa Square under Patnagarh police station limits in Balangir district on Wednesday.The deceased was identified as Mukesh Adsulia (55) of Rampur. Bhagyalaxmi Bag (40), also of Rampur, was shifted to Bhima Bhoi Medical College and Hospital, Balangir, as she was critically injured. Others were admitted to the sub-divisional hospital in Patnagarh.â€œThe vehicle skidded off the road at Bhainsa Chhak and overturned due to heavy rain. Passengers travelling in the van were trapped under it,â€ said Jyotirmaya Bhukta, sub-divisional police officer, Patnagarh. Police sources said troupe was travelling to Bandhanbadi from Rampur to attend Bahuda Yatra. As many as 35 members of the troupe were on board when the accident took place at 4.30 pm. Locals, fire bridge personnel and a police team jointly rescued the victims and admitted them to the nearest hospital, said Bhukta.

Title: 5 killed, 9 hurt as truck ploughs into marriage party in Keonjhar
content: Keonjhar: A speeding truck ploughed into a marriage procession on National Highway-20 in Sathipur, killing five persons and critically injuring nine others early on Wednesday morning.The accident occurred at around 1 am when the victims were accompanying the groom's car, walking and dancing on the street, barely 20 metres from the bride's house. Keonjhar SP Nitin Kusalkar said the marriage party from Manpur village under Harichandanpur police station was going to Sathipur village in Keonjhar Old Town. This is the third accident in three days in the state involving marriage parties. . .AdvertisementThe truck was coming from Ghatagaon side and travelling towards Joda when the accident took place. After the accident, the truck driver tried to escape but ended up hitting into an electric pole. The driver is on the run. Police, with the help of local people and fire service team, rescued the injured and shifted them to the district headquarters hospital. Four of them were later referred to SCB Medical College and Hospital, Cuttack, after their condition turned critical.The deceased were identified as Bapun Dehury (25), Rati Munda (65), Darshan Patra (22), of Old Town, and Satyajit Patra (13) and Sunil Kumar Mohanta (27) of Harichandanpur.After the accident, locals blocked the highway demanding a flyover at the site and compensation for the kin of the deceased. "As frequent accidents are taking place in the area, we want a flyover near the village," said Juli Patra, a local resident. Due to the blockade, traffic movement on NH-20 was disrupted for more than six hours. After getting assurance from the police and district administration, the protesters withdrew the blockade.Road accident deaths went up 17% in 1st 3 months of '23 | page 3 Bhubaneswar: Deaths in road accidents went up by around 17% across the state between January and March this year as compared to the first quarter in the previous year, according to the latest data compiled by the Odisha police.The findings came against the backdrop of the three fatal accidents in a row in the state where 20 people died. On Thursday, five died after a truck ploughed into a marriage procession in Keonjhar district. On Wednesday, a couple and their kid, who were on their way to a marriage, died on the spot when their car collided with a tanker and the day before, 12 of a marriage party were killed when their bus collided with another in Ganjam district. Besides, a few other districts also reported 1-2 accident-related deaths in the last three days.According to the statistics of Odisha police, the number of accidents went up from 2,895 (Jan-Mar 2022) to 3,370 during the corresponding period this year. Casualties during January-March this year increased to 1,594 from 1,359 during the same period last year. The fatality rate in the accidents stood at about 47% during the first quarter of both years."We found that 21 districts witnessed a rise in deaths, while eight districts reported a drop during the comparative period. Death figure in one district (Dhenkanal) neither increased nor decreased," a senior police official told TOI.Jajpur, Jharsuguda, Malkangiri, Nayagarh, Nabarangpur, Sambalpur and Gajapati reported nearly 95%, 76%, 62%, 60%, 58%, 55% and 54% rise in accident deaths, respectively, during the first quarter this year compared to the corresponding period of 2022. Death figures in Kendrapada, Khurda, Puri, Ganjam, Kalahandi and Nuapada were up by 30%-40% during the period.The transport department said steps are being taken to address the issue. "We are aware of the situation and joint effort by the police and regional transport officers are being carried out to improve the road safety scenario in the state. District collectors and SPs are being regularly asked to find out causes behind the accidents and draw plans of action to reduce the number. Enforcement drives against violators have been stepped up," a senior transport officer said.C hief minister Naveen Patnaik announced an ex gratia of Rs 3 lakh each for the kin of the deceased. Keonjhar district collector Ashish Thakre said a technical committee will visit the site and inspect requirements like barriers, lights, among others to avoid further accidents. â€œWe are providing treatment to the injured and compensation will be given to the families of the deceased as per government provision.â€ The administration has announced Rs 40,000 ex gratia to the families of the deceased from the district Red Cross fund and Rs 20,000 to the relatives of the injured. The district truck owners association also declared Rs 20,000 ex gratia for the kin of the deceased.the victims and admitted them to the nearest hospital, said Bhukta

Title: Six pilgrims from Karnataka killed, 11 injured in road accident in Maharashtra's Solapur
content: MUMBAI/NASHIK: Six pilgrims â€” including five women and a boy â€” returning to Karnataka after visiting temple towns of Pandharpur and Akkalkot were killed and 11 injured when their passenger vehicle collided with a cement mixer truck in southern Maharashtra's Solapur district on Friday evening, police said.The incident took place around 5.30pm near Shirwalwadi village in Akkalkot tehsil when the private SUV carrying the group of pilgrims was returning to Aland village in Kalburgi, Karnataka, PTI quoted the official as saying. They had visited the temple towns of Pandharpur and Akkalkot in Solapur district and were on their way back. A speeding cement mixer truck, heading towards Solapur, collided with the passenger vehicle, the official said, adding that police rushed to the spot and all the passengers were taken to a nearby government hospital. . .AdvertisementFive women and one boy were declared dead on arrival while 11 others â€” three women and eight men â€” were undergoing treatment, the police official said.With agency inputs

Title: Road crashes killed 33 in 6 months: Police data
content: CHANDIGARH: The city witnessed 33 road fatalities in the first six months of this year, and maximum 11 persons were killed in accidents between 6 pm to 12 am, mostly due to overspeeding. Data reveals that 26 victims were male and six were women. As per police record, 78 people were killed in 74 fatal accidents in 2022. As per traffic police data, 12 people riding two wheelers were killed, while 8 were pedestrians, 9 cyclists , two victims were travelling in autos and one each in pick-up and cart puller. As many as 81 accidents were reported in the city in six months and 78 people had received minor or grievous injuries. Data reveals that 5 were killed between 3 pm to 6 pm and 3 were killed between 6 am to 12 am.  A maximum of nine victims were in the age group of 25-35 years, eight were in the 45-60 year age group,while five were between the age of 35-45 years and six were in the 18-25 years age group. Four victims were above 60 years and one was a minor boy. . .AdvertisementThe maximum of three people, including a woman, were killed in one accident when a BA first year student and driver of a Volkswagen Beetle ploughed into bystanders on footpath at Dhanas in the evening of May 17,2023. Four other people were also injured in the accident. Later, the Beetle driver was arrested and sent to judicial custody. However, after spending 35 days in the Burail jail, accused car driver Paramvir Singh was released on bail. According to data, UT traffic police had issued several challans for overspeeding through CCTV cameras installed at traffic chowks and speed guns in the city. Police issued around 5.40 lakhs challans through these cameras between March 2022 to March 2023. Police added that as many as 177 driving licences have been suspended this year, and 100 DLs have been suspended for driving over permissible speed limit this year. Last year, UT police had suspended 1,118 driving licences, including 275 for over speeding. Under ICCC , more than 2000 CCTV cameras were installed in the city to keep a tab on traffic violators. Police officials said they have been issuing challans to other stateâ€™s vehicles too.

Title: Man killed, 2 injured after truck hits bike
content: Indore: A 32-year-old man was killed and a father-son duo was seriously injured after a speeding heavy vehicle collided head-on with the two-wheeler they were riding on in Manglia area on Wednesday night. Kshipra police said that the accident took place when Santosh, his friend Kamal and Kamalâ€™s son Ajay were on their way home in Swarna Bagh Colony from Tonkkhurd of Dewas. The truck was coming on the wrong side of the road when the accident took place. All of them fell on the road and received severe injuries. They were rushed to hospital where Santosh succumbed on Thursday. Family members told police that Santosh was a contractor. He was working in an under-construction building in Tonk Khurd area where he went every day with labourers. He was returning from work when he was hit by the truck around 9:30 pm.Police said that though the truck driver fled the spot, the vehicle was seized and taken to police station. Traffic on the road was eased after removal of the damaged vehicles. A case was registered against the driver.Also, owner of the truck was summoned for questioning. tnn. .Advertisement

Title: Pick-up hits bike, drags riders; 1 killed
content: Ludhiana: A 26-year-old man was killed on Thursday, after a speeding pick-up truck first rammed into the motorcycle he and his friend were riding, and then dragged the duo along with the car while trying drive away from the crime scene near Doraha. Manpreet Singh was from Keema Bhaini village. His friend, Amandeep Singh (25) of Madanpur village in Rajpura, has been admitted to a hospital, where his condition is said to be serious.The duo was headed for Gurdwara Rara Sahib, when a Mahindra Bolero hit their motorcycle. The two fell on the road. But, instead of stopping the four-wheeler, the driver dragged the two men along while trying to speed away. Payal DSP Harsimrat Singh said the victims were headed for Gurdwara Rara Sahib, when the accident happened on a service lane along Southern Bypass. As the accused could not drive the four-wheeler further, he fled on foot. Manpreet died on the spot. A case has been registered. Police have seized the vehicles and are trying to trace the accused.

Title: 3 killed, 1 hurt as bike crashes on muddy Kolar rd
content: Bhopal: Four persons travelling on a motorcycle were thrown into a pit when the bike crashed on an underconstruction road in Kolar early Thursday morning. Three of them died and the fourth, a woman, is in critical condition.Police said that the accident happened at Semri Jod where construction work of the Kolar six-lane project is on. The road is a muddy mess these days. However, the cops maintained that the bike rider was drunk and could not negotiate a turn because he was overspeeding.SHO Kolar Jay Kumar Singh named the deceased as Ajeet Meshram, 21, Satish Jharia, 25, and Lalla, 23. The injured woman is Ganpatia Dhurvey, 20. All of them are natives of Dindori district while Ajeet and Ganpatia lived here in Bawadia locality. All of them worked as labourers.Singh said that Satish and Lalla had come to Bhopal around 4am on Thursday, and Ajeet and Ganpatia had gone to pick them up at ISBT. When they were returning to Bawadia, they were passing by Semri Jod when Ajeet could not negotiate a turn as he was drunk and speeding, said police.. .AdvertisementThe force of the crash hurled them into a pit. According to SHO Singh, Ganpatia told police that she had asked Ajeet to slow down, but he did not listen.All four of them sustained severe injuries and were taken to Hamidia Hospital by police and passersby. Ajeet and Satish died within a few minutes. Doctors tried desperately to save Lallaâ€™s life, but he too died late Thursday night. Ganpatia is undergoing treatment and her condition is reported stable. She has suffered multiple injuries. Police have registered a case and are trying to find out why the bike crashed.

Title: UP: 7 dead, 2 injured after SUV carrying electrocuted man rams into truck
content: LUCKNOW: Seven people were killed and two others sustained grievous injuries after the SUV they were travelling in ploughed into a stationary truck in UP's Banda on Thursday night. Police said that the accident had taken place on Kamasin Road in Baberu police station area.Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath condoled the death of the victims and directed officials for the best possible treatment of those injured.Police said that one Katlu (13) of Tilotha village had touched a live wire and was rescued.. .AdvertisementLater, his mother Saira Bano (37), along with locals Mohammed Kaif (18), Shakil (25), Mushahid (24), Raj Khengar (28) and Jahid (27) along with two others had got into an SUV and rushed Katlu to CHC Baberu, said the police."Around 10 pm, when they reached Kasmin road, their vehicle rammed into a stationary truck, reducing the SUV to pulp," said SO, Baberu, Santosh Singh.Singh said the driver of the truck managed to escape and hearing the noise of a loud thud, the locals informed the police."With the help of locals and police gas cutters were used to pull out all the passenger and then they were taken to a local hospital where doctors declared them brought dead. Two who survived were referred to the district hospital their identities were being ascertained," said Singh.Superintendent of Police, Banda, Abhinandan, said that efforts to catch the errant truck driver were underway while prima facie it appeared that the SUV was cruising at 120 km per hour and the driver, who also died, failed to control the SUV. â€œWe were searching the errant truck driver,â€ he said. Police said that Mujeeb was inconsolable as he had lost his wife Saira Bano, two sons Katlu, Mushahid in the accident. â€œOn highways usually people drive fast and recently the government had ordered that no heavy vehicle will be allowed to park on roads by the police and the administration but all has fallen on deaf ears,â€ said Mujeeb while crying loudly.Watch UP: Seven killed as SUV rams into stationary truck in Banda

Title: Youth killed in bike-pickup truck collision
content:
Raipur: A youth was killed while three girls were seriously injured in a road accident near Bhaji Dongri village in Kawardha district on Thursday evening.According to police officials, the incident took place in the jurisdiction of Kukdoor police station. The accident involved a bike carrying four individuals: Dharmendra Sahu, Maya Baiga, Fulbati, and Urmila. All of them were residents of Pauni village in Kabirdham. They were on their way back after seeking admission to a nearby government college when their bike collided head-on with a speeding pick-up vehicle.The pick-up vehicle, coming from the opposite direction, was unable to avoid the bike and ended up colliding with it, causing the pick-up to overturn. Dharmendra, who was riding the bike, died at the scene, while the three girls sustained injuries and were admitted to the community health center in Pandariya for medical treatment. Upon receiving information, a crowd gathered at the site. Police arrived and sent Dharmendra's body for postmortem. Cops said that the driver of the pick-up fled the scene. TNN

Title: Five killed, 27 injured in three separate road accidents in Rajasthan
content: JAIPUR: Five persons were killed and 19 others injured in two separate accidents in Dausa, Bharatpur and Bikaner districts on Friday.Two people including a 6-year-old girl and a 30-year-old woman were killed and 19 others were injured when a Jugaad vehicle overturned after colliding with a truck. The accident took place late on Thursday evening. Locals came to the spot to bail out the trapped passengers out of the overturned Jugaad vehicle. Senior police officers including Vandita Rana, superintendent of police, Dausa also rushed to the spot. â€œThe deceased were identified as Kajori Dev and Gunnu. Out of the 19 injured six were referred to the SMS Hospital in Jaipur,â€ said a senior police officer in Dausa.Likewise, two persons were killed, and 8 others were injured after a pickup jeep overturned in a bid to save a cow at a village under Halena police station area of Bharatpur. â€œAll of them were returning from a religious programme on Friday when the accident took place. The deceased were identified as Divyansh(17) and Jitendra(35),â€ said a senior police officer of Halena police station.In Bikanerâ€™s Jai Narayan Vyas area, an 8-year-old boy was killed after being hit by a rashly driven car. â€œThe boy identified as Virat was playing outside his home when a rashly driven car hit him. He was rushed to the hospital where he was declared brought dead,â€ said a police officer of Jai Narayan Vyas colony. Police have seized the vehicle in this case. Locals including the grandfather of the deceased boy alleged that the driver of the vehicle was drunk.

Title: Nashik: Four people killed, nine injured in accident near Vani
content: NASHIK: Four people were killed in an accident involving a car and the multi-utility vehicle on Vani-Saputara road which leads to Gujarat on Friday evening. Nine others were seriously injured in the accident and now they are being treated in various hospitals. The condition of seven is stable and two others is serious the police officials from Vani police station in whose jurisdiction the accident occurred. According to the assistant police officer Nilesh Bodake the accident occurred late in the evening on Friday when the car that was coming from Saputara and the MUV that was going to Saputara collided with each other head on.All the four occupants of the car died in the accident. While three persons died on the spot, the fourth person died while being take to the hospital the police said. The deceased have been identified as Vinayak Kshirsagar, 37, Yogesh Wagh, 18, Jatin Pawade, 23, and Ravindra Chavan 22 all of them residents of Motha Koliwada in Vani. Nine others who were travelling by the MUV have been admitted in private hospitals in Nashik city, the police added. Medical office of Vani Rural Hospital Dr. Vishal Mahale, with the help of MO of Pandane Health Center Dr Prakash Deshmukh, Dr Anil Pawar of Vani Doctorsâ€™ Association, Dr. Anil Shelke, Dr. Aher, Dr. Yogesh Patil, Dr. Vaibhav Mohite and other private doctors went to the rural hospital to help treat the victims.

Title: Couple, toddler killed in Samruddhi accident
content: NASHIK: A couple and their one-and-a-half-year-old daughter died when the multi-utility vehicle they were travelling in from Jalna to Virar rammed a truck in Kopargaon city on Samruddhi expressway around 1.30am on Friday.The couple's older son and five others travelling in the vehicle, including the driver, were injured and admitted to a hospital for treatment. The driver escaped with minor injuries.This is the third fatal accident in the Shirdi-Sinnar stretch of the expressway since its inauguration on May 26 this year. Ten people have died on this stretch of the expressway since then. Couple & toddler killed as car rams truck on Samruddhi eway  The Kopargaon city police said the multi-utility vehicle (MUV) vehicle was heading from Mantha village in Jalna district to Virar in Palghar district.The couple, Santosh (35) and Varsha Rathod (29), and their children Avni and Arnav, were going to Virar. Santosh was looking for some work in Virar."Santosh, Varsha and Avni were in the front seat of the vehicle. The remaining five were in the rear seat. While Santosh and Avni died on the spot, Varsha died at the hospital during treatment," said Ramrao Dhikle, inspector of the Kopargaon city police station.The police said the driver of the MUV was speeding and it hit a moving truck from the rear. The first two rows of the MUV were completely damaged.The Kopargaon police have registered a case against the driver for negligent driving causing death. They suspect that the driver had either dozed off at the wheel or could not clearly see the truck.Alerted by the loud noise after the MUV crashed into the truck, some local residents rushed to the spot to rescue the injured. The police said it was difficult to remove the people stuck in the vehicle. The injured were taken to Atma Malik Hospital in Kopargaon for treatment.On June 11, four members of a family were killed when the MUV they were travelling in hit the safety wall of the expressway. They were on their way to Shirdi from Mumbai.On June 3, three people died on the same stretch as the car in which they were travelling from Nashik to Jalna hit the road divider.

Title: At least 51 people killed in road accident in western Kenya, 32 injured, police and Red Cross say
content: NAIROBI: A truck rammed into several other vehicles and market traders in western Kenya killing at least 51 people, police said.The Friday evening accident occurred at a location known for vehicle crashes near the Rift Valley town of Londiani, which is about 200 kilometres (125 miles) northwest of the capital, Nairobi.Officers at the scene counted 51 bodies, but more people were believed to be trapped in the wreckage, Rift Valley police commander Tom Odera told The Associated Press.The Kenya Red Cross Society said on Saturday 32 people were injured and hospitalised, and asked Kenyans to donate blood.It also said heavy rainfall interrupted rescue efforts and people were still trapped in wrecked vehicles.Transport Minister Kipchumba Murkomen visited the scene on Saturday morning and said the government would relocate markets away from the highways to prevent such future accidents.President William Ruto tweeted a condolence message to bereaved families describing the accident as â€œdistressingâ€ and urging motorists to be â€œextra cautious.â€Witnesses quoted by local media said the truck veered off the major highway and hit several vehicles before hitting pedestrians and traders. Witnesses shared photos of the vehicle wreckages mangled beyond recognition.Police had said on Friday rescue operations would continue into the night.The Kenyan Red Cross Society said they have set up stations at hospitals where people can report loved ones still missing and are providing psychological support to those affected.

Title: Man out on morning walk hit by bus, killed
content:
Pune: A 76-year-old pedestrian out on a morning walk died after he was hit by a PMPML bus in Kondhwa around 7am on Thursday.The police have identified the deceased as Bipin Dave from Salunke Vihar Road, and have arrested the PMPML bus driver, Mangesh Kolte (32) from Purandar, on the charge of causing death due to negligence.Investigation officer Dinesh Raskar said: â€œThe civic bus travelling from Katraj to Hadapsar was passing via Salunke Vihar Road when its driver lost control over the vehicle and hit Dave when he was passing near a mall. The bus driver stopped after the accident.â€Raskar said passersby rushed Dave in an auto to a nearby private hospital but he was declared dead on arrival by doctors. The Sassoon General Hospital, in its postmortem examination report, certified that the victimâ€™s death was caused due to a head injury. A witness told police that the road was empty when the bus hit the victim.. .AdvertisementThe driver told police that he was not at fault and claimed the accident occurred because the elderly man came in the way of the bus. The police have impounded the bus for an RTO inspection.The victimâ€™s son Hetab Dave (44), a businessman, has lodged a complaint under the Indian Penal Code and Motor Vehicles Act.
