Title: At least 48 killed in Kenya road disaster
content: NAIROBI: At least 48 people were killed when a truck apparently lost control and ploughed into other vehicles and pedestrians at a busy junction in western Kenya on Friday night, police said. "So far we can confirm 48 dead and we are suspecting one or two are still trapped under the truck," local police commander Geoffrey Mayek told AFP after the accident on the highway between the towns of Kericho and Nakuru. He said another 30 people had been seriously injured and rushed to various hospitals, warning that the toll could rise. "We are suspecting a truck which was being driven... towards Kericho lost control and rammed into matatus (local minibuses) that were packed at a bus stop, running over those matatus and injuring passengers and pedestrians who were standing by the bus stop," Mayek said. Collins Kipkoech, a senior doctor at Kericho County Hospital, said his facility had so far received 45 bodies while more victims were taken to other hospitals "and the rescue is still ongoing". Images posted by local television stations showed the mangled wrecks of several vehicles at an area known as Londiani junction. Heavy rains are hindering rescue operations, local media reports said.  "The truck was on high speed and it was hooting. It tried to avoid several vehicles before it came straight into the market," said one witness, Maureen Jepkoech. "I have heard people say it is like it lost the brakes. But I don't know. 'Happened in a flash'"All I can say is that I am lucky to be alive because I saw what happened and I am alive because I ran, I am just lucky," she added.  "I have seen a very bad scene, bodies and blood all over. So many people are dead." Another witness, Joel Rotich, said: "The accident happened in a flash, many of them had no time to escape. "There was a lot of confusion because people were screaming all over and everyone was running after the accident," he added. "It took some time before people gathered courage and started helping those injured." The number of people killed on Kenya's roads has increased in recent years, according to government statistics. "My heart is crushed," Kericho governor Erick Mutai wrote on Facebook, describing it as a "dark moment for the people of Kericho. "My heart goes out to the families who have just lost their loved ones," he said, adding that ambulances had been mobilised and all health facilities were on standby.

Title: Maharashtra: 25 killed as Pune-bound bus catches fire on Samruddhi Mahamarg Expressway in Buldhana
content: BULDHANA: As many as 25 people, including 10 women, were killed and eight others suffered injuries when a Vidarbha Travels bus travelling from Nagpur to Pune caught fire after an accident on the Samruddhi Mahamarg Expressway in Buldhana on Saturday.Maharashtra bus accident live updatesThe incident happened around 1.30am on Samruddhi Mahamarg near Pimpalkhuta village in SindhKhed Raja taluka of Buldhana district. The driver of the bus, a cleaner, and six passengers managed to jump out of the bus, while the rest were trapped inside and got charred.The ill-fated bus (MH 29 BE 1819) is said to have rammed into a crash barrier pole on the roadside to the right side before colliding once again with the wall of the underbridge. "A total of 33 people were travelling on the bus. The driver of the bus also survived and said that the bus overturned after a tyre burst leading to flames in the bus. Later the diesel tank of the vehicle caught fire," said Buldhana SP Sunil Kadasane.The SP added that there were three children among those who died. "The exact reason for the accident has not yet been ascertained," said the SP.Yashwant Solanke, SP, highway security police , said the passersby had alerted a traffic assistance booth located around 10km ahead. The booth, accompanied by a fire brigade team, arrived at the spot within 20 minutes. However, by that time, the damage had already been done, as the flames had spread rapidly and were beyond control."The police and fire brigade teams had tried their best to save the trapped passengers from the flames. But, it was too devastating," said Solanke.Buldhana police deputy SP Baburao Mahamuni said the injured were shifted to Buldhana Civil Hospital for treatment."Identification of the bodies is being done. We will hand over the bodies to the relatives after the DNA identification," said Buldhana district collector Dr HP Tummod.In the meantime, Nagpur Disaster Management Cell (8860018817) had opened its assistance window for helping the kin of the deceased and coordinate with Buldhana district administration. PM Modi announces 2 lakh ex-gratiaExpressing grief over the bus accident in Buldhana, PM Narendra Modi on Saturday announced an ex-gratia for the kin of the deceased."Deeply saddened by the devastating bus mishap in Buldhana, Maharashtra. My thoughts and prayers are with the families of those who lost their lives. May the injured recover soon. The local administration is providing all possible assistance to the affected," said PM Modi in a tweet."An ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh from PMNRF would be given to the next of kin of those who lost their lives in the bus mishap in Buldhana. Rs 50,000 would be given to the injured," he added. CM Shinde announces Rs 5 lakh ex-gratiaChief minister Eknath Shinde has expressed deep grief over the horrific accident in Buldhana district. Expressing condolences to the deceased and their families, the chief minister announced an aid of Rs 5 lakh each from the Chief Minister's Relief Fund to the relatives of the deceased.The chief minister also ordered an inquiry into the incident."As soon as the information about the accident came to light, the emergency medical service team deployed for the highway as well as the fire brigade reached the spot and started the rescue operation. The injured passengers were rescued and admitted to the hospital," said Maharashtra chief minister's office in a tweet.Maharashtra deputy CM Devendra Fadnavis said, "The state government will bear the cost of treatment of the injured. We are in touch with the district as well as the police administration and all kinds of help are being provided immediately."Union home minister Amit Shah too expressed grief over the loss of lives in the accident. "The road accident in Maharashtra's Buldhana district is heartbreaking. In this hour of grief, my prayers are with the families of those who lost their lives in this horrific accident. Quick treatment is being provided to the injured by the administration. I pray to God for their speedy recovery," tweeted Shah.Watch 25 passengers burnt to death after bus catches fire on Mumbai-Nagpur Expressway | Samruddhi Mahamarg accident

Title: Sr citizen from Nigdi run over by auto, dies
content:
Pune: A 65-year-old Nigdi resident was killed when a speeding auto hit him at Sainathnagar on Thursday.The deceased has been identified as Bansi Bhubal. His son Nitin (35) lodged a complaint with the Nigdi police.An officer from Nigdi police said that Bhubal was walking along the road when an auto knocked him from behind. The accident took place on the night of June 25. The elderly man succumbed to his injuries on Thursday night."The auto knocked him down near a temple and the driver fled the scenet," an officer said. The locals took Bhubal to a nearby hospital. The police are currently investigating the case. â€œWe have obtained CCTV footage from the spot to trace the vehicle,â€ the officer said. TNN

Title: Seven killed, 2 injured as SUV rams stationary truck in UP's Banda
content: JHANSI: Seven people were killed and two others sustained grievous injuries after the SUV they were travelling in ploughed into a stationary truck in Banda on Thursday night. Police said that the accident had taken place on Kamasin Road in Baberu police station area.Chief minister Yogi Adityanath condoled the death of the victims and directed officials for the best possible treatment of those injured.Police said that one Katlu (13) of Tilotha village had touched a live wire and was rescued. . .AdvertisementLater, his mother Saira Bano (37), along with locals Mohammed Kaif (18), Shakil (25), Mushahid (24), Raj Khengar (28), Jahid (27) and two others had got into an SUV and rushed Katlu to CHC Baberu, said police.SUV was cruising at 120 kmphAround 10 pm, when they reached Kamasin road, their vehicle rammed into a stationary truck,â€ said SO, Baberu, Santosh Singh. Singh said the driver of the truck managed to escape and hearing the noise of a loud thud, the locals informed the police. â€œGas cutters were used to pull out all the passengers with the help of locals and police and then they were taken to a local hospital where doctors declared them brought dead. Two who survived were referred to the district hospital. Their identities were being ascertained,â€ said Singh. Banda SP Abhinandan said that efforts to catch the errant truck driver were underway while prima facie it appeared that the SUV was cruising at 120 km per hour and the driver, who also died, failed to control the SUV. â€œWe are searching for the errant truck driver,â€ he said. Police said that Mujeeb was inconsolable as he had lost his wife Saira Bano, two sons Katlu and Mushahid in the accident. â€œPeople usually drive fast on highways and recently the government had ordered that no heavy vehicles will be allowed to park on roads, but all has fallen on deaf ears,â€ said Mujeeb while crying.

Title: Two killed, one injured as car hits two bikes in Visakhapatnam
content:
VISAKHAPATNAM: Two people were killed and another was injured after a speeding car hit them on the Telugu Thalli flyover in Vizag on Saturday night.Inspector of Two-Town police station, B Tirumala Rao said that Jaya Krishna (45) breathed his last while he was availing treatment in the hospital.Yashwanth was a native of Vizag and Jaya Krishna was a native of Vijayawada. The condition of another injured person Hari Kumar is said to be out of danger.The police said that one Kakani Charvak, a resident of Muralinagar locality in Vizag city, drove the car towards the Railway Station direction at high speed on the flyover and hit the two bikes.Soon after the accident, the local beat negligent driver Charvak before handing him to the police. A case has been registered against the driver and the investigation is on to ascertain whether the rider was inebriated at the time of the incident. The bodies were shifted to the morgue for postmortem.

Title: 2 killed in accident, family protests
content:
Trichy: Two people were killed and one sustained grievous injuries in a road accident in Ariyalur district on Friday. Condemning the fatal accident, the relatives of the deceased victims held a road blockade protest on Saturday.Police said that P Bhagyaraj, 38, working as a cook in a government hostel in Sendurai town was returning home in his two-wheeler on Friday evening. While riding on the Sendurai-Jayankondam highway, two women identified as S Amutha, 33, and P Amaravathi, 30 both relatives of Bhagyaraj hitchhiked in the two-wheeler. While the trio approached Maruvathur, a speeding tractor rammed them from behind. All three were thrown out of the two-wheeler. While Amutha died on the spot, Bhagyaraj succumbed to the injuries. He was referred from Sendurai government hospital to Ariyalur government medical college hospital. Amaravathi is under treatment at the Ariyalur government hospital. Meanwhile, as the tractor driver fled the spot by abandoning his vehicle, the relatives of two deceased victims conducted a protest in Sendurai. They demanded action against the driver and sought more doctors to be appointed at the Sendurai government hospital. They also sought compensation for the victimsâ€™ families. Sendurai police held a peace talk and assured action against the tractor driver. Based on the assurance, the protesters withdrew their stir.

Title: Six from Kalaburagi killed in accident
content:
Kalaburagi: Six persons â€” five women and a child â€” from Karnataka were killed in a road accident between Cruiser and a container at Shiraval Wadi village in Akkalkot taluk of Solapur district of Maharashtra. The deceased are Lalitha 50, Sundarabai 55, Rohini Pujari 40, Sainth Pujari, 10, Chaya, 46 and Sangitha Mane, 35.Two families The members of two different families in Annur village of Aland taluk in Kalaburagi district were returning from Pandarapur in Maharashtra. They had left their village early on Friday morning, and met with the accident when they were returning in the evening. They were just half-an-hour drive away from their homes when the accident happened.9 are under treatment in hospitalAround 25 people were travelling in Cruiser which rammed into a heavy container head-on. Nine others are being treated at a hospital in Solapur.

Title: 5-yr-old pillion rider killed in collision
content: Pune: A five-year-old girl riding pillion with her 32-year-old father on a scooter suffered head injuries and died later after a car collided head on with their vehicle on the Wadachiwadi Road in Undri around 1.35pm on June 29.The police have identified the deceased as Gatha Yadav from Wadachiwadi.The victimâ€™s father, Indrajeet Yadav, former gram sevak of Undri gram panchayat, is posted at the Mulshi panchayat samiti at present. He told TOI, â€œGatha and I rode out of the housing society to purchase flowers to offer prayers on Ashadi Ekadashi. We travelled a distance of about 150m. A car was coming from the opposite direction and the driver lost control over the vehicle, which collided head on with my bike.â€Yadav said he fell on the right side and escaped with minor injuries. Gatha fell towards the rear end of the scooter because of the jerk and sustained an injury to her head. â€œI rushed her to a private hospital, but she passed away while undergoing treatment. The accident happened a day before she was to appear for an interview to secure admission in UKG at a prominent English school in Undri on June 30.â€The car driver fled after the incident but local residents chased it and stopped the man at some distance, he added.Arun Mandhare (32), Yadavâ€™s friend, is the complainant in the case. He said, â€œYadav and his wife had spent weeks training Gatha for the school interview. The three were so excited. They had even purchased books, but she died in the tragic incident. Her funeral was performed at Nipani Road in Kolhapur.â€Investigating officer Dinesh Raskar from Kondhwa police said the Sassoon General Hospital in its advance post-mortem report has certified the cause of the victimâ€™s death due to head injury.Raskar said, â€œWe have seized the car for RTO inspection and issued a notice to the driver asking him to join police in investigation.â€ A complaint relating to causing death due to negligence has been lodged against the car driver under sections of the IPC as well as the Motor Vehicles Act.

Title: Youth killed as speeding trailer hits bike in Korba
content:
Raipur: A youth died on the spot after a speeding trailer hit the bike from behind on Saturday morning in Korba district. The accident took place in the Hardi Bazar police station area.The deceased was identified as Shankar Ram Patel, a resident of Baloda Bazar district, and was a Mason by profession. Police said he was headed towards Chaukabudha for his work around eight in the morning on Saturday when a speeding trailer coming from the opposite direction and heading towards the Baloda Bazar road hit Patelâ€™s bike. During interrogation, it was found that the trailerâ€™s driver had absconded leaving the trailer, while the deceased was sent for a further autopsy. The trailer was seized. The search for the absconded driver is still being done. TNN

Title: Flyover sanctioned at busy Nandur Naka chowk to ease traffic woes
content: NASHIK: The state government has sanctioned a flyover at the Nandur Naka crossroads to ease the traffic on Nashik-Aurangabad highway, a senior officer of the public works department said. According to the officials, Rs 50 crore has been sanctioned for the Nandur Naka chowk, which has two busy junctions just 20m apart. The place is becoming a major bottleneck on the Nashik-Aurangabad highway and is more accident-prone.Traffic from Nashik Road heading towards Adgaon has to cross this road in an â€˜Sâ€™ form. Vehicles coming from Nashik Road first join the Nashik-Aurangabad road at the first junction and then move 20m to the right before taking a left turn at the next junction to proceed towards Adgaon. â€œThis adds to the volume of vehicles on the road. Moreover, at night, there is the risk of heavy vehicles ramming each other while manoeuvring the junctions. Therefore, the government has sanctioned the flyover at this place as a priority,â€ said the officer. Once the flyover comes up, vehicles moving to and from Aurangabad will travel directly without any hitch. The PWD had proposed two flyovers â€” one at Nandur Naka and the other, barely 2km away at Mirchi hotel chowk. The Mirchi hotel chowk has seen several accidents in the past. A horrific accident on October 8, 2022, killed 13 people at the Mirch hotel junction. Talking about the Mirchi hotel chowk, the PWD officials said their proposal for this flyover is still with the government. Once it is sanctioned, the construction process will start there too. Various steps have been taken at the chowk, which is very wide, by installing safety measures, cat-eyes and speed bumps on the roads approaching it from all sides. Traffic signals have also been installed. The PWD officials said they were taking all measures to ensure there were no accidents at the Mirchi hotel chowk.

Title: Four killed, 9 injured in car-MUV crash
content: NASHIK: Four people died in a collision between a car and a multi-utility vehicle on the Vani-Saputara road that leads to Gujarat from Nashik on Friday evening. Nine others were seriously injured and are currently being treated at various hospitals, said Nilesh Bodake, the assistant police inspector of Vani police station. While seven are in stable condition, two are serious, he added. According to the police, the accident took place when a car, which was coming from Saputara and going toward Vani, collided with the MUV coming from the opposite side. All four occupants of the car died. While three people died on the spot, the fourth passed away while being taken to the hospital, the police said. The deceased have been identified as Vinayak Kshirsagar (37), Yogesh Wagh (18), Jatin Phawade (23), and Ravindra Chavan (22). All of them were residents of Motha Koliwada in Vani. Nine others, who were travelling in the MUV, have been admitted to private hospitals in Nashik city, the police added.The police officials said they had registered a case of accident. "As of now, an accident report has been filed. After receiving complaints, the people responsible for the accident will be booked under relevant sections of the IPC. All four occupants of the car died and were cremated on Saturday. Their relatives will take some time to register the complaints. Those injured are also recuperating. They too will be heard," said Bodake.The injured were first taken to Vani rural hospital. Medical officer Dr Vishal Mahale, MO of Pandane PHC Dr Prakash Deshmukh, Dr Anil Pawar of Vani Doctors' Association, Dr Anil Shelke, Dr Aher, Dr Yogesh Patil, Dr Vaibhav Mohite and other private doctors treated them.

Title: Two youths killed in road accident
content:
Prayagraj: Two youths died after the two-wheeler they were riding was allegedly hit by an unknown vehicle at Shastri Bridge on Saturday morning.After the collision, both the youths fell on the road and the other vehicle ran them over. The victims identified as Utkarsh Yadav (24), a resident of Bakshi Kala, Daraganj, was a student of BEd in Handia college. Utkarsh had his paper on Saturday morning. He was going to college on a two-wheeler with his friend Vikas Mishra (23), a resident of Bakshi Kala, when the accident took place. Vikas was driving the vehicle. Daraganj police reached the spot after receiving information. They later informed the family members of the two youths. As per Vikasâ€™s kin, he was pursuing BTech from NIT- Patna.Meanwhile, in a separate incident, a 35-year-old housewife, Rakha Yadav died after being electrocuted in her home under Holagrah police station of the district. According to police, Rakha mistakenly touched a live wire and was electrocuted.

Title: Two killed in Panaji, Chimbel road accidents
content:
Panaji: Two people lost their lives in two separate road accidents in Panaji and at Chimbel late Sunday night. In the first accident, Brij Mohan Yadav, a 35-year-old pillion rider, succumbed to injuries following an accident near the new Mandovi bridge at Panaji when the rider, Kamal Singh Chandrawat, skidded and fell while proceeding from Mapusa to Ribandar-Old Goa. Although the duo â€” both residents of Ribandar â€” was rushed to the GMC soon after the accident and Chandrawat was subsequently discharged, Yadav lost his life. Panaji police has booked Chandrawat in the matter.The second road accident occurred at Chimbel in which 30-year-old Ajay Prakash Kamble died of injuries after an autorickshaw that was proceeding from Chimbel to Ribandar dashed into him while he was heading in the opposite direction on his two-wheeler. Old Goa police said the rickshaw driver abandoned his vehicle and fled from the spot without taking the injured person to hospital or informing the police. Kamble, a resident of Chimbel, died while being moved to the GMC. The police consequently arrested the rickshaw driver Venkatesh Jadhav, 29, a resident of Santa Cruz, and later released him on bail.

Title: ASI killed, cops suspect hit-&-run
content:
Ludhiana: An assistant sub-inspector (ASI) was killed allegedly in a hit-&-run incident near Gurdwara Bhora Sahib on Kishanpura road. The deceased was identified as Jarnail Singh.Sidhwan Bet police have booked an unidentified person for causing death due to negligence.Cops said ASI Jarnail Singh was going to Sidhwan Bet on a motorcycle for some work. They added that near the gurdwara, his motorcycle met with an accident. Cops said another ASI was on his way back home, when he saw Jarnail Singh lying on the road in a seriously injured condition. His motorcycle was lying on the road near the spot. ASI Jarnail was rushed to Jagraon civil hospital where doctors declared him brought dead. Police officials, quoting the doctors, said the victim had suffered serious injuries in the head, which proved fatal.Sub-inspector Karamjit Singh, SHO, Sidhwan Bet police station, said an indicator light of another vehicle was lying on the spot which suggested that Jarnailâ€™s bike may have been hit by a vehicle. He said they have registered a case of causing death due to negligence against unidentified accused and are investigating the matter.

Title: Collision between BEST bus and auto on slippery patch kills couple
content: MUMBAI: A few hours after the BMC engineer surveyed the slippery patch on the Goregaon check naka bridge, two persons died after their autorickshaw collided with a BEST bus. The BMC officials surveyed the spot after the Vanrai police filed a complaint regarding a slippery patch. Had the rumblers been laid, at least 40 accidents, including four fatal ones could have been averted. The deceased couple have been identified as Johny Sankharam (42) and Sujata Panchaki (38), from Mira Road. "The duo was declared dead upon admission at Trauma Care Hospital. The autorickshaw driver survived with minor injuries," the police said. Vanrai police have arrested BEST bus driver Santosh Deolkar (53) for rash driving and causing death due to negligence. "I have written to the BMC to lay rumblers on the slippery patch, but no action has been taken. So far 40 accidents have occurred in a similar way. The civic officials who visited the spot-on Saturday said laying of rumblers is not feasible during rainy season as it will not get time to dry," said Vanrai police senior inspector RG Rajbhar.The accident occurred at around 1.45 pm when the two empty BEST buses were heading to Ghatkopar depot from Poisar depot for transfer. The BEST is conducting an internal probe. In Sunday's incident, they were BEST-owned buses driven by its own drivers, not wet lease buses. At least six people have died in BEST mishaps in a month. Two more accidents occurred at around 12 noon on Sunday on the same stretch.Meanwhile, several boulders fell during a major landslide at Karjat-Lonavala section of Monkey hill late Friday night. However, the rockfall barrier wall created by Central Railway recently helped preventing them from hitting the tracks, a senior official said on Sunday.

Title: 3 killed in road accidents in Nashik district
content:
Nashik: Three bikers were killed in two accidents in Nashik city and Chandwad on Sunday. Motorists responsible for both the accidents escaped from the scene and are booked on the charge of causing death due to negligence by the police.In Chandwad, three people were riding on a bike to Ganur village at around 8 pm, when they were hit by a car coming from the opposite direction. The police said that the car driver was on the wrong side of the road while trying to overtake a car ahead of him, when he ended up hitting the bikers. All the three bikers suffered serious injuries in the accident. Two of them, Balu Devram Jadhav (53) and Arun Uttam Gangurde (31) of Ganur village succumbed to the injuries whereas Sagar Thombre (25) of Parsul village, who was riding the bike escaped with injuries on his legs.In another incident, Bhimkumar Singh of Pathardi Phata was killed after he was hit by an unidentified motorist at around 11.30 am in Ambad industrial area. Police said the victim was returning home when he met with the accident. Singh suffered from serious injuries to his stomach and succumbed to them.

Title: Transgender, 21, killed after fall from two-wheeler
content:
Trichy: A 21-year-old transgender was killed in a road accident on Heber Road in Bhima Nagar in Trichy on July 1 after she lost control while passing over a speed breaker. According to police, S Akash alias Shalini of Ariyamangalam was riding a scooter near the district court at around 10.30pm. However, while crossing the speed breaker, she slipped from the vehicle and sustained head injuries. Though she was rushed to the Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Government Hospital (MGMGH), she was declared brought dead.Police registered a case and said that negligent driving caused the accident. However, activists said that all three speed breakers on the road have not been painted with white stripes. â€œNegligent driving may be a reason but the lapses in painting the speed breakers cannot be justified,â€ P Aiyarappan, a road safety activist said. Police were urged to enforce mandatory helmet compliance for riders. Meanwhile, the corporation has been urged to remove encroachments on Heber Road and widen the stretch as public transportation has not resumed. During the recent local body election, DMK had promised to reintroduce public transport on the stretch, but the promise remains unfulfilled. â€œHeber Road is chosen for a road redevelopment proposal. We will remove the encroachments and relay the road soon,â€ a corporation official said.

Title: Truck kills 13-yr-old returning home from school in Nangloi
content:
NEW DELHI: A 13-year-old boy was killed after a truck hit him on his way home from school near Nangloi metro station in Outer Delhi on Monday. The driver has been arrested.The death triggered a protest in the area. Local people kept the body on the road and tried to vandalise the truck. The boy, Mani Ram, lived with his mother in Nangloi. He was studying in Class 8 in a government school. His mother is a vegetable vendor. His father passed away a few years ago."The incident happened at 12.15pm at a traffic light. The child was crossing the road. The truck, driven at a rash speed, crushed the boy," an officer said. After hearing a loud noise, passersby rushed to the spot and saw the body lying in a pool of blood. They caught one of the occupants of the truck and handed him over to the police after beating him up.They also blocked the road that affected traffic movement for at least an hour..The medical report will confirm if the truck driver was intoxicated. A woman who was on the spot said: "There was no policeman at the spot to manage the traffic. It was horrific."Deputy commissioner of police (outer) Harendra K. Singh said a case was registered under the appropriate sections of IPC.

Title: Eight killed, 40 injured in separate accidents in UP
content: AGRA: Eight people were killed and at least 40 others injured in separate road accidents involving tractor-trolleys in Agra and Muzaffarnagar districts of Uttar Pradesh on Monday. In the first incident, five people were killed and 30 others injured in a collision between a car and a tractor-trolley on Agra-Delhi national highway under the jurisdiction of Farah police station of Mathura. According to police, the car rammed into the tractor-trolley from behind and overturned, killing three occupants inside, while one passenger of the trolley died on the spot. Another passenger of the trolley died during treatment.Those travelling in the Maruti Swift car were residents of Bharatpur district in Rajasthan and were on their way to visit an ailing relative in the Goverdhan locality. Meanwhile, 50 passengers on the trolley were part of a pilgrim turnout at Mathura on the occasion of Mudiya Mela which culminated on Guru Purnima on Monday. They were returning to Madhya Pradesh's Bhind district. SP City Martand Prakash Singh said, "Due to the collision, the tractor-trolley fell into an off-road ditch. Over two dozen people who got injured in the accident were taken to the district hospital. Bodies were sent for autopsy and six critically-injured were referred to SN Medical College in Agra. Family members of the deceased were informed. So far, no complaint has been received." The deceased pilgrims from Bhind were identified as Mukesh Kumar (34) and Gayadeen (63) while those from Bharatpur were identified as Rupesh Singh (45), his father Goverdhan Singh (65), and aunt Mukhtiyari (63). All the deceased come from a farming background, police said. In the second incident, three people died and nine persons were injured after a tractor-trolley, loaded with DJ equipment, was hit by a speeding dumper from behind on Delhi-Dehradun NH-58 in Muzaffarnagar.Those who died in the accident have been identified as labourers Rohit Singh (18), a resident of Ghaziabad, Anmol Kumar (27) from Meerut, and Ajay Kumar (22), a resident of Muzaffarnagar. The injured were shifted to the district hospital for treatment. SP Satyanarayan Parjapat said, "An FIR has been registered under IPC section 304A against the accused driver, who was also injured in the accident." Mansoorpur SHO Rojant Tyagi said, "Those who got minor injuries were sent to their home after providing first-aid. The bodies were sent for autopsy and further investigation is on."

Title: In Bengaluru, car mows down cop trying to help driver of stranded SUV
content: BENGALURU: Six car-borne youths, who set out on a trip to Nandi Hills after partying in the city, mowed down a police constable who was trying to help a cabbie whose SUV had broken down and was stranded in the middle of the road. The accident happened at Meenakunte Hosur village near Chikkajala on BIAL Expressway early Monday. One of the youths too was killed as the car hit the median before coming to a halt.The accident occurred at 1.50am when Suresh N, 40, a constable attached with Devanahalli police station, rushed to help Byregowda whose Toyota Innova Crysta had stopped on the middle lane of Doddajala flyover. Byregowda was explaining that his car had hit another vehicle before it broke down and he was waiting for assistance to tow his vehicle. At the same time, an allegedly speeding car coming from Bengaluru towards Devanahalli knocked them down crashing into the road median.The impact was huge. Suresh was hurled at least 10 feet away and died on the spot. Byregowda sustained severe injuries and slipped into a coma. All six people in the car sustained injuries. The seven injured were rushed to a nearby hospital. Sharath, 28, who was sitting next to the driver in the car, succumbed to injuries at the hospital. Sharathâ€™s friends Chandra Prakash, 30, Immanuel, 29, Priyadarshini, 26, Kejia, 24, and Sherin, 25, sustained severe injuries. Prakash was driving the car.DCP (traffic-north) Sachin Ghorpade said Chikkajala traffic police had registered a case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder under IPC section 304 against the driver as he was driving the car allegedly under the influence of alcohol. According to the FIR, all six passengers of the car prima facie looked like they had consumed alcohol. Police have collected their blood samples for scientific investigation.

Title: BEST bus jumps over drain, 7th accident since June 2
content: MUMBAI: In the seventh bus crash in the past month, a BEST bus crashed into the boundary of a nullah, jumped over it and dashed a nearby structure in Everard Nagar, Sion, at 3 am on Monday while the bus was being taken from one depot to another. Nobody was injured in the latest incident although it highlights the need to drive these red buses safely on city roads.Six people have died in mishaps involving BEST buses so far in the past month since June 2 as compared to an average 10 people dying every year in bus mishaps in Mumbai.Monday's incident involving a wet lease bus, comes close on the heels of two autorickshaw passengers being killed in a crash and one person being injured after a BEST bus hit them at Goregaon early on Sunday. This bus was also being ferried late at night for 'depot transfer'.Mumbaiâ€™s BEST was once literally considered one of the best public transport systems in the world. Sadly, in recent years not only has its service deteriorated but now even its safety track record has been called into question. The series of fatal mishaps involving BEST buses shows that the drivers are reckless and undisciplined. Many are seen driving rashly, cutting lanes and are a threat to motorists and pedestrians. Errant drivers should be cautioned by the BEST administration that they face dismissal if their reckless driving leads to deaths.Times View ReportA senior BEST official said that an advisory is being issued to all drivers of BEST-owned and wet lease buses to exercise caution while transferring empty buses late at night. "We transfer buses from one depot to another every three months, depending on need for additional buses at particular depots. This happens usually between 1am and 3 am," he said.Former BEST panel members demanded that safety of passengers and buses be a priority and there should be a curb on speed of buses while ensuring pedestrian safety. Two pedestrians died in bus mishaps in the past month.In most mishaps last year, drivers involved are those of wet lease buses and there is need to discipline them, demanded former BMC opposition leader and former BEST panel member Ravi Raja.BEST general manager Vijay Singhal had recently told TOI that he was analysing bus crashes for the past 1-2 years and would come up with directives to sensitise all drivers and conductors.

Title: Five killed in road accidents in city
content: AHMEDABAD: Rash driving in Ahmedabad resulted in five fatal accidents on Tuesday, with three of the deceased being elderly persons. Two of these accidents occurred on the SP Ring Road.In the first incident, a speeding car collided with an elderly man and woman from Kalol, who were riding a motorcycle, near Palm Hotel crossroads in Odhav on the SP Ring Road. The deceased, identified as Dalaji Thakor (70) and his relative Mangu Thakor (65), were residents of Moti Bhoyan village in Kalol. They were on their way to meet their religious guru in Hathijan when the accident occurred.Dalaji's son, Govind, reported in the FIR that the duo two at the scene while the driver of the car sped away. The police registered a complaint of causing death by negligent driving under the Indian Penal Code (IPC) and relevant sections of the Motor Vehicle Act.Another fatal accident took place on the SP Ring Road, where a 36-year-old man from Amraiwadi lost control of his scooter and died. Rahul Chauhan, as per the FIR filed with the I Division traffic police, was returning from Krishnanagar and lost control of his scooter on the Odhav flyover. He slipped on the road due to speeding and sustained fatal injuries.In Shahpur, a speeding bus belonging to the Ahmedabad Municipal Transport Service (AMTS) struck a 74-year-old man, leading to his death. Ghulam Hussain Momin, a resident of Shahpur, was riding his bicycle when the bus hit him. He died on the spot.In another incident in Bapunagar, a 77-year-old pedestrian died after being hit by a speeding autorickshaw driver on June 30. The victim, Sana Parmar from Bapunagar, was on his way to buy vegetables near Gareebnagar when the accident occurred.

Title: 3 children, 1 woman from family killed in Gurgaon road accident
content: GURGAON: A woman and three children were killed when a speeding canter truck rammed into a stationary car on the Delhi-Gurgaon Expressway near Jharsa Chowk, police said on Wednesday.Four others were injured in the incident. Police said a minor girl narrowly escaped from being hit by the canter. The accused driver fled from the spot after the accident which happened on the intervening night of Tuesday and Wednesday, they said.According to the police complaint, two men, three women and six children of a Ghaziabad-based family were heading towards Baba Mohan Ram Temple in Bhiwadi in a car. Due to a tyre puncture, they parked the on the side of the road. While the tyre was being changed, all women and children stepped out of the car, complainant Umesh Pal said.A speeding canter came from behind and hit them and fled away, he said. People gathered on spot who helped send the injured to the hospital, he added.Pal, a resident of village Bhopura in Ghaziabad, said the incident took place on the Jharsa flyover at 12:15 am. An FIR has been registered against the unidentified canter driver under Section 279 (rash driving), 336 (act endangering life or personal safety of others), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 304A (causing death by negligence) of IPC at Sector 40 Police StationAccording to the police, the deceased were identified as 26-year-old Satakshi, 2-year-old Prisha, 9-months-old Pari and 6-months-old Vidansh."We handed over the bodies to the kin after the post-mortem and took the canter in custody. We are trying to nab the accused driver," PTI quoted the SHO of Sector 40 police station as saying.With PTI inputs

Title: 3 farmers killed in road accident in Lalitpur
content:
JHANSI: Three farmers died and two others were injured when a tractor in which they were travelling lost its control and slipped into a roadside trench in Lalitpur district on Tuesday.The incident took place in village Todi falling under police station Baar where all the farmers were going to their farms for ploughing. The driver suddenly lost his control and as a result the tractor and the trolley overturned and three of the farmers came under it.Hearing the news, SO Baar police station Manoj Kumar Mishra along with police force rushed to the spot of accident and shifted the injured to the nearby CHC while the bodies were sent for post-mortem. The deceased have been identified as Gabbar (26), Sadhu Singh (500 and Shri Ram (45).

Title: 4 killed as speeding canter hits car on Delhi-Jaipur Expressway
content:
GURGAON: Four people were killed and four others were injured when a speeding canter hit a stationary car along the Delhi-Jaipur expressway in Gurgaon in the intervening night of Tuesday and Wednesday. "The incident took place when the car had stopped on the roadside to replace a punctured tyre," reported news agency PTI quoting police.The victims were members of a family from Ghaziabad who were on their way to a temple in Bhiwadi, police said, adding that a woman and two children died on the spot while another child died during treatment at a hospital. "Four other people sustained injuries," they said. The deceased were identified as Satakshi (30), Pari (2), Vidansh (3) and Prisha (2), all residents of Bhopura village in Ghaziabad. A case was registered against the canter driver under relevant sections of the law at the Sector 40 police station and the bodies were handed over to the family members after a post-mortem examination, police spokesperson Subhah Boken said.

Title: 4 dead, 8 injured in car accident in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri
content: RAJOURI: Four people, including three women, were killed and eight others sustained critical injuries when an Eeco vehicle carrying passengers fell into a deep gorge on Thanamnadi-Bhangai road in Thanamandi area of Rajouri early on Wednesday morning.â€œTwelve passengers were travelling in the vehicle when it met with the accident. Three persons died on spot, while one criticaly injured succumbed to injuries at sub-district hospital (SDH) in Thanamnadi,â€ said police sources.As per sources, the injured and the deceased had gone to attend a memorial service of a relative and were coming back to their homes. The deceased have been identified as Shamim Akhtar (55), Rubina Kouser (35), Zarina Begum (38) Azam and Muhammad Younis (38), all residents of Bhangai.The injured have been identified as Shaheen Begum (40), Zaitoon Begum (35), Shaheen Begum (45), Begum Jaan (50), Fatima Begum (60), Suriya Begum (35), Kulsoom Begum (40) and Muhammad Qasim (60), all residents of Bhangai-Thanamandi of Rajouri district.The sources said that eight injured passengers are being treated at Govt Medical College and Associated Hospital (GMC&AH) in Rajouri.Medical superintendent of GMC & AH in Rajouri, Dr Mehmood Hussian Bajar, said that eight critically injured patients have been admitted in the GNC Rajouri at around 3.45am.Dr Bajar added that one Muhammad Younis (38) succumbed to injuries on way to hospital and was declared brought dead.Watch J&K: 4 dead, 5 injured in car accident in Rajouri

Title: 3 youths killed after bike collide with truck in Satna
content:
Bhopal: Three youths were killed when their motorcycle collided with a stationary truck in Satna district late on Monday night. The accident occurred on the Satna-Chitrakoot road near Chitahra turn, under the jurisdiction of Majhgawan police station. The police have identified the deceased as Sitaram Kol, 48, son of Mangal Kol, a resident of Pindara village; Chhotelal Kol, 56, son of Chunuku, also a resident of Pindara village; and Chhotelal Kol, 28, son of Gangu Kol, hailing from Danditola village.According to the police, the victims were riding on a motorcycle when they collided with a stationary truck, resulting in the death of all three individuals. The bodies were taken to the hospital for post-mortem examination and were identified on Tuesday morning. The police have registered a case and are currently conducting a thorough investigation.Sources have revealed that the incident caused a traffic bottleneck on the road, leading to a subsequent collision between two other vehicles. No casualties were reported in this additional accident.

Title: Three road crashes in three states claim 18 lives
content: NASHIK/AGRA/HYDERABAD: Eighteen people were killed and 31 others injured, some of them critically, in three road accidents in Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh and Telangana in the last 24 hours.Ten people were killed and 27 injured after a stone chip-laden truck headed for Mumbai hit 11 vehicles on the Mumbai-Agra national highway and finally ploughed into a roadside dhaba and a bus stand at Palasner in Maharashtra's Dhule district around 10.30am Tuesday. The deceased include three school students and two women. The accident occurred 4km from the MP-Maharashtra border. Dhule collector Jalaj Sharma said it appears that the truck driver lost control over his vehicle due to a brake failure. In the second accident, six people were killed and three injured when a speeding car collided with an overloaded auto-rickshaw in Kheragarh in Agra district on Monday night. Agra police commissioner Preetinder Singh said primary investigation suggested that the car driver, Bunty Kumar, who fled the scene, was drunk. Three women on their morning walk were knocked down by a speeding car in Hyderabad on Tuesday. A mother-daughter duo died while the lone survivor is critical. A 19-year-old BBA student, Mohammed Bin Qadri, was behind the wheel. CCTV footage shows the car swerving at a bend at breakneck speed, mowing down the women before crashing into bushes 50m ahead. Watch Hyderabad: Mother, daughter killed as speeding car rams into morning walkers

Title: Truck kills 10 in Dhule after its 'brakes fail'
content: NASHIK: Ten people were killed and 27 injured after a stone chip-laden truck hit several vehicles on the Mumbai-Agra National Highway and finally ploughed into an eatery and a bus stand on Tuesday. The accident took place at Palasner in Dhule district at 10.30am. Three school students and two women were among the deceased. The injured persons are under treatment in Shirpur and Dhule. Dhule collector Jalaj Sharma told TOI that prima facie, it appears the truck driver lost control over his vehicle due to a brake failure. Chief minister Eknath Shinde and deputy CM Devendra Fadnavis have expressed their condolences. Shinde has also announced financial aid of Rs5 lakh for the relatives of those killed.CM Eknath Shinde called up Dhule collector Jalaj Sharma and SP Sanjay Barkund to seek more information on the accident and told them to provide immediate medical treatment to the injured people.Sharma said the condition of four of the injured people was critical.According to police, an open container truck, with a Rajasthan registration plate, was carrying stone chips. It was heading towards a cement company in Mumbai when thedriver lost control of the truck. The truck hit a series of bikes and cars on the road before ramming a road-side dhaba and a bus stand, where commuters were waiting.Dhule SP Sanjay Barkund said 11 small and big vehicles were damaged in the accident. The truck hit a car in which a couple, their two kids and the driver were travelling. API Jayesh Khalane told TOI that the deceased include the truck driver, his assistant, a woman travelling by acar, one woman standing at the bus stand and three kids, one aged seven years.Watch CCTV: At least 10 dead after truck hits vehicles on Mumbai-Agra Highway in Dhule

Title: Six killed after SUV rams into overloaded auto-rickshaw in Agra
content: AGRA: Six people died while three were critically injured when a speeding SUV collided with an overloaded auto-rickshaw around 11 pm on Monday under the jurisdiction of Kheragarh police station in Agra district.According to police, two people died on the spot while four others succumbed to injuries in hospital. The deceased were identified as Jai Prakash (45), his son Varun (10), wife Brajesh (38). Also, among the dead were two cousins - Manoj Sharma (45), Braj Mohan Sharma (60) - and the auto-rickshaw driver Bhola (30). The injured include Jai Prakash's eight-year-old son Sumit, Manoj Sharma's 16-year-old daughter Ananya and Dilip, 40. All the victims were locals, said police, adding that the SUV driver, Bunty Kumar, fled the spot.Commissioner of Agra Police, Preetinder Singh, said, "Preliminary investigation suggests that the car's driver was under the influence of alcohol and the accident took place when he was returning home after dropping his friends after a booze party. Two of his friends have been detained. The damaged car, Mahindra XUV 300, was recovered from the spot.""Directions have been issued to check overloading in auto rickshaws. Adding extra seats or any other sort of modification to adjust more passengers is a violation of the Motor Vehicles Act. Strict action, including dismantling autorickshaws, will be taken against repeat offenders," he added.Assistant commissioner of police (ACP) Mahesh Kumar said, "Based on a complaint filed by a relative of the deceased auto driver, an FIR has been registered against the car driver Bunty (35), a resident of Saiyan block, under IPC section 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 304 (causing death by negligence)."Jai Prakash, who used to work as a fruit vendor, was returning home with his family after attending the Mudiya Mela in Mathura. Manoj Sharma and Braj Mohan Sharma were returning from an Ashram in Morena district of Madhya Pradesh. They used to do farming, said officials.SHO of Kheragarh police station Rajeev Kumar said, "According to eyewitnesses, Bunty was recklessly driving on the wrong side of the road. He apparently lost control and rammed into the auto-rickshaw, which overturned. CCTV footage of the area is being examined."

Title: Triple-riding turns fatal for 3 engineering students in Telangana's Ibrahimpatnam
content:
HYDERABAD: Three engineering students riding a motorbike were killed after a speeding car rammed their two-wheeler in Ibrahimpatnam on Tuesday evening.The accident took place near a petrol bunk in Raipole village at around 5 pm. "The students were on their way to the college when the car knocked them down at great speed. They died on the spot with serious head injuries," Ibrahimpatnam inspector G Rama Krishna said. The students have been identified as Bhanu Prasad, Narayana Reddy and Naveen, all aged 20. Prasad was riding the bike. Police said the students were pursuing their engineering from a private college in Ibrahumpatnam. Police said that a case under section 304-A (negligence causing death) of IPC was registered against the car driver. The accused driver was reportedly taken into custody though police denied it.When the three students fell down on the road after the car hit them, locals rushed to their help. But, they were dead already, police said.

Title: Cops get training to improve road crash probe skills
content: BHUBANESWAR: Amid a spate of fatal road accidents in the state, the transport department on Wednesday launched a 20-day training programme for police personnel to enhance their skills to investigate such cases.Commissioner of police Saumendra Priyadarsi and transport commissioner Amitabh Thakur inaugurated the programme.â€œAs suggested by the Supreme Court committee on road safety, we will train investigators of 567 police stations of the state and 70 transport department officials on how to investigate accident cases. They will be trained on the use of electronic detailed accident report and integrated road accident database,â€ Thakur said. The transport department has invited experts from a Coimbatore-based institute to train police.During the training programme, officials tasked with ensuring safety of road users will learn ways to ascertain the cause of road accidents, corrective measures to be taken and effective monitoring.â€œAn investigator should immediately visit the accident spot and inquire from different angles. In many cases, cops normally draw a conclusion by attributing the accidents to rash and negligent driving,â€ Debu Nayak, a road safety campaigner said. Odisha recorded a 17% rise in deaths due to road accidents between January and March this year as compared to the first quarter in the previous year. The state reported three accidents in the last week of June when 20 people died. Five of a marriage party died after a truck rammed their vehicle in Keonjhar district. A couple and their child, who were on their way to a marriage, died on the spot when their car collided with a tanker. In Ganjam, 12 of a marriage party were killed when their bus collided with another.

Title: Cyclist killed in hit-and-run near Kanhai Chowk
content:
GURGAON: A 39-year-old sanitation worker was run over by an unknown vehicle near Kanhai Chowk while he was cycling back in the early hours of Wednesday. Indra Kumar, whose family said he was employed by MCG as a sanitation worker, succumbed to his injuries at a private hospital in the city. The accident took place around 2.20 am when Indra was going back home to Indira Colony in Sector 52, his brother-in-law Vijay Kumar told cops.â€œHe was near Kanhai Chowk and was taking a turn towards Ambedkar Chowk when an unknown vehicle rammed his cycle and sped away. Indra fell down on the road and was seriously injured. He was rushed to a private hospital where he succumbed to his injuries,â€ Vijay told cops at the Sector 53 police station.Investigators said on Wednesday that they got a call from the hospital about the accident victim. â€œWe are checking CCTV footage to know what kind of vehicle it was. We have registered an FIR and teams have been formed to nab the accused,â€ a police official said. An FIR has been registered under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304-A (death by negligence) of the IPC.

Title: 2 killed as bike rams wrongly parked truck
content:
Ludhiana: Two students died and one was injured after the motorcycle they were riding on rammed a wrongly-parked truck on Bandargah road near Lahera village here on Tuesday.The deceased were identified as Vipanjeet Singh, 19, and Naveen Muhammad from Ahmedgarh. Sarfaraz from the same area sustained injuries.The accident took place at around 2.30pm, when the trio was on its way to attend an IELTS class. Vipanjeetâ€™s father Kamaljit Singh said that on receiving information about the same, he rushed to the area and saw that his son had died on the spot and his two friends Naveen Mohamad and Sarfaraz were severely injured.He added that Naveen, who was taken to DMC hospital, died due to excessive blood loss during surgery. Meanwhile, police have registered a case under Sections 304-A (causing death by negligence) and 427 (mischief causing damage to the amount of fifty rupees) of the IPC against the driver of the parked vehicle, who is yet to be identified. tnn

Title: Woman, son killed, another injured as car falls into gorge
content:
UTTARKASHI: A woman and her son were killed after the car they were travelling in fell into a deep gorge at Badethi-Banchora Road in Uttarkashi district on Wednesday morning. The woman's other son, also in the car, was severely injured in the mishap. According to sources, the three members of a family from Indira village in Chinyalisaur block were returning home from Dehradun when their car fell into a 400-metre-deep gorge near Dharasu bridge. While 48-year-old Pawna Devi and her son Vikas Singh (22) died on the spot, her other son Bhupendra Singh (25) was severely injured in the accident. Soon after getting the information, a team of police and SDRF launched a rescue operation and recovered the bodies. Meanwhile, the injured person was sent to a community health centre in Chinyalisaur. Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer said, "The injured man has been referred to a higher medical facility in Dehradun. Bodies of the deceased have been handed over to their family after postmortem examination."

Title: 2 on bike trip to Ladakh crash on Yamuna e-way, 1 killed
content: AGRA: Two men from Nagpur on a biking trip to Ladakh met with an accident on the Yamuna Expressway near milestone 124 under Baldeo police station of Mathura district. One of them died in hospital later.  According to police, Prasad Bhore and Ankit Gaidhani (both aged 25 years) were on a Duke KTM 390 motorcycle. Prasad was riding pillion when he dozed off and lost his balance, causing the bike to wobble. Ankit, who was riding the bike, could not control it and the vehicle crashed on the road. Prasad Bhore suffered grave injuries and died during treatment at Mathura district hospital while Ankit Gaidhani escaped with minor injuries, police said. SHO of Baldeo police station Sanjay Kumar Tyagi said, "The two men had started from Nagpur around 8:30 pm on Friday and drove overnight to reach Agra, over 800 km away. Bhore rode for most of the distance during the night. When Gaidhani was riding the bike, an exhausted Bhore fell asleep on the pillion seat, causing the mobike to lose balance and it crashed on Saturday afternoon. No complaint has been received yet." "After the incident, Ankit informed his friends in Agra, following which two friends arrived on the spot and informed police. We called Prasad Bhore's father, who said that the two men were good friends, and refused to lodge an FIR. The body was handed over to the family after post-mortem examination," said sub-inspector Munendra Rathore. In May, a Dehradun-based YouTuber, Agastay Chauhan (25), died in a road accident while trying to touch 300 kmph on his sports bike on the Yamuna Expressway. He was riding a Kawasaki Ninja ZX-10R, a 1000 cc sports bike, when his bike crashed into a divider. The bike was shattered to pieces with the impact of the crash, killing Chauhan on the spot. Title: A bus careens into a gulch in southern Mexico, leaving 27 people dead
content:
MEXICO CITY: A passenger bus lost control and veered off a road and into a 75-foot-deep (25 metre-deep) gully in southern Mexico, killing at least 27 passengers, an official said.The crash occurred in the largely indigenous Mixteca region of the southern state of Oaxaca on Wednesday.The state's Interior Secretary, JesÃºs Romero, said 27 people had died in the crash, including a one-and-a-half-year-old toddler. He said that about 20 people were injured in the accident, some very seriously.Romero said the bus driver apparently lost control of the vehicle and plunged into the gully."It appears that a lack of skill and tiredness caused the accident," Romero said in remarks to a local television station.Photos distributed by police suggested the bus had flipped, totally crushing the passenger compartment.The bus set out from Mexico City to carry passengers to a number of tiny, remote mountain villages in the impoverished Mixteca region.The route, and the possessions, bundles and baskets strewn amid the wreckage, suggested the victims were people who worked in manual labour in the capital, and were returning to their home towns when the accident occurred.

Title: Akhilesh says BJPâ€™s claim of pothole-free UP a bundle of lies
content: Lucknow: Attacking the BJP government over its claims of making Uttar Pradesh roads â€œgaddha muktâ€ (pothole free), Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav on Wednesday said the campaign has turned out to be a bundle of lies. He said in many areas, the potholes are creating a challenge for the Kanwariyas who travel barefoot and might hurt themselves because of the boulders on roads coming out of these potholes.Akhilesh said monsoons have just begun and reports of people being killed in accidents caused by potholes and waterlogging are pouring in from all over the state. â€œThe BJP government in the state has spent six years and every year we come across government claims of making roads pothole free. Deadlines are set. But ground realities remain the same. The potholes remain unattended and so does the suffering of the commuters,â€ he said. On the contrary, crores of rupees are spent in the name of repairing roads only to add to the prevailing corruption in the government departments.â€œThe state government has showcased its Smart City project as a flagship scheme but road cave-ins are being reported from main city areas of the state capital itself, what to talk of other parts of the state. A road had caved in near Balrampur hospital, which resulted in blocking the way of ambulances heading to the hospital. Lucknow Nagar Nigam is facing waterlogging,â€ he said. The former UP CM said the stretch of highway between Jajmau Ganga bridge in Kanpur and Lucknow has thousands of potholes. â€œThe highway has claimed 90 human lives. Travelling on the 70 km stretch on Jhansi highway â€” between Barajord and Urai â€” is an even bigger challenge for expert drivers as well because of potholes and waterlogging,â€ Akhilesh said.

Title: One killed, two injured in road accident
content:
Kochi: A car crashed onto a roadside tree on the national highway stretch at Chakkaraparambu near Vyttila killing a 31-year-old man and leaving two persons with grievous injuries.The accident occurred around 10.50pm on Wednesday. Sources said that the car skidded before crashing onto the tree along the Vyttilla-Edappally stretch.The deceased has been identified as Anoop Venu of Adattu Road, Pala. Cheriyachan Francis, 37, and Manu, 26, both hailing from Pala were the injured. The car was severely damaged in the crash and passersby and authorities rescued them from the vehicle with great difficulty, police said. The trio were rushed to a nearby hospital where Venu succumbed to injuries. The body of the deceased was handed over to relatives after a postmortem on Thursday.Police suspect speeding to be a reason for the accident and CCTV footage would be inspected to find the exact cause. Palarivattom police have registered an FIR and launched a detailed investigation.In 2021, three including two models had died in a similar accident after their speeding car crashed onto a roadside tree along the same stretch.

content:

The deceased have been identified as S Mukilan, 44, and P Bharathi, 45, from the Bargur block development office. Mukilan was driving the bike while Bharathi was riding pillion.Bargur police said the incident took place near Devasamudram lake on the Salem-Bengaluru-Chennai national highway. â€œBoth Mukilan and Bharathi sustained grave head injuries and died on the spot,â€ said a police official.TNN

Title: Senior citizen killed in Nashik road accident
content:
Nashik: A senior citizen, grievously injured in an accident on Tuesday, died on Wednesday while crossing Gangapur Road. The victim, identified as Rukmini Dirawani, (75) resident of Gangapur Road, was crossing the road on Tuesday when she was knocked down by a speeding car, the Sarkarwada police said. â€œAs of now we are gathering the clues of which vehicle might have caused the accident. We have identified a couple of vehicles that could possible have caused it. The investigation is still on,â€ said the police officer. The police said that they were taking up the footages of the CCTVs set ups on the Gangapur Road near the site of the incident.Sarkarwada police has registered a case of death due to negligent driving against an unidentified driver of the vehicle that had knocked her down. She was taken to hospital where she was being treated for the injuries in head and chest but she succumbed to injuries on Wednesday.

Title: Vegetable vendor killed as auto hits divider in Chakan
content:

The vendor, Badshah Shaikh (58) of Walhekarwadi, was driving his vehicle to Chakan market to purchase vegetables.â€œShaikh lost control while driving along the road at Nanekarwadi. Title: 3 killed, 6 injured as bus carrying people to PM Modi's mega rally in Raipur collides with truck
content: About the AuthorRashmi DroliaFollow OnRashmi is a Special Correspondent with The Times of India in Chhattisgarh. She covers Politics, Left Wing Extremism, Crime and Human Rights among other areas of news value.

Title: Four killed in 4 separate accidents
content: RAJKOT: Four people died in four separate road accidents in Jamnagar and Rajkot districts on Thursday. In all the four incidents, the accused drivers fled and were yet to be arrested. According to an FIR registered at Panch B division police station of Jamnagar, one Hemal Patel (41) died after he was hit by a tanker when he was crossing the road. He died on the spot, while the tanker driver fled. In another complaint registered at the same police station, one Hemant Pambhar (60) who was riding a motorcycle was hit by a mini truck between Lalpur Chowkdi and Modpar village. He died on the spot. The truck driver fled.A third complaint of hit and run was registered at Kalawad town police station in Jamnagar district. According to the complaint, 50-year-old Jesing Khandhera was going to Dadvi village on his bike that was hit by a car on July 4. He was taken to a hospital where he died during the treatment on Thursday evening. The car driver who had fled was yet to be arrested. The fourth complaint of accident was registered at Ajidam police station in Rajkot. According to it, 30-year-old Sanjay Sadamiya was going on foot on the night of July 5 when he was hit by a vehicle the description of which was yet to be ascertained. He was taken to a hospital where he died during the treatment on Thursday evening.

Title: Couple killed as mini truck rams into bike
content:
GURGAON: A 35-year-old man and his 30-year-old wife were killed in a road accident near Sihol village of Palwal on Thursday night.Police said that both were returning to Faridabad on a motorcycle after meeting their family members in Uttar Pradesh when a mini-truck rammed into their bike, killing both on the spot.Following a written complaint by the manâ€™s father, who identified the bodies, the police registered an FIR under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 304A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC on Friday at Chand Hut police station. â€œWe are checking CCTV footage to know more about the accident,â€ said a police officer.

Title: Four-year-old boy killed in hit and run at Kudasan
content:
Ahmedabad: A speeding car hit a scooter driven by a woman with her son (4) and daughter (7) riding pillion in Kudasan on Thursday. The four-year-old boy was killed in the accident. According to an FIR filed at Infocity police station, 32-year-old Dharti Thakkar from Kanam Residency in Kudasan, with her daughter Jency and son Mantra, were going to drop Jency off at her school on a scooter.At around 4.10pm on Thursday, when they had just left their housing complex, a car hit them. Dharti phoned her husband, Jigar Thakkar, who runs a dairy products shop in Gandhinagar, and told him about the accident. Jigar, the complainant in the case, in the FIR said that Mantra suffered severe injuries to his head and was unconscious after the accident. Dharti and Jency suffered less severe injuries.Dharti rushed Mantra to a nearby hospital. He was then referred to another private hospital, where the doctor declared him â€˜brought deadâ€™. Dharti and Jency were treated at Gandhinagar Civil Hospital.Infocity police filed a complaint of causing death by negligent driving, rash driving and causing hurt by negligent act as to endanger human life against an unknown man.

Title: 1 dead, 2 hurt in accidents
content:
Kolkata: One person died and two others were killed in two separate accidents across the city on Friday. The first accident was reported from Canal West Road where Nitai Chandra Saha (64) was hit by a truck. He was declared dead at RG Kar Hospital. The truck driver has been arrested. In the second accident, biker, Sarafat Khan (27), and his pillion rider, Sara Khatun, both from Bright Street, were injured after their two-wheeler skidded on the AJC Bose flyover-Maa flyover link. Title: 35-yr-old, wifekilled as loaderhits their bike
content:
Kanpur: A husband-wife duo was killed after a speeding loader hit their bike near Tenapur turn in Ghatampur area of the district on Friday. Bike rider identified as Raju (35) of Seodhari Tadwadha and his wife Seema Devi (33) died in the accident.Angry over the accident, the villagers blocked the Kanpur-Sagar highway for nearly two hours causing traffic snarls.A police team reached the spot as soon as the information was received. The kin demanded to call senior district officials. After some time the joint magistrate reached the spot and assured the family to take action, but the protesters did not agree. Later, when the magistrate gave written assurance, the road blockade was lifted.Raju son Sughar Singh, a resident of Seodhari Tadwadha village of Ghatampur police station area, was returning to the village with his wife Seema Devi (35) carrying a cylinder on a motorcycle. A postal parcel loader coming from Ghatampur side hit the bike near Tenapur turn of Ghatampur police station area. Raju and Seema suffered serious injuries and died in the accident.

Title: Six killed in road accident in Andhra Pradesh's Tirupati district
content: TIRUPATI: Six persons were killed in a ghastly road accident reported near Srikalahasti in Tirupati district on Sunday.According to the local police, a group of seven persons from Vijayawada were on a pilgrimage to various shrines in Tirupati district.The group of devotees were heading to the Srikalahasti temple on Sunday morning when a speeding truck rammed into their car in a head-on collision near Mitta Kandriga village.The impact of the accident was so severe that the front portion of the vehicle got completely smashed up and got jammed with the truck.While six persons died on the spot, one survivor was immediately rushed to the local government hospital for treatment.The deceased were identified as Narasimha Murthy, Ramesh, Rajya Lakshmi Sri Latha, Akshaya and Venkata Ramanamma, all residents of Vijayawada. The survivor was identified as Bharat.Police registered a case and shifted the bodies of the deceased for postmortem to Tirupati. Meanwhile, former Chief minister N Chandrababu Naidu expressed anguish over the accident which claimed six lives. He appealed to the state government to extend appropriate support to the bereaved family.

Title: Woman among 8 killed as bus rams LMV in Jind
content:
JIND: A woman was among eight people killed while nine others were grieviously injured following a head-on collision between a Haryana roadways bus and a Cruiser (LMV) near Bibipur village on Bhiwani Jind-road on Saturday.Jind police SP Sumit Kumar said the accident took place when the bus driver was trying to overtake another vehicle. A case of rash driving has been registered against the driver. The deceased have been identified as Ravi (32) from Madanheri village, Bimla Devi from Bhaklana village of Hisar district, Manoj (45), Hardeep (37), Sukhwinder (30), all are from Mundhal village of Bhiwani district, Sanjay from Siwan village of Kaithal, and Rahul (21) from Dadri district. One passenger is still missing. All the passengers who died were travelling in the LMV.The Haryana Roadways bus left the Jind bus stand at around 9 am and the accident occurred barely 15 km from the bus stand. According to deputy civil surgeon Rajesh Bhola, seven people were brought dead to the hospital, one succumbed in hospital, and eight of the injured were referred to PGIMS Rohtak for treatment.

Title: Vehicle rams auto in Adilabad, 4 die
content: HYDERABAD: Three members of a family, who were returning after offering prayers at a church, an auto driver were killed and five others injured when an unidentified vehicle rammed the auto in which they were travelling at Mekalagandi village on Hyderabad-Nagpur national highway in Adilabad district on Saturday.The deceased have been identified as Enugula Gangamma, 53, D Sailaja, 34, M Sambaiah, 63, and auto driver Y Pochanna, 50. Under the impact of the crash, the auto fell into an open stormwater drain along the road. Passers-by rushed to their help and alerted police. Cops called an ambulance to shift the injured by pulling them out of the auto. The injured persons were M Deepak, 14, T Teja Varhan, 13, M Prem Sagar, 40, three-year-old D Aradhaya and eight-month-old Dasari Chinni. Condition of two of them is said to be critical. The injured persons were shifted to Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Medical Sciences in Adilabad. Officials said there were no CCTV cameras at the accident spot. As per information given by locals, police suspect a lorry hit the autorickshaw. Members of three families, who are relatives, hired the auto at Ichoda after offering prayers at a local church. â€œThey hail from Adilabad and the accident took place when they were returning to their homes,â€ police said, adding a case has been registered.

Title: Heavy rain wreaks havoc in Himachal Pradesh, five people killed; 11 houses damaged, several vehicles and bridges washed away
content: SHIMLA: Incessant heavy rainfall in most parts of Himachal Pradesh for over 24 hours has wreaked havoc. In all five people have been killed, seven-eight were stranded in different parts of the state. Besides, 11 houses, one hotel and two vehicles have been damaged. An Incident of landslide occurred at Madholi village in Kumarsain area of Shimla district in which three persons died after their house collapsed. Across the state, 736 roads are closed including 3 national highways.Another death was reported from Kakiyan under Chamba tehsil in the landslide incident where one person was later buried in the landslide and found dead. A woman was buried under the debris in the landslide that occurred at Lankabekar area of Kullu. A rescue operation was launched to save her but she was found dead.Chamba-Pathankot road blocked near Chaned.In the flash flood incident in Vridashram Sukhsagar in Dobhi area of Kullu district, six -seven people were trapped. After heavy rainfall, the Pandoh Bazar of Mandi district was submerged, and some people were trapped in their houses due to excessive discharge of water from Pandoh Dam. The State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) team along with necessary equipment was deployed for carrying out rescue operations. Six persons including one female and a physically challenged were successfully rescued by the SDRF team.Six buildings were damaged in the landslide that occurred at Village Sari chhaki Mode under Sub Division Kasauli in Solan district.Trains running on the heritage Kalka-Shimla and Ambala-Una tracks have also been stopped due to landslides. Electricity supply has also been affected in most parts of the state.In Kullu district, the Beas river and its tributaries including Parvati and Tirthan rivers are also on spate. Beas river has wreaked havoc right from Bahang, Manali to Mandi. Flooding Beas has washed away several bridges and vehicles while Mandi-Manali national highway has also been damaged at several places.The road is closed due to landslide near six along Mandi-Manali national highway. Even the Mandi-Manali road via Kandi-Kataula is also blocked due to landslides at Ghoda Farm near IIT Kamand.Mandi-Manali roadGovernment and private property worth Rs 365 crore has been damaged in the state since June 24. So far, 45 people have died and 80 have been injured. 44 have died due to road accidents and falling off decks, while one has died due to flash-flood.

content: TIRUPATI: At least six persons died and one sustained injuries in a ghastly road accident reported near Srikalahasti in the district on Sunday.The deceased were identified as Narasimha Murthy, Ramesh, Rajya Lakshmi Sri Latha, Akshaya and Venkata Ramanamma, all residents of Vijayawada. Meanwhile, former chief minister N Chandrababu Naidu expressed anguish over the accident. He appealed to the state government to extend appropriate support to the bereaved family.

Title: Police naik killed in bike accident
content:
Nashik: A 41-year-old police naik attached with the Upnagar police station died after suffering serious injuries in a road accident in the Upnagar area on Saturday afternoon.The deceased has been identified as Sachin Vatane, a resident of Dwarka Nagari, Old Saikheda Road, Nashik Road.The Upnagar police said Vatane was riding his bike in the Jail Road area around 4.30pm on Saturday when he fell off the bike and suffered serious injuries. He was immediately shifted to a private hospital where he was declared brought dead. He is survived by his parents, wife, a 14-year-old daughter and a 10-year-old son.Police said Vatane had joined the police force in Mumbai, and after a few years, he got a transfer to Nashik city. Before joining the Upnagar police station, he worked at the police headquarters of the city police and then the Nashik Road police station.

Title: 2 killed, 5 injured in accident on Biswanath bypass
content:
Guwahati: Two people died while five sustained critical injuries in Biswanath, Assam on Sunday. Police said a cargo van with the license plate AS25-EC-6149 lost control and overturned on the Biswanath bypass while it was going to a weekly market.Muzahidul Islam, one of the passengers, died on the spot while other passengers were taken to a hospital in Tezpur for treatment.Another critically injured person succumbed to injuries in the hospital while other injured are undergoing treatment at the hospital.On Saturday, chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma expressed concern over the rising fatal road incidents in the state. â€œIn June 23, the number of road traffic accidents increased compared to June 22. There were a total of 555 accidents, compared to 500 in June 2022. Additionally, the number of fatalities increased to 255, compared to 235 last year. I am deeply concerned,â€ Sarma had tweeted. agencies

Title: Woman dies as car hits parked bus in Tamil Nadu's Chengalpet district
content: CHENNAI: A 40-year-old woman was killed while her husband and two daughters survived after the car they were travelling in slammed into a parked government bus at Acharapakkam in Chengalpet district early on Sunday morning. The car driver escaped with injuries, police said.The family was travelling to Tuticorin along the Chennai-Trichy highway when the accident happened.A government bus proceeding from Acharapakkam to Orathi was parked along the highway. The driver of the car could not control his speeding vehicle and rammed it into the bus, eye-witnesses told police. The car was completely damaged.Passerby rushed to the rescue of the occupants and alerted the authorities. The occupants in the car were moved to a hospital nearby where the woman, Thanga Megala, was declared dead-on-arrival.. .AdvertisementHer husband, Muthu Durai, 45, and their two daughters are under treatment. Acharapakkam police recovered the body and sent it to Government Hospital for postmortem. Local residents alleged that government buses should enter Bazaar in Acharapakkam and pick passengers from the bus stop, but several buses do not enter the town and board passengers on the highway itself after stopping in the middle of the road, leading to accidents like this one. A probe is on.

Title: 6 pilgrims killed as truck hits car in Tirupati
content:
TIRUPATI: Six people were killed and one injured in a road accident near Srikalahasti in Andhra Pradesh on Sunday, reports Sandeep Raghavan. A group of seven people from Vijayawada, who were on a pilgrimage to various shrines in Tirupati, were driving towards the Srikalahasti temple, when a speeding truck rammed into their car near Mitta Kandriga village.The car was mangled with its front portion wrecked and jutting into the truck after the accident. While 6 passengers died on the spot, 1 survived and was rushed to the local government hospital.The deceased were identified as Narasimha Murthy, Ramesh, Rajya Lakshmi, Sri Latha, Akshaya, and Venkata Ramanamma, all residents of Vijayawada.The lone survivor was identified as Bharat. Police registered a case and sent bodies of the victims for autopsy. Former CM N Chandrababu Naidu appealed to the state government to extend appropriate support to the bereaved family.
