Title: Kanwariya, 22, killed in road accident
content:
RUDRAPUR: A 22-year-old kanwariya lost his life in a collision between a speeding truck and the bike he was riding while en route to Haridwar to collect Gangajal. His friend, who was riding pillion, sustained severe injuries. The deceased has been identified as Badal Singh, a resident of Subhash Colony in Rudrapur.According to the police, Badal and his friend Shivam, accompanied by two others on another bike, were travelling to Haridwar on Sunday night when the accident occurred near the toll plaza in the Kunda police station area in Kashipur around 3 am. Upon receiving the information, the police reached the spot, arrested the truck driver and seized the vehicle involved.Kunda police station in-charge Dinesh Chandra Fatryal said "The truck driver has been arrested and charged under relevant sections of the IPC."

Title: Kanwariya killed, four family members injured in road accident in Jharkhand's Dumka
content: DUMKA: One 'Kanwariya' died and four other members of his family sustained injuries in a road accident in Jharkhand's Dumka district on Tuesday, a police officer said. The accident took place when the SUV they were riding in struck a roadside tree on the Dumka-Bhagalpur route, said Rohit Kumar, officer-in-charge of Hansdiha police station.They were all natives of Telari village in Rohtas district of Bihar and were going home after visiting the well-known Basukinath shrine, the police officer said.The deceased was identified as Rahul Sharma. He was killed on the spot.The four seriously injured persons - Govind Sharma, Ritesh Sharma, Saroj Sharma and Kameshwar Sharma - were rushed to Community Health Centre in Saraiyahat in Dumka. The doctors, however, referred them to a hospital in Deoghar district. One member of the family escaped unhurt.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: 12 killed in road accident in Pratapgarh
content:
PRAYAGRAJ: At least 12 people, including four women and one kid, died and five others were injured when a speeding tanker hit and fell on an auto-rickshaw carrying 17 passengers near Sukhpal Nagar-Mohanganj road under Leelapur police station of Pratapgarh on Monday, police said.The accident also affected vehicular movement and additional police force was deployed to clear the route.The accident took place when the tanker coming from Lalganj hit the auto-rickshaw that was on its way to Jethwara. Locals told police that the driver of the tanker in an attempt to save a motorist lost control over the vehicle and first hit the auto-rickshaw and later tumbled upon it.

Title: Four killed in three separate accidents in Trichy, Thanjavur
content: Trichy: Four people, including a mini truck driver and a cleaner, were killed in three separate road accidents in Trichy and Thanjavur districts. In one of the accidents, the driver, P Arumugam, 45, and T Thamilselvan, 50, both from Thiruchotruthurai near Thiruvaiyaru, were returning to their native after unloading vegetables at Kamaraj market in Thanjavur around 11.30pm on Sunday. â€œArumugamâ€™s mini truck collided with a truck laden with cement bags coming from Ariyalur near Kandiyur. Both men died on the spot,â€ said a police officer attached with the Nadukavery police. After being informed, police rushed to the spot and sent the bodies to a government hospital for postmortem. Police also secured the driver of the other truck, Sudhakar, 42, of Vadakadu in Pudukottai district. In another accident, 47-year-old Vijayakumar, a resident of Kaliyampatti in Thuraiyur, was killed after the two-wheeler he was riding rammed a roadside tamarind tree on Musiri-Thuraiyur state highway on Sunday. His wife, V Nalayini, 36, who was riding pillion, was injured. The Jambunathapuram police said Vijayakumar lost control over his vehicle. They sent the body to the Thuraiyur government hospital for postmortemIn yet another accident on Sunday, S Jayaram, 60, a native of Kurumbapatti near Thuraiyur, was riding pillion with his son J Selvam on Musiri-Thuraiyur road. â€œSelvam suddenly applied the brakes. Jayaram lost balance and fell on the road. He suffered severe head injuries and died on the spot,â€ a police officer said.Police personnel from the respective station limits registered cases and initiated inquiries into the accidents.

Title: 2 kids among three dead in road accident
content:
Nagpur: Three persons, including two children, were killed in an accident on the Ramtek-Bhandara Road when the car they were travelling in rammed into a stationary truck from behind on Sunday. The family was returning to Bhandara after visiting a temple in Ramtek.The victims were identified as Parasram Bhendarkar, his eight-month-old grandson Himanshu and seven-year-old kin Bhargavi Bondre.The car was being driven by Himanshu's father Rajesh, who along with wife Durga, is posted with Bhandara police. The couple too were injured in the accident. Airoli police shifted the injured to a district hospital at Ramtek. They were later shifted to the Government Medical College and Hospital (GMCH) in the city, where the three were declared dead by the doctors.Airoli police registered an offence against the driver of the stationary truck. Police said the truck was parked on the roadside without the parking lights being on.. .Advertisement

Title: Nashik traffic cops fine over 4k helmetless bikers during drive
content: Nashik: The traffic branch of the city police has penalised over 4,000 bikers for violating rules in a special drive that began on June 28. The police, up to July 9, has penalised a total of 4,359 bikers for riding without helmet and issued challans amounting to Rs 21 lakh. Another 454 riders were slapped with fines totalling Rs 4.5 lakh for triple riding.During the drive, the police came across 88 repeat offenders, whose licences were impounded and a proposal has been sent to the RTo seeking their suspension or cancellation.ACP Sunil Bari of the city traffic branch said, â€œWe expect that motorists are following all the traffic rules. We took up the drive to target riders without helmet and triple riding as cases of accidents are on the rise in the city.â€ â€œA significant number of people either getting killed or injured in accidents are two-wheeler riders. Hence, it is necessary that riders not only follow traffic rules but also wear helmets, as mandated by teh Motor Vehicles Act,â€ he added. During the first five months of 2023, a total of 80 persons lost their lives in road accidents in the city. During the corresponding period last year, the death toll stood at 86. The data provided by the city police said the bulk of the victims who died in road accidents between January and May this year were two-wheeler riders which was 50. Moreover, 21 pedestrians lost their lives during the same period.

Title: 12 killed as tanker falls on auto-rickshaw in Pratapgarh

The accident also affected the vehicular movement and additional police force was deployed to clear the route.While eight died on the spot four others succumbed during treatment.

Title: Seven people killed after bus carrying marriage party falls into Sagar Canal in Andhra Pradesh's Prakasam district
content: AMARAVATI: At least seven people died and 30 others were injured after a bus carrying a marriage party fell into a canal in Darsi area of Andhra Pradesh's Prakasam district, on Tuesday early morning.According to the police, around 37 people who all are relatives, were travelling from Podili to Kakinada. They attended a marriage and hired an RTC bus from Ongole depot to attend a reception.The deceased have been identified as as Abdul Azeez (65), Abdul Hani (60), Shaik Rameez (48), Mulla Noorjahan (58), Mulla Jani Begum (65), Shaik Sabeena (35), and Shaik Heena (6). Those injured have been rushed to Darsi government hospital.The accident occured when another bus came from the opposite side and the bus driver, in a bid to avert collision, hit a concrete block on the road side and then lost control.The chief minister has ordered the officials to ensure better medical care to the injured.

Title: 2 killed in separate accidents on Trimbak Road
content:
Nashik: Two people were killed in separate road accidents on Trimbak Road between Sunday and Monday.  A 30 year-old-man riding a bike was killed when hit by a car on Monday afternoon. According to the Satpur police, the identified as Kamlakar Pardhi (30) resident of Pimpalgaon Bahula was riding his bike towards the Trimbakeshwar side with his wife and two children, when he rammed into a car coming on to the road through an opening in the road divider at around 3.30 pm. Pardhi suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to the same. The Satpur police said that his wife and children also suffered injuries and are admitted in the Nashik civil hospital. In another incident, a 24-year-old biker was killed when he lost control over his vehicle and rammed into the road divider. The accident took place at around 9 pm on Sunday. The deceased, Aman Pathan of Pimpalgaon Bahula was riding his scooter on the Trimbak Road when he apparently lost control over his scooter and rammed into a road divider. He suffered serious injuries in the accident. He was rushed to a private hospital in the city where he succumbed to the injuries on Monday morning. The police have so far registered cases of accidental deaths for both incidents.

Title: Seven killed, 18 injured as bus plunges into irrigation canal
content:
Vijayawada: Seven people died and 18 others were injured in a fatal road accident near Darsi in Prakasam district in the early hours of Tuesday. A group of 37 people were travelling in the APSRTC bus from Podili to Kakinada when the accident occurred.Police said the driver of the RTC bus lost control of the wheel while attempting to avoid hitting an oncoming private bus. The bus hit the roadside railing and plunged into the Nagarjunasgar irrigation canal. The bus fell on the edge of the canal. The casualty would have been higher had the vehicle got completely submerged. The deceased were identified as Abdul Azeez (65), Abdul Hani (60), Shaik Rameez (48), Mulla Noorjahan (58), Mulla Jani Begum (65), Shaik Sabeena (35), and Shaik Heena (6).Prakasam district SP Malika Garg visited the accident site and supervised relief and rescue operations. She said the victims had attended a marriage at Podili in Prakasam district on Saturday, and hired the bus to attend the marriage reception in Kakinada on Tuesday. The accident occurred soon after they left Podili for Kakinada.The victims were fast asleep and fell on one another as the bus plunged into the canal.

Title: 1 killed, 19 injured as Akola-bound bus falls into valley in Nashik district
content:
NASHIK: A woman was killed and 19 other passengers suffered injuries when a state transport bus fell about 250 feet down into a valley on Saptashrungi Gad, 60km from Nashik, on Wednesday morning.According to officials of the Maharashtra State Road Transport Corporation (MSRTC), the bus carrying 35 passengers started from Saptashrungi Gad and was heading to Khamgaon in Akola district. While negotiating the turn, known as Ganapati Point, the bus driver apparently lost control over the vehicle and it fell into the valley.On getting information, the police went to the accident spot.Kalwan police with the help of the local citizens began the rescue work and started shifting the injured people to the hospital."The injured people have been shifted to the Wani rural hospital," said Kalwan police.

Title: 2 killed as car hits truck near Chakan
content:
Pune: Two employees of a private company died after the car they were travelling in crashed into a container truck plying ahead along Chakan-Shikrapur Road at Mohitewadi on Sunday night.The Chakan police have identified the victims as Aditya Memane (27) of Kuruli in Khed taluka and Amol Lomate (32), a resident of Osmanabad district. The police have registered a case against Memane for negligent driving.Sub-inspector Vinod Shendkar of the Chakan police said that the accident occurred around 11.30pm, when the victims were travelling in the compact sedan towards Chakan from Shikrapur.â€œWhen they reached Mohitewadi, the car crashed into the rear side of a container truck plying ahead. Memane and Lomate died on the spot," Shendkar said.The police said the driver of the container truck, who was not at fault, had stopped the vehicle at the accident spot.

Title: Flood fury kills 25 in a day in North India, 13 of them in Uttar Pradesh
content: At least 25 fatalities were reported Wednesday in the rain-battered northern states of Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Punjab, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, taking the overall death count from this monsoonâ€™s relentless rain, landslides and flash floods in the region to 116.UP registered the most deaths at 13 in the past 24 hours. â€œNine people drowned, while lightning strike, snakebite and house collapse killed one each,â€ said an official. The weather office issued a â€œyellowâ€ alert for 58 districts, predicting heavy rainfall Thursday.300 people stranded at Chandratal Lake in Lahaul and SpitiAmid the growing death toll, rescue and evacuation efforts gained pace, especially in Himachal, the worst-hit state. Around 25,000 people stranded in Kullu district, including around 3,000 in the Kasol area, have been evacuated safely, CM Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu said. Among them were 52 schoolchildren stuck at Sissu in Lahaul and 70 girls from colleges of Maharshi Dayanand University (MDU) in Haryanaâ€™s Jind, who were marooned in Solang valley as landslides blocked the road to Manali.But rescuing nearly 300 people, mostly tourists, from tented camps near Chandratal lake, located at an altitude of 14,100 feet in Lahaul and Spiti district, has been facing various challenges because of bad weather the past four days. Teams were working in sub-zero temperature and in 3-4 feet of snow to repair the road leading to Chandratal. Around 12km of the road was restored Tuesday, another 25km remained.Relief camps have been opened at various places for people who lost their homes in the flooding and landslides. A total of 873 roads are still blocked for traffic and 1,956 transformers and 1,369 water supply schemes are disrupted in the state.Authorities used helicopters to rescue people, while bridges and houses were swept away in Himachal. NDRF rescuers fixed zip lines to help people wearing safety harnesses cross the swollen Beas river during evacuation drives. Streets across the northern states, including in Punjab, Haryana and Uttarakhand were flooded. In some areas, rescue personnel used rubber rafts to evacuate people trapped inside their homes.Portion of Gangotri NH washes away, restoration work in progressLandslides triggered by heavy rain disrupted traffic on key highways in Uttarakhand, prompting warnings for residents not to venture out of their homes unless necessary. The state reported one death when a car with five people skidded off the road in blinding rain and fell into the Khoh river in Kotdwar in Pauri Garhwal district. SDRF personnel said two people survived, one died and another two were missing.Rescue crews were racing to evacuate survivors as the rain overwhelmed hundreds of villages in Dehradun and Haridwar districts. The Ganga was flowing close to the danger mark in both Haridwar and Rishikesh. Over 250 roads remained blocked across Uttarakhand, including the Gangotri, Yamunotri and Gangotri highways.Flash floods continued to wreak havoc in more than 1,000 villages and various towns and cities across 14 districts of the state, which recorded at least 11 deaths till Wednesday. Three people lost their lives in Faridkot district; two each in Ropar, Fatehgarh Sahib and Mohali districts; and one each in Hoshiarpur and Nawanshahr districts. A person each has been reported missing from Moga, Mohali, Jalandhar, Ludhiana and Nawanshahr districts.Flood alerts have been sounded in Pathankot, Gurdaspur and Amritsar districts of the Majha region. Army rescued over 400 residents of Ghanie Ke Bet village situated along the Ravi river in Gurdaspur. Hundreds of acres in several villages of Sangrur district were inundated after the Ghaggar river breached its banks in three places.The state government has set up 127 relief camps across the state with the highest number of 33 in Jalandhar, 22 in Mohali and 18 in Ferozepur. As many as 13,574 people have been shifted to safer places by NDRF, SDRF, state police and Army rescuers.In neighbouring Haryana, the death toll in rain-related accidents increased to 11 as four more bodies were found Thursday in Ambala.The flood alarm went out in western UP as Ganga and Yamuna were flowing above the danger level in Saharanpur and Shamli districts. â€œAround 1.9 lakh cusecs of water has been released in Yamuna on Wednesday,â€ said Ashu Kumar, junior engineer at the irrigation department. In Bijnor district, officials were keeping a close watch on the rising waters of the Ganga.Watch UP Administration increases vigilance near bank of Yamuna River as water level rises due to downpour

Title: BRTS Rapidly Turning Into Deadly Speedway
content: Bhopal: The stretch of Hoshangabad Road falling under Bag Sewania and Misrod police stations right from RRL trisection till Gyarah Meel has turned into one of the most accident-prone areas of Bhopal. The cut points or intersections of the BRTS corridor in these have turned into accident-prone zones. Private vehicles can be seen freely entering and overspeeding right in the BRTS corridor. Vehicles move in the two lanes on both the sides of the corridor at high speed and when vehicles take turns or try to cross through the cut points, accidents happen. On Tuesday morning, a speeding car running in the BRTS corridor lost control and hit the railing of corridor and rammed into the bus stop bus stop right in front the Misrod police station. Three people including a couple and a woman were injured. One of the women died while the other is critical. Police officials maintained that earlier there were BMC employees deployed at BRTS cut points and also gates were put up to check unauthorised entry into BRTS corridors. Now these guards were missing so the vehicles enter and overspeed in the corridor. Not only vehicles but stray cattle enter the corridor and increase chances of road accidents. A couple of days before this incident, a stray cattle entered the BRTS corridor in Misrod leading to an accident. As per traffic police, the 300-metre stretch of Hoshangabad road in front of Ashima Mall in Misrod has been identified as â€˜black spotâ€™. Five road accidents took place during the last three years on this stretch and two people lost their lives. . .AdvertisementSimilarly, the 200-metre stretch of Hoshangabad Road at Bag Sewania Square in front of Bag Sewania police station has been identified by traffic police as another â€˜black spotâ€™. Six road accidents took place at this spot during the last three years and one casualty was reported. Misrod and Bag Sewania areas have been among the top ten most accident-prone zones of Bhopal. Police officials admitted that most of the road accidents take place on Hoshangabad road in these stretches and the cut points of the corridor are a major factor leading to the accidents.One third of the width of the stretch of Hoshangabad Road from RRL trisection in Bag Sewania till Misrod is covered by the corridor. Two narrow lanes are left for vehicles and the rest of the road is covered by â€˜bicycle trackâ€™ and service roads. The intersections are located near Misrod police station, in front of Shriram Colony, in front of Surendra Landmark, at Danish Nagar, Vidhya Nagar or Bag Sewania police station, BU and RRL trisection. Many times, private vehicles can be seen entering the corridor through the cut points. These vehicles pose danger to others as crossing the road. Encroachments in the service lanes on both the sides of the road by commercial establishments is also a major factor for traffic problems. Vehicles remain parked on the service road thereby leaving just a narrow lane for vehicles to pass. Vehicles also enter the wrong lanes. SHO Misrod RB Sharma said that during evening peak traffic hours, cops are deployed at major BRTS cut points to regulate traffic. He maintained that the flow of traffic, especially from Mandideep side is high in the evening hours so to regulate traffic, police force is deployed. He admitted that private vehicles enter the corridor like the car which crashed in a BRTS bus stop on Tuesday killing a 53-year-old woman. Earlier, there were gates at these trisections and BMC employees were deployed to check unauthorised entry into the corridor, but they are not present nowadays.

Title: 4 die in road accidents in Dausa, Tonk dists
content: JAIPUR: Four people were killed and one was injured in two separate road accidents in Dausa and Tonk districts late on Wednesday evening.Three people were killed near Jeerota Mode area on the Jaipur-Agra highway near Dausa when a milk-carrying tanker in which they were travelling was hit by an unidentified vehicle. "It was a nasty collision that took place at 11:45pm. The four people in the truck's cabin got trapped in the vehicle," said an officer of Sadar police station of Dausa on Thursday.A team of senior police officers rushed to the spot soon after the accident. "All the four people in the truck's cabin, who were seriously injured were pulled out with the help of metal cutters and rushed to the hospital, where three of them were declared brought dead. The deceased were identified as Jayanti Bhai Thakur, Thakurwas Devodar and Varda Bhai Bheel, all natives of Banskantha in Gujarat," added the officer. He injured man, Bhav Singh Vagehla, is under treatment.In the second accident, a 19 -year-old woman riding pillion on a bike got seriously injured when the man riding it lost control at a speed breaker and she fell down near Pachewar area in Tonk district. "The woman, identified as Nisha, sustained grave injuries and was rushed to SMS Hospital in Jaipur. She succumbed on Thursday morning," an officer of Panchewar police station in Tonk said. TNN

Title: 5 kanwariyas die as truck rams vehicle in northwest Delhi's Alipur
content: NEW DELHI: Five kanwariyas were killed and another 14 injured after a speeding truck hit the divider on GT Karnal road in northwest Delhi's Alipur area, crossed over to the other carriageway and rammed the yatris' truck coming from the opposite direction. The errant truck driver was later arrested.The deceased were identified as Sandeep Kumar (32), Rajat (24), Aryan (19), Jagminder (56) and Raju (37), who was driving the yatris' vehicle.Alipur police received a PCR call about the incident at 12.44am Thursday. When a team reached the spot, it found that two trucks had collided on the upper GT Karnal highway (NH-44). One of the trucks was carrying kanwariyas from Mianwali Nagar near Nangloi to Haridwar. The other truck, which had a Haryana number plate, was heading to Delhi, said DCP (outer north) Ravi Singh. The injured persons were sent to various city hospitals, including LNJP, Baba Saheb Ambedkar and Balaji Action Centre in Paschim Vihar.A case under sections of rash and negligent driving and death due to negligence was registered at Alipur police station. The accused, Pappu Kumar, originally from Kannauj, works as a driver of a private firm in Samaypur Badli area.Kumar told police he had unloaded rice at Hamidpur and was going to Azadpur for refuelling when the accident took place. He said a car in front of the truck suddenly applied brakes, forcing him to take an extreme right turn and hitting the other truck. Kumar said he panicked and instead of applying the brakes, he pressed the accelerator.Police said the yatris' vehicle was a lightweight Tata 407 truck but had been modified, which may have worsened the damage. Following the accident, kanwariyas blocked the road, triggering snarls on NH-44 in the early hours. The road was later cleared for vehicular movement, police said.Recalling the horror, some of the injured yatris said they were all enjoying the music in the truck and were waiting for two or three other friends to join in when the incident occurred. Two of the five victims -- Rajat and Aryan -- were cousins."I was getting ready for work when I saw the news of the accident on TV. When I saw the visuals and saw the banner on the screen, I realised it was the same vehicle in which my son had gone for the yatra. He had just finished Class 12," Aryan's father Pankaj told TOI.Rajat used to work with a private finance firm and was his family's sole breadwinner. His family members said they were keen that he get married and had even chosen a girl for him.

Title: Kanwar pilgrim on a bike takes DME, dies in crash
content: Ghaziabad: A 28-year-old kanwar pilgrim died and his friend was injured after the bike they were riding on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway was hit by a vehicle near Masuri on Thursday morning. Two-wheelers, auto-rickshaws and tractor-trolleys are banned on the DME because of the difference in their speed with other vehicles that cruise on the road at almost 100kmph.Police said Ramveer, a resident of Faridabad, and his friend Mohit were returning home after collecting Ganga water in Haridwar when they met with the accident around 9am.The fatal crash comes a couple of days after six members of a family died when the SUV in which they were travelling crashed into a private bus that was moving in the wrong direction for almost 8km of the DME.Tuesdayâ€™s accident had raised questions on enforcement of traffic rules on the DME, which has around 80 CCTV cameras. Many had questioned how the killer bus could travel for 8km on the expressway without being detected. The death of a kanwar pilgrim on a bike on Thursday reinforced the allegation.â€œCommuters informed us and a police team was rushed to the spot. They were taken to a nearby hospital, where Ramveer was declared dead on arrival. His friend was referred to GTB Hospital in Delhi for grievous injuries to the chest and head,â€ said Naresh Kumar, ACP (Masuri).A preliminary probe revealed that the bike was hit from behind. The police are trying to trace the vehicle. â€œWe have sent the body for an autopsy. Their families have been informed, but they are yet to lodge any complaint. We have asked NHAI to give us the CCTV footage of the stretch where the accident took place,â€ Kumar said.Despite issuing regular challans, the police have failed to stop two-wheelers and autos from using the DME. In August last year, the fine on banned vehicles found using the DME was raised from Rs 1,000 to Rs 20,000.According to commuters, the spots notorious for wrong-side driving and other traffic violations are near Media House in Khoda, near Green Gaur society in Indirapuram, ABES College in Vijay Nagar, IMS College, Vedanta farmhouse in Masuri, Daya Steel near Loha Mandi and so on.â€œA study has revealed that nearly 60% of the accidents on DME involved two-wheelers, which are banned anyway. In 90% of the crashes, the reason was speeding,â€ a police officer said.This year, till June 30, DME witnessed 37 accidents in which 28 people died and 27 were injured.Govt car on wrong side for 2kmTwo days after a bus on the wrong side killed six members of a family, a white Innova was found moving on the wrong lane for around 2km of the DME. A video of the Innova cruising on the expressway was circulated on social media.Ramanand Kushwaha, ADCP (traffic), said the driver had missed a turn. â€œWhen he realised his mistake, he started driving on the wrong side of the DME. But a team of traffic cops stopped the car. We have issued a Rs 2,000 challan. We have learnt it was a government car,â€ he said.

Title: Cyclist killed, six injured in accidents
content: KOLKATA: A 35-year-old man was killed in an accident on Thursday when a private bus hit his cycle on CIT Road near Hudco crossing.Police said Hiru Halder (35), a resident of Beraberi in Rajarhat, was trying to overtake the bus from the left when it suddenly sped up and hit the cyclist around 9pm. He was immediately taken to RG Kar Medical College and Hospital where he succumbed to his injuries shortly after admission.Cops have arrested the bus driver and have seized the vehicle.In four other accidents, six persons were injured in different parts of the city on Friday. On Basanti Highway, three youths on a bike were injured when an unidentified vehicle hit the bike around 9am. Moisuruddin Gayen (21), Tarikul Gayen (21) and Monirul Molla (23) were injured.Around the same time, a 45-year-old pedestrian, Bikash Nath, was injured when a bike hit him on KP Road off Maidan. Another biker, Samir Mondal (69), was injured when a taxi hit the bike from behind on Vidyasagar Setu around 10.30pm. Mondal sustained injuries and was taken to SSKM Hospital. The car driver was apprehended.Also on Friday, a 70-year-old man, Subir Kr Roy, was injured while alighting from a private bus on Lenin Sarani near Tipu Sultan mosque around 10.30am.

Title: Speed kills: Accidents rise by 14%, deaths by 17% in Ranchi
content: RANCHI: Despite the state governmentâ€™s multi-pronged efforts to ensure road safety, the number of accidents and fatalities in Ranchi district has registered a significant rise in the first six months of 2023 compared to the corresponding period last year. The figures could rise in the next six months.The district recorded a 14% rise in road accidents between January and June this year compared to the same period last year. The number of incidents in the first six months of current year is 366 compared to 321 last year. Moreover, the number of fatalities in road accidents this year is 268 in six months compared to 229 last year. The figures have registered an increase of 17%.The injuries in accidents have increased from 183 in first six months of last year to 237 in the corresponding period this year â€” a rise of 29%.District transport officer (DTO) Praveen Kumar Prakash said over-speeding on the broad and well-maintained highways is the major cause of accidents. He added that youths are also frequenting the outskirts of the city on bikes to drive at high speed and also perform stunts. In 2022, bike-related accidents made up 50% of all the accident cases in the district.The DTO said preventive measures such as speed guns are now being installed on the highways, adding that punitive action would hinder violation of speed norms. He said the transport department is making sure the speed-limiting devices in vehicles are working during fitness tests. The DTO said other causes of accidents are driving on the wrong side of the road and drink driving. The transport department has identified eight black spots in Ranchi district. The spot are located before Bargava bridge near Namkum Chowk, Tetri near Namkum police station and near Jamchua under the Namkum police station, near Ormanjhi chowk in Ormanjhi, BIT More under Sadar PS and Murpa under Tamar PS. All of them are located on National Highway 33.Department officials said accidents are common on Bundu and Rampur along NH-33, Tilsa Chowk on NH-75 and the Sithio ring road.

Title: In Hry, 7 kanwariyas killed in 3 accidents
content: KURUKSHETRA/AMBALA/ROHTAK: Seven kanwariyas, three from Haryana's Mahendergarh district, two from Kaithal district, and one from Jind district and one from Punjab's Ludhiana district, were killed in three accidents on Friday. Eleven kanwariyas were injured in these accidents.Three other kanwariyas are missing after they were swept away in the Markanda river on Friday afternoon.Three kanwariyas died and five sustained injuries after a generator set fell on them at Chidana village on the Panipat-Rewari highway. They were on their way back to Surheti village in Mahendergarh district from Haridwar when the generator set placed inside their pick-up vehicle fell on them. The injured are undergoing treatment at Khandpur medical college near Gohana of Sonipat district.Three other kanwariyas were killed and five were injured after being hit by a car on the Kurukshetra-Dhand road in the Jyotisar area of Kurukshetra district. The deceased kanwariyas have been identified as Rakesh and Rajinder, both of Shimla village in Kaithal district; and Sohan Lal of Gursar Narwana village in Jind district. Those injured have been identified as Satish, Kuldeep, Rahul, and Akshay of Dhundwa village in Kaithal district, and Darshan of Kamoda village in Kurukshetra.One kanwariya was killed and another was seriously injured after being hit by a canter near Jandli flyover in the Parao police station area of Ambala Cantonment. The deceased, Sandeep, and the injured man, Suresh (45), belong to Ludhiana.They were part of a group of 35 men from Ludhiana who were returning home from Haridwar at about 3 am when a speeding canter mowed them down. After the incident, the canter driver managed to flee and left his vehicle behind.

Title: 40-year-old driver from Gujarat dies in road accident in Thane
content:
THANE: A 40-year-old driver from Gujarat died in a freak mishap at Ghodbunder road in Thane during the wee hours of Saturday, after a container truck collided with another cargo-carrying truck.The deceased has been identified as Majid Khan, said the official from Regional Disaster Management Cell of Thane Municipal Corporation.The official further informed that the deceased was driving a container with 10 tonnes of cloth material from Rajasthan and was enroute to Nhava Sheva.At around 2.12am, the deceased driver reportedly collided with another truck carrying 18 tonne solar machine, killing him on the spot. The incident brought the traffic to standstill for about two hours and was diverted to the services road, the official said.The rescue operation went on for several hours and the road was later cleared for traffic.

Title: Kin of man killed in 2019 bus accident awarded Rs 21 lakh
content: THANE: The MACT has awarded a compensation of Rs 21 lakh to the family of a 44-year-old luxury bus cleaner who died in a road accident in November 2019.The claimants include the widow of the deceased, his four children and his mother, all residents of Pratapgarh in Uttar Pradesh.Appearing for the claimants, counsel Advocate AR Yadav told the tribunal that on November 17, 2019, at around 3pm, deceased Ikrar Ahmed Ashique Ali was on board a luxury bus proceeding to Malvani. As the driver of the bus was driving in a rash and negligent manner, the deceased was thrown out of the bus and came under the tyre which crushed him to death. Subsequently, the local police were alerted, and an offence was registered against the accused.The tribunal was also told that the deceased worked with the same travel agency which owned the offending bus and earned a salary of Rs. 14,000 per month.Adv. AK Tiwari appearing for the insurance company contested the claim on various grounds and made his submissions before the tribunal.The member of tribunal MM Walimohammed after hearing both the sides concluded that the kin of the deceased are awarded Rs 20.86 lakh and directed the owner of the travel agency and the insurance company to make the payment as soon as possible.The compensation amount also includes Rs 15,000 each towards loss of estate and funeral expenses and Rs 40,000 towards loss of consortium besides Rs 20.16 lakh for loss of income.

Title: CM likely to hand over keys of houses to families of 26 road accident victims
content: Kanpur: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath had announced that the families of the 26 people killed in a road accident in Narwal tehsil on October 1, 2022, would be given one house each under the Mukhyamantri Awas Yojana. According to sources, the CM may visit Kanpur to hand over the keys to the beneficiaries.In this regard, an on-site inspection of the development work of the colony of the houses in village Kortha was done on Thursday by district magistrate Vishak G Iyer. During the inspection, the district magistrate instructed the officials to ensure that the remaining work of the houses should be completed in a week on warfooting.Instructions were given by the DM to the district panchayat raj officer to lay the pipeline for the drainage of the colony. The block development officer, Bhitargaon was directed to ensure that all the houses were painted in one colour.During the inspection, it was informed by the power engineer that the work of electrification for all the houses has been completed, besides the work of lighting in the entire campus was also completed.Interlocking in the way leading to the colony and pathways have been constructed to reach the park and residence in the premises of the colony. Jal Nigam has also completed the work of laying pipelines in all the houses under Jal Jeevan Mission Yojana under Har Ghar Nal Se Jal Yojana. Instructions were given to assistant engineer RES to install mesh to cover the drains to be made in the premises of houses under construction. Twenty six people, mostly women and children, had died and several others sustained critical injuries when a recklessly driven tractor-trolley carrying around 35 to 40 people fell into a roadside pond on the highway in between Saadh and Gambhirpur of Ghatampur on October 1, 2022.

content:
Panchkula: A 16-year-old boy died after being hit by a motorcycle near Chandimandir cantonment railway station on Friday night. Police have registered a case. In his complaint, Harish Kumar (26), a resident of Bir Ghaggar, said the accident occurred around 10pm when he along with his friend Piyush Sharma (16) was walking on the road near the railway station.

content:

Title: Two techies killed as car hits truck in Jangaon dist
content:
RAGHUNATHPALLY (JANGAON): Two techies were killed in a road accident in the wee hours of Saturday when the car they were travelling in rammed into a parked sand truck on NH-163 at Komalla Toll Plaza.The deceased were identified as V Rakesh, 33, and his friend Sandeep, 36. Both the software professionals were residents of Pochamma Maidanm area in Warangal town. Police officials, with the assistance of locals, retrieved the bodies from the Bolero car. The bodies were sent to Jangaon Government Hospital for postmortem.According to Raghunathpally sub-inspector Raghupathi, Rakesh's father had died a week ago. He was travelling from Hyderabad to attend his father's 10th day ceremony with his friend Sandeep. At 5am, the accident took place at the Komalla Toll Plaza as the car was travelling at a high speed. Police suspect that Sandeep, who was at the wheel, may have dozed off while driving and the vehicle hit the stationary truck. Police officials said that a case had been registered under Section 174 of the CrPC.

Title: Man killed in Curchorem accident
content:
Margao: Manoj Sutar, 32, a resident of Amona, Quepem, died in a head-on collision between two motorcycles on the road leading to Ravindra Bhavan, Curchorem, late Saturday night.Police said that Sutar, who was riding a motorcycle, collided with a two-wheeler coming from the opposite direction, leading both the riders to fall on the road, grievously hurt. They were rushed to the hospital but Sutar succumbed to his injuries. The incident has brought to the fore the threat posed by the bad condition of roads across Curchorem. The roads, which were dug-up, havenâ€™t been properly restored. With both sewerage and underground power cabling works undertaken simultaneously at various places and left incomplete owing to monsoons, the uneven surface of roads with deep depressions and abrupt humps, has turned it into a death trap for users.

Title: Two die in separate road accidents
content: Rajkot: A 36-year-old man was killed when a dumper rammed into a parked truck near Sokhda village near Maliyasan crossroads in Rajkot district on Saturday. The deceased identified as Sanjay Kumar Jaiswal was repairing the truck when the dumper crashed into it from behind, said police.The deceasedâ€™s brother Ajay Jaiswal (31) lodged a complaint against the dumper driver at Kuvadva police station. Police filed the FIR for causing death due to negligence under IPC and under relevant sections of the Motor Vehicle Act.According to police, Sanjay was a native of Ghazipur district in Uttar Pradesh. He used to do labour work in Lalpari area of Rajkot. In another road accident, 50-year-old Rasikgiri Goswami succumbed to injuries he sustained after his bike was hit by a dumper truck near Khijadiya bypass road in Jamnagar district on Saturday. The deceased was riding with his wife Dhirajben Goswami (45) and four-year-old girl Riya. Both Dhirajben and Riya were injured in the accident.While Dhirajben suffered multiple injuries in the head, eyes, and other parts of the body, Riya sustained minor injuries and was discharged after first aid. Based on a complaint filed by Rasikgiriâ€™s son Pratik, Panch A Division police booked the dumper driver for rash driving and causing death due to negligence under IPC and under different sections of the Motor Vehicle Act. The dumper driver fled from the accident spot, said police.

Title: Two killed in hit-&-runs in Ludhiana
content:
Ludhiana: Two persons were killed in two hit-&-run incidents in Ludhiana. In the first case, Pankaj Kumar (35) died when a speeding car hit him near Pawa village. The accident took place when Pankaj and his friend was returning home after work on a bike. A speeding vehicle hit them and sped away. Pankaj Kumar was admitted to the Ludhiana civil hospital, where he died due to excessive blood loss during surgery. Sahnewal police have registered a case In another accident, Inder Mani (38) of Gaunsgarh village on Rahon road was hit by an unidentified vehicle near Kakka village when he was returning home. He was admitted to the PGI, but died on Sunday. tnn

Title: Retired Army man run over by bus, people vandalise vehicle
content: Jaipur: A 58-year-old man was killed after a speeding Jaipur City Transport Services Limited (JCTSL) bust ran over him on Sikar Road.According to SHO (Accident West) Naryan Singh, the incident occurred around 7pm when Narendra Kumar Gautam, a resident of Murlipura, was riding his scooter. The bus hit him and then run over Gautam near Vishwakarma Industrial Area (VKI) Road number 2, resulting in his death on the spot. â€œGautam was a retired defence personnel. He used to attend yoga classes every morning,â€ Singh said. Meanwhile, ASI Durga Prasad said that the victim retired from Army and his body was handed over to the family.The locals passing from the area helped to rush him to the hospital but he was pronunced dead by the docotors. Meanwhile, a large number of local residents and a group of passerby tried to stop the bus driver but he managed to flee.The enraged mob proceeded to vandalise the bus and demanded for the immediate arrest of the accused responsible for the incident. The residents said that JCTSL buses have been wrecking havoc on the Sikar Road, leading to one accident after the another. When questioned about the reason behind the bus vandalism, a local resident who also runs a small shop in the area explained that buses have turned the roads into a racing track, which has been a cause of concern for locals.â€œThe buses are locked in a reckless competition with one another, trying to get the maximum number of passengers from each stop. This aggressive behavior has led to many mishaps,â€ the resident said. Singh and his team, who reached the spot, pacified the angry protestors. He said, "It is an unfortunate incident, but vandalizing public property will not help anyone. We are investigating the case to ensure justice for the victim."The locals alleged that the bus driver was wearing earphones at the time of the accident, but the police responded by stating that they have not found any evidence to support this claim.Meanwhile, a senior traffic official informed JCTSL about the reckless driving of their drivers, but their efforts were in vain.

Title: 2 of family killed in road accident
content:
NAINITAL: Two out of seven of a family lost their lives, while the five others sustained injuries after their car lost control and fell into a gorge near Ghatgarh in Nainital late on Saturday. The SDRF rushed the injured to the hospital. The deceased have been identified as Rahul Srivastava (27) and Rajeev Srivastava (25), while Akash (22), Premchand Srivastava (52), Aruna Srivastava (50), Shalu Srivastava (25), and Rahul's two-and-a-half-year-old daughter Mishti are undergoing treatment. Sub-inspector Manish Bhakuni who led the SDRF rescue team said, â€œThe incident took place after Rahul lost control of the vehicle. We rescued five injured, two bodies were sent for autopsy. The family, from UP's Gorakhpur, were holidaying in Nainital.â€

Title: Hyderabad: ORR soaks in blood as five killed in two accidents
content: HYDERABAD: Five persons were killed in two accidents on the ORR stretch on Monday. In the first accident, three persons were killed and two, including a doctor, injured when a truck overshot a median on the Outer Ring Road (ORR) in Shamirpet on Monday morning and barreled into two vehicles coming in the opposite direction.According to police, the truck was heading towards Keesara from Shamirpet and the driver lost control over the wheel at around 8.30 am either due to fatigue or intoxication. The truck rammed a pick-up van and a car before coming to a halt after crashing through the safety railing."Driver of the van, V Narasimha, 48, of Ibrahimpantam and his associate, Subhash Pashwan, 28, from Bihar died on the spot. The van carrying grocery items was badly mangled due to the head-on collision. Two persons travelling in the car, which was trailing the van, were injured as the front portion of the four-wheeler was damaged," Shamirpet sub-inspector P Muneender said.  Tragedy hit the truck driver N Vinay, 22, too. When the truck hit the railing, Vinay, a native of Shadnagar, was tossed out of the vehicle. He came under the front tyre of the truck and died on the spot.Traffic police towed away the damaged van and car and cleared the way for the traffic. "We had to deploy two cranes to extricate the bodies of the two persons from the mangled van," Medchal traffic inspector Hanuman Goud said.During a preliminary investigation, police found out that Vinay might have dozed off due to exhaustion or been under the influence of alcohol. "We have to send the viscera of the driver for a lab analysis to verify if he was drunk." Dr Kishore Reddy and his car driver Nagulu suffered injuries. "They were shifted to a nearby hospital. They are out of danger. The doctor was on his way to Karimnagar after visiting his family in ECIL," police said.

Title: 6 dead, 5 injured in road accident on Mumbai-Nashik highway
content:
KALYAN: Six people were killed whereas five others sustained injuries after a container hit a jeep near Padgha-Khadavali turning point on Mumbai-Nashik highway on Tuesday morning. The accident took place at around 7.30am near Lucky hotel on the highway where in the past, too, many accidents were reported.According to Padgha police, the vehicle carrying passengers were going towards Padgha while another vehicle was coming from Nashik when the collision happened.The incident was captured on CCTV camera installed on the highway.. .AdvertisementAfter the accident, locals rushed the injured to nearby health centre where four of them were declared dead while seven others were admitted with multiple injuries. Later, two more people succumbed to their injuries during treatment. The condition of some of the injured was said to be critical.

Title: Man killed in road accident
content:
Mangaluru: A senior citizen was killed in a road accident reported in Mangaluru Traffic North police limits on Tuesday. The deceased is Titus Ferrao, 69. According to police, the accident took place when the victim was riding a two-wheeler from Baikampady Junction to Panambur at 11.10am. It is learnt that he was riding at a slow speed on that stretch of the road that was damaged and filled with potholes due to rain. He was hit by a goods vehicle from the rear, due to which, he fell on the road and was dragged forward. He sustained injuries and died on the spot. A probe is going on.Meanwhile, the Action Committee Against Surathkal Toll Gate has demanded legal action against the officials concerned from National Highways Authority of India for Ferraoâ€™s death. Title: Instagram reel-making claims innocent life: Motorcycle jumps off flyover as stunt fails
content: In today's world, it's not uncommon to see people doing dangerous road stunts for the sake of Instagram reels. By doing this, people not only break the traffic laws but also put their own and othersâ€™ lives at risk. In a similar case from Varanasi, two youngsters, who were allegedly filming a stunt-reel on a flyover, lost control of their â€œnew bikeâ€. The motorcycle then stumbled on the bridge and eventually fell off it, landing on a moving car underneath it. As per the local police, the riders, said to be a young boy and a girl, fled away from the scene, leaving behind their motorcycle without any registration number plates. On the other hand, the driver of the car on which the motorcycle fell, lost his life while his friend was injured severely and has been admitted to local hospital.Triumph Speed 400 First Ride Review | The best under Rs 3 lakh? | TOI AutoThe deceased was identified as Sarvesh Shankar, a railway engineer. He suffered head injuries and died on the spot while his friend Aditya Verma was rushed to a nearby hospital. A case has been registered against the missing perpetrators and the search is underway. This accident brings our attention back to road safety hazards caused by irresponsible riders and drivers in India. There has been a surge in the number of accidents where people have been putting their lives at risk for creating social media content. These videos might bring a few views or engagement on the internet but it is also a serious threat to life as well as a violation of law as roads are meant for responsible commute, not for performing stunts.

Title: 2nd crash in 10 days at black spot on Nashik highway kills 6
content: THANE: In the second mishap in 10 days on the Padgha-Khadavli Naka turn on the Mumbai-Nashik highway, six people were killed while five others were injured when a speeding container truck rammed into a SUV carrying 10 people to Khadavli railway station early Tuesday. Such was the impact of the crash that the truck dragged the vehicle for at least 100ft before halting. The victims included two college-going girls, a woman and a youth headed for a job interview. TimesViewThe recurrence of fatal accidents at the spot only goes to show that no one is serious about road safety. A three-step speed breaker there had no white markings for the container truck driver to see it in time and be warned. While the driver of the smaller vehicle should have waited for the container to pass, the container driver, too, could have been more careful at a naka.Locals have blamed an unmarked speedbreaker on the turn for multiple fatal accidents at the spot in recent years.Truck driver Shivkumar Prajapati (35) has been arrested and booked for culpable homicide and rash driving.Locals seek solution to accident black spot on Nashik highwayShivkumar Prajapati, driver of the container truck that crashed into an SUV on the Mumbai-Nashik highway, killing six people, has been booked under IPC and Motor Vehicle Act sections for culpable homicide and rash driving.All six deceased were residents of Padgha and its surrounding villages in the Bhiwandi taluka of Thane district.The five injured in the accident have been identified as SUV driver Javed Shaikh (50), Janhavi Walanj (19), Kunal Bhamare (24), Dilip Saroj (30) and Chetna Jare (19). Shaikh, who is serious, was shifted to KEM Hospital in Mumbai, Walanj to IGM hospital in Bhiwandi and the other three who suffered multiple injuries to Myra Hospital in Bhiwandi.The mishap took place around 6.30am when all the passengers, most of them office-goers and some students, were headed to Khadavli station in the share SUV. A CCTV installed at a restaurant at the spot shows while taking a right turn on the Mumbai-Nashik highway towards Khadavali Road, Shaikh slowed due to a speedbreaker but the oncoming container driver did not. Locals caught the driver and handed him to the police. Padgha police sources said the driver claimed the brakes had failed.Locals, with help from police, rushed the injured to the Padgha primary health centre. Two were taken to Chinmai Hospital where Firke died during treatment. Another victim died at IGM Hospital.Locals have demanded that highway authorities find a solution to the accident-prone Padgha-Khadavli Naka. Rakesh Jadhav, a relative of Vasant Jadhav, told TOI: "This is the second accident in 10 days at the same spot. There is a slope on the naka from Nashik towards Padgha where authorities have created a speedbreaker but not painted it, due to which drivers come in at full speed and hit vehicles taking a right turn from the highway towards Khadavli." Sanjay Sable, senior police inspector of Padgha police station, said: "The primary probe shows the driver was on his way to Nhava Sheva in Navi Mumbai from Nashik to deliver goods and was driving at full speed. Further probe is on."Bhiwandi (East) MLA Rais Shaikh on Tuesday raised the issue in Vidhan Sabha, saying there is no proper transportation for schoolchildren in Bhiwandi and there is no trauma centre in state-run IGM Hospital in Bhiwandi and demanded it be opened at the earliest.PWD minister Dada Bhuse visited Myra Hospital where three of the injured are admitted and asked if they were receiving proper treatment.

Title: Accident at Bheru ghat, one killed & 11 injured
content: INDORE: One person was killed and 11 injured in an accident involving two tractor trolleys, a passenger bus and a car at Bheru ghat in Simrol on late Monday night.Simrol police said that the two tractor trolleys collided while trying to overtake one another. The impact of the collision was such that one of the trolleys hit rocks on one side of the road while the other overturned.Due to the accident, a bus that was plying behind the trolleys rammed into one of them, injuring some passengers.The injured passengers told the police that driver of the bus tried to control the vehicle but it hit the trolley. A car that was behind the bus collided with it when it halted suddenly.. .AdvertisementThe driver of the trolley which had hit rocks was stuck inside and extricated with difficulty. He was rushed to hospital but succumbed during treatment. The man was identified as Sarang Wankhede. His body was sent for postmortem examination to Indore district hospital and later handed over to family members.Simrol police station in-charge Mansharam Bisen and other police officials reached the spot and rushed the injured to MY hospital with help of locals.The accident led to a traffic jam for almost three hours. tnn

Title: Cop on way to office killed after speeding bus hits bike
content: Bhopal: A 50-year-old cop was killed after his bike was hit by a speeding feeder bus near Raj Bhawan in Arera Hills on Tuesday morning. The policeman, who was on his way to the office at New Police Control Room, fell on the road after his motorcycle was hit by the bus. He was crushed under the wheels of the bus.The errant bus driver fled the spot and was later nabbed by police. The bus was seized, and a case was registered against the errant bus driver, and he was arrested. The bus driver in a bid to overtake a car hit the policemanâ€™s bike moving ahead on the road. SHO Arera Hills RK Singh said that the deceased Surendra Singh Bisht, a resident of government police quarters in 25th Battalion, was posted as a clerk in the Police Commissioner Office at New Police Control Room. He was going to the office by his bike around 11.30am on Tuesday. When he reached Malviya Nagar behind Raj Bhawan, a speeding feeder bus, which shuttles between Obedullaganj and Bhopal, coming from behind hit his bike. As he fell on the road due to the collision, the bus wheels ran over his waist. Locals and police immediately rushed him to a hospital located nearby where he was declared brought dead.SHO Singh said that Surendra hailed from Garhwal and lived with his wife here. He was appointed on compassionate grounds. The errant bus driver tried to run away after the accident and was nabbed by police. A case was registered.

Title: 4 killed as bus hits e-rickshaw on Ayodhya road
content: LUCKNOW: Four persons were killed and five others sustained injuries after an Uttar Pradesh State Roads Transport Corporation (UPSRTC) bus hit an e-rickshaw from the rear on Barabanki-Ayodhya highway in Barabanki on Wednesday. The e-rickshaw also got stuck in the bus and was dragged for several meters.The driver who tried to escape was taken into custody by the police. All the eight victims were passengers of the ill-fated e-rickshaw.SHO, Safdarganj, Brajesh Kumar Verma said eight people from Panditpurwa boarded an e-rickshaw to attend the last rites of one Virendra in Chhabka Ka Purwa area of Safdarganj. Barabanki road accident: E-rickshaw got stuck in busWhile returning when they reached near Godari area a roadways bus rammed the erickshaw from behind. The e-rickshaw got stuck in the bus. The onlookers ran for rescue and tried to stop the bus, which came to a grinding halt after hitting a tree on the roadside,â€ said the police. Someone from the crowd informed the police control room UP 112. â€œA police team rescued the passengers from the mangled pieces of the erickshaw,â€ said Verma. While Bindra (50), wife of Kallu, died on the spot, others were taken to the district hospital where Vijay Bahadur (50), Chandra (60), and Pinki (38) were declared brought dead. Vijay Kumari (45), Mithailal (60), Kallu (58), Mayarani (40) and Rajkumari (40) were referred to KGMU Trauma centre in Lucknow. Doctors at KGMU said that all the injured were critical but stable.

Title: Student killed in road accident
content: Mangaluru: A student studying architecture was killed in a motorbike accident reported in Adyar on Wednesday. The deceased is Mohammed Nashat,21, a first-year student from a private college, who hails from Uppala in Kerala. Speeding is suspected to have caused the accident. Nashat was riding from Padil towards his college, and first hit a scooter and then the divider. He was thrown off the vehicle and hit an electricity pole, after which he fell on the road. The bike moved 50m ahead after hitting the scooter. A case has been registered at the Mangaluru Traffic South police station.Commissioner assures holistic probe City police commissioner Kuldeep R Kumar Jain said that a holistic investigation will be conducted into the fatal accident that claimed the life of 69-year-old Titus Ferrao at Baikampady on NH-66 on Tuesday. Ferrao was killed on the spot, after his two-wheeler was hit by a goods vehicle. Commissioner Jain said that the police will carry out an investigation holistically into the issue, including the engineering aspect of the road. He said that a notice will be served to the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) during the investigation.The death of Ferrao triggered a public outrage, with netizens slamming the National Highways Authority of India for not keeping the highway in motorable condition, despite collecting toll from its users. Soon after the accident on Tuesday, the NHAI was seen filling the potholes on some stretches of the highway. Several organisations have stepped up their demand seeking action against the National Highways Authority of India. TNN

Title: 2 killed, 3 injured as car crashes into culvert wall in Rajasthan's Alwar
content:
JAIPUR: Two people died while three others sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in crashed into the wall of a culvert on Wednesday night near Akbarpur in Rajasthan's Alwar district. The victims, identified as Ashok Chaudhary, also known as Ashu (32), and Dinesh Jogi (28), were returning home after attending a birthday party when the accident happened, police said.The authorities conducted post-mortem examinations on Thursday and handed over the bodies to their relatives. The injured victims are receiving medical treatment.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: Speeding luxury SUV kills nine people gathered at car accident site in Gujarat
content: At least nine people have been reported dead after a speeding Jaguar F-PACE luxury SUV rammed into a crowd gathered around another accident site. The accident happened on July 19 at around 1:15 AM on the ISKCON flyover on Sarkhej-Gandhinagar (SG) highway in Ahmedabad, Gujarat. A number of people were gathered at the site after a Mahindra Thar SUV had hit a dumper when the luxury SUV ploughed into them.The Jaguar F-PACE is reported to be doing speeds of over 100 kmph, as reported by PTI quoting a police official.Read AlsoFisker Ocean Extreme electric SUV India launch soon: Deliveries start in Q4 2023California-based car manufacturer states that it will produce the top-spec Extreme Vigyan Edition of the Fisker Ocean for the Indian market which will be limited to 100 units.Among the nine that lost their lives in the tragic accident was a police constable, meanwhile 13 others reportedly sustained injuries. Reports also claim that when the luxury SUV rammed into the crowd, people were flung into the air, falling at a distance of about 20 - 25 feet from the site.Three people were reportedly seated inside the luxury SUV at the time of the accident, which was captured by a biker's camera who was riding on the opposite side, showing disturbing scenes as the crash happened live. The suspect was also seen being caught by the onlookers and thrashed.This is the decade & century of India and EVs will drive BMW's growth: Vikram Pawah As per statistics from 2020 and 2021, over four lakh severe road accidents take place every year in India. This means that 46 serious road accidents happen every hour! TOI Auto urges its readers to follow all traffic rules, especially staying in permissible speed limits at all times. Following traffic rules not only ensures the safety of you and your loved ones, but also other road users. One needs to be more cautious while driving at night when visibility is low, and while passing a sensitive area like a crash site.

Title: 4 killed, 5 injured as bus hits e-rickshaw in Uttar Pradeshâ€™s Barabanki district
content: LUCKNOW: Four people were killed and five others sustained injuries after a Uttar Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation (UPSRTC) bus hit an e-rickshaw in Barabanki district on Wednesday.The incident took place under Safdarganj police station area on the Barabanki-Ayodhya highway.The e-rickshaw got stuck in the bus and was dragged for several meters.The driver, who tried to escape, was taken into custody by the police. All eight victims were passengers of the ill-fated e-rickshaw.SHO Safdarganj, Brajesh Kumar Verma, said that eight people from Panditpurwa boarded an e-rickshaw to attend the last rites of one Virendra in Chhabka Ka Purwa area of Safdarganj."While returning, they reached near the Godari area when the roadways bus rammed the e-rickshaw from behind. After the accident, the e-rickshaw got stuck in the bus. Onlookers ran for rescue and tried to stop the bus, which finally came to a grinding halt, hitting a tree on the roadside," the police said. In the meantime, others informed the police control room UP 112."A police team reached the spot first, nabbed the driver, and then rescued the passengers from the mangled pieces of the e-rickshaw," Verma said.

Title: Uttarakhand: 'Tired of interference', woman kills boyfriend with cobra, flees with new lover
content: NAINITAL: When Haldwani police found the body of a young businessman, Ankit Chauhan, 32, inside his car on July 15, it was taken to be a case of death due to asphyxiation. However, as cops started unravelling the case, it turned out to be a well-planned murder executed by his girlfriend in a manner that would give any Bollywood potboiler a run for its money.Read AlsoWoman, lover held for killing husband in BengaluruPolice have arrested a woman and her boyfriend for allegedly murdering the formerâ€™s husband to ensure they could continue their relationship. Three others who assisted the duo in committing the crime have also landed in police net.Woman kills lover, chops body up in Tamil NaduA 29-year-old man employed with a private airline in Chennai was murdered by his girlfriend and three men in Pudukottai. His body was cut into pieces, transported 400 km and buried in the sands at Kovalam, police said on Tuesday. They have arrested the woman, Bhagyalakshmi, 38, and are on theJilted lover stabs woman over 16 times to death in full public view in BengaluruA 28-year-old youth allegedly stabbed his 25-year-old estranged lover in full public view outside her office in Murugeshpalya, east Bengaluru on Tuesday evening.The body of Chauhan, a resident of Rambagh Colony, Rampur Road, was found on the backseat of his car near Teenpani railway crossing. Police found that carbon monoxide was emanating from the car's AC, which they suspected to have led to the businessman's death. However, the post-mortem revealed snake bites on both of Chauhan's legs and the cause of death, it turned out, was poisoning by snake venom. Cops then began probing further and examined nearby CCTV cameras and Chauhan's call details, which is when Mahi Arya's name came to the fore. Mahi and Chauhan were alleged to be dating each other. Meanwhile, Chauhan's sister Isha filed a report at Haldwani police station, accusing Mahi and her friend Deep Kandpal of murdering Chauhan.  Examining the call details of Mahi further led the cops to a snake charmer Ramesh Nath, who then revealed the grisly murder plot during interrogation.The snake charmer told the police that Mahi had approached him and sought his help in getting Chauhan bitten by a snake to make the killing appear as an accident. She paid him Rs 10,000 for the job, he claimed, adding that Mahi was upset with Chauhan's constant interference in her life and now wanted to get rid of him. According to the snake charmer, Mahi and all the other accused have fled to Nepal. Nainital SSP Pankaj Bhatt said that two teams have been formed to trace the accused.

Title: Ahmedabad car accident: Bright futures crushed at 120kmph
content: Hours after a speeding SUV cut short the lives of nine young Amdavadis, 19-year-old Tathya Patel, who was behind the wheels of the killer vehicle, and his father were taken to the accident site on Iskcon flyover to reconstruct the crime on Thursday. Surrounded by the police and media, the two caught hold of their ears and did sit-ups in an act of remorse, but it was too little, too late to compensate for the loss of the nine lives.The deceased were identified as Krunal Kodia, 20, Ronak Vihalpara,19, and Akshar Patel, 21, from Botad; Aman Kacchi, 21, and Arman Vadhvania, 19 from Surendranagar; and Nirav Ramanuj, 22, a resident of Chandlodia. Dharmendrasinh Parmar, 35, from Chuda in Surendranagar and Jashwantsinh Chauhan, 45, a resident of Raikhad police lines who were posted with SG-2 traffic police as well as Nilesh Khatik, 38, a Vejalpur resident working as a home guard jawan with Bodakdev police also lost their lives.Ronak, Krunal, and Akshar were friends from Botad where their parents wept for the loss of sons who were poised for a bright future. Ronak was a final year computer engineering student at Silver Oak University in Ahmedabad and wanted to start a business. Krunal recently got a job as a government schoolteacher in Vasna. Akshar, a farmerâ€™s son, was pursuing MBA. The three had just moved into a flat in Vastrapur.Aman and Arman were friends from Surendranagar. Aman was pursuing physiotherapy and hoped to start his own clinic. Arman, an only child, had come here to prepare to go abroad for studies. Chuda village too plunged into mourning as Parmar was cremated with police honours. Parmar, who married two years ago, has a one-year-old daughter.Khatik was on duty with his cousin, Rakesh Mohansinh. â€œHe had a headache and was searching for tea when he saw the crowd, went to help and got killed,â€ said Mohansinh.

Title: Kin of power lineman killed in accident get â‚¹85L compensation
content:
Bhopal: A sessions court has awarded a compensation of Rs 85.54 lakh to family of a senior lineman of MP power discom central region, who was killed by a truck in a road accident. The court ordered the driver and owner of the truck along with the insurance company to pay Rs 85 lakh 54,662 to the family of the deceased. Advocate Sunny Hingorani, who along with advocate R K Hingorani appeared in the case for the family of the deceased, said that the deceased Samad Khan, resident of Krishak Nagar, bypass road, Karond, was a senior lineman with the central region power discom . On June 15, 2020, he was returning home from his native village when he was hit from behind by a truck near Bhopal-Biaora Doraha at 7.30 am. He was taken to Hamidia hospital in a serious condition where he was declared dead by doctors. The court of additional district & sessions judge Atul Saxena, in his order, said that the salary of Samad Khan as senior lineman was Rs 86,411 per month. He was also an income tax payee. With the money he earned, he was looking after his family, which had been rendered without any source of income in his absence. They have asked for compensation of Rs 1,03,00,000. Two of the three respondents- driver of the truck Asif and owner Ismail Khan, both residents of Haryana, didn't appear for hearing in court and therefore an ex-parte order has been passed in the case, the court said, while awarding Rs 85, 54,662 to dependents of the deceased.

Title: Bank manager killed after MSRTC bus hits his bike in Khadki
content:
PUNE: A manager of a public sector bank died after an MSRTC bus hit his motorcycle in Khadki on Pune-Mumbai road on Tuesday. The deceased, Rohit Kumar (34), was returning home after work when the accident happened. Sub-inspector Anna Gunjal of the Khadki police told TOI, â€œThe bus driver failed to spot him on the road and directly hit the motorcycle from the rear.â€Bus driver Prakash Kadam (53) of Maan in Satara was arrested by the Khadki police on Wednesday on charges of rash and negligent driving causing death. Kumarâ€™s cousin Alok Singh, a resident of Khadki, has lodged a complaint with the police.

Title: 9 dead as speeding car ploughs into crowd on flyover in Ahmedabad
content: In one of the most tragic accidents on city roads in recent history, at least nine people were killed and 13 injured on Iskcon flyover after midnight on Thursday. The devastating chain of events unfolded when a 19-year-old driver, Tathya Patel, in a speeding car crashed into a crowd that, ironically, had gathered at the spot to check out another accident that had occurred earlier.

Title: No signage to stop commuters from halting on six flyovers
content: AHMEDABAD: The SG Highway has been witnessing vehicles being driven at high speeds of 80-100kmph, yet there is no signage along the entire stretch of the road and the six flyovers built over the crossroads, to prohibit commuters from halting on the road. The absence of signs warning pedestrians against gathering, standing, or sitting on the road can be seen as an open invitation for people to congregate, potentially leading to accidents like the grim incident that killed nine people on Thursday.It has been observed that people halt their two-wheelers and stand where no footpaths have been constructed. Some even take late-night walks on these flyovers. The authorities have not built a single footoverbridge at any crossing, reflecting utter negligence towards pedestrian safety.A significant sum of Rs 1,000 crore has been invested by the government to upgrade the highway, including the addition of four new flyovers. Furthermore, an underpass was constructed at the Thaltej junction. Despite this, there is no provision for foot overbridges to facilitate safe pedestrian crossings. The lack ofpedestrian safety boards on flyovers indicates that pedestrians face risks while crossing the SG Highway.Experts pointed out that flyovers, such as the Iskcon and Gota flyovers, are prone to accidents due to the absence of proper signage and footpaths. Sources indicate that since it is a national highway, NHAIâ€™s design rules are applicable, which do not allow footpaths on the flyovers.

Title: Speeding trolley collides with car, kills driver
content:
Ludhiana: A man died in a head-on collision between a car and a trolley on the road along Sirhind canal near Palmajra village of Samrala on Friday night. The deceased has been identified as 32-year-old Gurinder Singh of Mushkabad village. He has a two-year-old daughter. Reports suggested that Gurinder was returning to his village from Doraha, travelling on the road along the canal on Friday night. When he reached Palmajra village near Samrala, a speeding trolley coming from the wrong side rammed into his car, crushing it badly. Gurinder was alone at the time of the accident. It seems that he tried hard to avoid the collision, swerving onto a dirt road on the bank of the canal. ASI Harjinder Singh said that the police has registered a case against the trailer driver, who has been identified as Balwant Chand of Jammu and Kashmir. tnn

Title: Driver dies in road accident near Dindigul
content:
Dindigul: The 34-year-old driver of a private omnibus was killed and 12 passengers were injured after the vehicle hit a truck near Ambilikai in Dindigul district in the early hours of Saturday. The Ambilikai police identified the deceased as Kalidas, of Tenkasi. A police officer said the omnibus with 15 passengers was travelling from Coimbatore towards Tenkasi on Oddanchatram - Dharapuram Road. â€œWhen the bus neared Ambilikai around 4am, the bus driver lost control over the vehicle, which smashed into the median and then hit a lorry coming from Tuticorin. The front portions of both vehicles were damaged in the impact. Kalidas died on the spot. Twelve passengers suffered minor injuries and they were sent to nearby hospitals.â€Police registered a case and are investigating. TNN

Title: Tickles, loud music, rain led to Jaguar crash that killed 9 in Ahmedabad
content: AHMEDABAD: Teen driver Tathya Patel and his friends were reportedly engrossed in boisterous activities inside his fatherâ€™s Jaguar SUV, including tickling each other and playing loud music while racing down the road, leading to distraction and the eventual accident on the Iskcon flyover in Ahmedabad that claimed nine lives after midnight on Thursday.Read AlsoAhmedabad car accident: Witness in distress, faces social pressureThe main eyewitness, who captured the video of the Iskcon flyover horror, was among a group of 25 riders who were riding down SG Highway post-midnight on Thursday. The witness, who was in the lead and was wearing a dash camera on his helmet, spotted the crowd gathered at the collision spot betweenâ€˜Bulldozer justiceâ€™ looms on Ahmedabad Jaguar crash teenâ€™s dadSpeculations are rife that the state home department is mulling â€˜bulldozer justiceâ€™ to Pragnesh Patel, the father of the accused in the Iskcon flyover accident, similar to the powers exercised in Uttar Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh.Police said Saturday the reckless behaviour of 19-year-old Tathya and his friends â€” three girls and two boys â€” resulted in the Jaguar careening out of control and crashing into a group of people gathered on the rain-soaked flyover to check out another accident that had occurred shortly before when a 16-year-old boy crashed his fatherâ€™s Mahindra Thar into a dumper truck.ISKCON bridge carnage: Driver Tathya Patel arrested for deadly Ahmedabad car crash that claimed 9 livesTathya confessed during interrogation that he and his friends were traveling from Karnavati Club to Rajpath Club when the accident took place. They were enjoying their ride, paying no attention to the crowd that had gathered after the earlier accident. The needle in the SUV allegedly hovered over 100kmph.Due to the combination of rainy weather and inadequate lighting on the flyover, visibility was limited, further exacerbating the situation, a traffic cop said. Tathya also cited the challenging visibility conditions as a contributing factor, claiming that he couldnâ€™t spot the crowd in time to apply the brakes effectively.In an effort to determine the probable collision speed, the traffic police reconstructed the entire sequence of events on Saturday using the same models of the SUV and dumper truck involved in the initial crash and the Jaguar responsible for the second fatal accident.Initial forensic analysis indicates that Tathya was driving at a speed of approximately 120kmph on a flyover with a speed limit of only 40kmph. Tathyaâ€™s statement regarding his speed has been inconsistent throughout. â€œWhen the crowd beat him, he claimed to be driving at 120kmph, but later altered his account, asserting he was within the permissible limit,â€ an officer said.Watch ISKCON bridge carnage: Driver Tathya Patel arrested for deadly Ahmedabad car crash that claimed 9 lives

Title: Three kanwariyas killed in Roorkee road accident
content:
ROORKEE: Three kanwariyas on their way to Delhi after fetching holy water from Haridwar, were killed after the motorbike they were travelling on collided with an oil tanker on Roorke-Manglaur (bypass) national highway near Roorkee on Sunday morning. According to police, the incident took place when a car tried to overtake the bike, but collided with an oil tanker coming from the wrong direction, as a result of which the bike too got hit. The car driver fled the spot. The dead were identified as Manoj (26), a resident of Dholpur in Rajasthan, Anil Kumar (22), a resident of UP's Firozabad, and Pradeep (24) from Agra. While Manoj and Anil died on the spot, Pradeep was taken to a nearby hospital where he succumbed to his injuries. "The bodies were sent for autopsy and a detailed investigation will be done," Abhinav Sharma, senior sub-inspector at Civil Lines police station, told TOI.

Title: Driver killed as ambulance rams into median in Hyderabad
content:
HYDERABAD: The driver of an ambulance was killed after the vehicle crashed into a median at BN Neddy Nagar in Hyderabad's Vanasthaliputam on Tuesday morning. The accident happened when the private ambulance was going towards Sagar Road after dropping a patient at Ibrahimpatnam. After police shifted the victim from the accident spot, the ambulance caught fire and the two oxygen cylinders in the vehicle exploded after which the entire ambulance got engulfed in flames. "The ambulance driver succumbed to the injuries at the spot after he was pulled out of the vehicle. No one suffered injuries in the oxygen cylinder explosion, " Vanasthalipuram police said. A case was registered under sections 304-A of the IPC and sections 183 and 184 of the MV Act.
