Title: Ahmedabad: Car with drunk men flips over in Maninagar
content: AHMEDABAD: Four days after a speeding car killed nine people on the Iskcon flyover, a recklessly driven sedan with a drunk man behind the wheel, and his three friends drinking beer inside it, turned turtle in Maninagar, in the wee hours of Monday.According to an FIR filed with J Division traffic police, the police control room got a message that there were four drunk men in a car on the footpath of Vallabhvadi Road in Maninagar.A J Division traffic police team went to the spot and saw the damaged Ciaz car. One of the four men was slightly injured and was also found to be drunk. When police checked the other three men, they found that they were drunk as well.An angry crowd had gathered and roughed up the men. Members of the public found two bottles of beer in the car and an empty bottle, which they gave to the police.Police officers said the car driver was identified as Kedar Dave, 25, a resident of Anand Society-1 near Jaymala Society in Isanpur. His three friends were Preet Soni, 25, a resident of Jaygopal Society in Maninagar; Rutvik Mandalia, 23, a resident of Anand Society in Isanpur and Swaraj Jadhav, 23, a resident of Ashok Society in Kankaria. They have all been arrested.Onlookers said four people were sitting on a bench with others gathered on the foot path when Dave drove the car straight at them. Fortunately, all of them evaded the car, which turned turtle after hitting the footpath.J Division traffic police registered a case of rash driving and causing damag e under the IPC along with charges under the Motor Vehicles Act for drunk driving.

Title: Ahmedabad ISKCON flyover accident: Tathya Patel broke speed regulations 25 times in past month, say cops
content: AHMEDABAD: After Tathya Patel killed nine people and left 13 injured when he drove a speeding Jaguar into a crowd on Iskcon flyover on Thursday, the police combed through CCTV records and found the 19-year-old was allegedly a 'history speedster', allegedly speeding over the limit a staggering 25 times in the past month.Ironically, while the city traffic police claim to be cracking down on speedsters with over 100 e-challans issued on SG Road, the teenager's Thar SUV and Jaguar car somehow evaded their radar. Neither of these luxury vehicles attracted a challan. Tathya was also allegedly involved in two accidents during this period, which were reportedly brushed under the carpet.Based on statements from witnesses, the city traffic police mapped Tathya's locations between June 20 and July 20, during which he was spotted driving different high-end cars, said sources. "CCTV footage revealed that he frequently drove at speeds ranging between 120kmph and 140kmph on stretches like SG Road, Sindhu Bhavan Road, SP Ring Road, and even internal roads of the city," said a senior traffic officer.And if that wasnâ€™t enough, the investigation into the Iskcon flyover tragedy also revealed that Tathya had blatantly jumped a red light five times in a month. Surprisingly, he neither received an e-challan nor was confronted by any traffic police for these violations.. .Advertisement Before Tathya was involved in the Iskcon flyover tragedy, he had allegedly lost control of his vehicle and rammed it into a restaurant on Sindhu Bhavan Road. A video timestamped around 6.13am on July 3 this year showed the driver starting a black Thar and crashing it into the gate of a restaurant merely 100m away. The car was purportedly driven by Tathya Patel, allegedly accompanied by a group of youths. The 19-year-old was also involved in an accident under the Gandhinagar police jurisdiction in the past month, said N N Chaudhary, additional CP (traffic), Ahmedabad. â€œWitnesses told us about the accident for which he was paid insurance. We are investigating the matter,â€ regarding which an inquiry is on,â€ said N N Chaudhary. The forensic science laboratory (FSL) in Gandhinagar on Monday submitted its report of Thursdayâ€™s accident to the city traffic police. The findings revealed that Tathya wasnâ€™t inebriated at the time of the accident. However, according to the report, the Jaguar was clocking a bone-chilling 141.27kmph at the moment of impact. When asked how the speedster who has been tearing through the cityâ€™s roads escaped notice, DCP traffic (admin) Baldevsinh Vaghela said, â€œWe are equipped with speed guns and have been issuing echallans daily, but we will add 30 more speed guns to curb the speed menace.â€ Bringing about one of the most tragic accidents on city roads in recent history, Tathya Patel, in a speeding Jaguar, had crashed into a crowd that had gathered to check out another accident at the flyover. â€œJust before the accident occurred, he was zooming through internal roads around Karnavati Club. He and his friends then decided to drive on S G Highway. They had the music at full blast and were indulging in reckless revelry. His distraction cost nine innocent people their lives,â€ said an officer

Title: 3 killed, 14 hurt as truck hits SRTC bus
content: LUCKNOW: Three persons were killed and 14 others injured after a speeding truck hit an Uttar Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation (UPSRTC) bus near Tala Mod of Ramsanehighat on Lucknow-Ayodhya highway on Monday evening.Barabanki additional SP Akhilesh Kumar Singh said that NHAI had closed one side of the NH for repairs, thus allowing single-lane movement of traffic on the stretch."In the meantime, a bus coming from Ayodhya Depot collided head-on with the truck," said ASP Singh.The impact was such that some of the passengers were tossed out of the bus, which eventually hurtled and hit a tree before coming to a grinding halt.A police team immediately reached the spot and took all the passengers to the local CHC, where doctors declared Shobha Devi (60), Vineet Saxena (55), and Rajmal Gautam (55) dead.The additional SP added that Rajmal Gautam was at the wheels of the bus, while the truck driver escaped after the accident."Three others identified as Ravi Kumar Chauhan (30), Sunil Kumar (55) and Raheysham Gupta (52) were referred to KGMU trauma centre in Lucknow as their condition was critical," said the police.Five others were shifted to district hospital in Barabanki and others were let off after primary treatment at the CHC.

Title: Tathya violated speed limit 25 times last month, claim Ahmedabad cops
content: AHMEDABAD: After Tathya Patel killed nine people and left 13 injured when he drove a speeding Jaguar into a crowd on the Iskcon flyover on July 20, cops combed through CCTV records and found the 19-year-old was allegedly a â€˜history speedsterâ€™, who has been playing with danger, speeding over the limit a staggering 25 times in the past month.Ironically, while the city traffic police claim to be cracking down on speedsters with over 100 e-challans issued on SG Road, the teenagerâ€™s Thar SUV and Jaguar car somehow evaded their radar. Neither of these luxury vehicles attracted a challan. Tathya was also allegedly involved in two accidents during this period, which were reportedly brushed under the carpet.Based on statements from witnesses, the city traffic police mapped Tathyaâ€™s locations between June 20 and July 20, during which he was spotted driving different high-end cars, said sources. â€œCCTV footage revealed that he frequently drove at speeds ranging between 120kmph and 140kmph on stretches like SG Road, Sindhu Bhavan Road, SP Ring Road, and even internal roads of the city,â€ said a senior traffic officer.And if that wasnâ€™t enough, the investigation into the Iskcon flyover tragedy also revealed that Tathya had blatantly jumped a red light five times in a month. Surprisingly, he neither received an e-challan nor was confronted by any traffic police for these violations.Before Tathya was involved in the Iskcon flyover tragedy, he had allegedly lost control of his vehicle and rammed it into a restaurant on Sindhu Bhavan Road.The 19-year-old was also involved in an accident under the Gandhinagar police jurisdiction in the past month, said N N Chaudhary, additional CP (traffic), Ahmedabad. â€œWitnesses told us about the accident for which he was paid insurance. â€œWe are investigating the matter, regarding which an inquiry is on,â€ said Chaudhary.On Monday, the forensic science laboratory (FSL) in Gandhinagar submitted its report of the Iskcon flyover accident to the city traffic police. The findings revealed that Tathya wasnâ€™t inebriated at the time of the accident. However, according to the report, the Jaguar was clocking a bone-chilling 141.27kmph at the moment of impact.

Title: Haryana: 2 children among 4 killed, woman critical as canter rams into parked car on KMP Expressway
content: NEW DELHI: In a devastating incident on the Kundliâ€“Manesarâ€“Palwal Expressway (KMP) Expressway, four people, including two children and a woman, lost their lives when a speeding canter collided with a parked Tata Tiago. Another woman sustained serious injuries in the accident.The collision occurred near Mandothi village in Bahadurgarh district, and authorities were quick to respond to the scene. The injured woman was initially taken to Bahadurgarh General Hospital for immediate medical attention before being rushed to PGI Rohtak due to her critical condition.All the victims hailed from Meerut and Moradabad districts. The driver of the canter, identified as Arun Kumar, revealed that they were on their way back to Meerut via Pushkar in Rajasthan after visiting Khatu Shyam temple and Ajmer Sharif dargah. At approximately 3.30 pm, Arun pulled over the vehicle on the roadside near Mandothi village and disembarked briefly. Tragically, at that very moment, a canter coming from behind collided with their parked car.Efforts were made to rescue the injured woman from the wrecked vehicle. However, despite the efforts, she sustained severe injuries in the accident.Following the incident, the canter driver fled the scene, prompting a search by the police. Authorities have begun their investigation into the cause of the accident and have cleared the debris from the expressway.The bodies of the deceased have been transferred to the Bahadurgarh General Hospital for post mortem examinations. The police have also notified the family members of the victims, and further actions will be taken upon their arrival.

Title: Four of family dead in Bijnor road accident
content:
BIJNOR: Four members of a family were killed and two others sustained injuries when the auto-rickshaw they were travelling in was hit by an unidentified vehicle in Bijnor district late Monday night. The incident occurred on Haridwar- Nainital National Highway (NH 74) close to Sikanderpur crossing area under Kotwali Dehat police station area of Bijnor, said police.Meera Saini (26), her daughter Priya (9) and son Shivam (8) died on the spot, Kotwali SHO Jaiveer Singh said. Meera's husband Rohit Saini (28), another daughter Mahi (4) and brother Vikas Saini (21) were injured and rushed to a hospital in Meerut in critical condition, he said. Saini succumbed to injuries during treatment. The family, hailing from Mandi Dhanaura, was on its way to AIIMS Rishikesh to admit Rohit for a gallbladder operation, the SHO said.

Title: Illegal parking on Mum-Agra highway flyover claims 6 lives
content: Nashik: There have been six deaths so far this year due to stationary vehicles on the flyover of the Mumbai-Agra Highway in the city even as police claim that they are patrolling the stretch continuously.The latest victim was a 21-year-old biker, who slammed into a stationary vehicle on the flyover. In May, a 29-year-old woman and her daughter were killed after their car was hit by a container truck on the flyover. Similar accidents were reported in March, February and January as well.â€œWhile driving towards Mumbai, I take the flyover of the highway from Dwarka. I regularly come across vehicles that are halted on the side of the road. The speed of vehicles is high and motorists assume that the road is free from any obstructions. However, if a motorist following a heavy vehicle suddenly switches lanes to overtake the truck in front of him and if there is a vehicle halted on the roadside, the motorist is taken by surprise. Then it depends on the luck of the motorist whether he meets with an accident or not,â€ Ravi Godse, an entrepreneur said. The city police claim illegal halting of vehicles on the flyover is largely responsible for accidents on the elevated corridor of the highway. Commissioner of Police Ankush Shinde said, â€œI have advised the traffic branch to take serious cognizance of illegal halting of motorists on the flyover in view of the accidents. The traffic police will be patrolling the flyover and will appeal motorists to not halt their vehicles. If there is any mechanical issue with the vehicle, commuters should contact police for further help. I also appeal to motorists to put up warning triangles or reflectors in order to alert other motorists on the flyover that they are approaching a stationary vehicle.â€

Title: 24-year-old man speeding in his car rams into wall, dies
content:
Vadodara: A 24-year-old man died after his car crashed into a compound wall near Pandya bridge in the city, late on Tuesday night. Deceased Gunjan Swami, who was driving the car, died from the severe injuries he had sustained. The police have registered a case in the incident and begun investigations. According to cops, Swami along with his friend Arjunsinh Thakur were on their way home at about 2am when he lost control of the car and rammed into the compound wall of a food and drugs laboratory near the bridge. The police are investigating the carâ€™s speed as the vehicle suffered extensive damage. Some passers-by rushed to help the duo and took them to the hospital, where Swami, a resident of Harni Road, died during treatment. Over the past three days, the police have put up barricades on certain roads in the city to ensure no speeding during late nights. The police said that it has started a special drive to impound vehicles and penalize those driving without proper documents, licenses or PUCs in the city. The move follows the gruesome accident in Ahmedabad where nine people were killed after being hit by a speeding luxury vehicle.

Title: Lab technician trying to dodge uneaven road run over by truck
content: THANE: A 35 year old lab technician from Thane east was killed after he fell off his bike and got run over by a loaded container truck at the city outskirts on Ghodbunder road, Thursday morning, officials informed.Regulars and neighbours of the deceased didnâ€™t deny the possibility that the mishap may have occurred when he tried to dodge an uneven patch of road on the stretch opposite Gaimukh chowpatty. The road stretch was recently battered following heavy rains. Incidentally, the state public works department that owns and maintains the highway denied the road was badly maintained and claimed to have recently repaired it inlcuding near Gaimukh chowpatty where the mishap occurred.The incident occurred on the Ghodbunder to Thane lane of the arterial highway around 8am when the victim identified as Devidas Chavan, a resident of Chendani Koliwada, Thane east was returning to his lab in Thane city after collecting a blood sample from a patient in Borivali. Chavan was riding solo when he apparently lost balance and skid off the road and came under the wheels of the heavy truck that was also moving towards Thane city and got crushed, informed a Kasarwadavli police official.Passersby and the police rushed him to the nearest hospital where he was declared dead by the doctors. The police have arrested the truck driver Manojkumar Chabilal (33) under relevant sections of the IPC. Further investigations are on. Chavan is survived by his aged mother, banker wife and a 1.5 years old child.Bhushan Jadhav, a neighbour and friend of Chavan said they will follow up with the authorities to know if the highway condition where the mishap occurred was bad.

Title: 72 dead in natural disasters, road accidents since June 15
content:
DEHRADUN: As many as 72 people have been killed in natural disasters and road accidents since the start of rains this year (June 15) in Uttarakhand, state government data has revealed. According to the natural disaster report of the State Emergency Operation Centre (SEOC), 29 people have lost their lives in natural disasters - landslides being the biggest killer - in the last one-and-a-half months. Of the 29 fatalities, 19 were reported in the last 20 days. On June 14, TOI had reported that over a dozen people were killed and three went missing after they were hit by falling boulders in the hills, after merely four days of heavy rainfall in the state. Besides, 32 people have suffered injuries in these incidents, with the majority of them being reported since July 10, when rain-triggered natural disasters increased in the state. Uttarkashi and Haridwar districts have been the worst affected, with five deaths each. Dehradun witnessed three deaths due to natural disasters, while Chamoli and Rudraprayag witnessed two deaths each. As far as road accidents are concerned, 43 people were killed, and 147 injured since June 15 this year, according to SEOC data.

Title: Speeding bike kills student, biker shows no remorse
content: KOCHI: Ernakulam Rural police, probing the horrific accident near Muvattupuzha Nirmala College in which a 20-year-old BCom student died after a speeding bike rammed into her around 4.45pm on Wednesday, has invoked charges for culpable homicide not amounting to murder against the biker Anson Roy (22), a native of Kalloorkad.Police have collected blood samples of the accused as passersby and hospital authorities have raised suspicion that he may have been under the influence of intoxicants. It was Namitha R, a fifth semester student of BCom at Nirmala College Muvattupuzha, who died in the accident. Another student, Anushree Raj (20), sustained serious injuries in the incident. The college premises on Thursday witnessed emotional scenes as Namitha's body was brought for paying last respects.Police said the accused was a history sheeter and had cases of substance abuse and attempted murder against him. There were reports that Anson was agitated even after the accident and reportedly told rescuers that it was common for vehicles to meet with accidents, police said. The students of the college had protested outside the hospital, irked by his behaviour.CCTV visuals show the speeding bike ramming the victims when they were trying to cross the road. Anushree got tossed off in the impact while Namitha was dragged for a distance before the bike stopped.The students were on their way home after writing exams at the college. Anson too sustained injuries and passersby had rushed all the three to a nearby hospital, where Namitha succumbed to injuries.A senior Rural police officer said that Anson was later admitted to the Kottayam Medical College Hospital and his arrest would be recorded at the earliest. "He was involved in narcotics cases in the past. We will know if he was under the influence of liquor or any other substance only when his blood test results come in. We are collecting statements from other students who witnessed the incident. If needed, more charges would be invoked against the accused," said an officer. Police plan to record Anson's statement as soon as he becomes stable. Police said that some of the students have raised allegations that Anson was earlier warned to not speed through the area. Authorities have also initiated action to suspend his license and cancel the bike's registration. Anushree's condition is reported to be stable. Namitha's body was cremated after last rites were held at her house in Valakom. Namitha is the daughter of Raghu and Girija.

Title: Married womanâ€™s father, brother among 9 held for killing her lover
content:
Indore: Nine members of the family of a married woman have been booked in Betma for beating her lover to death and making it look like an accident.On July 8, police found two men lying unconscious on the roadside, next to a wrecked motorcycle, Rural SP Hitika Wasal said. Initially, they thought it was an accident. The duo was taken to hospital, where one of them â€” later identified as Sudama â€” was declared dead on arrival.The other victim, Manoj Choudhary, was in a critical condition and unable to speak till Wednesday, when he recovered enough to give a statement to the cops. â€œHe said Sudama had an affair with a married woman, Sadhna, of the same village. When her husband and his family came to know about it, she was sent back to her parentsâ€™. Sadhna called Sudama on her sister-in-lawâ€™s phone and asked him to meet her,â€ said Wasal.The sister-in-law told her husband and the family planned to kill Sudama, said the SP. â€œThey held Manoj and Sudama captive in a room in the house where Sadhnaâ€™s father Mukesh, brother Balram, his two friends and her sister-in-law attacked them brutally,â€ said Wasal.â€œWhen both of them fell unconscious, the family decided to make it look like an accident. They planted fake evidence on the road and damaged the bike. It was only because Manoj regained consciousness that he could reveal what happened,â€ said an officer. Wasal said that a case was registered against Sadhna and her kin for murder. Her mother and grandmother have been booked for tampering with evidence.

Title: UP: Woman, 3 daughters, die as vehicle hits ambulance carrying husbandâ€™s body
content: KANPUR: In a tragic accident, a 70-year-old woman and her three daughters were killed when the ambulance in which they were bringing home the mortal remains of the head of the family was hit by an unidentified vehicle early Friday morning.Another daughter of the woman was critically injured in the accident which took place at Purva-Mohanlalganj road near Tusraur village.Chief minister Yogi Adityanath has expressed grief over the incident and ordered the administration to make all necessary arrangements for the treatment of the injured person.The impact of the accident was such that the ambulance was blown into pieces. Surprisingly, the ambulance driver went missing after the accident and was untraceable till the last reports came in.The deceased were identified as Prema Savita (70), wife of Dhaniram, Manjula (45), Anjali (40) and Ruby (30).The condition of Sudha (36), the other daughter of Prema, is stated to be critical.The bodies have been sent for postmortem.Dhaniram Savita (75), a native of Maurawan area, had retired as a class IV employee from KNPN Inter College Maurawan in 2007. Search operation to locate whereabouts of ambulance driver: PoliceAbout a week ago, he had suffered a paralysis attack and was having difficulty breathing. The family members had taken him to the district hospital on July 24. Doctors, however, referred him to Kanpurâ€™s Lala Lajpat Rai hospital where he died on Friday. The family members were heading home with his mortal remains in a private ambulance. As soon as it reached near Tusraur village on the Purva-Mohanlalganj road around 5am, an unknown vehicle rammed into an ambulance. On information, superintendent of police (SP) Siddharth Shankar Meena, ASP Shashi Shekhar and CO Deepak Singh reached the spot and supervised the rescue work. The SP said, â€œWe have launched a search operation to locate the whereabouts of the ambulance driver, who has been missing since the accident occurred. In order to know more details pertaining to the accident, the ambulance driverâ€™s interrogation is relevant.â€

Title: Six killed, 21 injured as two buses collide in Maharashtra's Buldhana district
content:
MUMBAI: At least six people died and 21 others injured after two private buses collided in the early hours of Saturday in Maharashtra's Buldhana district.The accident took place around 2.30am on a flyover in Malkapur town in Buldhana. Two women are among those killed in the accident.One of the buses involved in the accident was on its way to Hingoli after Amarnath Yatra, while the other private bus was going towards Nashik, reported PTI quoting official.The bus going towards Nashik tried to overtake a truck and ended up coming in front of the second bus, resulting in a collision, official added.Those injured have been admitted to a nearby hospital.The accident disrupted traffic on the road for some time.

Title: Girl, 7, teen among 4 dead in 2 accidents
content:
Bengaluru: Four people, including a seven-year-old girl, were killed in two accidents since Thursday. In the first, a teenager and his 38-year-old aunt and her daughter, riding pillion on his bike, were killed after a speeding truck hit them near Hosakote on Thursday. The second accident was reported in the wee hours of Friday when a 28-year-old private company employee died after his car jumped the median and hit a car coming from the opposite direction on KIA expressway near Hunasemaranahalli, Yelahanka.In the Hosakote accident, police identified the victims as homemaker N Shweta, her daughter N Sukruta and nephew Manohar, a first-year degree student, all from Hosakote. The accident occurred around 5.30pm near Jadigenahalli when the truck rear-ended Manoharâ€™s bike, throwing all the three on the road. They were crushed to death under the truck wheels; the driver is absconding.In the second accident, Varun BR from Sanjaynagar was driving towards KIA to pick up a relative when his car jumped the median near Chikkajala and collided with a car coming from the opposite direction at 2am. tnn

Title: Two techies killed, 1 missing after car falls into Nira-Devghar dam
content: PUNE: Two software engineers were killed, one escaped with injuries and another was missing till late Saturday night after the car they were travelling in fell into the Nira-Deoghar dam at Shirgaon village in Bhor taluka around 8.30am.The accident occurred on the Bhor-Mahad highway near Varandha Ghat. The four software engineers of a private firm in Baner had gone there in the car despite police banning outsiders' vehicles on the stretch last week.The Bhor police recovered the bodies of two software engineers from the car. Additional superintendent of police Anand Bhoite of the Pune rural police told TOI, "We have found the bsodies of Akashay Dhade (27) of Ravet and Harshpreet Harpreet Bamba (30) of Pashan. Bamba hailed from Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh."The police said Dhade was driving the car.A Bhor police officer said, "The injured techie, Sanket Joshi, survived after he fell from the car before it plunged into the water of the dam. He has been admitted to a hospital and we have recorded his statement."Bhoite said, "A search for the other techie, Swapnnil Shinde (28) from Hadapsar, was conducted with the help of the Sahyadri Rescue Team from Bhor, Bhoiraj Mandal, Disaster Management Cell personnel and other youngsters till late on Saturday. The operation was finally called off because of bad light. The search operation will resume on Sunday morning."After accident, cops to maintain vigilAccording to Joshiâ€™s statement to the police, the four software engineers had spoken among themselves over the phone on Friday night and had planned a day out at Varandha Ghat on Saturday. The police said their car was travelling towards the ghat from Pune on Saturday morning. The road remains damp near Varandha Ghat due to the ongoing rain, the source said. The police said, â€œIt is suspected that the driver lost control over the car at a turn. It must have veered off the road and plunged into the waters. It fell about 50 metres into the dam water.â€ Inspector Rekha Wani of the Bhor police, which is investigating the case, said, â€œWe have decided to conduct a naka bandi operation on the road, following this accident, which has lead to loss of lives. We will not allow picnickers to visit the area in their private vehicles. We will allow only residents of the area to use their vehicles and the road.â€

Title: 6 die, 49 injured after 2 buses collide head-on near Malkapur
content: NAGPUR: Six persons, including three women, were killed and 49 were injured â€” 18 seriously and 31 with minor injuries â€” after two buses collided head-on on an under-construction flyover on NH6 near Malkapur city in Buldhana district at 2.45am on Saturday. All the six deceased persons were pilgrims returning to Hingoli district after a 19-day tour. After visiting Vaishno Devi shrine, Amarnath, Vrindavan, Mathura and such places, the pilgrims were just a few hours from home when they met with the accident.The deceased were identified as Shivaji Jagtap, 65, owner of Balaji Tirth Yatra Company, its bus driver Santosh Jagtap (38), Radhabai Gade (60), Archana Buske (28), Kanupatra Tekale (45) and Sachin Mahade. One of the injured persons, Sangeeta Potdar, 52, a resident of Ambazari, was in the another bus that was heading to Nashik.The 18 seriously injured pilgrims are admitted to different hospitals in Buldhana city and around. Another 31 received minor injuries. â€œThe bus had returned from Rameshwaram and other places of pilgrimages in south India around 15 days ago. We were part of that earlier tour. The tour to north India had started on July 10 and was set to conclude in the next few hours in Hingoli,â€ said Devidas Jagtap who rushed to Hingoli after learning about the accident to help pilgrims from his district.A six-lane flyover is under construction near Malkapur. Three lanes on one side have been opened for traffic in both directions despite absence of any barricade, marking or proper lighting.Police said that a Royal travels bus, driven by Vikrant Samrit, was heading to Nashik from Nagpur. Itâ€™s learnt, Samrit tried to overtake a truck and rammed the bus of Balaji Tirth Yatra Company on the driverâ€™s side. An offence of causing death due to negligence was registered against Samrit. Balaji Coâ€™s bus driver, owner and a pilgrim died on the spot. Two were declared dead at government hospital in Buldhana while one person died on the way to hospital. SP Sunil Kadasne, who reached the spot at 4.30am, said the impact was so devastating that casualties could not be avoided despite early intervention. Deputy RTO Prasad Gajare, who too visited the spot, said the documents and road worthiness of both the vehicles were found to be updated. â€œRegional traffic office (RTO) has suggested that cones, catâ€™s eye or ropes are needed for lane demarcation on the flyover. Double continuous lanes must be drawn on the road to prohibit overtaking. Solar bulbs are required for illumination,â€ said Gajare. Devotees from a gurdwara in Malkapur rushed to the spot after an appeal from SP Kadasne. The injured were taken to the gurdwara where a doctor attended to them. â€œThe serious ones were taken to the hospital but the others were in shock too. They were offered food, shelter and help at the gurdwara before buses were arranged for them to return,â€ said Kadasne.

Title: School student run over by speeding sumo in Raigarh, dies
content:
Raipur: A 17-year-old girl student was run over by a speeding Sumo on Friday evening in Pauri village in Raigarh district, killing her on the spot. Enraged by the incident, the villagers set the vehicle on fire. According to police officials, the deceased has been identified as Preeti Kaste, a class 10 student, who was waiting for an auto on the side of the road when a speeding Sumo carrying people returning from a wedding knocked her down.Angry local people had gathered around the four-wheeler and set it on fire before the police arrived.The locals who witnessed the accident informed the police, following which the police reached the spot and calmed the situation. During interrogation, it was found that the driver of the vehicle had absconded. The protestors demanded compensation for the deceased's family. The girl's body was later sent for post-mortem. The vehicle that hit her was seized, and the investigation is still ongoing, added the police.

Title: 6 die, 18 seriously injured after 2 buses collide head-on near Malkapur
content: Nagpur: Six persons, including three women, were killed and another 18 were seriously injured after two buses collided head-on on an under-construction flyover on NH6 near Malkapur city in Buldhana district at 2.45am on Saturday. All the six deceased persons were pilgrims returning to Hingoli district after a 19-day tour. After visiting Vaishno Devi shrine, Amarnath, Vrindavan, Mathura and such places, the pilgrims were just a few hours from home when they met with the accident.The deceased were identified as Shivaji Jagtap, 65, owner of Balaji Tirth Yatra Company, its bus driver Santosh Jagtap (38), Radhabai Gade (60), Archana Buske (28), Kanupatra Tekale (45) and Sachin Mahade. One of the injured persons, Sangeeta Potdar, 52, a resident of Ambazari, was in the another bus that was heading to Nashik.The 18 seriously injured pilgrims are admitted to different hospitals in Buldhana city and around. Another 31 received minor injuries. â€œThe bus had returned from Rameshwaram and other places of pilgrimages in south India around 15 days ago. We were part of that earlier tour. The tour to north India had started on July 10 and was set to conclude in the next few hours in Hingoli,â€ said Devidas Jagtap who rushed to Hingoli after learning about the accident to help pilgrims from his district.A six-lane flyover is under construction near Malkapur. Three lanes on one side of the bridge have been opened for traffic which is moving in both directions despite absence of any barricade, marking or proper lighting.Police said that a Royal travels bus, driven by Vikrant Samrit, was heading to Nashik from Nagpur. Itâ€™s learnt, Samrit tried to overtake a truck and and rammed the bus of Balaji Tirth Yatra Company on the driverâ€™s side. An offence of causing death due to negligence was registered against Samrit.Balaji Coâ€™s bus driver, owner and a pilgrim died on the spot. Two were declared dead at government hospital in Buldhana while one person died on the way to hospital.SP Sunil Kadasne, who reached the spot at 4.30am, said the impact was so devastating that casualties could not be avoided despite early intervention.Deputy RTO Prasad Gajare, who too visited the spot, said the documents and road worthiness of both the vehicles were found to be updated. â€œRegional traffic office (RTO) has suggested that cones, catâ€™s eye or ropes are needed for lane demarcation on the flyover. Double continuous lanes must be drawn on the road to prohibit overtaking. Solar bulbs are required for illumination,â€ said Gajare.Devotees from a gurdwara in Malkapur rushed to the spot after an appeal from SP Kadasne. The injured were taken to the gurdwara where a doctor attended to them. â€œThe serious ones were taken to the hospital but the others were in shock too. They were offered food, shelter and help at the gurdwara before buses were arranged for them to return,â€ said Kadasne.

Title: Two bikers killed in separate accidents
content: PUNE: A 48-year-old and a 57-year-old motorcyclist were killed in two separate road accidents on Saturday.In the first incident, Ashok Sonawane of Tathawade was killed after a private bus dashed straight into his scooter while he was trying to take a U-turn at Thergaon."The victimâ€™s brother, Shankar, told us that around 10.30 p.m. on Saturday, Ashok left home for some work at Dange Chowk. On reaching Thergaon, he was taking a U-turn to reach Dange Chowk when the bus hit him. The victim and his scooter were trapped, and they skidded under the bus for some distance," the officer from Wakad police station said."We have registered a case of causing death by negligence against the bus driver, whose identity is yet to be established," he added.In the second incident, Samiruddin Ansari, a 57-year-old resident of Chakan, died after his motorcycle was hit by a truck on the Pune-Nashik highway near Talegaon Chowk in Chakan. The accident happened when Ansari was driving his motorcycle towards Ambethan Chowk. The Chakan police have arrested the truck driver on the charge of causing death by negligence.

Title: Truck rams car, 57-year-old policeman dies in west Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: A 57-year-old Delhi Police inspector posted with the security unit of the President's cell was killed when a truck rammed into his car on Rohtak Road near the Madipur metro station in west Delhi early on Sunday morning.The truck driver, who left the vehicle and fled, has not been arrested. Police are scanning CCTV footage to track him down.The deceased officer, Jagbir Singh, was from Haryana and lived in the Vikaspuri police colony.According to police, around 4 am on Sunday Singh was returning from a function when the mishap occurred. DCP (west) Vichitra Veer said Singh's car had stopped because of a mechanical problem when the truck hit it from behind."The truck was driven negligently and at a high speed. The impact was such that the rear side of the car was damaged," an officer said.An inquiry revealed that at the time of the incident, the inspector was outside the car and his body was flung aside after the truck hit his car. The truck dragged the sedan for about 20 meters and rammed into a divider. "The victim had just pushed his car to the roadside when the truck hit the vehicle," police said.A passer-by informed cops about the incident. "The passer-by informed us that he saw two vehicles in an accident," the officer said.The police have registered a case under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way) and 304 A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC. They said they are scanning CCTV cameras in nearby areas and have formed teams to nab the accused.Singh, a native of Jind, had joined the police in 1994. He was posted in the security unit and in 1996, he was transferred to the southwest district. In 2000, he was transferred to the security unit.Later, he was deployed with PCR operations. In 2021, he was again transferred to the security unit. The victim was a postgraduate in Hindi. He is survived by wife, a son and a daughter.Delhi Police tweeted out an obituary. "All officers and personnel of #DelhiPolice pay homage to the departed soul of Inspector Jagbir Singh and express deep condolences to the bereaved family," the tweet said.

Title: Engineer dies after car hits tree on NICE Rd
content:
Bengaluru: A 32-year-old software engineer working in a private firm was killed after his car hit a tree on NICE Road near Vrishabhavathi bridge past midnight on Saturday.The deceased is Nikhil Kumar, a resident of Tataguni in south Bengaluru. Citing preliminary investigation, Kengeri traffic police said that Kumar was driving his Hyundai i20 car and was heading towards Kengeri from Kanakapura Road. After passing the Vrishabhavathi bridge around 12.30am, he lost control of the vehicle and drove the car into the trench on the side of the road and came to a halt only after hitting a tree. Kumar died inside the car. The carâ€™s front portion was severely damaged due to the impact, an officer said.The officer said Kumar tried to overtake a truck when the right rear view mirror of his car touched the vehicle. Kumar swerved to the left and lost control. â€œWe are verifying the statements of other motorists,â€ the officer added. Police said the impact of the accident shows that Kumar was over-speeding. According to his parents, Kumar received a call from his friend asking him to pick him up. He told them about it and left home. A case has been registered in Kengeri traffic police station limits.

Title: School van rammed by loader, 7-year-old girl dead, 7 children injured in UP's Pratapgarh
content:
PRAYAGRAJ: A seven-year-old girl student was killed whereas seven other children sustained injuries when the school van carrying them got hit by a speeding loader near Laxmanpur block road under the limits of Leelapur police station of Uttar Pradesh's Pratapgarh district on Monday. The deceased, identified as Mansi Saroj, died on the spot. Upon being alerted, senior cops and administrative officials rushed to the spot and carried out rescue operation. All the seven injured including Yash Prajapati (7), Ayush Prajapati (8), Naina Saroj (8), Kartik Saroj (8), Sanjana (9), Vaibhav Singh (8) and Arpit Singh( 8), all residents of Patulki were admitted to Leelapur CHC. Doctors later referred Yash Prajapati and the injured driver named Sariq (50) to district hospital in Pratapgarh for treatment. According to police, the incident took place when the van ferrying school children was on its way to drop them at a block-based school and a speeding loader coming from opposite direction collided with it.

Title: Couple killed as minibus hits bike in Garden Reach
content: Kolkata: A young couple on a bike were killed trying to dodge potholes on a crater riddled road in Garden Reach when they were hit by a minibus from behind on Sunday afternoon.Md Kalim (20), and Reshma Khatun (19), who were in a relationship, were returning from Reshmaâ€™s home in Kidderpore to their home at Fatehpur Village Road in Garden Reach when the accident took place on CGR Road and Sick Lane crossing around 1pm.â€œThe road here is full of potholes. The biker was trying to dodge a crater and slowed down in the middle of the road when the bus hit them. Had the road been in a better shape, the two could have been saved and the accident may not have occurred at all,â€ said Md Rafique who was at a nearby store when the accident happened.The impact flung both the riders off the two-wheeler. As they were not wearing helmets both suffered severe head injuries. There was profuse loss of blood before locals could call cops who immediately rushed the two to SSKM Hospital, more than seven km away. However, they succumbed to their injuries on the way to hospital.â€œIt was a tragic accident. We have arrested the bus driver and have seized the minibus,â€ said an officer of West port police station.Kalimâ€™s family members said he was a friendly young man who had recently started work as a tailor and was also into the business of supplying cut pieces to multiple garment manufacturing units in Metiabruz. â€œTheir deaths have left the two families shattered,â€ said Akbar Ali, a relative of Kalim.In another accident early on Sunday, a rickshaw puller, Labad Ali Baidya (46) was killed when he was hit by an unidentified vehicle on Sekharpur Road near Bhojerhat bridge off Bantala. The body of another 45-year-old accident victim was found on EM Bypass near Chhitkalikapur who was supposedly hit by a vehicle. The victimâ€™s identity hasnâ€™t been ascertained yet.Seven other persons were injured on Sunday in four separate road accidents through the day on Basanti Highway, near Maidan, Park Circus connector and Prince Anwar Shah connector.

Title: Two youth killed after bike crashes into divider
content:
Hyderabad: In a suspected case of drunken driving, two persons, who were travelling on a bike, died after they rammed their two-wheeler into the median at ECIL Crossroads in the wee hours of Sunday. Kushaiguda police said that Naresh and Kranthi, both 23 years, were residents of Moula Ali. Around 4.40am, the two reached ECIL X roads on the bike. However, the rider lost control over the bike and hit the divider and fell off the vehicle. They died on the spot. They were suspectedly returning home, but en route the accident took place. â€œWe have taken the blood samples to ascertain whether it was a case of drunken driving as there was suspicion,â€™â€™ Kushaiguda inspector B Praveen Kumar told TOI. . .AdvertisementPolice said that one of the deceased was a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) student, while the other a private employee. A case was registered under Section 304-A (Negligence causing death) of the IPC.

Title: Teen killed after car falls off bridge, 4 hurt in 2nd mishap
content:
KALYAN: A 14-year-old boy from Kalyan died after the car in which he was travelling with his family fell off a bridge near Malshej ghat, near Kalyan, in Thane district, on Sunday. His parents and three other relatives who were also in the car escaped with minor injuries. In another accident, four men from Nashik were injured after their car overturned, also near Malshej ghat. The boy and his family had gone for a day's outing to Kalu waterfall. As their car was passing over a bridge, the driver lost control and the vehicle flipped over and veered off the road, landing in a gorge about 25 feet below. The boy, Harshavardhan Wagh, a class 9 student, had sustained serious injuries. He was taken to the primary health centre where doctors declared him 'brought dead'. The teenager's father was driving when the mishap took place, said assistant inspector of Kinhavali police station, Sachin Kulkarni. In the other accident, four youths who had come to Malshej ghat sustained injuries after their vehicle flipped over. They suffered minor injuries and were taken to hospital, said police sub-inspector Sachin Sansare. Malshej ghat is a popular tourist attraction due to its beautiful surroundings. Many picnickers from Mumbai, Thane, Nashik and Pune visit the place.

Title: Woman cop dies in mishap
content:
Jaipur: A woman constable was killed and a sub-inspector (SI) was injured when the car in which they were travelling rammed into a vehicle on the express highway on Ajmer Road on Sunday. SHO (Accident West) Narayan Singh said constable Babita and SI Sajjan were injured when the vehicle ahead applied brake suddenly, causing their car to collide with it. The accident took place in Kardhani police station area and both were rushed to hospital where Babita scummbed to injuries.
