Title: Biker mows down two women in Bollaram
content: HYDERABAD: In a ghastly re-run, two women on their morning walk died after being hit by a man on a high-end sports bike in Bollaram in the city early on Sunday. The woman were crossing the road around 5 am to enter the Bollaram cantonment park when the biker crashed into them.While Radhika (48), a resident of Risala Bazar, was killed on the spot, her friend P Balamani Yadav (60), a resident of Sai Colony in Alwal, died while being treated in hospital. Two other women in the group were unhurt. Cops said the group of four were regular walkers at the park.The accused, Aditya, a software engineer, was also injured and is undergoing treatment in a private hospital in Kompally.Earlier this month, in a similar accident, in Sun City in Narsingi on city outskirts, two morning walkers - mother and daughter - were killed after a speeding car smashed into them from behind. A third woman was admitted to hospital with grievous injury. The driver, who had tried to escape from the scene, was nabbed and handed over to the police by locals.  On Sunday, accused Aditya, who lives in Uppal, was on his way to Shamirpet from Secunderabad when the accident happened. The vehicle, Kawasaki Z 900, was seized by the police. Police said that as per eyewitnesses the accused was alone."Had the women completed a few more steps, they would have entered the park. These women have been walking in this area for the last two decades. Immediately after the accident, injured Balamani was shifted to Gandhi Hospital where she succumbed to injuries," Bollaram SI M Veera Swamy said.

Title: 7-year-old girl killed, 8 injured as loader rams school van
content: PRAYAGRAJ: A seven-year-old girl student was killed and seven other children sustained injuries when the school van carrying them was hit by a speeding loader near Laxmanpur block road under the limits of Leelapur police station in Pratapgarh district on Monday morning. The incident, according to police, took place at 7:30 am when the van was ferrying children of a local private school in Leelapur. The deceased, identified as Mansi Saroj, resident of Patulki village, died on the spot. All the seven kids who were injured, Yash Prajapati (7), Ayush Prajapati (8), Naina Saroj (8), Kartik Saroj (8), Sanjana (9), Vaibhav Singh (8) and Arpita Singh( 8), all residents of Patulki, were taken to the Lalganj Community Health Centre. Doctors referred Yash Prajapati along with the injured driver Shariq (50) to the district hospital in Pratapgarh. Senior police and administrative officials rushed to the spot for rescue, while scores of villagers too assembled at the incident site and hospital and demanded action against the errant driver of the loader. Locals blocked the Laxmanpur road and protested against the incident. The driver of the loader was later arrested. Leelapur SHO Subhash Yadav said " Police have been probing if driver of the school van had valid documents and driving licence to as the van was associated with the school."

Title: Jabalpur man, 2 sons killed in Narsinghpur road accident
content: Jabalpur: A 80-year-old man and his sons were killed after their car was hit by an unidentified vehicle in Narsinghpur district on Sunday night.The incident took place at around 11 pm near Manegaon village in Narsinghpur district. Additional SP Narsinghpur, Sunil Kumar Shivhare said Omkar Agarwal, his sons Sanjay Agarwal, 54, and Sandeep Agarwal, 51, a daughter and his two grandsons had gone to Kareli and were returning to Jabalpur when the accident took place, killing the elderly and his two sons, while leaving the other family members injured.The car was being driven by 27-year-old Sanket Agarwal, grandson ofOmkar Agarwal, when the accident occurred. As they approached the vicinity of the Manegaon power house, an unidentified vehicle collided with their car, causing the fatal accident, he said.Sandeep and Sanjay died on the spot, and Sanket, Omkar Agarwal, and the other two injured family members were rushed to a private hospital in Jabalpur for treatment. Omkar also succumbed to his injuries during treatment, and the condition of the other injured individuals remains critical, he added.A case has been registered, and detailed investigations are underway.

Title: 4 killed in car-truck collision in Madurai
content: Madurai: Three members of a family were among four people killed on Monday after the car in which they were travelling went out of control and hit a container truck near Thirumangalam in Madurai district.Police said two brothers, S James Martin, 34, Sam Davidson 30, and their relative, C Kamalesh, from Sengudi in Kanyakumari district, were travelling in the car from Kanyakumari to Chennai in the early hours of Monday. Around 1:30 am, when they were on National Highway 7 near Kallikudi, the driver allegedly lost control. The vehicle jumped the median, landed on the other side and collided with a container lorry proceeding from Virudhunagar to Madurai in the opposite direction.Under the impact, the three occupants of the car, James Martin, Sam Davidson and Kamalesh, as well as the driver of the container lorry, M Selvakumar of Virathanur in Madurai, died on the spot due to severe injuries. Kalligudi police arrived and with the help of fire & rescue services personnel extricated the bodies of the deceased and sent them for post-mortem examination. Kalligudi police are investigating.Meanwhile, in another accident near Thirumangalam on Sunday, a 38-year-old man and his two-year-old daughter died after the two-wheeler they were travelling on hit the median. Police said P Ayyanar of Thiruparankundram was travelling with his sister, S Senthamarai, 37, his daughter, Subasri, 2, and his son, Hariventhen, 6, towards Kalligudi in the afternoon to meet his three other children there.However, when the two-wheeler was on the Dindigul - Tirunelveli four-way road near Usilampatti bridge, Ayyanar allegedly lost control and the two-wheeler hit the median. All of them were thrown off the vehicle on the road side. Ayyanar and Subasri died on the spot due to severe head injuries. Senthamarai and Hariventhan were rescued and admitted to Government Rajaji Hospital for treatment. The Thirumangalam Town police are investigating.

Title: Trichy schoolgirl run over by bus as scooter skids on bad road
content: Trichy: A 17-year-old schoolgirl riding pillion with her brother on a scooter was killed in an accident in Trichy city on Monday. The victim was run over by a private city bus while travelling on a damaged city road. Condemning the bad condition of the road managed by the state highways department, local people gathered to protest but were dissuaded from going ahead.Police identified the deceased as A Jegajothi, a class XII student. Around 8 am, she was going to school with her elder brother A Vijayakumar on a scooter. As the scooter was approaching Melachinthamani road near Chathiram bus stand, it skidded after running over the stones on the road damaged for underground drainage works. The girl fell on the road and was instantly run over by a private bus which was running alongside. Police said Jegajothi was killed on the spot while her brother survived. Apart from the poor shape of Melachinthamani road, police suspect rash driving as a cause too. Local residents converged to launch a protest, but police assured them that the highways department will be asked to relay the road. Trichy corporation officials said they have already paid Rs 3.5 crore to the highways department for restoring roads dugs for the UGD project. They said that UGD works in Melachinthamani were completed a month ago but the road was not relaid by the highways department. â€œWe have been demanding for a month now that the authorities restore damaged roads near Chathiram bus stand. In the blame game between the local body and the highways department, a life was lost,â€ P Aiyarappan, a road safety activist, said.

Title: 2 food delivery boys killed in accident near Ekana Stadium
content: Lucknow: Two food delivery boys were killed in a road accident on Shaheed Path while they were returning home on Monday night.Police said the victims identified as Akash Lodhi (18) and Ajay Lodhi (21) of Itaunja were hit by an unidentified speeding vehicle near Ekana stadium.â€œDue to heavy impact both suffered critical injuries and were pronounced dead when they were brought to RMLIMS hospital in the area. First their postmortem was done and thereafter the bodies were handed over to their families,â€ said a police officer. While narrating the sequence of events, witnesses informed the police that the two were on a bike and suddenly hit from behind. The impact was so strong that the two were tossed up and thrown on the road getting carried away for five meters before being hit with a divider.The two were first taken to a private hospital by the commuters but were referred to RMLIMS. A pall of gloom descended on the village when the bodies arrived. Ajay was the only child in his family while Akash is survived by two brothers and his family.Ajayâ€™s father Ishwadeen Lodhi of Harda village in the area said Akash was his brotherâ€™s son. SHO, Sushant Golf City, Atul Srivastava, said that efforts are on to trace the unidentified driver who hit them and the CCTVs were being scanned.

Title: CPM holds protests over girlâ€™s death in accident in Trichy
content: Trichy: A day after a class XII girl was killed in a road accident in Melachinthamani in Trichy, the Communist Party of India (Marxist) conducted a protest in front of the junior engineer office of Trichy corporation on Tuesday. Following the protest, a peace meeting was conducted jointly by the Trichy corporation, revenue, and police department officials. CPM cadres said that the civic body has assured to restore the road on which the fatal accident was reported. â€œWe demanded compensation for the family of the deceased girl, authorities have assured to provide solatium,â€ P Lenin, CPM area secretary, Rockfort said. They also demanded a temporary job in Trichy corporation for one of the family members of the victim. Meanwhile, Trichy corporation officials said that the underground drainage (UGD) project was completed on the Melachinthamani road around a month ago. Since the road is managed by highways department, the corporation said that they insisted the department to restore the damaged road in a phased manner where the UGD works were completed. However, the state highways agreed to restore the road only if the UGD work was completed in the entire vicinity.

Title: 63-yr-old killed in road accident
content: Nashik: A motorist died in a road accident when his vehicle was knocked down by a tempo at Nashik Road on Tuesday The victim, Dinkar Tajanpure (63), was riding his motorcycle at Nashik Road on Monday evening when a tempo coming from behind allegedly hit him, causing him to fall down. The driver fled the scene.A complaint over causing death due to negligent driving was registered with Nashik Road police stationâ€œTajanpureâ€™s condition was critical. He received grievous injuries in his head and chest. He succumbed to treatment while being in the hospital,â€ said an officer. The police said that though the motorist has fled the scene along with the vehicle, CCTV footage will help track him. â€œWe have various organisations on the road whose CCTV footages are being used to track the details of the vehicle and reach the motorist,â€ the officer said.

Title: Pillion rider killed in Setu accident
content: KOLKATA: A 30-year-old pillion rider died and the biker was severely injured after being hit by an unidentified vehicle on the Esplanade exit ramp of Vidyasagar Setu around 3.50pm on Wednesday. In the impact, the two were thrown off the bike. Cops said Shibpur resident Subhasish Duttaâ€™s head was crushed under the wheels of the vehicle. He was declared dead at SSKM Hospital. The biker, Sarfarajuddin Sardar (25), also a resident of Shibpur, sustained multiple fractures and his condition was stated to be serious at the hospital. With no CCTV footage available, the witnessesâ€™ accounts suggested that the two were riding the bike on the extreme left lane of the bridgeâ€™s Kolkata-bound flank. They tried to swerve right towards the AJC Bose Road ramp when the killer vehicle was trying to take the Esplanade ramp and hit them. â€œWe will identify and trace the vehicle involved in the accident,â€ said an officer. This was the third accident reported within a week from the bridge.

Title: Helmet advocate riding without one killed in accident
content: Bengaluru: A 27-year-old man working as a security officer in a private firm, who would insist everyone ride a two-wheeler only after wearing a helmet, was killed after a bike he was riding without donning the headgear hit a road median in Indiranagar on Tuesday midnight. Aditya Luke Kujur was a resident of HSR Layout and from Jamshedpur in Jharkhand. According to police, Kujur had borrowed a BMW bike bearing registration number JH-05-DJ-5847 from one of his friends late Tuesday night. Around 12.30am, he was riding near Magnolia Bakery on 12th Main, 100 Feet Road, when he lost control of the vehicle and hit the median. As he was not wearing a helmet, he sustained severe head injuries. An auto driver rushed Kujur to nearby CMH Hospital, where doctors declared him brought dead. According to his colleagues and friends, Kujur was very particular about wearing helmet while riding. They were surprised that he was found not wearing one. tnn

Title: 1 killed, 2 injured as truck overturns on Mumbai-Pune Expressway; traffic affected
content: MUMBAI: In a tragic incident on Thursday's early hours, a speeding glass-laden truck collided with another truck on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway in Maharashtra's Raigad district, resulting in one fatality and leaving two others injured. The mishap occurred when the truck lost control and overturned after the collision.Vehicular traffic on the lane heading toward Mumbai experienced disruptions due to its closure from 5.30am to 8.30am. The closure was necessary to facilitate the removal of a truck and scattered glass materials from the road surface.The accident took place when the truck carrying the glass material rammed into another truck from its rear side and then overturned on the expressway in Khopoli area.During the incident, three individuals sustained severe injuries and were promptly transported to the hospital. Among them was Sadanand Patil (43), the driver of the glass-laden truck. Tragically, despite receiving medical treatment, he did not survive and succumbed to his injuries.The Mumbai-bound lane was opened for traffic after 8.30am and the vehicular movement was slow.

Title: Over a month for work at Samruddhi accident site to restart: Official
content: NASHIK: Construction work at the accident site at Shahapur on the Samruddhi expressway will take over a month to resume."The company assigned the work at the site has to deploy a new girder launcher that can place the girders to make the carriageway. The company has a spare launcher at its disposal, and it will take over a month to assemble it and make it operational," said a senior official of the Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation (MSRDC).Twenty people were killed when the girder launcher suddenly collapsed at the construction site on Monday night. The official said had the construction firm not had a spare girder launcher, the work at the site would have taken at least five-six months to restart.An agency report quoting police in Thane said a "big spark" in the pole providing power to the girder launcher preceded its collapse during the construction of the viaduct for the expressway. â€˜Will not miss deadline for Samruddhi constructionâ€™Officials have ruled out the possibility of any delay in meeting the deadline for the project due to the accident. "We are in the last stage of construction of the expressway - this involves only about 37km. About 75% of the work is completed. We will be able to meet the deadline of early next year," the official said.State PWD (public undertakings) minister Dada Bhuse too had said the completion of the project will be as scheduled - in May 2024.According to MSRDC insiders, prima facie a machinal snag of the girder launcher was responsible for its collapse. The contractor concerned will submit a report to us. Our technical experts will also analyse the reasons why the launcher collapsed," said a senior MSRDC official.Chief minister Eknath Shinde, who visited the accident site on Tuesday, announced that a probe will take place to ascertain the reason for the accident.The police registered an offence against the engineering company in connection with the incident. The girder launcher, along with a segment launcher (crane), weighing 700 tonnes had come crashing down from a height of 35m, the MSRDC had said. One of the injured labourers lodged a complaint in this connection. The complainant works for the company that was given the sub-contract by the engineering firm for the girder launcher, police said. "Based on the complaint, the Thane district rural police registered an offence against the engineering company, which was given the task of installing the girders for the viaduct on the Samruddhi Expressway," a police officer said citing the first FIR.The FIR was registered against the engineering company under Indian Penal Code (IPC) sections 304-A, (causing death by negligence), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others), 34 (common intention) and others, police said, adding that further investigation is on. (With agency inputs)

Title: Three killed, one hurt as truck hits car in Karauli
content: Jaipur: Three men lost their lives and one woman sustained injuries in Karauli on Wednesday when their car was hit by an oncoming truck.According to Assistant Sub-Inspector (ASI) Hari Singh of the district Mansalpur police station, deceased have been identified as Sahfique Khan (32), Chotu Lal (22), and Amit Singh (20). One woman, Raj Kumari (19), sustained injuries in the mishap. The victims were travelling in a car from Karauli to Saramathura in Dholpur district when the accident occurred. A vehicle coming from Saramathura collided with their car on NH-11B, near Machani village, he said.The impact of the crash was severe, and three victims sustained critical injuries. They were immediately rushed to a hospital by ambulance. Three of them succumbed to their injuries and were pronounced dead by the doctor. After conducting a post-mortem at a hospital, the police handed over bodies to their respective families. According to Singh, the tragic accident may have been caused by an attempt to save an animal that quickly crossed the road. The severity of the collision was evident from the condition of the car, which was reduced to a pile of wreckage with its front completely destroyed.

Title: One dead, 12 injured as school bus falls into canal
content: SHAMLI: An eight-year-old student of a primary school was killed and 12 others were injured after a school bus they were travelling in fell into a canal in Bijnor's Seohara region on Wednesday. Victim, Lucky Kumar, from village Aliyarpur died on the spot while other students who sustained injuries were rushed to a nearby hospital. According to locals, the water level in the canal was relatively low, otherwise, the situation could have been worse. SP (Bijnor) Neeraj Kumar Jadaun said, "The school bus was passing through a narrow path along the canal at around 1.30 pm when driver, Kripal Singh, lost control of the vehicle while trying to avoid a pothole and the bus skidded off the road and fell into the canal."He added, "We have received a written complaint from the parents. Circle officer Dhampur has been instructed to ensure legal action after conducting necessary investigation."

Title: Truck runs over motorcycle in Durg, 1 killed
content: Raipur: A 32-year-old man was killed late on Tuesday evening when a truck carrying ballast ran over his bike at Borsi Road in Durg.  On Tuesday, around 8 pm, the deceased Shubham Bhatt was returning home from his office while crossing the Maharaja Chowk, a speeding Hyva truck coming from another direction could not control and hit him. The accident was witnessed by the local people who informed the police officials. The body was later sent to the hospital for further autopsy, while the driver was found absconding from his vehicle. The parents of the deceased were informed about the incident. The truck was seized while a case was registered against the driver. Besides, the investigation is still on, added the police. tnn

Title: Bikes collide head-on, one killed
content: Indore: An elderly man was killed and three sustained injuries when two two-wheelers collided head-on on Barod road late Wednesday late night in Agar-Malwa district. The passers-by informed the police about the accident and rushed the injured to district hospital. Deceased were Rodu Lal Prajapat, 50. Gopal Prajapt, 30, Neeraj Thakur, 21 and Arbaaz, 18, suffered injuries in the accident, the police said. â€œRodu and Gopal were going towards their village on a bike when they collided head-on with another bike on which Neera and Arbaaz were riding,â€district hospital police outpost in-charge ASI Santosh Kumar told TOI. TNNTOIdistrict hospital police outpostin-charge ASI Santosh Kumar told TOI in-charge ASI Santosh Kumar told TOIin-charge ASI Santosh Kumar told TOI. TNN

Title: 22-yr-old Indian student dies in accident in Australia
content: MELBOURNE: A 22-year-old Indian student working as an Uber Eats driver in Australia was killed after his bike collided with an SUV in Sydney.Akshay Deepak Doultani, a Mumbai-native pursuing a master's degree, died in the crash last week, the Australia Broadcasting Cooperation reported on Wednesday. Doultani had come to Australia in February after receiving a scholarship in finance at Macquarie University. He worked as a delivery rider at least thrice a week to cover his expenses. "He wanted to come abroad not (just to) have a good life but to provide more amenities for his family," his cousin was quoted as saying. The number of food delivery riders killed in Australia since 2017 has now reached 12, Transport Workers Union said. Uber Eats said it was committed to delivery workers' safety and had policies in place to enhance road safety.

Title: HC indicts cops over â€˜cooked-up storyâ€™
content: Cuttack: The Orissa high court indicted the Rairangpur town police, Mayurbhanj, for initiating criminal proceedings against a person with a â€œcooked-up storyâ€ which was â€œinherently improbableâ€. The indictment came on Monday while quashing criminal proceedings against one Abhisek Pattnaik for allegedly abusing policemen in obscene language and threatening to kill them in June 2018. The criminal proceedings were pending in the court of SDJM, Rairangpur. The accused had challenged it in the HC.After assessing the materials on record, the HC found that the accused, on being asked to stop by the police, sped away on his motorcycle, and for which he lost balance while crossing the humps on the road resulting in an accident and subsequent injuries on his body. Then he abused the policemen in obscene language, threatened and attempted to assault them.Justice Sashikanta Mishra said, â€œIt must be kept in mind that there were five policemen on the spot. First, it does not stand to reason as to why the accused would suddenly flare up and abuse the policemen who had not ill-treated him at all. Secondly, it seems rather far-fetched that a person as badly injured as the accused at the relevant time would have the mental and physical courage and even strength to abuse, threaten and assault two policemen from out of the group of five, including the ASI of police. The further allegation that he threatened to kill the policemen by engaging goondas seems entirely out of context as there was no plausible reason whatsoever for him to say so.â€â€œThe very nature of the accusations appears to be improbable or in any case, difficult to believe,â€ Justice Mishra observed.

Title: Doctor run over by lorry, dies
content: Visakhapatnam: A 25-year-old doctor was killed in a road accident after being run over by a lorry on NH-16 near the Pantulagarimeda locality under Gajuwaka police station limits on Thursday. Another woman also sustained severe injuries in the accident. The deceased was identified as M Bhavya from Srikakulam district. The incident occured when both the victims were heading towards Old-Gajuwaka locality. A lorry heading in the same direction hit the scooter and ran over Bhavya, who was a pillion. A case has been registered. TNN

Title: 6 devotees killed, 8 injured in UP's Hathras as truck hits tractor-trolley
content: HATHRAS: Six people died while eight others sustained injuries when a truck collided with a Mathura-bound tractor-trolley carrying devotees. The incident occurred on Sadabad Road during Friday night. The speeding truck, approaching from the opposite direction, struck the trolley, causing the casualties, superintendent of police Devesh Kumar Pandey said.The tractor-trolley, transporting 45 pilgrims, including women and children, was en route from Jalesar in Etah district to Govardhan in Mathura, as stated by Pandey."Five individuals perished on the scene, with one more succumbing to injuries later. The deceased have been identified as Vikram (45), Madhuri (22), Hemlata (12), Lakhmi (18), Abhishek (20), and Vishnu (20)," reported the SP.The wounded individuals were admitted to various medical facilities for treatment, including the community health center in Sadabad, Hathras district hospital, JN Medical College in Aligarh, and SN Medical College in Agra, according to him.The bodies of the deceased have been sent for post-mortem examinations, confirmed the police.The truck driver and two co-drivers have been taken into custody, and authorities have seized the truck, as stated by officials.Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath conveyed his "profound grief at the loss of lives in the road accident in Hathras district" in a tweet written in Hindi.(With inputs from PTI)

Title: Dumper hits woman on bike, kills her
content: MUMBAI: A 32-year-old woman was killed after a dumper crashed into her motorcycle on the New Link Road at Kandivli on Thursday. The dumper driver has been booked by the Charkop police. Neha Pandya, the deceased, was a widow. She lived with a cousin at Charkop for the past two-and-a-half years and worked in the imitation jewellery industry.On Thursday, Pandya was riding a motorcycle on New Link Road. Around 1pm, she was crossing the traffic signal at Vasant Complex when a dumper rammed into her motorcycle. Pandya suffered serious head injuries in the crash. She was rushed to the Babasaheb Ambedkar Hospital in Kandivli where she succumbed to her injuries.Police later registered a case of negligence against the dumper driver identified as Rahul Jagtap, 28.

Title: 5 of family killed as car rams into tree while saving 'Nilgai' in UP's Shravasti
content: LUCKNOW: Five members of a family were killed after the driver of a car ferrying them rammed into a tree in order to save a 'Nilgai' (blue cow) in Shravasti area late on Saturday night. The incident occurred on the Buddha circuit (Bahraich-Balrampur) highway in Shravasti area of Uttar Pradesh.Prachi Singh, superintendent of police, Shravasti, said that one Nilansh Gupta of Bahraich was coming to Balrampur on Saturday after booking an SUV of driver Ajay Mishra.According to the SP, Nilanshâ€™s sister Niti Gupta (18), a family member Vaibhav (28), another woman and two children (their identities could not be ascertained), were also with him in the vehicle.â€œAs soon as they reached Sitadwar turn on the Buddhist circuit, a 'Nilgai' came on the passage. To save it, the driver lost control of the vehicle and collided with a tree on the roadside. The impact was such that the SUV was tossed, it fell into a ditch and then overturned,â€ said the officer.Singh added that some locals spotted the incident and informed the nearest Ikauna police, who arrived at the scene. After cutting open the vehicle's door, they rescued the victims and transported them to the nearest hospital.â€œNilansh, Niti, a woman and two children were declared brought dead. Vaibhav was referred to Medical College in Bahraich, where he is in critical condition. Ajay, who was driving the SUV, is undergoing treatment in a hospital in Ikauna,â€ said SP Shravasti.

Title: 4 killed, several injured as bus falls into river in Jharkhand's Giridih
content: GIRIDIH: Four people were killed and several others suffered injuries when a bus fell into a river from a bridge in Giridih district of Jharkhand on Saturday night, reported news agency PTI quoting police.The accident took place at Giridih Dumri Road at around 8.40pm when the bus fell into the Barakar river while on way to Giridih from Ranchi, Giridih sub-divisional police officer, Anil Kumar said. "More details cannot be assessed right now as rescue and relief operations are being carried," the official said.Meanwhile, Jharkhand chief minister Hemant Soren said the district administration and police are engaged in rescue operations. Soren in a tweet in Hindi said, "Sad news has been received that a bus from Ranchi to Giridih met with an accident in the Barakar river in Giridih, Jharkhand. Rescue work is being done by the district administration."Giridih superintendent of police, Deepak Sharma said he was on the spot overseeing rescue operations. The exact number of passengers on the bus is still to be ascertained.

Title: 6 dead as container hits tractor-trolley in Hathras
content: AGRA: Six devotees, including a minor, were killed and eight were injured when the Mathura-bound tractor-trolley they were riding was hit in UP's Hathras district by a speeding container around 11pm Friday.The accident took place on Sadabad Jalesar Road when the trolley was hit on one side by the container coming from the opposite direction, police said.According to police sources, the trolley carrying 50 pilgrims, including women and children, was going from Jalesar in Etah district to Mathura for the Govardhan Parikrama. After receiving information, local police and emergency services rushed to the scene and launched a rescue operation. Those injured were initially rushed to the district hospital and later transferred to SN medical college in Agra, said superintendent of police Devesh Kumar Pandey. Five people were declared dead, while one succumbed to injuries during treatment on Saturday morning.The dead are Vikram Singh (28), a resident of village Magkash under Amapur police station in Kasganj district, Madhuri Singh (22), Lakhmi Singh (18), Abhishek Singh (20), Vishnu Singh (19), all four residents of village Gadhiya under Sakrauli police station in Etah district, and Hemlata (12), resident of village Bajirpur Kotla under Narkhi police station in Firozabad district. The minor girl was a Class 6 student. The injured are Krishna Kumar Singh (35), Ankit Singh (26), Vrajesh Singh (16), Pushpa Singh (40), Sultan Singh (48), Ramvati Devi (40), all of them residents of Etah, and Pawan Singh (16) and Baburam Singh (50), both residents of Firozabad.SP Pandey said, "The container driver was arrested and truck seized. The bodies will be handed over to families after the post mortem." SHO Sahpau police station, Amit Kumar said, "A case under IPC sections 304A, 279 , 337, 338 has been registered against the driver."

Title: Woman killed in road crash
content: Ujjain: A 37-year-old woman was killed and and her husband seriously injured after their vehicle was hit by a speeding dumper truck in Nagjhiri area on Dewas Road on Sunday. CSP OP Mishra said a couple, Manoj and Pinki Tiwari, residents of Dewas had reached Ujjain to meet some family members and were on their way back home when the accident took place. They were passing from Parshavnath City square when a dumper coming from the wrong side mowed the couple down. The woman was seriously injured and by the time they could be rushed to hospital, the woman had died whereas the man was admitted in a critical condition.tnn

Title: Three killed in Surjagarh mine accident, 2 serious
content: Nagpur: Three persons, including an assistant engineer, were killed while another two are battling for life after a Hyva tipper overturned on a camper jeep carrying a mechanical team on Sunday at the Surjagarh mining site in Etapalli, Gadchiroli. Police had to toil hard to control an aggressive mob, shift the bodies, rush the injured to hospital, and avert a clash between local residents and mining company employees, reports Soumitra Bose.A company source said kin of the deceased and injured would be duly compensated. Preliminary information said Sonal Rangerwar, a local native of Nagepalli, was among those dead. Assistant engineer Om Prakash too died at the spot. Another person, identified as Rana so far, too was confirmed dead. Two others were seriously injured, but are learnt to be out of danger.SP Neelotpal said the heavy tipper was descending the hill with a load when it toppled onto the camper jeep parked on the roadside for road clearance. â€œWe are trying to ascertain the cause of the accident. At a preliminary stage, it has come to fore that the tipper driver was unable to use the brakes due to some technical snag, but we can confirm this only after expert examination by the concerned departments,â€ said the SP, adding an offence would be registered at Etapalli police station for causing death due to negligence. Sources said the heavy tipper tried to avoid the accident, but ended up overturning on the camper jeep. The tipper driver too was injured. The camper driver had a miraculous escape as he was standing outside the vehicle.

Title: Goa road accident: Mercedes driver arrested for car crash that claimed 3 lives
content: PANAJI: The Goa police on Monday arrested the driver of the speeding Mercedes-Benz SUV which rammed into two bikes on the Banastarim bridge on Sunday evening, resulting in the deaths of three people. The luxury car, which had bottles with suspected liquor in it, also hit three other cars. The accused has been identified as Shripad alias Paresh Sinai Sawardekar (48), a businessman and a resident of Miramar.Read AlsoIn Goa, speeding Merc rams into 5 vehicles; 3 dead, 6 injured, driver not arrestedThree persons were killed and six others injured after a speeding Mercedes-Benz SUV rammed into two bikes on the Banastarim bridge on Sunday evening. The luxury car, which had bottles with suspected liquor in it, also hit three other cars.Mardol police station registered the accident case under various sections of IPC including 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) and various section of Motor Vehicle act.Three people were killed and six others sustained injuries after the speeding SUV rammed into two bikes on the approach road of the Banastarim bridge on Sunday evening. The accident took place at around 8pm. The Mercedes-Benz, bearing registration number GA07 K 7311, is registered in the name of Meghna Sawardekar.Among the dead were Suresh Phadte, 58, and his wife Bhawana, 52, from Divar, as well as Anup Karmakar, 26, from Undir-Bandora. Police said that after the speeding car crashed into the two bikes ridden by Suresh and Karmakar, the carâ€™s direction changed and then rammed into three other cars coming in the opposite direction (proceeding towards Ponda). Several people sustained injuries in this second crash.

Title: Three of a family run over by truck, killed in Chandauli
content: Varanasi: At least three persons, including two women, of a family were killed when a speeding truck hit their motorcycle in Alinagar area of Chandauli district on Sunday. CO Alinagar Aniruddh Singh said that Anil Kumar (20) of Kalimahal area under Mughalsarai police station, and his mother Savitri Devi (45) died on the spot while maternal grandmother Ramavati Devi (70) died on the way to hospital. All three bodies were sent for postmortem examination. Police had seized the killer truck and arrested the driver, he added.According to reports, Anil, along with his mother and grandmother had left home in Kalmahal for some work on Sunday. They were passing through Alampur area under the limits of Alinagar police station when a truck hit their motorcycle. The trio fell on the road and came under the wheels of the same truck.Locals and passers by chased the truck and caught it at some distance. The vehicular movement remained disrupted on Varanasi-Chandauli highway due to the accident till the mutilated bodies were sent for postmortem.

Title: Man killed after speeding vehicle hits bicycle
content: INDORE: A 48-year-old man on his way to an ashram on Khandwa Road was mowed down by a speeding heavy vehicle at Bhanwarkuan on Sunday morning. Atmaram Pariyani, a resident of New Govind Colony in Banganga was on his way to Radha Soami Ashram on his bicycle when he was suddenly hit by a speeding SUV head-on . Atmaram was thrown off his bicycle and sustained severe injuries on his head and torso. Locals rushed him to hospital where he was declared brought dead by the doctors. He had lost a lot of blood, doctors said. The driver of the vehicle fled the spot soon after the accident but the vehicle was seized. Police said that a case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder was registered against the driver. The body of the victim was handed over to family members after postmortem examination. Atmaram had been working for the ashram for the past 35 years and went there every morning at 6 am and returned home in the evening. His family members were in a state of shock after the accident. He would work as a tailor to make a living.tnn

Title: Goa recorded three drunk driving cases every day over past 3.5 years
content: Panaji: Goa police have booked over 4,000 cases of drunken driving across the state over the past three and a half years.A senior police officer said that most of these cases were booked during special drives at night. He said that many people consume alcohol at parties or events and then drive in a rash and negligent manner. Those booked by Goa police are foreigners, domestic tourists, and Goans.The accident on Sunday night at Banastarim, in which a speeding Mercedes-Benz SUV driven by an allegedly drunk driver killed three people and injured several more, has brought back into focus the issue of drunken driving on Goan roads.The accident revived memories of another horrific accident, the July 2022 tragedy in which a car drove off the Zuari bridge and plunged into the river, killing all four occupants.Goa police, in an effort to prevent accidents and fatalities by curtailing speeding and drunken driving, regularly conduct special night drives for such cases. CM Pramod Sawant is on record saying that 95% cases of accidents at night are caused by drunken driving.In 2020, Goa police booked 1,662 cases of drunken driving. This number reduced to 266 in 2021 before increasing again to 1,368 in 2022.On the issue of drunken driving, opposition and ruling MLAs are on the same side, and demanded strict action to curb the menace.â€œDrunken driving is a big issue,â€ said Fatorda MLA Vijai Sardesai. â€œDrunken driving accidents take place because the state is not serious. The government has reduced the budget on road safety.â€ He also said, â€œDriving a vehicle under the influence is not right, and there has to be strict action and a banâ€.Sardesai further said that Mumbai had a similar problem of drunken driving, but the state cracked down on it and stopped it.Former chief minister and Margao MLA Digambar Kamat agreed, saying, â€œAction against drunken driving should be done as per law; the law has been laid downâ€.Calangute MLA Michael Lobo said that when a person drinks and drives, thereâ€™s a possibility they would put their and their familyâ€™s lives at risk. â€œIf a person is found to be drunk driving, he should be put behind bars as per international law. We have to start these things in Goa,â€ he said. â€œWe have found accidents have increased 10-fold. Every day, one person dies in a road accident,â€ Lobo said.

Title: Lecturer killed in road accident
content: Visakhapatnam: A 40-year-old lecturer at a private college was killed after an unidentified vehicle hit his bike near RR Venkatapuram under Pendurthi police station limits in Vizag city on Monday morning. The deceased was identified as G Rajasekar, a native of Pedabayalu in ASR district. The incident took place when he was traveling on a bike towards a private degree college near Urvasi Junction in Vizag city, where he was an English lecturer. The police have registered a case. Rajasekar is survived by wife and two children. TNN

Title: Three killed in accident
content: Visakhapatnam: Three persons, including a woman and her husband, were killed in a road accident when a speeding car hit a bike on beach road near a private hotel at Rushikonda locality under Arilova police station limits in Vizag city. Police said the couple on a bike were hit by a speeding car at high speed. Arilova police said one person in the car was killed and two others suffered injuries. A case has been registered. Efforts are on to identify the deceased, said inspector of Arilova station, Somasekhar. TNN

Title: Four kanwariyas killed in two separate road accidents
content: BUDAUN/SITAPUR: Four kanwariyas died in two separate incidents in Uttar Pradesh, police said on Monday.In Budaun, three kanwariyas were killed after an unidentified vehicle rammed into their motorcycle on the Bareilly-Budaun road, police said.The incident took place on Sunday night when Gajendra (30), Veerpal (28) and Ram Bahadur (30) were returning home, Circle officer, Alok Mishra, said.All the three kanwariyas (devotees of Lord Shiva) were rushed to a hospital where they succumbed to their injuries, he said. Meanwhile in Sitapur, a kanwariya died after he was hit by a (contractual) bus of the UP roadways department, police said. Police added that the incident took place in Hargaon area of Sitapur when a group of kanwariyas were on their way home to perform the 'jalabhishek' ritual at Narmadeshwar temple in Oel, Lakhimpur Kheri.Superintendent of police of Sitapur Chakresh Mishra said that the deceased has been identified as Anoop Mishra (48), a resident of Lakhimpur Kheri district.He added that a group of kanwariyas were standing on the roadside when Anoop was hit by the bus who died on the spot.The SP said that the family of the deceased has been informed about the accident, adding that the truck has been seized, and its driver detained by the police.

Title: 1 killed, 1 injured as truck hits bike on Strand Road
content: Kolkata: A biker was run over by a truck on Strand Road near the M G Road crossing on Sunday night, accounting for the fourth fatal accident involving heavy goods vehicles in Kolkata in the past seven days.The accident took place shortly before midnight when a Howrah-bound truck hit the bike. The rider, Abul Khayer (40) from Bhangar in South 24 Parganas, and the pillion rider, Sumit Thakur (29) of College Street, were taken to Medical College and Hospital. Khayer, who had suffered a huge loss of blood, died early on Monday, while Thakur was shifted to another hospital. Police said both had their helmets on. In another accident, also on Strand Road but near the Kings Way crossing, two other bikers suffered leg injuries when a bus hit their vehicle on Monday. Rahul Biswas (32) and Tania Dey (31) of Barrackpore were admitted to SSKM Hospital.

Title: 3 killed in car accident in Visakhapatnam
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: Three people, including a young couple, were killed road accident involving drunk driving, in Vizag city. The couple, traveling on a two-wheeler vehicle, were knocked down by a car on Beach Road locality under Arilova police station limits in Vizag city on Monday night. A man, under the influence of alcohol, was driving the car.Assistant commissioner of police (Dwaraka sub-division), RVSN Murthy, said that a group of six friends hired a car. Five of them bought liquor and consumed it inside the car. The car was traveling from Sagarnagar towards Yendada on the beach road. The intoxicated rider, Vinay, lost control of the vehicle due to excessive speed. He veered to the right side of the road, collided with the road-divider and a tree, and then crossed to the opposite side of the road, colliding with a bike that was approaching from Sagarnagar.The young couple from Odisha, identified as S Prudhvi Raj (28) and his wife Priyanka, traveling on the bike and one of the six persons in the car died on the spot. Our preliminary investigation has revealed that the accident resulted from tipsy driving. The car's rider, Vinay, and two others managed to flee from the scene. Two more individuals in the car suffered injuries and were transported to the hospital, stated RVSN Murthy.Before the accident, Vinay and his friends engaged in a heated argument with some youngsters at Sagarnagar over a minor issue. They shattered liquor bottles on the road and caused a disturbance, even snatching a mobile phone from one of them.The police have established a special team to apprehend Vinay and the other two individuals. The bodies have been taken for posmortem examination.

Title: 35-year-old man killed in accident
content: Hyderabad: An unknown man was killed in a hit and run case at Dundigal on Monday. At 9 am, police received an alert about a road accident at Dundigal ORR Rotary-1 through Dial-100 call. Immediately, police officials reached the spot and found a 35-year-old man lying dead on the road in his underwear. The victim had suffered multiple injuries on his head as well as on his chest. Police shifted the body to Gandhi Hospital mortuary and registered a road accident case under section 304-a of the Indian Penal Code.

Title: 17 cattle killed after collision with bus
content: Raipur: A fortnight after 18 stray cattle were killed in Bilaspur after a collision with a speeding truck, 17 more stray cattle met similar fate in a road mishap on National Highway 53 in Mahasamund district, approximately 60 km from Raipur on Monday.The incident took place at around 8 AM near the forest depot on NH-53 when a privately-operated bus, coming from Sarai Pali to Raipur, collided with a herd of cows. The driver fled the spot after accident.Local authorities were alerted and Tumgaon police promptly reached the spot. They found that the bus was severely damaged, and cows and calves were in need for urgent medical attention. The authorities have launched a comprehensive investigation into the matter, said Tumgaon SHO Manorath Joshi. The driver has been booked under Section 429 of the IPC, he said.When questioned about steps taken to capture stray in compliance with the HC order, Joshi said that he had not observed any such activities yet.On July 17, 2023, the HC directed state government to establish a committee led by state chief secretary. This committee was tasked to oversee execution of the Courtâ€™s order to remove cattle from NHs, state highways and city roads. A recent state government circular urged all district collectors and municipal bodies to take effective measures in elimination of stray menace on highways and streets.TNN

Title: 3 killed as truck falls into Meghalaya gorge
content: Shillong: Three persons were killed and two others injured after a truck in which they were travelling fell into a gorge in Meghalaya's East Khasi Hills district on Monday, police said.The accident happened around midnight when the truck with 12 buffaloes was heading towards Laitlyngkot from Pynursla town, they added.â€œThree bodies were pulled out of the gorge,â€ a police officer said.After the accident, B Khongkliam, who was travelling in the truck, somehow managed to get out of it, climbed around 300 feet to reach the road and call the police. Another person is still stuck at the spot, police said, adding that work is on to rescue him. Most of the buffaloes died in the accident, while those alive are yet to be brought out, they said.It is suspected that there were some glitches in the truck and coupled with low visibility due to heavy rain and fog, the driver lost control of the vehicle, police said. PTI

Title: 3 killed, child among four injured in accident on NH-8
content: SILCHAR: At least three persons were killed and four others, including a child, critically injured in a road accident on National Highway-8 (Assam-Tripura road) in Dengarbond under Bazaricherra police station in Karimganj district late on Monday night.Sources said the accident took place when an auto-rickshaw in which seven persons, including its driver were travelling, rammed into a cement-laden stationary truck on the highway in Dengarbond, following which one of the passengers - Radha Das (48) - died on the spot. The injured people were rescued by police and sent to Makunda Christian Leprosy and General Hospital. As the condition of three - auto driver Apu Suklabaidya, Sanju Das and Rajen Das - turned critical, they were referred to Silchar Medical College and Hospital (SMCH). Apu died of his injuries before reaching the hospital while Sanju died at the hospital on Tuesday evening. Rajen is under treatment at SMCH, while the other injured people are being treated at the Makunda hospital.The truck driver tried to flee from the spot after the accident but was intercepted and detained by Churaibari police. Both the vehicles (truck and the mangled auto-rickshaw) were seized. Apu's body was handed over to his family on Tuesday after autopsy. Sanju's body, however, was not handed over to his family.

Title: 4 including two brothers killed in Bulandshahr accident
content: MEERUT: Four people, including two brothers, were killed while one was critically injured in a head-on collision between a van and an unidentified vehicle on Tuesday near Dhanpur village in Dibai of Bulandshahr.As per police, the dead were identified as Neeraj Kumar, 25, Pramod Kumar, 18, both brothers, Jitendra, 20 and Pushpendra Singh, 18. They were all natives of Sambhal district and were headed to Noida, where they worked as daily wagers. Mukesh Kumar, 30, a resident of the same village was injured in the incident. The victims were travelling in a Maruti Eeco van owned by Jitendra, when the accident took place. Bajrang Bali Chaurasiya, SP (rural), Bulandshahr, said that their van was hit by an unidentified vehicle around 2 am at Aligarh-Anoopshahar road that leads to Noida via Bulandshahr. Police had to use gas cutters to pull out bodies from the mangled van.The SP said, "One person who survived the mishap is critical. The bodies were sent for autopsy. Their families have been informed, but we have not received any complaint yet. An FIR will be registered and the other vehicle involved in the accident will be identified. Further investigation is on."

Title: Four crushed to death by apple-laden trucks in separate accidents in Shimla
content: SHIMLA: In a span of around 14 hours, four people were killed while several vehicles were damaged in Dhalli and Chhaila areas of Shimla district after speeding trucks ran over them in two separate accidents. In the first incident, four vehicles came under the grip of an apple-laden truck on Tuesday evening in the Chhaila area of the district in which 2 people, including a woman, travelling in a car lost their lives.The accident took place at around 8am.A horrifying video of the accident has emerged in which the speeding truck can be seen turning turtle on the road and hitting a car. The truck, coming from Narkanda, was going outside Himachal via Rajgarh-Solan route but by mistake, the driver went towards Chhaila market from Chhaila Kanchi and lost control over the vehicle.The truck hit four vehicles that came under its grip, severely damaging three of them. The fourth vehicle, a car, was crushed by it and bodies of two people were recovered from it.In the second incident, two people were killed when an apple-laden truck coming towards Shimla collided with a pickup vehicle. The truck and the pickup driver who sustained injuries in the mishap were admitted to Indira Gandhi Medical College and Hospital in Shimla. The pickup vehicle was moving towards Theog.Three other vehicles were also damaged in the accident which took place on Shimla-Kinnaur National Highway-5 near Mashobra bifurcation.

Title: Man killed in road accident
content: Mangaluru: A person died and four others sustained injuries in an accident reported between a car and a tipper truck at Aikala near Kinnigoli on Wednesday evening. The deceased is Ramanna,48. The accident took place when the victims were returning from work. The Mangaluru Traffic North police are investigating the case. tnn

Title: 2 kids killed, 7 injured in road accident
content: Bengaluru: Two children were killed and seven others sustained injuries after a rashly driven mini-goods vehicle rammed them at Gollaradoddi village, Ramanagara, on Wednesday evening. The deceased are Rohit, 5, and Shalini, 8. Seven other children sustained injuries in the accident that occurred at 7pm. The condition of three â€” Suchit, Gautam and Lekhana, all aged seven â€” is said to be critical. The children were returning home after tuition when the goods vehicle hit them. Police said the driver sped away and abandoned the vehicle in a nearby village.

Title: 4 killed, 2 injured in two road accidents in Shimla
content: Shimla: Within a span of 24 hours, four persons were killed, two others were injured while several vehicles were damaged in two separate road accidents in Shimla district. The first incident was reported from Chhaila area of Shimla on Tuesday when a speeding truck hit four other vehicles killing two persons, including a woman, while the second incident was reported from Dhalli on Wednesday morning when a truck collided with a pickup resulting in the death of two persons.The first incident occurred at around 6.30pm on Tuesday when a speeding truck on the Theog-Hatkoti-Chaila road of upper Shimla crushed three vehicles, including a bike. A couple travelling in a car were killed. In the preliminary investigation, the cause of the accident is said to be brake failure of the truck. Theog police station has registered a case and started an investigation.The truck, which was carrying apples from Narkanda via Chhaila towards Andhra Pradesh, suddenly lost control after its brakes failed. More than 600 apple boxes were loaded in the truck. The uncontrolled truck had rammed three vehicles one after the other.The second incident occurred on Wednesday morning at around 8am when a truck driver lost control of his vehicle and rammed a pickup. Two people died in the accident. After strenuous efforts, the injured were pulled out of the damaged vehicles and admitted to a hospital. According to sources, a truck overturned on a pickup near Shimla's Dhalli Mashobra bifurcation.The truck driver and conductor received serious injuries and were admitted to a hospital. At the same time, the pickup driver was also admitted to the hospital. Two people got trapped inside the truck. Both were extracted after a lot of effort. Traffic was moved to one side of the road due to the accident. The truck and pickup were removed with the help of a crane.

Title: Traffic cop dies after dumper hits his bike
content: Pune: Constable Yogesh Dhavale (40), attached to the Chakan traffic division of the Pimpri Chinchwad police, died after a dumper hit his motorcycle near a petrol pump on Chakan road around 10am on Wednesday.The accident occurred when Dhavale was heading towards the Chakan traffic division office to take pledge for the nationwide â€œMeri Mati, Mera Deshâ€ programme. The dumper driver was arrested on the charge of causing death by negligence.â€œDhavale was wearing a helmet,â€ assistant commissioner of police (traffic) Vitthal Kubade said. His body was sent to his native place in Ambegaon for last rites after post-mortem, he said.Marketing professional killed in accidentA marketing professional (19), riding pillion on a scooter, died after a container truck hit the two-wheeler on Chakan-Shikrapur Road around 7.30am on Tuesday. Police identified the deceased as Rupali Kulkarni from Beed. Her elder sister, driving the scooter, was admitted to a hospital in Chakan with severe injuries.

Title: 65-year-old writer among 2 killed in road accidents
content:
Ludhiana: A 65-year-old writer died in a road accident near his house in Samrala on Thursday evening.Tarlochan Singh was returning home on his two-wheeler after meeting his acquaintance when a speeding Thar crashed into him. His body was pulled out from under the vehicle with great difficulty, sources said.Samrala police said the vehicle has been taken into possession and the driver of the vehicle has been identified.Tarlochan was a known name in the literary circles and had written many films. He was also associated with Punjabi Sahitya Akademi for decades.. .AdvertisementMeanwhile, chairman of Lekhak Manch Samrala Parminder Singh Benipal expressed shock over the tragic death of Tarlochan Singh.In another incident, 28-year-old Amandeep Singh of Ramgarh Nawan Pind village near Khanna was killed after being hit by a car on the GT road on Wednesday night. Police have booked the car owner for causing death due to negligence. Amandeep was crossing the Ludhiana-Delhi highway when a car allegedly hit him. Khanna police said they have identified the owner of the car who belongs to Ludhiana.

Title: Ten killed, 13 injured in accident on Bavla-Bagodara highway in Ahmedabad district
content: AHMEDABAD: Ten people, including three children and five women, lost their lives, while 13 others sustained injuries when a mini-truck they were traveling in collided with a stationary truck on the Bavla-Bagodara highway in Gujarat's Ahmedabad district on Friday morning.The accident occurred near Bagodara village on the Rajkot-Ahmedabad Highway as a group of individuals was returning to Ahmedabad from a pilgrimage site in Chotila, located in the Surendranagar district."A total of five women, three children, and two men lost their lives in the morning accident," stated a police officer from the Ahmedabad rural police department.According to the police, the mini-truck was carrying 23 people from Solka village in Kapadwanj, Kheda district, as they were returning from Chotila on Friday.The incident happened when a truck, which had experienced a punctured tire, was stationary in the middle of the highway. The driver of the mini-truck failed to notice the stationary truck in time, resulting in the fatal collision, said Ahmedabad Range IG Prem Vir Singh.The accident was attributed to the overloaded condition of the mini-truck, which is typically used for transporting goods.The impact of the collision resulted in severe head and body injuries, leading to the fatalities. The injured individuals were promptly admitted to various hospitals, and post-mortem examinations were conducted for the deceased, said Singh.Bagodara police have begun an investigation into the case.In response to the incident, Gujarat chief minister Bhupendra Patel expressed his condolences for the victims via a tweet. He wrote, "The tragic accident on the Bavla-Bagodara highway in Ahmedabad district is deeply saddening. May the souls of the deceased rest in peace, and I pray for the swift recovery of the injured individuals. My sympathies are with the family members of the victims."Watch Tragic incident in Gujarat: Mini truck collides with parked vehicle, claims 10 lives in Ahmedabad

Title: Biker killed, five injured as truck hits 13 vehicles on Katraj-Kondhwa bypass
content: PUNE: A motorcyclist was killed and five others were injured after a container truck carrying cement pipes hit 13 vehicles, including a school bus, on Katraj-Kondhwa bypass around 8.45am on Thursday.Police identified the deceased biker as Prashant Krishna Chaure (45), a resident of Gulabanagar in Dhankawadi. He was going to his workplace when the accident occurred.Nine students from Sanskriti School in Undri in the bus had a close shave. But its driver suffered minor injuries as the truck hit the vehicle from the rear. "The truck driver, Tejas Kakade (22) of Kej taluka in Beed district, claimed that he had lost control over the vehicle due to brake failure on a slope," inspector (crime) Sanjay Mogle of the Kondhwa police said. Kakade claimed that he could not apply the emergency air brake as the distance between the truck and the vehicles ahead was less, he said.Will verify brake failure claim of truck driver after accident: PoliceInspector (crime) Sanjay Mogle of the Kondhwa police said: "The truck hit six motorcycles, the school bus, a tempo and five other four-wheelers before crashing into a median and coming to a halt. We are verifying Kakade's claim regarding the brake failure and will get the truck examined by RTO experts."Mogle said, "The injured have been taken to nearby hospitals. They are Narayan Katkar (45) and Ravi Bharate (32) from Kondhwa Budruk, Subhash Bandagale (49) and Pramod Shilamkar (38) from Bibvewadi, and Radhika Bodhe (40) from Ambegaon."He said, "We have detained Kakade and the process is on to register a case for offences punishable under sections 304A (causing death by negligence), 279 (driving a vehicle in a negligent manner) and 338 (act endangering life and safety of others) of the IPC and under the Motor Vehicles Act."Truck owner Anand Nimbalkar said, "Kakade was going to Khadi Machine Chowk from the Kanha hotel lane on the bypass to deliver the cement pipes in Undri."The accident disrupted the traffic along the 7km bypass stretch from Katraj Chowk to Khadi Machine Chowk for more than an hour-and-a-half. The vehicular flow was restored after the traffic police got the accident-hit vehicles moved aside to clear the main carriage way.Pramod Chaure, the sibling of the deceased biker, told TOI, "My brother was an in-charge at a sewing machine godown in Uruli Devachi. He was going to work. I got a call from a passer-by at the accident site around 9.15am, informing me that my brother had met with an accident. I reached the spot and saw him lying on the roadside. He had injured his hands, shoulder, legs and face. He later succumbed to injuries."Navnath Kamthe, a resident of the area, said, "I heard a loud noise while I was in a lane. I rushed out and found that a truck had hit multiple vehicles. The injured people were lying on the road. With help from some local people, we took them to hospitals."Autorickshaw driver Adil Pathan said, "Two injured bikers had come under the tempo. Some passers-by and I pulled them out and took them to the roadside."Building contractor Anil Kumar Kanje said, "The bypass has become heavily congested and it needs to be widened. Movement of heavy vehicles should be regulated."Activist Kailash Kamthe said, "Most parts of the bypass have slope, uneven surface and are riddled with potholes. Tyre bursts and brake failures are major causes of accidents. I have been hearing for 15 years that the bypass would be widened, but till date nothing has happened."

Title: 74-year-old biker killed in accident
content:
Nashik: A 74-year-old biker died after his vehicle was knocked down by a cement-mixer truck on Wednesday evening. The accident took place near the weigh bridge on Wadala road, Mumbai Naka police said. The deceased has been identified as Dadaji Shewale, a resident of Deepalinagar. Shewale was riding his bike on Wadala Road at 5pm, when a cement-mixer truck hit him. Police said the driver of the truck has been booked for death due to negligent driving and a case has been registered by the police officer on duty. tnn

Title: Madhya Pradesh: Mother dies in road accident, son killed in crash on way to last rites
content: BHOPAL: In a double tragedy, a mother and son died in accidents barely 12 hours apart in Madhya Pradesh. The woman was killed in a bike crash in Rewa district and her son died while rushing from Indore to attend her last rites.Rani Devi, 55, and her second son Sooraj Singh, 22, were cremated together at their native village, Jatri, in Rewa on Thursday.Rani Deviâ€™s husband had passed away years ago, and she brought up her three sons and three daughters. She lived with her eldest and youngest sons, Prakash and Sunny, in the village, which is close to the Uttar Pradesh border. Sooraj lived in Indore, 830km away.On Wednesday, Rani Devi was going to her maternal place with Sunny on a two-wheeler when a motorcycle rammed into them in Dabhaura, around 12km from their village. They were taken to a local hospital, where doctors referred her to Rewa (80km away), but she died on the way, sarpanch of Jatri village, Santraa Devi, told TOI. Sunny suffered fractures.. .AdvertisementAs soon as he heard of his motherâ€™s death, Sooraj set off from Indore for his native village. A friend, Abhishek Singh, offered his car and the duo set off for Jatri with a hired driver.Around 100km short of the village, the car crashed at Rampur Baghelan in Satna district. â€œWe received information around 7am. It seems a tyre burst and the car spun out of control and hit a parked truck. All three were rushed to Rewa Hospital, where Sooraj died and the other two are being treated,â€ Rampur Baghelan police station in charge Sandeep Chaturvedi told TOI. â€œThe entire village is anguished. No one can bear two back-to-back tragedies. It was heart-wrenching to see the mother and son being cremated together,â€ sarpanch Santraa Devi said.

Title: Youth dies after falling into pit dug up for underground electricity cable work in Chennai
content:
CHENNAI: A youth riding on a two-wheeler died after he fell into a pit dug up for underground electricity cable work at Senneerkuppam in Poonamallee on the outskirts of Chennai on Thursday night. His friend, who was riding pillion on the two-wheeler, suffered injuries.The deceased has been identified as Guna, 22, of Thanjavur who was employed at a MNC in Poonamallee. He was staying in a rented a house in the area. His friend, Madhivanan, was riding pillion with him. They were going for night shift. When they were entering a service road on the Chennai-Bengaluru National Highway, they failed to notice a 10-foot-deep pit dug up by TANGEDCO for underground cable work. The pit had protruding iron rods. The duo fell into the pit. The iron rods pierced Guna, killing him on the spot. Mathivaanan took the help of a few passersby and alerted police. The body of Guna was sent for a postmortem. . .AdvertisementMathivanan was admitted to hospital.

Title: Seven killed, four injured after SUV rolls down into river in Himachal Pradesh's Chamba district
content: SHIMLA: Seven people including six police personnel were killed and four others were injured after the SUV they were in rolled down into a river on Tissa-Bairagarh road in Churah sub-division of Himachal Pradesh's Chamba district on Friday morning.The policemen, all from the 2nd Indian Reserve Battalion, were on patrolling duty in the area, which borders Jammu and Kashmir, when the accident happened.The deceased cops were identified as sub-inspector Rakesh Gora, head constable Parveen and constable Kamaljeet, constable Sachin, constable Abhishek, and constable Lakshay. The SUV driver Chandu Ram also died in the accident.The injured include constable Sachin Kumar, head constable Rajender Kumar, constable Akshay Chaudhary and Pankaj Kumar, a local resident of Churah.According to police, prima facie the accident happened after the driver lost control. A police officer said that there were total 11 people in the SUV including nine cops and the accident happened near Tarwai bridge at around 9.30 am.The injured were taken to the nearby government hospital in Tissa from where they were referred to the zonal hospital in Chamba.Meanwhile, Churah MLA Hans Raj, who visited the spot in the morning accused the Public Works Department of negligence.Demanding an inquiry into the accident, the MLA said that the accident happened due to a landslide. He said that landslides were frequently occurring at many points on this road and despite repeated demands the PWD department did not take any action."We had got this road closed also and asked the PWD department to take necessary measures to ensure that no mishap occurs on this road. I had specifically asked them to cut the mountain where there was a possibility of landslides. But the department did not do anything and today this unfortunate accident happened," said the MLA.Chief Minister Sukhwinder Sukhu and Deputy Chief Minister Mukesh Agnihotri have expressed their grief at the accident and ordered immediate monetary relief to the kin of the deceased and medical assistance to the injured.According to DIG (Northern Range) Abhishek Dular, who visited the accident spot, the SUV fell down into the river after it was hit by a boulder at a landslide-prone area on the Tissa-Baraghar road.â€œThe vehicle rolled down 150-200 meters after it was hit by a boulder and plunged into a river. This particular spot on the road is highly landslide-prone,â€ said the DIG, adding that only those who survived fell out of the SUV while it was rolling down the hill.

Title: Man,43, killed as wild boar crashes into autorickshaw
content:
Coimbatore: A 43-year-old man was killed on Wednesday after an autorickshaw in which he was travelling overturned on the Kurunthamalai-Pungampalayam Road. The vehicle overturned after a wild boar, which was crossing the road, suddenly crashed into the vehicle.The deceased was identified as M Senthilkumar, 43, a daily wager from MGR Colony at Mangalakaraipudur. The Karamadai police said the accident took place around 8.30pm when Senthilkumar was travelling home in an autorickshaw from Karamadai. A wild boar hit the front wheel of the auto, which overturned in the impact. Senthilkumar suffered severe head injuries after an iron rod in the auto pierced his head, while the auto driver, Palanisamy, escaped with minor injuries. Motorists who were passing by alerted police and rushed Senthilkumar to a private hospital at Karamadai, where doctors declared him dead. Karamadai forest range officer N Divya said wild boar movement was being reported in the locality for the first time. â€œWe have given an immediate relief of 50,000 to the family members of the deceased on Thursday. The remaining compensation of 4.5 lakh will be given to the family soon,â€ the officer said. Some villagers also confirmed sighting wild boar movement in the locality. Meanwhile, the Karamadai police have registered a case and further investigation is on.

Title: Road hit kills young tiger on state highway near Nagzira
content: NAGPUR: In a big blow to Navegon-Nagzira Tiger Reserve (NNTR) struggling to revive its tiger population, a young male died in a road hit near Murdoli village in the buffer zone of the reserve on Thursday night. The tiger was offspring of NNTR's T-14 tigress. This is the fourth tiger death in roadkills in the region in less than a year.The accident occurred at 9.30pm on the Kohmara-Gondia state highway (NH753). The accident was first witnessed by Sanjay Ghasne and Hivraj Raut, two field coordinators of wildlife NGO TRACT, Nagpur, who were behind the speeding car (MH35-AR-9397) which dashed the tiger head-on, leaving it injured. The volunteers immediately informed Goregaon forest officials.The accident has brought back focus on lack of mitigation measures on the stretch of the highway passing through the reserve."The impact was so severe that the bumper of the car was smashed. But the airbags saved its occupants," said forest officials.Gondia deputy conservator of forest (DyCF) Pramod Panchbhai said, "The incident occurred in compartment no. 419 of Palewada. For nearly two hours, an intense search was launched at 10pm to trace the injured tiger, but without success. Early morning, we again searched for the animal and it was found sitting near a watercourse in the forest hardly 100 metre from the accident spot."The animal was unable to stand on its feet due to serious injuries. "The rapid rescue team (RRT) from NNTR tranquilized the tiger for shifting it to Gorewada Rescue Centre, Nagpur, but it died on the way near Kohmara," Panchbhai said.The postmortem revealed that the tiger's left and right shoulders and cupola were completely crushed. "One can imagine the speed of the car as both its airbags had opened after hitting the tiger. It is a forested road passing through the tiger reserve. This particular sub-adult was a resident of the area," said Panchbhai.The DyCF said that investigation will be done and a preliminary offence will be registered. He was not sure whether the car driver will be arrested or not.As per the All-India Tiger Estimation (AITE-2022), the 656sqkm NNTR has 11 tigers. To augment tiger numbers, five tigresses (two already released) will be translocated in the park. A young male tiger can sire at least a dozen cubs.Honorary wildlife warden Sawan Bahekar said, "The cub that died was from the third litter of T14, a resident of the area. Two years ago, a cub died in a train hit. Time and again we have been raising the issue of mitigation measures on the said patch, but to no avail."According to Bahekar, there have been many incidents in which herbivores are killed in the said critical forest patch. "Regular travelers have been telling us about close encounter with tigers, and today it turned out to be a reality. No signages, display boards, speed breakers or rumble strips have been installed in forest patches. These temporary steps are needed immediately till mitigation measures are taken," said Bahekar.Panchbhai promised to take necessary steps after meeting district authorities and road agency officials. "Similar mitigation steps will be needed on the stretch between Gondia and Deori," he said.

Title: 2 dead, 7 injured in separate road accidents in west UP
content: BIJNOR: Two people were killed and seven others were injured in separate road accidents in Bijnor and Amroha districts on Saturday.In the first incident, three cars collided in Didoli area of Amroha early in the morning, leaving six persons injured. After the accident, all victims were rushed to a local hospital where doctors described their condition as stable. The injured were identified as Harpal Singh, 40, a resident of Delhi, Mukesh Bhatt, 39, and Anjali Bhatt, 35, from Noida, and Harjendra Singh, 45, ofShahjahanpur. Two other victims are yet to be identified.Didoli SHO Arvind Kumar said, â€œSix people were injured in the collision and they have been admitted to a local hospital for treatment. Further investigation is on and action will be taken accordingly.â€Meanwhile, in the second incident, two people died and one was critically injured after a speeding car hit a bike on Bijnor-. .AdvertisementMoradabad state highway. The dead were identified as Suresh Singh, 55, and Samarpal Singh, 45, both residents of Gauhar Zaid village.On information, police rushed to the spot and sent the bodies to district hospital for autopsy and admitted the injured person identified as Vipin Kumar, 35, to a private hospital for treatment, where his condition is stated to be critical. The accused car driver fled the spot after the accident, and efforts are being made to nab him, police said.

Title: 2 killed, 2 hurt after car falls into ditch in Unnao
content:
Kanpur: Two persons were killed and three others injured, after a speeding car they were travelling in, overturned and fell into a ditch on Maurawan-Mohanlalganj road in Unnao district late Thursday night. The dead included the car owner and his neighbour.On getting information of the accident, police reached the spot and sent the injured to the health centre, from where they were referred to Lucknow's Trauma Centre. Vipin Yadav (29), son of Ram Vilas Yadav, resident of Mohalla Shanti Nagar, Lucknow, had left his home for somewhere along with four others in a car.Mohit Kumar (28), son of Rajjan Lal, Vivek Kumar (35), son of Yadunath Yadav, Rajveer (25), son of Ambika Yadav, all residents of the same locality and Pankaj (20), son of Sarvesh Yadav, resident of Ruchi Khand, Lucknow, were accompanying him. The accident took place near the Kudra village on the Maurawan-Mohanlalganj Road,at around 5 am on Friday morning, when Vipin lost control over the vehicle and it overturned and fell into a roadside ditch. Vipin and Mohit suffered critical wounds and died on the spot.Circle officer Purwa Deepak Singh inspected the accident site.According to eyewitnesses, the incident took place when the driver was trying to save a stray dog which suddenly came on the road. The bodies have been sent for post-mortem examination, the CO said.

Title: Four dead as truck rams into bus on UP highway
content: MEERUT: Four passengers, including a woman and her daughter, were killed while 11 others were seriously injured in a collision between a Uttar Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation bus and a truck on NH-91 in Bulandshahr district around 2 pm on Friday. The bus with nearly 30 passengers on board was heading to Delhi from Budaun.ASP, Bulandshahr, Anukriti Sharma, said, "After crossing Bulandshahr's Kotwali Dehat police station area, the bus reached the NH-91 intersection when a speeding truck, bearing a Nagaland registration number, rammed it from behind on the highway." According to sub-inspector Devendra Kumar Shukla, the deceased have been identified as Dhanveer Singh (26), Sauraj Kumar (18), both brothers who used to run a jaggery mill in Budaun district, Naniya (70), a resident of Dibai in Bulandshahr and her daughter Brijbala (35). Brijbala's five-year-old daughter suffered injuries, but is out of danger."Nearly 11 injured passengers were rushed to a local hospital. Two of them were later referred to higher medical centres in Meerut and Aligarh. Four passengers have been confirmed dead by doctors at the hospital and their bodies have been sent for post-mortem," Shukla added. SHO of Kotwali-Dehat, Dharmendra Rathore said, "We have informed the victims' families. We are yet to receive a complaint in this matter."

Title: 7 of Sikar family killed in road accident near Banthari
content:
JAIPUR: Seven people of one family, who were going from Rajasthanâ€™s Sikar to Nagaur to attend a wedding function, were killed and two others critically injured in a head-on collision between their eight-seater car and a bus near Banthari under Khunkhuna police station in Deedwana district on Saturday. The two critically injured included a six-year-old boy.According to police, the accident took place at 7pm when some members of the extended family were going to attend the wedding ceremony at Nagaur. Suddenly a rashly driven bus coming from the opposite direction hit their car. â€œThe front portion of the car was badly smashed and all the passengers got trapped inside the vehicle,â€ said a senior police officer of Khunkhuna police station. Soon after the accident, locals called the police, who rushed to the spot. With the help of metal cutters, the trapped passengers were brought out but all of them were already dead.â€œThe deceased were identified as Shahrukh (22), Saddam (28), Mohammed Tohit (15), Mohamed Juber (18), Asif (30), Mohammed (17), and Mohammed Rashid (21),â€ the officer said. tnn
