Title: One killed, 3 injured in road accident in Jaisalmer dist
content:
Jaisalmer: A speeding jeep hit a motorcycle in which an elderly woman died on the spot while the rider was seriously injured. Three persons in the jeep were also injured.The incident took place near Kahla village Fanta on Sam route.The jeep driver fled after the accident. The injured were sent to Jawahar hospital from where the seriously injured was referred to Jodhpur. Sadar police seized both vehicles. A search was launched for the absconding driver. tnn

Title: 54-year-old woman riding pillion dies in road accident
content:
Nashik: A 54-year-old woman was killed in a bike-tractor collision at the Dwarka junction in Nashik on Friday morning.The woman was riding pillion on the bike driven by her husband when it collided with the tractor. The deceased woman is identified as Sunanda Chordia, a resident of Ashoka Marg.According to the cops, Chordia and her husband Rajendra were on their way on their two-wheeler towards Thakkar Bazar via Dwarka Junction.At around 6.30 am on Friday, when their bike reached Dwarka junction, they were hit by a tractor moving towards Mumbai. Sunanda suffered serious injuries to the head, hands, and other parts of her body.Her husband immediately took her to the Nashik civil hospital in a private vehicle. The doctors attending Sunanda checked her and found that she was dead.The Mumbai Naka police have registered a case of accidental death. The police said that, on the complaint of the victimâ€™s husband, they would soon be registering an FIR against the unidentified tractor driver that hit the couple.It must be noted that Dwarka Junction has police bandobast throughout the day. Despite this, accidents continue to occur at this junction, given the flow of traffic from either side of the junction. This year, until June, the Nashik police have registered 110 cases of death due to negligence, mainly by motorists.

Title: BSF truck overturns, 1 jawan killed, 12 injured in Jaisalmer
content: JAISALMER: A BSF truck met with an accident in Shahgarh bulj area in Jaisalmer district in which a head constable died and 12 other jawans were injured. The injured were rushed to Jawahar hospital in Jaisalmer. Condition of three injured jawans is serious. The deceased BSF head constable is a resident of Bihar. Senior BSF, police and administration officers reached the spot and coordinated with the doctors for treatment of the injured. The truck left from Dabla and was headed to Shahgarh bulj with 16 jawans. The incident took place near Ghotaru, 170km from Jaisalmer district headquarters. Reportedly, the truck driver saw a big pit on the road. He swivelled the steering wh eel and lost control causing the vehicle to overturn. Thirteen out of 16 jawans sitting in the truck were injured. Head constable Sanjay Kumar Dubey (40), a resident of Bihar, died while being taken to the hospital.The jawans deployed in a nearby border outpost were informed and they reached the spot with ambulance and took the injured to Jaisalmer district hospital where the doctors declared Dubey dead. The body will sent to deceased's native place after postmortem. Dy SP Priyanka Kumawat said that police and BSF were investigating the case.

Title: Two killed in separate road accidents on Saturday
content:
Nashik: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in the city on Saturday in the Satpur and the Mumbai Naka area of the city. The Satpur police arrested a 48-year-old bus driver Ganesh Shinde of Ozar on the charge of causing death due to negligence. The police said that Shinde was speeding a private bus on the Trimbak Road on Saturday at around 2pm. While passing through a junction on Trimbak Road, he suddenly took a turn and knocked down a biker coming from the other side. . .AdvertisementThe biker identified as Vaibhav Deshmukh of Shramik Nagar, Satpur suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to them. In the other case registered by the Mumbai Naka police, a 20-year-old person who was replacing a flat tyre of his truck was run over by a speeding truck on the flyover of the Mumbai Agra highway at around 4.15am. The deceased person identified as Rahul Yadav is a resident of Shivpuri, Madhya Pradesh. Yadav was replacing the flat tyre of his truck on the flyover when a speeding truck rammed into his vehicle. Yadav who was underneath the truck got run over and died on the spot. The Mumbai Naka police have arrested the driver, 45-year-old Bhura Babar, who is a resident of Indore, Madhya Pradesh. The flyover of the Mumbai Agra highway passing through the city witnesses frequent accidents. Most of these accidents are linked to illegal halting.

Title: 3 on a bike: 1 killed, 2 hurt after crash
content:
Lucknow: A 32-year-old man was killed while two of his companions suffered critical injuries in a road accident in which their uncontrolled bike rammed into a pole put outside a marriage hall in Krishnanagar on Sunday afternoon.A seven seconds CCTV footage of the incident also went viral on social media.The three youths identified as Vikas Verma, Prabhat Verma and Rinku Verma, all residents of Para, were on a single bike when the incident occurred. Vikas was driving the vehicle. â€œWhen they reached the marriage lawn in Pandit Khera locality , the vehicle rammed into a pole on the roadside killing Vikas. ,â€ said SHO, Krishnanagar, Vikram Singh. Title: Woman killed as boulder falls on car
content:
Idukki: Somini (67), a resident of Upputhara, died after a boulder fell on the car she was travelling in at Valanjanganam near Kuttikkanam on Sunday evening.According to the personnel of Peermade police station and highway police, who conducted the rescue operation, the accident occurred at around 6.30pm near Valanjanganam waterfalls when the car was parked by the road. The entire top portion of the car was crushed in the impact.Six persons, including Bibin Divakaran, a civil police officer attached to Cumbummettu police station, along with his wife and two kids were in the car when the accident occurred. Except for Somini, all other persons in the car were rescued with minor injuries. Somini was sitting on the left side of the car in the back seat and this was the portion where the boulder landed. Somini was working as a maid to take care of the kids, said police.â€œIt took nearly 45 minutes for the police and fire and rescue personnel to take out the aged woman as that portion of the car was fully mangled. She was rushed to a private hospital in Mundakayam but was declared brought dead,â€ said a highway policeman who took part in the rescue operation. tnn. .Advertisement

content:
Visakhapatnam: In a tragic incident, a 55-year-old woman died on the spot and another woman suffered injuries when their scooter was knocked down by a lorry near Aganampudi under Duvvada police station limits in Vizag city. The deceased was identified as Hashmatunnisa (55). The police have registered a case and shifted the body for post-mortem. Meanwhile, police said rash driving has been causing road accidents on NH-16 stretch in north coastal AP. Special teams will be deployed to control such vehicles, police said. TNN

Title: Maharashtra: Speeding SUV runs over, kills two in Nagpur
content: NAGPUR: A two-wheeler rider and an elderly pedestrian were killed when a speeding SUV ran over them in Nagpur city in Maharashtra, police said on Monday. The accident took place at Ram Nagar square in the city on Sunday afternoon, an official said. Pawas Rajendrakumar Chaurasia (33) was riding his two-wheeler and Kusum Ishwar Bhoyar was walking on the side of the road when a SUV driven at a high speed hit them, he said. Chaurasia was trapped under the four-wheeler, while Bhoyar was crushed to death, the official said. . .AdvertisementThe injured man was extricated from under the car and was rushed to a nearby hospital, where he died last night, he said. A case under sections 279 (rash driving), 304 (a) (causing death by negligence) and other relevant provisions of the Indian Penal Code and Motor Vehicle Act was registered against the driver who fled the scene leaving the car behind.(With PTI inputs)

Title: Boy dies in hit-&-run on Kalewadi BRTS route, pedestrian injured in Vimannagar
content: Pune: An eleven-year-old boy was killed while a 25-year-old pedestrian was seriously injured in two separate accidents that took place on the the Bus Rapid Transit System (BRTS) in the Kalewadi and the Vimannagar routes, respectively.In the Kalewadi accident on Thursday night, Ashish Chaudhary and his friend were walking along the BRTS route when an unidentified vehicle hit him, causing fatal injuries, an officer from the Wakad police station said.The officer said that Chaudhary hailed from Nepal and his father works at a construction site in Kalewadi. According to the victimâ€™s father, Birbahadur Chaudhary (41), around 8.30pm on Thursday, his elder son went to the BRTS route after he heard that there had been an accident there. â€œThe boy came home and told his father that he had seen his younger brother lying on the BRTS route,â€ the officer said.When his father reached the spot he found that the injured person was taken to a hospital in Thergaon. When he reached the hospital, the doctorâ€™s told him that his son had succumbed to his injuries. The victimâ€™s father later found out that his son and his friends were walking on the BRTS route, when a car hit him. â€œHis sonâ€™s friend was terrified after the accident and fled from the spot,â€ the police officer said.In the Vimannagar accident, the pedestrian, whose identity has not been established as yet, was also hit by an unidentified vehicle around 11pm on Friday.An officer from the Airport police station said that the victim was crossing the road, when a car knocked him down. He was immediately taken to a nearby hospital, where he is currently undergoing treatment. â€œThe spot where the accident happened did not have any CCTV cameras. We are investigating,â€ the police officer said.

Title: Flyover proposed at accident-prone blind spot that claimed four lives
content: MEERUT: Following the death of four persons in a collision between a UP Roadways bus and a truck on Friday at the Adhauli-Khurja bypass cut in Bulandshahr, the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) announced on Monday the proposal for a flyover at the accident spot. Four passengers, including a woman and her daughter, were killed after the bus, en route to Delhi from Badaun, was hit by a speeding truck with a Nagaland registration carrying goods on NH-91 in Bulandshahr. Eleven passengers suffered serious injuries. CCTV footage of the accident revealed that the truck driver failed to notice the sudden appearance of the bus in front of it. A senior police officer from Bulandshahr, speaking anonymously, acknowledged that with appropriate safety measures, the accident may not have happened. PK Kaushik, project director, NHAI, said, "Six flyovers are planned for at accident-prone blind spots along the highway. Two are intended for Bulandshahr district. In light of the urgent requirement, we have adjusted our operations and will accelerate construction of these flyovers as soon as we secure approval.". .Advertisement Kaushik expressed confidence that these flyovers at critical locations will effectively curb recurring road accidents. TOI had highlighted the lack of safety measures at the Adhauli-Khurja bypass cut, identified as a blind turn, due to the frequency of accidents. No speed breakers are present on either side of the road at this intersection, which has witnessed multiple accidents. In all, 35 people have died in accidents over six months in Bulandshahr district alone.

Title: 4 laborers killed as truck rams into autorickshaw in Telangana's Warangal
content: ILLANDA (WARDHANNAPET): Four laborers, who were honey sellers, were killed while three others suffered serious injuries when a speeding truck rammed into the autorickshaw carrying them in Warangal district of Telangana on Wednesday morning. The incident took place on the Warangal-Khammam Highway at Yellanda village outskirts in Wardhannapet Mandal of Warangal district.Upon getting the information, Wardhannapet ACP Suresh Kumar and his staff went to the spot. The injured people were shifted to MGM Hospital in Warangal for treatment, while the deceased were shifted for postmortem.. .AdvertisementAccording to Wardhannapet ACP Suresh, the people were travelling in an autorickshaw headed towards Thorrur. Unfortunately, an overspeeding truck hit the autorickshaw. The truck was coming from the opposite direction from Khammam."The accident was so severe that the autorickshaw was completely crushed beneath the truck, leading to the instant death of four labourers," said Suresh.The accident resulted from the truck driver being in a drunken state.

Title: Five of family killed in road accident in Gujarat, miraculous escape for two-year-old boy
content: AHMEDABAD: Five people from a family died while a two-year-old boy escaped miraculously with minor injuries after two cars collided in Gujarat's Bharuch district on Wednesday.The mishap occurred near Hansot village during the afternoon hours, police said.In the ill-fated vehicle, six people were traveling from Bharuch to Surat. Inspector Karansinh Chudasama of Hansot police station said that the toddler, though injured, managed to survive the impact with minor wounds. . .AdvertisementThe other car's driver also suffered injuries due to the accident. Among the casualties were four women, including an elderly couple named Imtiyaz Patel and Salmaben, their married daughters Maria and Afifa, and Jamila Patel, the wife of Imtiyaz Patel's brother. These unfortunate souls, all residents of Bharuch, were en route to Surat when the tragedy unfolded.The young boy, who happened to be the son of Maria Patel, displayed an extraordinary stroke of luck by surviving the collision. He was promptly admitted to a nearby medical facility under the care of local law enforcement.Upon receiving notification of the accident, the police swiftly arrived at the scene. The investigation carried out by Chudasama unveiled that Imtiyaz Patel, aged in his late 50s, had been driving the vehicle and was wearing his seatbelt at the time of impact. The circumstances leading to the collision involved Patel's car attempting to overtake another vehicle while traversing a straight section of road near Hansot. Regrettably, control was lost, causing the car to veer into the opposing lane where a car driven by Hitendrasinh was traveling.Despite both cars being equipped with functional airbags, the devastating impact claimed the lives of five family members from the Patel family. In a stroke of fortune, Hitendrasinh and the young toddler emerged from the crash with their lives intact. (With inputs from PTI)

Title: 5-yr-old among 2 killed in hit-&-run crashes in 2 days
content: GURGAON: Two persons have lost their lives in separate road accidents in the city in the last two days. On Monday, a five-year-old boy died after a speeding vehicle hit him on Sultanpur-Farrukhnagar road.The boy, a resident of Sultanpur, was a class 1 student. He strayed onto the road accidentally while playing when an unidentified vehicle suddenly hit him around 11am and sped off, police said.The boy suffered critical head and leg injuries. Passersby rushed him to SGT Hospital in Budhera, which later referred him to PGI Hospital in Rohtak. He succumbed to his injuries on Monday night, cops said. The boy's uncle, Vikram, said that he was informed of the incident by a local fruit seller. "When I reached the spot, I was told by passersby that my nephew had been taken to a nearby hospital," he said.. .AdvertisementAn FIR was filed at Farrukhnagar police station under IPC sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death by negligence) late on Monday. "No arrests have been made so far. We questioned the locals, but nobody saw the accused or the vehicle. CCTV cameras are also far from the accident spot, so there is no footage of the accident," a senior police officer said. In the second incident, a speeding car allegedly hit a pedestrian on Sohna Road on Sunday. Vijay (25), a resident of Sohna, was walking near a government school when the unidentified vehicle mowed him down. His father Jagdish said he saw his son's body in a pool of blood when they reached the spot. He succumbed to his injuries while undergoing treatment at the Sector 10 Civil Hospital on Monday night. "I want the people who killed my son to be arrested at the earliest. This is not the first time that such an accident has taken place in this area. Similar accidents take place every month an the accused remain unidentified. The police should investigate the matter efficiently," Jagdish said. Based on his complaint, a case was registered under sections 279 and 304A of the IPC at at Bhondsi police station on Monday.

Title: Multiple road accidents on Tuesday, one dies
content:
PANAJI: A 28-year-old native of West Bengal, Surjit Kohli, was killed after a four-wheeler rammed into his two-wheeler near Damian de Goa on the Porvorim highway on Tuesday. He has been residing in Goa. The police arrested Harshit Tamrekar, 21, a native of Chhattisgarh, for rash and negligent driving.In another incident on Tuesday, multiple vehicles rammed into one another at the landing of the Atal Setu bridge at Porvorim. A third incident saw a four-wheeler dash into a pole at Miramar. No one was hurt in either incident. Road safety in Goa has been brought into question after drunk driver Paresh Sawardekar allegedly rammed into multiple vehicles on Banastarim bridge on August 6, causing three deaths and serious injuries to three others.. .Advertisement

Title: Two killed in road accidents on I-Day
content: Nagpur: Two persons were killed in separate road accidents in the jurisdiction of Ajni and Hingna police stations on Independence Day. The first incident took place on Hingna Road. The deceased Khushi Manish Patle (20, a resident of Selu) was returning home after meeting her relative at Waghdara in Hingna. While going back, her husband Manish had stopped their car on the roadside. Another four-wheeler came from the wrong side and hit her. Khushi was taken to a private hospital where she was declared brought dead. The second accident was reported from Manewada Ring Road. Police said Shubham Laxmanrao Thakre (28), a resident of Binaki Mangalwari, was going towards Omkar Nagar on Tuesday at 11pm when a speeding car hit his two-wheeler. He fell on the road and another car coming from behind crushed him. TNN. .Advertisement

Title: Girl going to preschool dies as bus rams bike
content: BENGALURU: A four-and-half-year-old girl and three others died in road accidents since Tuesday night.Poorva Rao died after a rashly-driven BMTC bus rammed the bike she was riding to preschool along with her father on Subramanyapura Main Road, south Bengaluru. The accident occurred at 8.45am on Wednesday when the bus hit the two-wheeler from the rear. Her father Prasanna MS, a private company employee, sustained injuries on his right leg. Police blamed BMTC driver E Basavaraj Poojari for the accident. An officer said Prasanna lost control over the bike when the bus rammed it. He fell on one side of the road; Poorva fell on the other side and came under the bus wheels. Cops arrested the bus driver, identified as E Basavaraj Poojari.Lecturer, teacher die . .Advertisementas two-wheelers collideIn another accident on Tuesday, a 55-year-old college lecturer died and a 21-year-old kindergarten teacher succumbed to injuries when their bikes collided near Kengeri at 11.40pm. The victims are D Narasappa, biology lecturer with Kengeri government college, and G Raksha, resident of Andhrahalli. Raksha's friend Chandan, who was riding the bike, is undergoing treatment at a hospital and out of danger, police said."Narasappa was riding back home and Chandan and Raksha were coming in the opposite direction when their bikes collided near Marutinagar Main Road," a police officer said.Narasappa is survived by his wife and two sons. Raksha, the only child of her parents, had lost her father Girish one year ago and was staying with her mother, police said.20-year-old riding to Nandi Hills dies, brother hurt A 20-year-old youth was killed on the spot and his sibling sustained injuries when their gearless scooter collided with a sports motorbike near Doddaballapur on Tuesday morning.The victim is Adil, a resident of Gurappanapalya and from Bihar. His younger brother Murishid, 18, escaped with minor injuries. Adil quit his job in a private company recently, police said."The siblings were riding towards Nandi Hills when their scooter collided with the bike near Vishwanathapura at 9.30am. Adil sustained fatal head injuries," a police officer said.

Title: Pune: IT professional killed, 2 others injured as car crashes into tree
content: PUNE: An IT professional (23) was killed and her two friends were injured after the car they were in jumped a pavement and crashed into a tree on Sangamwadi Road around 4am on Thursday. Police identified the deceased as Rujuta Bhalerao from Vimannagar. The injured victims, Riti Garg (24), also an IT professional from Vimannagar, and Pratik Ghule (26) from Narhe are under treatment in a private hospital. Ghule was driving the car. Sub-inspector Mahadev Linge from the Yerawada police said, "Ghule and the two women had started from Yerawada for Camp in a hatchback car to have food [when the accident occurred]."Techie killed in car crash. .AdvertisementSub-inspector Mahadev Linge from the Yerawada police said: â€œWhile proceeding along the Sangamwadi road from Shadwal Baba Dargah Chowk to the Patil Estate junction, car driver Ghule lost control over the vehicle. The car jumped the pavement and crashed into the tree. Bhalerao was in the rear seat and died of serious injuries. Garg, sitting next to Bhalerao, and Ghule survived with injuries.â€Ghule told the police during his questioning that a motorcyclist came in front of their car. He said in a bid to save the biker, he steered the hatchback on the other side. The car jumped the pavement before crashing into the tree, the police said. Ghule was booked on the charge of causing Bhaleraoâ€™s death and injuring others under the Indian Penal Code and Motor Vehicles Act.

Title: 6-year-old schoolgirl killed, three injured as jeep overturns in Jaipur
content: Jaipur: A six-year-old student was killed and three kids were injured when a school jeep ferrying kids overturned on Amarsar Road near Jaipur on Thursday.SHO (Amarsar) Dharam Singh said the deceased has been identified as Akshita Jat, a resident of Shivsinghpura village. â€œThree kids were also injured in the incident and were discharged after initial treatment at a local hospital. The deceased was a resident of Shivsinghpura village. Singh said that the jeep belonged to a private school which ferried kids from their houses to the school and back. The jeep belonged to a private school which ferried kids from their houses to the school and back.â€He said, â€œThe jeep was travelling from Amarsar and headed towards Jaisinghpura when it overturned on the road and skidded,â€ he said.Police stated that the precise cause of the accident could not be immediately determined, but there is a possibility that it occurred due to the vehicle losing control due to excessive speed.. .AdvertisementPolice reported that 12 individuals, including the jeep driver, were present when the accident occurred. â€œAll the children were removed from the jeep and rushed to the nearby hospital. However, a six-year-old girl who had sustained multiple injuries succumbed to her wounds at the hospital,â€ the official said.Police mentioned that the students were residents of nearby villages and would travel to the school on a daily basis.Sources within the Jaipur (Rural) police added that they will also inspect the condition of the jeep to ascertain whether it met all the standards set for transporting schoolchildren. â€œIt is still to be determined whether the jeep was owned by the school authorities or used in a sublet manner,â€ the official added.

Title: BTech student rams car into auto, kills man
content: BENGALURU: A 19-year-old BTech student has been arrested on charges of ramming his car into an auto while in an inebriated condition, killing its driver on the spot, near Devanahalli late on Wednesday night. The accused, Stephen Santosh, a resident of BDS Nagar in Kothanur and from Kerala, was driving back home after attending a close friend's birthday party at a pub, police said. The deceased auto driver, Naveen Kumar, a resident of Kamakshipalya, was driving back home after dropping a passenger. Santosh, a student with a private university near Yelahanka, was arrested under IPC 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder). His blood samples showed 120mg/100ml alcohol, as against the permissible 30mg/100ml. He was produced before a local court on Thursday evening and handed over to judicial custody at Bengaluru Central Prison.. .AdvertisementScooter skids, 1 deadIn another accident, a 35-year-old private company employee was killed after his gearless scooter skid off the road after midnight on Wednesday on HMT Main Road, Jalahalli. The deceased has been identified as J Samuel, a resident of Jalahalli. Police said the accident occurred around 12.15am while Samuel was riding back home. " Cops in patrolling police vehicles reached the spot and shifted the seriously injured Samuel to KC General Hospital in Malleswaram, and later to Nimhans where he succumbed to his injuries," a cop said.

Police identified the deceased as S Varshath Kumar, a student of Sri Krishna College. Varshath was rushed to the Coimbatore Medical College Hospital but he died on the way. The TIW (West) police registered a case based on a complaint from the victimâ€™s father Suresh Kumar. TNN

He was admitted to Government Stanley Hospital, while the two bodies were sent to the morgue at the hospital. A case has been registered.

Title: 60 killed in road accidents in Uttarakhand this monsoon
content: DEHRADUN: As many as 60 people have lost their lives in road accidents in Uttarakhand since the start of the monsoon season on June 15 this year. After Pithoragarh, which recorded 15 fatalities, Uttarkashi, where seven pilgrims from Gujarat were killed in a bus accident on Sunday, has been the second worst- affected district with 11 deaths. Pauri Garhwal and Tehri Garhwal districts have witnessed seven and six deaths respectively.  Incidentally, the bus accident on Sunday was the second fatal accident at Gangani in Uttarkashi district this monsoon season. On July 10, three Char Dham yatra pilgrims from Madhya Pradesh were killed after a massive landslide had occurred on the Gangotri highway at Gangnani. In another road accident involving Char Dham pilgrims, three persons, who were from Andhra Pradesh, Delhi and Bihar, were killed and three others went missing after the vehicle they were travelling in, fell into a gorge on Badrinath highway near Rsishikesh on July 9.  Devendra Patwal, district disaster management officer, Uttarkashi, said, "The patch of Gangotri highway in Gangnani area is narrow with slope and sharp turns. Due to this, the place becomes vulnerable to accidents. Therefore, drivers need to be very cautious while driving through it."  He added that the state disaster management authority has identified a total of 16 accident-prone spots in both Gangotri and Yamunotri national highways, with prominent ones being Dabrani, Gangnani, Nalupani, Dharasu, Radi Top, Damta and Sarigaad.

Title: Tamil Nadu ADGPâ€™s nephew killed in road accident
content:
Salem: Tamil Nadu ADGP (law and order) A Arunâ€™s 23-year-old nephew died in a road accident in the city late Saturday night.The deceased, K Deepan Kumar, of Chinna Tirupathi, was the son of Arunâ€™s elder brother A Kumar. He was a final year MBBS student of the Salem Government Mohan Kumaramangalam Medical College and Hospital (SGMKMCH).On Saturday night, Deepan was returning home from Reddipatty on his bike. When he neared a bridge at Suramangalam in the city, he rammed into an Aavin milk tanker truck from behind and went under the rear wheel. He was crushed to death.â€œHe suffered a grave head injury and died on the spot,â€ said an officer from the Suramangalam police. Police sent the body to the SGMKMCH for a postmortem. The deceased was not wearing a helmet, police said. A case has been registered, and further investigation is on.Dean of SGMKMCH Dr R Mani said, â€œDespite our instructions in class, many students are not wearing helmets.â€ He said he had instructed all HoDs and professors to advise students to wear helmets and drive safely with a minimum speed limit.

Title: Mini truck rams bike, man dies on spot
content: Gurgaon: Two men died in separate road accidents in less than 24 hours in the city â€” one in a hit-and-run near Ambience Mall and another on the Southern Peripheral Road (SPR) in Badshapur.Yadram Bairva (45), who worked as a driver for a Korean couple in the city, was on his way to Park Palace in Sector 54 around 4am on Saturday when a mini-truck ran over his motorcycle near Ambience Mall.Yadramâ€™s brother-in-law Tarachand said the couple who had employed the 45-year-old used to play golf early in the mornings. â€œThatâ€™s why he would leave for work on the weekends. A speeding mini-truck rammed his motorcycle a few metres ahead of Ambience Mall, killing him on the spot in DLF-3 area. The accused driver fled from the spot,â€ Tarachand told cops.Police said on Sunday that a passerby informed them about the crash. â€œWhen we reached the spot, we found the victim lying dead on the road. He had received deep cuts on his head and legs that are likely to have led to his death. We found his ID card in his pocket, through which we contacted his family,â€ said inspector Virender of DLF-3 police station.A case was registered under sections 279 (rash driving), 304 (death by negligence), and 427 (mischief causing damage) of the IPC against the unidentified driver on Saturday. Investigators are scanning CCTV footage from the area to identify the absconding accused.Sixteen hours before the hit-and-run, an elderly man driving a mini-truck crashed into a dumper truck parked in the middle of SPR in Badshapur area around noon on Friday.Inspector Surendra of the Badshapur police station said the driver â€” Radhesham, a resident of Mujesar village in Faridabad â€” was stuck inside the mini-truck, battling for his life.â€œAs soon as we reached the spot, we saw that the Canter was stuck behind a dumper parked in the middle of SPR. The driver who was seriously injured was stuck inside. We had to separate the vehicles with a crane to rescue the driver. He was then taken to the Civil Hospital in Sector 10 where he was declared brain dead. He died soon after,â€ the inspector said on Sunday.The truck driverâ€™s body was sent for autopsy. Cops have seized the dumper, whose driver is absconding.Radheshamâ€™s brother Sitaram (37) filed a complaint at the Badshapur police station, based on which an FIR was registered under sections 283 (danger or obstruction on a public way), 304-AA (death by negligence), and 427 (mischief causing damage) of the IPC.

Title: 1 killed, 20 pilgrims injured as bus overturns on highway in Uttar Pradesh's Auraiya
content: KANPUR: A woman passenger was killed while 20 others suffered injuries when a private bus carrying 60 pilgrims from Khaga town of Fatehpur district to Vrindavan Dham Mathura, overturned on the highway near Janetpur village of Auraiya district on Tuesday. The injured people have been admitted to a district hospital.Upon receiving information about the accident, senior police officers, including the circle officer (CO) and the SDM, reached the scene and supervised the rescue operation. According to the passengers on the bus, the private travel bus had departed for Mathura and Vrindavan from Khaga in Fatehpur district at 10pm on Monday, carrying 60 passengers. "Around 4am on Tuesday, when the bus reached in front of Janetpur village in Auraiya district, the driver lost control, and the bus overturned on the road. Shakuntala (50), the wife of Kripal Singh and a resident of Misa, police station Thariyaon Fatehpur, who was traveling on the bus, died on the spot," they said, adding, "Simultaneously, 20 other passengers were severely injured."Upon receiving information about the accident, the Kotwali police arrived at the scene and initiated a rescue operation to extricate the trapped individuals from the bus. The bus was subsequently removed from the road with the assistance of a crane.CO Mahendra Pratap Singh and SDM Akhilesh Kumar visited the hospital to inquire about the well-being of the injured individuals who were being attended to by the doctors.The CO said, "The body of the deceased woman has been sent for postmortem, while the injured are undergoing treatment at the district hospital. The accident seems to have been caused by the driver apparently dozing off."

Title: 6-yr-old, dadâ€™s friend die as car hits bike
content:
Hyderabad: Two persons, including a six year-year-old boy, died in a road accident after their bike was hit by a car at Pahadishareef on Sunday night.Police said Janamjit Sahu, 6, and his fatherâ€™s friend Satyajit Mahapatra, 31, were killed in the accident.They were coming back from a movie theatre when the accident took place. Sridhar Reddy, who was at the wheel, hit the bike driven by Satyajit. Both Sahu and Satyajit fell down and suffered serious injuries.Both were shifted to hospital where they died while undergoing treatment. Pahadishareef police registered a case under section 304-A (negligence causing death) of IPC. Sahu was a class I student and Satyajit was working in a private company. Since Sahuâ€™s father was occupied with some personal work, he went with Satyajit to watch the movie.

Title: Two women on way to work killed in accident
content: Kochi: Two women who were on their way to work met with a tragic end on Monday morning as a pickup truck rammed into them at Athani near Nedumbassery.The deceased have been identified as Sheeba Satheesan, 49, of Thaivalappil House, Nedumabassery, and Mariyamma, 60, of Valathukaran House, Nedumbassery. They were working as canteen staff at a company about one-and-a-half kilometres away from their houses. They were trying to cross the road when the speeding truck rammed into them.Sheebaâ€™s husband Satheesan, a lottery vendor, was yet to recover from the shock of the accident. â€œShe usually goes to work early in the morning and likewise she had left home on Monday. She and Mariyamma used to go to work together. I came to know about the tragedy from a relative. Our daughter is carrying. My son is working as a security staff at Nedumbassery,â€ said Satheesan, adding that the funeral was held on Monday evening.Nedumbassery police registered an FIR under Sections 304 A (causing death by negligence and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC against the driver of the vehicle. According to police, the incident happened around 7am on Monday.Police said that the truck driver was driving the vehicle in a rash and negligent way. The vehicle was going towards Ernakulam from Angamaly side.Fire Force officials said they received an alert about the accident soon after the crash. â€œOne among the women was rushed to a hospital by passersby while the other woman was trapped under the truck. We removed the vehicle and took the woman to the hospital,â€ said a fire official. Officials said that both of them seemed to have died on the spot.Police took the truck driver, Velu of Tamil Nadu, and the truck, a Tamil Nadu registration vehicle, into custody. Police said that the truck was speeding and that they were probing if the driver had dozed off leading to the accident. Bodies of the victim were handed over to the kin after an autopsy on Monday. Fatal road accidents continue to remain a major cause of concern in the district. In 2022, 351 persons were killed and 3,142 persons sustained grievous injuries in accidents in rural Ernakulam. A total of 108 people were killed and 1,662 persons sustained injuries in accidents that happened in rural Ernakulam till April 30 this year.

Title: Police vehicle kills 57-yr-old woman on Kamaraj Salai
content:

She was apparently trying to cross the road along Kamaraj Salai when the accident happened. According to police, the woman suddenly ran across, and the police tempo traveller (SB-CID) hit her. Mary was severely injured and was rushed to the government hospital where she was declared dead. She had suffered severe head injuries. The Anna Square Traffic Investigation Wing (TIW) police have registered a case and are investigating. The constable who was driving the vehicle, Sathyamurthy, was later arrested.

Title: Ambulance rams bike near Annur, 1 killed
content:
Coimbatore: A 21-year-old youth died after an ambulance rammed his bike on the Coimbatore-Annur Road on Sunday and sped away without stopping to help.The deceased was identified as Sathish, of Salaipalayam near Annur. The accident occurred when Satish and his friends, Kanagaraj, 21 and Santhosh, 21, were on their way to watch a movie at a theatre on a bike. Title: Woman falls off scooter, crushed by truck
content: GHAZIABAD: A 40-year-old woman riding pillion fell off a scooter and was crushed to death under the wheels of a truck in Shalimar Garden on Monday morning.Delhi resident Mahima Ahmad was on her way to her uncleâ€™s home in Ghaziabad when the accident occurred on Bhopura road near Bhagwan Das Public School around 11.30am. She was on a scooter with her brother-in-law and his eight-year-old son.Police said Mahimaâ€™s brother-in-law, who was driving the scooter, tried to overtake the truck from the left. When he slammed the brakes suddenly, Mahima lost her balance, slipped from the seat and came under the rear wheels of the truck, which was loaded with LPG cylinders. Ramanand Kushwaha, ADCP (traffic), said the driver of the truck and his helper fled after the accident, leaving the vehicle behind. â€œPreliminary investigation revealed the truck was headed for Bhopura. The scooter tried to overtake the truck from the left when the two-wheelerâ€™s driver had to slam the brakes for lack of space. The woman was badly crushed under the wheels,â€ he added.. .AdvertisementRavi Shankar Pandey, the SHO of Shalimar Garden police station, said a few passerby informed them about the accident. â€œBy the time a police team reached the spot, she had died. We are looking for the truck driver and helper,â€ he added.Following a complaint by the brother-in-law, an FIR was registered against unknown persons under sections 304A (death due to negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC. â€œWe have formed two teams â€” one to trace the accused and another to scan CCTV footage of nearby areas to get leads. We are also checking details of the vehicle from its registration number,â€ Pandey told TOI.On August 9, the relative of an IPS officer was killed in an accident on NH-9. Akshit Mishra of Indirapuram was riding a bike when he crashed into a truck.According to police data, Ghaziabad has reported 386 accidents till May 30 this year. There have been 124 fatalities in these accidents.

Title: Three of a family die in road accident in Kurukshetra
content:
KURUKSHETRA: A man, his wife and their seven-year-old son were killed after being hit by a speeding dumper truck which was being driven on the wrong side of the road in Ladwa area of Kurukshetra district on Tuesday.The deceased were identified as Tony (31), his wife Deepika Rani (29), and Naksh (7) of Gundiana village in Yamunanagar district.The police have arrested the accused dumper driver Jatinder Kumar (24), who will be produced before the court on Thursday.On the complaint of Tonyâ€™s brother Rajesh Kumar, the police registered a case against the driver of the dumper under Section 279 (rash driving), 304-A (causing death by negligence), and 427 (mischief) of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) at Ladwa police station on Tuesday.. .AdvertisementA police said that the post-mortem examination of all the three deceased was conducted on Wednesday, and their bodies were handed over to their family members for last rites.

Title: Eight killed, 15 injured in bus accident in Nepal
content:
KATHMANDU: A passenger bus skidded off the main highway and plunged into a swollen river in Nepal's Bagmati province on Wednesday, killing at least eight people and leaving at least 15 others injured, according to a media report.The accident happened when the bus en route to the scenic city of Pokhara from Kathmandu veered off and fell into the Trishuli River at Chalise in the province's Dhading district, myRepublica.com reported."In the accident, at least eight people have been killed and 15 more injured. The number of those killed might go up. Rescue is ongoing," Deputy Superintendent of Police Santulal Prasad Jaiswar at the District Police Office, Dhading, was quoted as saying in the report.He said that the bus was partly submerged in the Trishuli River after the accident, but rescuers were able to pull out many of the passengers alive from the wreckage.The river had been swollen by continuous rainfall during the monsoon season.Highway accidents in Nepal, which are mostly covered by mountains, are mostly blamed on poorly maintained vehicles and roads.

Title: Woman dead, 20 injured afterbus with 60 pilgrims overturns
content: Kanpur: A woman passenger was reportedly killed while 20 others were injured after a private Volvo bus carrying as many as 60 pilgrims from Khaga town of Fatehpur district to Mathura and Vrindavan overturned on the highway near Janetpur village of Auraiya district on Tuesday. The injured have been admitted to the district hospital.Senior police officials including the Circle Officer (CO) City and the sub-divisional magistrate (SDM) reached the spot and supervised the rescue operation. According to the passengers, the bus had departed for Mathura and Vrindavan from Khaga in Fatehpur district at 10 pm on Monday, with 60 to 65 travellers.. .Advertisementâ€œAround 4 am on Tuesday, when the bus reached in front of Janetpur village of Auraiya district, the driver lost control, and the bus overturned on the road. Shakuntala (50), wife of Kripal Singh, resident of Misa, police station Thariyaon Fatehpur, who was traveling on the bus, died on the spot,â€ they said adding, â€œSimultaneously, 20 other passengers were seriously injured.â€The Kotwali police reached the spot after getting information and initiated a rescue operation to rescue trapped passengers from the bus. CO Mahendra Pratap Singh, SDM Akhilesh Kumar met all injured passengers in the hospital and said, â€œThe body of the deceased woman has been sent for postmortem, while the injured are undergoing treatment at the district hospital. The accident seems to have been caused by the driver apparently dozing off.â€

Title: Woman dies as stray cattle came before bike
content:
Bhopal: A 38-year-old woman was killed after her husband lost control of his bike allegedly due to stray cattle sitting in the middle of the road and rammed it into the railing on Hoshangabad Road in Misrod on Sunday. Investigation officer ASI Khet Singh said that Rekha Singh, a resident of Obedullaganj in Raisen, along with husband Jeet Singh went to visit their son in Sehore. Near Radhapuram Colony in Samardha in Misrod, Jeet, spotted cattle sitting on the road. He took a sudden turn and rammed the bike into the railing on the road. Rekha sustained severe injuries. They were rushed to a hospital in Mandideep from where Rekha was referred to Hamidia hospital where she died while undergoing treatment on Monday morning. A case was registered at Misrod police station. TNN

Title: 17 killed after under-construction railway bridge collapses in Mizoram
content: AIZAWL: At least 17 people were killed after an under-construction rail-road bridge collapsed near the proposed Sairang/Sihmui rail station, about 25 kilometre west of Aizawl, on Wednesday.According to reports, there were around 40 workers at the site.Mizoram chief minister Zoramthanga took to X (formerly Twitter) and informed about the tragedy."Deeply saddened and affected by this tragedy. I extend my deepest condolences to all the bereaved families and wishing a speedy recovery to the injured. Sending gratitude to the people who have come out in large numbers to help with rescue operations," Zoramthanga posted on X.PM Modi announces ex-gratia"Pained by the bridge mishap in Mizoram. Condolences to those who have lost their loved ones. May the injured recover soon. Rescue operations are underway and all possible assistance is being given to those affected," PM Modi posted on X.PM also announced an ex-gratia of Rs 2 lakh from PMNRF to the next of kin of each deceased and Rs 50,000 to the injured.Watch Mizoram: Railway under-construction bridge collapses, at least 17 dead

Title: Journo killed in road accident in Tamil Nadu's Tirunelveli
content: MADURAI: A cameraman with a news channel died in a road accident near Nanguneri in Tirunelveli district in the wee hours of Thursday. The deceased, identified as Shankar (32), along with a reporter and two cameramen went to Thiruvananthapuram on Wednesday to interview ISRO scientist Nambi Narayanan in view of Chandrayaan 3 mission.The accident took place when they were returning after the press meet. Shankar was driving the car. When the vehicle approached a private mill near Nanguneri, he lost control over it and it rammed into the median and turned upside down. Shankar was killed on the spot due to severe head injury while others who suffered injuries were rushed to Tirunelveli Government Medical College Hospital.. .AdvertisementThe injured include Nagarajan, Vallinayagam and Narayanan. Their condition is said to be stable and Tirunelveli district collector KP Karthikeyan met them and enquired about their health.Nanguneri police recovered Shankar's body and sent it for postmortem. Shankar is survived by wife and a son.

Title: Youth killed, four hurt in Ambikapur
content:
Raipur: One youth was killed and four were injured in a road accident in Ambikapur late on Tuesday.The three injured were returning from chief minister Bhupesh Baghelâ€™s youth dialogue programme in Amikapur, while the other two after meeting their friend on their motorbike when the vehicles collided head-on on the main road of Ambikapur-Surajpur road in Kalighat at around 8PM.They reportedly didnâ€™t receive any immediate help on the deserted road and the golden hour wasnâ€™t utilised. Later the youths were shifted to hospital in ambulance where one was declared dead. The deceased was identified as Nikhil Bhagat, 20, a college student. Those injured are between the age of 18 and 20 years and are college students.Chief minister Bhupesh Baghel has extended condolence on the demise and has announced a compensation amount of Rs 4 lakh each for the deceased and two severely injured youths undergoing treatment in the ICU. Other two will be provided with Rs 1 lakh each and â€œbest possible treatmentâ€ at Surguja district hospital.

Title: 5-yr-old killed, parents injured after truck hits their bike on NH
content: JAIPUR: A five-year-old boy, who was travelling with his parents on a bike, died in an accident on the Jaipur-Delhi national highway near Shahpura on Wednesday morning."The victim identified as Ayush Kumawat (5) was travelling with his father Jitendra, and mother Maya. The couple have sustained injuries in the accident," said an official.The Shahpura police reported that the accident occurred when an unidentified vehicle, suspected to be a multi-axle truck, ran over the family's bike.Police said that the family was travelling from Khejroli near Jaipur and heading to Bhabroo village to meet Maya's family. Local witnesses informed the police that a truck ran over the bike, causing severe injuries to the boy. He was immediately taken to a hospital with his family, but he succumbed to his injuries while receiving treatment.Following the accident, the truck driver fled the scene with the vehicle. The Shahpura police then reached the spot and took the family to the hospital where Ayush's body was shifted to the mortuary hospital while the injured couple was undergoing treatment at the same hospital.Cops stated that Maya sustained a leg injury, while Jitendra suffered injuries to his face. Both of them are currently receiving treatment, and their conditions are reported to be normal. After the road accident, there was a traffic jam of about 2 kilometres on the Jaipur-Delhi highway, which the police managed to clear after approximately 30 minutesPolice have filed a case against the vehicle driver and are currently examining CCTV footage on the highway to identify the vehicle and apprehend the driver responsible for the reckless driving.

Title: 40-yr-old killed after bike hit by ambulance
content:
Hyderabad: A 40-year-old man was killed when his bike was hit by a private ambulance at Thukkuguda in the cityâ€™s outskirts on Wednesday night. The deceased, Banoth Ravindar, was a project manager at a private company and hailed from Prashanth Nagar area of LB Nagar. The accident happened at 10 pm on Wednesday when Ravindar was travelling from Thukkuguda to Adibhatla on the ORR service road. At Viceroy Hotel junction, an ambulance travelling from Hyderabad towards Kalwakurthy government hospital on Srisailam highway, hit the bike crossing the road.â€œRavindar suffered severe injuries in the accident and died on the spot,â€ Pahadishareef SO K Madhusudhan said. After the incident, police alerted the family members of the victim using his cell phone contacts. Based on the complaint of Ravindarâ€™s brother-in-law, a case was registered against the ambulance driver under section 304-a of the IPC. The body was handed over to the family members of the victim on Thursday after the postmortem examinaton.

Title: 42-yr-old dies as truck ploughs into auto
content: GHAZIABAD: A pickup truck driver crashed into an auto, killing a 42-year-old passenger, near the Kalka Garhi crossing on Tuesday. The deceasedâ€™s parents are fighting for their lives in the hospital, and the auto driver has also received injuries. Police have filed an FIR and are investigating the case.Police identified the deceased as Lalit Sharma, a Vivekananda Nagar resident employed with a private company in Noida, and the injured as his parents, Madan Mohan Sharma (78) and Kiran (68), and auto driver Ravi.The Sharma family was on their way home from the Ghaziabad railway station when a white Mahendra pickup hit the auto and knocked Kiran out. She landed on the footpath and hit her head.. .AdvertisementSwati, the deceased's elder sister, lodged a complaint against the truck driver with the Sihani Gate police station. She said on August 20, her daughter had turned 5, and her brother and parents had come to her house in Farrukhabad to bless the baby.â€œOn August 22, their train reached Ghaziabad railway station at 5 am and took an auto to go home. The accident happened at 5.20am. The truck driver did not stop and fled away. The CCTV cameras captured the whole thing,â€ she said.â€œMMG Hospital admitted my parents and brother, but Lalit died of trauma injuries. My parents are now in Yashoda Hospital for specialised treatment, but their condition is critical. My brother's loss is a huge blow for us; he supported my parents,â€ she said.Ravi Kumar, ACP of Nandgram, said police filed the FIR against the unidentified truck driver under IPC sections 279 (rash driving), 304A (causing death by negligence) and 338 (causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others). â€œA team is looking for the accused,â€ he said.Car falls off Gzb bridgeA man drove his car into the railing of the Kavi Nagar railway overbridge on Thursday, following which the car lunged down and landed between the Sihani Gate police station and the Nandgram ACPâ€™s office.Police said that the man survived as the airbag opened on time. The ACP identified the injured person as Nitin Kumar, a resident of Kavi Nagar. â€œInitial investigation revealed he was going to his office in Navyug market, and another car hit his Eco sports car, making him lose control. We have not received a complaint yet. The victim is stable,â€ he said.

Title: Nine killed after jeep plunges into gorge in Kerala's Wayanad
content: KOZHIKODE: At least nine plantation workers were killed and three persons injured when the jeep in which they were travelling fell into a deep gorge at Kannothmala near Thalappuzha in Thavinjal panchayat in Kerala's Wayanad district on Friday afternoon.All the deceased are women workers who were returning home after picking tea leaves in a tea plantation. They were residents of Makkimala.The jeep was totally damaged in the accident which happened at around 3.45 pm.Elsy Joy, president of Thavinjal panchayat, said that the jeep fell into the gorge around 30 metres deep while it was negotiating a curve down a steep slope in the road. She said that the workers were returning from a tea plantation in the Venmani area.After the accident, it was local residents who started rescue operations though they had difficulty in the rescue attempts due to the steep nature of the gorge.Mananthavady government medical college authorities said that the nine persons were brought dead.Wayanad district panchayat president Shamsad Marakkar said that all the nine deceased were women plantation workers who had gone to pluck tea leaves and they used to rely on jeeps to go and return to work.State forest minister A K Saseendran has started off to Wayanad to oversee the arrangements including medical treatment for the injured.

Title: Auto driver dies after being hit by vehicle, suspect on run
content:
NEW DELHI: An unidentified vehicle hit an autorickshaw on Janakpuri flyover on Wednesday night killing the latter's driver. There were no passengers in the auto at the time of accident, police said.Deputy commissioner of police (Outer) Harender Singh said after receiving a PCR call about the accident around 4 am, police found the damaged autorickshaw and a pool of blood on the spot. "Around the same time, we received information from a hospital about an accident victim, who was declared dead on arrival." the officer added.According to police, victim Bhupinder Kumar Arora, a resident of Shahdara, was brought to the hospital by two persons in a critical condition. His body was transferred to mortuary at Sanjay Gandhi Memorial Hospital for post-mortem and later handed over to the deceased's family members, police added.During inspection, a team of experts found the auto having significant impact to its front left side and a shattered windshield, with broken glass scattered on the road. "We have obtained CCTV footage along the flyover to ascertain the sequence of events leading to the crash and identify the offending vehicle," said an official.. .Advertisement

Title: 2 killed in separate accidents
content:
Chennai: Two people died in different road accidents in Chengalpet district on Thursday and Friday.In Maraimalai Nagar, a 20-year-old man driving back home after work died after he came under the wheels of a government bus. The deceased M Dhayanidhi, 20, and his friend A Vignesh, 19, worked as construction labourers employed at a site in Guduvanchery. On Friday night, they were returning home. While Vignesh was driving, Dhayanidhi was on the pillion. When the two were near Maraimalai Nagar municipal office, a truck brushed against the two-wheeler, and then ran over Dhayanidhi. Vignesh escaped with injuries. The truck driver was booked.In Guduvanchery, a 32-year-old woman driving to work was fatally knocked down by a van early on Friday. Radhika, 32, from Otteri, worked as a domestic maid in a private company in Perungudi. On Friday morning, she was driving her scooter to work when a van carrying goods knocked her down.

Title: 9 women plantation workers killed as jeep falls into gorge in Kerala's Wayanad
content: KOZHIKODE: Nine women plantation workers were killed and five others injured when the jeep in which they were travelling fell into a deep gorge at Kannothumala near Thalappuzha in Thavinjal panchayat of Wayanad around 3.45pm on Friday. The workers were returning home after plucking tea leaves at a private tea plantation at Kattimoola. They were all residents of the Aram Number area of Makkimala. The jeep was totally damaged in the accident.The deceased were identified as Rani (57), Santha (55), Chinnamma (60), Leela (60), Shaja (47), Rabia (62), Karthiyayini (65), Shobana (55) and Chitra (32). Of these, Chitra is the daughter of Santha. The jeep was carrying 13 women workers and the driver.Thavinjal panchayat president Elsy Joy said the jeep fell into the gorge around 30 metres deep while it was negotiating a curve down a steep slope on the road.. .AdvertisementWayanad district police chief Padam Singh said as per the jeep driver's statement, he had tried to apply the brakes while negotiating the curve but he could not stop the vehicle. "There could be some brake failure, but it has not been confirmed. We will be doing a detailed vehicle inspection tomorrow, after which only the real reason can be ascertained," he told TOI.District collector told to submit reportT halapuzha police have begun an investigation. They are also examining if overloading of the jeep had in any way contributed to the accident. The road did not have proper side crash barriers. Local people started the rescue operations soon after the accident though they had difficulty in accessing the jeep wreck. Elsy Joy said they used ropes to get down to the gorge and brought the deceased and injured persons to the road above. Four persons -- Umadevi (40), Jayanthi (45), Latha (38), Mohana Sundari and jeep driver Mani (44) -- have been admitted to Mananthavady government medical college hospital. A seriously injured woman, Latha (38), was shifted to Kozhikode government medical college hospital. The autopsies will be held on Saturday and the bodies will be kept for public homage at Makkimala Govt LP School at 12 noon. Wayanad district panchayat president Shamsad Marakkar said the women plantation workers relied on jeeps to go and return to work. Forest minister A K Saseendran has reached Wayanad to oversee the arrangements, including medical treatment for the injured, as directed by chief minister Pinarayi Vijayan. He said district collector Renuraj had been asked to submit a report on the accident. The collector has also been directed to provide Rs 10,000 each to the bereaved families for funeral expenses. Saseendran said the government would provide ex-gratia payment to victimsâ€™ families later. The government will also ensure proper medical treatment for the injured, he said. Congress leader and Wayanad MP Rahul Gandhi said he was deeply saddened by the accident and he had spoken to the district collector and sought speedy intervention.

Title: 10 killed in four road accidents
content:
Guwahati/Silchar: As many as 10 people were killed in four separate road accidents across the state on Saturday.A mother-son duo who were travelling on a two-wheeler lost their lives on the spot after colliding with a vehicle recovery crane in Changsari under Kamrup district. The deceased have been identified as Joytan Bewa, aged about 55 years, and Jasmat Ali, 37. Officer-in- charge of the Changsari police station Nirakshan Ramsiyari told TOI, â€œBoth the victims were heading towards Mangaldai from Guwahati. A bus and a vehicle recovery crane were coming from the same direction. When the bus stopped to pick up passengers, the biker at that moment lost control and collided with the crane. They died on the spot.â€Ramsiyari added that the bodies of the deceased were sent to Gauhati Medical College and Hospital for post-mortem examination and later handed over to the family members.. .AdvertisementIn another accident, four women travelling on an e-rickshaw were killed and several others injured near Alamganj at Gauripur. A speeding pick-up van reportedly collided head on with the e-rickshaw. Meanwhile, a vegetable seller, Nilakanta Saikia was hit by a speeding dumper at Ghilamara in Lakhimpur district on Saturday.Also, two people travelling on a bike died after they collided with a dumper in Dima Hasao on Saturday. The deceased have been identified as Klorson Hanse (17) and Pabitra Bay (39).

Title: 4 members of AP family killed in Saudi road accident
content:
Vijayawada: Four members of a non-resident Indian family from Andhra Pradesh died in a road accident near Riyadh in Saudi Arabia on Friday. Dandu Ghouse Basha (35), a native of Madanapalle in Annamayya district, who was working in Kuwait, went to Saudi Arabia along with his wife and two children. The family was returning to their home in Kuwait when the accident occurred. The car they were travelling in collided with a truck and was gutted in a fire.M Venkata Ramana Reddy, a senior official in Andhra Pradesh Non-Resident Telugu Society (APNRTS), told TOI that Ghouse Bashaâ€™s family migrated to Bengaluru when he was a child. After completing high school in Bengaluru, Basha pursued his bachelorâ€™s degree in electronics and communications engineering at Anna university in Chennai in 2009 and started working at a private company as a database administrator in Pune.Later, he completed his masterâ€™s in project management from Anglia Ruskin university in London in 2019 and left for Kuwait where he worked with multiple companies.. .AdvertisementAround 10 days ago, Basha along with his wife Tabarak Sarwar (31) and two sons, Ehan (4) and Dameel (2), went to Mecca in Saudi Arabia in a recently purchased car.

Title: 4 of Andhra Pradesh family killed in road crash in Saudi Arabia
content: VIJAYAWADA: Four members of a non-resident Indian family from Andhra Pradesh died in a road accident near Riyadh in Saudi Arabia on Friday. Dandu Ghouse Basha (35), a native of Madanapalle in Annamayya district, who was working in Kuwait, went to Saudi Arabia along with his wife and two children. The family was returning to their home in Kuwait when the accident occurred. The car they were travelling in collided with a truck and was gutted in a fire.M Venkata Ramana Reddy, a senior official in Andhra Pradesh Non-Resident Telugu Society (APNRTS), told TOI that Ghouse Basha's family migrated to Bengaluru when he was a child. After completing high school in Bengaluru, Basha pursued his bachelor's degree in electronics and communications engineering at Anna university in Chennai in 2009 and started working at a private company as a database administrator in Pune.Later, he completed his master's in project management from Anglia Ruskin university in London in 2019 and left for Kuwait where he worked with multiple companies.. .AdvertisementAround 10 days ago, Basha along with his wife Tabarak Sarwar (31) and two sons, Ehan (4) and Dameel (2), went to Mecca in Saudi Arabia in a recently purchased car.Family was returning to Kuwait after visiting Madina when tragedy occurredAfter performing Umrah and visiting Madina, the family was returning to their home in Kuwait when the tragedy occurred. The car was gutted in the mishap on the Hafnaâ€“Tuwakh road, nearly 120 kilometres away from Riyadh. The bodies were shifted to Rumah general hospital and legal formalities are yet to be completed. The parents of Ghouse Basha were admitted to a hospital in Bengaluru after hearing about the tragedy. Ramana Reddy also said it was difficult to identify the victims since most of their belongings, including travel documents, were burnt when the car caught fire. APNRTS officials are coordinating with the Indian embassy in Riyadh to repatriate the mortal remains.

Title: Kurubur villagers stage protest after sr citizen dies in accident
content: Mysuru: Farmers and villagers of Kurubur village staged a flash protest against authorities after a 65-year-old pillion rider died in a road accident, while another sustained serious injuries on T Narasipur-Kollegal Road near Kurubur village, on Saturday.This is the second fatal accident after ten tourists from Ballari were killed in a road accident, on the same stretch within three months. According to the police, the deceased has been identified as KG Nagaraju of Kurubur village. The injured KN Rajesh is undergoing treatment at JSS Hospital in Mysuru.An speeding motorbike rider coming from the opposite direction hit the two-wheeler of Rajesh, who was returning from an agriculture field. Nagarajappa died on the spot, police said. Following the mishap, villagers of Kurubur and members of Raita Sangha held a protest by blocking the highway, demanding action against the authorities for not removing the bushes that were grown on both sides of the highway and responsible for the death as the road is not clearly visible for bike riders.. .AdvertisementThey also demanded the government to provide compensation.They condemned the state government for not taking any measures to prevent the mishaps on the stretch as people were killed near Kurubur village in May.Karnataka State Sugarcane Growers Association president Kurubur Shantahkumar urged the police to book authorities concerned for the mishaps. T Narasipur Police have registered a case and an investigation is underway.

Title: Student dies as car hits roadside tree
content:
VISAKHAPATNAM: An engineering student was killed and his two friends suffered injuries when their car hit a roadside tree on beach road under Bheemili police station limits in city. The deceased was P Prakyat (20), a third-year engineering student of Gitam university. Police said rash and negligent driving was the reason for the accident.

Title: Pbâ€™s 1cr each to kin of soldiers killed in Leh
content:
Chandigarh: Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann on Sunday handed over Rs 1-crore cheque as financial assistance to the distressed family members of each soldier who lost their lives on August 19 in a road accident at Leh in Ladakh.Mann visited the houses of Ramesh Lal, of Sarsiri village in Faridkot district, and Taran Deep Singh from Bassi Pathana and offered his condolences to the bereaved family members. He said the entire country was indebted to these soldiers who had sacrificed their lives for the sake of the country.The chief minister also announced a job for Taran Deepâ€™s sister after due verification of certificates and construction of a stadium, equipped with synthetic track and other ultramodern facilities, on the late soldierâ€™s name so that the youngsters could be motivated to join the armed forces.At Sarsiri village, Mann also announced a job for Ramesh Lalâ€™s wife and construction of a stadium in the village. . .AdvertisementHe also said the dispensary in the village would be revamped and Panjgrain to Nangal road would be named after the late soldier.Mann said the financial assistance to martyrsâ€™ families was in consonance with the commitment of the state government to ensure the wellbeing of the martyred soldiersâ€™ families.

Title: Villagers protest after car knocks down elderly man in Raigarh
content: Raipur: A speeding SUV knocked down an elderly man in the village Padigaon of Raigarh, killing the old man on the spot late on Saturday night. Angry villagers staged a chakka jam on the spot.According to the police, Faganu Chauhan, 60, a resident of village Padigaon, was heading towards his home from a near by shop on his bicycle at around 8:30 pm on Saturday evening. He had just come a short distance when a SUV coming from Raigarh side hit the old man while driving in a rash and negligent manner. The old man was seriously injured in the accident and died on the spot.Angry people caught two youths driving SUV and thrashed them and also vandalized the vehicle. People informed the police about the incident. When the police reached the spot, the villagers handed over both the accused to them.Angry villagers also staged a protest, due to which the road to Sariya, Barmakela, Sarangarh was completely blocked. As soon as the information about the incident was received, the administrative officials also reached the spot and explained to the people. . .AdvertisementThe villagers demanded proper compensation to the families of the victims. After which the officials gave an immediate assistance amount of Rs 25 thousand to the family members of the victims. After this, the agitated villagers called off the chakkajam. Late night traffic could start smoothly.While, an FIR was registered against the driver for negligence in driving, investigation is still on, added the police.

Title: Woman killed in Sirsi Road accident
content:
Jaipur: A 30-year-old woman died while four others are currently under treatment following an accident in which a pickup vehicle overturned on Sirsi Road on Monday. ACP (Bagru) Anil Kumar Sharma said the victim was idenitfied as Meera Devi.Police said that the accident occurred in the afternoon when a pickup vehicle was transporting 15 women from Bagru to a farmland. The driver swerved to avoid a collision with a motorcycle, but the vehicle overturned. Police said the women were promptly taken to hospital, where 10 of them were discharged after receiving initial medical assistance.Police said that the driver also suffered injuries. â€œAccording to information, these women commuted to nearby farmlands for work regularly. On this particular day, as on any other day, they were en route to the farm in a pickup when the driver, in an attempt to avoid colliding with a motorcycle, lost control, leading to the vehicle overturning,â€ said an officer.. .Advertisement

Title: 3 killed as car hits group returning from cultural event in Jharkhand's Palamu
content: MEDININAGAR: At least three people were killed and five others suffered serious injuries when speeding car hit them in Palamu district of Jharkhand on Monday night. The incident took place at around 9.30pm near Baraon village under Chainpur police station limits in Palamu district.The deceased included a 12-year-old boy and two adults, said the police on Tuesday. The five injured people are undergoing treatment at MMCH in Daltonganj.The officer in charge of Chainpur police station, Rupesh Kumar Dubey, said, "The deceased were identified as Rohit Chourasia (12), Udal Chourasia (40), and Madhu Mahto (45). Rohit and Udal hailed from Nagwa village. They came from the same Chourasia clan but were not family members." Madhu belonged to Danda village under Garhwa police station. . .AdvertisementThe postmortem of the deceased was conducted at MMCH Daltonganj on Tuesday.Dubey said, "A new luxury car coming from Garhwa to Daltonganj first hit a biker. The biker fell, and the two-wheeler was badly damaged. Thereafter, he lost control of the car and further trampled the pedestrians."The police have seized the car, but its driver managed to escape. The police are trying to identify the owner of the car.Chief minister Hemant Soren has expressed his condolences over the loss of lives.

Title: Call centre employee & sr citizen killed in separate road mishaps
content:
THANE: A call centre employee and an elderly lady died in separate road accidents within a span of 12 hours in Thane city, police informed on Monday.In the first incident, an unidentified elderly woman was mowed down by a speeding car at Gawandbaug on Sunday. Locals alleged the stretch was used by trainee motorists as it is rarely used by other vehicle drivers but the same is posing risk for pedestrians.In another incident, a 25 year old biker identified as Nilesh Rajput was killed on the spot after his bike hit a road divider on Ghodbunder road. â€œThe victim was on his way to Mahape and was probably speeding when he lost control and crashed into the divider. We have booked the deceased under relevant sections of the IPC and MVA,â€ informed a police official.

Title: Motorist, biker die in two road accidents
content: Nashik: Two people were killed in separate road accidents in the Ambad and Saptur areas of the city on Sunday and Monday.In the first incident, 41-year-old Rajkumar Tulsiprasad Singh was killed after the bike he was riding was knocked down by a truck on the Ambad-Link Road area. The Ambad police said that the accident took place at around 8am near the Ambad MIDC area. The truck knocked Singhâ€™s bike from behind. He suffered serious injuries in the accident and succumbed to them.. .AdvertisementOn Sunday, 46-year-old Mayur Kale was killed in an accident on the Trimbak Road, which falls under the jurisdiction of the Satpur police station. The deceased was a resident of Cidco and was working in the Satpur divisional office of NMC. At around 10 pm, he was at Trimbak Road on his way to his residence when his car rammed against the road divider.Upon receiving information, police officials reached the spot and immediately shifted him to the Nashik civil hospital, where he died during the course of treatment.The Satpur and Ambad police are in the process of registering FIRs, respectively, concerning the fatal accidents.Road accidents are a major concern in Nashik. A large number of people die or suffer serious injuries in road accidents. According to official data, 170 people die in road accidents every year in the Nashik district.

Title: 3 govt staff among six killed as SUV, KSRTC bus collide in Karnataka's Ramanagara
content: BENGALURU: Six persons, including three Vidhana Soudha employees, were killed when their SUV collided with a KSRTC bus near Sathanur, Ramanagara district, on Monday evening. Over 10 persons travelling in the KSRTC bus have sustained injuries. The bus driver has been hospitalised. The deceased were identified as Jyothirlingappa aka Jyothirlinga Kumar, Shantha Kumar and Nagesh, all employees of Legislators' Home, Govind, a garment store owner in Alankar Plaza near Majestic, and Puttaraju, an auto driver.  According to police sources, the accident occurred between 4.30pm and 4.45pm near Kemmale Cross on Sathanur Road, about 80km from Bengaluru. The injured were rushed to nearby hospitals and treated as outpatients.Citing preliminary investigation, police said all six persons travelling in the SUV had visited Male Mahadeshwara hills and were returning home. They are residents of Chandapura and surrounding areas on the outskirts of Bengaluru. Jyothirlinga was driving the SUV.The bus was heading towards Halaguru from Kanakapura. The bus and SUV collided head-on. The front portions of the SUV were completely crushed, while that of the bus was damaged due to the impact. The SUV's mangled metal had to be disentangled from the bus with the help of an earthmover. Sathanur police have registered a case. A senior police officer said they have got the ID cards of the three persons working in LH. The faces of three of the dead were disfigured. Family members rushed to the mortuary and identified their respective family members. Hundreds of people gathered at the spot helped police shift the bodies and injured persons to nearby hospitals.The bodies of the deceased persons were shifted to Dr Chandramma Dayanand Sagar Institute of Medical Education and Research near Harohalli. The postmortem procedures are still on.The senior police officer said both bus and SUV were overspeeding. "When we reached the spot, the bus was found in the middle of the road blocking the way of the opposite direction. The SUV was also found in the middle of the road. After a thorough investigation we will be able to say who was at fault."A KSRTC release said the bus from the Ramanagara depot was heading towards Halaguru after coming from Kanakpura. Near Kemmale Gate, the driver noticed a four-wheeler haphazardly coming from the opposite side and moved the vehicle from the left side road to almost middle of the road when the four-wheeler rammed the bus, it said.

Title: 7 of family killed as SUV rams into truck in Bihar's Rohtas
content:
By: Kshitiz SinghPATNA: As many as seven members of a family were killed after their speeding SUV rammed into a stationary container truck in Biharâ€™s Rohtas district on Wednesday. According to the police, the accident took place at Shivasagar on NH-2 near Pakhnari Petrol Pump in the district. The SUV driver lost control over the vehicle and rammed into the truck.. .AdvertisementSix people died on the spot while one succumbed to injuries during treatment at the Sadar hospital. The deceased were identified as Arvind Kumar Sharma (45), his wife Rani Premlata Priyadarshi (42), his son Aditya (12) and daughter Riya. Arvindâ€™s niece Tara (22) and Chandni (20) also died. Rajmati Devi (60), mother-in-law of Arvind also died in the accident. The family was native of Kaimur district. Two more people were admitted to the hospital in serious condition. All were going from Rohtas towards Kaimur.

Title: 7 killed in road accident in Bihar's Rohtas district
content:
SASARAM: Seven people died and five others were injured after an SUV rammed into a trailer truck parked on the roadside in Bihar's Rohtas district in the early hours of Wednesday, police said.The deceased include six members of a family.The accident occurred on National Highway-2 in Shivsagar area of Sasaram town at around 4 am, they said.According to the police, apart from the driver, there were 11 people in the SUV. The driver lost control of the vehicle due to overspeeding.. .AdvertisementChief minister Nitish Kumar expressed deep sorrow over the loss of lives, and directed the officials concerned to ensure proper treatment of the injured, a statement issued by the CMO said. The injured persons have been admitted to a nearby hospital, a police officer said. "The bodies of the deceased have been sent for post-mortem examination. Police have taken possession of the two vehicles involved in the accident," said Rakesh Gosai, the Station House Officer (SHO) of Shivsagar.

Title: 1 killed, 5 injured as car falls into ditch in Kerala
content:
THIRUVANANTHAPURAM: A youth was killed and five others suffered injuries when the car they were traveling in fell into a ditch dug up as part of the construction of the Attingal bypass on Tuesday night.The police said that the accident occurred at around 11.30pm on Tuesday night. They were traveling from Kadakkavoor to Alamcode when the car fell into the ditch. The construction works of a bridge were under way at Palamkonam near Kattadimukku where the accident happened.Panchayat representatives said that the place had warning boards and the driver may have missed it at night which caused the accident.The deceased was identified as Dominique, 22, a resident of Paripally. Five others who were injured are Flemins, Vishnu, Akshay, Brown, and Stephen.According to the police, the condition of one of the persons admitted to Medical college hospital is critical while the others are presently stable.

content:
