Title: 2-wheeler rider killed on AJC Bose flyover
content:
Kolkata: A 65-year-old scooterist, Madan Saha, a resident of Sarat Chatterjee Road in Shibpur, Howrah, was killed on AJC Bose Road flyover after he was hit by a mini truck on Tuesday morning. The accident, said police, might have been a result of a mistake committed by a third rider or motorist who managed to flee.The incident sparked a major traffic chaos for around 20-30 minutes with vehicles being diverted via Hospital Road and Red Road. Major traffic snarls were reported from Exide crossing and Rabindra Sadan. The situation was brought under control quickly by officers from South, East and Bhowanipore traffic guards.At least six flyover and bridge accidents have been reported in the past two months, most of them involving two-wheelers leading Lalbazar to propose a host of measures to avert such incidents.â€œThe riderâ€™s helmet had come off after the accident. When he fell, his head hit the ground first. If the helmet had been strapped in place, probably his life could have been saved,â€ said an officer from South Traffic Guard. Officers said some motorists had informed cops on Maa flyover that the mini truck was fleeing. â€œThat is how we nabbed the driver,â€ said an officer.. .Advertisement

Title: Man, four-year-old son die in road accident
content: LUCKNOW: A 30-year-old man and his four-year-old son were killed in a road accident in Gosaiganj on Tuesday afternoon.Police said the man identified as Vikas Rawat (30) of Loni Katra in Barabanki along with his wife Roshni (29), son Shani (4) and two-month-old daughter Piya had visited his in-laws house in Gosaiganj."Around 1 pm, he along with his son left for his native house, dropping his wife and their daughter at his in-laws house. On the way, he was hit by a pick-up van from behind. Due to heavy impact, Shani was thrown away and he banged against the road divider while Vikas and the bike stuck under the vehicle and Vikas was dragged to some 50 metre in this position," said the police.SHO, Mohanlalganj, Santosh Arya, said that Vikas suffered multiple fractures and head injuries. "It was found that he had turned into a pulp literally when he was taken out from under the chassis of the pick-up van," police said.Shani also suffered critical injuries. The errant driver sped off the scene abandoning the vehicle on the crime scene. A police team reached the scene and they rushed the victims to a hospital where both were pronounced dead.

Title: Truck driver killed after collision with mining vehicle
content:
Nagpur: A truck driver transporting public distribution system (PDS) grains was killed on the spot when his vehicle collided with an oncoming truck engaged for carrying ore from Surjagarh mines, on Gadchiroli-Chamorshi road on Monday. According to sources, the deceased has been identified as Mayur Alone. Police said Alone had supplied grains at a fair price shop at Alapalli and was heading toward Gadchiroli via Chamorshi. On the way, his vehicle rammed the mining truck which was heading toward Ashti from Chamorshi. Senior inspector of Ashti police station Kundan Gawde said, â€œAloneâ€™s vehicle had veered to the right into the path of oncoming vehicles. The mining truck driver tried to avoid collision by steering the vehicle to the left off the road. However, Aloneâ€™s truck rammed the mining truck in the middle.â€â€œDue to the impact, cabin of Aloneâ€™s truck got completely smashed and he was trapped between the steering and the seat. Alone sustained grievous injuries and in a bid to free himself he fell on the road and breathed his last,â€ said Gawde.. .AdvertisementAshti police have registered a case of accidental death. â€œWe are yet to register an offence against the mining truck driver as there is still no clarity about how the accident occurred,â€ said the PI. There have been a series of accidents involving mining trucks in Etapalli and Alapalli in the past triggering agitations.

Title: 3 killed after speeding bus hits van in Seoni
content:
Bhopal: Three people were killed when the van they were travelling in was hit by a speeding passenger bus in Seoni district in the wee hours of Wednesday. The incident took place on Lakhnadon-Ghansore road at around 4.15 am under Lakhnadon police station.The deceased â€” identified as Purshottam, 34, Satyendra Yadav, 34 and Narendra Yadav, 28 â€” were travelling in the van that was going to Ghansore from Lakhnadon when it was hit by the passenger bus near Sahasrana Banjari temple.â€œThe bus was going to Mandla from Bhopal. The accident was so intense that all the three persons, who were in the van, died on the spot. We rushed to the spot after getting the information and they were taken to hospital where doctors declared them brought dead,â€ Lakhnadon police station in-charge, NP Chowdhary told TOI.He added, â€œA case of unnatural death was registered and then driver of the bus was booked under relevant sections of IPC. Detailed investigations are going on. All three deceased were residents of Lakhnadon, apart from deceased, no one sustained any major injuries.â€

Title: SUV rams auto in Satna, 3 killed
content:
Bhopal: Three women were killed after the autorickshaw they were travelling in was hit by a SUV in Satna district. The incident took place at Rewa-Jabalpur road near Khamarkheda village under Amarpatan police station at around 4.30 pm on Tuesday.The deceased were identified as Mamta Patel, 48, Kali Patel, 60, and Bhilli Kol, 48, all residents of Khamarkheda village. All these women were travelling in the autorickshaw, besides around half a dozen including the people travelling in the SUV that had hit the auto-rickshaw also sustained minor injuries.â€œVillagers from Khamarkheda had gone for shopping in nearby market and were returning home when the accident took place. The vehicle hit the auto-rickshaw and lost balance. Of the three deceased one died on the spot while two others died during treatment. Besides a few people including those who were in the SUV had sustained severe injuries,â€ Amarpatan police station in-charge, Aditya Sen told TOI.He added, â€œPolice teams rushed to the spot after getting the information and injured were rushed to community health centre in Amarpatan. A few were also referred to district hospital.â€ â€œA case under relevant sections of IPC has been registered against the accused SUV driver and investigations are going on,â€ he said.

Title: Two on a joyride killed in road accident
content:
NEW DELHI: Two men, out on a joyride, were killed after a speeding truck ran into their bike on main Rohtak Road in west Delhi, police said on Thursday. Public handed over truck driver Pankaj Kumar (28), a native of Uttar Pradesh, to the police.According to Harender Singh, DCP (outer), after the accident around 1:04am near Multan Nagar red light, the victims Achin and Rohit (both 23), residents of Raghubir Nagar, were rushed to a nearby hospital where they were declared dead on arrival.After the driverâ€™s licence was found to be valid and his medical examination ruled out any signs of intoxication, a case under Section 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence) was registered, the DCP added.The deceased had reportedly borrowed the motorcycle from their neighbour for a drive following dinner, police said, adding Achin was employed as a salesman and Rohit worked as a delivery driver for an online company.. .Advertisement

Title: Six-year-old boy from AP killed in accident on Kalhatti Ghat Road
content: Udhagamandalam: A six-year-old boy from Andhra Pradesh died in a road accident on Thursday after the car in which he was travelling plunged into a 150 feet deep gorge on the Kalhatti Ghat Road near Ooty. Five others, including the boyâ€™s parents who were travelling in the car, were injured.Police said the accident took place when J Faizil Ahamed, 26, his wife F Asna, 26, and their children F Ahamed Ali, 6, and 7 month-old F Ibrahim Ali, Asnaâ€™s sister Sana Rahim, 27, and her husband Sabhash Ahamed, 28, were returning to Hyderabad after a two-day trip to Ooty. The Kalhatti Ghat Road, which has seen several accidents over the years, is prohibited for tourists driving downhill, given the risk involved in the 30km road with 36 hairpin bends. â€œHowever, people who use google maps take the Kalhatti Road via Ullathi village Road which joins the Kalhatti Road after the check post at Thalaikundha,â€ a police officer said. The officer added that the vehicle fell into a 150 feet deep gorge after crossing at least four hairpin bends. The public also assisted the fire and rescue personnel, and police in rescuing the passengers. â€œHowever, Ahamed Ali was found dead by the time he was rescued, while others had sustained severe injuries. Three of them were unconscious when rescued,â€ the officer said.The injured have been admitted to the ICU at the Ooty government medical college and hospital. The officer said there was not much detail yet as none of them are in a position to talk. â€œWe will register an FIR when at least one of the passengers can give a statement to police,â€ the officer said.

Title: Man dies as car rams into bike on airport road
content:
Mohali: A man was killed after a car hit his motorcycle on the airport road under Kharar police station on Thursday night. Kharar Sadar police station house officer inspector Jagjit Singh said the victim is yet to be identified. He said the Hyundai Verna driver fled, leaving his vehicle behind. The impact was so hard that the front portion of the car was badly damaged, while the bike became a mangled heap of steel. Police said further action would be taken once the victim was identified.

Title: Man killed in hit-run near Iskcon flyover
content: Ahmedabad: Barely a month after nine people lost their lives in the Iskcon flyover tragedy, a 47-year-old man died after being hit by an unknown vehicle driver between the flyover and Karnavati Club on SG Road late on Thursday.SG-2 division traffic police have registered a complaint of negligent driving causing death and rash driving against the unknown driver and began an investigation.The deceased was identified as Yatendra Singh, a native of Uttar Pradesh who was working for a security firm here. Singh was crossing the road around 8pm when he got run over, said inspector V B Desai of SG-2 division (traffic). The police authorities are unaware of the vehicle involved in the accident. Till reports last came in, the police did not have any CCTV footage of the accident, and the identity of the driver remains unknown.Ironically, this incident follows a fatal mishap on July 20, where nine individuals lost their lives, and 12 were injured after a Jaguar driven by Tathya Patel ploughed through a crowd on the Iskcon flyover.Despite this tragic history, it appears as if the city's traffic police have not heeded the lessons, and another accident has occurred near the flyover. Although the traffic police claim to have implemented speed guns to curb reckless driving, sources within the department suggest these measures have not yielded effective results.After the Iskcon flyover accident, the city police initiated a vehicle-checking drive. However, this effort also faced criticism after two traffic police officers and a TRB member were accused of extorting Rs 60,000 from a Bopal couple by threatening them with legal action.ACP S J Modi of A-division (traffic) was unavailable to comment on the issue.

Title: Four-year-old boyrun over by truckin hit-and-run case
content:
Pune: A four-year-old boy was run over by a truck while he was crossing a lane at Wadarwadi off Senapati Bapat Road on Wednesday evening. The truck driver fled the spot after the accident around around 4.30pm, an officer from the Chatushrungi police said. On Thursday, the police traced and impounded the truck, which was carrying construction material at the time of accident, and served a notice to its driver asking him to join the investigation as and when summoned by the investigating officer. The victim, Sarthak Birajdar, had arrived in Wadarwadi from Ghorpadi with his family to celebrate Rakshabandhan. His father Mallinath (31), a casual worker, later lodged a complaint, in which he stated that Sarthak was crossing the lane to buy ice cream when he was run over by the truck.â€œBirajdar family is a native of Lavhara in Osmanabad district,â€sub-inspector Pranil Chougule of Chatushrungi police said. . .Advertisementâ€œThey had come to Wadarwadi on a motorcycle. While entering their relativeâ€™s house, Sarthak asked for an ice cream. He stepped out on the road to get one from a shop across the lane for which he ran on to the road. The truck driver failed to spot the boy and thus Sathak came under the rear wheel of the truck,â€ Chougule told TOI.Recently, a bike pillion rider was killed after a heavy vehicle dashed his motorcycle in Bhosari.

Title: Pakistan: Seven people killed in accident in Sindh's Thatta
content: ISLAMABAD: Seven people died in an accident near Kenjhar Lake in Thatta district of Pakistan's Sindh on Saturday, Pakistan-based reported. The accident occurred when a van and a truck collided in Thatta.The accident occurred on the national highway between Thatta's Chalia area and Kenjhar Lake when a van-carrying fisherman collided with an oncoming truck. Four people died on the spot and one person died while being taken to hospital, reported. Meanwhile, a van driver and one other person succumbed to their wounds after the ambulance got stuck in traffic due to the ongoing protests over inflation and exorbitant electricity bills, according to a report. On August 29, one student was killed after a vehicle carrying students plunged into a ditch in Swat, Pakistan-based reported. The accident occurred in the Fazal Banda Matta area of Swat, resulting in the death of a student.. .AdvertisementAccording to the details, the accident occurred after a car carrying a student from the government college Matta rolled into a ditch. The students were travelling to Swat on a college outing, reported.Earlier, at least six people, including women, were killed in Mansehra after a van carrying passengers fell into a gully. Such accidents have become a regular affair in Pakistan due to poor road infrastructure, according to a report. Details indicated that the passenger van's tyres blew out, causing the driver to lose control of the vehicle, causing it to crash into the ravine. The passenger van was travelling from Battagram to Mansehra.Earlier in April, nine people died when a van collided with a truck in Thatta's Chalia. All of the victims were residents of Karachi, according to police, reported. The truck driver involved in the accident managed to flee from the site.

Title: 6 die in twin mishaps on Pb highway
content: BATHINDA: Six persons died in two separate road accidents in a span of a few hours on the Barnala-Ludhiana highway, police said on Friday.While two persons were killed late on Thursday when a container fell on their pick-up van after a collision, four people died when the car they were travelling in rammed into a loaded stationary tractor-trailer with no reflectors early on Friday. The pick-up van coming from the Ludhiana side collided with the truck near Sehjara village and the container fell on it. Heavy machinery had to be pressed into service to lift the container and retrieve the bodies. The second accident took place near Bhadalwad village, only a few kilometres away from Sehjara on the same highway, wherein four persons from Haryanaâ€™s Hisar on way to Ludhiana were killed. The police pulled the persons out of the car and took them to the Barnala civil hospital, where they were declared brought dead. The police havenâ€™t disclosed the identity of the victims, but the oldest of them was 40 years old, and the youngest was 14.

content:
TIRUPATI: Three people were killed and five others sustained injuries in a road accident near Kadiri in Andhra Pradesh's Sri Sathya Sai district on Sunday. According to police, a speeding car rammed into an autorickshaw on the national highway near Erradoddi in Kadiri mandal, earlier in the day. The deceased were identified as Chinna Swamy Naik, Chalapathy Naik and Bhaskar Naik (auto driver). Police registered a case and shifted the bodies for postmortem. . .AdvertisementFamily members of the deceased alleged that reckless driving by car driver Rajaneesh Reddy led to the mishap.They demanded that police conduct a fair investigation and take action against the Reddy.

Title: â€˜Biharâ€™s accident fatality rate highest in countryâ€™
content: PATNA: The most recent data from MORTH (2021) concerning traffic accidents in India reveals a staggering reality â€“ approximately 4.12 lakh road accidents occur in the country each year, leading to a harrowing daily toll of 1,130 accidents and the loss of 422 human lives. Regrettably, Bihar showcases an even grimmer scenario, with an accident severity index surpassing 80, positioning it among the poorest performers in road safety. Notably, Bihar ranks in the top 10 states for accidents and holds the disheartening sixth position for the number of fatalities on National Highways (NHs). Adding to this, Biharâ€™s fatality rate, calculated as accidents per 10,000 registered vehicles, ranks among the nation's highest. Patna alone witnessed approximately 210 fatalities and 285 severe injuries on its roads in 2021. Given these disconcerting statistics, itâ€™s evident that urgent and collective efforts are imperative to address the dire road safety situation in the state.The multifaceted road safety issues can be broadly categorized into three overarching aspects â€“ enforcement-related challenges, education-related challenges, and engineering-related challenges.Enforcement: MORTHâ€™s report (2021) underscores that the most significant contributor to road crashes is over-speeding, responsible for over 70% of all accidents in India. This concern is further exacerbated by rampant over speeding along with underage driving prevalent in India, Bihar being no exception. Additionally, accidents resulting from overloading account for over 7% of all incidents. Instances of wrong-side driving, cellphone use while driving, jumping red lights, and cases of driving under the influence continue to occur in substantial numbers, despite liquor prohibition in the state. The situation becomes worse when the heavy vehicle drivers become violators as these vehicles are difficult to handle due to their large sizes and heavy load therein. Irony is that more people are killed on Indian roads than all guns, still it is easier to get a driving license than an arms license. Encroachments on roads result in narrowing down of pavements and pedestrian facilities, thus exposing road users to accidents.Education: The inadequate enforcement of traffic regulations has deeply impacted the driving behaviour of road users, leading to rampant violations. The issuance of driving licences without proper trials and to individuals unfamiliar with traffic rules has created a hazardous environment, threatening the safety of all road users. Another concerning factor is the lack of awareness and knowledge among road users about essential road safety principles. Unfortunately, presently the desired road user behaviour and rules etc. are not given much emphasis in families, schools or colleges depriving them from this important knowledge. Road users without having formal education have often been observed to show dangerous behaviour and their number is higher in Bihar.Engineering: Both vehicular and road-user elements play pivotal roles in road safety. Substandard vehicular conditions, including inadequate lighting and braking efficiency, contribute to road accidents and fatalities. Similarly, road conditions characterized by poor maintenance, sharp curves, steep roadside features, and neglected drainage and cross-drainage repairs significantly compromise road safety. Many hotspots have been identified in Bihar and the work of identification of hotspots is on and engineering solutions to enhance safety is being worked on.In conclusion, the road safety landscape in Bihar demands immediate attention and comprehensive action. By addressing the enforcement, education, engineering and emergency response aspects, all the stakeholders can collectively strive toward a safer and more secure road environment for everyone.(The author is professor of civil engineering and dean, academic, at NIT-Patna)

Title: Out to celebrate birthday, man killed in car crash in Lucknow
content: LUCKNOW: A 23-year-old man, on his way back home after celebrating his birthday, was killed in a car accident in Lucknow's Gomtinagar around 2.30am on Sunday.According to police, Sarthak Pahwa was with his friend Ananya Saxena (21). The speeding subcompact SUV car hit a divider, cartwheeled, and crashed into a pole, killing Sarthak on the spot.The eight-second CCTV footage of the incident went viral, showing the speeding vehicle hitting the divider, flipping over, and coming to a stop after hitting the pole.The accident took place near Ambedkar Udyan crossing.The impact of the crash was so severe that the front part of the vehicle got destroyed.Some locals saw the damaged SUV and alerted the police.A team of police officers arrived at the scene and extricated the bodies of the two occupants from the vehicle. Sarthak was pronounced dead at the scene, while Ananya was taken to a nearby hospital for treatment. She was later discharged after receiving primary treatment, said the police.The victimâ€™s father, Raman Pahwa, is a businessman who deals in tyres. He has a shop called Shilpi Traders on Sitapur Road. SHO, Gomtinagar, Deepak Pandey, said that speeding was the cause of the accident.The victim's family members and relatives who reached the hospital told journalists that Saturday was Sarthak's birthday. He had recently completed his BBA from a private institute in Mumbai. His sister, Khushi, is also studying in Mumbai.

Title: Car crash at Porvorim kills three of a family, 1 critical
content: PANAJI: A planned get-together of cousins turned tragic early Sunday after their electric vehicle smashed into a tree at Porvorim, killing three occupants on the spot and leaving two injured, one of whom is critical.Goa police said the vehicle was travelling at such a high speed that when it slammed into the tree, a male passenger was flung out and landed on his head several metres away from the vehicle.The car was being driven by Shawn Fernandes, 26, who died in the accident along with his cousin sister, Abigail Dâ€™Souza, 21, and brother-in-law Veldrofe Dâ€™Souza, 26.Fiona Dâ€™Souza, 22, has suffered serious head injuries and is â€œbattling for lifeâ€, while Ninoshka Fernandes, 21, has multiple fractures but is stable, police said. Both are undergoing treatment at GMC.â€œPrima facie, it appears that the vehicle was overspeeding and while trying to overtake a jeep, the vehicleâ€™s driver lost control. It doesnâ€™t look like alcohol was involved though we cannot say so conclusively,â€ North Goa SP Nidhin Valsan told TOI.â€œIt seems like Shawn had been working through the day and had only come to pick up the others. He was tired and it appears that he failed to assess the situation correctly,â€ Valsan said. â€œTyre skid marks begin 30 metres before the accident site, and veer from the left side of the road to the right and towards the tree.â€Police said the accident occurred on the Panaji-Mapusa highway at around 1.20am, when the car on its way to Parra banged into the tree in front of a stationery store at Porvorim just after Delfinoâ€™s supermarket.â€œThe car had five passengers, including the driver. Two of the occupants of the car had gone to Quepem for a birthday party. Shawn had picked them up to bring them to his house at Canca Parra for a celebration,â€ Porvorim PI Anant Gaonkar said.Shawnâ€™s social media feeds show that he loved cooking and was preparing stuffed chicken and chorizo along with Ninoshka merely hours before his death. Police said it appeared from the fishing rods found near the car that the cousins had planned to go angling on Sunday morning.The postmortem examination of the deceased is yet to be carried out, Valsan said.The high-speed collision stripped the bark of the tree and tore away the rear door of the car. The man who was thrust out of the car appears to have fallen in the middle of the road.Abigail was an Anjuna resident and Veldrofe lived at Badem. Of the survivors, Fiona is a resident of Merces and Ninoshka resides at Anjuna.The accident brings back memories of the horrific Banastarim crash nearly a month ago on August 6, when a drunk driver Paresh Sawardekar in a Mercedes-Benz SUV killed three persons and injured many others, some of whom are still bedridden.

Title: Karnataka: 4 killed, 3 injured as car rams into lorry in Chitradurga
content: CHITRADURGA: As many as four people were killed and three others suffered injuries when the car they were travelling in rammed into a parked lorry near in Chitradurga district of Karnataka on Monday.The accident took place at Mallapura-Golarhatti in Chitradurga, reported news agency ANI quoting Chitradurga SP.Upon receiving the information, Chitradurga Rural police went to the spot and took up the investigation. "A car with seven passengers rammed into a parked lorry on National Highway-48," said police.. .AdvertisementAccording to the police, the deceased were identified as Samsuddin (40), Mallika (37), Khalil (42) and Tabrej (13).Police said, "Four persons were killed on the spot while three others identified as Nargish, Rehan and Rahman were seriously injured."As per preliminary information, the car was en route to Tumkur from Hospet."The injured have been shifted to the district hospital in Chitradurga and the lorry driver has been detained," said police.

Title: Woman dies, 2 hurt after heavy vehicle hits bike
content:
Indore: A woman was killed and her daughter and son-in-law were injured after their bike was hit by a speeding heavy vehicle in Amjhera of Dhar on Sunday morning. Title: 4 of family dead after car hits parked mini-truck in Pilibhit
content: PILIBHIT: Four persons of a Lucknow-based family -- all aged between 21 and 25 years -- were killed after their speeding car rammed into a commercial vehicle parked along the road near a village on Pilibhit-Basti NH-730 on Sunday. Two members of the family were critically injured. The car, with six persons on board, was on its way from Lucknow to Nainital via Pilibhit when the incident occurred at Kajri Niranjanpur village under Sehramau North police limits. The stationary 'mini-truck' was being used to transport poultry.SHO (Sehramau North), Madan Mohan Chaturvedi, said, "The accident took place around 4am. It was dark and the collision may have been caused since the parked vehicle did not have its parking lights or indicators switched on. Further investigations are on."He added, "The bodies of the victims -- Abdulla Naushad (25) who was at the wheel, his wife Sayima Rahat (23) and his cousin sisters Mariyam (20) and Batul (21) -- have been sent for autopsy. Abdulla's 8-month-old daughter, Abiya, and cousin, Amin Ali (18), were critically injured. The victims belonged to Khadra in Hasanganj area of Lucknow." The impact of the collision was so severe that the entire front portion of the car, including its wheels, were completely damaged. The injured were initially admitted to a community health centre in Puranpur and then shifted to the district government hospital. Later, due to their critical condition, they were referred to a medical facility in Lucknow. An FIR was registered against the mini-truck driver under IPC sections 304-A (causing death by negligence) and 338 (causing grievous hurt to any person by doing any act so negligently as to endanger human life) after a written complaint by Abdulla's uncle, Naseem Ali. The accused driver fled from the spot, the SHO said. The victims, which included three women, were relatives of Samajwadi Party (SP) state general secretary (minority cell), Akram Siddiqui. Following news of the accident, a large number of SP workers and office-bearers converged near the post-mortem house to express condolences to the bereaved family.

Title: 1 killed in road accident in Dykundi province in Afghanistan
content: KABUL: Amid increased incidents of traffic-related mishaps, one person was killed in a road accident on Tuesday in Dykundi province of Afghanistan, Khaama Press reported citing sources.The accident was due to a vehicle overturning in the Sharistan district.The primary cause of the vehicle overturning was brake failure and technical issues, the sources said, according to Khaama Press.However, the provincial officials have not provided any statement regarding the same.. .AdvertisementIt is not the first time that road accidents in Dykundi have claimed the lives of the people.Citing recent statistics, Khaama Press reported that 400 people were killed in road accidents in the country during the previous three months.The recent spike in traffic accidents in the country is attributable to a variety of factors such as irresponsible driving, crumbling roads, a lack of attention to traffic laws and regulations, high-speed driving, old automobiles, and congested highways and roads.

Title: Biker killed in accident
content:
Mangaluru: A bike rider succumbed to the injuries, suffered after a car rammed the bike that he was riding at Kapinandka in Belthangady taluk. According to police, the deceased is Sudheer. Car driver Deepak Kumar allegedly drove his car in a negligent manner from Guruvayanakere and rammed the two-wheeler, following which the bike rider fell on the road. Though he was rushed to the hospital, he failed to respond to the treatment and died. Venoor police have registered a case under IPC sections 279 and 304 (A).KSRTC bus breaks down A KSRTC bus breakdown was reported at late night near Dharmasthala on Sunday. An alternative bus was sent from Dharmasthala and reached the location by 12.30am. The bus that broke down was sent back to the depot. The public had to face hardship because of the breakdown. Title: Goa to dedicate two ambulances to hasten road accident response
content: PANAJI: The directorate of health services will be placing two dedicated ambulances on state roads, especially highways, for accident victim response. This is being done at the behest of the directorate of transport to ensure round-the-clock service.â€œUntil now, there was no roadside accident trauma response in Goa,â€ said director of transport Rajan Satardekar. â€œThese will be the first ambulances to focus exclusively on road safety,â€ he added.These ambulances will be operated under the directorate of health services by the GVK. One ambulance each will be deployed in each district.â€œIf required, we shall call for two more ambulances to be on standby. This is a ready set-up in terms of communication, connection with facilities, and the availability of drivers and crew. The vehicles will be positioned in places where the probability of road accidents is higher,â€ Satardekar said.The move comes a day after a fatal accident in Porvorim on the intervening nights of September 2 and 3, involving an electric SUV, resulted in the deaths of three people and the injuries of two others. The self-accident occurred within a month of the Banastarim drunk-driving accident, in which three people were killed by a Mercedes-Benz SUV.â€œThere are several problem areas, and the solutions differ. We will be tapping into the i-RAD data to make Goaâ€™s roads safer,â€ he said.

Title: Two run over by vehicle after e-rickshaw overturns in Delhi's Burari
content: NEW DELHI: Two men were killed after the e-rickshaw they were travelling in overturned and they were run over by a vehicle in north Delhi's Burari area on Tuesday. "Two others were injured in the incident," said police.Police were informed on Monday night that an e-rickshaw has overturned in which one person has died on the spot and three others got injured, reported news agency PTI quoting deputy commissioner of police (north) Sagar Singh Kalsi. "The injured were rushed to a hospital where one died during treatment," the DCP said, adding the victims were returning from catering work in Jahangirpuri. Preliminary investigation has revealed that the e-rickshaw overturned, following which its occupants fell on the road. They were run over by a vehicle, possibly a dumper, police said. The deceased have been identified as Sanjeev (45), the e-rickshaw driver and a resident of Swaroop Nagar, and Amar Singh (36), a resident of Nathupura, Kalsi said. A case under IPC sections 279 (rash driving), 304A (causing death by negligence) and 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) has been registered, the DCP added.

Title: Three killed as tractor overturns in Raisen district
content:
Bhopal: Three persons including a tractor driver was killed when he lost control over the vehicle that careened off the road and overturned at a culvert in Raisen district. The incident took place late on Sunday night in Kamaton village under Bareilly police station. The deceasedidentified as Akash 16, son of Kalyan Singh, Pawan, 21, son of Mukhram, and Rahul 17, son of Jeevan Singhwere crushed under the weight of the overturned tractor.Kalyan Singh Adivasi, father of one of the deceased, notified the Bareilly police about the accident. Police recovered the bodies of the three deceased and were subsequently transported to the Civil Hospital Bareilly, where post-mortem examinations were conducted on Monday morning.A case has been registered. tnn

Title: Bus driver without licence kills woman crossing road
content: Colva: Rosarinha Simoes, 64, was killed in a road accident on Monday. She was hit by a bus driven by 37-year-old Vilesh Lotlikar, who did not possess a valid motor driving licence at the time of the incident, the police said.The Cuncolim police registered a case against Lotlikar under Sections 279 and 304-A of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) and Section 3 of the Motor Vehicle Act.The complaint, filed by head constable Vinod Kankonkar, states that the incident occurred at 8am when Lotlikar was driving the bus from Betul to Margao. The bus hit Simoes as she was attempting to cross the road. Simoes fell and was, subsequently, run over by the rear wheel of the bus.Bystanders called the ambulance, and she was rushed to the South Goa District Hospital. However, medical personnel declared Simoes as brought dead.

Title: Anurager Chhowa update: Deepa meets with an accident; rushed to hospital
content: In the latest episode of Anurager Chhowa, Deepa finally meets her daughters, who are at school. She somehow sneaks inside the school premises while the security personnel repeatedly ask her to leave. Finally, Sister Margaret is called and she asks the kids whether she knows Deepa, who claims to be Sona-Rupaâ€™s mom.A big setback for DeepaThe girls, who were crying holding their mom a few moments back, lie in front of Sister Margaret. Rupa remembers how Surya threatened to kill himself if Sona-Rupa go back with Deepa. Rupa denies knowing Deepa or even meeting her before. Sona, too replies the same. When Sister Margaret threatens to call cops, Sona and Rupa request her not to. Deepa is in utter shock and wonders why her daughters are lying.Mishika thrown out of Sengupta householdTabla, who is Deepaâ€™s brother, talks to Prabir and Labanya about the current situation. Suddenly, Mishika storms into Sengupta household. Labanya loses her temper the moment she notices Mishika. Later, when Mishika confesses going to Sona-Rupaâ€™s school to enquire about Surya. This leaves the family members furious. They throw Mishika out.Deepa meets with an accidentLocal people, who were present near the school premises, think Deepa is an imposter and she wanted to abduct kids. They manhandle her. Deepa gets injured when a lady throws a stone at her. Meanwhile, Deepaâ€™s brothers are in search of her and reach the bus depot from where she took the bus for Bhubanpur.Deepa prays to god for the well-being of her daughters and wonders whether they would have a better future without her. Unfortunately, a vehicle hits Deepa, who has been walking on the road lost in her thoughts. She lies on the street covered with blood.Rupa feels guiltyRupa feels guilty for hurting her mom. Sona too feels equally guilty. Rupa decides to find her mom and runs towards the street. Meanwhile, Sister Margaret is informed about the accident. She rushes to the accident spot and recognizes Deepa. Local people intend to beat the driver up but Deepa vows for his innocence. Sister Margaret is surprised to see the kind side of Deepa whom she thought of an imposter earlier. Deepa is taken to the hospital.Labanya-Tabla teach Mishika a lessonLabanya and Tabla suspect Mishika is hiding something. They force her to reveal all the information she is aware of.Deepa rushed to the hospitalDeepa is rushed to the hospital in an injured state. In the hospital, Surya is informed about Rupa falling unwell. He rushes to the school.

Title: Teen killed in road accident
content:
Chandigarh: A 14-year-old boy was killed and his friend injured after an auto-rickshaw rammed into them in a hit-and-run near the government press light point in Sector 17 in the early hours of Monday. The two ragpickers were walking back home in Sector 25. As they were trying to cross the press light point, a speeding auto driver coming from sectors 17-18 light point hit them and sped away, said Varun, the injured. His friend, Goli, was taken to Government Multi-specialty Hospital, Sector 16, where he succumbed to his injuries.

Title: Five killed in 3 accidents on SG Road
content: Ahmedabad: The 24 hours preceding 9am on Tuesday saw five people killed in three road accidents on SG Road, with two accidents taking place on Monday night. In the first accident, Rahul Prajapati (23), a resident of Naranpura, stated that his friend Naresh Prajapati (23), a resident of Nava Vadaj, called him to Akhbarnagar and told him that Kaushal Prajapati (25), Pravin Prajapati, Nimesh Panchal and Mitesh were coming over and the six of them would go to SG Road to have snacks in Kaushalâ€™s uncleâ€™s car. They then went to the food park near Karnavati Club. When they were returning, Nimesh was driving, and Rahul was sitting next to him. At about 3am on Tuesday, they were driving through the underpass near the gurudwara at high speed when the right wheel touched the road divider causing the car to overturn and smash into a light pole. Rahul then called an ambulance as some of them were injured. Naresh was declared dead on the spot while Mitesh and Kaushal were declared â€œbrought deadâ€ at Civil Hospital. Rahul said he was unaware of the injuries suffered by Nimesh, while he and Pravin were being treated at the hospital. The driver of the car, Nimesh Panchal, was booked for rash driving and causing death by negligence, among other charges. . .AdvertisementIn the second accident, Kushal Prajapati (21), a resident of Daskroi, stated in his complaint that his brother, Ankit Prajapati, who worked at EPIC Hospital left for work on Monday morning. When he was on the Sola overbridge, a bus (GJ-01-PZ-7506) operated by Parth Travels, which was a staff bus, was coming in speed and it hit Ankitâ€™s two-wheeler, causing his death. The complainant said the rear wheel of the bus had crushed Ankit, killing him on the spot. Police registered a case against Lokesh Meena, the driver of the bus. In the third accident, Arvindsinh Chauhan (33), a resident of Ghatlodia, said that he and his wife Payal (33) had gone for a walk along SG Road on Monday night after parking their two-wheeler. An unidentified four-wheeler then hit his wife. She was rushed to the trauma centre at Sola Civil Hospital but died there during treatment.

Title: Man killed, wife hurt in road accident
content: Prayagraj: A man was killed while his wife sustained serious injuries after the two-wheeler in which they were travelling was hit by a speeding dumper near Dandi trisection under the limits of Naini police station in trans Yamuna pocket on Tuesday. The deceased Niranjan Chaudhary was a resident of Dandi road. His wife Reena Devi was admitted to SRN hospital where her condition was stated to be critical.Police said the incident took place around 10:30am when the couple was going to their relativeâ€™s house in Naini. An FIR has been lodged in this connection and a manhunt on for absconding dumper driver. They added that the body has been sent for postmortem. . .Advertisement

Title: Duo killed in accident on way to Ramdevra
content:
Jaisalmer: Three persons were killed in two separate road accidents in Barmer district, police said on Tuesday, including an uncle and nephew who were part of a group from Gujarat going to the religious shrine of Ramdevra in Jaisalmer.A group of Gujarati pilgrims from Ahmedabad was travelling on 8-10 bikes to Ramdevra on Tuesday when a tanker hit one of the bikes in Dhorimanna police station area. The uncle and nephew riding the bike, identified as Pratap and Ranchid, both residents of Ahmedabad, were badly injured and immediately taken to a private hospital, where they were declared as dead. Police have kept the bodies at the Dhorimanna hospital mortuary. Another accident took place in Chohatan police station area of Barmer district on Monday night in which a pick-up vehicle hit a bike. One of the bike riders died on the spot while the other was injured. The two were brothers-in-law. The deceased was identified as Sonu Khan, 26, and the injured as Razak Khan. The pick-upâ€™s driver ran away from the spot, police said.

Title: Six, including one-year-old, killed in road accident in Tamil Nadu's Salem
content: SALEM: Six people, including a girl child, died and two others suffered injuries when a minivan they were travelling in rammed into a truck from behind near Sankari area in Salem district of Tamil Nadu in the early hours of Wednesday.The deceased have been identified as Selvaraj, 50, M Arumugam, 48, his wife Manjula, 45, Palanisami, 45, his wife Pappathi, 40 and R Sanjana, 1.Most of them were from Harijan Colony at Kuttampalayam near Perundurai in Erode district.R Priya, 21, daughter of Palanisami, was married to Rajadurai of Salem two years ago. It is learnt that the couple decided to separate due to issues between them. Priya's parents Palanisami and Pappathi, their relatives Arumugam, his wife Manjula and Selvaraj came to Salem on Tuesday evening to take Priya and her daughter Sanjana to their home. They came in a minivan. Arumugam's son Vikky alias Vignesh, 25, drove the vehicle.After talks with Rajadurai's family members, the team returned to Perundurai with Priya and Sanjana at night.When the van neared Chinnagoundanur village near Sankari, the minivan rammed a truck that was parked on the roadside. "Six people died while Priya and Vikky sustained injuries," said an officer at the Sankari police station. The police sent the two injured to Salem Government Mohan Kumaramangalam Medical College and Hospital. The bodies were sent to the Sankari Government Hospital for postmortem. A case was registered, and further inquiries were on.Watch Chilling visuals: Minivan rams into truck from behind; 1-year-old girl child among 6 killed in Tamil Nadu road mishap

Title: 3 killed in separate incidents
content:

He was rushed to the Nashik civil hospital where he was declared dead. The Satpur police have registered a case against the building contractor for causing death due to negligence.In the other case, Pankaj Waghmare, a resident of Sharanpur Road, who was working as a labourer at the government womenâ€™s hostel in the Ashok Stambh area, lost his balance and fell down. The Sarkarwada police have registered a case of accidental death.One more labourer, Nilesh Rajpur (23), who was working in Satpur MIDC area, died when machinery part on a crane fell on him. . .Advertisement

Title: 3 killed, 2 injured as car jumps divider, hits truck on Mumbai-Ahmedabad National Highway in Vasai
content: MUMBAI: Three people were killed and two others sustained injuries after the car they were travelling in jumped the divider and collided with a truck on Mumbai-Ahmedabad National Highway in Vasai on Wednesday morning.According to police, the accident took place at around 6am on Sativali bridge. The car with five occupants, including the driver, was plying towards Gujarat. The mishap took place when the car reached Sativali and the driver lost control over the vehicle. The car jumped the road divider and landed on Mumbai-bound road on the national highway and hit the truck. Eyewitnesses said the three people died on the spot. Two passengers sustained serious injuries and were moved to the nearby hospital. Police suspect that the driver of the Swift car was speeding when the accident took place.The front portion of the car was badly damaged, making it difficult for rescuers to remove the mangled bodies of the victims. The driver of the truck escaped with minor bruises, police said. The car was later moved away with the help of a crane. Vehicular traffic on the highway had slowed down following the accident. Police said details of the victims was being ascertained.

Title: 7 killed, 11 injured in road accident in Tinsukia district
content: DIBRUGARH: In one of the deadliest road accidents in the state in recent times, seven persons were killed and 11 injured in a head-on collision between a truck and a passenger vehicle at Kakopathar in Tinsukia district on Tuesday night. Police said a loaded truck coming from neighbouring Arunachal Pradesh rammed into the vehicle carrying passengers from a weekly market in Tinsukia's Doomdooma town at a curve at Bordirak Tiniali on National Highway 52 around 9.30pm.Truck driver was drunk, arrestedWhile seven persons, including the driver of the passenger vehicle, died on the spot, 11 injured persons were shifted to Tinsukia civil hospital. Among them, the condition of seven injured persons was found to be critical and they were referred to Assam Medical College and Hospital (AMCH) at Dibrugarh. The rest are being treated at the civil hospital.. .AdvertisementThe deceased have been identified as Binita Baruah (52), Pallabi Rabha (29), Rina Gogoi 48), Mihidhar Neog (50), Jadhav Tairai (54), Kamal Sonowal (30) and the driver of the passenger vehicle, Prabin Moran (50). They were said to be the breadwinners of their families and had come to Doomdooma to sell vegetables.The critically injured persons being treated at the Assam Medical College and Hospital are Makon Gogoi (60), Pinky Moran (30), Munu Moran (28), Jonali Moran (30), Banjit Moran (35), Budheswar Moran (55) and Bikash Neog (9). Those who are being treated at the Tinsukia civil hospital are Atul Gogoi (38), Yashoda Moran (50), Lakshmi Moran (30) and Abolata Moran (35).

Title: No accident blackspot on 50km Pune-Mumbai expressway stretch
content: PUNE: The 50km Pune-Mumbai expressway stretch between Kiwale exit and Amrutanjan Point does not have any accident blackspot after the demolition of the 190-year-old Amrutanjan bridge on April 5, 2020, a highway safety patrol (HSP) study revealed.â€œThe stretch around the Amrutanjan bridge was a major blackspot where accidents took place frequently but after the demolition of the bridge and widening of lanes along the stretch, the spot has not witnessed any fatal accident in the last three years,â€ HSP Puneâ€™s superintendent of police Lata Phad told TOI. â€œWe have since removed Amrutanjan Point from the list of eway accident blackspots. In fact, there are no blackspots now along the stretch under our jurisdiction,â€ Phad added.An accident blackspot, according to the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, is a stretch of a national highway or expressway of about 500m length where either five road accidents have taken place in three years involving fatalities and grievous injuries or 10 fatalities have occurred in three years.â€œThere is no section on the expressway where repeated fatal or serious accidents have taken place over a period of five years,â€ Phad said. â€œTwo major fatal accidents took place near the Urse toll plaza Pune corridor in which five people were killed in the last 12 months. We considered this spot as an accident hotspot and a series of measures were taken to avert accidents. In the last four months, the spot has not witnessed any major accident,â€ she said.â€œWe painted rumble strips, installed blinker signals, painted the side margin lines, and put up no parking/no halting boards. Since then, there have been no accidents,â€ she said.On the entire 50km stretch 22 accident fatalities were reported till the end of July. However, none of the accidents occurred on the 500m stretch around the Amrutanjan Point. Phad said, â€œWe are working on some schemes to reduce accidents like our teams at toll plazas are telling motorists to wear seat belts and follow the speed limit.â€

Title: 3-year-old sole survivor in Ajmer family as parents, grandparents die in accident on way to Nathdwara
content: Ajmer: Two generations of an Ajmer-based businessmanâ€™s family were wiped out in an accident on Bhilwara-Chittorgarh national highway on Tuesday morning, after the car in which they were travelling hit a road divider before colliding head-on with a truck near Pansal village, killing four members.A three-year-old girl was the only family member to survive, police said. Radheyshyam Khandelwal (66) belonged to a well-known business family in Ajmer. His only son Manish (35) and daughter-in-law Yaashika (32) came to India from the US with their three-year-old daughter last week to participate in the rituals of Radheyshyamâ€™s mother, who passed away recently.This was completed two days ago, following which the Khandelwals were on their way to pray at the Shrinathji temple in Rajasmand on Tuesday when the tragedy struck. Around 7am, a tyre of the car burst, and the vehicle crashed into the divider before hitting the truck coming from the opposite side of the road.The car turned into a mangled mass of metal in the impact. Radheyshyam, his wife Shakuntala (64), Manish, and Yaashika died on the spot while the child and the driver survived with injuries, Pur SHO Shivraj Gurjar said. Villagers rushed to the spot and alerted cops.â€œThe injured were taken to Mahatma Gandhi Hospital for treatment while the bodies of the four were sent for post-mortem,â€ said the SHO, adding that the road was blocked for about an hour while police managed traffic.The familyâ€™s relatives arrived in the afternoon after being informed. The business community in Ajmer was devastated after learning of the tragedy, and scores of mourners thronged the cremation in the evening. Radheyshyam had been living in the city with his three brothers and they ran a dairy shop together.Dinesh Khandelwal, a relative, said the family was very popular. â€œShakuntala was working for social causes, while Radheyshyam was a good-hearted man who had a long line of relations in Ajmer,â€ he said, adding that the couple also had four daughters, settled in various places.

Title: Tractor mows down 3-yr-old running to dad
content:
BENGALURU: A three-year-old boy, who was rushing to cross the road with his grandmother to jump into the arms of his father standing on the opposite side, was killed after a tractor rammed them in Doddaballapur on Wednesday morning. H Dasharath Singh, was the only child of Harish Kumar Singh, a jalebi seller at Doddaballapur bus stand. The accident took place at 9am when Dasharath and Harishâ€™s mother Kamalesh Devi were walking near Ayyappa temple on D-cross road. â€œHarish, who left home earlier in the morning, was on the other side of the road. On seeing his father, Dasharath, grabbing his grandmother, began running to the other end. But a speeding tractor rammed them: The boy took the brunt of the impact and died, while Kamalesh Devi sustained fractures in her right hand. The tractor driver is absconding,â€ an officer said.

Title: 5 killed , 16 including children injured in accident in Churu

Title: All within 24 hours: 5 killed in 3 hit-and-run accidents in Gurgaon
content: GURGAON: Five people lost their lives in three separate accidents within the last 24 hours in the city.In the first incident, a speeding truck rammed a motorcycle on the Wazirpur-Hayatpur road around 11.30am on Wednesday, leading to the death of a 24-year-old woman. She has been identified as Babita, a resident of Hayatpur.Babliâ€™s brother Sumit, who was on the bike with her, said: â€œNear the petrol pump between Wazirpur and Hayatpur, a speeding truck suddenly made a sudden left turn without turning on the indicators, which led to a collision with my bike. Due to the impact, I fell on the left, and my sister fell near the truck. The rear tyre of the truck came off and hit her, causing serious injuries. The truck driver abandoned his vehicle and fled. I rushed my sister to a hospital, but she passed away during treatment.â€. .AdvertisementBased on his complaint, an FIR was filed against the absconding truck driver under sections 279 (rash driving), 427 (causing mischief), and 304A (death by negligence) of the IPC at Sector 10A police station.Inspector Surender, the investigating officer, said: â€œThe victim sustained severe injuries on her back, thighs and stomach, resulting in heavy bleeding that led to her death. The accused driver, Ramjeet (32), was arrested a few kilometres away from the petrol pump.â€Around 2.30pm the same day, a 49-year-old man working with a private company died after a speeding canter truck hit his bike near Signature Towers. Deepak Kumar Ray, who was originally from Bihar and was staying in Sector 85, was heading towards Signature Towers from Huda City Centre for work, his wife Ranjeeta told cops.â€œMy husband had left his office for some company-related work. A speeding vehicle suddenly hit Deepak. He suffered critical head injuries. Police said he was rushed to hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries,â€ she said in her complaint to police.An FIR was filed against the driver of the truck, under sections 279 (rash driving), 427 (causing mischief), and 304A (death by negligence) of the IPC at Sector 40 police station. SHO Surender Singh said, â€œThe victim was rushed to Meo Hospital in South City 1, where he was declared dead on arrival. His body was then sent to the district mortuary, where autopsy reports confirmed head injuries and heavy bleeding as the cause of death. An investigation is under way. We have seized the truck and are actively searching for its driver."In Sohna, a rashly driven tractor laden with precast cement poles overturned, trapping three labourers working along the road under it around 3.30pm. All of them died on the way to hospital.The accident happened on Palwal-Sohna road near Sancholi village. An eyewitness, Praveen Kumar, said, â€œI own a shop along the road and was sitting under a tree in front of it when I saw a tractor loaded with poles coming from the Palwal side. It was being driven recklessly at a high speed. Due to the driverâ€™s negligence, the tractor lost balance and overturned, with many of the cement poles slipping out. Three labourers working nearby got trapped under them. We rushed there and rescued them, and immediately took the injured to the government hospital in Sohna.â€ The poles were cleared with the help of an earthmover. â€œThe three labourers, Kanhaiya Lal, Kanhaiya Singh, and Dinesh, were declared brought dead at hospital. Another injured labourer, Dhannalal, was on the tractor during the accident. He suffered serious injuries and was referred to PGI Rohtak for treatment. The accused driver, Bintu Yadav, was arrested on Wednesday,â€ SHO Santosh Kumar said.An FIR was registered under sections 279, 337 (causing hurt), and 304A of the IPC at Sadar Sohna police station, he added.

Title: Liquid oxygen cylinder blast kills stone quarry worker in Tamil Nadu
content:
COIMBATORE: A 35-year-old worker died in a liquid oxygen cylinder blast in a stone quarry at Palladam in Tirupur district of Tamil Nadu on Friday morning. Two other workers sustained severe injuries and were admitted to a private hospital. The accident happened when they were unloading oxygen cylinders from a truck.The deceased has been identified as Sathishkumar, 35, of Kattampatti Pudur near Pollachi. M Chinnasamy, 38 of Perumagoundenpalayam near Karanampettai and Sivakumar, 35, of Kattampatti Pudur near Pollachi suffered injuries.The accident happened in SVA blue metals stone quarry on the Somanur Road. The quarry is owned by R Ramakrishnan, 49, of Karanampettai.The Palladam police registered a case.. .AdvertisementLiquid oxygen explosives are used in some stone quarries.

Title: Speeding car runs over bystanders, 1 dead
content:
Agra: An out-of-control car plowed into three bystanders in Aligarh's Harduaganj area killing a 50-year-old man and injuring two men. Prem Shankar alias Lalla alias Ustad from Dauji Colony worked as a labourer in the grain market. A relative said the accident occurred around 12.15pm near Hanumangarhi. Family members obstructed traffic by placing his body on the road, demanding strong action against the driver. Police cleared the blockade and rescued three youths in the car from an enraged crowd and took them into custody.Atrauli DSP Mohsin Khan said, â€œJeetpal, 80, and Shivansh Singh suffered injuries and were released after receiving medical aid.â€Harduaganj police station SHO Ravi Chandrwal added that a case was registered under IPC section 304 A and the driver was arrested.

Title: Five killed as two motorcycles collide
content: Rajkot: Five persons were killed after collision between two motorcycles near Rajkot city late on Wednesday nightThe accident took place on Sardhar-Bhupgadh road around 8 pm on Wednesday. Three of the victims were travelling on one two-wheeler, while the other victims were riding the second bike. Preliminary probe indicated that one of the victims was riding carelessly and rammed his bike into a two-wheeler coming in the opposite direction, said A J Parmar, sub-inspector of Ajidam police station.. .AdvertisementThose who lost their lives were identified as Dilip Bhuriya (25), Arjun Meda (18), Dinesh Rathod (30), Devgan Makwana (22) and Rajesh Rathod (22), all residents of Sajadiali village of Rajkot district.While Bhuriya, Meda and Dinesh were going towards Sardhar village on one bike to buy groceries, Makwana and Rajesh were coming towards Bhupgadh from Sardhar on their two-wheeler.As per the FIR, Makwana was driving recklessly and crashed his motorcycle into the bike coming from the opposite direction on Sardhar-Bhupgadh road, said Parmar.â€œAfter the collision, Bhuriyaâ€™s bike hit an autorickshaw, while Makwanaâ€™s motorcycle hit another vehicle. Though the five were admitted to a nearby hospital, they died soon after due to serious injuries on their heads and bodies,â€ said Parmar.The police have registered a case against Makwana for causing death by negligence, he added.

Title: Woman killed in road accident, irate relatives block natâ€™l highway
content: Times News NetworkLudhiana: Ludhiana-Delhi National highway was blocked for about one hour after a pick-up van hit a woman who was crossing the highway near Kailash Nagar. The road blocade caused a long traffic jam late on Friday night. The accused managed to escape and the woman died on the spot. Her grief-stricken family reached the spot and blocked the highway for about one hour. The resultant traffic jam stretched for about two kilometers. Police had to use mild force to disperse the agitators and traffic movement was restored.The deceased woman has been identified as Menika, 39, a factory worker from Kailash Nagar. She was on her way home at around 10:15pm when the incident took place.Inspector Gurmukh Singh, SHO, Jodhewal police station said that the woman was coming from Bal Singh Nagar side and had to cross the highway to reach Kailash Nagar. She was crossing the road when a pick-up van hit her, killing her on the spot. A CCTV camera installed at the spot captured the pick-up van, which police are tying to trace.. .AdvertisementProtesting relatives and neighbours of the woman said that an accident took place at the spot every other day but the administration looked the other way. They said that in the absence of a foot overbridge, people had to cross the road, rendering them vulnerable to accidents.Relatives of the woman said that she was a widow survived by two daughters and a son. Her husband died of an ailment about one and a half years ago. She used to drop her children at a relativeâ€™s house while going to work. A case has been registered under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304-A (causing death by negligence) of the IPC.

Title: Six killed in three separate accidents in Chennai and Chengalpet
content: CHENNAI: At least six people were killed in three different accidents in Chennai and Chengalpet in the past two days.Four people on their way to Chennai in a car were killed after their vehicle collided with a van. All the deceased were from Madurantakam, while an injured person, Venkatesan, was admitted to the hospital, said police. Their hired car was coming near Madurantakam on Chennai-Trichy National Highway when a van carrying staff to a private company collided while trying to overtake the car," said a police officer. Six private company staff in the van also suffered injuries.The Cheyyur police registered a case.In Chennai, a 22-year-old man who took a U-turn while riding triples died in a road accident on Saturday. The deceased Sundar, 22, from Pallikaranai was riding a motorbike while his friends Shankar, 21 and Rashid, 22, were riding pillion. The accident occurred at Medavakkam flyover when a truck coming in the opposite direction rammed the motorbike.In Adyar, an unidentified man in his mid-60s was knocked down by a speeding van. The victim was crossing L B Road when the van knocked him down. tnn

Title: Fatal road accidents on the rise in Mohali dist
content: MOHALI: A three-year crash data analysis on road safety has revealed that there has been a marginal rise in fatal road accidents across Mohali. The data analysis report points out that in 2023, till July 30, a total of 163 road fatalities have been reported on the roads of Mohali district, while in 2022 the fatality count was 151 and in 2020, the count was 157. â€œThough the fatality count has risen, but the total number of accident cases have also gone up, resulting in a dip in the percentage of fatal accidents in 2023.â€ As compared to three years data, in 2023, total cases reported in the district are 281, out of which 163 were fatal, computing to 58% of the total fatalities; in 2021, the total accidents reported were 255, out of which fatality count was 151, computing to 59.2%. â€œIn the year 2020, the total cases reported were 257, out of which 158 were fatal, at 61%,â€ the report said. The report also reveals that 30% of the mishaps observed are hits from the rear, followed by 26% involving pedestrians and 17% hit from the flanks. . .AdvertisementAlso, maximum road accident fatalities occurred between 6 pm to 9 pm in the Mohali district. â€œOn an average, 22 people die every month in various road accidents within Mohali district. This indicates the need for better and safe infrastructure for the pedestrians,â€ the report said. The road accidents report also finds that usage of helmet and seat belt was minimal in Mohali area. As per the socio-economic cost analysis, Rs 855 crores were lost due to road accidents in Mohali district in the past three years, says the report. â€œThe maximum road accident fatalities occurred between 6 pm to 9 pm in the entire Mohali District. It accounts for 24% of total road accident fatalities in the past three years. So, in that slot, extra force in traffic enforcement and control can be deployed to reduce the number of road accident fatalities,â€ advised road safety experts. Fatalities high among 2-wheelers, pedestrians The crash data analysis also finds that the two wheelers and pedestrians comprise 81% of total victims in road accidents. In 2023, out of the total road fatalities of 163, there are 132 two-wheeler riders and pedestrians who were killed.

Title: 23-year-old man killed after being hit by car
content:
Nagpur: A 23-year-old youth was killed in a car collision in front of a wine shop on Ghat Road, said Ganeshpeth police. The identity of the deceased has not been established yet. A car hit the youth at 8am on Tuesday. After the accident, the driver fled the spot with the car. The police admitted the injured youth to Government Medical College and Hospital (GMCH) where he died during treatment.Body found in Mayo Hospital campusA body of 40-year-old unidentified man was found near nursesâ€™ hostel inside Indira Gandhi Government Medical College and Hospital (IGMCH) campus. Tehsil police have registered a case of accidental death.. .AdvertisementFarmer electrocutedin KanholibaraA farmer identified as Mohan Bunde was electrocuted on Friday at his farm near Kanholibara. tnn

Title: 2 killed, 1 injured in separate road accidents
content:
Guwahati: Two persons were killed and one sustained grievous injuries in two separate road accidents on Saturday.While one accident took place at Mendipathar in lower Assamâ€™s Goalpara district, the other occurred at Goreswar in Baksa district on Saturday night.A 45-year-old taxi driver, identified as Gagan Deka, sustained injuries in his chest and head after colliding with a truck at Naromari area of Goreswar under Baksa district. The officer-in- charge of Goreswar police station, Mridul Hazarika, said, â€œAround 12:30 am on Saturday night, a truck moving from Baihata Chariali towards Goreswar, collided head-on with a taxi at Naromari. After hitting the taxi, the truck sped away while the victim got stuck inside the taxi, as the front side of the vehicle got damaged due to the collision. Police helped him out and sent him to Naokata primary health centre. The truck has been intercepted by the local police at Khairabari in Udalguri district. However, the driver is absconding.â€In the second accident, at Dhanubhanga in Goalpara, two youths died on the spot after being hit by an unidentified vehicle on Saturday night. One of the youths has been identified as Bikash Rabha, while the identity of the other person is yet to be ascertained. The youths were reportedly on their way home from Guwahati when the vehicle hit them, before speeding away. An officer of the Dudhnoi police station said as the accident occurred at night, details were yet to be properly ascertained.. .Advertisement

Title: Stray menace: In 2 months, 50 cows killed in accidents
content: Raipur: Despite the Chhattisgarh high court order and relentless efforts by the state government to clear national highways, state highways, and streets of stray cattle menace, the issue continues to plague Raipur. Areas like Ring Road No.1 and Ring Road No.2, Vidhan Sabha Road, along with upscale neighbourhood areas including Gayatri Nagar, Avanti Vihar, Devendra Nagar, Shankar Nagar, Choubey Colony, and Samata Colony, are grappling with the presence of wandering cattle, posing a significant threat to commuters and the animals themselves. Shockingly, around 50 cows have lost their lives in various road accidents in Chhattisgarh in the past two months alone.Ring Road No.2, which traverses the industrial corridor of Chhattisgarh and borders industrial areas like Siltara and Urla, remains heavily congested due to the movement of heavy trucks. These trucks often have to navigate dangerously around stray cattle that either wander onto the road or choose it as a resting place.In a recent proactive move to address the issue, Zone 9 of the Raipur Municipal Corporation took an action against a livestock owner who had left five animals unattended on the Fundhar main road route, frequently used by VVIPs, as it connects to the airport.This incident, which garnered the attention of concerned citizens, led to a fine of Rs 5000 being imposed on the livestock owner, along with a stern warning letter. Zone commissioner Santosh Pandey, zone assistant revenue officer Vijay Sharma, and sanitation supervisor Bhola Tiwari issued this penalty in line with directives from the higher authorities. Each of the five animals received an immediate fine of Rs. 1000, totalling Rs 5000.. .AdvertisementTo ensure accountability and prevent future incidents, the Municipal Corporation obtained a written commitment from the livestock owner. In this commitment, the owner pledged not to leave animals in the open in the future and to provide proper restraint and care.However, it has been noted that some cattle owners only take care of their milking animals and neglect the others, allowing them to roam freely.In strict compliance with the high courtâ€™s directives, the Raipur Municipal Corporation, in collaboration with the Urban Administration and Development Department of the Chhattisgarh government and the Raipur District Administration, initiated a programme to enhance the safety of cows and bulls by equipping them with radiation belts. Remarkable progress has been made, with approximately 4000 cows and bulls set to be equipped with these belts in the first phase of the initiative. Mayor Aijaz Dhebar and commissioner Mayank Chaturvedi are overseeing this campaign, which aims to protect these animals from road accidents.Chaturvedi has appointed deputy commissioner Dr. R. K. Dongre as the nodal officer to oversee the initiative's necessary arrangements.The issue of stray cattle has been a topic of debate and action for years, with the civic bodies, including the Raipur Municipal Corporation, complying with a 2017 order to auction unclaimed cattle after a certain period. This decision triggered a political dispute between the BJP and Congress, with the BJP opposing the auction and accusing the Congress government of mistreating the cows. However, members of the mayor-in-council from the Raipur Municipal Corporation clarified that the auction rules were formulated by the BJP government on May 23, 2017, and the RMC issued the auction order based on these rules. Meanwhile, the RMC had to withhold its auction order following the political slugfest. The high court division bench comprising Chief Justice Ramesh Sinha and Judge Rajani Dubey on July 17, 2023, asked the state government to constitute a committee headed by the chief secretary to look into the matter and ensure stray cattle remains off the roads.

Title: Two engg students killed, two hurt after car rams into tree
content:
Hyderabad: Two engineering students were killed and their two friends injured when the car in which they were travelling hit a tree at Aloor village in Chevella on Sunday morning. Officials suspect that lack of sleep may have led to the accident. They are also checking if it was a case of speeding. According to Chevella police, the accident happened when C Sony, M Pradeep Kumar, Aryavarthan and Akhil â€“ all aged around 19 â€“ were returning to the city from Ananthagiri Hills after an overnight stay. The four are students of different engineering colleges. â€œThe group started their return journey around 7 am and the car was driven by Aryavarthan. He seems to have lost control and hit a tree near the road,â€ a cop told TOI.Sony and Pradeep, who were in the rear seat, died on the spot, while Aryavarthan and Akhil sustained injuries. The two have been shifted to a hospital. Sony was a resident of LB Nagar, whereas Pradeep was from Dharma Reddy colony. The car belongs to Aryavarthan, cops said. Police have registered a case under IPC section 304 a (negligence causing death) against the driver.

Title: 2 brothers killed as car catches fire in Mumbai's Matunga
content: MUMBAI: Two brothers were killed and three other people suffered injuries when a car they were travelling in caught fire in Mumbai early on Monday morning. The incident took place at around 4am on B A Road in Matunga area after the CNG car hit a road divider when the vehicle occupants were going on a joyride after having a party, said officials. "The car suddenly burst into flames and its occupants did not get time to get out of the vehicle," reported news agency PTI quoting a fire official.  Some locals later alerted the fire and police personnel who rushed to the spot. The deceased have been identified as Prem Vaghela (18) and Ajay Vaghela (20), the fire official said. An official from Sion police station said all the car occupants were residents of suburban Mankhurd. They were on way to the Marine Drive in south Mumbai for a joyride after a party, he said. During the incident, both the left side door locks of the car got jammed, due to which the occupants were unable to get out, he said. Car occupant Harsh Kadam (20) suffered 60 to 70 per cent burn injuries, while another occupant Hitesh Bhoir (25) and driver Kunal Attar (25) also received major burn injuries, the fire official said. The fire personnel later doused the flames and rushed the injured persons to the Sion Hospital, he said. The police official said it was a CNG car and as it dashed against the road divider, it caught fire. "On the basis of primary information, we have registered an Accidental Death Report (ADR). Further investigation is underway," the official added.

Title: UP woman taking children to school killed in 'road rage'
content: BIJNOR: A 40-year-old woman who was in a hurry to drop her two children, aged 7 and 10, to school was brutally stabbed to death by her 25-year-old neighbour after she asked him to make way and let her pass on a waterlogged lane barely 100m from her house. The horrific murder took place in front of her children around 7am on Monday at Afzalgarh in Bijnor district.Local residents said Zebunissa got into a brief argument with the accused, Mohammad Sartaj, who quickly went into his house and returned with a knife to attack her even as her children begged him to spare their mother's life. Womanâ€™s death in road rage, 1 held. .AdvertisementAfter repeatedly stabbing Zebunissa, the accused fled the place. Hearing the children cry, a few passersby reached the spot and took the woman to a local hospital but she died on the way. The womanâ€™s body was later sent for post-mortem. Her husband was not at home at the time of the crime.SP (Bijnor) Neeraj Kumar Jadaun said, â€œAn FIR was filed against Sartaj under IPC sections 302 (murder), 504 (intentional insults) and 506 (criminal intimidation) following a complaint by the victim's father Shakir. The accused has been arrested.â€This is the second killing in the district in 48 hours. About 50km away at Dudhali village under Kiratpur police, a man, 30, was beaten to death by his in-laws over a â€œmarital issueâ€. In a widely circulated video on Monday, Asif can be seen being hit by a large brick by a few others. Police said the incident hapened on Saturday and Asif's brothers-in-law Liyakat and Najakat have been arrested.

Title: Two killed in Dibrugarh road accident
content:
Dibrugarh: Two persons were killed and six others were injured in a head-on collision between a car and a truck on NH-37 in Leptakatta area of Dibrugarh district on Sunday. The accident took place at 7pm when the rented car (AS-01-JC 3076) carrying seven passengers (three from Guwahati and four from Sivasagar district) apart from the driver was heading back home after an â€˜annaprasanaâ€™ (rice feeding) ceremony at their relativesâ€™ house at Chabua, 29 km from Dibrugarh. One of the deceased was identified by police as Puspa Surekha Agarwal (65) of Guwahati. The other deceased was the driver of the car, Abu Bakkar Dewan. The three-member family from Guwahati were residents of Hi-Tech Plaza, ABC, Bhangagarh, Guwahati. The others were from Sivasagar town. . .AdvertisementThe injured are being treated at a private hospital in Dibrugarh. They have been identified as Satish Agarwal (45), Pompy Agarwal (42), Krishna Agarwal (25), Nirmal Agarwal (70), Namal Agarwal and Golo Agarwal.

Title: Lightning kills 138 sheep; 3 persons die in rain accidents
content: Kanpur: Around 138 sheep were killed by lightning, and 15 were injured in Unnao district late on Sunday night. Three persons were killed in wall collapse incidents reported from different parts of the district due to continuous rain.Lightning struck a flock of sheep in Muraira village in Nawabganj area on Sunday night.â€œAround 138 sheep were struck by lightning and died. Fortunately, there was no loss of human life as the owner and his family members were sleeping in their house at some distance away from the spot where lightning struck.â€Rajkumar Pal, son of Mangali, a resident of village Muraira, had 153 sheep of which 138 died due to lightning while 15 got injured. Rajkumar said that his main source of livelihood was destroyed causing a huge loss to him. Lekhpal Ashish reached the spot and assessed the loss. He has sent the report to the tehsil. Sub-divisional magistrate Narendra Singh said that according to the loss, a report has been sent to the government and estimated assistance will be provided to the victim. Meanwhile, in Behta village five monkeys died after lightning struck a Peepal tree.. .AdvertisementIn another rain related incident in Devkali village of Bilhaur, a man died after he got buried under the debris of a mud wall which collapsed due to rain on Sunday night. Police reached the spot and sent the body for postmortem. Police station in-charge Premchand Kanojia said that the mud wall collapsed due to continuous rain. In another incident, a 50-year-old man Ram Swaroop died after a mud wall collapsed in Suikheda village of Purva area. The third incident took place in Gajjanfar Nagar of Hasanganj tehsil, where 60-year-old Sundara was sleeping in the room of her kutchcha house late at night when her room collapsed and she got buried under the debris. By the time people reached there to rescue on hearing the sound of collapse, she was already dead. The continuous rain caused loss due to power disruptions and snags. The electricity system in Shuklaganj collapsed due to the heavy rainfall causing inconvenience to residents of many localities of the city. Electrical equipment installed in the houses of localities including Adarsh Nagar, Pony Road got damaged due to power snag.

Title: Accident kills two bikers on Sadar flyover
content:
Nagpur: A rashly driven car claimed two lives on Sadar flyover on Monday. One of the victims fell on the road below from the flyover.The victims, identified as Pawan Zade and Anuttar Shambarkar, were heading towards Mankapur when their two-wheeler had a head-on collision with an oncoming car. Zade and Shambarkar were riding without helmets.It's learnt the impact of the dash was such that Shambarkar was thrown down from the bridge. Eyewitness said he landed near Sadar bus stand and died on the spot.An injured Shambarkar was rushed to Mayo hospital where he succumbed to his injuries. Police said driver of the car, Amol Gadve, was driving rashly under the influence of alcohol. An offense was registered at the Sadar police station and further investigations are underway, the cops told TOI.
