Title: Seven women killed in freak accident in TN
content: TIRUPATTUR: Seven women were killed and 14 others were injured in a freak accident near Natrampalli in Tirupattur district early on Monday morning. Police said 45 people from a village near Ambur in Tirupattur district, who went on a tour to Bengaluru in two vans on September 8, were returning to their hometown. One of the vans carrying 19 passengers, including four men, developed a flat tyre while crossing Sandiyur near Natrampalli on the ChennaiBengaluru National Highway around 3am. The driver stopped the van on the highway and was changing the tyre. The people travelling in the van got down and sat near the median on the road in front of the van. A speeding lorry going in the same direction hit the van from behind and in the impact the van ran over the people sitting near the median and seven women died instantly. All the deceased hailed from Onankuttai village near Pernambut in Vellore district. Fourteen people, including the lorry driver and cleaner, were injured.. .AdvertisementA highway patrol team and a team from the Natrampalli police station rescued the injured with the help of local villagers and took them to government hospitals in Natrampalli, Tiruppattur and Vaniyambadi. The teams also recovered the bodies and sent them to Tiruppattur and Vaniyambadi government hospitals for post-mortem examination. The Natrampalli police registered a case and began an investigation.Public works minister E V Velu and Tiruppattur collector D Baskara Pandian visited the hospitals and met the families of the deceased. Chief minister M K Stalin announced an ex gratia of 1 lakh to each of the families of the seven deceased and 50,000 to each of those injured in the accident.

Title: One killed, two injured in 3 accidents in Trichy district
content:
Trichy: A man was killed and two others were injured in three hit-and-run cases in Trichy district in the past two days. Police said K Gokulan, 50, from Keezha Kondaiyampettai in Trichy was walking on Trichy â€“ Chennai bypass service road around 1am on Sunday. An unidentified vehicle hit him and fled the scene. He sustained severe injuries on the back of his head, ankles and face. Passersby rushed him to the government hospital in Srirangam, but doctors declared him dead. The north traffic wing attached to the city police booked a case and launched an investigation. In another incident, I Murugan, 50 from Thanthonimalai in Karur district was walking on Asath Road at Saveriyarpuram on Saturday night when an unidentified vehicle hit him. He suffered injuries on his left leg and abrasive injuries on left hand. The Vaiyampatti police registered a case. Murugan was admitted to Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Government Hospital (MGMGH) in Trichy. . .AdvertisementSimilarly, P Ravichandran, 58, of Anna Nagar area, was hit by a bike when he was standing on a road near Samathuvapuram on Saturday night. Ravichandran suffered injuries on his left leg. He was admitted to a private hospital in Trichy. The Navalpattu police booked a case. tnn

Title: Car spin after booze bash turns tragic in Mumbai, 2 brothers die
content: MUMBAI: A late-night liquor party in Ghatkopar followed by a plan to go to Marine Drive for a spin ended in the death of two brothers in a car fire triggered by an accident in Sion at 4am on Monday. Ajay Waghela (21) and his brother Pravin (18) were in the speeding car with three friends when it spun out of control and rammed into a divider before catching fire after descending the Sion Circle flyover. The doors and windows got locked and had to be broken open by passers-by who pulled out the driver, Kunal Attar (33), and two others, Harsh Kadam (19) and Ritesh Bhoir (25). The Waghelas could not be saved as the fire had spread by the time the others were pulled out. The last to be taken to Sion Hospital, the siblings were declared dead with 80% to 90% burns. The three who survived are in hospital with serious burn injuries.According to police, all five friends were drunk, and to avoid nakabandis on the Freeway they decided to take Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Road from Sion. The Waghelas were residents of Anushakti Nagar in Mankhurd, and Ajay worked for a private catering concern in a navy canteen.Driver of car lost control after Sion flyover, say copsBrothers Ajay and Pravin Waghela, out for spin with three friends, were killed after the group's car caught fire upon hitting a divider in Sion in the early hours of Monday."Kunal Attar was driving the i20 at a high speed," a police official said. "After getting off the Sion Circle flyover, Attar could not control the car and it rammed into the divider. The car was severely damaged and caught fire."The police filed an accidental death report. They said they had collected blood samples of the injured.The police said the five friends were on a Sunday outing. Their drinking session at a bar in Ghatkopar spilled over into the wee hours.Attar and Ritesh Bhoir were the first to come out of the car. Harsh Kadam was pulled out next by the people outside. The three were taken to Sion Hospital.Ajay Waghela was in the front seat and right behind him was his younger brother. The fire brigade broke open the door on arrival at the spot and pulled out the brothers.

Title: 4 dead as truck falls into gorge due to landslide on Jammu-Srinagar National Highway, vehicular movement suspended
content: BANIHAL/JAMMU: As many as four people were killed when their truck plunged into a deep gorge after being hit by a landslide along the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway early on Tuesday morning. Due to the landslide that hit the highway near Sherbibi in Ramban district at around 5am, vehicular movement was suspended, reported news agency PTI quoting police. A police official said the truck was headed to Srinagar from Jammu. A rescue operation was immediately launched by local volunteers and police, officials said, adding all four bodies were retrieved from the mangled remains of the truck. Officials identified the deceased as truck driver Mohd Afzal Garoo (42), his brother Altaf Garoo (36) of Kulgam, Irfan Ahmed (33) and his brother Showkat Ahmad (29) of Anantnag. Around six cattle, which were being transported in the truck for domestic use, also perished in the accident, they said. The landslide blocked the highway, the only all-weather road linking Kashmir with the rest of the country, and efforts are on to make it traffic-worthy, officials said.

Title: 2 killed in road accident
content:
Ludhiana: Two persons died while one suffered severe injuries as their car crashed into a tree at Moti Nagar. Police said that the accident was caused by overspeeding. The deceased have been identified as Gurmukh Singh and Karamjeet Singh, both aged 35, of Tajpur road while their injured friend is Gaurav of the same area. Karamjeet was driving it. They were passing by a school at high speed when the car crashed into a tree on the roadside. tnn

Title: Rajasthan accident: 12 killed as trailer rams into bus on Jaipur-Agra highway in Bharatpur
content: JAIPUR: As many as 12 people from Gujarat were killed and at least 15 others suffered injuries when a trailer rammed into the bus they were travelling in from behind on Jaipur-Agra highway in Bharatpur district of Rajasthan early on Wednesday morning. The bus was on its way from Gujarat to Mathura in Uttar Pradesh when the accident occurred around 4.30am, reported news agency PTI quoting police. The bus had halted at Antra flyover in the Lakhanpur area when the trailer rammed into it from behind. Mridul Kachawa, Bharatpur SP, said, "The bodies have been taken to a hospital." Gujarat chief minister Bhupendra Patel announced an ex-gratia of Rs 4 lakh each for the next of kin of the deceased and Rs 50,000 for the injured by the state government.

Title: 4 dead, 9 injured in two-vehicle collision in Kanpur
content:
KANPUR: Four people were killed and nine others sustained injuries in a collision between a pickup van and a truck in Uttar Pradesh's Kanpur district on Wednesday, police said. The collision took place at Afzalpur turn in Bidhnu area, they said. The victims were identified as Sadiq (55), his wife Shahnaz (45), Hajra (42), and Golu (4). The bodies of the deceased have been sent for post-mortem examination, police said.Additionally, the nine individuals who sustained injuries in the collision have been promptly admitted to a hospital for medical care.. .Advertisement(With inputs from PTI)

Title: 4 dead in separate road accidents in Meerut
content: MEERUT: Four people were killed in separate road accidents in Meerut district on Monday, said police on Tuesday. In the first incident, Chaman Nath, 50, and his brother Gautam Nath, 35, both labourers, were on their way home to Amargarh village from Meerut court. While riding on the Meerut-Parikshitgarh road, a speeding car struck them from behind. The accused driver fled the scene, abandoning the car. SHO of Kila Parikshitgarh, Chaman Prakash Sharma, said, â€œPolice are in the process of filing an FIR against the driver.â€ In a separate incident in the Hastinapur region, Raju Pahwa, 40, was knocked off his bike by a tempo. After losing control and falling onto the road, another commercial vehicle ran over him. On his way to the hospital, Pahwa's injuries proved fatal. In the third incident, Neetu Kumar, 45, from Lakhwaya village, was struck by a truck while traveling home on Sardhana Road, falling under the jurisdiction of Kankar Khera police. CO Daurala, Abhishek Patel, said, â€œThe victim was declared dead by the doctors. An FIR has been registered under IPC section 304A (causing death by negligence) against the accused truck driver, who is at large.â€

Title: Two killed as speeding truck rams bike in Kanker
content: Raipur: Two youths died in a road accident after a speeding truck rammed into their bike in Kanker late on Tuesday night. Angered by the accident, people blocked the Kanker-Dudhwa road, leading to long traffic jam on both sides.Two youths identified as Tulsi Kunjam and Ajay Jain, who were in their 20s, resided in Pataud village. They were heading towards their home from Karap village. When they reached the Kanker-Dudhawa road, a speeding truck coming from the opposite direction hit them and two died on the spot.On witnessing the accident, the locals gathered and informed the police officials. The truck driver, who tried to flee the spot, was caught by the crowd. By the time the police reached, the angered crowd had blocked the road and did not allow them to take the bodies. This led to long queues of vehicles on both sides of the road. The crowd demanded that the movement of heavy vehicles should be stopped on the state highway in view of increasing accidents.After efforts by the police, the situation was controlled. The bodies were then sent for the postmortem examination around 2AM on Wednesday. The crowd has demanded if their demands are not met in the coming days, they will come out on roads to protest.The truck driver later was taken into custody and his truck was seized. A case of negligent driving was registered against the driver and the investigation is still on, added the police. Due to construction of Nandmara bridge on NH and temporary road being washed away due to rains, heavy vehicles have been diverted. TNN

Title: US Rep. Mary Peltola's husband killed in plane crash in Alaska
content: In a tragic incident, the husband of U.S. Rep. Mary Peltola lost his life in an airplane crash in western Alaska, reported AP. This unfortunate event adds to a series of fatalities involving politicians and their loved ones in Alaska, where flights are common due to the limited road infrastructure.Anton McParland, Mary Peltola's chief of staff, conveyed the heartbreaking news, saying, "We are devastated to share that Maryâ€™s husband, Eugene Peltola Jr. â€” â€²Buzzyâ€™ to all of us who knew and loved him â€” passed away earlier this morning following a plane accident in Alaska."Mary Peltola, who made history as the first Alaska Native elected to Congress in the previous year, was returning home to be with her family during this difficult time. The statement also requested that the family be granted privacy to mourn their loss.The airplane involved in the incident, a Piper PA-18-150 Super Cub, appears to have crashed during takeoff after dropping off a hunter and equipment approximately 65 miles (105 kilometers) northeast of St. Mary's. National Transportation Safety Board chairperson Jennifer Homendy shared this information during a press conference.. .AdvertisementAlaska State Troopers identified Eugene Peltola, 57, as the pilot and the sole occupant of the plane. Two hunters who were present at the scene, though not involved in the crash, provided medical assistance to Peltola.Following the distress signal received by the Alaska Rescue Coordination Center at 8:48 p.m. on Tuesday, an investigation into the circumstances surrounding the crash is underway. The crash site, located approximately 450 miles (724 kilometers) west of Anchorage, is in a remote and mountainous area, according to Homendy.Details about the plane's origin and destination before the crash remain unknown at this time.Eugene Peltola had obtained his commercial pilot's license in 2004, which required the use of corrective lenses for all distances. The plane's FAA registry indicates it was manufactured in 1952, with ownership attributed to Bruce Werba of Holy Cross, Alaska.Werba, along with his wife Connie, operates Alaska Pike Safaris and Wilderness Adventures, offering activities such as trophy brown bear and moose hunts, as well as pike fishing in western Alaska. Connie Werba declined to comment on the incident when contacted by The Associated Press.Condolences poured in for Eugene Peltola, who had previously served as the Alaska regional director for the Bureau of Indian Affairs, worked extensively with the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, served as a city council member in Bethel, and held leadership positions in the Alaska Native community.In a statement, President Joe Biden expressed his condolences after speaking with Mary Peltola, acknowledging Eugene's dedicated public service and highlighting his role as a beloved husband and father.Governor Mike Dunleavy and First Lady Rose Dunleavy also conveyed their deep sadness over the loss of their former neighbor in Kotzebue, western Alaska. They praised Eugene's commitment to public service and his efforts to benefit Alaskans.The Bristol Bay Native Corp. extended sympathies to the congresswoman and her family, describing Eugene as a devoted family man and dedicated servant.U.S. Sen. Lisa Murkowski, in a heartfelt message on the X platform (formerly known as Twitter), expressed her profound grief over the loss of "Buzzy." She emphasized his warmth, generosity, and charm, which endeared him to many Alaskans and his family.Mary Peltola had made history as Alaska's first Native congresswoman, winning the seat in a ranked vote special and general election following the passing of U.S. Rep. Don Young, who had held the position for 49 years. Notably, Alaska has seen several prominent politicians meet tragic ends in plane crashes, including former U.S. Sen. Ted Stevens in 2010 and U.S. Rep. Nick Begich, one of Mary Peltola's predecessors, in 1972.(with inputs from AP)

Title: Nine persons killed, over ten injured in two separate road accidents in Andhra Pradesh's Chittoor
content:
VIJAYAWADA: Nine persons were killed and over 10 others injured in two separate road accidents in Chittoor on Friday morning.An ambulance met with a road accident at Tella Gundlapalli on the Bangalore-Tirupati National Highway in Tavanampally mandal.The ambulance driver who was sleepy, hit a parked oil tanker. Four persons died on the spot and one man was injured. The victims hailing from Orissa are taking their relative back to their hometown in an ambulance after being treated at Bangalore Victoria Hospital. In another incident, people from Belgavi in Karnataka were returning home after visiting Tirumala. Their vehicle collided head-on with a lorry near Matampali Cross on Friday morning. Five persons died on the spot and nine were injured.

Title: 80-year-old woman killed after earth-mover machine hits her in Nagpur
content:
NAGPUR: An 80-year-old woman was killed after being hit by the front loader of an earth-moving machine in Maharashtra's Nagpur city, police said on Friday. The accident took place in Koradi area of Nagpur city, where road works were underway on Wednesday afternoon, an official said. The woman was riding pillion on the motorcycle when the operator lost control of the earth-moving machine, causing the loader to hit her on the head, he said, adding that the motorcyclist was also seriously injured in the incident.A case has been registered under section 304(A) (causing death by negligence) and other relevant provisions of the Indian Penal Code against the machine operator, he added.With PTI inputs

Title: Nine killed in two accidents in Chittoor
content: VIJAYAWADA: Nine people were killed and 10 others suffered injuries in two separate road accidents in undivided Chittoor district on Friday morning.According to the police, an ambulance met with a accident at Tella Gundlapalli on Bengaluru-Tirupati national highway in Tavanampalle mandal. The victims hailing from Odisha were taking their relative back to their home town in an ambulance after being treated at a hospital in Bengaluru. The ambulance driver, rammed a parked oil tanker. Four persons including a woman died on the spot and three others were injured. The injured were rushed to a local government hospital and are undergoing treatment. The deceased were identified as Umesh Chandra Sahoo (46), Trilochan Nayak (63), Rashmita Sahoo (45), and Bijoy Nayak (56).Five of a family died, nine injuredAll are from Kendrapara district in Odisha. In another accident, 14 people from Belgavi in Karnataka were returning home after visiting Tirumala when their vehicle collided head-on with a lorry near Matampali cross road on Friday morning. Five persons died on the spot and nine others were injured. While two persons sustained severe injuries, five others received minor injuries. Two other occupants escaped unhurt. The injured were admitted to Pileru government hospital and later shifted to Ruia government general hospital in Tirupati for better treatment. All of them belong to the same family, the police said. The deceased persons were identified as Hanumanth Jadav (35), Hanumanth Ajur (46), Ambica (14), and Sananda (35).

Title: Indore-Jodhpur bus crashes in heavy rain; 3 killed, 7 hurt
content:
INDORE/UJJAIN: Three persons of a family were killed and seven seriously injured when a sleeper bus, traveling from Indore to Jodhpur, overturned in Ujjain district late Friday night.Heavy rainfall was a factor in the accident, ASP Nitesh Bhargav said. The bus crash on the rainswept road near Farnakhedi, around 75km from Ujjain, on state highway 17, said police.Local residents played a crucial role in the rescue by alerting police immediately and helping evacuate the victims despite the pounding rain. Police teams and a rescue team rushed to the spot from Khachrod, Nagda, Jawra, and Ujjain areas. Asha Bai, 29, Shakuntala Bai, 26, natives of Siddhi Ganj in Ashta, died on the spot. Their relative, Ramcharan, 35, died during treatment in Nagda. He was from Udaipur, Rajasthan. TNN. .AdvertisementPolice deployed at accident site  Shakuntala's husband Anil, 30, is severely injured and being treated in an Ujjain hospital. He was extricated from the wrecked vehicle with great difficulty. The condition of the other injured is stable in hospitals in Jawra and Nagda, said officials.Police were deployed at the accident site to ensure the safety of personal belongings and luggage. The remaining passengers were accommodated on other buses.tnn

Title: Toddler killed, mother and aunt seriously injured as container truck hits two-wheeler in Nagpur
content:
NAGPUR: A 14-month-old child died and his mother and aunt suffered serious injuries after the scooter they were travelling on was hit by a container truck in Maharashtra's Nagpur city, an official said on Sunday. The truck driver escaped from the spot. Atharva Ashish Barmate, his mother Sandhya and her sister Deepali Prakash Patil were on their way to a temple at Takalghat, more than 30 km from the city, when the accident took place on Saturday, the official said. The toddler was in the arms of his mother, while his aunt was riding the scooter. A container truck rammed into their two-wheeler on Wardha road near Jamtha within the limits of Hingna police station, he said. . .AdvertisementThe two women and the child were tossed in the air due to the impact and suffered grievous injuries. While Atharva was taken to the Government Medical College Hospital, his mother and aunt were rushed to AIIMS. However, doctors declared the child dead, said the police. The container driver fled the spot, the official said, adding that a case has been registered under the Indian Penal Code and Motor Vehicles Act.(With PTI inputs)

Title: At least 20 mine staff killed in South Africa road accident
content:
JOHANNESBURG: At least 20 employees of mining giant De Beers have been killed in a road accident in South Africa, local officials said Sunday.The bus involved was ferrying staff from the Venetia mine, one of the biggest diamond mines in the country, said a transport official in Limpopo province, in the north of the country. "The bus came into collision with a lorry," said Vongani Chauke. Chauke told AFP the accident happened at around 1600 GMT, some 25 kilometres (15 miles) from the mine, at the village of Musian on the border with Zimbabwe. The cause of the accident has not been established.. .Advertisement South Africa has one of the most developed road networks on the continent, but also has one of the worst road safety records. The Venetia mine, which lies near the borders with Botswana and Zimbabwe, has been run by the De Beers group for more than 30 years. It accounts for more thatn 40 percent of the country's annual diamond production, employing more than 4,300 staff including many local people.

Title: Libya says four members of Greek rescue team killed in road accident
content: CAIRO: Four members of a Greek rescue team en route to the flood-ravaged city of Derna and three members of a Libyan family were killed in a road accident on Sunday, the health minister for the Libyan eastern government said. Fifteen of the Greek rescue team were injured, including seven in a critical condition, Othman Abduljaleel told a televised news conference. Two of the Libyan family were also critical, he said. The Greek Armed Forces said a bus carrying medical personnel collided with a vehicle moving in the opposite direction, but it did not confirm the deaths. "The causes and circumstances of the incident are being investigated in cooperation with the Libyan authorities, while an operation is underway to gather the personnel in Benghazi and repatriate them," it said in a statement. The Greek foreign ministry was not immediately available to comment on Abduljaleel's statement regarding the killing of four Greeks. According to a diplomatic source, the Greek rescue team had 16 members plus three interpreters. Thousands of people were killed after two dams above Libya's eastern city of Derna broke on Sept. 10 during a powerful storm, bringing down residential blocks lining a usually dry riverbed as people slept. Many bodies have been washed out to sea.

Title: 4 killed as car rams into container in Nashik district
content: NASHIK: Four people were killed in a major accident that occurred near Chandwad town at taluka headquarters in Nashik district, 70km from Nashik, on Monday. BJP corporator from Dhule Municipal Corporation Kiran Ahirrao was among the deceased.The assistant police officer from Vadnerbhairav, Mayur Bhamre, said that the car was heading from Nashik towards Malegaon when it rammed into a moving container from behind, killing all four persons in the car on the spot."The bodies were sent to Chandwad Primary Health Centre for postmortem," the police added. The police said that the car appeared to be traveling at a high speed when the accident occurred, as the car's body was completely damaged. After the incident, the car drifted towards the divider but did not cross over to the opposite lane.The accident led to the stalling of traffic for over an hour as locals rushed to help the victims.The police had to deploy a crane to separate the car from the container and then move it off the road to clear the traffic.

Title: Young and restless account for chunk of road accident victims in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: It isn't surprising that young adults - the group that loves the thrill of speed though it may kill - constitute the largest chunk of victims of road traffic accidents in Delhi. According to data provided by AIIMS Trauma Centre, though the mortality rate appears low at 2-3%, it hides the fact that temporary or permanent disability affects almost 50% of all survival cases.In the first seven months till July this year, there were 3,780 road accident cases involving those in the age group of 21-30 years, with a total of 9,875 victims, including 474 women. Last year, there were 5,436 cases and 15,399 victims, including 674 women, in this age group.According to doctors, the young adults are often victims of accidents because as drivers they are immature, overenthusiastic and excited. They indulge in more rash driving than people in other age groups, increasing their risk of road crashes.. .AdvertisementProfessor Kamran Farooque, head of AIIMS Trauma Centre, told TOI that though the survival rate at the trauma centre is high, almost 50% of the victims suffer post-accident disability, either temporarily or worse, permanently. Of these, 5-10% are unable to return to pre-injury level or functional status.According to the data, after soft tissue and external injuries, the majority of victims suffered lower limb injuries due to the maximum number of two-wheeler accidents reported after soft tissue injuries/external injuries. Till July, of the 3,835 vehicles involved in road accidents, 73.8% were two wheelers. In some cases, the limbs had to be amputated due to severity of injuries and in some cases the victims needed months of recovery to get back to their normal routines. Other injuries that are common in traffic accidents were injuries to the head, face and chest. Till July this year, 77.7% of the victims were from the capital, the rest being referral cases from Uttar Pradesh (10.6 %), Haryana (6.9%), Bihar and other states. Similarly, in 2022, 78.5% of the cases were from Delhi and rest referrals from other states.As per the World Health Organization, road accidents are the 8th leading cause of deaths, with more than 1.3 million deaths and 50 million serious injuries every year. It is the leading cause of death among youths and kids aged five to 29 years, the world body said.Prof Kamran said that the commonest cause of death in trauma cases is bleeding and head injuries. "People should follow the traffic rules. They are for their own safety. The commonest cause of an accident is riding or driving on the wrong side of the road. This both endangers your own life and of those who are on the right side," he said, adding that a helmet is the only protection for two-wheeler riders and every rider should wear one.

Title: Nagaland: 8 killed as SUV falls into gorge after being hit by goods vehicle
content:
KOHIMA: Eight people were killed as an SUV fell into a gorge after being hit by a goods vehicle in Nagaland's Tseminyu district on Wednesday, officials said. The accident happened near K. Station, about 65 km from state capital Kohima, in the early hours, they said. The impact of the crash was such that the truck also slipped off the road and fell into the gorge over the SUV, they added. The SUV was heading towards Mokokchung from Kohima when the accident happened, officials said. . .AdvertisementSeven people died on the spot, while one died on the way to the hospital, they said. An investigation has been started to ascertain the cause of the accident, officials said. Among the victims were three women who recently cleared the Nagaland Staff Selection Board (NSSB) examination and got appointment letters to join government service as grade-3 personnel.(With PTI inputs)

Title: Vendor dies, another hurt after being hit by car; 1 held
content: NEW DELHI: A 30-year-old street vendor was killed while another was seriously injured when a speeding car rammed into them in Shalimar Bagh area of northwest Delhi, police said on Tuesday.The suspect, Rakesh (40), is a native of Bihar and works as a driver. He has been arrested and his blood samples have been sent to ascertain whether he was intoxicated, police said.Following the accident, the injured were rushed to a hospital where Sunil succumbed to his injuries, police said. "The other injured was reported to be in critical condition. The body of the deceased was handed over to his family after post-mortem," said an officer, adding the other victim, Naresh Yadav, was still receiving treatment.Upon receiving a PCR call about the accident at 6.45pm on Sunday, police reached the spot to find the vending cart and cycle of the victims in a damaged condition. "The involved vehicle was also parked on the roadside, with its front damaged due to the intensity of the crash," the officer said.. .AdvertisementWitnesses present at the scene informed police that the accused had been driving the car recklessly and at a high speed, thereby endangering multiple individuals on the road. "He works at a shop and had gone to deliver some items when the incident took place. The accused had fled the spot and was arrested later," the officer added.

Title: 8, including 5 women, killed in Nagaland road accident
content:
Dimapur: Eight people, including five women, were killed when a Mokokchung-bound passenger vehicle was reportedly hit by a truck in the K Station area of Nagaland's Tseminyu district on Wednesday morning. Both the vehicles fell into a gorge after the collision. While seven people died on the spot, another one succumbed to his injuries on way to hospital in Kohima. All the deceased, including the driver, were occupants of the passenger vehicle. Three of the deceased women are from Mokokchung district. The driver of the truck and its two other occupants have been arrested. Chief minister Neiphiu Rio, deputy chief ministers TR Zeliang and Y Patton have expressed their grief at the tragic accident. "I am deeply saddened by the tragic road accident in Tseminyu district where eight precious lives have been lost," Rio said on 'X', (formerly Twitter).

Title: Two women among three dead in separate hit-&-run incidents in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: Three people, including two women, died in separate cases of hit-and-run in the city.A 38-year-old woman, Humra Ansari, died and her husband Irshad (38) was injured after their scooter was hit by a truck in north Delhi's Kashmere Gate on Wednesday night, police said.The deceased's husband, who runs a spectacle shop, said that they were returning to their home in JJ Colony Madipur from an acquaintance's house, when the truck hit their scooter from behind and fled in the direction of Sadar Bazaar.After receiving a call about the accident around 11pm, police rushed to find the damaged scooter at the spot and to be informed that the injured had been shifted to a hospital.. .AdvertisementIn the second incident, a 58-year-old woman died, and her husband was injured after being hit by a car on September 19 while crossing the road in Alipur area, police said. The deceased, Laxmi Devi, lived with her family in Swarn Jayanti Vihar in Narela. Her husband, Bhole Shankar (60), was receiving treatment.The victims' son, Bittu Kumar, told police the accident occurred around 11.20pm when he was looking for an autorickshaw to go to his sister's residence in Pratap Nagar. "My father told me that they were crossing the road when a white car hit them," the complainant said, adding that they were taken to a hospital in a car and his mother died during treatment.In another case, a 24-year-old biker was killed, and a woman and her child, who were riding pillion, sustained injuries after their bike was hit by a truck in Fatehpur on September 17. The deceased Randhir lived in Bhati Mines area. Complainant Mamta, the deceased's neighbour, and her four-year-old son were injured in the accident.The complainant said that they were going to a market in Fatehpur, adding "When we were near the T point, our motorcycle was hit by the truck. The accused fled the spot".As the victims fell on the road, people present there helped them. "They stopped the autorickshaw, and we were being taken to the hospital, and in the meantime, my husband reached the spot," Mamta added. Randhir was declared dead at the hospital.

Title: Cousins killed in hit-and-run incident in Ambalaâ€™s Shahzadpur
content:
AMBALA: Two cousins hailing from Bihar died in a hit-and-run incident near Kakkar Majra village under Shahzadpur police station area of Ambala district on Saturday.The deceased were identified as Gandhi and Sita Ram, both residents of Gangti village of Sheikhpura district in Bihar. The worked as brick kiln labourers in Ambala.The bodies of the deceased have been kept by the police at the mortuary of the civil hospital in Naraingarh.The police have initiated an investigation and started scanning the CCTV cameras of eateries and petrol pumps near the accident spot.. .AdvertisementThe police said post-mortem examination of the deceased would be conducted after completing the paperwork and taking statement of family members.

Title: School principal, girl student killed in bus-truck collision in Rajasthan's Barmer
content: BARMER: In a tragic incident, a school principal and a student were killed after the bus they were travelling in rammed into a dumper parked on roadside in Rajasthan's Barmer district on Saturday night, reported news agency ANI quoting police.The deceased principal has been identified as Mohammad Ibrahim while the girl student as Savina.This incident took place at around 8pm near Sehlau village.According to officials, the passengers onboard included 24 girl students from Swami Vivekananda Government Model School in Detani and four others, including the school principal and three teachers."They were returning after a victorious tournament organised in Raniwara," they said.Officials said that the bus driver couldn't see a dumper truck that was parked on the side of Bharat Mala Road near Sehlau village and ended up ramming into it."The exact reason behind the accident is, however, not clear," they said.Police said that the truck was parked with its parking lights switched off, which led to the bus driver not noticing the vehicle, resulting in a collision.The accident was so severe that the conductor's side of the bus sustained extensive damage, and the school principal, who was seated there, died on the spot.Local residents in the area heard the sound of the crash and rushed to the scene, calling for an ambulance.Upon receiving information, a police team also arrived at the scene and, with the assistance of the villagers, shifted those injured in the accident to the hospital."20 of them were seriously injured and taken to Chauhatan Hospital, while nine others have been sent to Gagariya Hospital," they said.While the school principal died on the spot, and the girl student died during treatment."Students whose condition is critical have been referred to Jodhpur for treatment," ADM Anjum Tahir Samma said. (ANI)

Title: Rash driving by private bus causes yet another accident
content: Kochi: In yet another case of rash driving by private buses in the city, a bus rammed into the rear part of another bus near Vyttila Metro station on Saturday evening injuring 19 passengers.Safa, a private bus plying between Vyttila hub and Panangad, hit another bus, Cherupushpam, operating between Vyttila hub and Vaikom around 5.30pm. According to Maradu police, the bus to Vaikom had stopped on the roadside to allow a passenger to board the vehicle when Safa, which was speeding after leaving the hub, tried to overtake it. Seeing an autorickshaw coming from the opposite direction, Safa driver tried to negotiate the autorickshaw and hit Cherupushpam bus. Passengers in both the buses suffered minor injuries. Enraged over the rash driving, autorickshaw drivers in the area allegedly tried to manhandle the driver of Safa. â€œWe will collect CCTV visuals in the area to ascertain the reason for the accident,â€ said a police officer. A case has been registered against the bus driver, Jomes, under Sections 279, 337 and 338 of the IPC for causing hurt to passengers by doing any act rashly or negligently as to endanger human life and negligent driving.Vyttila division councillor Sunitha Dixon said rash driving by vehicles is not a new issue. â€œThere were instances where two-wheeler drivers were thrown away from the vehicle. It is a narrow road filled with potholes and drivers have to take utmost care while plying on the stretch. Saturdayâ€™s accident was caused by rash driving and thatâ€™s why the driver could not control the vehicle. However, the drivers are going to blame the potholes,â€ she said. On September 6, Abhijith (25), a scooter rider, was killed after his vehicle was hit by a private bus from behind at Madhava Pharmacy Junction. A two-wheeler driver had died in a similar accident at the spot in February.The high court had last year directed the authorities to implement restrictions on autorickshaws and private buses in the city limits.Police have been conducting special drives occasionally to curb rash driving by vehicles, but in vain. The motor vehicles department has failed to put an end to rash driving despite such fatal accidents.An MVD official said many private bus operators have become more aggressive to make their services profitable unlike in the past when the operators were friendly with other operators in the sector. â€œWe donâ€™t have enough manpower to rein in the stakeholders in the sector. If we try take strong action against them, they go to court and manage to secure stay order on it. Considering the traffic congestion in the city, we donâ€™t take action against private bus operators for failing to reach destinations in the allotted time,â€ said an official with the Ernakulam RT office.

Title: Five killed as car rams into tree in Madhya Pradesh's Umaria
content: BHOPAL: Five people were killed after their car rammed into a tree in Umaria district of Madhya Pradesh on Monday.The incident took place near Majhgawan village on national highway 43, under Ghunghuti police outpost between 12.30am and 1am.The deceased were identified as Pushpendra Tripathi, who was currently serving as a mining inspector in Shahdol, Avnish Dubey, who was currently holding the position of manager in public services management in Shahdol, Prakash Jagat, who was posted at the district panchayat in Shahdol, Dinesh Sariwan, who was working as a sub-engineer in MNREGA at Gopahru in Shahdol, and Amit Shukla, who is reported to be a relative of Pushpendra Tripathi and a resident of Rewa district, said police.â€œAll of the deceased are aged between 35 and 40 years. The car was going towards Shahdol and instead of taking a turn near Majhgawan village it rammed into a tree. All the occupants of the car are dead hence it cannot be exactly confirmed where they had come and where they were going investigations are on. We received information from the control room as some passerby had informed us about it and our teams rushed to the spot. Of the five, three had died on the spot but Avinsh Dubey and Amit Shukla were alive and rushed to the hospital in Shahdol. They died during treatmentâ€, Ghunghuti police outpost incharge, Shailendra Chaturvedi said.. .Advertisement

Title: 5, including an infant, killed, 6 hurt as bus hits tempo in Dausa
content: JAIPUR: Five people, including an infant, were killed and at least six others injured after a speeding bus of Rajasthan State Road Transport Corporation (RSRTC) collided head-on with a tempo and also ran over some pedestrians in Dausa district on Sunday.The incident occurred around 11:30am on the Mahwa-Hindaun Highway when the passengers of the tempo were returning from Kaila Devi temple in Karauli to Mahwa. The bus also hit some pedestrians, killing three of them, including the infant, on the spot. Police said two people travelling in the tempo died.The deceased pedestrians were identified as Mangti Jogi, 22, a resident of Gohandi village, his one-and-a-half-year-old son Priyanshu Jogi, and Devkinandan Jogi, 36, a resident of Mahwa.Passengers of tempo were returning after temple visitThe deceased occupants of the auto-rickshaw were identified as its driver Aashik Ali, a native of Karauli, and passenger Gulab Devi, 35, a resident of Panna in Madhya Pradesh. While the passengers of the tempo were returning after the temple visit at Hindaun City in Karauli to Mahwa, the pedestrians were walking in a religious procession when the bus hit them, said police. â€œAll the injured people were rushed to a government hospital at Mahwa, where the doctors declared five of them dead. Four seriously injured people were referred to Jaipur,â€ said a police officer. A complaint was registered against the bus driver, who fled after the accident, and a search for him started, he added. The stretch of the highway between Jaipur and Bharatpur has seen several fatal accidents recently. On Saturday, three people, including a young couple, were killed when their car collided with a speeding Bolero near Kaimasi village in Bharatpur district. About 11 pilgrims from Gujarat died after a speeding truck hit a private bus in Bharatpur on September 13.

Title: 3 killed in collision between two trucks in UP's Amethi
content: LUCKNOW: Three people were killed after a tipper truck and a truck collided head-on in Jagdishpur area of Amethi district on Tuesday.The drivers of both vehicles and a helper died in the accident.Police said that the deceased were identified as: Manoj Mishra (35), Pradeep Chaubey (47), and Sunil Kumar (25).Police said that the truck was going to Banda to bring construction material via Ayodhya when it collided with a tipper truck coming from the opposite direction on the Rae Bareli Highway in the Jagdishpur Kotwali area.. .AdvertisementThe horrific collision, which took place around 3 am in the night, created chaos. Nearby villagers rushed to the spot and informed the police.The police reached the spot on receiving information about the accident and rescued the three bodies from the mangled vehicles with great difficulty.SHO, Jagdishpir, Rakesh Singh said that cranes were used to remove the trucks and the highway has been cleared for movement."The kin of the deceased have been informed and post-mortem examinations will be conducted on the bodies soon," he said. Singh added that prima facie it appears that the accident took place due to speeding.

Title: 70-year-old man among three dead in separate road accidents in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: Three people, including a 70-year-old man, died in separate road accidents. In the first case, a 40-year-old man was killed and four people were injured after a truck rammed a tractor trolley in Shastri Park on Monday. The identity of the deceased was yet to be ascertained. The remaining four, Anil (40), Hari Lal (30), Saleem (55), and Aman (17), were undergoing treatment. "All the five victims were sitting on the tractor. The person who died had taken a lift from Kashmere Gate ISBT," police said. A case was registered in this regard and both the vehicles were seized.In the second incident, a 70-year-old man died and a mother-son duo were injured after they were hit by an SUV in Rohini. The deceased was identified as Mohammad Yunus, whereas the two injured, Swarne Arora (63) and her son Kamal (37), were receiving treatment.. .AdvertisementThe incident took place at 6.37 am on Monday near the E-block area of Rohini Sector I. Cops detained the driver, Rishab Singh, a resident of Budh Vihar, and sent his blood sample for a test, which will ascertain whether he was drunk.The third incident was reported from Mukherjee Nagar, wherein a 50-year-old man died and another was injured after their car was hit by a truck. The incident took place on September 23. The deceased was identified as Alok, whereas the injured, Ankit, was receiving treatment. The truck driver who was allegedly driving rashly at the time of the incident has been arrested. ea

Title: Koyambedu sees three accidents; 3, including an elderly man, killed
content: CHENNAI: Three people, including an elderly man and a college student, died in different road accidents at Koyambedu on Sunday night.A youth died and his friend suffered severe injuries when their bike was hit by a speeding truck on Sunday night.The victim, Abishek, 20, of Nerkundram, was riding the bike with his friend Jagadish, 20, of Maduravoyal on the pillion. Abishek was employed at a private firm and Jagadish was studying at a private college in Velappanchavadi. When they reached Koyambedu, a truck carrying a load of bricks hit their bike in which both were thrown off. They were rushed to the government kilpauk medical college and hospital where Jagadish succumbed to injuries and Abishek is undergoing treatment.In another accident at Koyambedu on Sunday night, a 43-year-old load auto driver died and the cleaner escaped with injuries when a speeding truck hit them.The deceased Syed Moosa of Nellore was carrying a chicken load along with cleaner Abdul Rehman, 20, when a speeding truck hit the vehicle from behind. In the impact, the vehicle toppled in which Syed Moosa died on the spot and Abdul Rehman escaped. Koyambedu traffic investigation caught hold of the truck driver Saravanan, 30. In the third incident, also near Koyambedu market, an elderly man died when an electric bus hit him while he was crossing the road. He died on the spot. The deceased is yet to be identified. The employees at Koyambedu market complained to police that private buses and trucks were speeding on the narrow stretch resulting in accidents.

Title: Maharashtra: Auto falls into well in Jejuri; 3 killed, 2 survive after spending night inside
content:
PUNE: A newly-wed couple from Dhayari and one of their relatives (17) drowned after the autorickshaw they were travelling in veered off the road and fell into a farm well at Goteshwar Mala on Saswad-Jejuri road, about 50km from the city, on Monday night.The groom's aunt, Sheetal Sandip Shelar (38), and the auto driver, Aditya Gholap (22), survived miraculously after spending the night in the 20ft-deep well. Two passersby heard their cries for help around 6.15am on Tuesday and rescued them. The couple - Rohit Shelar (23) and Vaishnavi (18) - got married just two days ago. They were returning home after performing some rituals at the Khandoba temple in Jejuri. Rohit's aunt, Sheetal, and her daughter, Shravani, had accompanied the couple to the temple. They were going back home in the auto driven by Gholap, of Dhayari, when the accident occurred.

Title: Kancheepuram Congress president killed as truck rams into eatery
content: CHENNAI: The Kancheepuram district Congress president was killed and two others suffered injuries in a road accident when a goods carrier ran into them at a roadside eatery in Kamarajapuram near Tambaram on Monday night.Alavur Nagaraj, 57, who was also the Walajabad union councillor, was having food at the eatery along with a few others when the accident took place. On Monday night, he took part in a public programme and accompanied Congress leader K S Alagiri to his residence. Nagaraj was returning home in his car when he stopped the vehicle to have dinner.Three others, who came in the truck, parked their vehicle near the shop and had food at the eatery. Police said one of them, who was drunk, started the vehicle and drove into the eatery, killing Nagaraj on the spot. Police said Udhaya Seelan, 23, who drove the vehicle at the time of accident had not driven before. Kumar, 46, owner of the eatery and Amavasai, 40, friends of Nagaraj were injured. They were rushed to the Chromepet government hospital.Pallikaranai traffic investigation wing has registered a case and arrested two people. Meanwhile, Nagaraj's son Gautham has lodged a complaint with the police alleging that there is suspicion in his father's death. Police checked the CCTV footage. After a detailed probe, the police confirmed that it was a clear case of accident.

Title: All within 4 hours: 3 bikers killed in 3 hit-and-run crashes, 2 on Delhi-Gurgaon Expressway
content: GURGAON: Three bike riders succumbed to the injuries they had sustained in three separate accidents - two on the Delhi-Gurgaon Expressway and another on the Pataudi-Rewari road - all within a span of four hours late on Sunday night.In all the three crashes, the drivers of the vehicles that hit the motorcycles fled the spot. Cases have been registered, but no arrests have been made as yet, police said on Tuesday.The first accident was reported near DLF-2 on NH-8 around 9pm on September 24.Anurag Singh (38), who belongs to Bihar's Begusarai, was driving when his bike was struck by a speeding car on the highway. His cousin Ram Singh told cops at the DLF-2 police station that Anurag was rushed to Max Hospital and later referred to Safdarjung Hospital, where he died of his injuries on Monday.Gurugram Rolls-Royce accident CCTV video comes out: Footage challenges initial police report"Unfortunately, despite the medical attention he received, Anurag couldn't survive, and he passed away on Monday. Hospital staff informed me that Anurag was traveling from Delhi towards Jaipur via Gurgaon on his bike when a speeding car collided with his bike," Ram, a Faridabad resident, said in the complaint.ASI Manoj of DLF-2 police station said the autopsy report concluded that Anurag died of head trauma. "There were no CCTV cameras near the accident site. We are inquiring about the crash. Thousands of vehicles use NH-8 every day, making it challenging for us to investigate hit-and-run cases," the ASI said.An hour later some 8-9km away, a truck allegedly hit a motorcycle on which 20-year-old Upanshu Singh was riding pillion with his friend Himanshu Bajaj near the Jharsa flyover. Police said Upanshu died on the spot, and Himanshu sustained some cuts and scratches to his arms and legs.Upanshu's father Ram Pal Singh said in his complaint that the 20-year-old had left home in the city around 10pm that night. "But he did not return and calls were unresponsive. On Monday, we learned around 3pm that Upanshu had been found dead," he said.ASI Pavindra of Sadar police station said the truck was being driven rashly."The truck driver fled the scene. We are currently investigating the matter, and Himanshu is undergoing treatment at Tirath Ram Hospital on Basai Road," the ASI said.The third crash of the day occurred around 11.30pm near Moinabad village on the Pataudi-Rewari road, police said. Umesh (23), a resident of Farrukhnagar, was traveling from Pataudi to Rewari with his friends Harikesh and Jitendra on his Splendor when a green-coloured loading autorickshaw collided with their bike head-on. All the riders fell to the ground while the auto driver fled. Umesh fractured his leg, Harikesh has head and leg injuries, and Jitendra fell unconscious after bleeding from his head. "Umesh managed to flag down a private vehicle and took help to reach the Civil Hospital in Rewari for medical attention. Jitendra succumbed to his injuries during treatment. We are currently investigating the matter," said ASI Man Singh of the Farrukhnagar police station.FIRs for all three accidents were registered under sections of 279 (rash driving), 304 A (death by negligence) and 338 (causing grievous hurt due to negligence) of IPC.

Title: 4 techies killed as car hits bus in Karnataka
content:
MYSURU: Four techies who were heading back to Bengaluru after a holiday in Udupi were killed after their car rammed into a Karnataka State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) bus on Wednesday morning, at Bellur cross in Mandya district of Karnataka.The deceased are Namita N, 24, a resident of Bendiganahalli in Bengaluru, Vamshi Krishna, 25, from Hosakote, Bengaluru, Pankaj Sharma, 24 from Rajasthan, and Raghunath Bhajantri, 26, a resident of Dharwad. Bhajantri was reportedly at the wheel when the accident occurred Namitha and Raghunath Bhajanthri worked with Accenture and Pankaj and Vamsi with Infosys.The KSRTC bus was parked by the roadside at the time of the accident.Police said that the Swift car was speeding and the driver rammed into the bus, killing all four on the spot.. .Advertisement

Title: Teacher killed, 20 students hurt in state as bus overturns
content:
KORAPUT: A physical education teacher (PET) was killed and 20 students were injured when their bus overturned on the Chandahandi ghat road in Nabarangpur district on Wednesday. The accident occurred when 40 students and several teachers of different schools were travelling from Umerkote to Chandahandi for a district-level sports event.The deceased was identified as Prabhas Kumar Jena, a teacher of Swami Vivekanand Vidya Mandir, Umerkote. After preliminary treatment at Jharigaon community health centre, five grievously injured students were referred to the district headquarters hospital.Senior administration officials and police rushed to the spot and transported the injured students to the CHC. "Condition of the injured students is stable and an investigation into the circumstances leading to the accident has been initiated," said collector (Nabarangpur) Kamal Lochan Mishra.

Title: Bihar: 3 crushed to death by truck on Nawada-Gaya road
content:
NAWADA: Three persons were killed and one was injured when a speeding truck run over them on Nawada-Gaya road near Government Agriculture Farm here on Thursday. The victims were identified as Akash Chouhan, Nandu Chauhan and Prahlad Chauhan, all residents of ward 36 under Bhadauni panchayat in Nawada district. The seriously injured man, Praduman Chauhan, was undergoing treatment at sadar hospital. According to police, the incident occurred when the victims were on way to a vegetable market at Nawada. "The driver of the truck managed to escape with the vehicle after the accident. Efforts are on to arrest him," police added. "A Nawada-bound speeding truck knocked us down," Praduman said at the hospital.Nawada BDO Anjani Kumar handed over a cheque for Rs 20,000 as ex gratia to the next of the kin of each of the deceased.

Title: Speeding car mows down labourer sleeping on footpath in Bhubaneswar
content: BHUBANESWAR: A daily labourer sleeping on a footpath at Raj Mahal Square in Bhubaneswar was mowed down by a speeding early on Saturday. The deceased was identified as Dinabandhu Baral (45) from Pipili area in Puri district, police said.Following the hit-and-run incident, the driver drove his car into a river, about 5km from the accident spot, and escaped. Dhauli police seized the car. A manhunt has been launched to arrest the driver."The vehicle is registered in the name of one Bikash Pal of Kakatpur area in Puri district. We will find out whether Pal or someone else was driving the car when the accident took place around 1am. We will question Pal, who could not be traced in his village. We came to know that he lives in Bhubaneswar. Efforts are on to trace him,â€ said inspector-in-charge of Capital police station Padmanabh Pradhan. Police found a cheque book in the name of Bikash Pal in the car.Examining the CCTV cameras, police said the car came from AG Square towards Raj Mahal Square at breakneck speed around 1am. The car suddenly veered off the road and steered over the footpath at Raj Mahal where the labourer was sleeping. A few other homeless persons were also sleeping on the footpath but at a distance from the accident spot.. .Advertisementâ€œI woke up with a loud sound. I found that the car dragged the man some distance and fled after crushing him under its wheels. We shouted, but nobody was around to help late in the night. We informed the police,â€ said Jharana Bag, an eyewitness.Police rushed the injured man to Capital Hospital where he was declared dead upon arrival.

Title: Bike hits cash van, couple & son in-law killed in Odisha's Keonjhar district
content:
KEONJHAR: A couple and their son in-law were killed after the bike in which they were travelling in collided with a cash van near Padmapur gate Chowk on the Anandpur-Karanjia road in Keonjhar district on Friday.The deceased are identified as Sahadev Behera (42), his wife Chandini Behera (38) and their son in-law Abhiram Behera (20) of Kalabila village under Gohira panchayat of Ghasipura police station in the district.Bike & van seized, cops to take further action Police said the accident happened when the three deceased were going to Panasadiha village from Anandpur. At about noon, suddenly a cash van going ahead of the bike slowed down while giving way to a truck coming from the opposite side. This led the bike to dash into the cash van from behind. The trio fell down on the road due to the impact and sustained critical injuries. They were shifted to Anandpur sub-divisional hospital, where they were declared dead. . .AdvertisementDebaki Nayak, inspector in-charge of Anandpur police station, said, "Both the bike and van were seized. After completion of investigation, further action will be taken."
