Title: Eight tourists dead as bus falls into gorge in Nilgirison Ooty-Mettupalayam ghat road
content: UDHAGAMANDALAM: Eight tourists from Tenkasi were killed when their bus fell into a gorge near the ninth hairpin bend near Marapalam on the Ooty-Mettupalayam ghat road on Saturday.Police said the driver lost control while driving downhill as the brakes failed and the bus fell into a 50-foot gorge. The road was slippery as it had rained all day and visibility had been affected by thick mist, police said.A group of 57 people from Tenkasi had come to Ooty and Coonoor on a day's trip on Saturday morning. The accident happened when they were returning home in the evening.Fire and rescue service personnel and police rushed to the spot and used ropes and stretchers to pull up passengers to the road. . .Advertisement All the rescued passengers were taken to the Coonoor Government Lawley Hospital.Eight passengers were declared dead at the hospital, while four are in a serious condition. The deceased were identified as V Nithin, 15, S Babykala, 36, S Murugesan, 65, P Muppidathy, 67, R Kowsalya, 29, Devikala, 42, Jaya, 50,, and Thangam, 64. All were from Kadayam in Tenkasi.Nilgiris collector M Aruna and senior police officials supervised the rescue and relief operations. A case has been registered with the Coonoor town police station. Inquiry is underway, said police.

Title: 10 Cuban migrants killed in Mexico truck accident, 17 injured
content: At least 10 Cuban migrants died and 17 others were seriously injured when a cargo truck ferrying them had an accident in the southern Mexican state of Chiapas, Mexico's migration institute said on Sunday. The accident took place along the Pacific coast stretch of the Pijijiapan-Tonala highway in Chiapas, a common route taken by migrants who cross into Mexico from Guatemala on their way north towards the United States. The driver of the Ford truck, which is not designed to carry so many people, fled the scene of the accident, the National Migration Institute (INM) said in a statement. "According to the first reports, the driver was speeding, lost control of the unit and overturned," the INM added.. .Advertisement Images shared by authorities showed a truck without number plates turned on its side in a ditch by the highway, with half of the wooden bed and the tall side panels on its right side torn off. The 17 injured people were taken to hospitals and are being monitored, INM said. One of the dead migrants was a child. Road accidents involving migrants are not uncommon in Mexico, where many people crossing the country to the United States travel in unauthorized and poorly maintained vehicles. In December 2021, at least 54 people believed to be of mostly Central American descent were killed when the truck they were in flipped over in Chiapas.

Title: Maharashtra: Leopard dies in accident on Samruddhi highway near Igatpuri
content: NASHIK: A three-and-a-half-year-old male leopard was killed in an accident on Saturday evening on the Samruddhi highway near Igatpuri in Nashik district.The incident took place at around 9.30pm when the big cat was crossing the highway and got hit by an unknown vehicle. A forest department official said the leopard died on the spot."We sent the carcass for post-mortem following which the cremation was carried out as per the laid down procedure,â€ the forest official said.This is the second incident about the leopard that died in an accident in the last one month.. .Advertisementâ€œIn the last two years, around 15 leopards have died in accidents on roads at various locations in the district,â€ the official said.

Title: Kannada actor drives car into couple, kills woman; held
content: BENGALURU: A 48-year-old woman died and her husband was severely injured after a car driven by Kannada actor Nagabhushana NS allegedly hit them in Bengaluru on Saturday night.Nagabhushana was charged under various sections, including rash driving and causing death by negligence, after Prema S died and husband Krishna B, 58, was wounded in the crash on Vasanthapura Main Road. The actor was arrested but got bail from the police station.Police said Nagabhushana, who is in his late thirties, was â€œnot found drunkâ€ at the time of the crash. But DCP (traffic south) Shiva Prakash Devaraj said the accident occurred because of negligent driving by the actor.Nagabhushana claimed that the couple â€œsuddenly entered the road from a footpathâ€ and he was â€œtaken abackâ€. He also said that he rushed the couple with some others to a nearby hospital but Prema could not be saved.. .AdvertisementPolice filed the case on a complaint by Premaâ€™s son Partha K, a software engineer. Krishna B, his injured father, is an autorickshaw driver.

Title: Kannada actor arrested after his car mows down woman
content: BENGALURU: A 48-year-old woman was killed and her husband sustained severe injuries after a car driven by Kannada film actor Nagabhushana NS allegedly hit them on Vasanthapura main road in south Bengaluru on Saturday night.Police identified the deceased as Prema S and her injured husband as Krishna B, 58, an autorickhaw driver, and a resident of Vasanthapura.According to police, the accident occurred around 9.45pm near the couple's house in Vasanthapura. Nagabhushana claimed he rushed the injured persons to the hospital, but Prema could not be saved.Police have registered a case against Nagabhushana based on a complaint filed by Prema's son Partha K, a software engineer. KS Layout traffic police arrested and released the actor on station bail. Police may summon him for further probe.. .Advertisement DCP (traffic south) Shiva Prakash Devaraj told reporters that the accident occurred due to rash and negligent driving by the suspect. Police said preliminary investigation revealed that there were road humps near the accident spot and suspect rash and negligent driving might have been the main reason behind the accident. Police said the driver was not found drunk at the time of the accident.Nagabhushana told police he was returning to his JP Nagar residence from RR Nagar when the accident occurred. "A woman and a man suddenly entered the road from a footpath. I was taken aback and hit them. The vehicle stopped after hitting a roadside electric pole. I noticed the injured woman was not in the condition of speaking and the man was conscious. Passers-by and I rushed them to a hospital, where doctors declared her brought dead. The injured man was shifted to another hospital for treatment. Later, I came to know that the deceased was Prema and the injured was her husband Krishna. I was driving my car when the accident occurred," Nagabhushana said in his statement to police later in the night.Nagabhushana has played supporting roles in recent films like Kousalya Supraja Rama, Nodi Swami Ivanu Irode Heege and Daredevil Musthafa. He had played the lead role in Ikkat, drawing acclaim.

Title: 5 labourers crushed to death by truck in Maharashtra's Buldhana district
content: BULDHANA: Five labourers were killed and as many others injured after being run over by a truck on a highway in Maharashtra's Buldhana district on Monday, police said. The accident took place at around 5.30 am when the labourers were sleeping in makeshift huts along the side of a service road being constructed in Wadner Bholji village of the district, a senior police official told PTI. A truck plying on the road ran over the labourers, he said. Three persons died on the spot and two others succumbed to injuries in hospital, the official said. . .AdvertisementFive persons received injuries in the incident, he said. The truck driver is absconding, the official said, adding an investigation is on into the incident.(With PTI inputs)

Title: Speeding BMW kills CISF jawan at Mumbai airport's T2 checkpost, teen held
content: MUMBAI: A Central Industrial Security Force constable, Rahul Sharma (35), succumbed to head injuries a day after a speeding car knocked him down at a checkpost on Mumbai airport's elevated road early Sunday. Sahar police arrested Hruday Sajjanraj Kawar, a 19-year-old college student from Vile Parle (W) who was driving his father's BMW, for the crash.The incident happened around 4am when Sharma and a head constable were on duty at checkpost No. 1 on the elevated road. Kawar, along with four friends all aged 18-19, took the elevated road to pick up a friend, who had arrived from abroad, from the departure area-vehicles are not allowed in the arrival area. Kawar did not stop at the checkpost and as he allegedly tried to speed away, he knocked down a plastic barricade and then hit Sharma.A medical examination of Kawar showed no trace of alcohol, police sources said.Teen driver claims didnâ€™t notice airport barricades. .AdvertisementA 19-year-old college student held for fatally knocking down a CISF constable at a checkpost of Mumbai international airport told police he failed to notice the barricades as it was dark. Hruday Kawar was driving his father's BMW when he allegedly knocked down Rahul Sharma (35) on Sunday around 4am. "The accused and his friends had planned to go for his birthday celebration after picking up the friend," said a cop. "The accused has a valid driving licence." Kawar's father is the director of a textile company.CISF constable Ramesh Kumar said: "Sharma was thrown away by the impact and sustained serious head injuries." Head constable Mohammed Rehman, who was with Sharma at check post number 1, detained the five teens and called the CISF control room. By then CISF inspector Pankaj Kumar and his team rushed Sharma to SevenHills Hospital. He died on Monday morning. "The accused was initially booked under IPC sections 279 (rash driving) and 338 (grievous hurt by act endangering life), and under the Motor Vehicles Act Section 184 (dangerous driving). But as the CISF constable has died, IPC Section 304 (A) (causing death due to negligence) will be added to the FIR," said zonal DCP Dikshit Gedam.Sharma moved into the CISF quarters in Kharghar with his wife, six-year-old daughter and four-year-old son after he got posted at Mumbai airport in March. He was earlier at New Delhi's IGI airport. "His body will flo-wn to his village in Madhya Pradesh where the final rites will be perform-ed after giving the guard of honour," said a CISF official.

Title: Two women killed in separate road accidents in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: Two women have died in separate hit-and-runs reported within 24 hours. Several police teams have been tasked to trace the errant drivers involved in the accidents.In the first accident, a speeding truck hit two sisters riding a scooty in Khajuri Khas area, killing a 31-year-old woman on the spot and injuring the other, police said.Police received a call regarding the accident around 10pm on Monday. "The woman, Priya (31), had been declared dead on arrival by the hospital, while her sister was undergoing treatment. A case under Section 304A (death due to negligence) of the IPC has been registered," a police officer said. Police said that the women were returning home on a two-wheeler when a brick-laden truck hit them from behind, crushing one of them to death. "We have seized the truck left behind by the driver after the accident. The CCTV cameras installed around the accident site are being scanned to nab the suspect," the officer added. . .AdvertisementIn the second accident, a Punjab Roadways bus hit a 27-year-old woman inside ISBT. The police have arrested the accused driver, Inderjit Singh. According to the police, the victim, Indu, along with her family had reached ISBT around 10am to board a bus. "The victim was proceeding towards a toilet when the speeding bus hit her. Injured Indu was rushed to a hospital but she succumbed to her injuries on Tuesday," a police officer said.The accidents involving commercial vehicles have seen a rise of late. The traffic police's drive to check on them during the night seems to have changed little on the ground.

Title: 3 killed as bus collides with trailer in Rajasthan's Bharatpur
content: JAIPUR: Three persons, including the bus driver, were killed when a bus collided with a stationary trailer on NH21 in Bharatpur district of Rajasthan on Tuesday night. The victims have been identified as Kamlesh (bus driver), aged 40, the helper Vijendra Singh, aged 40, and Bunty, aged 22. Two seriously injured persons were subsequently referred to Jaipur for treatment.The police team reached the spot, extracting injured passengers and transporting them to the hospital for treatment. Vinod Meena, the station house officer (SHO) of Chiksana police station, said "The incident occurred around 2am on Tuesday night. Authorities were alerted to a collision involving a bus and a stranded trailer near Barso village on NH21.". .AdvertisementIn total, 24 people sustained injuries as a result of this accident.The accident unfolded when a trailer carrying stones broke down while traveling on the Jaipur-Agra National Highway near Barso village around 2am. The driver parked the disabled trailer on the side of the highway. Subsequently, a sleeper coach bus en route from Jhunjhunu to Gwalior collided with the stationary trailer due to poor visibility caused by darkness.

Title: 7 killed in car-truck collision in Varanasi; CM Yogi Adityanath expresses grief
content: VARANASI: In a tragic incident, seven members of two families from Pilibhit and a driver lost their lives while a nine-year-old boy sustained serious injuries in a road accident in Karkhiyaon area in UP's Varanasi district on Wednesday. According to reports, the accident took place at around 4.30am when a speeding car collided with a truck from the rear side. The truck driver fled from the scene after the accident.The deceased were identified as Mahendra Pal (45), his wife Chandrakali (42), his brother Damodar Prasad (35) and Damodarâ€™s wife Nirmala Devi (32), all residents of Muzaffarnagar village, Vipin Yadav (35) and his mother Ganga Devi (55), residents of Rudrapur, Rajendra Yadav (55) of Gharmangadpur village, and the driver Aman Kashyap (25) of Pipariya Dulai village. The injured boy is the son of Damodar Prasad and Nirmala Devi, who were killed in the accident.. .AdvertisementDamodar Prasad had come to Varanasi to immerse the ashes of his mother Midhana Devi in the Ganga, while Vipin Yadav had come to immerse the ashes of his father Satyapal Yadav. The accident took place when they were returning home.The car was damaged severely in the collision. The local residents rushed to the accident place to rescue the people trapped in the car. But, all the eight people died on the spot. The police reached the accident site after getting information from locals. The joint commissioner of police, K Ejilearassane reached the spot to take the firsthand account of the incident. The Phoolpur station officer Deepak Ranawat said that the car driver hit the truck from the rear side.CM expresses griefChief minister Yogi Adityanath condoled the tragic deaths and directed officials to ensure proper treatment of the injured. A release from the chief minister's office said that CM Yogi instructed the district administration officials to immediately take the injured to the hospital and provide them with proper treatment. The CM wished for the speedy recovery of the injured.The injured boy was admitted to the Deendayal Upadhyay Government Hospital for treatment. The district magistrate, S Rajalingam went to the Deendayal Upadhyay Government Hospital to see the condition of the injured boy. He was later sent to the Trauma Center of Banaras Hindu University by ambulance along with a doctor and a counselor for better treatment. After talking to the district magistrate of Pilibhit, Rajalingam also contacted their family members. Other necessary action is being taken by the police.On cam: Two injured as bike collides with police vehicle in UP's Gorakhpur

Title: Mom, 2 girls killed after car collides with truck
content: BENGALURU: A 31-year-old homemaker and her two-year-old daughter were charred to death after the car they were travelling in collided head on with a truck before catching fire on a NICE Road section in Talaghattapura in the wee hours of Tuesday. The woman's six-year-old elder daughter succumbed to the injuries later in the morning and her husband is undergoing treatment in a private hospital.The deceased were identified as M Sindhura and her daughters M Kushavi, 2 and M Pranavi, 6. Sindhura's husband Mahendran B, a software engineer, was driving the car.The family is from Salem in Tamil Nadu and lived in Vijinapura of Ramamurthynagar, police said. The car was passing close to Somapura Junction when it jumped the median, crashed into the truck coming from opposite side and toppled. In no time, fire broke out. The truck driver and others managed to pull out Mahendran and Pranavi, who were in the front. The fire spread to other parts of the car, making it impossible to pull out the woman and younger girl from the back seat, police quoted eyewitnesses as saying.A fire tender was pressed into service and the car with Sidhura and Kushavi was almost gutted by then. Mahendran and Pranavi were shifted to a nearby private hospital where the girl died around 11am, police said.. .AdvertisementOn Monday night, the family had visited a furniture showroom on Tumakuru Road and then a hotel close to Nelamangala for late-night dinner. They entered NICE Road from Tumakuru Road side. Police said they will know where the family was headed only after Mahendran is able to speak."The accident took place around 2.50am. We suspect Mahendran may have dozed off or the vehicle developed a technical problem," police said.

Title: 2 Army soldiers killed, 12 injured as military transport vehicle crashes in Alaska
content: ALASKA: Two US Army soldiers were killed and 12 others injured after a military transport vehicle flipped on a dirt road leading to a training area in Alaska, officials said. The single-vehicle accident happened on Monday as the soldiers headed to the Yukon Training Area near Salcha, or about 48 km southeast of Fairbanks, the 11th Airborne Division said in a statement late Monday.  Army spokesperson John Pennell said on Tuesday that the driver lost control of the vehicle on a dirt road going into the training area, causing the large transport vehicle to flip.  There were 17 soldiers in the vehicle at the time.  First responders extracted soldiers from the vehicle and provided on-scene treatment. The 12 injured soldiers were taken by ground ambulances and air to Fairbanks Memorial Hospital. . .Advertisement Of those, the two with the most severe injuries were taken in air ambulances to Providence Alaska Medical Center in Anchorage, Pennell said. Their conditions were not immediately available Tuesday.  "We're a real tight-knit group in the military, so right now, the entire 11th Airborne Division is grieving and preparing to provide whatever support is necessary for the families of the deceased and to help support those who are still in the hospital and dealing with their injuries," Pennell said.  The Army will investigate the cause. Because there were deaths involved, Pennell said, the Army Combat Readiness Center in Alabama will also send an investigatory team.  The names of the two soldiers killed will be released 24 hours after relatives are notified.  It's the second deadly episode involving Alaska-based soldiers this year. In April, three soldiers were killed when two military helicopters collided while en route to Fort Wainwright near Fairbanks from a training mission.

Title: Nearly 200 killed in Goa accidents this year, Road Safety Week is wake-up call
content: Panaji: A devastating road accident involving an intoxicated couple in a Mercedes-Benz SUV in Banastarim serves as a stark reminder of the life-threatening risks on Goaâ€™s roads. Equally heart-wrenching is the case of a late-night meet-up of cousins that ended in a road accident in Porvorim. Goa is witnessing some of the most gruesome road accident fatalities in 2023. So far, nearly 200 lives have been lost this year in accidents.Road Safety Week, commencing from October 6 to 12 in Goa this year, presents a crucial opportunity to contemplate these grim statistics and unite in efforts to enhance road safety.In a world where mobility is essential, road safety emerges as a shared responsibility that can save countless lives. Road Safety Week is an annual wake-up call, underscoring the significance of safe and responsible driving.Goa faces an array of road safety challenges. These include road engineering, black spots, reckless speeding, and the irresponsible driving patterns of tourists. Furthermore, concerns about accidents caused by stray dogs have been raised, with chief minister Pramod Sawant drawing attention to the problem at the recently held World Rabies Day event.. .Advertisementâ€œGoaâ€™s rising vehicle population, combined with the influx of tourists, exerts immense pressure on the stateâ€™s roads. During prolonged monsoon, repairing these roads becomes an arduous task for the authorities,â€ said Umesh Kulkarni, a retired PWD engineer. He stresses that new road engineering projects featuring concrete roads promise extended longevity compared to asphaltic roads, which require periodic maintenance.â€œTo address these concerns, several road development projects including four-lane expansions and bypass constructions are underway in Goa,â€ Kulkarni added. â€œProper traffic management, such as channelling highway traffic away from city roads, is essential for effective control.â€Year after year, road accidents claim thousands of lives worldwide. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), approximately 1.35 million people succumb to road accidents annually. These accidents not only bring immeasurable human tragedy but also impose a significant economic burden on societies.In Goa, 300 road fatalities, on average, occur each year, emphasising the urgent need for improved road safety measures. In the current year alone, from January to August, 2,014 road accidents were recorded, resulting in 196 deaths.State authorities have identified 56 accident-prone locations, including 33 accident-prone zones and 23 black spots. While the state machinery is working to rectify these black spots, self-inflicted accidents caused by recklessness remain a problem.â€œStatistically, a majority of the accidents in Goa stem from human error. Our i-RAD data reinforces this fact,â€ director of transport Rajan Satardekar told TOI. He said that the Imagine Panaji Smart City Development Limited is revamping traffic enforcement infrastructure, including countdown timers at signals. Offences such as riding without helmets, triple riding, and using the phone while driving are being added to the e-challan system. New software updates enable the detection of violations relating to fitness certificates and insurance, automatically generating e-challans. Satardekar said this initiative has reduced violations over four months, as drivers become cautious about licence suspension. The systemâ€™s transparency and electronic surveillance have minimised human interventions.To combat reckless driving, the state has resorted to suspending driving licences for a period of three months. In the current year, 2,865 driving licences were suspended due to various traffic violations between January and August. This stringent action underscores the gravity of the issue and the importance of adhering to traffic laws.â€œAll accidents can be traced back to a lack of self-discipline. Until discipline becomes ingrained, accidents will occur. It is imperative to follow all traffic rules and road signs,â€ said Cedric Cordeiro, the assistant director of transport, enforcement section. He added, â€œWe are doing our utmost to enforce our laws and educate motorists to instil a sense of responsibility.â€Governments, law enforcement agencies, and individuals must take proactive steps to reduce road fatalities and minimise traffic violations by nurturing a culture of responsible driving.To sustain that culture, road safety activist Roland Martins called upon local civic bodies to engage with motorists actively. â€œConverting road-related statistics into real-time interventions is vital,â€ he said. â€œClear signage, mandatory helmet usage on rural roads, and an overarching focus on responsible driving can significantly contribute to road safety.â€

Title: MP minor kills teen over social media post, held
content:
INDORE: A 17-year-old was stabbed to death by a 15-yearold schoolboy and some friends, all of them aged 15-16, after a dispute over social m edia posts in MPâ€™s Ratlam district on Wednesday.Ratlam SP R ahul Lodha said that the victim and accused knew each other and a dispute over a bike accident two months ago. â€œThis fight escalated after they started posting about each other on social media. On Tuesday ni ght, the accused called the victim to 80-Feet Road to strike a compromise in the case. Here, the quarrel escalated and one of the accused stabbed him several times and fled the spot,â€ said Lodha. The prime accused is a Class 8 student.Industrial Area police took the injured youth to hospital but he died during treatment. All the accused are school children and from middle-cla ss families, the SP said. None of them has any prior criminal case. All six were produced before the juvenile justice board, which sent them to a rehabilitation home on Wednesday.

Title: Two workers from Bihar killed, one injured as car hits their bike in Gurgaon
content: NEW DELHI/GURGAON: Two employees of a private company lost their lives, while another sustained critical injuries in Gurgaon, Haryana, when a truck collided with their motorcycle.Police said on Friday they were returning from a friend's birthday party. Naveen Ram, originally from Bihar, has filed a complaint in which he states that he is employed at a private company alongside Arun Ram and Vakil Ram, both of whom also hail from Bihar.According to the police, they rode a motorcycle to attend a friend's birthday party late on a Wednesday evening.Naveen stated in his complaint, "Arun Ram was the one driving the motorcycle when a speeding truck collided with the rear of our two-wheeler on Pataudi Road near Wazirpur village. This resulted in serious injuries to all three of us. The local residents rushed us to a nearby private hospital, where tragically, both Arun Ram and Vakil Ram succumbed to their injuries."Based on the complaint, an FIR (First Information Report) was filed against the unidentified truck driver under various sections of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) at Sector 10 A police station. These sections include:Section 279 - Rash driving.Section 337 - Causing hurt by an act endangering life or personal safety of others.Section 304A - Causing death by negligence.The FIR was lodged in connection with the incident involving the collision between the motorcycle and the speeding truck on Pataudi Road near Wazirpur village.The police have reported that the truck driver is currently evading authorities, and efforts are underway to apprehend him. Following the completion of the autopsy, the bodies have been released to the respective families of the deceased individuals.With agency inputs

Title: FIRST photo of 'Swades' actress Gayatri Joshi and her husband Vikas Oberoi after car accident goes viral
content: Shah Rukh Khan's 'Swades' co-star Gayatri Joshi and her husband Vikas Oberoi recently escaped a car accident in Italy. The accident reportedly killed two elderly couples.Now, a new photo of the duo after the accident has surfaced on the internet and has gone viral on social media. It gives a closer look at the aftermath of the accident.Check out the photo here:. .AdvertisementA picture was posted on a social media account called ShTimes Social, which used to be known as Twitter.Read AlsoGayatri Joshiâ€™s husband Vikas Oberoi to face 'Road Homicide' probe after fatal Ferrari-Lamborghini crashVikas Oberoi, husband of former Bollywood actress Gayatri Joshi, is under investigation for 'road homicide' following a deadly collision between a Ferrari and a Lamborghini on the Italian island of Sardinia. Two Swiss nationals lost their lives in the crash, and Oberoi may face up to seven years inHow driver error caused tragic supercar crash in Italy involving Bollywood actress Gayatri JoshiThe multiple-vehicle collision involved supercars worth crores, resulting in a Swiss couple losing their lives after their high-end supercar caught fire.The photo showed a woman who looked like Gayatri sitting on the road, and some men were standing near her. Italian police officers were also present at the scene. You could see the blue Lamborghini that was part of the car accident in the picture.According to multiple media reports, the collision took place when a red Ferrari and a blue Lamborghini attempted to overtake the camper van at the same time, causing it to overturn. Tragically, the Ferrari caught fire, claiming the lives of the elderly couple inside, identified as Melissa Krautli, 63, and Markus Krautli, 67, from Switzerland, according to a report by Free Press Journal. Oberoi may face up to seven years in prison if charged and found guilty.Italian officials have started an inquiry into the incident, treating it as a 'double road homicide.' This means that Vikas Oberoi could possibly receive a maximum prison sentence of seven years if he faces legal charges and is ultimately proven guilty.Shah Rukh Khan's 'Swades' actress Gayatri Joshiâ€™s picture from minutes after horrific car crash surfaces on internet; husband Vikas Oberoi to be investigated for 'road homicide'Watch Shah Rukh Khan's 'Swades' actress Gayatri Joshiâ€™s picture from minutes after horrific car crash surfaces on internet; husband Vikas Oberoi to be investigated for 'road homicide'

Title: Mexico bus crash kills 18 Venezuelan, Haitian migrants
content: MEXICO CITY: A bus crash in the south of Mexico on Friday killed 18 Venezuelan and Haitian migrants and left 27 others injured, state authorities said.Among the dead are 13 men, two women and three children, according to the attorney general's office in the state of Oaxaca, where the crash occurred.It said the 27 injured people were taken to nearby hospitals, but did not specify their nationalities.Photos from the Oaxaca state civil protection agency show the bus, which was traveling on the highway connecting Oaxaca and the central state of Puebla, tipped over along a tight curve in the road.. .AdvertisementThe accident comes amid high levels of migrants arriving at the US-Mexico border. Migrants attempt to cross Mexico by bus, in trucks or aboard cargo trains. However, the journey is often dangerous.

Title: Two killed in separate accidents in different parts of capital
content:
NEW DELHI: Two people were killed in two separate accidents on Friday. In the first case, a truck driver reversing his vehicle on Church Road allegedly mowed down a motorist. "A witness caught hold of the accused driver, Sumant Kumar Shah (19). He has been arrested," police said. In the second incident, a tempo driver died after his vehicle overturned on the Geeta Colony Pushta road. Nagender Singh (52) was rushed to the hospital but was declared dead on arrival, police said.

Title: Pregnant woman, minor among 4 killed in accident in Shajapur
content: INDORE: Four members of a family, including a pregnant woman and a minor, were mowed down by a speeding truck when they were standing by the roadside, waiting for a tempo to go back home in MP's Shajapur on Friday.Police identified the victims as Leelabai Baroliya, 45, Aman Ganpat, 26, Aman's wife Varsha, 23, and 12-year-old Naitik, all residents of Khedi Nagar village.Shujalpur Mandi police station in charge Sanjay Mandloi said they had gone to harvest soybean crops on their farmland and were standing on the roadside when a truck ran them over. The victims were rushed to the district hospital but they couldn't be saved. An eyewitness, Prem Singh, said that the truck was on its way to Akodiya (10km from the accident site) when it collided with a tractor near a petrol pump at Ranoganj. Cops from 3 stns clear road after accident. .Advertisement An eyewitness, Prem Singh, said that the truck was on its way to Akodiya (10km from the accident site) when it collided with a tractor near a petrol pump at Ranoganj. The truck driver lost control and hit the four people on Akodia-Shujalpur road. Protesters blocked the highway. Cops from three police stations were called to clear the road. Bodies and body parts lay scattered all over the road. A water tanker had to be used to clean it up.

Title: 2 killed, 6 injured as car falls into culvert in Madhya Pradesh's Khargone
content: KHARGONE: Two persons were killed and six injured when a SUV carrying them fell off a bridge into a culvert of Choral river in Balwara area of Khargone district late on Saturday.Barwaha SDOP Archana Rawat said that the SUV was carrying devotees from Indore to Omkareshwar. The driver apparently lost control of the vehicle.Villagers of nearby village Barjhar broke the glass and pulled out the people trapped in the vehicle.Injured were taken to the government hospital at Barwaha.. .AdvertisementRawat said that Kunal Pal (24), a resident of Banganga in Indore, died on the spot while Akash Jat, 28, died during treatment at the Civil Hospital.The remaining 6 injured were referred to Indore after first aid, she said adding the injured were identified as Sandeep, Shiv Kushwaha, Aman, Lucky, Shubham and Ajit -- all in the age group of 24 to 30 years.

Title: Drunk driver held after car kills biker, injures 4 on Kelkar Road in Pune
content: PUNE: A drunk man's escapade in a car on Friday night cost a biker his life and four others suffered injuries near 'Z' bridge on Kelkar Road.The car steered by the inebriated man hit several vehicles before coming to a halt around 10.30pm. An irate mob damaged the car's front and rear windshields. The four-wheeler driver, Umesh Hanumant Waghmare (48) of Manikbaug on Sinhagad Road, and co-passenger Natraj Baburao Suryawanshi (44) from Bavdhan were arrested. Both are real estate agents.Police identified the deceased 60-year-old motorcycle rider as Vishwanath Ramchandra Rajopadhyay from Narayan Peth.Realtor does not slow down, drags two-wheelers for almost 15 feetSenior inspector Vitthal Dabade of the Vishrambaug police said, â€œRajopadhyay was a farmer. His home is in Narayan Peth. His son, a lawyer, and other family members stay on Law College Road. Rajopadhyay was going to Prabhat Road on his motorcycle.â€ He said, â€œWaghmare and co-passenger Suryawanshi are real estate agents. We conducted their medical examination and the report stated that the two were in an inebriated condition. Thus, we have invoked provisions of Section 304 (II) [culpable homicide not amounting to murder] of the IPC.â€ Sub-inspector Priyanka Nikam of the Vishrambaug police said, â€œThe car was heading towards the chowk, where â€˜Zâ€™ Bridgeâ€™s Narayan Peth-end meets Kelkar Road. Many twowheeler riders were taking a right turn. Waghmare did not slow down while approaching the chowk, and it hit the twowheelers and the autorickshaw. The car dragged the vehicles for almost 15 feet on Kelkar Road as he continued to press the accelerator.â€ The police said the deceased motorcyclist and the four other injured people were taken to a nearby private hospital. Rajopadhyay was declared dead there. The four others were shifted to the Sassoon hospital. According to the police, four two-wheelers and an autorickshaw were damaged in the accident. The injured autorickshaw driver, Ganesh Nakhati (44) of Kothrud, lodged a complaint with the police.

Title: Woman killed, husband injured after scooter gets hit by bus in Delhi's Safdarjung Enclave area
content: NEW DELHI: A 58-year-old woman died and her husband was injured after their scooter got hit by a bus in the Safdarjung Enclave area.The deceased has been identified as Sharda Devi, who lived with her family in Sadiq Nagar. Her husband Chandrabhan has retired from the ministry of defence. The victim's husband, Chandrabhan, told police that they were going on their scooter to the IGL office in RK Puram from their home on October 6.They reached the Raj Nagar flyover when their scooter got hit by a Rajasthan Roadways bus from behind, the complainant said. . .Advertisement "My wife fell on the road, and instead of stopping, the bus driver continued to drive the bus and crushed her legs and hands. The comutters who were at the spot tried to stop the vehicle, but the accused fled with the offending vehicle," the complainant said. Police were alerted by commuters, and the victim was taken to the hospital, where she succumbed to her injuries. An inquiry has revealed that the driver was speeding, which is why he failed to control the bus. The accused fled towards Gurugram. Police said they are scanning CCTV footage of the spot and nearby areas to get clues in the case. A case under sections 279 (rash driving or riding on a public way), 337 (causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others) and 304 A (causing death by negligence) of IPC at Safdarjung Enclave police station.

Title: Maharashtra: 1 killed, 4 injured as mini-bus falls into gorge in Pune
content: PUNE: One person lost his life, and four others sustained injuries when a mini-bus plunged into a 60-feet-deep gorge, in Maharashtra's Pune district on Sunday. The accident happened at approximately 2 am along the Bhor-Mahad Road at Varandha Ghat, situated about 100 km from Pune.The private bus had nearly ten passengers and had departed from Swargate in Pune at 11 pm on Saturday, en route to Chiplun in Ratnagiri district, according to a police official.Upon reaching Varandha Ghat, the driver lost control of the vehicle, causing it to veer off the road and into the gorge.. .AdvertisementAmong the five individuals injured, unfortunately, the bus driver, identified as Ajinkya Sanjay Kolte, succumbed to his injuries, the police reported.(With agency inputs)

Title: Karnataka: Boy among 7 killed in road accident in Hosapete
content: HOSAPETE (VIJAYANAGARA): Seven people, including a four-year-old boy, died and three people were seriously injured when the crusher in which they were traveling crashed into two mining tippers on the national highway-50 near Hosapete tunnel in Karnataka's Vijayanagara district. The incident occurred on Monday evening.The victims â€” who belonged to Hosapete city â€” were identified as Ummavva (45), Kenchavva (80), Bhagya (32), Anil (30), Goni Basappa (65), Bheemalingappa (50) and four-year-old Yuvaraj, according to police.. .AdvertisementPolice have been putting efforts to evocate bodies from the accident vehicle. Those injured were being shufted to the hospital. Death toll will likely increase.According to police, the crusher was traveling from Harapanahalli. The deceased were returning after visiting Kulahalli Goni Basaveshwara temple. But two mining tippers collided with the crusher directly and after the incident, the crusher's front shape was damaged drastically. A tipper was also down the roadside after the incident. SP Srihari Babu and other senior police officials were on the spot.

Title: 9 feared dead after rockfall hits vehicle near India-Nepal border in Uttarakhand
content: PITHORAGARH: Around nine people were feared to have been killed after boulders and debris fell on a Sports Utility Vehicle (SUV) at Lamari on Dharchula-Lipulekh road near the India-Nepal border in Uttarakhand's Pithoragarh district on Sunday afternoon. "The vehicle, belonging to Harish Singh Nabiyal, a homestay owner, was carrying six passengers. It was travelling from Nabi village to Dharchula. On the way, it is believed to have picked up a few more passengers. The exact number is yet to be ascertained, but the fatality figure could be around nine, " Bhupendra Singh Mahar, district disaster management officer, Pithoragarh, said.The accident site is located in a remote region where there are no mobile signals, which was posing a challenge for communication and rescue efforts. "As of now, no bodies have been recovered. We will continue our operations on Monday morning," Mahar said.Sources said that since the boulders are extremely heavy, rescue teams might need to organise a controlled blast before they are able to recover bodies. More than 50 people have died in incidents of landslides and boulder falls this year in Uttarakhand. Of these, over 10 deaths have been due to rockfall incidents, data collated by TOI till August, has revealed.. .Advertisement

Title: 7 killed, 6 injured as 2 lorries collide with SUV in Karnataka's Hospet
content:
BENGALURU: Seven people, including a child, lost their lives in a tragic road accident near the tunnel on the outskirts of Hospet city in Karnataka's Vijayanagara district. The incident occurred on Monday evening when two mining tipper lorries collided with an SUV. Six others sustained injuries in the accident. The victims have been identified as Uma, Kenchavva, Bhagya, Anila, Goni Basappa, Bhimalingappa, and Balaka Yuvaraja, all residents of Hospet City.According to Vijayanagar SP Srihari Babu, the SUV was returning from Koolahalli Goni Basaveshwara Temple in Harapanahalli taluk of Vijayanagara district when the accident happened. The SUV was carrying a total of 13 people at the time of the collision. The injured individuals are currently in critical condition and have been admitted to a government hospital for treatment.

Title: Two killed, three injured after car rams into truck in Tamil Nadu's Krishnagiri
content: KRISHNAGIRI: Two people were killed while three others sustained injuries after a premium hatchback car in which they were travelling rammed into a truck from behind near Polupalli in Tamil Nadu's Krishnagiri district on Wednesday morning.The deceased have been identified as S Sandeep, 26, M Aman, 26, residents of Kollam in Kerala.A group of five people including the deceased and their friends J Riyaz, 24, A Mithujilal, 26 and P Krishnan Sandh, 22 were heading to Bengaluru from Kerala via Krishnagiri. When they were near Polupalli on Krishnagiri - Bengaluru national highway, the car suddenly rammed into a truck, also heading to Bengaluru."Both Sandeep and Aman died at the spot while the rest of the occupants sustained injuries," said a police official from the Kurubarapalli police station.. .AdvertisementThe injured were rushed to the government hospital in the town for treatment while the bodies were sent to the same hospital for postmortem.Kurubarapalli police have registered a case and initiated an inquiry.

Title: 2 carjackers run over cabbie, drag body for 1km near Delhi airport
content: NEW DELHI: A 43-year-old taxi driver was killed after being run over and dragged for over a kilometre on the busy NH-8 in the Vasant Kunj area on Tuesday night by two carjackers from Meerut. Late on Wednesday night, the police claimed to have arrested the two men and recovered the vehicle, a Swift Dzire.The stretch where the incident took place is close to Terminal 3 of IGI airport.Read AlsoDelhi hit-and-drag case: 'Accused knew woman was trapped under car, kept driving to avoid getting caught'The accused in the Delhi hit-and-drag case were aware of the 20-year-old woman trapped between the axles of their car. However, they did not attempt to rescue the woman out of fear of being seen by others, police sources said on Monday.Cab driver dragged on SUV's bonnet for 3km in DelhiA 30-year-old cab driver was allegedly dragged on the bonnet of an SUV for nearly 3km after he stopped to confront its driver who had overtaken him. The SUV driver has been arrested.Several vehicles were passing by during the incident. A video of the man being dragged by the cab - which belonged to the victim - surfaced on social media on Wednesday morning, leading to widespread outrage. It has been shot by the occupants of another vehicle which was chasing the car. The victim was identified as Bijender Shah, who lived with his family in Surya Colony in Faridabad. He had bought the car in March with a loan. Police have invoked murder charges against the accused, said DCP (southwest) Manoj C.Cab driver dumped on NH-8 in pool of bloodThe two men have been identified as Mohammad Asif and Mohammad Mehraj, who are residents of Meerut and have a criminal record (15 cases), cops said. They boarded the cab at Saket, posing as passengers, and attacked Shah near Delhi Cantonment.. .AdvertisementShocking: Viral video shows car dragging body of a man in Delhi, body found near NH8He seems to have put up a stiff resistance, which eventually led to his death. The police had received a PCR call about the incident at 11.20 pm. The caller informed them that a body with injuries was lying near a service road of NH-8. When a team reached the spot, they found the victim's clothes were torn and there were serious injuries on his head and hands and a lot of blood. The victim's PAN card was found on the spot which helped the cops get in touch with his family.The video shot by the occupants of another vehicle shows that the man was trapped at the gate of the driver's seat and was dragged. The two men who recorded the clip were heard saying that the man being dragged was a driver and that he was dead. They were continuously honking to alert the driver of the car, perhaps unaware that these were criminals. The carjackers ended the chase by suddenly applying the brakes because of which the body got detached from the car.During their inquiry, the police figured out that it was a case of carjacking and could imagine the scenario of the killers dragging the victim along when he resisted and refused to let go. The cabbie was robbed at gunpoint as well."After studying the footage from CCTV cameras along the way, we found that the accused had taken the car to Meerut," said a source."Shah would usually leave home around 2 pm and return in the morning, between 3am and 4 am," a family member said. They said Shah had called his wife at 7.30 pm and told her that he was picking up his first passenger. At 10.28 pm, his phone was switched off in the Delhi Cantt area. "His younger brother, Ranjit, who is in Bihar, received a call from police at 1.30 am asking him to come to hospital with the photograph of Shah," said Rakesh, Shah's brother. Ranjit informed Shah's wife and the family started calling Shah on his phone. After one-and-a-half hours, they went to the police station.The incident is a grim reminder of a similar incident in Kanjhawala on New Year's Eve in which a woman was run over and dragged for several kilometres by a car.Watch Shocking: Viral video shows car dragging body of a man in Delhi, body found near NH8

Title: How 2 men waylaid cabbie in Delhi, killed him when he resisted
content: NEW DELHI: Taxi driver Bijender Shah dropped some passengers in Saket and was idling when two men approached him. Asif and Mehraj hired the cab for Rs 500 for a trip to Indira Gandhi International Airport. The investigators probing the carjacking and murder on National Highway 8 on Tuesday night revealed after interrogating the two accused that when the car approached Shankar Vihar, one of the men asked Shah to take another route to the airport.The cabbie got suspicious and asked the duo to get down from the car. However, the pair convinced him into believing they only wanted to take the shortest route to the airport. But as soon as they reached an isolated service lane, Mehraj, 34, who was sitting in the back seat, began strangling the cabbie with a piece of cloth.A police source said, "Asif, 24, who was sitting next to the victim, started pulling at the driver's seat belt. When he resisted, Asif pushed him out of the door. However, Shah's hand was allegedly entangled in the seat belt and he was dragged by the car."Another source said that realising the car was dragging Shah, the duo tried to dislodge him by hitting his body into a road divider. Only when they got rid of the seat belt was Shah freed. The criminals then fled to Meerut. A video of the incident later surfaced in which the cab driver can be seen being dragged by the vehicle.. .AdvertisementPolice got the first clue to the two criminals' identity from CCTV footage of the stretch between Saket and Malviya Nagar. "We saw all three of them in the car. We mapped the route taken by the cab from CCTV footage and discovered that it had been driven towards Meerut," a source said. From the CCTV grabs of the two men, police started matching the faces with the records of criminals and were able to identify the pair.Shocking: Viral video shows car dragging body of a man in Delhi, body found near NH8Manoj C, DCP (Southwest), immediately formed a special investigation team comprising four inspectors. The DCP and the team reached Meerut and coordinated action with UP police. "In a joint operation, we nabbed the two accused from Meerut within 24 hours," the DCP said on Thursday. A case charging the men with murder has been registered.The cops said the two men had carried out a reconnaissance of the area before committing the crime. "We are also probing whether they had stolen any other car recently," an officer said. During interrogation, the thieves told police that they stole the car to sell it.

Title: Two children among 7 killed in road accident in Tamil Nadu's Tiruvannamalai
content: CHENNAI: Seven of a family, including two children, died in a road accident when a lorry rammed a car, in which they were travelling, in Pakkiripalayam bypass road near Chengam in Tamil Nadu's Tiruvannamalai district. A lone survivor, a woman, is undergoing treatment at the government hospital in Tiruvannamalai.One of the deceased, identified as Sathish, from Tumkur in Karnataka, along with his wife, two children, two brothers-in-law and parents-in-law, offered worship at Angalaparameswari temple in Melmalayanur in Villupuram district and were returning to Bengaluru in the car. When the car was traveling on the Pakkiripalayam bypass road there was a head-on collision with a lorry leaving seven of the eight passengers dead.Immediately, local residents rushed to the spot and tried to rescue them. But as the car was badly smashed, recovering the bodies proved a tough task for them. After police reached the spot, they recovered the bodies with the help of mechanical instruments. Sathish's wife C Kaviya alone was alive inside the car and was fighting for life.Immediately, Kaviya was rescued and taken to the government hospital in Tiruvannamalai. Chengam police filed a case and are investigating. Chengam police are in the process of collecting the information of the deceased through their relatives.. .AdvertisementMeanwhile, condoling the incident chief minister M K Stalin announced Rs 2 lakh as ex gratia for the families of the deceased and Rs 1 lakh for Kaviya undergoing treatment.

Title: At least 27 dead with dozens more missing after boat capsizes in northwest Congo
content: KINSHASA: A boat capsized in Congo's northwest killing at least 27 people, and more than 70 others were missing, a senior government official said Saturday as rescuers searched frantically for survivors. The locally made boat capsized late Friday in the city of Mbandaka in Equateur Province as it transported more than 100 passengers along the Congo River to the town of Bolomba, according to Taylor Nganzi, deputy provincial governor. "Already 27 bodies of victims have been removed from the waters (and) transported to the morgue of the general hospital in Mbandaka," said Nganzi, adding that an investigation to find out the cause of the accident had begun. The New Civil Society of Congo, a local civil society group, said 49 people died in the accident, which it said occurred after an engine failure. "Everything started to sink," Jean-Pierre Wangela, president of the group, told reporters.. .Advertisement The contradictory death tolls, which is common in such incidents in Congo, could not immediately be reconciled. Volunteers joined rescuers in the search for survivors and for bodies, while families mourned loved ones who were among the victims. "We are supervising the search for bodies with the river services and accompanied by the victims' families," said Nganzi. Boat accidents are common on the Congo River and on the nation's lakes because of the prevalent use of makeshift boats that are often overloaded. The majority of the population in the country's northwest use the rivers to travel because of a lack of good roads and because it is a less expensive. The Congolese government had banned night travel throughout the country to avoid accidents, although many defy the directive.

Title: 6 killed, 3 injured in road accident in Karnataka's Gadag district
content:
GADAG: As many as six commuters of a Tata Sumo died, and 3 others got injured in a road crash in Naregal in Karnataka's Gadag district.Itâ€™s said that a mishap occurred when a Gadag-Gajendragad NWKRTC bus collided with a Tata Sumo. Sumo passengers Shivakumar Kalashetti, 50, Chandrakala Kalashetti, 42, Rani Kalashetti, 32, all were residents of Madena Hipparagi village in Aland taluk, Kalaburagi district; Sachin Katti, 31, Drakshayani Katti, 33, were residents of Afzalpur in Kalaburagi district, died on the spot while Dingalesh Kalashetti, 05, succumbed to injuries in Gadag Institute of Medical Sciences (GIMS) in Gadag.

Three injured children Mahesh Katti, 11, Anita Katti, 05, and Allamaprabhu Kalashetti are admitted in GIMS Gadag. Some bus passengers also sustained minor injuries and they were treated at government hospital in Naregal.Naregal police rushed to the spot and removed injured persons and bodies from the Tata Sumo. They sent injured person to Gadag and ascertained the identity of injured and deceased persons with the help of identity cards found in bags in the vehicle. Following the mishap, traffic was jammed for hours between Naregal and Gajendragad.. .AdvertisementAll commuters were said to be travelling to Shirahatti to visit Fakireshwar Math. As soon as the news was received, Fakir Dingaleshwar Swamiji visited the GIMS and enquired about health condition of three children.

Title: Indo American man killed in US road crash
content: Washington: A 42 year old Indian American man died in a head on collision in Indianapolis last week, the police said. The deceased, Sukhwinder Singh, died of injuries on October 13, at a hospital. The accident occurred in rural Greenwood near Indianapolis.  Singh, who hailed from Hoshiarpur district of Punjab, arrived in the US at the age of 15 in 1996.  According to local police, they responded to reports of a crash with severe injuries on the evening of October 12. Then the first responder found two vehicles with three people trapped after a head-on collision.  A preliminary investigation revealed that a Honda Accord, driven by Singh, swerved into the opposite lane, crashing into a Cadillac Escalade, the police said. . .Advertisement Singh died from his injuries at the hospital. He is survived by his wife, a 15 year old son and a 10 year old daughter.  He had been living in Indianapolis since 2010. The driver of the Cadillac, a 52 year old Indianapolis man, and his 52 year old female passenger suffered severe injuries and were transported to IU Health Methodist Hospital, the police said.

Title: Tamil Nadu: 7 of family dead in car-lorry collision in Tiruvannamalai
content:

A lone survivor is undergoing treatment at the government hospital in Tiruvannamalai and her condition is said to be critical. One of the victims, identified as Sathish, from Tumkur in Karnataka, along with his wife, two children, two brothers-in-law and parents-in-law, was visiting Angalaparameswaritemple in Melmalayanur in Villupuram district.

Title: 33-year-old killed after being hit by scooter, bus in E Delhi
content:

Police said they have booked the scooter rider and the bus driver and are looking for them. Initial investigation suggests that the man was riding a motorcycle and had first collided with the scooter being driven on the wrong carriageway.

The man was thrown off his bike towards the central verge and was crushed by the speeding bus coming from behind, a senior police officer said.According to police, they were alerted about the accident around 11.30pm and immediately left for the spot. The victim, later identified as Parveen Kumar, was found lying in a pool of blood and was rushed to hospital. He was declared dead on arrival. An FIR was registered under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence) of IPC on the complaint of Kumar's brother who also witnessed the accident.. .AdvertisementSpeaking to TOI, Kumar's brother, Pawan, said that he was on another bike, a little ahead of his brother, when the accident took place. "I stopped after I heard the screeching of tyres and the noise of the collision. My brother fell on the main road and was struggling to get up. I rushed to help him. But a speeding Volvo came and crushed his head," he said.Pawan added that the bus slowed down for a brief moment but when the driver realised he had run over a man, he sped away. He said he tried to note down the number of the bus but could not as the vehicle was being driven in a zigzag manner.Meanwhile, the driver of the scooter also fled, Pawan claimed. Police said Parveen, a native of Etawah in Uttar Pradesh, worked as a shopkeeper and was living in the city with his family. He is survived by his wife and two children. This incident marks the second bus-related crash the city has witnessed in the last one week.

content:

Title: 13 killed after car rams truck on National Highway 44 in Karnataka
content:
CHIKKABALLAPUR: Thirteen people, including a six-year-old-boy, four women and a first year PU student died after the Multi Utility Vehicle (MUV) they were travelling rammed a cement mixer truck parked by road side on Bengaluru-Hyderabad highway in the outskirts of Chikkaballapur, around 50 kilometer from Bengaluru early Thursday morning.The accident occurred at Chitravati junction at around 6.30am when the Tata Sumo, carrying 12 passengers and its driver rammed into the cement mixer truck.

While 10, including the MUV driver died on the spot, two died enroute to hospital and one in the hospital, Chikkaballapur police said.Superintendent of Police (SP) Chikkaballapur-DL Nagesh told TOI that many of the deceased passengers were residents of Gorantla village and nearby hamlets in Andhra Pradesh and some were their relatives or family friends. In different groups, they had visited Gorantla to celebrate Dussehra and were travelling back to Bengaluru."The Tata Sumo driver-Narasimhamurthy, was driving back his empty vehicle to Bengaluru. The 12 passengers were waiting for Bengaluru heading bus at Gorantla bus stand and other places. Offering them a lift, Narasimha asked them to board the vehicle around 3am," Nagesh said adding, "Most of the deceased men were either daily wage workers or self-employed while women and the boy were their family members. Mainly, the deceased hailed from different places of Gorantla. Some of them had settled in Bengaluru," he said.. .AdvertisementAccording to Shiva, an eyewitness, the driver of the MUV failed to notice the truck parked on the left lane of the highway, due to thick fog. The truck was parked very close to the highway edge, without turning on the indicators, they said. Shiva said that the MUV, minutes before the accident, had overtaken him at Harobande village junction, at high speed. "Few minutes later, I saw that the same MUV had rammed into the truck. Many of them had died while some were crying for help. Quickly, I called ambulance service and informed the police. By then, other passersby stopped and offered a helping hand in giving water to the injured ones and trying to pull them out," Shiva said.Police said, "Also, the MUV was moving at high speed. Prima facia, we can say that the vehicle was moving at around 100kmph at least during the collision. The impact was so hard that we used gas cutters and iron rods to cut open the jammed doors of the vehicle and pull out the bodies and the injured ones," an investigating officer told TOI.Police are yet to identify the driver of the cement mixture truck.The deceased are identified as Aruna A, 32, her six-year-old-son Rutvik Kumar from Doddaballapur, MUV driver K Narasimhamurthy, 47, of Kaval Byrasandra in Bengaluru, Narasimhappa, 40, N Perimil Pavan Kumar, 32, N Bellala Venkatadri, 32, his wife Bellala Lakshmi, 20 and first PUC student M Ganesh, 17, all from Gorantla, V Venkatasubbamma alias Subbamma, 40, of Basaveshwaranagar, K Shantamma, 37, of Hongasandra, S Rajavardhan, 15 from Gaunipenta village, A Venkataramana, 51, of Kamakshipalya, B Narayanappa, 50 of Yelahanka.

Title: Doctor, patient among 10 dead in two accidents in Beed
content: Two separate accidents in Beed district, India, resulted in the deaths of ten people, including a doctor and a patient traveling in an ambulance. The injured were taken to nearby hospitals for treatment.

Title: 4 students among 5 dead as 2 school vehicles collide head on in UP's Budaun

Title: Delhi tops road fatalities among million-plus cities
content: NEW DELHI: Delhi reported the highest number of deaths due to accidents among cities with a population of over a million last year. A report of the Union road transport and highways ministry also said that the total number of road accidents in the city last year was around 18% higher than in 2021. Experts responded to this by saying that fixing accountability and scientifically analysing the cause of rising accidents was important.The city reported 4,720 accidents in 2021 compared with 5,652 in 2022.According to the ministry report, 1,461 people were killed and 5,201 were injured in last year's accidents. In 2021 and 2022, Delhi reported the highest number of deaths owing to accidents in cities in the category. Road condition, human error and vehicle condition were attributed as the cause of the accidents. Human error meant speeding, drunk driving, driving into oncoming traffic, jumping the red light and using mobile phones while driving. Most of the accidents occurred between 6pm and 9pm.Rohit Baluja, president, Institute of Road Traffic Education, said, "Fixing accountability is a must. We currently don't know which department or authority is responsible for different issues. In the end, it comes down to passing the buck. In 2021 too, a ministry report had pointed out that 46% of accidents in the city comprised hit-and-run cases. We have to look beyond the increase and decrease in numbers and analyse them to find out a logical solution".Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeGolden Gate UniversityAdd a â€˜Dr.â€™ to your nameEligibility: Master's or Bachelorâ€™s Degree with 5+ years of work experienceGolden Gate UniversityLearn MoreUndoBaluja added that when there was a disease, the doctors had to look at the cause of the disease, not just its symptoms. "The diagnosis is rudimentary and not scientific. Regarding the reasons behind the accidents, apart from faulty roads, road traffic violations there are various other causes of accidents," he said.According to Prince Singhal, founder of Community Against Drunken Driving, if we analyse the fatal road accidents data, incidence had slightly dipped during the pandemic but otherwise accidents had been consistently rising since 2000. "The data also shows that accidents caused by drunk driving have also gone up significantly, especially during the night hours. The so-called car-o-bar, or drinking in a car, is common among Delhi drivers, whether commercial or private vehicles and often among cab drivers late at night," noted Singhal. "There is a direct correlation between visible and effective police presence, which unfortunately is lacking in the capital. The ratio is abysmal because there are just around 80,000 policemen for 20 crore vehicles in the city."Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: Road accident deaths in Vij increase by 46%
content:
Vijayawada: The number of deaths in road accidents in Vijayawada city in 2022 increased by around 46 per cent while it came down by around 3 per cent in Vizag city when compared to 2021. As many as 418 people were killed in 1,543 road accidents in Vijayawada city in 2022 while 1,531 road accidents claimed 358 lives in Vizag city in the same year.The â€˜Road Accidents in India-2022â€™ data published by the transport research wing of the ministry of transport show that Vijayawada city stood 13th and Vizag city 18th in the number of deaths in road accidents among 50 cities (million plus cities) during 2022.

Title: Speeding caused 71 per cent of all road deaths last year: Government data
content:
NEW DELHI: Over-speeding accounted for seven out of every 10 deaths on Indian roads in 2022 as road fatalities hit a record 1,68,491 with another 4.4 lakh injured, including close to 2 lakh who were grievously injured. While wrong side driving was the second biggest killer among "traffic rule violations", almost 67,000 persons lost their lives as they did not use the prescribed "safety devices" - helmets and seat belts.
Top Picks For You
Will discovery of a lost IAF plane bring closure to 29 families?
Ram Temple: How to watch live telecast of consecration
AP priest saves Rs 1.3L in coins over 3 yrs, buys â€˜dream scooterâ€™
Annual data released by the road transport and highways ministry showed that more than 50,000 two-wheeler riders died because they were not wearing helmets, and twice that number were left injured. Similarly, 16,715 deaths were linked to not wearing seat belts, which also resulted in 42,300 injuries. Health and road safety experts have often termed helmets as the "vaccine for head injuries".India has the dubious distinction of having the highest number of road fatalities - with a share of around 11% of global deaths, although its share in vehicular population was estimated to be less than 2%, which does not include non-motorised.A comparative analysis of road crashes and deaths over the last five years showed that the share of speeding as the main cause of road deaths has increased from 64% in 2018 to 71% in 2022, pointing to the urgent need for strict enforcement at a time when the government is focusing on building better quality roads. This is also evident from numbers which estimated that nearly 78% of the fatalities on national highways (35,488) were on account of speeding. Overall, highways, national and state, accounted for 60% of the deaths, although they account for barely 5% of the country's road network.The "Road Accident in India" report also revealed that there has been more violation of traffic rules, from drunk driving to jumping traffic lights and use of mobile phones while driving. The fatalities due to jumping traffic signals more than doubled from 679 in 2021 to 1,462 last year (see graphic).The report highlighted that despite claims of both the central and state governments of improving road conditions and complying with norms at under-construction sites, there has been a 25% spike in pothole-related deaths - from 1,481 fatalities in 2021 to 1,856 during last year - and even the number of persons killed during ongoing road works and at under construction sites have increased.There was an increase in the number of fatalities across all types of collisions with the maximum spike in accidents of vehicles with stationary or parked ones. In 2022, 6,012 persons were killed in such crashes compared to 4,925 during the previous year. Similarly, the number of persons who died in hit and run cases increased to 30,486 from 25,938 in 2021 and deaths due to vehicles hit from back or tailgating also went up to 32,097 in 2022 from 28,712 in the previous year.Interestingly, experts suggested that some of these road deaths could have been wrongly classified. â€œThe police investigation officers who are writing the reports are not trained in understanding driving regulations, nor do they understand the basis of codes of practice of traffic control devices,â€ said road safety expert Rohit Baluja.Raghavendra Singh: The Helmet Hero saving lives on the roadExplore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.Watch India ranks first in global road fatalities: Data exposes alarming trends among Indians

Title: With over 3.7k deaths, Maha 2nd in hit-and-run fatalities
content:
MUMBAI: Maharashtra lost 3,723 lives to hit-and-runs in 2022, second only to Uttar Pradesh, where 3,994 deaths were reported, revealed data on road crashes released by the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways. Over 30,400 people were killed across India in hit-and-run crashes in 2022, with Maharashtra accounting for 12% of the total. In fact, overall road fatalities in the country touched a record 1,68,491 in 2022, with Maharashtra taking the third spot for such deaths for the fifth consecutive year.

The ministry enhanced compensation for victims of hit-and-run crashes last year. Experts have welcomed the move, but pointed out that awareness on how to avail the benefits is still quite low. Survivors with grievous injuries would be awarded a compensation of Rs 50,000 (up from the previous Rs 12,500), while families of those killed would be awarded a compensation of Rs 2 lakh (up from Rs 25,000), the ministry had announced. This refers to immediate relief and not the final compensation amount awarded by Motor Accident Claims Tribunals."Survivors can use the funds for surgeries or other medical requirements. But the big challenge is how does one access the benefits? So while the intent is there, the process is not clear," said Piyush Tewari, founder of non-profit SaveLIFE Foundation.Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeGolden Gate UniversityAdd a â€˜Dr.â€™ to your nameEligibility: Master's or Bachelorâ€™s Degree with 5+ years of work experienceGolden Gate UniversityLearn MoreUndoPedestrians, cyclists or two-wheeler riders are road users impacted the most in hit-and-runs. The Western Express Highway (WEH) in Mumbai frequently witnesses hit-and-runs.In July this year, nine-year-old Alageshwari Harijan had strayed away from her parents and was walking towards the WEH service road in Kandivli when a speeding car hit her and fled. Her parents, seated in a nearby temple, rushed her to a hospital where she died two days later."In urban areas, surveillance cameras equipped with automatic number plate reading capability can help enforcement agencies track down the culprit. For state and national highways in Maharashtra, my suggestion would be to mount cameras at strategic locations, the way it has been done in Kerala," said former DGP, state police, Praveen Dixit. "These cameras should be equipped with night vision to capture offenders even when it's dark," he added.According to the ministry's data report, hit-and-runs accounted for 18.1% of the total road crash deaths in the country. The leading factor was rear-end collisions with a share of 19.5% in total deaths. Maharashtra reported 3,950 fatalities from rear end collisions in 2022. Title: Afghanistan: Four people killed, 10 others injured in two accidents in Balkh province
content:
KABUL: Four people have been killed and 10 others were injured in two separate traffic accidents in the Balkh province of Afghanistan, Khaama Press reported on Thursday. The Taliban-appointed security commander of Balkh province on Wednesday said that the first accident occurred during lunchtime on Tuesday in the Burka area when a vehicle overturned.

The people who died in the accident included a child. According to Taliban-appointed Balkh province officials, the primary reason behind traffic accidents is reckless driving. Title: Accidents claim lives of 4 pedestrians daily
content:
Visakhapatnam: Roads are unsafe not only for motorists but also pedestrians in the state. At least four pedestrians are killed every day in the state. The pedestrian deaths increased by 20 per cent in the state in 2022 when compared to 2021. A total of 1,604 pedestrians were killed in road accidents in Andhra Pradesh in 2022 and 1,346 pedestrians were killed in 2021, according to data released by the ministry of road transport and highways.

AP priest saves Rs 1.3L in coins over 3 yrs, buys â€˜dream scooterâ€™
Tamil Nadu ranked number one in pedestrian deaths in the country with 4,427 people dying in 2022. A total of 1,274 among the dead (pedestrians) were men and 330 were women in the state in 2022. In 2021, among the dead, 1,060 were male and 284 were female. The safety of pedestrians has been taken for a ride in Vizag, Vijayawada and a few other cities and towns in the state with no proper provisions being made to cross roads at busy junctions.Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeGolden Gate UniversityAdd a â€˜Dr.â€™ to your nameEligibility: Master's or Bachelorâ€™s Degree with 5+ years of work experienceGolden Gate UniversityLearn MoreUndo The road traffic volume has been increasing in cities and medians are being raised on the busy roads for easy traffic flow. But, at the same time, the civic bodies and other stakeholders are not setting up any amenities such as foot overbridges (FOBs), and subways or implementing zebra crossings to cross the road without hassle.The denizens are seen struggling to get to the other side of the road. Sometimes, they endanger their lives as they proceed despite spotting vehicles speeding towards them. Walking is the most common mode of transport in the rural and semi-urban pockets and pedestrian safety is considered a serious public health issue. Pedestrians meet with accidents mostly at crossings and pavements. The footpaths are not safe for pedestrians at some places in the state, said experts in road safety.While there are several factors that make roads unsafe in the state, experts point out that some motorists jump the red signal and ride on pavement to escape traffic. This is a major reason for pedestrian deaths, they said. Despite an increase in the number of pedestrian deaths, there continues to be no major improvement in road infrastructure to ensure their safety on NHs and other roads in the state, said B Ganga Rao, an activist in Vizag city. The pedestrians have been deprived of their right to use the footpath and zebra crossings in the cities due to encroachments and unplanned changes to the road designs. The civic bodies and police have failed to end the encroachments on the footpaths, resulting in the pedestrians losing their lives on the roads, said D Sudhakar, a resident of MVP Colony in Vizag city. If the situation continues, elderly people and children will face a lot of hardship crossing the roads, he added.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: Unlicensed AP drivers involved in over 2,600 road accidents
content: VISAKHAPATNAM: As many as 2,621 road accidents (over 12 per cent of the total 21,249 accidents) that happened in Andhra Pradesh in 2022 involved drivers without licence and in 3,115 accidents, the licence details of the drivers were not known. In 719 accidents, the drivers had a learner's licence. One in every three road accidents in AP is caused by motorists without a driving licence or motorists with or without a driving licence involved in hit and run cases.Though there is no clarity on how the licence details are missing, the officials say it is difficult to identify the drivers in some hit-and-run cases.70% accidents in AP caused by drivers with licenceÂ Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeGolden Gate UniversityAdd a â€˜Dr.â€™ to your nameEligibility: Master's or Bachelorâ€™s Degree with 5+ years of work experienceGolden Gate UniversityLearn MoreUndoThe 'Road accidents-2022' report published by the Union ministry of road transport and highways says Andhra Pradesh stood sixth in the country in reporting accidents involving unlicensed drivers. Around 15% of the accidents involved drivers whose licence details were not known. There was over 12% increase in these types of accident cases in 2022 when compared to that of 2021.A senior police officer said driving without a helmet and licence is becoming common among most students. Most parts of the state lack traffic awareness when it comes to wearing helmets, fastening seat belts and having valid driving licences. The vehicles driven by untrained and unqualified drivers are serious traffic hazards, he added.Experts in road safety opined that though the problem (riding vehicles without a driving licence) is basically an enforcement issue, it must also be addressed with better understanding. There is a need to impose hefty fines for riding vehicle without licence and insurance, they added.Around 70% of road accidents in the state were caused by drivers with valid licences. Holders of valid driving licences were responsible for over 14,794 road accidents in the state in 2022. Officials say drivers with valid driving licences are violating traffic norms and indulging in other malpractices. There is a need to create better facilities and opportunities for training or skilling, and evaluation or testing.Most of the licence holders are untrained drivers. Some people never took a driving test and only a few understand the road rules. And a vehicle in the hands of an untrained driver is no less than a weapon, said police officers. A total of 8,293 people were killed and 21,340 injured in 21,249 road accidents in the state in 2022.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: Two killed, four injured in truck-vancrash on Katraj-Dehu Road bypass
content:
PUNE: Two people died and four were injured in a collision involving a container truck, a pickup van and a private bus on the Katraj-Dehu Road bypass slope near Dari Pul around 3.45am on Saturday.Both the container truck and pickup van drivers died on the spot. With this, the number of people killed in accidents on Katraj Dehu-Road bypass went up to 21 this year.
