This was the 16th serious accident in 2023 on the bypass, which has over the years obtained the "killer road" tag. An officer with the Bharati Vidyapeeth police said the accident occurred when the driver of the container truck, on its way from Belgaum to Mumbai, lost control over the vehicle along the slope between New Katraj Tunnel and Dari Pul. The truck went on to hit the van and a street lamp pole, which fell on the private luxury bus travelling to Kolhapur from Mumbai on the other side of the bypass. Senior inspector Vinayak Gaikwad of Bharati Vidyapeeth police told TOI, "The container truck driver, Vishalkumar Navik (22), and the pickup van driver, Shahbaz Zulfikar Munshi (30), died. Two persons on board the bus and the cleaners of the container truck and pickup van were injured in the accident."Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeGolden Gate UniversityElevate your business career with a Doctorate.Golden Gate UniversityLearn MoreUndoOne of the injured people left the spot, while the remaining three were taken to private hospitals in the Katraj area. "All the injured people are out of danger," an officer said.Gaikwad said the driver of the container truck, which was carrying fruit juice packets, lost control over the wheel while moving downhill and the vehicle rammed the pickup truck from the rear. "Following this, the container truck and the pickup van veered off the road, crashed into the divider and went to the other side of the bypass," he said. "As the truck turned on its side, its container got detached from the body. However, its chassis with the wheels continued to move further ahead and finally toppled. The pickup truck also turned on its side on the other side of the road," the officer said. After hitting the road divider, the truck also hit the street lamp pole. "Seeing the crash, the driver of the private luxury bus took a sharp left turn in a bid to avert a collision with the container truck and the street lamp pole. However, the lamp pole fell on the bus and injured two passengers on board," Gaikwad said. Fire brigade officials went to the spot and rescued the injured people stuck in the cabins of the container truck and pickup van. They also cleared the oil spill on the road. Traffic was restored after nearly two hours. Gaikwad said it appeared the driver of the container truck had dozed off in the pre-dawn hour. Title: Oil tanker hits car and van in Gurgaon, 4 killed
content: GURGAON: Four persons died after an oil tanker broke past a divider to the other side of the Delhi-Jaipur highway and collided with a Datsun Go and a pickup van on Friday night.The car went up in flames after its CNG cylinder exploded under the impact of the crash, killing all three occupants in their 30s. The fourth victim was the driver of the pickup van into which the tanker collided.Police said three engineers of JSG InnoTech Pvt Ltd in Sonipat were on their way to distribute Diwali gifts among the private company's vendors when the accident occurred near Triveni Hotel.They were identified as Jitendra Singh from Panipat, Lokesh Saini of Jind and Pawan Dubey, who was from Bihar.According to cops, the tanker was at such speed that the driver failed to control the vehicle even after it jumped the divider and crashed into the car. The truck did not stop and rammed into the van that was on the service road.Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeGolden Gate UniversityElevate your business career with a Doctorate.Golden Gate UniversityLearn MoreUndoThe front portion of the tanker - which was on its way to Delhi - got stuck in the bonnet of the van, whose driver, Trilok Sharma, died on the spot. The truck driver fled, leaving the vehicle behind.Sandeep Singh, Jitendra's brother who lodged a police complaint, said he had met the three engineers around 9pm at Rajiv Chowk. "As part of Diwali ritual, they were going to distribute gifts among vendors of the company where they worked. A couple of hours later, I got a call from police that my brother and his friends had met with an accident. I rushed, hoping to take them to hospital at the earliest. But by the time I reached, they had burnt to death," he added.Police said they were scanning footage from nearby CCTV cameras to trace the tanker's driver. "He was speeding towards Delhi and lost control of the vehicle. The truck jumped the divider and went to the other carriageway. It crashed into the Datsun Go coming from the opposite side and then rammed the van on the service lane," said Arvind Yadav, the SHO of Bilaspur police station.The car caught fire after the CNG cylinder exploded, Yadav said. "The occupants were likely to have been stuck inside because the doors got jammed. The vehicle went up in flames."The legs of the van driver were almost severed, the SHO said, adding that it took them an hour to bring him out. "He, too, had died by the time we reached him," he added. Commuters said they could do little but watch the car go up in flames. "The flames were leaping out of the car along with thick plumes of smoke. The doors were locked too. So, it was difficult to go near it. We were also scared that the vehicle might explode any time," said Ved Prakash, a passerby.The four bodies were taken to a mortuary and relatives were called to identify them. "Three of the bodies had 90% burns. DNA samples were taken for conducting forensic tests," said Mohit, a worker at the mortuary.An FIR was registered at Bilaspur police station against the tanker driver under sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (death by negligence) of the IPC on Saturday. Title: Clear days claimed more lives in accidents in state
content:
Visakhapatnam: Contrary to the popular perception that road accidents occur during adverse weather conditions, over 76 per cent of road accidents and 75 per cent of deaths in road crashes in Andhra Pradesh took place during sunny or clear weather conditions.The â€˜Road Accidents in India-2022â€™ report released by the ministry of road transport and highways (MoRTH) says that sunny weather accounted for as many as 16,246 road accidents which claimed 6,295 lives and caused injuries to 16,513 people, including serve injuries to 4,068 people in the state in 2022.

Though the ministry did not mention the exact reasons behind the rise in accidents during clear weather, experts in road safety said greater traffic and lesser vigilance and more visibility of riders may have led to more accidents during the sunny weather.A total of 8,293 people were killed and 21,340 injured in 21,249 road accidents in 2022. The weather conditions - sunny or clear, rainy, foggy or misty and hail or sleet - affect the road surface and the driverâ€™s visibility, thereby increasing chances of mishaps. Adverse weather conditions - foggy, rainy and hailstorm - make driving little riskier as visibility reduces and the road surface gets slippery, resulting in the drivers not riding the vehicles at high speed, and the chances of crashes may reduce, experts added.Accidents during adverse conditions accounted for 13 per cent of the total road accidents in the state in 2022. As per the data, a total of 21,340 road accidents took place in the state in 2022. Of these 16,246 (76 per cent) occurred during sunny weather, 1,102 (4.5 per cent) during rainy weather, 1,656 (7.7 per cent) during foggy weather, 49 (0.22 per cent) during hail and 2,196 (10 per cent) during other types of weather conditions.In Andhra Pradesh, accidents due to rainy weather conditions claimed 438 lives, foggy weather claimed 661 lives and hail weather conditions claimed 13 lives in 2022.Though foggy or misty weather conditions accounted for around 8 per cent of the road accidents, the severity of death is usually high in winter. There is a need to create more awareness among commuters to take maximum precautions during winter as the fog will lower visibility, said a senior police officer on condition of anonymity. During foggy weather conditions, there were several incidents of speeding SUVs or cars hitting the stationary vehicles on the NHs from behind, he added.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: 91% of those killed in road accidents in the Delhi in 2022 were men
content:
NEW DELHI: More men than women lost their lives in motor vehicle crashes in the city last year, with the former accounting for 91% of the total 1,461 deaths and 86% of the 5,201 injuries, according to Delhi Traffic Police data.The data showed 21.9% of the total victims (6,662) lost their lives in 2022, compared to 22.4% in 2021. In the case of both men and women, most of the fatalities were in the 40-plus age group.

Most of the injured men were in the 19-30 age bracket; in the case of women, most of the injured were aged over 40.Road engineering issues, including improper curvature, obstructed line of sight, lack of standard speed-calming measures, improper illumination and absence of signages, were found to be among the major causes of the crashes. Pedestrians accounted for 1,777 (34.1%) injuries, showing only a marginal decrease in percentage share from 2021 (35.9%).According to the data, the number of fatal crashes involving two-wheelers increased last year and the injuries involving such riders also rose, from 1,868 in 2021 to 2,263 in 2022. One in every three death or injury victims was a two-wheeler rider, the data stated. "The percentage of two-wheeler riders as victims (fatalities and injuries) has been gradually increasing," an officer said.Cyclists accounted for 2-4% of all road crash victims. More than half of the victims are pedestrians, cyclists and motorcyclists, who too often remain neglected in the road traffic design system."Although pedestrians, cyclists, and motorised two- and three-wheelers are more vulnerable to accidents due to being less protected as compared to car occupants, the heavy burden of deaths borne by these road users is also a reflection of infrastructure and vehicle design that prioritises cars and other motorised transport," said the officer.He said further that correct use of a helmet can reduce the risk of fatal injuries by 42% and cut the risk of head injuries by 69%. Title: 39 killed, 17 injured as bus falls into deep gorge in JK's Doda; PM announces ex-gratia
content:
JAMMU: At least 39 passengers, including six women, were killed and 17 others injured when a bus they were travelling in skidded off and rolled down into 300-foot-deep gorge in Doda district of Jammu and Kashmir on Wednesday. Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced Rs 2 lakh each as ex-gratia to the kin of the deceased.Police said that at least 39 people were killed and many injured after a passenger bus fell into a deep gorge in the Assar area of Doda district.

"A passenger bus (JK02CN-6555) that left Kishtwar at around 0800 hrs skidded off the road and rolled down into a 300-foot-deep gorge near Trungal-Assar of Doda on the Batote-Kishtwar highway when driver of bus failed to negotiate a sharp cure," police said.Police added that a rescue team rushed to the spot and launched a rescue operation and men and machinery were pressed into service to shift the injured for medical treatment. Four critically injured people have been airlifted to Jammu for treatment,â€ Police said."Twenty-one deceased including six females have been identified while process of identification of the dead is on. The 18 injured include four minors." "Later two of airlifted critically injured person succumbed to their injuries," Police said.PRO Defence Jammu Lt Col Sunil Bartwal said that in a commendable display of promptness and coordination, the Indian Army, in collaboration with civil administration, conducted a swift rescue operation in response to a grievous accident on National Highway at Assar.â€œThe Indian Army's Quick Reaction Team (QRT) and medical personnel from COB Assar swiftly reached the accident site and evacuated the injured to Government Hospital Dodaâ€.Col Bartwal said, â€œThe unfortunate incident resulted in a total of 38 casualties, with 32 passengers declared dead initially while 20 individuals sustained injuries and immediate medical assistance is being providedâ€.â€œA total of 58 individuals were on board the ill-fated bus at the time of the incident, " he said, adding, â€œArmy expresses its deepest condolences to the families affected by this tragic accident.â€SSP Doda Abdul Qayoom, who personally supervised the rescue operations confirmed the accident toll to 39 while 17 others are being hospitalized.Deputy commissioner Doda Harvinder Singh said over phone that police and civil administration immediately swung into action and launched a massive rescue operation and evacuated injured to Government Medical College Doda."Four critically injured were airlifted to Jammu for specialized treatment," DC Doda Singh added. He said the identification process of the deceased is on. After hearing the news of the tragic accident, Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union home minister Amit Shah expressed grief over the incident."The bus accident in Doda, Jammu and Kashmir is distressing. My heartfelt condolences to bereaved families and wishing for a speedy recovery of those injured in the accident. Directed Div Com and Dist Admin to provide all necessary assistance to affected persons.â€PDP president Mehbooba Mufti, National Conference president Farooq Abdullah and vice president Omar Abdullah, president, J&K BJP, Ravinder Raina, working president J&K Congress Raman Bhalla and others also have expressed grief and shock over the loss of lives.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.Watch Tragic bus accident in J&K: At least 36 dead, several injured as bus plunges into gorge in Doda

Title: 3 dead, 15 hospitalized when bus carrying students and truck crash on Ohio highway, officials say
content:
OHIO: A charter bus carrying students from a high school was rear-ended by a semi-truck on an Ohio highway Tuesday morning, leaving three people dead and 15 others injured, according to an emergency official.Five vehicles were involved in the crash, including a Pioneer Trails charter bus that was transporting students from the Tuscarawas Valley Local School District in eastern Ohio, Licking County Emergency Management Agency Director Sean Grady said.

There were a total of 57 people onboard, he said.Lt. Nathan Dennis of the Ohio State Highway Patrol confirmed that people were killed in the crash and said â€œ18 people were taken from the bus (overall),â€ but he declined to specify how many deaths had occurred and declined further comment since authorities were still notifying the victims' families.The bus was carrying Tuscarawas Valley students and chaperones to an Ohio School Boards Association conference in Columbus, Superintendent Derek Varansky said.â€œRight now, our focus is on getting in touch with our Tusky Valley families who had loved ones on the bus and providing support to our entire school community,â€ Varansky said in a Facebook post.The conference was canceled after organizers learned of the crash.â€œGiven this devastating news, we have canceled our conference. Right now, our focus is on providing support to Tuscarawas Valley. That includes making grief counselors from our trade show available,â€ said Jeff Chambers, the associationâ€™s director of communication services.The accident occurred shortly before 9 a.m. on Interstate 70 West in Licking County, near the Smoke Road underpass and about 26 miles (42 kilometers) east of Columbus. The cause was not immediately known.The injured were being treated at seven hospitals.In a statement, Pioneer Trails, the charter bus company, confirmed there was an incident involving one of its buses Tuesday morning.â€œPioneer is fully cooperating with the authorities as we work to find the cause of the accident,â€ the companyâ€™s statement said. â€œOur thoughts and prayers go out to all of those impacted by this accident. As this is an ongoing investigation, there will be no further comments.â€The American Red Cross of Central and Southern Ohio said it fulfilled a request for more blood from one hospital in the area, sending 30 units to a hospital in the Mount Carmel Health System, said Marita Salkowski, regional communications director. A center was set up at a United Methodist Church in Etna for bus passengers not in need of medical attention to go to and contact loved ones, she said.Numerous emergency responders were at the scene, and Ohio Department of Transportation cameras from the area showed smoke coming from the crash site. Police officers were blocking the entrance ramps to I-70 East and West near the scene of the accident, increasing traffic along the road leading to the interstate. Dozens of emergency response vehicles were still at the scene of the accident several hours after it occurred.Mickey Lymon, an investigator with the Licking County Coronerâ€™s Office, said they had been called to the scene, but deferred other questions to the state police.â€œThis is our worst nightmare, when we have a bus full of children involved in a crash,â€ Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine said during a news conference at the scene. â€œPrayers go out to the families, everyone who was on the bus.â€It was the second recent fatal crash involving high school students on a charter bus. In September, two people were killed and several others injured when a charter bus carrying high school students to band camp veered off a New York highway.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: At least 39 killed, 17 hurt as bus rolls into a gorge in J&Kâ€™s Doda
content: JAMMU: An â€œovercrowdedâ€ bus carrying 56 passengers from Kishtwar to Jammu met with a tragic accident in Doda district of J&K on Wednesday, resulting in the loss of at least 39 lives and leaving 17 others injured, including four children. Title: The unknown menace on unsafe roads: Vehicles not traced in 47% of fatal crashes
content:

Most of the vehicles responsible for road fatalities were registered in Delhi. Of the accidents involving two-wheelers, 780 were caused mainly by cars/taxis/jeeps, while 677 involved unknown vehicles.

Title: Cops, PMC take measures to reduce fatal road accidents

The DCP said these measures were necessary considering the rise in the number of road fatalities over the last few years.â€œLast year, the city reported 325 road fatalities. Since January this year, 296 people have died in road accidents. Many of these victims were pedestrians,â€ he said.The majority of the pedestrian fatalities were reported from the peripheral areas of the city, such as Ahmednagar Road, Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass, Warje, Kothrud, Yerawada and others. â€œWe are discussing with the local civic authorities for proper road dividers and refuge areas on these roads to reduce pedestrian accidents,â€ Magar said.â€œOur study also reveals that the fatal accidents reported in the night hours have been attributed to mistakes made by the drivers. Motorists also tend to speed at night hours due to empty roads,â€ Magar said.Recently, four people were killed on the Katraj-Dehu Road Bypass after a truck driver decided to chase down a motorcycle rider who had abused him. Some of these accidents have also been caused due to the infrastructure works currently under way across the city. â€œWe are discussing with various PMC departments to expedite the various infrastructure projects in the city like drainage lines, road widening projects, footpath developments and others as these works also have caused regular fatal accidents,â€ Magar added. Title: After train kills three, residents protest lack of overbridge
content:
CHENNAI: The continued apathy of the railways towards providing amenities in suburban railway stations could be considered one of the causes behind the tragic accident that robbed the lives of three members of a family at Veppampattu railway station on Sunday morning. The railway station where several people board suburban trains to reach Chennai, Tiruvallur, and Arakkonam daily does not have a pedestrian subway or a foot overbridge for people to cross and reach the platform to board trains.

Irate commuters and residents staged a protest demanding that the subway work and an incomplete road overbridge be completed. They sat on the streets outside the railway station. Many said that they had been demanding that the district administration and the railways provide basic amenities at the station because Veppampattu was a rapidly developing residential area with many educational institutions.A subway was sanctioned, and work began a few years ago. A shaft was also placed, but the work remained abandoned for more than five years after the work site got flooded. The subway site remains flooded and covered in plastic waste throughout the year. Officials are yet to find a solution to the problem. The worksite is also not barricaded and there is also no board warning people.The passengers are at risk as the railways have increased the maximum permissible speed of express trains on the route to 130mph.K Baskar, a former member of the divisional rail users consultative committee (DRUCC), said, "Southern Railway should prioritise the work to build amenities like foot overbridges and subways at the railway station. Three Vande Bharat trains that run at high speed pass by the railway station. People have to cross the tracks to reach the platform.." Construction of foot overbridges or subways has not been completed in Putlur, Ambattur, and a few other stations.Southern Railway has installed foot overbridges, closed level crossings and barricaded the tracks at many vulnerable points since the speed of express trains were increased on the Chennai to Tiruvallur and Arakkonam route.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: Guard hit and dragged by SUV in Greater Noida, dies
content: NOIDA: A 37-year-old guard at a housing society was killed after an SUV crashed into him from behind and dragged him for several metres near Achher T-point in Greater Noida on Saturday.Police said they had tracked down the vehicle and its driver, who had fled after the accident. A couple of guards, who were walking at a distance, took him to the hospital, where he was declared dead on arrival," said Raj, who filed a complaint at Beta 2 police station.A case under IPC sections 279 (rash driving) and 304A (causing death by negligence) was registered on Sunday. Title: Bypass accident victimsâ€™ kin struggle to stay afloat
content:
Pune: Suryakanth Vaghule still shudders recalling the night of January 29, when a speeding truck rammed his motorcycle and ran over his wife on Katraj-Dehu Road bypass. The 46-year-old man regained consciousness after a few moments of the accident, only to find his wife lying dead on the other side of the bypass.Jyoshna Vaghule was among the 23 people who lost their lives in accidents this year on Katraj-Dehu Road bypass, which has, over the years, obtained the â€œkiller bypassâ€ tag.

After every accident, particularly the major ones, the authorities swing into action to make people aware of the risks involved with dangerous driving and policymakers return to the drawing board making long-term infrastructure development plans.The accident that killed Jyoshna had occurred when the couple were returning home around 10.30pm on a bike. Two container trucks were following on both ends of the road when one of the heavy vehicles overtook the bike.â€œEverything after that was a blur. The container truck was speeding and trailing my bike at a close distance. While overtaking, the truck hit me in my right leg and arm. I lost balance and the bike overturned. My wife too fell to her side and the truck hit her head, arms and the wheels went over her torso. She died on the spot,â€ Vaghule said.â€œIf you ask me if I want revenge against the truck driver who had run over my wife, my answer is â€˜noâ€™. The truck driver might be the sole breadwinner of his family. I want him to repent and learn from the mistake,â€ he said.The coupleâ€™s children â€” â€” a 20-year-old son and a 16-year-old daughter â€” were on another motorcycle and trailing their parents. â€œThey stood at the Vadgaon bridge for long pleading for help. No one was around. Finally, a man in a car took us to a nearby hospital where my wife was declared dead,â€ said Vaghule.Jyoshna was one of the breadwinners for her family and the sole support system for her parents. The accident has left both the families shattered. The coupleâ€™s children were in college. Jyoshna earned a living by performing chores for families in the neighbourhood and tailoring jobs, while Vaghule was a trade officer in a firm.â€œI lost my will to live and motivation to work. I could not work properly and lost my job. Without Jyoshna or me earning, my children had to stop going to college,â€ said Vaghule, who now runs a vegetable stall. His son takes up a few jobs to help his father. But Jyoshnaâ€™s parents have no one to support them.Most of the major accidents on the bypass have occurred involving overspeeding trucks and two-wheelers in rear-end collisions. While the heavy vehicle drivers contribute to the accident majorly, local traffic plays a significant role as well.In another accident caused by a motorcyclist riding on the wrong-side near Navale bridge near the bypass, a 27-year-old man, Madan Bahiparya, died due to fatal injuries to his head. He was admitted to a local hospital after the accident by some local residents. However, his brother, Ram Bahiparya, had no information.The Bahiparyas were originally from Nepal and settled in Pune for business purposes. On October 2, Madan was walking to a marble factory near the bridge where he worked when the motorcycle knocked him down.â€œHe has left behind his wife and three children, all aged below six. There is no source of income for them anymore. His wife will be forced to farm and do some odd jobs for a living,â€ he said. Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

The insurance company, however, turned down the claim on grounds that the car was not owned by him at the time of the accident.Aggrieved, he filed a case against the insurance company. The insurance company argued in the court that Prajapati had no insurable interest in the matter as he had already sold the car. It also said if any liability was established, it was only to the tune of Rs 3,21,540, as estimated by the insurance surveyor.At his end, Prajapati argued that he was the registered owner of the car at the time of the accident. The insurance company, meanwhile, furnished an investigation report and a copy of the FIR to support its argument that Prajapati had sold the car.The consumer forum, however, observed that the FIR and statements given to the police were not conclusive evidence. This had also been observed in a case by the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. The forum said the insurance company cannot claim from FIR and statements related to it that the car was not owned by Prajapati. It also held that since the car was in Prajapatiâ€™s name on the day of the accident, he had insurable interest in it.The complainant had not challenged the amount decided by the surveyor, hence the forum ordered that Prajapati should be given a claim of Rs 3,63,294 based on the report of the surveyor with an interest of 6%. It also granted Rs 2,000 towards mental agony and expenses for the complaint.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

content:

Ansari was killed while the two others were injured. Multiple breathalyser tests on Malik, a resident of Elliot Road, did not find any alcohol.

Title: Truck crushes jaywalking 10-yr-old; car driver stops to check, leads to pileup on Delhi-Meerut Expressway
content: GHAZIABAD: A 10-year-old boy jaywalking on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway was crushed to death by a truck near Sikroda in Ghaziabad on Friday morning. His body lay on the speedway for nearly 30 minutes and was spotted after four cars collided with each other as one of the drivers slowed down, seeing a person lying dead on the road.While none of the occupants was injured, the cars were damaged, and the pile-up led to a traffic jam on the expressway that was eased after the cars were towed away an hour later.Additional DCP (traffic) Ramanand Kushwaha said the boy, Samad, a resident of Sikroda, was accompanied by three minor friends. A police team deputed on the expressway, rushed to the spot and found the body," Kushwaha said, adding that the body was sent for autopsy and the report is awaited.Samad's father Aarif said he had asked his son to skip school on the day. Title: Now, highway engineers must visit fatal accident site within 48 hours of incident
content:
FARIDABAD: The government has made it mandatory for highway engineers to visit fatal accident sites under their jurisdiction within 48 hours of any such incident. They will take corrective measures to prevent future accidents, Union road transport and highways secretary, Anurag Jain, said on Monday while adding that the ministry will also roll out â€œcashless treatmentâ€ for immediate medical care for victims by March next year.

â€œWe have issued instruction to our engineer that they need to visit the site in 48 hours and will look into all the engineering aspect. Instead of waiting for 2-3 years for declaring the stretch as black spot, we have to start taking immediate corrective action. monthly analysis of data and taking corrective measures,â€ Jain said while addressing the Global Road Safety Initiative meet organised by Institute of Road Traffic Education (IRTE) and two major UN organisations, UNESCAP and UNECE, which formulate global norms.On the cashless treatment for road crash victims, which was provisioned in the amended Motor Vehicle Act of 2019, but has been delayed, Jain said, â€œWe are working on this and hopefully in the next three months we will be able to roll out a programme, ensuring emergency care for every road accident victim... Now we have taken a decision. While the health ministry is coming up with an overall trauma care plan, we are hopeful of rolling out our scheme before March. This scheme can be plugged into the bigger programme.â€The cashless scheme aims to provide free medical care till any crash victim stabilises.Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeGolden Gate UniversityDoctorate in Emerging Technologies (Generative AI)Golden Gate UniversityLearn MoreUndoHighlighting how India faces a different challenge than many other countries, Jain said while all along the government has been focussing more on cars and safety of four-wheelers, the number of two-wheeler occupants killed is very high and the number of pedestrians killed is also a cause of major concern. As per road transport ministry data, 44% of the total 1.68 lakh persons killed in road crashes in 2022 were two-wheeler occupants.While flagging that road user behaviour and enforcement of traffic rules remain the key to reduce road fatalities, Jain said they have taken up with the education ministry for including road safety in the school and college curricula. â€œThey have agreed and hopefully road safety will become the part of course curriculum. The new generation will get more sensitised,â€ he added.The three-day Global Road Safety Initiative meet is deliberating upon finalising suggestive codes of practice to reduce road crash fatalities and injuries in low and middle-income countries.Rohit Baluja, president of IRTE said that the global road safety experts will be working towards deliberating and finalising suggestive codes of safe practice, driver certification, diagnosis of road crashes through multi-disciplinary crash investigation and safety of vulnerable road users. â€œThe text books with road safety curriculum for classes VI to X have already been provided to the state government for necessary adoption by the schools. our effort would be to inculcate good road behaviour through awareness campaigns in schools, inbuilt road safety curriculum in the existing text books, etc. Road Safety Films, Road Safety reels are being provided for screening on television and playing on radio,â€ Baluja added. Title: Many Lives Cut Short, By Accident Or Design
content:

In 2021, 4,720 accidents were recorded. Two-wheelers and pedestrians continue to be the highest number of victims. The data has revealed that the maximum number of accidents took place in the winter months (November-December) between 9 pm and 1 am.

According to the report, 68,236 traffic accidents were reported in 53 cities in 2022. The accidents killed 17,680 people and injured 57,246. The maximum fatalities in accidents were reported in Delhi, followed by Jaipur (918 deaths) and Kanpur (795 deaths).Experts said that one of the reasons for a higher number of accidents in winter was low visibility because of fog.Prince Singhal, a road safety expert and founder of Community Against Drunken Driving, said road accidents have been steadily increasing in India and the NCRB report is just a testament to that. â€œAn approximate 18% increase in road accidents is disastrous for a country and road accidents will soon become the biggest public health hazard. Itâ€™s a scene of total chaos where citizens are holding governments accountable on the promise of an affordable, accessible and a sustainable transport system, whereas the road users themselves are shunning safe road use behaviour,â€ Singhal said.He added that almost 83% of road crashes occur when rules are broken by road users as most often, they donâ€™t value the rights of others. Singhal said that this is one of the reasons that pedestrians continued to remain the victims of road accidents.â€œThere are several contributing factors to this as the number of cars has almost doubled in the last decade, but the infrastructure is limited and a lot of what is available is also flawed. The critical time of accidents which is described between 9 pm-1 am also points to drunk driving and since itâ€™s a non-peak hour, people usually are speeding,â€ Singhal added.Other experts pointed out that one must start looking at accidents in a scientific manner. Post-accident victim care, black spots, penalising offenders, vehicle safety standards, quality infrastructure, difficult weather conditions like fog should be accounted for.Delhi Traffic Police recently identified in its Delhi Crash Report, 2022, 117 locations where most of the fatal accidents happen. These include Mukarba Chowk in north Delhi, Khampur village in northwest Delhi and Dhaula Kuan and Mayapuri Chowk in southwest Delhi.Globally, road traffic injury is considered a major public health problem. It is among the leading causes of deaths, disabilities and hospitalisation, imposing a huge socio-economic cost on society. According to the Global Burden of Disease, 2016, road injuries were the eighth leading cause of death. Road accidents are also among the top 10 causes of death and disability combined, as measured by disability adjusted life years.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: At least 16 dead and 12 injured as passenger bus falls off ravine in central Philippines
content:
MANILA: A passenger bus lost control while negotiating a downhill curve in a central Philippine mountain village and plunged into a deep ravine, killing at least 16 people and injuring 12 others, officials said Wednesday. Eight of the wounded in the accident, which happened Tuesday afternoon in Hamtic town in the province of Antique, were in critical condition at a local hospital, officials said.

 The air-conditioned bus, which came from the nearby Iloilo province, was maneuvering through the accident-prone mountain road when the driver, who was among those killed, lost control. The bus smashed onto a concrete railing then fell into the ravine, officials said. "The driver was repeatedly sounding his horn because he had apparently lost control of the bus before it plummeted into the ravine," Ronniel Pabustan, an Antique provincial crisis responder, told The Associated Press by telephone, citing accounts by some of the passengers. Dozens of rescuers, including police, army troops and provincial emergency responders, worked to extricate the victims from the wreckage. They used stretchers and ropes to bring the victims up the ravine in an hourslong rescue and retrieval work that dragged late into the night, Pabustan said. "It's so tragic and painful because this happened close to Christmas," Pabustan said, adding that among the dead was a baby, who remained unidentified. The search for victims ended overnight but provincial officials urged village leaders to alert emergency personnel if they find any more victims in the site of the accident, a thickly wooded area in the bottom of the ravine. Title: In separate hunting accidents, two boys killed in US
content:

In Mississippi, the unfortunate incident occurred as two siblings, aged 11 and 12, were hunting rabbits with their 19-year-old stepbrother near their grandmother's residence on Alex Knight Road in Jones County on Saturday night.

Title: J'khand among top 5 states in road accident deaths
content:
RANCHI: Jharkhand is among one of the top states where road accidents have caused more deaths than injuries contrary to the national trend, according to the latest report - Accidental deaths and suicides in India 2022, published by the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB). Other states with high fatalities are Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Bihar and Mizoram.In 2022, Jharkhand saw 5,175 road accidents in which 3,898 people died and 3,747 got injured, registering a 9.5% rise in accidents vis-a-vis 2021.

Of the total deaths, 3,481 were male and 417 female. In 2021, total road accidents stood at 4,728. Contrary to this, as many as 4,46,768 road accidents were reported in the country in 2022 in which 1,71,100 died and 4,23,158 were injured.According to the report, over-speeding has been the biggest killer on the state roads and accidents and fatalities involved mostly two-wheelers. As many as 1,929 deaths were reported involving two-wheelers and 1,801 were injured.Most of the road accidents cases took place on national highways (2,125) in which 1,699 died. It was followed by state highways with 1,069 cases in which 812 died and 836 were injured. Maximum (933) road accidents took place between 3pm and 6pm. Among the road accident trends, heavy vehicle (truck/lorry) killed 276 people and injured 218. Bus accidents claimed 102 lives and injured 230, while SUV/cars etc killed 473 and injured 527. As many as 83 pedestrians too died and 61 injured after being hit by vehicles.On the cause of accidents, the report stated that as many as 339 cases of callous/over-taking incidents were reported in which 290 died and 532 injured. "In 4,128 incidents of overspeeding, 2,469 died and 4,128 injured and 203 case of drink driving was reported in which 125 died and 219 injured," read the report.Other reasons included fatigue of drivers which led to 23 incidents in which 54 died and 62 injured, while 51 cases of vehicle defect led to 37 deaths and 57 injuries followed by 90 cases of accidents due to animal crossing killing 91 people and injuring 142 persons. Title: Hry among states with highest accident death rates last year
content:
Gurgaon: Haryana is second only to Chhattisgarh among Indian states with the highest accidental deaths per lakh of their population in 2022, data by the National Crime Records Bureau showed.At 56.4, Chhattisgarh topped the list, followed by Haryana at 53.5. Only two Union territories â€” Puducherry (65.7) and Ladakh (78.7) ranked higher. The countryâ€™s average is 31.2.Accidental deaths are fatalities from road accidents, railway accidents, railway crossing accidents, natural calamities, unnatural causes and unreported illnesses.

According to the NCRB report, the number of accidental deaths in Haryana rose 8% from 14,830 in 2021 to 16,041 last year. Of this, 85%, or 13,782, were men.The number of road, railway and railway crossing accidents across the state also went up almost 8%, recording a total of 11,015 in 2021 and 11,876 in 2022, the NCRB report said. Last year, 10,654 road accidents were reported the state.Another 1,127 cases of railway accidents and 95 cases of railway crossing accidents were also recorded in the same period. Over a thousand people (1,101) died in railway accidents and 95 died in railway crossing accidents.Of 36 states and UTs, 19 in the country have recorded accidental deaths higher than the national average.Data for Haryana showed that pedestrians and two-wheeler riders made up for the majority of casualties in accidental deaths.Of the total 5,228 deaths on roads, 2,812 people who died were two-wheeler riders and 1,663 were pedestrians. The months of March (1,131) and December (1,091) recorded the maximum number of traffic accidents, including road, railway and railway crossing accidents. But the lows werenâ€™t too low â€“ at 817, January saw the fewest traffic accidents among the months.Going by the time that the accidents were reported at, rush hours from 6pm to 9pm appear to be the most dangerous. The total number of accidents in that period was 2,345 last year.The others were â€” 1,140 from 12-3am; 902 from 3-6am; 1,411 from 6-9am; 1,513 from 9am-noon; 1,402 from noon to 3pm; 1,705 from 3-6pm; 2,345 from 6pm; and 1,458 from 9pm to 12am.Haryana data also showed that highways were the deadliest for road accidents.Around 3,600 accidents, which claimed 2,924 lives, were reported on national highways, and 922 people were killed in 1,942 accidents on state highways last year. Another 127 accidents and 107 fatalities were reported on expressways.Most of the road accidents were also caused by careless driving or while overtaking. Almost 2,000 people (1,955) died in these crashes. Speeding on roads is another worry. It led to 3,805 accidents that killed 1,976 people, data showed. He sent the video to his friends and colleagues and others at 3am on Thursday. Ramesh was a son-in-law of late Valmiki Naik, the former MLA of Chittapur. Public have expressed anger over agitators leaving the body in hanging state for 14 hours, saying that they could have removed it and agitated by placing it in front of ACC cement administrative building.Fee exemption for children of devadasis, transgendersBallari: To bring the children of transgenders and devadasis to the mainstream, Sri Krishnadevaraya University has decided to exempt them from admission fees, said Registrar SN Rudresh. This measure will be implemented in the current academic year itself. In society, children of transgenders and devadasis are not getting proper facilities to lead a respectable life. Realising that they are being neglected, the university administration has taken this step. The sheep belonged to shepherds from Belagavi and Chikodi, who were taking them for grazing. The estimated value of the dead sheep is said to be Rs 10 lakh.AAP leader bats for caste censusHubballi: Present reservation system is unscientific as the last caste census was done way back in 1931, and giving reservation based on the latest caste census will help small castes also, said Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) Karnataka president Mukhyamantri Chandru. Speaking to reporters here on Thursday, he said that the caste census should be conducted across the country, and the caste census report done in Karnataka should be accepted by the chief minister. "Siddaramaiah should take a bold decision of accepting the caste census report and tabling it in the winter session,â€ he added. Commenting on former minister Goolihatti Shekar's allegation that he was not allowed to enter RSS' Hedgewar Museum in Nagpur for being a Dalit, Mukhyamantri Chandru said it is condemnable if it had really happened. Recalling his days with the BJP, he said he was also not invited for some meetings. Alleging that corruption in Smart City works in Hubballi, Chandru asked the government to conduct an inquiry, failing which a protest will be staged, he said. He also demanded the formation of ward committees at the earliest.Ballari DC releases progress card of six monthsBallari: DC Prashant Kumar Mishra, while releasing the governmentâ€™s six-month progress card for the district, said that the district administration is ready to handle the drought situation. In the details of achievements from June to December, several achievements were described, including social security schemes and the distribution of compensation for farmersâ€™ suicide cases. A total of six farmers have died by suicide in the district and each family has been given a compensation of Rs 5 lakh. A total of 73 new revenue villages have been identified for conversion in the district and a proposal has been submitted to the government to issue a preliminary notification for 14 villages and a final notification for seven villages, DC said. A total of 114 villages in the district are expected to face drinking water problems, and plans have been made to hire private tube wells and supply water through tankers. For this, he said that Rs 37 crore has been kept in the SDRF PD account of the DC and Rs 2.1 crore has been kept with tahsildars.Music promoter Anant Harihar to be felicitatedDharwad: GB Joshi Memorial Trust, Kshamata and Sri Anant Harihar Felicitation Committee will jointly organise a music concert to celebrate the 75th birthday of music promoter and organiser Anant Harihar at Srijana Auditorium in Dharwad on Sunday. Harihar will be felicitated at the event, which will be held from 9.30am to 9.30pm. Hubballi-Dharwad Central MLA Mahesh Tenginakai and former mayor Iresh Anchatageri will be the chief guests at the inaugural session. This will be followed by a vocal recital by Poornima Bhat Kulkarni, and Pandit M Venkateshkumar. After the lunch break, flautist Praveen Godkhindi will perform a concert from 2pm to 3.30pm. This will be followed by a vocal recital by Pt Ganapati Bhat Hasanagi. Union minister Pralhad Joshi, MLA Arvind Bellad and Kshamata convenor Govind Joshi will be the chief guests at the felicitation function commencing at 5.30pm. The programme will conclude with a vocal recital by Pt Raghunandan Phanshikar.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: Over 500 pedestrians die on Delhi roads every year
content:
NEW DELHI: This year 526 pedestrians were killed and 1,698 injured in road accidents. Delhi Traffic Police data shows pedestrians as the most vulnerable group in fatal road crashes, underlining the need to take corrective measures.In 2021, 1,536 pedestrians were injured, of whom 1,269 were men and 267 women. Of the 504 pedestrians killed, 446 were males and 58 females.
Top Picks For You
How the world is facing a 'perfect storm of disinformation'
Microsoft, Flipkart, Swiggy, and more cut hundreds of jobs
Activists throw soup on glass-protected Mona Lisa in Paris
A year later, there was an increase in pedestrian victims, with a total of 1,777 (1,413 men and 364 women) suffering injuries. The number of fatalities also went up to 629 (553 males, 76 females). Till date in 2023, there has been a small decrease since last year both in people injured and killed."Data shows a distinct pattern of pedestrian accidents based on time of day," said SS Yadav, special commissioner of police, Traffic. "In 2021, there were 262 fatalities in daylight hours and 242 at night. The pattern shifted in 2022, when 301 fatalities were recorded during the day and 328 at night. Subsequently, in 2023, the statistics show 240 fatalities during daytime and 284 in the night hours."Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeMaxLife Term Plan Don't settle for less coverage when you can get more.MaxLife Term Plan Get QuoteUndoSpatial analysis carried out by the traffic police, part of Delhi Police's Delhi Crash Report 2022 released on Tuesday, identified 117 crash-prone zones in 2022, based on the criteria of three or more fatal crashes within a diameter of 500 metres or ten or more total road crashes on the same stretch. Ring Road (25 accidents), GT Karnal Road (17) and Outer Ring Road (15) suffered the maximum number of road crashes.TimesViewPedestrians are eco-friendly commuters and must be encouraged and protected. The high number of their deaths is worrying. Often pavements are invaded by two-wheelers. This must end. However, some pedestrians too break traffic rules while crossing red lights. They should be discouraged. Many two-wheelers donâ€™t follow the rules of the road, often to their own peril. They need to be disciplined. A sustained drive to make them wear helmets is also needed.Delhi's urban landscape has areas characterised by an elevated propensity for accidents involving vulnerable road users, notably pedestrians and two-wheelers. Pedestrians face heightened exposure to risks at Britania Chowk, the proximity of GB Pant Hospital, Peera Garhi and the surroundings of metro stations in Madipur. In 2021, Shastri Park (15 accidents), Harnam Palace in Ashok Nagar (12) and Peera Garhi Chowk (9) were determined to have a notable incidence of accidents. According to the crash report, 81% of the accident victims were pedestrians and two-wheeler riders. Head injuries were the leading cause of death and major injuries for two-wheeler users. In 2022, a total of 2,12,440 riders and 44,475 pillion riders were prosecuted by the traffic police for not using helmets, recording a more than 100% growth in riders and pillion riders prosecuted for not using helmets compared with 91,036 riders and 16,724 pillion riders prosecuted in 2021.Yadav said, "Since pedestrians, cyclists and motorcyclists are the most vulnerable users of the roads, the focus will continue to be on road safety measures for these groups. It will also mean prosecuting riders not using helmets and creating awareness about helmets, zebra crossings, subways, encroachment-free safe pedestrian walkways and footpaths. Delhi Traffic Police has recommended design interventions at crash-prone locations to reduce fatalities." Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: 13 out of every 100 killed in road crashes in 2021 were from India: WHO report
content:
NEW DELHI: Thirteen out of every 100 killed in road crashes across the world in 2021 were from India, according to the latest global report on road safety released by the World Health Organisation (WHO) on Wednesday. India is one of the 65 countries where road deaths have increased, with the numbers of fatalities going up from 1.34 lakh in 2010 to 1.54 lakh in 2021.

â€œIt is not a success story of my departmentâ€¦â€ Why Nitin Gadkari apologised in Lok Sabha
Last year, the highest ever at 1.68 lakh lost their lives on roads.Going by the Global Status Report on Road Safety, which compared the fatalities of 2010 and 2021, India had almost 46% of the 3.3 lakh such persons killed in the entire South-East Asia Region. In absolute numbers, India remains at the top among 174 countries in road deaths since the WHO started bringing out the global report.â€œIt is not a success story of my departmentâ€¦â€ Why Nitin Gadkari apologised in Lok SabhaExperts said until India achieves significant reduction in road fatalities, which has increased from 11% of all deaths on the roads in 2010 to 13% in 2021, the global target to halve such fatalities by 2030 can't be met. "When global road crash deaths have fallen, unfortunately it is still rising in India. We need a robust framework and need to take urgent action at all levels to reduce these numbers and with the same intention as we did to deal with Covid-19 pandemic. Deaths are deaths," said Prerna Singh Arora, a member of the Global Alliance of NGOs for Road Safety. Globally, road deaths have fallen by 5% between 2010 and 2021.Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeMaxLife Term Plan A   â‚¹1Cr Term Insurance at just â‚¹702 per month*MaxLife Term Plan Get QuoteUndoAs per the report while the Indian government had provided the number of people killed in 2021 for preparation of the report, the WHO has estimated the fatalities around 2.16 lakh considering the probability of many victims succumbing to road crash injuries months after meeting with the accidents.It has also highlighted how India can step up enforcement of use of child restraints in cars, tighten the legislation on drunk driving and improve post-crash response to reduce the fatalities. Title: Bus crashes into 5 vehicles after driver falls unconscious, 4 die
content:
NOIDA: A UP Roadways bus crashed into three motorcycles, a car and a tractor, killing four men - all of them bikers - after its driver lost consciousness at the wheel while passing through Greater Noida's Dankaur on Wednesday afternoon.Karan (22) from Sikanderabad, Madan (26) from Hathras and Sunil (30) from Bulandshahr died on the spot, while Kamlesh (35) from Mirzapur succumbed to injuries in hospital.
Top Picks For You

'Competition to chameleons': Cong hits out at Nitish Kumar
None of the 20 bus passengers was injured.Police said the bus driver, Devendra, was taken to a clinic in the city and tests will be carried out to diagnose what may have caused him to fall unconscious. An FIR was registered at the Dankaur police station against the bus driver."A complaint was filed by the family members of the bikers who died, and a case of rash driving and causing death by negligence was registered. We will record the statement of the driver once he is fit," said ACP Sushil Ganga Prasad.This is the second such accident in the past few months. In September, a Roadways bus veered off the Delhi-Meerut Expressway in Ghaziabad and rammed into the railing after its driver suffered a heart attack. Some of the 50 bus passengers were injured. The latest incident occurred around 1pm on Wednesday as the UPSRTC bus was moving towards Bulandshahr from Dankaur."The bus was approaching a main road from Mandi Shyan Nagar bridge when the driver apparently had a medical episode and lost consciousness. As passengers tried to take control of the steering wheel, the bus collided with an Apache motorcycle, killing its rider Karan (22) on the spot," said additional DCP (Greater Noida) Ashok Kumar.The bus veered off course, sped and hit the motorcycle that Sunil was riding. "He was flung from the bike, and he died immediately," Kumar said. The bus, while descending from the bridge, collided with a third bike with two passengers. "Madan died on the spot as well, while Kamlesh was critically injured... the bus was still out of control and hit a parked car and a tractor, both with no occupants, before coming to a halt. By that time, the driver was starting to gain consciousness and the passengers managed to take the steering wheel," the additional DCP said.The ACP told TOI that Kamlesh was rushed to NIMS hospital in the city, where he died of his injuries.Cops were also deployed to keep a check on the protests by locals near Astauli village after the crash. Prasad said autopsies will be carried out, and the driver will be made to undergo tests to check if he suffered from any medical ailment. "The matter is being investigated," the ACP said.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: CPR & cloverleaf shut again, traffic headed to SPR now chokes NH-8
content:

There have been several head-on collisions and other accidents on the expressway â€” which is yet to be officially opened to the public â€” in which at least two people have been killed.Several commuters took to social media and questioned the NHAIâ€™s decision of not allowing traffic on Dwarka Expressway when its Gurgaon section is complete.â€œI was headed from Sector 83 to MG Road on Wednesday morning. Going there took me an hour and returning to Sector 83 took 1.5 hours. The traffic chaos will be a daily affair now until the administration doesnâ€™t open CPR and the cloverleaf,â€ said Amresh Mishra, president of Vatika Lifestyle Homes RWA in Sector 83.While NHAI maintained that it has not opened the strech for traffic and commuters frequently remove barricades set up there, residents of new sectors have been demanding that it be opened on a trial basis at least. â€œThe Dwarka Expressway has already overshot several deadlines. Now that it is fully ready, NHAI has decided to keep it closed and is waiting for a VIP to inaugurate it. The road has been built with taxpayersâ€™ money and NHAI has no right to make commuters suffer,â€ said Ivon Singh, a resident of Sector 92 .â€œWhen the infrastructure is ready, why canâ€™t the authorities open the expressway? It seems that the government is intentionally trying to provide benefits to the Kherki Daula toll operator by not allowing traffic to ply on Dwarka Expressway,â€ said Prakhar Sahay, a homebuyer in Sector 82.Traffic cops blamed the chaos on NHAI. â€œTraffic movement has slowed down because of the barriers installed by NHAI. We have deployed additional manpower to ensure smooth traffic flow,â€ an officer said.NHAI officials, meanwhile, said a safety audit of the expressway, including CPR, is under way. â€œWhen we have not opened something to the public, the question of closing it down does not arise. While most of the work has been completed, the safety audit is still going on and it is not advisable to use it until all safety parameters are met,â€ a senior official said. He was tightlipped when asked whether NHAI is waiting for a VIP to inaugurate it.Deputy commissioner Nishant Yadav said he has taken up the issue with NHAI. â€œI am aware about the issue and the administration is taking up the matter with NHAI to resolve the deadlock,â€ he told TOI.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: Cabbie dies in yet another road rage incident in Delhi
content: NEW DELHI: A middle-aged app-based cab driver who told two men in a car next to his to drive properly after alleged close shaves because of their rashness on the road earlier was thrashed to death by the duo at a traffic light near Tagore Garden metro station on Sunday evening.Police tracked down the duo, after watching CCTV footage from several cameras, and arrested them at their home in Paschim Vihar.Jatin and Pawan Samariya, both aged 21 years, deal in scrap and are cousins.Their victim, 56-year-old Ravinder Singh, who was a driver with a cab service, was in his Wagon-R with his nephew Noor, who was in the front seat, and son Taranjot in the rear seat.Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeCare HealthNo Upper Age Limit For Health Insurance.Care HealthGet QuoteUndoNoor said the three were on their way to Motinagar to get the car's headlight fixed the day his uncle became the victim of the fatal road rage crime.He told TOI that when the Samariyas' Creta and their car were standing parallel to each other at the traffic intersection, Singh told them to drive properly. "At this, they just got out of the car and started assaulting us. The driver and the co-driver got out and just started thrashing us. It looked like they were drunk. As I did not have my phone with me, I started asking people for help and to make a call. In the meantime, they started thrashing Taranjot," Noor said.According to Taranjot, the men punched his father. "The back windshield was tinted so we could not see how many people were in the car," he said. Unlike Noor, Taranjot said he saw three people get out of the Creta. "They were definitely drunk. One of them while punching my face also picked up a liquor bottle to hit me but a passer-by saved me. My father's turban came off in the scuffle and they insulted it too," Taranjot said.He said that the Samariyas' car was being driven in such a rash manner that the Wagon-R could have met with an accident twice earlier. "Once, we almost lost control of our car as a woman and her child on the road could have got hit. They were just rash and abusive for no reason and even jumped a red light," Taranjot said.Raja Banthia, additional deputy commissioner of police, west, said: "Singh was in his Wagon-R car with his nephew, Noor, in the co-driver seat, while his son Taranjot was seated behind. They could not note the number of the vehicle and the only clue they provided us was that the other vehicle was a white Creta."Police said Ravinder was declared dead when taken to a hospital.Multiple teams were deployed to track the vehicle down and to catch the accused. After checking CCTV footage and tracing the route taken by the accused, the vehicle was traced and then the Samariya cousins were caught. "A case under sections of voluntarily causing hurt, culpable homicide not amounting to murder and wrongful restraint was registered at Rajouri Garden police station," Banthia said.Singh was the sole breadwinner of his family. The son, Taranjot, was pursuing his Class 10 studies in an open school. Singh's wife Parminder and Taranjot had recently moved to Sant Nagar in Tilak Nagar.Explore Your Financial Landscape with Personalized Credit Insights.

Title: Underpass crash kills 2 as SUV smashes into divider & hits a biker on other side
content: GURGAON: A speeding black Scorpio overturned at the exit of the Rajiv Chowk underpass on Sunday evening, crashing into the divider and landing on the other carriageway, where the SUV hit a bike.The biker- Badshapur-based property dealer Suresh Chandra (55) - died on the spot and the Scorpio driver, construction materials contractor Vipin Yadav (28), died while being treated at Medanta hospital.There were also two men and two women, all residents of Manesar, in the SUV when the accident occurred. The four were injured and are being treated at different hospitals in the city.Police said on Monday they are still trying to identify those injured and get in touch with their families.Eyewitnesses told cops that the Scorpio driver appeared to have lost control at the wheel while exiting the underpass. The Scorpio was likely being driven above the speed limit on the underpass, they said. Cops said they suspect the car was touching 80-90kmph when it crashed. "Eyewitnesses said the Scorpio driver was speeding. The car coming from Sohna's direction hit the divider right before the exit of the underpass. It flipped to the other carriageway and crashed into the bike that was approaching from the opposite side," said ACP (crime) Varun Kumar Dahiya. He added that several cases of breaking the speed limit of 30-50kmph have been observed on underpasses in the city. Another biker who was seconds behind Chandra's Platina motorcycle told TOI he managed to stop "just in time". "The biker in front of me could not have escaped... I was just behind him, and I managed to swerve," he said.Cops said the group in the SUV may have been celebrating a birthday. "We found flowers and birthday greeting cards with photos of one of the women in the crashed car. The SUV's windows were tinted and it was found to be registered (HR26FB4746) under the name of Sanjay Kumar, a Nakhraula resident," a police official said. A complaint was filed by Chandra's nephew Tarun Kumar at the Civil Lines police station. "An FIR was subsequently registered against the Scorpio driver under sections 304A (death by negligence) and 279 (rash driving) of the IPC," SHO Poonam Hooda said. Cops said they are trying to get CCTV footage from the area to see if the accident was caught on camera. "Investigation is underway," the SHO said.2 pedestrians run overHours before the underpass accident, two pedestrians - brothers Mohit Kumar (21) and Vivek (25), workers at a warehouse - were run over by a car in Bilaspur in the early hours of Sunday.The car driver fled the spot, though an eyewitness Rajesh managed to note down the registration number (HR43B4237)"I was returning from work and the duo, also residents of my village Pathreri, were walking in front of me. Around 4am, a speeding car came from the side of the Pathreri bus stand and hit the brothers. They were flung off the road and landed far off. The driver didn't even stop and sped away," Rajesh, who filed the police complaint, said. A police official said an investigation was underway and cops were trying to trace the car driver. Title: Samajwadi Party leader dies after speeding hearse car hit his scooter, colleague critical
content: LUCKNOW: In yet another hit-and-run incident in Lucknow, Samajwadi Party district president from Ballia, Rajmangal Yadav (50) died in a road accident on Sunday.Reportedly, the incident happened around 8:30 am when he had gone out for a walk in the park on his scooter and while coming back, he was hit by a hearse car from behind. His friend Prashant Mishra, who was on the pillion, got seriously injured and is said to be critical.â€œThe accused driver of the vehicle Mohammed Awez, 38 was arrested and the vehicle has also been seized,â€ said Vikram Singh, SHO, Hazratganj.â€œAn FIR is also registered under the relevant sections on the complaint of the deceasedâ€™s son,â€ the SHO added. Title: Overspeeding led to 85% crashes in Telangana as 17 lives lost daily in 2022

In Telangana, overspeed accounted for 85% of total road crashes.

While the total road accidents reported last year was 21,619, collisions caused due to overspeeding was 18,395.During 2020-2022, more than 19,000 persons were killed due to overspeeding in the state. In terms of ranking, the state stands at seventh position in overspeeding accidents, which is higher than bigger states such as Gujarat, UP, Bihar and Odisha.Road safety experts said overspeeding of vehicles has turned out to be a big problem as more than 80% of road crashes are caused due to such menace. While in urban areas, mostly two-wheelers and pedestrians are affected due to overspeeding, four-wheelers and heavy vehicles involve in crashes on highways. "Most of the bikes moving in the city are above 500 cc and our roads are not capable of handling heavy speed. Based on the width of the roads, markings and signages have to be designed for speed limits. Title: PM Modi expresses grief over loss of lives in MP road accident; CM Mohan Yadav meets victims
content:
BHOPAL: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday expressed grief over the loss of lives in a tragic road accident in Madhya Pradesh's Guna district which claimed 13 lives and left 17 injured. The Prime Minister's Office posted on X, "The road accident that occurred in Madhya Pradesh's Guna is heartbreaking. My condolences to those who lost their lives in the accident." "Along with this, I wish for speedy recovery of all the people injured in this accident.

Under the supervision of the state government, the local administration is engaged in providing every possible help to the victims," PM Modi said. A passenger bus collided with a dumper truck and caught fire on Guna-Aron road in Guna district on Wednesday night, killing at least 13 and injuring 17 others, according to the police. The injured are undergoing treatment in the Guna district hospital. Â Â Â Â by TaboolaÂ by TaboolaÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Sponsored LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ Promoted LinksÂ You May LikeOur Better WorldThe Malayan Tiger Is Nearly ExtinctOur Better WorldLearn MoreUndoMadhya Pradesh chief minister Mohan Yadav on Thursday visited the Guna district hospital and met with people injured in the accident and were undergoing treatment there. After meeting the victims of the accident, CM Yadav told ANI, "The incident occurred around 8:30 pm last night. 13 people have died in the accident. I am trying to get every possible detail of the accident and strict action will be taken against the responsible people in it." "After analysing the preliminary investigation, I have decided that the RTO will be suspended immediately for not conducting a checking on vehicles and the CMO (Chief Municipal officer) will also be suspended for the delay in reaching of fire brigade. Strict actions will also be taken on the high-level officials responsible for the incident," he added. Meanwhile, Member of Parliament (MP) from Guna Lok Sabha constituency K P Yadav said that the chief minister took the matter seriously and announced an ex-gratia of Rs 4 lakh each to the family of the deceased. "It is a tragic incident and the chief minister has taken the matter seriously. He has announced financial assistance of Rs 4 lakh each to the family of the deceased and Rs 50,000 each to the injured. Action has also been taken against those who have been negligent following which RTO and CMO have been suspended," KP Yadav said.Guna collector Tarun Rathi said that 13 people died and 17 sustained injuries in the accident. Those who are missing are also being searched and two authorities have been suspended.Besides, a case has been registered against the bus driver and bus operator in the matter, the collector added.
